kikuyu: “Wee Jehova-rĩ, njetereire kũhonokania gwaku.
english: His brothers also went and fell down before his face; and they said, “Behold, we are your servants.”

kikuyu: Matukũ-inĩ ma tene nĩndamenyire kuuma kĩrĩra-inĩ gĩaku, atĩ wagĩthondekire kĩrĩ gĩa gũtũũra nginya tene. Kũhooya Ũteithio
english: Of old I have known from your testimonies, that you have founded them forever. RESH

kikuyu: Ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ, aca! No rĩrĩ, mwaga kwĩrira, o na inyuĩ inyuothe mũkaniinwo o tao.
english: I tell you, no, but unless you repent, you will all perish in the same way.

kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũiyaga, nĩatige gũcooka kũiya, nĩ kaba arute wĩra, akenogagia, na ekage maũndũ mega na moko make, nĩgeetha agĩe na kĩndũ angĩgayana na arĩa mabataire.
english: Let him who stole steal no more; but rather let him labor, producing with his hands something that is good, that he may have something to give to him who has need.

kikuyu: Wee wonanagia wendani waku kũrĩ andũ ngiri na ngiri, no ũkaherithania nĩ ũndũ wa mehia ma maithe, ũkaũkinyĩria ciero-inĩ cia njiarwa ciao iria igooka thuutha wao. Wee Mũrungu mũnene na ũrĩ hinya, o wee rĩĩtwa rĩaku arĩ Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe,
english: “I have surely heard Ephraim grieving thus, ‘You have chastised me, and I was chastised, as an untrained calf. Turn me, and I will be turned, for you are Yahweh my God.

kikuyu: Ndĩramwandĩkĩra, inyuĩ maithe, tondũ nĩmũmenyete ũrĩa warĩ kuo kuuma o kĩambĩrĩria. Ndĩramwandĩkĩra, inyuĩ andũ ethĩ, tondũ nĩmũtooretie ũrĩa mũũru. Ndĩramwandĩkĩra, inyuĩ twana twakwa nyendete, tondũ nĩmũmenyete Ithe witũ.
english: I write to you, fathers, because you know him who is from the beginning. I write to you, young men, because you have overcome the evil one. I write to you, little children, because you know the Father.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo Ũrĩa-Ũrĩ-Igũrũ Mũno, o We ũtũũgĩrĩtio na ũtũũraga tene na tene, ũrĩa wĩtagwo mũtheru; ekuuga atĩrĩ: * 57:6 Mahiga manyoroku maahũthagĩrwo gũtungatĩra ngai cia mĩhianano.
english: For the high and lofty One who inhabits eternity, whose name is Holy, says: “I dwell in the high and holy place, with him also who is of a contrite and humble spirit, to revive the spirit of the humble, and to revive the heart of the contrite.

kikuyu: “ ‘Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, wee mũmaraya ũyũ, igua kiugo kĩa Jehova!
english: “Therefore, prostitute, hear Yahweh’s word:

kikuyu: Ndagakenera tũrũũĩ, njũũĩ iria ithereraga ũũkĩ na ngorono.
english: My breath is offensive to my wife. I am loathsome to the children of my own mother.

kikuyu: na Kaini, na Gibea, na Timina; mothe maarĩ matũũra ikũmi na tũtũũra twamo. Na Halihuli, na Bethi-Zuru, na Gedoru,
english: Kain, Gibeah, and Timnah; ten cities with their villages.

kikuyu: Igũrũ na thĩ nĩigathira, no ciugo ciakwa itigathira. Mũthenya na Thaa Itiũĩkaine
english: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will not pass away.

kikuyu: Arokũrehere ũteithio kuuma handũ harĩa hake haamũre, na agũtiirĩrĩre arĩ kũu Zayuni.
english: My God, I cry in the daytime, but you don’t answer; in the night season, and am not silent.

kikuyu: Nguraro na kwĩnyararithia nĩcio igai rĩake, na cio thoni icio ciake gũtirĩ hĩndĩ igaathario ithire;
english: He will get wounds and dishonor. His reproach will not be wiped away.

kikuyu: O ta ũrĩa makara magwatagia marĩa mangĩ mwaki, na o ta ũrĩa ngũ ciakanagia mwaki, noguo mũndũ mũũgiti aakagĩrĩria haaro.
english: As coals are to hot embers, and wood to fire, so is a contentious man to kindling strife.

kikuyu: Ngĩĩra Beliteshazaru, mũnene wa ago othe atĩrĩ, “Nĩnjũũĩ atĩ roho wa ngai iria theru ũrĩ thĩinĩ waku, na gũtirĩ ũndũ ũrĩ hitho-inĩ ũngĩkũrema. Gĩkĩ nĩkĩo kĩroto gĩakwa; nawe he ũtaũri wakĩo.
english: “Belteshazzar, master of the magicians, because I know that the spirit of the holy gods is in you and no secret troubles you, tell me the visions of my dream that I have seen, and its interpretation.

kikuyu: Isiraeli agĩtunya Aamori matũũra mothe mao marĩa manene na magĩtũũra kuo, o hamwe na Heshiboni na tũtũũra tuothe tũrĩa twarigainie nakuo.
english: Israel took all these cities. Israel lived in all the cities of the Amorites, in Heshbon, and in all its villages.

kikuyu: Paũlũ kwambĩrĩria Rũgendo rwa gũthiĩ Roma Na rĩrĩa gwatuirwo atĩ nĩtũkũhaica marikabu tũthiĩ Italia, Paũlũ na andũ arĩa angĩ mooheetwo makĩneanwo kũrĩ mũnene-wa-thigari-igana rĩmwe, warĩ wa mbũtũ ya Agusito, wetagwo Juliasi.
english: 1 When it was determined that we should sail for Italy, they delivered Paul and certain other prisoners to a centurion named Julius, of the Augustan band.

kikuyu: O na gũtuĩka Elinathani, na Delia, na Gemaria nĩmathaithire mũthamaki ndagacine ibuku rĩu rĩa gĩkũnjo-rĩ, nĩaregire kũmathikĩrĩria.
english: Moreover Elnathan and Delaiah and Gemariah had made intercession to the king that he would not burn the scroll; but he would not listen to them.

kikuyu: Makomaga o ro ũndũ ũmwe tĩĩri-inĩ, na eerĩ-rĩ, igunyũ ikamahumbĩra.
english: They lie down alike in the dust. The worm covers them.

kikuyu: Akiuga atĩrĩ, “Onei, nĩndĩrona igũrũ rĩhingũkĩte nake Mũrũ wa Mũndũ arũgamĩte guoko-inĩ kwa ũrĩo kwa Ngai.”
english: and said, “Behold, I see the heavens opened and the Son of Man standing at the right hand of God!”

kikuyu: Jehova ekuuga atĩrĩ, “Ũkai tũciire. O na mehia manyu mangĩkorwo matunĩhĩte ta gakarakũ-rĩ, nĩmagathera o ta ira; o na maatuĩka matunĩhĩte ta thakame, nĩmakerũha o ta guoya wa ngʼondu.
english: “Come now, and let’s reason together,” says Yahweh: “Though your sins are as scarlet, they shall be as white as snow. Though they are red like crimson, they shall be as wool.

kikuyu: Nĩũkagũa werũ-inĩ ũtarĩ kĩndũ, nĩgũkorwo nĩ niĩ njarĩtie ũhoro ũcio, ũguo nĩguo Mwathani Jehova ekuuga.
english: You will fall on the open field, for I have spoken it,” says the Lord Yahweh.

kikuyu: Ndeto njega nĩ igua rĩa ũũkĩ, irĩ mũrĩo ngoro-inĩ, na harĩ mahĩndĩ nĩ ũgima.
english: Pleasant words are a honeycomb, sweet to the soul, and health to the bones.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo nguona ũiyũrĩtwo nĩ marũrũ na ũgatuĩka ngombo ya mehia.”
english: For I see that you are in the poison of bitterness and in the bondage of iniquity.”

kikuyu: Kĩhuhũkanio kĩnene gĩkaamahaata kĩmombũkie, na magongona mao nĩmakamaconorithia.
english: The wind has wrapped her up in its wings; and they shall be disappointed because of their sacrifices.

kikuyu: Ũkĩrai, tũthiĩ! Ũrĩa ũkũngunyanĩra nĩarooka.” Jesũ Kũnyiitwo
english: Arise! Let’s get going. Behold, he who betrays me is at hand.”

kikuyu: Wĩteithũre o ta ũrĩa thwariga ĩĩteithũraga kuuma moko-inĩ ma mũguĩmi, o na kana ta ũrĩa nyoni ĩĩteithũraga kuuma mũtego-inĩ wa mũtegi.
english: Free yourself, like a gazelle from the hand of the hunter, like a bird from the snare of the fowler.

kikuyu: Nũũ mũtumumu tiga o ndungata yakwa, o na kana nũũ ũtaiguaga ta mũtũmwo ũcio ndũmĩte? Nũũ mũtumumu ta ũrĩa wĩheanĩte biũ kũrĩ niĩ, na nũũ mũtumumu ta ndungata ya Jehova?
english: Who is blind, but my servant? Or who is as deaf as my messenger whom I send? Who is as blind as he who is at peace, and as blind as Yahweh’s servant?

kikuyu: na ndegwa igĩrĩ, na ndũrũme ithano, na thenge ithano, na tũtũrũme tũtano twa mwaka ũmwe, irutwo irĩ igongona rĩa ũiguano. Ici nĩcio indo iria ciarutirwo nĩ Abidani mũrũ wa Gideoni.
english: and for the sacrifice of peace offerings, two head of cattle, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs a year old. This was the offering of Abidan the son of Gideoni.

kikuyu: Hamwe na ndegwa, na ndũrũme, na tũtũrũme tũu, mũhaaragĩrieimaruta macio ma mũtu, na maruta ma kũnyuuokũringana na mũigana ũrĩa ũtuĩtwo.
english: He shall stand before Eleazar the priest, who shall inquire for him by the judgment of the Urim before Yahweh. At his word they shall go out, and at his word they shall come in, both he and all the children of Israel with him, even all the congregation.”

kikuyu: Wee Jehova, hingũra igũrũ ũikũrũke; hutia irĩma, nĩgeetha irute ndogo.
english: I will meditate on the glorious majesty of your honor, on your wondrous works.

kikuyu: Tĩĩri wa gĩtuamba nĩũrĩ mũrĩo harĩ we; andũ othe nĩwe marũmagĩrĩra, na kĩrĩndĩ gĩtangĩtarĩka gĩgathiiaga mbere yake.
english: The clods of the valley will be sweet to him. All men will draw after him, as there were innumerable before him.

kikuyu: “Ha ũhoro wa mĩhĩrĩga ĩyo ĩngĩ ĩtigarĩte: Benjamini akaaheo gĩcigo kĩmwe; nakĩo gĩgaatam- bũrũka kuuma mwena wa irathĩro nginya mwena wa ithũĩro. DANIELI Gũthomithio kwa Danieli
english: In whatever tribe the stranger lives, there you shall give him his inheritance,” says the Lord Yahweh.

kikuyu: Ũrĩa ũmbaga irĩma nowe ũmbaga rũhuho, na akaguũrĩria mũndũ meciiria make, o we ũtũmaga rũciinĩ kũgĩe nduma, na agathiĩra thĩ kũrĩa gũtũũgĩru, rĩĩtwa rĩake nĩ Jehova Ngai Mwene-Hinya-Wothe.
english: Therefore a prudent person keeps silent in such a time, for it is an evil time.

kikuyu: Wee Ngai-rĩ, nyũmbĩra ngoro theru thĩinĩ wakwa, na ũnjerũhĩrie roho wa kwĩhokeka thĩinĩ wakwa.
english: Create in me a clean heart, O God. Renew a right spirit within me.

kikuyu: “Ningĩ-rĩ, mũndũ mũthingu angĩgarũrũka atigane na ũthingu wake eke maũndũ mooru, na niĩ ndĩmwĩkĩrĩre mũhĩnga mbere yake-rĩ, nĩagaakua. Kũngĩkorwo ndwamũkaanirie-rĩ, nĩagaakua nĩ ũndũ wa mehia make. Maũndũ marĩa aneeka ma ũthingu matikaaririkanwo, nawe nĩwe ngooria thakame yake. No rĩrĩ, wee ũngĩkaania mũndũ ũcio mũthingu atige kwĩhia, nake aage kwĩhia- rĩ, ti-itherũ nĩagatũũra muoyo nĩ ũndũ nĩathikĩrĩirie ũguo wamũkaanirie, nawe-rĩ, nĩũgaakorwo ũhonoketie muoyo waku.”
english: “Again, when a righteous man turns from his righteousness and commits iniquity, and I lay a stumbling block before him, he will die. Because you have not given him warning, he will die in his sin, and his righteous deeds which he has done will not be remembered; but I will require his blood at your hand.

kikuyu: Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe ekuuga atĩrĩ, “Muugaga atĩrĩ, ‘Kaĩ ũhoro ũcio nĩ mũritũ-ĩ!’ Na mũgatembania iromo.” Jehova ekũũria atĩrĩ, “Rĩrĩa mũkũrehe nyamũ iria ndiihangie, na iria iigũthua, kana iria ndwaru mũgaaciruta irĩ magongona-rĩ, no ndĩgĩciĩtĩkĩre kuuma harĩ inyuĩ?”
english: You say also, ‘Behold,§ what a weariness it is!’ And you have sniffed at it”, says Yahweh of Armies; “and you have brought that which was taken by violence, the lame, and the sick; thus you bring the offering. Should I accept this at your hand?” says Yahweh.

kikuyu: magĩcookanĩrĩra hamwe nĩguo mahũũrane na Joshua na Isiraeli. No rĩrĩ, rĩrĩa andũ a Gibeoni* maaiguire ũrĩa Joshua ekĩte itũũra rĩa Jeriko o na rĩa Ai,
english: they gathered themselves together to fight with Joshua and with Israel, with one accord.

kikuyu: njigua na ũnjĩtĩke. Meciiria makwa nĩmathĩĩnĩtie na ndĩ mũgiĩku
english: Attend to me, and answer me. I am restless in my complaint, and moan

kikuyu: na nĩ guo hitho ĩrĩa ĩtũire ĩrĩ hithe kuuma o tene, njiarwa na njiarwa, no rĩu nĩũguũrĩirio arĩa aamũre.
english: the mystery which has been hidden for ages and generations. But now it has been revealed to his saints,

kikuyu: “ ‘Ngombo cianyu cia arũme na cia andũ-a-nja iriumaga ndũrĩrĩ-inĩ iria imũrigiicĩirie; kuuma kũrĩ ndũrĩrĩ icio no mũgũrage ngombo.
english: “ ‘As for your male and your female slaves, whom you may have from the nations that are around you, from them you may buy male and female slaves.

kikuyu: “Nake akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, ‘Ndĩ na thiirĩ wa marebe magana meerĩ ma maguta ma mĩtamaiyũ.’ “Mũrori ũcio akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, ‘Oya marũa maku ma thiirĩ ũikare thĩ narua wandĩke atĩ ũrĩ na thiirĩ wa marebe igana rĩmwe.’
english: The Lord said, “If you had faith like a grain of mustard seed, you would tell this sycamore tree, ‘Be uprooted and be planted in the sea,’ and it would obey you.

kikuyu: Ũguo nĩguo ndũũrĩte: Ndaarugagwo nĩ ũrugarĩ wa mũthenya na ngarĩĩo nĩ heho ya ũtukũ, naguo toro ũkĩnjũrĩra.
english: This was my situation: in the day the drought consumed me, and the frost by night; and my sleep fled from my eyes.

kikuyu: kũu “ ‘kĩgunyũ kĩrĩa kĩmarĩĩaga gĩtikuaga, naguo mwaki ũrĩa ũmacinaga ndũhoraga.’
english: ‘where their worm doesn’t die, and the fire is not quenched.’ *

kikuyu: Nũũ ũtangĩgwĩtigĩra, na agoocithie rĩĩtwa rĩaku, wee Mwathani? Nĩgũkorwo Wee wiki nĩwe Mũtheru. Ndũrĩrĩ ciothe nĩigooka ihooe irĩ mbere yaku, nĩgũkorwo ciĩko ciaku cia ũthingu nĩiguũrĩtio.”
english: The third poured out his bowl into the rivers and springs of water, and they became blood.

kikuyu: Andũ eerĩ maahota gũtwarana atĩa matarĩ na karĩĩko?
english: Do two walk together, unless they have agreed?

kikuyu: Nĩndaringĩrĩirie Tito oke kũrĩ inyuĩ, na ngĩtũma mũrũ wa Ithe witũ moke nake. Tito ndaigana kũmũhatĩrĩria, kana nĩamũhatĩrĩirie? Githĩ tũtiarutire wĩra thĩinĩ wa roho o ro ũmwe, na tũkĩrũmĩrĩra njĩra o ro ĩmwe?
english: For those who are such don’t serve our Lord Jesus Christ, but their own belly; and by their smooth and flattering speech they deceive the hearts of the innocent.

kikuyu: Ngai akiuga atĩrĩ, “Nĩ niĩ Ngai, Ngai wa thoguo. Ndũgetigĩre gũikũrũka ũthiĩ Misiri, tondũ nĩngagũtua rũrĩrĩ rũnene mũno kũu.
english: He said, “I am God, the God of your father. Don’t be afraid to go down into Egypt, for there I will make of you a great nation.

kikuyu: Wee Jehova, ũrĩ mũthingu, na mawatho maku nĩmagĩrĩire.
english: You are righteous, Yahweh. Your judgments are upright.

kikuyu: “Kũgĩa na mbaũ nyingĩ mũno cia mĩtarakwa-rĩ, no gũtũme ũtuĩke mũthamaki? Githĩ thoguo ndaarĩ o na gĩa kũrĩa na gĩa kũnyua? Ekaga maũndũ marĩa maagĩrĩire na ma kĩhooto, nĩ ũndũ ũcio maũndũ make mothe makĩgaacĩra.
english: “Should you reign because you strive to excel in cedar? Didn’t your father eat and drink, and do justice and righteousness? Then it was well with him.

kikuyu: Ihinda rĩrĩa mũthamaki aatuĩte atĩ andũ acio magaatwarwo nyũmba yake thĩinĩ rĩathira-rĩ, mũrori wa anene arĩa angĩ agĩkĩmatwara harĩ Nebukadinezaru.
english: “The east side, between Hauran, Damascus, Gilead, and the land of Israel, shall be the Jordan; from the north border to the east sea you shall measure. This is the east side.

kikuyu: Nĩmahinganĩtie ngoro ciao nyũmũ, † 16:5 Gĩkombe gĩkĩ nĩ kĩrĩa mũndũ aaheagwo hĩndĩ ya gũkũngũĩra maũndũ.
english: They close up their callous hearts. With their mouth they speak proudly.

kikuyu: Gatagatĩ-inĩ ka andũ acio nĩ haarĩ na mũtumia ũmwe watũire oiraga thakame mĩaka ikũmi na ĩĩrĩ, na gũtirĩ mũndũ ũngĩamũhonirie.
english: Simon answered, “He, I suppose, to whom he forgave the most.” He said to him, “You have judged correctly.”

kikuyu: Joshua akĩmatooria kuuma Kadeshi-Barinea nginya Gaza na kuuma mwena ũcio wothe wa Gosheni† nginya Gibeoni.
english: Joshua struck them from Kadesh Barnea even to Gaza, and all the country of Goshen, even to Gibeon.

kikuyu: Ũrĩa ũrĩ na matũ ma kũigua nĩaigue.
english: He who has ears to hear, let him hear.

kikuyu: Jesũ Gũtũma Arutwo arĩa Ikũmi na Eerĩ * 9:17 Ndibei yekagĩrwo mondo ciatumagwo na njũũa cia mbũri.
english: Take no bag for your journey, neither two coats, nor sandals, nor staff: for the laborer is worthy of his food.

kikuyu: Gũtirĩ hĩndĩ ĩngĩ mũndũ mũkĩĩgu agaacooka kũgĩa igweta, kana kĩmaramari gĩcooke gũtĩĩo mũno.
english: The fool will no longer be called noble, nor the scoundrel be highly respected.

kikuyu: Ũũ nĩguo Mwathani Jehova ekuuga: Nĩgũkorwo kĩrĩma-inĩ gĩakwa gĩtheru, kĩrĩma kĩrĩa kĩraihu gĩa Isiraeli, kũu bũrũri ũcio nĩkuo andũ a nyũmba yothe ya Isiraeli† makaandungatagĩra, na kũu nĩkuo ngaametĩkĩrĩra. Kũu nĩkuo ngaabatario nĩ maruta manyu na iheo cianyu iria njega, o hamwe na magongona manyu mothe marĩa maamũre.
english: For in my holy mountain, in the mountain of the height of Israel,” says the Lord Yahweh, “there all the house of Israel, all of them, shall serve me in the land. There I will accept them, and there I will require your offerings and the first fruits of your offerings, with all your holy things.

kikuyu: Ehumbĩte nguo ĩrindĩtwo thakame-inĩ, narĩo rĩĩtwa rĩake etagwo Kiugo-kĩa-Ngai.
english: He is clothed in a garment sprinkled with blood. His name is called “The Word of God.”

kikuyu: magĩtherera igũrũ wa irĩma, magĩikũrũka cianda-inĩ, magĩtherera na kũrĩa watuĩte mathiĩ.
english: The mountains rose, the valleys sank down, to the place which you had assigned to them.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩngaherithia arĩa othe maamũhinyagĩrĩria; nĩngahonokia arĩa ithua, na njookererie arĩa mahurunjĩtwo. Nĩngatũma magĩe igweta na ndĩmagoocithie mabũrũri-inĩ mothe kũrĩa manaconorithio.
english: Behold,† at that time I will deal with all those who afflict you; and I will save those who are lame and gather those who were driven away. I will give them praise and honor, whose shame has been in all the earth.

kikuyu: nake Eliasafu mũrũ wa Deueli nĩwe warĩ mũtongoria wa gĩkundi kĩa mũhĩrĩga wa Gadi. Nao Akohathu makiumagara makuuĩte indo iria nyamũre. Hema-ĩrĩa-Nyamũre yarĩ ĩkorwo yaambĩtwo matanakinya.
english: but a whole month, until it comes out at your nostrils, and it is loathsome to you; because you have rejected Yahweh who is among you, and have wept before him, saying, “Why did we come out of Egypt?” ’ ”

kikuyu: O na gũtuĩka ndirĩ bata na nĩnyararĩtwo-rĩ, ndiriganagĩrwo nĩ mawatho maku.
english: I am small and despised. I don’t forget your precepts.

kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo nĩmaremeire Roho wa Ngai, nake Musa akĩaria ciugo itaagĩrĩire.
english: because they were rebellious against his spirit, he spoke rashly with his lips.

kikuyu: Kaĩ ngʼombe nĩicaaĩte-ĩ. Ndũũru cia ngʼombe iroorũũra o ũguo, tondũ itirĩ na gwa kũrĩithio; o nacio ndũũru cia ngʼondu nĩirathĩĩnĩka.
english: How the animals groan! The herds of livestock are perplexed, because they have no pasture. Yes, the flocks of sheep are made desolate.

kikuyu: Mũkaamũtua ta mũruti wa wĩra wa mũcaara kana ta mũndũ ũrarĩrĩire kwanyu; mũndũ ũcio akaamũrutĩra wĩra nginya Mwaka wa Jubilii.
english: As a hired servant, and as a temporary resident, he shall be with you; he shall serve with you until the Year of Jubilee.

kikuyu: Bũrũri wanyu ũkirĩte ihooru, matũũra manyu manene magacinwo na mwaki; mĩgũnda yanyu ĩramathwo ĩgatigwo ũtheri nĩ andũ a kũngĩ o mũkĩonaga, ĩkoonũhwo ta ĩngʼaũranĩtio nĩ ageni.*
english: Their land is full of silver and gold, neither is there any end of their treasures. Their land also is full of horses, neither is there any end of their chariots.

kikuyu: Mũira wa maheeni ndakaaga kũherithio, na ũrĩa ũitũrũraga maheeni nĩakoorwo nĩ muoyo.
english: A false witness shall not be unpunished. He who utters lies shall perish.

kikuyu: Matũũra ma mbere ma ũthamaki wake maarĩ Babeli, na Ereku, na Akadi na Kaline, kũu Shinaru.‡
english: The beginning of his kingdom was Babel, Erech, Accad, and Calneh, in the land of Shinar.

kikuyu: “ ‘Ningĩ andũ a Suriata nĩmarutithanirie wĩra wa wonjoria nawe tondũ wa indo ciaku kũingĩha; nĩmakũũranirie indo ciaku cia wonjoria na tũhiga twa thumarati, na mataama ma rangi wa ndathi, na nguo iria ngʼemie, na gatani ĩrĩa njega, na maricani, o na tũhiga twa goro tũrĩa tũtune.
english: “ ‘ “Syria was your merchant by reason of the multitude of your handiworks. They traded for your wares with emeralds, purple, embroidered work, fine linen, coral, and rubies.

kikuyu: “Matũũra matandatũ ma macio mũkaahe Alawii magaatuĩka ma kũũrĩrwo, kũrĩa mũndũ ũraganĩte angĩũrĩra. Hamwe na macio matandatũ, mahei matũũra mangĩ mĩrongo ĩna na meerĩ.
english: “The cities which you shall give to the Levites, they shall be the six cities of refuge, which you shall give for the man slayer to flee to. Besides them you shall give forty-two cities.

kikuyu: “Mũhehenjaga andũ akwa, na mũkamemenda mothiũ ma athĩĩni nĩkĩ?” nĩguo Mwathani Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe ekũũria.
english: What do you mean that you crush my people, and grind the face of the poor?” says the Lord, Yahweh of Armies.

kikuyu: Thiĩ rũũru-inĩ rwa mbũri ũndehere tũũri twĩrĩ twa mbũri tũrĩa twega biũ, nĩguo thondekere thoguo irio njega, o ta ũrĩa endaga ithondekwo.
english: Abimelech called Isaac, and said, “Behold, surely she is your wife. Why did you say, ‘She is my sister’?” Isaac said to him, “Because I said, ‘Lest I die because of her.’ ”

kikuyu: Kuuma ũkengi ũrĩa warĩ harĩ we, mbura ya mbembe na heni igũtũrĩkanĩria matu-inĩ make.
english: Who can discern his errors?  or, seed

kikuyu: Nĩndahũũrirwo na thanju maita matatũ, na riita rĩmwe ngĩhũũrwo na mahiga nyuguto,* na maita matatũ ndanathũkĩrwo nĩ marikabu ndĩ thĩinĩ, na ngĩraara o na ngĩtinda iria-inĩ ũtukũ ũmwe na mũthenya mũgima.
english: §  The word for “brothers” here and where context allows may also be correctly translated “brothers and sisters” or “siblings.”  TR places

kikuyu: “Nĩwoinangire icooki rĩaku rĩa ngingo o tene, na ũgĩtuuanga mĩhĩndo yaku; woigire atĩrĩ, ‘Ndigũcooka gũgũtungata!’ Tondũ-rĩ, tũrĩma-inĩ tũrĩa tuothe tũraihu na igũrũ, na rungu rwa mũtĩ o wothe mũruru nĩkuo wakomaga ũkĩhũũra ũmaraya.
english: “For long ago I broke off your yoke, and burst your bonds. You said, ‘I will not serve;’ for on every high hill and under every green tree you bowed yourself, playing the prostitute.

kikuyu: Maũndũ mega nĩmaragio igũrũ rĩaku, wee itũũra inene rĩa Ngai:
english: For my soul is full of troubles. My life draws near to Sheol.†

kikuyu: Ndũrĩrĩ nĩikoona ũthingu waku, nao athamaki othe mone riiri waku; nawe ũgeetagwo na rĩĩtwa rĩerũ, rĩrĩa rĩkaagwetwo na kanua ka Jehova. Ningĩ ũgaatuĩka ta tanji ya ũthaka ũnyiitĩtwo guoko-inĩ kwa Jehova, na ũhaana ta thũmbĩ ya ũthamaki ĩnyiitĩtwo guoko-inĩ kwa Ngai waku.
english: The nations will see your righteousness, and all kings your glory. You will be called by a new name, which Yahweh’s mouth will name. “Behold”, from “ἰδοὺ”, means look at, take notice, observe, see, or gaze at. It is often used as an interjection.  TR adds “the Beginning and the End”  TR omits “God”  TR adds “I am the Alpha and the Omega, the First and the Last.”  TR adds “which are in Asia”

kikuyu: Andũ othe arĩa maagaĩirwo gũikara kambĩ ya Juda, kũringana na ikundi ciao-rĩ, nĩ andũ 186,400. Acio nĩo makaamba kuumagara.
english: “All who were counted of the camp of Judah were one hundred eighty-six thousand four hundred, according to their divisions. They shall set out first.

kikuyu: Endanagai mũndũ na ũrĩa ũngĩ, mũrĩ na wendani ta wa ciana cia ithe ũmwe. Na mũndũ nĩatĩĩage ũrĩa ũngĩ gũkĩra we mwene.
english: In love of the brothers be tenderly affectionate to one another; in honor prefer one another,

kikuyu: Arata akwa endwa, o na gũtuĩka nĩndĩrendete mũno kũmwandĩkĩra ũhoro wa ũhonokio ũrĩa tũgwatanĩire na inyuĩ-rĩ, nĩndĩronire kwagĩrĩire ndĩmwandĩkĩre nĩguo ndĩmũringĩrĩrie mũrũagĩrĩre wĩtĩkio ũrĩa wehokeirwo andũ arĩa aamũre ihinda rĩmwe rĩa kũigana.
english: Beloved, while I was very eager to write to you about our common salvation, I was constrained to write to you exhorting you to contend earnestly for the faith which was once for all delivered to the saints.

kikuyu: Cioneki ciao nĩ cia maheeni, na ũragũri wao o naguo no wa maheeni. Moigaga atĩrĩ, “Jehova oigĩte atĩrĩ,” o rĩrĩa Jehova atamatũmĩte; merĩgagĩrĩra atĩ ciugo ciao nĩikahinga!
english: They have seen falsehood and lying divination, who say, ‘Yahweh says;’ but Yahweh has not sent them. They have made men to hope that the word would be confirmed.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo mathĩĩna matangĩtarĩka nĩmandigiicĩirie; mehia makwa nĩmanginyĩrĩire, ngaremwo nĩkuona. Maingĩhĩte gũkĩra njuĩrĩ cia mũtwe wakwa, na ngoro yakwa nĩyũrĩtwo nĩ hinya.
english: As for me, you uphold me in my integrity, and set me in your presence forever.

kikuyu: Wee nĩwe waringire ciama o na ũkĩonania morirũ kũu bũrũri wa Misiri, na no wĩkaga ũguo nginya ũmũthĩ, gũkũ Isiraeli o na gatagatĩ-inĩ ka andũ arĩa angĩ othe, namo magatũma wĩgĩĩre igweta rĩrĩa rĩrĩ rĩaku.
english: Is Ephraim my dear son? Is he a darling child? For as often as I speak against him, I still earnestly remember him. Therefore my heart yearns for him. I will surely have mercy on him,” says Yahweh.

kikuyu: na Dimuna, na Nahalali, hamwe na ithaka ciamo cia ũrĩithio; mothe maarĩ matũũra mana;
english: Dimnah with its pasture lands, and Nahalal with its pasture lands: four cities.

kikuyu: Magĩtunyana itũũra rĩu, na makĩrĩniina na rũhiũ rwa njora hamwe na mũthamaki warĩo, na tũtũũra twarĩo, na arĩa othe maarĩ thĩinĩ watuo. Matiatigirie mũndũ o na ũmwe arĩ muoyo. Nao makĩrĩananga biũ hamwe na arĩa othe maarĩ thĩinĩ warĩo, o ta ũrĩa meekĩte Egiloni.
english: They took it, and struck it with the edge of the sword, with its king and all its cities, and all the souls who were in it. He left no one remaining, according to all that he had done to Eglon; but he utterly destroyed it, and all the souls who were in it.

kikuyu: Nĩũũmenyithĩtie njĩra ya gũthiĩ muoyo-inĩ; nĩũkanjiyũria gĩkeno ndĩ harĩa ũrĩ, hamwe na mwago wa tene na tene ndĩ guoko-inĩ gwaku kwa ũrĩo.
english: They have now surrounded us in our steps. They set their eyes to cast us down to the earth.

kikuyu: Mũnene ũcio wa mbũtũ akĩnyiita mwanake ũcio guoko, akĩmũtwara keeheri-inĩ, akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Nĩ atĩa ũrenda kũnjĩĩra?” Nake mwanake ũcio akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Ayahudi nĩmaiguanĩire makũũrie ũmarehere Paũlũ nĩguo arũgame mbere ya kĩama rũciũ, metuĩte ta marenda gũtuĩria ũhoro wake wega.
english: The commanding officer took him by the hand, and going aside, asked him privately, “What is it that you have to tell me?”

kikuyu: nake akĩaria ndũmĩrĩri yake, akiuga atĩrĩ: “Ĩno nĩyo ndũmĩrĩri ya Balamu mũrũ wa Beori, ndũmĩrĩri ya mũndũ ũrĩa riitho rĩake rĩonaga wega,
english: He took up his parable, and said, “Balaam the son of Beor says, the man whose eyes are open says;

kikuyu: Arokũhe maũndũ marĩa ngoro yaku ĩĩrirĩirie,
english: He asked life of you and you gave it to him, even length of days forever and ever.

kikuyu: “Jehova ekuuga atĩrĩ: ‘Ũguo nĩguo ngaaniina rũtũrĩko rwa Juda o na rũtũrĩko rũnene rwa Jerusalemu.
english: Why should you be like a scared man, as a mighty man who can’t save? Yet you, Yahweh, are in the middle of us, and we are called by your name. Don’t leave us.

kikuyu: Ndũkanoohie mũndũ mũthingu njĩra-inĩ ũtharĩkĩre nyũmba yake ta mũitũ, ndũkanahithũkĩre gĩikaro gĩake;
english: Don’t lay in wait, wicked man, against the habitation of the righteous. Don’t destroy his resting place;

kikuyu: Wee nĩwĩtĩkĩtie atĩ Ngai no ũmwe. Ũguo nĩ wega! O nacio ndaimono nĩciĩtĩkĩtie ũguo, na ikainaina.
english: You believe that God is one. You do well. The demons also believe—and shudder.

kikuyu: Nyũmba ĩrĩa nini ĩkongerereka ĩtuĩke andũ ngiri, nayo ĩrĩa ĩtarĩ andũ aingĩ ĩtuĩke rũrĩrĩ rũrĩ hinya mũno. Niĩ nĩ niĩ Jehova. Nĩngeeka ũndũ ũcio narua ihinda rĩaguo rĩakinya.”
english: The little one will become a thousand, and the small one a strong nation. I, Yahweh, will do this quickly in its time.”

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo Jesũ nĩathĩte ngoma ĩyo thũku yume thĩinĩ wa mũndũ ũcio. Nĩyamũnyiitaga kaingĩ, na o na gũtuĩka nĩoohagwo moko na magũrũ na mĩnyororo, na akarangĩrwo-rĩ, nĩatuangaga mĩnyororo ĩyo yamuohaga, na ndaimono ĩyo ĩgatũma athiĩ kũndũ gũtarĩ andũ.
english: When all the people and the tax collectors heard this, they declared God to be just, having been baptized with John’s baptism.

kikuyu: “ ‘Mũndũ mũrũme angĩkoma na nyamũ-rĩ, no nginya mũndũ ũcio ooragwo, na no nginya mũũrage nyamũ ĩyo.
english: Give diligence to present yourself approved by God, a workman who doesn’t need to be ashamed, properly handling the Word of Truth.

kikuyu: Tiga gwĩtigĩra, wee Jakubu kagunyũ gaka, o wee Isiraeli gakundi kanini, niĩ mwene nĩ niĩ ngũgũteithia,” ũguo nĩguo Jehova ekuuga, ũcio Mũkũũri waku, Ũrĩa Mũtheru wa Isiraeli.
english: Don’t be afraid, you worm Jacob, and you men of Israel. I will help you,” says Yahweh. “Your Redeemer is the Holy One of Israel.

kikuyu: Andũ a Zebuluni nĩmetwarĩrĩire ũgwati-inĩ wa gĩkuũ; o nake Nafitali agĩĩka o ũguo kũu gũtũũgĩru werũ-inĩ.
english: Please don’t go away until I come to you, and bring out my present, and lay it before you.”  The word translated “Lord” is “Adonai.”

kikuyu: Matukũ-inĩ ma Joiakimu, aya nĩo maarĩ atongoria a nyũmba cia athĩnjĩri-Ngai: ya Seraia, aarĩ Meraia; ya Jeremia, aarĩ Hanania;
english: and their brothers who did the work of the house, eight hundred twenty-two; and Adaiah the son of Jeroham, the son of Pelaliah, the son of Amzi, the son of Zechariah, the son of Pashhur, the son of Malchijah,

kikuyu: Agĩcooka agĩtũma ndungata ciake kũrĩ andũ arĩa meetĩtwo iruga-inĩ, ikameere moke, nao makĩrega gũũka.
english: and sent out his servants to call those who were invited to the wedding feast, but they would not come.

kikuyu: Ndĩ na kĩeha mũno tondũ gũkuoneka atĩ wĩra ũrĩa wothe ndanenogia naguo nĩ ũndũ wanyu nĩ wa tũhũ!
english: I am afraid for you, that I might have wasted my labor for you.

kikuyu: Mwĩhumbei nguo cia makũnia, inyuĩ athĩnjĩri-Ngai, na mũcakae; girĩkai, inyuĩ mũtungataga kĩgongona-inĩ. Ũkai, mũraare mwĩhumbĩte nguo cia makũnia, inyuĩ mũtungatagĩra Ngai wakwa; nĩgũkorwo maruta ma ngano, na maruta ma kũnyuuo-rĩ, nĩmagĩte gũtwarwo nyũmba-inĩ ya Ngai wanyu.
english: Put on sackcloth and mourn, you priests! Wail, you ministers of the altar. Come, lie all night in sackcloth, you ministers of my God,‡ for the meal offering and the drink offering are withheld from your God’s house.

kikuyu: Ndũgathikagĩrĩrie ũrĩa wothe andũ maraaria, tondũ wahota kũigua ndungata yaku ĩgĩkũruma:
english: She is not afraid of the snow for her household, for all her household are clothed with scarlet.

kikuyu: Niĩ njũkĩte gũkũ thĩ ndĩ ũtheri, nĩgeetha gũtikagĩe o na ũmwe ũnjĩtĩkĩtie ũngĩikara nduma-inĩ.
english: I have come as a light into the world, that whoever believes in me may not remain in the darkness.

kikuyu: “Kuuma rũciaro-inĩ rwa mũndũ ũyũ, Ngai nĩareheire andũ a Isiraeli Mũhonokia, na nĩwe Jesũ, o ta ũrĩa eeranĩire.
english: From this man’s offspring, God has brought salvation‡ to Israel according to his promise,

kikuyu: Nĩngamũherithia kũringana na ciĩko cianyu, ũguo nĩguo Jehova ekuuga. Nĩngaakia mwaki mĩtitũ-inĩ yanyu, ũrĩa ũkaaniina kĩrĩa gĩothe kĩmũrigiicĩirie.’ ”
english: who says, ‘I will build myself a wide house and spacious rooms,’ and cuts out windows for himself, with a cedar ceiling, and painted with red.

kikuyu: o ĩmwe ĩnyiitanĩte na ĩrĩa ĩngĩ, ũũ atĩ gũtirĩ rĩera rĩngĩtoonyera gatagatĩ ga cio.
english: One is so near to another, that no air can come between them.

kikuyu: Hwaĩ-inĩ wa mũthenya wa ikũmi na ĩna wa mweri ũcio, o maambĩte hema kũu Giligali werũ-inĩ wa Jeriko, andũ a Isiraeli magĩkũngũĩra Gĩathĩ kĩa Bathaka.
english: Joshua commanded the people, saying, “You shall not shout nor let your  “Gilgal” sounds like the Hebrew for “roll.”

kikuyu: Mũrata wake akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Ũyũ ti ũndũ ũngĩ tiga rũhiũ rwa Gideoni mũrũ wa Joashu, ũrĩa Mũisiraeli. Ngai nĩaneanĩte Amidiani na kambĩ yao yothe moko-inĩ make.” Rĩrĩa Gideoni aiguire ũhoro wa kĩroto kĩu na ũtaũri wakĩo, akĩhooya Ngai. Agĩcooka nginya kambĩ-inĩ ya Isiraeli akĩmeeta akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Ũkĩrai! Jehova nĩaneanĩte kambĩ ya Amidiani moko- inĩ manyu.”
english: He caught a young man of the men of Succoth, and inquired of him; and he described for him the princes of Succoth, and its elders, seventy-seven men.

kikuyu: Mũthenya ũmwe Jakubu nĩarugaga gĩtoero kĩa ndengũ ndune, nake Esaũ agĩũka kuuma werũ-inĩ ahũtiĩ mũno.
english: Jacob boiled stew. Esau came in from the field, and he was famished.

kikuyu: Angĩkorwo nĩ gũtungata, nĩatungatage; na angĩkorwo nĩ kũrutana, nĩarutanage;
english: or service, let’s give ourselves to service; or he who teaches, to his teaching;

kikuyu: Anjikĩtie ndoro-inĩ, ngatuĩka ta rũkũngũ na ta mũhu.
english: He has cast me into the mire. I have become like dust and ashes.

kikuyu: We oimbũraga maũndũ marĩa onete na akaigua no gũtirĩ mũndũ o na ũmwe wĩtĩkagĩra ũira wake.
english: What he has seen and heard, of that he testifies; and no one receives his witness.

kikuyu: Moima Kadeshi, magĩthiĩ makĩamba hema ciao Kĩrĩma-inĩ kĩa Horu, mũhaka-inĩ wa Edomu.
english: They traveled from Kadesh, and encamped in Mount Hor, in the edge of the land of Edom.

kikuyu: Ndũkanarute ũira ũigĩrĩre mũndũ wa itũũra rĩaku kĩgeenyo, kana ũheenanie na mĩromo yaku.
english: Don’t be a witness against your neighbor without cause. Don’t deceive with your lips.

kikuyu: Woigire atĩrĩ, ‘Kaĩ ndĩ na haaro-ĩ! Jehova nĩanyongereire kĩeha igũrũ rĩa ruo rũrĩa ndĩ naruo; nĩnogetio nĩ gũcaaya, na ngaaga ũhurũko.’ ”
english: ‘You said, “Woe is me now! For Yahweh has added sorrow to my pain! I am weary with my groaning, and I find no rest.” ’

kikuyu: “Mũndũ ũcio angĩkorwo arĩ na mwanake mũtunyani, mũitithia wa thakame kana wĩkaga ũndũ o na ũmwe wa maũndũ macio mangĩ mooru
english: “ ‘Your mother was like a vine in your blood, planted by the waters. It was fruitful and full of branches by reason of many waters.

kikuyu: Nowe Ishumaeli mũrũ wa Nethania na andũ ake anana nĩmetharire kuuma harĩ Johanani, makĩũrĩra kũrĩ andũ a Amoni. Kũũrĩra Bũrũri wa Misiri
english: But Ishmael the son of Nethaniah escaped from Johanan with eight men, and went to the children of Ammon.

kikuyu: Nduta gwĩkaga wendi waku, nĩgũkorwo Wee nĩwe Ngai wakwa; Roho waku mwega arondongoragia, ngeragĩre kũrĩa kwaraganu.
english: All your works will give thanks to you, Yahweh. : This is an acrostic psalm, with every verse (including the second half of verse 13) starting with a consecutive letter of the Hebrew alphabet.

kikuyu: Nĩmagĩte kwĩhokeka harĩ Jehova; nao magaciara ciana itarĩ na ũngai. Na rĩrĩ, ciathĩ ciao cia Karũgamo ka Mweri nĩikaamameria, o hamwe na mĩgũnda yao.
english: As they were multiplied, so they sinned against me. I will change their glory into shame.

kikuyu: Mũndũ o wothe agekĩrwo mũcamo wa cumbĩ nĩ mwaki, (na igongona o rĩothe rĩĩkĩrwo mũcamo na cumbĩ).
english: For everyone will be salted with fire, and every sacrifice will be seasoned with salt.

kikuyu: Niĩ njathĩtwo ndathimane; nake arathimanĩte-rĩ, ndingĩhota kũgarũra ũndũ ũcio.
english: Behold, I have received a command to bless. He has blessed, and I can’t reverse it.

kikuyu: Marĩ kũu Kĩrĩma-inĩ kĩa Horu, hakuhĩ na mũhaka wa Edomu, Jehova akĩĩra Musa na Harũni atĩrĩ,
english: Yahweh spoke to Moses and Aaron in Mount Hor, by the border of the land of Edom, saying,

kikuyu: Jusufu akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Ũtaũri wakĩo nĩ ũyũ: Honge icio ithatũ nĩ mĩthenya ĩtatũ.
english: Joseph said to him, “This is its interpretation: the three branches are three days.

kikuyu: Aarĩkia kuuga ũguo, akĩoya mũgate, agĩcookeria Ngai ngaatho mbere yao othe. Agĩcooka akĩwenyũranga, akĩambĩrĩria kũrĩa. Nao othe makĩigua momĩrĩria, makĩoya irio makĩrĩa.
english: When he had said this and had taken bread, he gave thanks to God in the presence of all; then he broke it and began to eat.

kikuyu: Mũraika wa Jehova ningĩ akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ: * 15:10 Kĩrĩkanĩro o gĩothe kĩarĩkia kũiguithanĩrio nĩ kwarutagwo igongona rĩa nyamũ ĩrĩa yatinanagio maita meerĩ, nao arĩa maathondekaga kĩrĩkanĩro kĩu makagera gatagatĩ ga icunjĩ icio cierĩ. * 16:2 Kwaga kũgĩa ciana kuonekaga taarĩ iherithia kuuma kũrĩ Ngai. Mũtugo wa mahinda macio nĩwetĩkĩrĩtie mũtumia athuurĩre mũthuuriwe mũtumia, no ciana iria maagĩa igakorwo irĩ cia mũtumia ũcio thata.
english: Don’t speak against one another, brothers. He who speaks against a brother and judges his brother, speaks against the law and judges the law. But if you judge the law, you are not a doer of the law but a judge.

kikuyu: Andũ acio magĩikara magĩcaragia Jesũ, na rĩrĩa maarũngiĩ nja ya hekarũ, makĩũrania atĩrĩ, “Mũgwĩciiria atĩa? Kaĩ hihi ategũũka Gĩathĩ-inĩ?” No athĩnjĩri-Ngai arĩa anene na Afarisai nĩmarutĩte watho atĩ mũndũ o wothe ũngĩamenyire harĩa Jesũ aarĩ, amarehere ũhoro nĩgeetha mamũnyiite.
english: Then they sought for Jesus and spoke with one another as they stood in the temple, “What do you think—that he isn’t coming to the feast at all?”

kikuyu: “Ambata, ũkinye Gileadi, ũgĩĩre ndawa, wee Mũirĩtu Gathirange, Mwarĩ wa bũrũri wa Misiri. No rĩrĩ, ũingĩhagia ndawa icio mũno, o tũhũ; gũtirĩ ingĩkũhonia.
english: Go up into Gilead, and take balm, virgin daughter of Egypt. You use many medicines in vain. There is no healing for you.

kikuyu: “Mũthamaki ũcio ũrĩ gatagatĩ-inĩ kao nĩagakuua mĩrigo yake na ciande kũrĩ mairia oimagare, naruo rũthingo nĩrũgatũrĩkio irima oimĩre ho. Nake nĩakehumbĩra ũthiũ nĩguo ndakone bũrũri.
english: “ ‘The prince who is among them will bear his baggage on his shoulder in the dark, and will go out. They will dig through the wall to carry things out that

kikuyu: Mĩaka yothe ĩrĩa Ishumaeli aatũũrire muoyo yarĩ mĩaka igana rĩmwe na mĩrongo ĩtatũ na mũgwanja. Agĩcooka agĩtuĩkana agĩkua, na agĩthikwo hamwe na andũ ao arĩa maakuĩte.
english: These are the years of the life of Ishmael: one hundred thirty-seven years. He gave up his spirit and died, and was gathered to his people.

kikuyu: Akĩĩhũgũra, akĩmarora othe, agĩcooka akĩĩra mũndũ ũcio atĩrĩ, “Tambũrũkia guoko gwaku.” Nake agĩĩka o ro ũguo; nakuo guoko gwake gũkĩhonio biũ. Nao makĩrakara mũno, makĩambĩrĩria kũũrania ũrĩa mangĩĩka Jesũ. Atũmwo arĩa Ikũmi na Eerĩ
english: He looked around at them all, and said to the man, “Stretch out your hand.” He did, and his hand was restored as sound as the other.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo ngʼondu ikaarĩĩaga ta irĩ ũrĩithio-inĩ wacio; natuo tũgondu tũkaarĩithagio kũndũ kũrĩa kwanangĩku gwa itonga.
english: Then the lambs will graze as in their pasture, and strangers will eat the ruins of the rich.

kikuyu: Nĩcio iheaga nyamũ ciothe cia gĩthaka maaĩ ma kũnyua; nĩkuo njagĩ cia werũ-inĩ inyootoragĩrwo.
english: They give drink to every animal of the field. The wild donkeys quench their thirst.

kikuyu: Merirĩria ma mũndũ kĩgũũta nĩmo makaamũũraga, tondũ moko make matiendaga kũruta wĩra.
english: The desire of the sluggard kills him, for his hands refuse to labor.

kikuyu: “ ‘Mũndũ wa bũrũri wanyu angĩthĩĩna na endie gĩcunjĩ kĩa mũgũnda wake, mũndũ ũrĩa wa hakuhĩ mũno wa nyũmba yake nĩagooka na akũũre mũgũnda ũcio mũndũ wao eendetie. No rĩrĩ, mũndũ angĩkorwo ndarĩ na mũndũ wa kũmũkũũrĩra mũgũnda, na we mwene atonge agĩe na indo ciiganĩte kũũkũũra,
english: “ ‘If your brother becomes poor, and sells some of his possessions, then his kinsman who is next to him shall come, and redeem that which his brother has sold.

kikuyu: Gũtĩĩra mũndũ maũthĩ ti wega: no mũndũ no eke ũũru nĩ ũndũ wa kenyũ ka irio.
english: He who pampers his servant from youth will have him become a son in the end.

kikuyu: Korwo nĩmwamenyete gĩtũmi gĩa ciugo ici, atĩrĩ, ‘Kũiguanĩra tha nĩkuo nyendaga, no ti magongona,’ mũtingĩatuĩrĩire andũ mateehĩtie mahĩtia.
english: But if you had known what this means, ‘I desire mercy, and not sacrifice,’* you wouldn’t have condemned the guiltless.

kikuyu: Korwo ndaarĩ na mehia ngoro-inĩ yakwa-rĩ, Mwathani ndangĩanjiguire;
english: If I cherished sin in my heart, the Lord wouldn’t have listened.

kikuyu: Ũrĩrĩĩaga irio cia thithino ya mĩnoga yaku, o nginya rĩrĩa ũgaacooka tĩĩri-inĩ, tondũ nokuo warutĩtwo; tondũ wee ũrĩ rũkũngũ na ũgaacooka o rũkũngũ-inĩ.”
english: Lamech took two wives: the name of the first one was Adah, and the name of the second one was Zillah.

kikuyu: “Ĩra andũ a Isiraeli atĩrĩ, ‘Mũndũ o wothe ũngĩrehere Jehova iruta rĩa ũiguano, akaarehe gĩcunjĩ kĩarĩo kĩrĩ igongona rĩake mwene kũrĩ Jehova.
english: “Speak to the children of Israel, saying, ‘He who offers the sacrifice of his peace offerings to Yahweh shall bring his offering to Yahweh out of the sacrifice of his peace offerings.

kikuyu: O na gũtuĩka ndũũraga nyiitĩrĩire muoyo wakwa moko-inĩ makwa-rĩ, ndingĩriganĩrwo nĩ watho waku.
english: My soul is continually in my hand, yet I won’t forget your law.

kikuyu: Tungatagai na ngoro yothe mũtarĩ na kĩnyiria, taarĩ Mwathani mũratungatĩra, no ti andũ,
english: with good will doing service as to the Lord and not to men,

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo manyariiraga arĩa wee ũgurarĩtie, na makaaria ũhoro wa ruo rwa arĩa ũtiihĩtie.
english: For they persecute him whom you have wounded. They tell of the sorrow of those whom you have hurt.

kikuyu: Nĩmagire wĩhokeku na makĩaga wĩtĩkio o ta maithe mao, makĩaga kwĩhokeka o ta ũta mũgathũku ũrĩa ũtangĩĩhokwo.
english: but turned back, and dealt treacherously like their fathers. They were twisted like a deceitful bow.

kikuyu: Njĩra cia itũũra rĩu inene ikaiyũra tũhĩĩ na tũirĩtu tũgĩthaaka.”
english: The streets of the city will be full of boys and girls playing in its streets.”

kikuyu: nĩwe waringire marigithathi ma Misiri, Wendo wake nĩ wa tene na tene.
english: to him who struck down the Egyptian firstborn, for his loving kindness endures forever;

kikuyu: Niĩ ndirĩ ndacuumĩkĩra betha kana thahabu, o na kana nguo ya mũndũ o wothe.
english: They brought Alexander out of the multitude, the Jews putting him forward. Alexander beckoned with his hand, and would have made a defense to the people.

kikuyu: Haha nĩhabataire ũkirĩrĩria harĩ andũ arĩa aamũre, o arĩa maathĩkagĩra Maathani ma Ngai, na magatũũra marĩ ehokeku harĩ Jesũ.
english: Here is the perseverance of the saints, those who keep the commandments of God and the faith of Jesus.”

kikuyu: Ririkanai magegania marĩa ekĩte, na ciama ciake, na matuĩro ma ciira marĩa atuĩte,
english: Remember his marvelous works that he has done: his wonders, and the judgments of his mouth,

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio makĩeheria ihiga. Jesũ agĩcooka agĩtiira maitho, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Baba, nĩndagũcook- eria ngaatho nĩ ũndũ nĩũnjiguaga.
english: So they took away the stone from the place where the dead man was lying.§ Jesus lifted up his eyes and said, “Father, I thank you that you listened to me.

kikuyu: O na gũtuĩka kĩambĩrĩria gĩaku no kĩoneke kĩrĩ kĩnini, thuutha-inĩ nĩũkagaacĩra mũno.
english: Though your beginning was small, yet your latter end would greatly increase.

kikuyu: Gideoni mũrũ wa Joashu aakuire arĩ mũkũrũ mũno, na agĩthikwo mbĩrĩra-inĩ ya ithe Joashu kũu Ofira ya Aabiezeri.
english: Gideon the son of Joash died in a good old age, and was buried in the tomb of Joash his father, in Ophrah of the Abiezrites.

kikuyu: O ta ũrĩa thwariga ĩnyootagĩra tũrũũĩ twa maaĩ, no taguo ngoro yakwa ĩkũnyootagĩra, Wee Ngai.
english: We have heard with our ears, God; our fathers have told us what work you did in their days, in the days of old.

kikuyu: Athamaki a Tarishishi† na a mabũrũri marĩa marĩ hũgũrũrũ-inĩ cia kũraya nĩmakamũrehagĩra igooti; nao athamaki a Sheba na Seba nĩmakamũrehagĩra iheo.
english: The kings of Tarshish and of the islands will bring tribute. The kings of Sheba and Seba shall offer gifts.

kikuyu: Akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Ĩno nĩyo thakame yakwa ya kĩrĩkanĩro, ĩrĩa ĩitĩtwo nĩ ũndũ wa andũ aingĩ.
english: Crucifying him, they parted his garments among them, casting lots on them, what each should take.

kikuyu: Mũtikaheane ũhoro ũcio kũu Gathu, mũtikarĩre o na hanini. Ningĩ kũu Bethi-Ofara mwĩgaragariei rũkũngũ‑inĩ.
english: Don’t tell it in Gath. Don’t weep at all. At Beth Ophrah† I have rolled myself in the dust.

kikuyu: Othe makuuaga ngoro; mookaga makĩinainaga moimĩte ciĩhitho ciao cia hinya.
english: The foreigners shall fade away, and shall come trembling out of their strongholds.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo ũũ nĩguo Jehova, Ngai wa Isiraeli, ekuuga ũhoro ũkoniĩ nyũmba iria irĩ itũũra rĩĩrĩ inene, o na nyũmba cia ũthamaki wa Juda o icio ciatharirio nĩguo indo iria ciakĩte kuo ihũthĩrwo kwĩgitĩra kuuma kũrĩ ihumbu cia tĩĩri iria cikũrigiicĩirie o na kuuma kũrĩ rũhiũ rwa njora,
english: For Yahweh, the God of Israel, says concerning the houses of this city and concerning the houses of the kings of Judah, which are broken down to make a defense against the mounds and against the sword:

kikuyu: “Bũrũri wa Juda nĩũracakaya, namo matũũra makuo makoorwo nĩ hinya; nĩmararĩrĩra bũrũri, nakĩo kĩrĩro nĩkĩambatĩte na igũrũ kiumĩte Jerusalemu.
english: “Judah mourns, and its gates languish. They sit in black on the ground. The cry of Jerusalem goes up.

kikuyu: Nĩngamatua rũrĩrĩ rũmwe marĩ kũu bũrũri ũcio, o kũu irĩma-igũrũ cia Isiraeli. Gũkaagĩa mũthamaki ũmwe igũrũ rĩao othe, na matigacooka rĩngĩ gũtuĩka ndũrĩrĩ igĩrĩ, kana magayũkanio matuĩke mothamaki meerĩ.
english: I will make them one nation in the land, on the mountains of Israel. One king will be king to them all. They will no longer be two nations. They won’t be divided into two kingdoms any more at all.

kikuyu: Mũcaara wa andũ arĩa athingu ũmarehagĩra muoyo, no indo iria cionagwo nĩ andũ arĩa aaganu imarehagĩra iherithia.
english: The labor of the righteous leads to life. The increase of the wicked leads to sin.

kikuyu: Mũthenya wa ikũmi na ũmwe, Pagieli mũrũ wa Okirani, mũtongoria wa andũ a Asheri, nake akĩrehe maruta make.
english: On the eleventh day Pagiel the son of Ochran, prince of the children of Asher,

kikuyu: Mũtikanarute wĩra ũrĩa mũrutaga, no mũrutagĩre Jehova iruta rĩa gũcinwo na mwaki.’ ” Mũthenya wa Kũhoroherio Mehia Ningĩ Jehova akĩĩra Musa atĩrĩ,
english: You must not offer any of these as the bread of your God from the hand of a foreigner, because their corruption is in them. There is a defect in them. They shall not be accepted for you.’ ”

kikuyu: Airagĩria ta kĩroto, akaaga kuoneka rĩngĩ, akaingatwo ta kĩoneki kĩa ũtukũ.
english: He will fly away as a dream, and will not be found. Yes, he will be chased away like a vision of the night.

kikuyu: Nacio thigari igĩcookeria aciirithania ũhoro ũcio, na rĩrĩa maaiguire atĩ Paũlũ na Sila maarĩ raiyaa Roma, makĩmaka. Nao magĩũka kũmahooreria, na makĩmoimagaria kuuma njeera, makĩmathaitha moime itũũra rĩu mathiĩ.
english: The sergeants reported these words to the magistrates, and they were afraid when they heard that they were Romans,

kikuyu: Wee Jehova-rĩ, njũrĩire harĩwe; ndũkanakĩreke njonorithio.
english: Hurry, God, to deliver me. Come quickly to help me, Yahweh.

kikuyu: “Mũheei njoohi anyue arĩĩo, tondũ nĩangʼathĩirie Jehova. Moabi nĩarekwo egaragarie matahĩko-inĩ make;
english: “Make him drunk, for he magnified himself against Yahweh. Moab will wallow in his vomit, and he also will be in derision.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo mũtiaheirwo roho wa kũmũtua ngombo rĩngĩ nĩguo mwĩtigagĩre, no mwaheirwo Roho wa kũmũtua ciana ciake cia kwĩrererwo. Na nĩguo ũtũmaga twanĩrĩre tũkoiga atĩrĩ, “Abba, Baba.”
english: For I don’t understand what I am doing. For I don’t practice what I desire to do; but what I hate, that I do.

kikuyu: “Wee Jehova Ngai Mwene-Hinya-Wothe, Ngai wa Isiraeli, o wee ũikarĩire gĩtĩ kĩa ũnene gatagatĩ ka makerubi, Wee wiki nowe Ngai igũrũ rĩa mothamaki mothe ma thĩ. Nĩwe wombire igũrũ na thĩ.
english: Don’t listen to Hezekiah, for the king of Assyria says, ‘Make your peace with me, and come out to me; and each of you eat from his vine, and each one from his fig tree, and each one of you drink the waters of his own cistern;

kikuyu: Namo magĩthiĩ maathiiaga na mwena o wothe wa ĩyo ĩna kũrĩa ciũmbe icio cierekeirie ũthiũ; magũrũ macio ma ngaari matiagarũrũkaga rĩrĩa ciũmbe icio igũthiĩ.
english: When they went, they went in their four directions. They didn’t turn when they went.

kikuyu: o nginya ngoro yake ĩgathũũra irio, na roho wake ũgathũũra irio iria njega mũno.
english: so that his life abhors bread, and his soul dainty food.

kikuyu: Wee Mũfarisai ũyũ mũtumumu! Amba ũthambie gĩkombe na mbakũri mwena wa thĩinĩ, naguo mwena wa nja o naguo nĩũgũthera.
english: “If therefore they tell you, ‘Behold, he is in the wilderness,’ don’t go out; or ‘Behold, he is in the inner rooms,’ don’t believe it.

kikuyu: Kuuma kũrĩ ariũ a Jusufu: Kuuma njiaro-inĩ cia Efiraimu: Arũme othe arĩa maakinyĩtie mĩaka mĩrongo ĩĩrĩ na makĩria arĩa mangĩahotire gũthiĩ ita-inĩ, makĩandĩkwo o mũndũ o mũndũ na rĩĩtwa rĩake, kũringana na maandĩko ma mbarĩ ciao na nyũmba ciao.
english: Of the children of Joseph: of the children of Ephraim, their generations, by their families, by their fathers’ houses, according to the number of the names, from twenty years old and upward, all who were able to go out to war:

kikuyu: kuuma mũhĩrĩga wa Nafitali, ikĩheo Kedeshi kũu Galili (itũũra inene rĩa kũũrĩrwo nĩ mũndũ ũrĩa ũraganĩte), na Hamothu- Dori na Karitani, hamwe na ithaka ciamo cia ũrĩithio; mothe maarĩ matũũra matatũ.
english: Out of the tribe of Naphtali, Kedesh in Galilee with its pasture lands, the city of refuge for the man slayer, Hammothdor with its pasture lands, and Kartan with its pasture lands: three cities.

kikuyu: Nayo nda ĩrĩa yamaciarire ĩkariganĩrwo nĩo, igunyũ nacio ikamarĩa; andũ arĩa aaganu maticookaga kũririkanwo,
english: The womb will forget him. The worm will feed sweetly on him. He will be no more remembered. Unrighteousness will be broken as a tree.

kikuyu: o nginya rĩrĩa ndatoonyire handũ-harĩa-haamũre ha Mũrungu; hĩndĩ ĩyo ngĩkĩmenya ũrĩa makaarĩkĩrĩria.
english: until I entered God’s sanctuary, and considered their latter end.

kikuyu: O rĩngĩ Jesũ akĩmooria atĩrĩ, “Nũũ mũracaria?” Nao makĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Nĩ Jesũ wa Nazarethi.”
english: Again therefore he asked them, “Who are you looking for?” They said, “Jesus of Nazareth.”

kikuyu: Mũndũ o wothe angĩĩtua mũndũ wa ndini no aremwo nĩ gwatha rũrĩmĩ rwake-rĩ, mũndũ ũcio nĩ kwĩheenia eheenagia, na ũndini wake nĩ wa tũhũ.
english: If anyone among you thinks himself to be religious while he doesn’t bridle his tongue, but deceives his heart, this man’s religion is worthless.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ciothe rĩrĩa tũkũmũhoera, nĩtũcookagĩria Ngai ngaatho, o we Ithe wa Mwathani witũ Jesũ Kristũ,
english: 1 If then you were raised together with Christ, seek the things that are above, where Christ is, seated on the right hand of God.

kikuyu: Tũnyũmba twakĩo twa arangĩri, na thingo ciakĩo, na gĩthaku gĩakĩo nĩciaiganainie ithimo na icio ingĩ. Itoonyero rĩu na gĩthaku kĩarĩo ciarĩ na mĩanya mĩena yothe. Narĩo itoonyero rĩarĩ rĩa ũraihu wa mĩkono mĩrongo ĩtano, na wariĩ wa mĩkono mĩrongo ĩĩrĩ na ĩtano.
english: with its lodges, its posts, and its arches, according to these measurements. There were windows in it and in its arches all around. It was fifty cubits long, and twenty-five cubits wide.

kikuyu: na akaara mĩgamba ya tũnyũmba twake twa ngoroba igũrũ rĩa maaĩ. We atũmaga matu matuĩke ngaari yake ya ita, na agakuuo nĩ mathagu ma rũhuho.
english: Glory in his holy name. Let the heart of those who seek Yahweh rejoice.

kikuyu: “Na rĩrĩ, hĩndĩ ĩyo mũriũ ũrĩa mũkũrũ aarĩ mũgũnda. Na rĩrĩa aakuhĩrĩirie mũciĩ akĩinũka-rĩ, akĩigua gũkĩinwo nyĩmbo na ndũhiũ. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio mũriũ ũcio agĩĩta ndungata ĩmwe ya mũciĩ, akĩmĩũria gĩtũmi kĩa maũndũ macio.
english: “Now his elder son was in the field. As he came near to the house, he heard music and dancing.

kikuyu: Nacio njiaro cia Zebuluni kũringana na mbarĩ ciao ciarĩ ici: kuuma kũrĩ Seredi, nĩ mbarĩ ya Aseredi; kuuma kũrĩ Eloni nĩ mbarĩ ya Aeloni, na kuuma kũrĩ Jahaleeli, nĩ mbarĩ ya Ajahaleeli.
english: The sons of Zebulun after their families: of Sered, the family of the Seredites; of Elon, the family of the Elonites; of Jahleel, the family of the Jahleelites.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩmakoerera icakaya rĩgũkoniĩ, makwĩre atĩrĩ: “ ‘Kaĩ ũrĩ mwanange-ĩ, o wee itũũra rĩrĩ igweta, o wee watũũragwo nĩ andũ arĩa maathiiaga iria-inĩ-ĩ! Wee nĩwe warĩ na ũhoti kũu iria-inĩ, wee na atũũri aku; nĩwatũmaga arĩa othe maatũũraga kuo manyiitwo nĩ guoya.
english: They will take up a lamentation over you, and tell you, “How you are destroyed, who were inhabited by seafaring men, the renowned city, who was strong in the sea, she and her inhabitants, who caused their terror to be on all who lived there!”

kikuyu: Athuuri nĩmatigĩte gũikara kĩhingo-inĩ gĩa itũũra inene; aanake nao nĩmatigĩte kũina nyĩmbo ciao.
english: The elders have ceased from the gate, and the young men from their music.

kikuyu: ZEKARIA Isiraeli Gwĩtwo Macookerere Jehova Na rĩrĩ, mweri wa ĩnana wa mwaka wa keerĩ wa ũthamaki wa Dario, ndũmĩrĩri ya Jehova nĩyakinyĩire mũnabii Zekaria, mũrũ wa Berekia, mũrũ wa Ido, akĩĩrwo atĩrĩ:
english: In the eighth month, in the second year of Darius, Yahweh’s† word came to the prophet Zechariah the son of Berechiah, the son of Iddo, saying,

kikuyu: makoiga atĩrĩ, ‘Mũndũ ũyũ aambĩrĩirie gwaka nyũmba akĩremwo nĩ kũmĩrĩkia.’
english: Behold, there are some who are last who will be first, and there are some who are first who will be last.”

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo Jeremia akĩarĩria andũ othe, arũme na andũ-a-nja, acio maamũcookagĩria ũhoro, akĩmeera atĩrĩ,
english: Then Jeremiah said to all the people—to the men and to the women, even to all the people who had given him an answer, saying,

kikuyu: Ndũrĩrĩ irogũtungatagĩra nayo mĩhĩrĩga ya andũ ĩkũinamagĩrĩre. Tuĩka mwathi wa ariũ a thoguo, nao ariũ a nyũkwa marokũinamagĩrĩra. Arĩa mangĩkũruma na kĩrumi marocookererwo nĩkĩo, nao arĩa magaakũrathima marorathimagwo.” Na rĩrĩ, Isaaka aarĩkia kũmũrathima na Jakubu aarĩkia kuuma harĩ ithe o ro ũguo-rĩ, mũrũ wa nyina Esaũ agĩũka kuuma kũguĩma.
english: Let peoples serve you, and nations bow down to you. Be lord over your brothers. Let your mother’s sons bow down to you. Cursed be everyone who curses you. Blessed be everyone who blesses you.”

kikuyu: Mũndũ o wothe mwĩbaarĩrĩri ekaga maũndũ make na ũmenyi, no mũndũ mũkĩĩgu aguũragia ũrimũ wake.
english: A wise man fears and shuns evil, but the fool is hot headed and reckless.

kikuyu: “Warĩkia gũtheria Alawii acio na kũmaruta matuĩke ta igongona rĩa gũthũngũthio-rĩ, nĩmarutage wĩra wao Hema-inĩ-ya-Gũtũnganwo.
english: “After that, the Levites shall go in to do the service of the Tent of Meeting. You shall cleanse them, and offer them as a wave offering.

kikuyu: Nĩagatema ihinga cia mũtitũ na ithanwa; Lebanoni nĩĩkagũithio nĩ Ũrĩa-ũrĩ-Hinya.
english: He will cut down the thickets of the forest with iron, and Lebanon will fall by the Mighty One.

kikuyu: “Ingĩkorwo ndanaima athĩĩni kĩrĩa maanerirĩria, kana ngareka maitho ma mũtũmia wa ndigwa morwo nĩ hinya-rĩ,
english: “If I have withheld the poor from their desire, or have caused the eyes of the widow to fail,

kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo nĩmatambuurĩte Jakubu * 78:60 Matukũ-inĩ ma tene mũno, Shilo nĩrĩo rĩarĩ itũũra itheru, na ithandũkũ rĩa kĩrĩkanĩro rĩaigĩtwo kuo.
english: Turn us again, God of Armies. Cause your face to shine, and we will be saved.

kikuyu: Mbarathi nĩĩharagĩrio nĩ ũndũ wa mũthenya wa mbaara, no ũhootani uumaga kũrĩ Jehova.
english: The horse is prepared for the day of battle; but victory is with Yahweh.

kikuyu: Ndeciiragia atĩrĩ, ‘Ũkũrũ nĩwaagĩrĩire warie; ũingĩ wa mĩaka nĩwaagĩrĩire kũrutana ũũgĩ.’ No nĩ roho ũrĩa ũrĩ thĩinĩ wa mũndũ, o yo mĩhũmũ ya Mwene-Hinya-Wothe, ũheaga mũndũ ũmenyo.
english: Behold, my terror will not make you afraid, neither will my pressure be heavy on you.

kikuyu: Itua rĩake rĩarĩ atĩ, na ũndũ wa kanitha, ũũgĩ wa Ngai ũrĩa wa mĩthemba mĩingĩ ũmenyithanio kũrĩ aathani, na kũrĩ arĩa marĩ na ũhoti kũu igũrũ,
english: to the intent that now through the assembly the manifold wisdom of God might be made known to the principalities and the powers in the heavenly places,

kikuyu: Nĩnjũũĩ nĩũkanginyia o gĩkuũ-inĩ, ũndware kũrĩa gwathĩrĩirwo arĩa othe marĩ muoyo.
english: For I know that you will bring me to death, to the house appointed for all living.

kikuyu: Anga mũrĩ na maitho na mũtionaga, na mũrĩ na matũ na mũtiiguaga? Kaĩ mũtararirikana? Rĩrĩa ndenyũrangĩire andũ arĩa ngiri ithano mĩgate ĩrĩa ĩtano-rĩ, nĩ ciondo ciigana muonganirie ciiyũrĩte cienyũ cia mĩgate?” Nao makĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Ciarĩ ciondo ikũmi na igĩrĩ.”
english: He said to them, “Are you also without understanding? Don’t you perceive that whatever goes into the man from outside can’t defile him,

kikuyu: Jesũ akĩĩra kĩrĩndĩ kĩu atĩrĩ, “Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa muona itu rĩkĩambata riumĩte ithũĩro, o rĩmwe muugaga atĩrĩ, ‘Nĩgũkuura mbura,’ na gũkoira.
english: He said to the multitudes also, “When you see a cloud rising from the west, immediately you say, ‘A shower is coming,’ and so it happens.

kikuyu: Thĩinĩ wa rĩĩtwa rĩayo ndũrĩrĩ nĩikaagĩa na kĩĩrĩgĩrĩro.” Jesũ na Beelizebuli
english: yet he has no root in himself, but endures for a while. When oppression or persecution arises because of the word, immediately he stumbles.

kikuyu: Musa akĩruta Harũni nguo ciake, agĩcihumba mũriũ Eleazaru. Nake Harũni agĩkuĩra kũu kĩrĩma igũrũ. Nake Musa na Eleazaru magĩikũrũka makiuma kũu kĩrĩma igũrũ,
english: Moses stripped Aaron of his garments, and put them on Eleazar his son. Aaron died there on the top of the mountain, and Moses and Eleazar came down from the mountain.

kikuyu: agaathira nginya tene o ta mai make mwene; arĩa manamuona mooragie atĩrĩ, ‘Kaĩ aathiire kũ?’
english: yet he will perish forever like his own dung. Those who have seen him will say, ‘Where is he?’

kikuyu: no rĩrĩ, nĩndakarĩtio mũno nĩ ndũrĩrĩ iria ciĩiguaga irĩ ngitĩre. Ndaarakarĩire andũ akwa o hanini, no ndũrĩrĩ icio igĩkĩrĩrĩria kũmanyamaria.’
english: I am very angry with the nations that are at ease; for I was but a little displeased, but they added to the calamity.”

kikuyu: Mũndũ mũtaana nĩakagaacĩra; ũrĩa ũnyootoraga arĩa angĩ o nake mwene nĩakanyootorwo.
english: The liberal soul shall be made fat. He who waters shall be watered also himself.

kikuyu: hĩndĩ ĩyo congʼe ũrokũra kuo handũ ha ngano, na riya handũ ha cairi.” Ndeto cia Ayubu nĩciathira.
english: let briers grow instead of wheat, and stinkweed instead of barley.” The words of Job are ended.

kikuyu: “Atĩrĩrĩ, wee mũrũ wakwa, ngũkwĩra atĩa, o wee mwana wa nda yakwa, ngũkwĩra atĩa o wee mwana wa mĩĩhĩtwa yakwa,
english: “Oh, my son! Oh, son of my womb! Oh, son of my vows!

kikuyu: o mwĩhĩtwa ũrĩa eehĩtĩire ithe witũ Iburahĩmu:
english: the oath which he swore to Abraham our father,

kikuyu: kuuma thakame ya Habili nginya thakame ya Zekaria ũrĩa woragĩirwo gatagatĩ-inĩ ga kĩgongona na handũ harĩa hatheru. Ĩĩ nĩguo, ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ, rũciaro rũrũ nĩrũkaherithĩrio thakame ĩyo. * 11:44 Ayahudi nĩmahakaga mbĩrĩra ciao coka nĩgeetha gũtikagĩe na mũndũ ũngĩcihutia tondũ ũguo nĩkũngĩatũmire mũndũ anyiitwo nĩ thaahu.
english: from the blood of Abel to the blood of Zachariah, who perished between the altar and the sanctuary.’ Yes, I tell you, it will be required of this generation.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩngameera wega atĩrĩ, ‘Niĩ ndirĩ hĩndĩ ndaamũũĩ o na rĩ. Eherai harĩ niĩ, inyuĩ mwĩkaga ũũru!’
english: But if your eye is evil, your whole body will be full of darkness. If therefore the light that is in you is darkness, how great is the darkness!

kikuyu: Agĩcooka akĩhumbĩra moko make na ngingo kũrĩa gũtaarĩ guoya na njũũa cia tũũri tũu.
english: Abimelech said to Isaac, “Go away from us, for you are much mightier than we.”

kikuyu: na mũtongoria wa gĩkundi gĩa thigari mĩrongo ĩtano, na mũheani kĩrĩra, na mũbundi mũũgĩ, o na mũndũ-mũgo mũũgĩ.
english: the captain of fifty, the honorable man, the counselor, the skilled craftsman, and the clever enchanter.

kikuyu: Jesũ agĩcooka akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Nĩ ũndũ wa ũguo wacookia-rĩ, wĩthiĩre; ndaimono nĩyoima thĩinĩ wa mwarĩguo.”
english: He said to her, “For this saying, go your way. The demon has gone out of your daughter.”

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩngaruta andũ arĩa aremi nĩguo mamenye njĩra ciaku, nao ehia magũcookerere.
english: Then I will teach transgressors your ways. Sinners will be converted to you.

kikuyu: Maũndũ marĩa ndĩrona nĩmaranjiguithia kĩeha ngoro, tondũ wa andũ-a-nja othe a itũũra rĩakwa inene.
english: My eye affects my soul, because of all the daughters of my city.

kikuyu: Rũciinĩ, makĩhĩtũka makĩona mũkũyũ ũrĩa Jesũ aarumĩĩte ũũmĩte wothe nginya mĩri.
english: As they passed by in the morning, they saw the fig tree withered away from the roots.

kikuyu: Iburahĩmu agĩtuĩkana na agĩkua arĩ mũthuuri mũniaru, arĩ mũkũrũ na akaingĩhia mĩaka, agĩthikwo hamwe na andũ ao arĩa maakuĩte. Nao ariũ ake, Isaaka na Ishumaeli, makĩmũthika ngurunga-inĩ ya Makipela hakuhĩ na Mamure, gĩthaka-inĩ kĩa Efironi mũrũ wa Zoharu ũrĩa Mũhiti,
english: When he had been there a long time, Abimelech king of the Philistines looked out at a window, and saw, and, behold, Isaac was caressing Rebekah, his wife.  “Edom” means “red”.

kikuyu: Ndigũtĩĩra mũndũ o na ũ maũthĩ, kana ngaathĩrĩrie mũndũ o na ũrĩkũ tũhũ;
english: Please don’t let me respect any man’s person, neither will I give flattering titles to any man.

kikuyu: Tondũ ũcio, Jehova agĩkĩrakario mũno nĩ andũ a Isiraeli, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Tondũ rũrĩrĩ rũrũ nĩrũthũkĩtie kĩrĩkanĩro kĩrĩa ndaarĩkanĩire na maithe mao ma tene, na nĩ rwagĩte kũnjigua-rĩ,
english: Yahweh’s anger burned against Israel; and he said, “Because this nation transgressed my covenant which I commanded their fathers, and has not listened to my voice,

kikuyu: Mũthamaki ũcio nĩarakarire mũno, agĩtũma mbũtũ yake ĩkaniine oragani acio, na ĩgĩcina itũũra rĩao inene.
english: When the king heard that, he was angry, and sent his armies, destroyed those murderers, and burned their city.

kikuyu: Mũnene ũcio wa thigari cia mũthamaki nĩakuuire mbakũri iria nene, na Ngĩo, na mbakũri cia kũminjaminjĩria maaĩ, na nyũngũ, na mĩtĩ ya matawa, na thaani, o na mbakũri iria ciahũthagĩrwo na kũruta maruta ma kũnyuuo, iria ciothe ciathondeketwo na thahabu ĩrĩa therie kana betha.
english: The captain of the guard took away the cups, the fire pans, the basins, the pots, the lamp stands, the spoons, and the bowls; that which was of gold, as gold, and that which was of silver, as silver.

kikuyu: Huhai coro hĩndĩ ya Karũgamo ka Mweri, na ningĩ rĩrĩa mweri waiganana, mũthenya ũrĩa wa Gĩathĩ giitũ;
english: “Defend the weak, the poor, and the fatherless. Maintain the rights of the poor and oppressed.

kikuyu: (Thahabu ya bũrũri ũcio nĩ njega; ningĩ kũu no kũrĩ maguta manungi wega metagwo bendora, na tũhiga twa goro twĩtagwo onigithi.)
english: and the gold of that land is good. Bdellium‡ and onyx stone are also there.

kikuyu: Jehova anjĩkĩire maũndũ kũringana na ũthingu wakwa; andĩhĩte kũringana na ũtheru wa moko makwa.
english: Yahweh has rewarded me according to my righteousness. According to the cleanness of my hands, he has recompensed me.

kikuyu: nuthu ya Gileadi, na Ashitarothu na Edirei (matũũra ma mũthamaki ma Ogu marĩa maarĩ kũu Bashani). Kũu nĩkuo kwarĩ kwa njiaro cia Makiru mũrũ wa Manase, ũguo nĩ kuuga nuthu ya ariũ a Makiru, kũringana na ũrĩa mbarĩ ciao ciatariĩ.
english: Half Gilead, Ashtaroth, and Edrei, the cities of the kingdom of Og in Bashan, were for the children of Machir the son of Manasseh, even for the half of the children of Machir according to their families.

kikuyu: na icũhĩ cia mũhũũri cia moko, na icũhĩ cia maniũrũ,
english: the signet rings, the nose rings,

kikuyu: Ũkaiyũrwo nĩ thoni, handũ ha kũgĩa na riiri. Rĩĩrĩ nĩrĩo ihinda rĩaku! Nyua, ũrĩĩo, nayo njaga yaku yoneke! Gĩkombe kuuma guoko-inĩ kwa ũrĩo kwa Jehova nĩkĩragũkora, na njono nĩikũhumbĩra riiri waku.
english: You are filled with shame, and not glory. You will also drink and be exposed! The cup of Yahweh’s right hand will come around to you, and disgrace will cover your glory.

kikuyu: Ĩ irĩma cia Bashani ti irĩma irĩ riiri; irĩma cia Bashani irĩ tũcũmbĩrĩ tũingĩ.
english: The mountains of Bashan are majestic mountains. The mountains of Bashan are rugged.

kikuyu: ũgwati-inĩ nĩgeetha amũhonokie moko-inĩ ma Amidiani, 18(no ũmũthĩ nĩmũũkĩrĩire nyũmba ya baba, mũkooragĩra ariũ ake mĩrongo mũgwanja ihiga-inĩ rĩmwe, na mũgatua Abimeleku, mũrũ wa ngombo yake ya mũirĩtu, mũthamaki wa atũũri a Shekemu tondũ nĩ mũrũ wa ithe wanyu),
english: The people, the princes of Gilead, said to one another, “Who is the man who will begin to fight against the children of Ammon? He shall be head over all the inhabitants of Gilead.”

kikuyu: Danieli agĩcooka akĩarĩria mũrangĩri ũrĩa waheetwo wĩra nĩ mũrori wa anene arĩa angĩ wa kũroraga Danieli, na Hanania, na Mishaeli, na Azaria, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ,
english: But its swamps and marshes will not be healed. They will be given up to salt.

kikuyu: Ndũgatũherithie nĩ ũndũ wa mehia ma maithe maitũ; tha ciaku irohiũha itũkinyĩrĩre, nĩgũkorwo tũrĩ ahinyĩrĩrĩku mũno.
english: But learning that the men of Jamnia intended to do the same thing to the Jews who lived among them,

kikuyu: athamaki a thĩ, na ndũrĩrĩ ciothe, na inyuĩ anene, na aathani othe a thĩ,
english: kings of the earth and all peoples, princes and all judges of the earth,

kikuyu: nake Palali mũrũ wa Uzai agĩcookereria rũthingo kũngʼethera harĩa thingo ciacemanĩirie na mũthiringo ũrĩa wacomokete nyũmba-inĩ ya igũrũ ya mũthamaki hakuhĩ na nja ya arangĩri. Kũrigania nake, Pedaia mũrũ wa Paroshu
english: Palal the son of Uzai made repairs opposite the turning of the wall, and the tower that stands out from the upper house of the king, which is by the court of the guard. After him Pedaiah the son of Parosh made repairs.

kikuyu: Indo cia itonga nĩ gĩtũũro kĩao kĩirigĩre na hinya, no ũkĩa nĩguo mwanangĩko wa andũ arĩa athĩĩni.
english: The rich man’s wealth is his strong city. The destruction of the poor is their poverty.

kikuyu: Andũ a Nineve nĩmakarũgama hĩndĩ ya ciira hamwe na rũciaro rũrũ marũtuĩre ciira; nĩgũkorwo nĩmeririre maahunjĩirio nĩ Jona, na rĩu ũmwe mũnene gũkĩra Jona arĩ haha. Tawa wa Mwĩrĩ
english: The men of Nineveh will stand up in the judgment with this generation, and will condemn it, for they repented at the preaching of Jonah; and behold, one greater than Jonah is here.

kikuyu: Tũnyũmba tũu twathiũrũrũkĩirie hekarũ twakĩtwo tũkĩararamaga o ũrĩa ngoroba yathiiaga na igũrũ. Mwako wa gũthiũrũrũkĩria hekarũ ĩyo waakĩtwo wambatĩtio na matĩĩna, nĩ ũndũ ũcio, tũnyũmba tũu tũkaaraarama o ũrĩa mũndũ aambataga. Nayo ngathĩ yambatĩte kuuma ngoroba ĩrĩa yarĩ thĩ mũno nginya ĩrĩa ĩrĩ igũrũ biũ ĩgereire ngoroba ya gatagatĩ.
english: The side rooms were wider on the higher levels, because the walls were narrower at the higher levels. Therefore the width of the house increased upward; and so one went up from the lowest level to the highest through the middle level.

kikuyu: Nĩwe ũtũmaga anene matuĩke ta kĩndũ gũtarĩ, na akanyiihia aathani a thĩ akamatua ta marĩ o tũhũ mũtheri.
english: When he was cursed, he didn’t curse back. When he suffered, he didn’t threaten, but committed himself to him who judges righteously.

kikuyu: “O na ningĩ wĩre andũ acio atĩrĩ, ‘Ũũ nĩguo Jehova ekuuga: Atĩrĩrĩ, nĩngũiga njĩra ya muoyo, o na njĩra ya gĩkuũ mbere yanyu.
english: “Many nations will pass by this city, and they will each ask his neighbor, ‘Why has Yahweh done this to this great city?’

kikuyu: “Nĩnjarahũrĩte mũndũ wa kuuma gathigathini, na nĩarooka: mũndũ uumĩte irathĩro rĩa riũa ũrĩa ũkayagĩra rĩĩtwa rĩakwa. Arangagĩrĩria aathani a thĩ o ta marĩ ndaka, akamarangĩrĩria o ta ũrĩa mũmbi akimaga rĩũmba.
english: “I have raised up one from the north, and he has come, from the rising of the sun, one who calls on my name, and he shall come on rulers as on mortar, and as the potter treads clay.

kikuyu: Inyuĩ nĩmũrĩkĩtie kuohorwo mũkoima mehia-inĩ, mũgacooka gũtuĩka ngombo cia ũthingu.
english: So then as through one trespass, all men were condemned; even so through one act of righteousness, all men were justified to life.

kikuyu: nake Eliudi aarĩ ithe wa Eleazaru, nake Eleazaru aarĩ ithe wa Maathani, nake Maathani aarĩ ithe wa Jakubu,
english: Eliud became the father of Eleazar. Eleazar became the father of Matthan. Matthan became the father of Jacob.

kikuyu: Maitho ma Jehova macũthagĩrĩria andũ arĩa athingu, na matũ make magathikĩrĩria kĩrĩro kĩao;
english: Yahweh’s eyes are toward the righteous. His ears listen to their cry.

kikuyu: Ingĩ nacio ikĩgũa tĩĩri-inĩ ũrĩa mũnoru, ikĩgĩa na maciaro imwe ĩgĩciara maita igana, iria ingĩ igĩciara maita mĩrongo ĩtandatũ, na iria ingĩ igĩciara maita mĩrongo ĩtatũ ma iria ciahaandirwo.
english: Others fell on good soil and yielded fruit: some one hundred times as much, some sixty, and some thirty.

kikuyu: gũtirĩ o na ũmwe tiga Kalebu mũrũ wa Jefune, ũrĩa Mũkenizi,* na Joshua mũrũ wa Nuni, nĩ ũndũ nĩmarũmĩrĩire Jehova na ngoro ciao kũna.’
english: They brought the captives with the prey and the plunder, to Moses, and to Eleazar the priest, and to the congregation of the children of Israel, to the camp at the plains of Moab, which are by the Jordan at Jericho.

kikuyu: Mũrutwo ti mũnene kũrĩ mũmũruti, no ũrĩa wothe mũrute wega, no atuĩke ta mũmũruti.
english: A disciple is not above his teacher, but everyone when he is fully trained will be like his teacher.  TR adds “to you”  TR adds “all”  literally, into your bosom.

kikuyu: Agĩthaama Hema-ya-Gũtũnganwo ĩrĩa yarĩ Shilo,* hema ĩrĩa aahaandĩte atũũranagie na andũ.
english: so that he abandoned the tent of Shiloh, the tent which he placed among men,

kikuyu: mũrũ wa Maathi, mũrũ wa Matathia, mũrũ wa Semeini, mũrũ wa Joseki, mũrũ wa Joda,
english: the son of Maath, the son of Mattathias, the son of Semein, the son of Joseph, the son of Judah,

kikuyu: kũrĩ na mĩgambo ya aini itahĩro-inĩ rĩa maaĩ. Mainaga ciĩko cia ũthingu cia Jehova, ciĩko cia ũthingu cia njamba ciake kũu Isiraeli. “Ningĩ andũ a Jehova magĩikũrũka nginya ihingo-inĩ cia itũũra inene.
english: Yahweh’s angel came and sat under the oak which was in Ophrah, that belonged to Joash the Abiezrite. His son Gideon was beating out wheat in the wine press, to hide it from the Midianites.

kikuyu: “Ithuĩ athĩnjĩri-Ngai, na Alawii o na andũ aya angĩ nĩtũcuukĩte mĩtĩ tũgatuanĩra rĩrĩa o nyũmba iitũ ĩrĩĩrehaga ngũ nyũmba-inĩ ya Ngai witũ, cia gũikaragia mwaki wakanĩte kĩgongona-inĩ kĩa Jehova Ngai witũ mahinda marĩa matuanĩirwo o mwaka, o ta ũrĩa kwandĩkĩtwo Watho-inĩ.
english: We, the priests, the Levites, and the people, cast lots for the wood offering, to bring it into the house of our God, according to our fathers’ houses, at times appointed year by year, to burn on Yahweh our God’s altar, as it is written in the law;

kikuyu: Tũma tũkene matukũ maingĩ o ta marĩa ũtũnyamarĩtie, na mĩaka mĩingĩ o ta ĩrĩa tuonete thĩĩna.
english: Blessed are the people who learn to acclaim you. They walk in the light of your presence, Yahweh.

kikuyu: Magaatambũrũkĩria moko mao kuo, o ta ũrĩa mũthambĩri atambũrũkagia moko agĩĩthambĩra. Ngai nĩwe ũkaaharũrũkia mwĩtĩĩo wao, o na marĩ na wara wa wĩra ũrĩa marutaga na moko mao.
english: For we who live are always delivered to death for Jesus’ sake, that the life also of Jesus may be revealed in our mortal flesh.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩũkeyũria na ngoro yaku atĩrĩ, ‘Nũũ wanjiarĩire ciana ici, kuona atĩ nĩndakuĩrĩirwo nĩ ciana ciakwa, ngĩtigwo ta ndĩ thaata; ndaarĩ mũtahe na ngamenwo. Nũũ warerire ciana ici? Niĩ ndatigirwo nyiki, no rĩrĩ, ciana ici ciumĩte nakũ?’ ”
english: Then you will say in your heart, ‘Who has conceived these for me, since I have been bereaved of my children and am alone, an exile, and wandering back and forth? Who has brought these up? Behold, I was left alone. Where were these?’ ”

kikuyu: Andũ arĩa eetigĩri Ngai nĩmathirĩte bũrũri‑inĩ, gũtirĩ mũndũ o na ũmwe mũrũngĩrĩru ũtigarĩte. Andũ othe maikarĩte metereire gũita thakame; o mũndũ araguĩma mũrũ wa nyina na wabu.
english: The godly man has perished out of the earth, and there is no one upright among men. They all lie in wait for blood; every man hunts his brother with a net.

kikuyu: Maanyũrũragia ta andũ matooĩ Ngai, makaahagaranĩria magego mao.
english: Like the profane mockers in feasts, they gnashed their teeth at me.

kikuyu: Agiripa akĩĩra Fesito atĩrĩ, “Mũndũ ũyũ nĩangĩarekererio korwo ndaracooketie ciira riiko kũrĩ Kaisari.”
english: Agrippa said to Festus, “This man might have been set free if he had not appealed to Caesar.”

kikuyu: “Nĩ ũndũ ũcio ngĩmũneana harĩ endwa ake, nĩo andũ a Ashuri o acio eeriragĩria.
english: Slanderous men have been in you to shed blood. In you they have eaten on the mountains. They have committed lewdness among you.

kikuyu: O na ithuĩ ithuothe-rĩ, kũrĩ hĩndĩ twatũũranagia nao, twaathagwo nĩ thuti cia mĩĩrĩ iitũ, na tũkarũmagĩrĩra merirĩria na maũndũ marĩa
english: We also all once lived among them in the lusts of our flesh, doing the desires of the flesh and of the mind, and were by nature children of wrath, even as the rest.  “Christ” means “Anointed One”.  TR reads “understanding” instead of “hearts”

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo Firaũni akĩĩra Jusufu atĩrĩ, “Kĩroto-inĩ gĩakwa-rĩ, ndĩrarũgamĩte hũgũrũrũ-inĩ cia Rũũĩ rwa Nili,
english: Pharaoh spoke to Joseph, “In my dream, behold, I stood on the brink of the river;

kikuyu: makĩĩra Musa atĩrĩ, “Nĩ tũnyiitĩtwo nĩ thaahu nĩ ũndũ wa kũhutania na kĩimba kĩa mũndũ, no nĩ kĩĩ gĩgũtũma tũgirio kũneana indo cia kũrutĩra Jehova tũrĩ hamwe na andũ a Isiraeli arĩa angĩ ihinda rĩrĩa rĩtuĩtwo?”
english: Those men said to him, “We are unclean because of the dead body of a man. Why are we kept back, that we may not offer the offering of Yahweh in its appointed season among the children of Israel?”

kikuyu: Nĩanyiihagia andũ arĩa maikaraga kũrĩa gũtũũgĩru, o na itũũra rĩrĩa itũũgĩru akarĩharũrũkia, akarĩharagania thĩ, agacooka akarĩtungumania rũkũngũ-inĩ.
english: 1 For we know that if the earthly house of our tent is dissolved, we have a building from God, a house not made with hands, eternal, in the heavens.

kikuyu: Mũthenya wa ikũmi Ahiezeri mũrũ wa Amishadai, mũtongoria wa andũ a Dani, nake akĩrehe maruta make.
english: On the tenth day Ahiezer the son of Ammishaddai, prince of the children of Dan,

kikuyu: kana mũthenya ũrĩa onanirie ciama ciake kũu bũrũri wa Misiri, kana magegania make marĩa onanirie kũu werũ-inĩ wa Zoani.
english: how he set his signs in Egypt, his wonders in the field of Zoan,

kikuyu: Nĩngonania ũtheru wa rĩĩtwa rĩakwa inene, rĩrĩa rĩthaahĩtio ndũrĩrĩ-inĩ icio, rĩĩtwa rĩu inyuĩ mũthaahĩtie gatagatĩ-inĩ ga cio. Hĩndĩ ĩyo ndũrĩrĩ icio nĩikamenya atĩ niĩ nĩ niĩ Jehova, hĩndĩ ĩrĩa ngeyonania atĩ ndĩ mũtheru maitho-inĩ mao kũhĩtũkĩra harĩ inyuĩ, ũguo nĩguo Mwathani Jehova ekuuga. * 36:8 Ũyũ warĩ ũndũ wa kuonania maciaro maingĩ (17:8, 23), o hamwe na ũtugi wa Ngai.
english: I will sanctify my great name, which has been profaned among the nations, which you have profaned among them. Then the nations will know that I am Yahweh,” says the Lord Yahweh, “when I am proven holy in you before their eyes.

kikuyu: Matigacũthĩrĩria igongona iria mathondekete na moko mao, na matikarũmbũiya itugĩ cia Ashera, na magongona marĩa methondekeire ma gũcinĩra ũbumba.
english: They will not look to the altars, the work of their hands; neither shall they respect that which their fingers have made, either the Asherah poles or the incense altars.

kikuyu: Ciathĩ iria Njathane Ningĩ Jehova akĩĩra Musa atĩrĩ,
english: 1 Yahweh spoke to Moses, saying,

kikuyu: na rũũa rwa ndegwa ĩyo, na nyama ciayo ciothe hamwe, na mũtwe na mathagiro, na nyama cia nda na mahu,
english: He shall carry the bull’s skin, all its meat, with its head, and with its legs, its innards, and its dung

kikuyu: Ariũ a Rakeli maarĩ: Jusufu na Benjamini.
english: The sons of Rachel: Joseph and Benjamin.

kikuyu: Wee igũrũ ina nĩ gũkena, nĩgũkorwo nĩ Jehova wĩkĩte ũndũ ũcio; nawe thĩ ũrĩ rungu rwa igũrũ-rĩ, anĩrĩra. Na inyuĩ irĩma-rĩ, inai rwĩmbo, o na inyuĩ mĩtitũ na mĩtĩ yanyu yothe inai, nĩgũkorwo Jehova nĩakũũrĩte Jakubu, na akoonania riiri wake kũu Isiraeli. Jerusalemu Nĩgũgatũũrwo
english: Sing, you heavens, for Yahweh has done it! Shout, you lower parts of the earth! Break out into singing, you mountains, O forest, all of your trees, for Yahweh has redeemed Jacob, and will glorify himself in Israel.

kikuyu: Ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ, ũguo noguo gũkoragwo na gĩkeno harĩ araika a Ngai nĩ ũndũ wa mwĩhia ũmwe wĩrirĩte.” Ngerekano ya Mwanake ũrĩa Worĩte
english: He who is faithful in a very little is faithful also in much. He who is dishonest in a very little is also dishonest in much.

kikuyu: Nĩmamũtũũgĩrie kĩũngano-inĩ kĩa andũ, na mamũgooce marĩ kĩama-inĩ kĩa athuuri.
english: Let them exalt him also in the assembly of the people, and praise him in the seat of the elders.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo ũrĩa wothe wĩtũgagĩria nĩakanyiihio, na ũrĩa wothe wĩnyiihagia nĩagatũũgĩrio.
english: Because iniquity will be multiplied, the love of many will grow cold.

kikuyu: Nĩngamũrehithĩria rũhiũ rwa njora rũrĩhĩrie kĩrĩkanĩro kĩrĩa mũthũkĩtie. Muorĩra matũũra-inĩ marĩa manene manyu, nĩngamũrehithĩria mũthiro thĩinĩ wanyu, na ndĩmũrekererie moko-inĩ ma thũ cianyu. Rĩrĩa ngaamwehereria makũmbĩ manyu ma irio, andũ-a-nja ikũmi makaarugagĩra mĩgate yanyu riiko rĩmwe, nao macooke kũmũrehera mĩgate ĩyo ĩthimĩtwo na ratiri. Mũkaamĩrĩĩaga na mũtihũũne.
english: I will bring a sword upon you that will execute the vengeance of the covenant. You will be gathered together within your cities, and I will send the pestilence among you. You will be delivered into the hand of the enemy.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo arĩa othe matũũraga bũrũri wa Misiri nĩmakamenya atĩ niĩ nĩ niĩ Jehova. “ ‘WeeũkoretwoũrĩmũthĩgiwakamũrangiwagũtiiranyũmbayaIsiraeli.
english: “ ‘ “All the inhabitants of Egypt will know that I am Yahweh, because they have been a staff of reed to the house of Israel.

kikuyu: Igua mũgambo wakwa kũringana na wendo waku; Wee Jehova, tũũria muoyo wakwa, kũringana na mawatho maku.
english: Hear my voice according to your loving kindness. Revive me, Yahweh, according to your ordinances.

kikuyu: Ndeciiragia ũhoro wa njĩra ya muoyo; njĩra ciake nĩ njogomu, nowe ndooĩ ũguo.
english: She gives no thought to the way of life. Her ways are crooked, and she doesn’t know it.

kikuyu: O mbere nĩngũcookeria Ngai wakwa ngaatho thĩinĩ wa Jesũ Kristũ nĩ ũndũ wanyu inyuothe, tondũ ũhoro wa wĩtĩkio wanyu nĩũmenyithanĩtio thĩ yothe.
english: but to those who are self-seeking and don’t obey the truth, but obey unrighteousness, will be wrath, indignation,

kikuyu: Ndĩgaatetania kana ĩgũũthũke; na gũtirĩ mũndũ ũkaigua mũgambo wayo njĩra-inĩ.
english: The Son of Man came eating and drinking, and they say, ‘Behold, a gluttonous man and a drunkard, a friend of tax collectors and sinners!’ But wisdom is justified by her children.”‡

kikuyu: na thenge ĩmwe ya igongona rĩa kũhoroherio mehia;
english: Yahweh spoke to Moses, saying,

kikuyu: Joshua akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Mũtigetigĩre kana mũkue ngoro. Gĩai na hinya na mũũmĩrĩrie. Ũũ nĩguo Jehova arĩĩkaga thũ cianyu ciothe iria mũrĩrũaga nacio.” Nake Joshua akĩringa athamaki acio akĩmooraga, agĩcooka akĩmacuuria igũrũ rĩa mĩtĩ ĩtano, nao magĩtinda macunjurĩte mĩtĩ-inĩ ĩyo nginya hwaĩ-inĩ.
english: Joshua said to them, “Don’t be afraid, nor be dismayed. Be strong and courageous, for Yahweh will do this to all your enemies against whom you fight.”

kikuyu: Andũ arĩa Ahũthũ na arĩa marĩ Hinya Na rĩrĩ, mũndũ ũrĩa ũkoragwo arĩ na wĩtĩkio mũhũthũ mwamũkĩrei, mũtekũmũtuĩra ciira nĩ ũndũ wa maũndũ marĩa mataiguanĩirwo.
english: 1 Now accept one who is weak in faith, but not for disputes over opinions.

kikuyu: na Shaaraimu, na Adithaimu, na Gedera (kana Gederothaimu); mothe maarĩ matũũra ikũmi na mana na tũtũũra twamo.
english: Shaaraim, Adithaim and Gederah (or Gederothaim); fourteen cities with their villages.

kikuyu: Nomũndũowothewĩthaahĩtie angĩkaarĩa nyama cia iruta rĩu rĩa ũiguano rĩrutĩirwo Jehova-rĩ, mũndũ ũcio no nginya akaingatwo kuuma kũrĩ andũ ao.
english: He cut the ram into its pieces; and Moses burned the head, and the pieces, and the fat.

kikuyu: Jona Gũthiĩ Nineve Ningĩ ndũmĩrĩri ya Jehova ĩgĩkinyĩra Jona hĩndĩ ya keerĩ, akĩĩrwo atĩrĩ:
english: Therefore now, Yahweh, take, I beg you, my life from me, for it is better for me to die than to live.”

kikuyu: Jeremia nĩandĩkĩte ibuku-inĩ rĩa gĩkũnjo ũhoro wothe ũkoniĩ mwanangĩko ũrĩa ũgaakinyĩrĩra Babuloni: maũndũ marĩa mothe maandĩkĩtwo makoniĩ Babuloni.
english: Jeremiah wrote in a book all the evil that should come on Babylon, even all these words that are written concerning Babylon.

kikuyu: Narĩrĩ, kahindagakegaakuhĩrĩriagakwambatamatu-inĩ, JesũnĩatuireituarĩagũthiĩJerusalemu. Nake agĩtũma andũ mathiĩ mbere yake kũhaarĩria maũndũ nĩ ũndũ wake, magĩthiĩ magĩtoonya gatũũra kamwe ga Samaria;*
english: It came to pass, when the days were near that he should be taken up, he intently set his face to go to Jerusalem

kikuyu: Nayo mbura ĩkiura thĩ matukũ mĩrongo ĩna, mũthenya na ũtukũ.
