english: God saw the earth, and saw that it was corrupt, for all flesh had corrupted their way on the earth.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo guoko kwa Jehova gũgaikaraga kĩrĩma-inĩ gĩkĩ; no Moabi gũkaarangĩrĩrio rungu rwake, o ta ũrĩa nyeki nyũmũ ĩrangagĩrĩrio thumu-inĩ.
english: always carrying in the body the putting to death of the Lord Jesus, that the life of Jesus may also be revealed in our body.

kikuyu: Andũ nĩmathamĩtie tũnua twao maathekerere; mangũthaga rũthĩa makĩĩnyũrũragia, na makanyiitana hamwe manjũkĩrĩre.
english: With us are both the gray-headed and the very aged men, much older than your father.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo o ta ũrĩa iria rĩthũcagwo, rĩkoima thiagĩ, na o ta ũrĩa iniũrũ rĩthioragwo rĩkoira thakame-rĩ, ũguo noguo gũthogotha marakara kũrehaga ngũĩ.”
english: For as the churning of milk produces butter, and the wringing of the nose produces blood, so the forcing of wrath produces strife.”

kikuyu: Bũrũri wa Misiri ndũgacooka gũtuĩka mwĩhoko wa andũ a Isiraeli, no ũgaatũĩka kĩririkania kĩa mehia mao ma gũcaria ũteithio kuo. Hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩmakamenya atĩ niĩ nĩ niĩ Mwathani Jehova.’ ”
english: It will no longer be the confidence of the house of Israel, bringing iniquity to memory, when they turn to look after them. Then they will know that I am the Lord Yahweh.” ’ ”

kikuyu: Ciana ciakwa nyendete mũno, o inyuĩ arĩa njiguagĩra ruo ta rwa mũtumia akĩheo mwana, nginya hĩndĩ ĩrĩa Kristũ agaakorwo eyũmbĩte thĩinĩ wanyu,
english: My little children, of whom I am again in travail until Christ is formed in you—

kikuyu: Maritha aarĩ na mwarĩ wa nyina wetagwo Mariamu, nake aikarĩte magũrũ-inĩ ma Mwathani athikĩrĩirie ũrĩa oigaga.
english: She had a sister called Mary, who also sat at Jesus’ feet and heard his word.

kikuyu: Aathiire Misiri na ariũ ake na tũmwana twa ariũ ake, agĩthiĩ na airĩtu ake na tũirĩtu twa airĩtu ake, ũguo nĩ kuuga rũciaro rwake ruothe.
english: God sent me before you to preserve for you a remnant in the earth, and to save you alive by a great deliverance.

kikuyu: nĩ ũndũ wao akĩririkana kĩrĩkanĩro gĩake, na nĩ ũndũ wa wendo wake mũnene akĩringwo nĩ tha.
english: He remembered for them his covenant, and repented according to the multitude of his loving kindnesses.

kikuyu: Aca, ngoro-inĩ cianyu mũthugundaga maũndũ matarĩ ma kĩhooto, na moko manyu mageeka maũndũ ma kũruta haaro gũkũ thĩ.
english: I cry out to God Most High,  Sheol is the place of the dead.

kikuyu: na akĩrũgama thuutha wake, o hau magũrũ-inĩ make, akĩrĩraga, na akĩambĩrĩria kũmũihũgia nyarĩrĩ na maithori make. Agĩcooka akĩmũgiria nyarĩrĩ na njuĩrĩ yake, agĩcimumunya, na agĩciitĩrĩria maguta manungi wega. Rĩrĩa Mũfarisai ũrĩa wamwĩtĩte onire ũndũ ũcio, akĩĩra na ngoro atĩrĩ, “Korwo mũndũ ũyũ nĩ mũnabii, nĩangĩamenya ũrĩa ũramũhutia nĩ mũndũ-wa-nja wa mũthemba ũrĩkũ, na atĩ nĩ mwĩhia.”
english: Standing behind at his feet weeping, she began to wet his feet with her tears, and she wiped them with the hair of her head, kissed his feet, and anointed them with the ointment.

kikuyu: Mũndũ-wa-nja agĩciara nĩakoragwo na ruo tondũ hĩndĩ yake nĩ nginyu; no aarĩkia gũciara kaana, akariganĩrwo nĩ ruo nĩ ũndũ wa gũkena nĩgũkorwo kaana nĩ gaaciarwo gũkũ thĩ.
english: A woman, when she gives birth, has sorrow because her time has come. But when she has delivered the child, she doesn’t remember the anguish any more, for the joy that a human being is born into the world.

kikuyu: Ngoretwo itarĩ na ũcuuke ndĩ mbere yake, na ngemenyerera ndikehie.
english: I was also blameless with him. I kept myself from my iniquity.

kikuyu: Kwanangwo kwa Ai Ningĩ Jehova akĩĩra Joshua atĩrĩ, “Ndũgetigĩre na ndũgakue ngoro. Thiĩ na mbũtũ yothe ya ita, mwambate mũgatharĩkĩre itũũra rĩa Ai. Nĩgũkorwo nĩneanĩte mũthamaki wa Ai moko-inĩ manyu, hamwe na andũ ake, na itũũra rĩake rĩu inene na bũrũri wake.
english: No destroyer of the desert or ravisher of the plain injured me, nor did the destructive, deceitful snake make plunder of my chaste virginity. I remained with my husband during the time of my maturity.

kikuyu: Nĩmwĩrĩga kwĩrira, na mũgĩĩka maũndũ marĩa magĩrĩire makĩonwo nĩ niĩ: Tondũ o ũmwe wanyu nĩanĩrĩire ũhoro wa kuohorwo kwa mũndũ o mũndũ bũrũri-inĩ. O na ningĩ mũkĩgĩa na kĩrĩkanĩro kĩu mũrĩ mbere yakwa thĩinĩ wa nyũmba ĩrĩa ĩĩtanĩtio na rĩĩtwa rĩakwa. No rĩu nĩmũgarũrũkĩte mũgathaahia rĩĩtwa rĩakwa; mũndũ o mũndũ wanyu nĩacooketie ngombo yake ya mũndũ mũrũme na ya mũndũ-wa-nja ĩrĩa ararekereirie ĩĩthiĩre o kũrĩa ĩngĩenda. Nĩmũmatuĩte ngombo cianyu rĩngĩ.
english: I have sent also to you all my servants the prophets, rising up early and sending them, saying, ‘Every one of you must return now from his evil way, amend your doings, and don’t go after other gods to serve them. Then you will dwell in the land which I have given to you and to your fathers;’ but you have not inclined your ear, nor listened to me.

kikuyu: Mũndũ wandĩkaga mũndũ mũkĩĩgu kana mwĩhĩtũkĩri nĩ ta ũrĩa ũikagia mĩguĩ o ro ũguo agatiihia andũ.
english: lest one who hears it put you to shame, and your bad reputation never depart.

kikuyu: Gĩcigo kĩa mũigana wa ũraihu wa mĩkono 25,000, na wariĩ wa 10,000 gĩgaatuĩka kĩa Alawii, arĩa matungataga thĩinĩ wa hekarũ, gĩtuĩke igai rĩao rĩa gwakwo matũũra ma gũtũũrwo.
english: Twenty-five thousand cubits in length and ten thousand in width shall be for the Levites, the ministers of the house, as a possession for themselves, for twenty rooms.

kikuyu: Nĩngũgoocithĩtie gũkũ thĩ na ũndũ wa kũrĩĩkia wĩra ũrĩa waheire ndute. Na rĩu Baba, ngoocithia ndĩ mbere yaku na riiri ũrĩa ndaarĩ naguo hamwe nawe o mbere thĩ ĩtaanagĩa. Jesũ Kũhoera Arutwo ake
english: But I have told you these things so that when the time comes, you may remember that I told you about them. I didn’t tell you these things from the beginning, because I was with you.

kikuyu: Ndũngĩgũrĩka na thahabu ya Ofiri, kana ũgũrĩke na onigithi ĩrĩa ĩrĩ goro mũno, o na kana yakuti ĩrĩa ya bururu.
english: It can’t be valued with the gold of Ophir, with the precious onyx, or the sapphire.†

kikuyu: Mahinda ma tene Ngai ndaigana kũrũmbũiya ũhoro ũcio wa kwaga ũmenyo, no rĩu nĩathĩte andũ othe a kũndũ guothe merire.
english: The times of ignorance therefore God overlooked. But now he commands that all people everywhere should repent,

kikuyu: no atĩ nĩetĩkagĩra andũ kuuma ndũrĩrĩ-inĩ ciothe arĩa mamwĩtigagĩra na mageeka ũrĩa kwagĩrĩire.
english: but in every nation he who fears him and works righteousness is acceptable to him.

kikuyu: Ngĩkuhĩrĩria ũmwe wa acio maarũngiĩ hau, ngĩmũũria andaarĩrie ma ya ũhoro ũcio wothe. “Nĩ ũndũ ũcio akĩndaũrĩra maũndũ macio mothe, akĩnjĩĩra atĩrĩ:
english: I came near to one of those who stood by, and asked him the truth concerning all this. “So he told me, and made me know the interpretation of the things.

kikuyu: Nĩnjigĩte arangĩri a thingo ciaku, wee Jerusalemu, nao matigakira mũthenya kana ũtukũ. Inyuĩ arĩa mũkayagĩra Jehova-rĩ, mũtikanahurũke,
english: I trod down the peoples in my anger and made them drunk in my wrath. I poured their lifeblood out on the earth.”

kikuyu: Mwathani Jehova ekuuga atĩrĩ: “Itũũra inene rĩrĩa rĩatũmaga thigari ngiri cia Isiraeli-rĩ, no thigari igana igaatigara; narĩo itũũra rĩrĩa rĩatũmaga thigari igana-rĩ, no thigari ikũmi igaatigara.”
english: For the Lord Yahweh says: “The city that went out a thousand shall have a hundred left, and that which went out one hundred shall have ten left to the house of Israel.”

kikuyu: “Niĩ mwene nĩngacookereria matigari ma rũũru rwa mbũri ciakwa kuuma mabũrũri mothe marĩa ndaacitwarithirie, ndĩcicookie ũrĩithio-inĩ wacio, na nĩigaciaranĩra kuo, cingĩhe.
english: “I will gather the remnant of my flock out of all the countries where I have driven them, and will bring them again to their folds; and they will be fruitful and multiply.

kikuyu: “O na ũmũthĩ gũteta gwakwa no kũrũrũ; guoko gwake nĩkũnditũhĩire o na ngĩcaayaga ũguo.
english: “Even today my complaint is rebellious. His hand is heavy in spite of my groaning.

kikuyu: Gayaniai indo cia iria ndahe gatagatĩ ga thigari iria ciathiĩte mbaara-inĩ na kĩrĩndĩ kĩrĩa kĩngĩ.
english: and divide the plunder into two parts: between the men skilled in war, who went out to battle, and all the congregation.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo nĩahinyĩrĩirie athĩĩni, akamatiga matarĩ na kĩndũ; nĩatunyanĩte nyũmba iria ataakire.
english: All my familiar friends abhor me. They whom I loved have turned against me.

kikuyu: Korinelio Gũtũmanĩra Petero Na rĩrĩ, kũu Kaisarea nĩ kwarĩ mũndũ wetagwo Korinelio, ũrĩa warĩ mũnene-wa-thigari-igana rĩmwe cia mbũtũ ĩrĩa yoĩkaine ta Mbũtũ ya Italia.
english: He became hungry and desired to eat, but while they were preparing, he fell into a trance.

kikuyu: Ndũkanyingate nyume harĩwe, o na kana weherie Roho waku Mũtheru kuuma thĩinĩ wakwa.
english: Don’t throw me from your presence, and don’t take your Holy Spirit from me.

kikuyu: Angĩkorwo nĩ mĩaka mĩingĩ ĩtigarĩte, nĩakarĩha gĩcunjĩ kĩnene gĩa thogora ũrĩa aagũrĩtwo naguo. No angĩkorwo no mĩaka mĩnini ĩtigarĩte Mwaka wa Jubilii ũkinye, ĩyo noyo agaatara arĩhe gũkũũranio gwake kũringana na ũrĩa kwagĩrĩire.
english: If there are yet many years, according to them he shall give back the price of his redemption out of the money that he was bought for.

kikuyu: Mwaka-inĩ wa ikũmi na ĩĩrĩ wa Mũthamaki Ahasuerusu, mweri wa mbere, mweri wa Nisani, magĩcuuka puri (ũguo nĩ ta kuuga gũcuuka mĩtĩ) mbere ya Hamani nĩguo mathuure mũthenya na mweri. Naguo mũtĩ ũkĩgwĩra mweri wa ikũmi na ĩĩrĩ, nĩguo mweri wa Adari.
english: In the first month, which is the month Nisan, in the twelfth year of King Ahasuerus, they cast Pur, that is, the lot, before Haman from day to day, and from month to month, and chose the twelfth month, which is the month Adar.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa mekuuma matoonye nja ya nyumĩrĩra kũrĩa andũ marĩ-rĩ, makaruta nguo iria megũtungataga nacio, na magacitiga tũnyũmba-inĩ tũrĩa twamũre, na makehumba nguo ingĩ, nĩgeetha matikae kwamũra andũ acio na ũndũ wa nguo icio ciao.
english: When they go out into the outer court, even into the outer court to the people, they shall put off their garments in which they minister and lay them in the holy rooms. They shall put on other garments, that they not sanctify the people with their garments.

kikuyu: “Ũrĩa uumĩte igũrũ nĩ mũnene kũrĩ andũ othe; nake ũrĩa uumĩte thĩ nĩ wa gũkũ thĩ, na maũndũ marĩa aragia no ma gũkũ thĩ. Ũrĩa uumĩte igũrũ arĩ igũrũ rĩa andũ arĩa angĩ othe.
english: “He who comes from above is above all. He who is from the earth belongs to the earth and speaks of the earth. He who comes from heaven is above all.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo andũ othe nĩmonaga atĩ o na andũ arĩa oogĩ nĩmakuuaga; mũndũ mũkĩĩgu na mũndũ kĩrimũ o nao nĩmakuaga, magatigĩra andũ angĩ ũtonga wao.
english: For every animal of the forest is mine, and the livestock on a thousand hills.

kikuyu: Ta njĩĩrai, inyuĩ arĩa mũrenda gũkorwo mũgĩathwo nĩ watho-rĩ, kaĩ mũtooĩ ũrĩa watho uugaga?
english: Tell me, you that desire to be under the law, don’t you listen to the law?

kikuyu: Ithuĩ nĩ tũrĩ na kĩgongona kĩrĩa andũ arĩa matungataga kũu hema-inĩ ĩyo matarĩ na rũtha rwa kũrĩĩaga magongona marĩa marutagĩrwo ho.
english: not stealing, but showing all good fidelity, that they may adorn the doctrine of God, our Savior, in all things.

kikuyu: no marigĩrĩrio nĩmũrũrũ ta maaĩ ma nyongo, nĩ mũũgĩ ta rũhiũ rwa njora rũrĩa rũũgĩ mbarĩ cierĩ.
english: but in the end she is as bitter as wormwood, and as sharp as a two-edged sword.

kikuyu: Mbũkũ o na aakorwo nĩĩcookagia ithagumia-rĩ, mahũngũ mayo matirekanĩtie; ĩyo ĩrĩ thaahu harĩ inyuĩ.
english: The hare, because it chews the cud but doesn’t have a parted hoof, is unclean to you.

kikuyu: thĩinĩ wakwa haana ta ndibei ĩrĩ cuba ngunĩke, ngahaana ta mondo njerũ ya ndibei ĩkirie gũtuthũka.
english: Behold, my breast is as wine which has no vent; like new wineskins it is ready to burst.

kikuyu: Igai rĩakwa rĩtuĩkĩte ta mũrũũthi ũrĩ gĩthaka-inĩ harĩ niĩ. Nĩkũrarama ũndaramĩire, nĩ ũndũ ũcio ngaũthũũra.
english: My heritage has become to me as a lion in the forest. She has uttered her voice against me. Therefore I have hated her.

kikuyu: “Mbeũ nĩĩgakũra wega, naguo mũthabibũ nĩũgaciara matunda maguo, na tĩĩri nĩũkaruta magetha maguo, narĩo igũrũ nĩrĩgatatia ime. Ngaaheana maũndũ maya mothe matuĩke igai rĩa matigari ma andũ aya.
english: “For the seed of peace and the vine will yield its fruit, and the ground will give its increase, and the heavens will give their dew. I will cause the remnant of this people to inherit all these things.

kikuyu: Nĩnjũũĩ atĩ ngĩũkĩra kũu kwanyu, ngaakorwo njiyũrĩrĩirwo nĩ irathimo cia Kristũ.
english: I know that when I come to you, I will come in the fullness of the blessing of the Good News of Christ.

kikuyu: Isaaka agĩcookeria Esaũ atĩrĩ, “Ndĩmũtuire mwathi waku na andũ ao othe ndaamatua ndungata ciake, na ndamũhe ngano na ndibei ya mũhihano imũtũũragie. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio nĩ atĩa ingĩhota gũgwĩkĩra, mũrũ wakwa?”
english: Isaac answered Esau, “Behold, I have made him your lord, and all his brothers I have given to him for servants. I have sustained him with grain and new wine. What then will I do for you, my son?”

kikuyu: Rũngagĩrĩria makinya makwa kũringana na kiugo gĩaku; ndũkareke njathagwo nĩ wĩhia o na ũrĩkũ.
english: Establish my footsteps in your word. Don’t let any iniquity have dominion over me.

kikuyu: Andũ arĩa othe matũũraga ituri ciothe cia thĩ nĩmakaririkana na macookerere Jehova, andũ a nyũmba ciothe cia ndũrĩrĩ nĩmakainamĩrĩra mbere yake,
english: All the ends of the earth shall remember and turn to Yahweh. All the relatives of the nations shall worship before you.

kikuyu: Nyama icio cia iruta rĩu rĩake rĩa ũiguano rĩa gũcookia ngaatho no nginya irĩĩo mũthenya o ro ũcio ikaarutĩrwo Jehova; ndakanatigie gĩcunjĩ o na kĩmwe gĩkinyie rũciinĩ.
english: He shall take from there his handful of the fine flour of the meal offering, and of its oil, and all the frankincense which is on the meal offering, and shall burn it on the altar for a pleasant aroma, as its memorial portion, to Yahweh.

kikuyu: “Edomu arĩ kũu, hamwe na athamaki ake, na anene ake othe; o na maarĩ na hinya-rĩ, makometio hamwe na arĩa maanooragwo na rũhiũ rwa njora. Makomete hamwe na arĩa mataruĩte, o hamwe na arĩa maharũrũkagio irima.
english: “There is Edom, her kings, and all her princes, who in their might are laid with those who are slain by the sword. They will lie with the uncircumcised, and with those who go down to the pit.

kikuyu: tũtiathĩkĩire Jehova Ngai witũ kana tũkarũmia mawatho make marĩa aatũheire na tũnua twa anabii, ndungata ciake.
english: A fiery stream issued and came out from before him. Thousands of thousands ministered to him. Ten thousand times ten thousand stood before him. The judgment was set. The books were opened.

kikuyu: Ndũrĩrĩ nĩikaigua ũhoro wa gũconorithio gwaku; kĩrĩro gĩaku gĩkaaiyũra thĩ. Njamba ĩkaahĩngagwo nĩ ĩrĩa ĩngĩ; cierĩ ikaagũa thĩ hamwe.”
english: The nations have heard of your shame, and the earth is full of your cry; for the mighty man has stumbled against the mighty, they both fall together.”

kikuyu: kũrĩa aaikarire mĩeri ĩtatũ. Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa Paũlũ eeharagĩria kũhaica marikabu athiĩ Suriata, agĩtua itua gũcookera Makedonia, tondũ Ayahudi nĩmaciirĩire kũmwĩka ũũru.
english: When he had spent three months there, and a plot was made against him by Jews as he was about to set sail for Syria, he determined to return through Macedonia.

kikuyu: Kũrigania nake, Meremothu mũrũ wa Uria, mũrũ wa Hakozu, nĩacookereirie gĩcunjĩ kĩu kĩngĩ kuuma itoonyero rĩa nyũmba ya Eliashibu nginya mũthia-inĩ wayo. * 3:13 nĩ ta mita
english: After him, Meremoth the son of Uriah the son of Hakkoz repaired another portion, from the door of the house of Eliashib even to the end of the house of Eliashib.

kikuyu: Ũndũ ũyũ wekĩkire maita matatũ, nakĩo gĩtama kĩu gĩkĩguucio na igũrũ.
english: This was done three times, and all were drawn up again into heaven.

kikuyu: Moragia atĩrĩ, ‘Nĩ kĩĩ gĩtũmĩte twĩhinge kũrĩa irio, nawe ũkaaga kuona? Nĩ kĩĩ gĩtũmĩte twĩnyiihie, nawe ũkaaga kũrũmbũiya ũhoro ũcio?’ “No rĩrĩ, mũthenya wanyu wa kwĩhinga kũrĩa irio mwĩkaga o ũrĩa mũkwenda, na aruti othe anyu a wĩra mũkamahinyĩrĩria.
english: ‘Why have we fasted,’ they say, ‘and you don’t see? Why have we afflicted our soul, and you don’t notice?’ “Behold, in the day of your fast you find pleasure, and oppress all your laborers.

kikuyu: Ha ũhoro wa ciĩko ciake cia hinya na ũhoti, hamwe na ũhoro wothe wa ũnene wa Moridekai, nginya harĩa mũthamaki aamũnenehetie-rĩ, githĩ ti mwandĩke mabuku-inĩ ma maũndũ ma athamaki a Media na Perisia?
english: Aren’t all the acts of his power and of his might, and the full account of the greatness of Mordecai, to which the king advanced him, written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Media and Persia?

kikuyu: “ ‘Aarĩkia gũtiga kuura, nĩageterera mĩthenya mũgwanja, na thuutha ũcio nĩagathirwo nĩ thaahu.
english: “ ‘But if she is cleansed of her discharge, then she shall count to herself seven days, and after that she shall be clean.

kikuyu: Aamagereirie njĩra nũngarũ, nginya magĩkinya itũũra inene kũrĩa mangĩonire gwa gũtũũra.
english: He led them also by a straight way, that they might go to a city to live in.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa magaathiĩ na ndũũru ciao cia mbũri na cia ngʼombe kũrongooria Jehova-rĩ, matikamuona; we nĩamehereire.
english: My people are destroyed for lack of knowledge. Because you have rejected knowledge, I will also reject you, that you may be no priest to me. Because you have forgotten your God’s law, I will also forget your children.

kikuyu: Nĩũgaciruta igongona hau mbere ya Jehova, nao athĩnjĩri-Ngai nĩmagaciminjaminjĩria cumbĩ, rĩtuĩke igongona rĩa njino rĩrutĩirwo Jehova.
english: The doors had two leaves each, two turning leaves: two for the one door, and two leaves for the other.

kikuyu: Gũtirĩ igai thĩinĩ wa Isiraeli rĩkoima mũhĩrĩga ũmwe rĩtuĩke rĩa mũhĩrĩga ũngĩ, nĩgũkorwo Mũisiraeli o wothe nĩagatũũria gĩthaka kĩa mũhĩrĩga kĩrĩa maagaire kuuma kũrĩ maithe mao ma tene.
english: So shall no inheritance of the children of Israel move from tribe to tribe; for the children of Israel shall all keep the inheritance of the tribe of his fathers.

kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa wanyu ũngĩenda gũtũũra muoyo, na erirĩrie kuona matukũ maingĩ mega,
english: Who is someone who desires life, and loves many days, that he may see good?

kikuyu: Ndigathũkia kĩrĩkanĩro gĩakwa kana ngarũrĩre ũrĩa iromo ciakwa ciarĩtie.
english: I will not break my covenant, nor alter what my lips have uttered.

kikuyu: Petero akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Nĩ kĩĩ gĩtũmĩte mũrĩĩkanĩre mũgerie Roho wa Mwathani? Atĩrĩrĩ, magũrũ ma andũ arĩa maathika mũthuuriguo marĩ mũromo-inĩ, nĩmegũkuumia nja o nawe.”
english: But Peter asked her, “How is it that you have agreed together to tempt the Spirit of the Lord? Behold, the feet of those who have buried your husband are at the door, and they will carry you out.”

kikuyu: Andũ arĩa maatongoretie na arĩa maamumĩte thuutha makĩanĩrĩra makiuga atĩrĩ, “Hosana!”* “Kũrathimwo-rĩ, nĩ ũrĩa ũgũũka na rĩĩtwa rĩa Mwathani!”
english: Those who went in front and those who followed cried out, “Hosanna!‡ Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord!*

kikuyu: no Jehova ndakamatiganĩria moko-inĩ ma andũ acio aaganu, kana areke matuĩrwo atĩ nĩ ehia rĩrĩa magaaciirithio.
english: Yahweh will not leave him in his hand, nor condemn him when he is judged.

kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo marigithathi mothe nĩ makwa. Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa ndooragire marigithathi mothe ma Misiri, noguo ndeyamũrĩire marigithathi mothe ma Isiraeli, marĩ ma mũndũ kana ma nyamũ. Macio nĩ makwa kĩũmbe. Niĩ nĩ niĩ Jehova.” Ningĩ Jehova akĩĩra Musa kũu Werũ-inĩ wa Sinai atĩrĩ, 15“Tara Alawii† kũringana na nyũmba ciao na mbarĩ ciao. Tara arũme othe arĩa makinyĩtie mweri ũmwe na makĩria.” Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, Musa akĩmatara o ta ũrĩa aathĩtwo nĩ kiugo kĩa Jehova.
english: for all the firstborn are mine. On the day that I struck down all the firstborn in the land of Egypt I made holy to me all the firstborn in Israel, both man and animal. They shall be mine. I am Yahweh.”

kikuyu: Mwaki ũgĩcina aanake ao, nao airĩtu ao makĩaga kũinagĩrwo nyĩmbo cia ũhiki;
english: Fire devoured their young men. Their virgins had no wedding song.

kikuyu: “Ambatagia matata ma maaĩ, marĩa macookaga gũtaatĩra tũrũũĩ ta mbura;
english: For he draws up the drops of water, which distill in rain from his vapor,

kikuyu: “No inyuĩ mwĩtigĩrĩte rĩĩtwa rĩakwa-rĩ, riũa-rĩa-ũthingu rĩkaamwarĩra rĩrĩ na ũhoro wa kũhonania mathagu-inĩ marĩo. Na inyuĩ nĩmũkoima nja mũtũũhe ta tũcaũ tũhingũrĩirwo tũkoima kiugũ. Na nĩmũkarangĩrĩria arĩa aaganu matuĩke mũhu rungu rwa makinya manyu mũthenya ũrĩa ngeeka maũndũ macio,” ũguo nĩguo Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe ekuuga.
english: “O Lord, you are righteous, and all your works and all your ways are mercy and truth, and you judge true and righteous judgment forever.

kikuyu: Aanake marutithagio wĩra wa hinya ithĩi-inĩ; tũmwana natuo tũtũgũũgaga nĩgũkuuithio mĩrigo ya ngũ.
english: The young men carry millstones. The children stumbled under loads of wood.

kikuyu: “Nũũ ũyũ ũrekĩra kĩrĩra gĩakwa nduma na ciugo itarĩ na ũmenyo?
english: “Who is this who darkens counsel by words without knowledge?

kikuyu: Rũhũũraga hĩ rũkamũkenerera, na rũkagamba ta nyoka rũkamũruta kũrĩa atũũraga.”
english: “God understands its way, and he knows its place.

kikuyu: Ndĩramũtũma kũrĩ inyuĩ nĩ ũndũ wa gĩtũmi gĩkĩ kĩũmbe, nĩguo mũmenye ũhoro witũ ũrĩa tũtariĩ, na nĩguo amũũmĩrĩrie ngoro.
english: I have sent him to you for this very purpose, that you may know our state and that he may comfort your hearts.

kikuyu: O mũirĩtu aarĩ athiĩ harĩ mũthamaki ta ũũ: Nĩaheagwo kĩndũ kĩrĩa gĩothe angĩendire gwĩkuuĩra kuuma nyũmba ĩyo yatũũragwo nĩ andũ-a-nja gĩa gũthiĩ nakĩo nyũmba-inĩ ya mũthamaki.
english: The young woman then came to the king like this: whatever she desired was given her to go with her out of the women’s house to the king’s house.

kikuyu: Kũrathimwo nĩ mũndũ ũrĩa ũtarahĩngwo nĩ ũndũ wakwa.”
english: Blessed is he who finds no occasion for stumbling in me.”

kikuyu: Gwĩtigĩra Jehova-rĩ, kuongagĩrĩra mũndũ matukũ ma gũtũũra muoyo, no mĩaka ya mũndũ mwaganu nĩkũnyiihio ĩnyiihagio.
english: The fear of Yahweh prolongs days, but the years of the wicked shall be shortened.

kikuyu: Nĩndĩĩtuĩte kĩrimũ, no nĩ inyuĩ mwatũmire njĩke ũguo. Nĩ inyuĩ mwagĩrĩirwo nĩkũngaathĩrĩria, nĩgũkorwo niĩ ti niĩ mũnini mũno kũrĩ “atũmwo acio anene,” o na gũtuĩka niĩ ndĩkĩrĩ kĩndũ gĩtarĩ kĩene.
english: Greet Herodion, my kinsman. Greet them of the household of Narcissus, who are in the Lord.

kikuyu: Namo mahiga manene ma mbura ya mbembe, ma ũritũ wa ta kilo mĩrongo ĩtano o ihiga, makĩgwĩra andũ. Nao makĩruma Ngai nĩ ũndũ wa mũthiro ũcio wa mahiga ma mbura ya mbembe, tondũ mũthiro ũcio warĩ wa kũmakania mũno.
english: Great hailstones, about the weight of a talent,‡ came down out of the sky on people. People blasphemed God because of the plague of the hail, for this plague was exceedingly severe.

kikuyu: O na gũtuĩka haana ta mondo ya rũũa ya ndibei ĩigĩtwo ndogo-inĩ,* ndiriganagĩrwo nĩ kĩrĩra kĩa watho waku.
english: For I have become like a wineskin in the smoke. I don’t forget your statutes.

kikuyu: Nyũmba iria irĩ Jerusalemu, o na cia athamaki a Juda nĩigathaahio o ta gũkũ Tofethi: nyũmba ciothe iria maacinĩire ũbumba igũrũ rĩacio, makĩũcinĩra mbũtũ ciothe cia njata, na magĩitagĩra ngai ingĩ maruta ma indo cia kũnyuuo.’ ”
english: The houses of Jerusalem and the houses of the kings of Judah, which are defiled, will be as the place of Topheth, even all the houses on whose roofs they have burned incense to all the army of the sky and have poured out drink offerings to other gods.” ’ ”

kikuyu: nĩ ũndũ ũcio nĩngũgũũkĩrĩra na njũkĩrĩre tũrũũĩ twaku, na nĩngatũma bũrũri wa Misiri wanangĩke na ũkire ihooru, kuuma Migidoli nginya Aswani, o na nginya mũhaka-inĩ wa Kushi.
english: therefore, behold, I am against you and against your rivers. I will make the land of Egypt an utter waste and desolation, from the tower of Seveneh even to the border of Ethiopia.

kikuyu: Mũndũ-wa-nja mũthaka ũtarĩ ũgereri ahaana ta gĩcũhĩ gĩa thahabu kĩrĩ iniũrũ-inĩ rĩa ngũrwe.
english: Like a gold ring in a pig’s snout, is a beautiful woman who lacks discretion.

kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũingĩhagia ũtonga wake na ũndũ wa gũkombithia mbeeca na gwĩtia uumithio mũkĩru aũigagĩra mũndũ ũngĩ, ũrĩa ũkaiguĩra athĩĩni tha.
english: He who increases his wealth by excessive interest gathers it for one who has pity on the poor.

kikuyu: “ ‘Mwathani Jehova ekuuga ũũ: “ ‘Nĩngagũikĩria wabu wakwa, ndĩ hamwe na kĩrĩndĩ kĩingĩ, nakĩo nĩgĩgakũguucũrũria na wabu ũcio wakwa.
english: if, when he sees the sword come on the land, he blows the trumpet and warns the people,

kikuyu: Ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ na ma, ũrĩa wothe ũtakamũkĩra ũthamaki wa Ngai ta kaana ndakaũtoonya o na atĩa.” Nake akĩoya twana tũu, agĩtũigĩrĩra moko, agĩtũrathima.
english: Immediately all the multitude, when they saw him, were greatly amazed, and running to him, greeted him.

kikuyu: Rĩ na mwanũko wa mĩcaarica, na mũrurumo wa magũrũ ma ngaari, na mbarathi itengʼerete, na ngaari cia ita ikũrũgarũga!
english: Yahweh answered me, “Write the vision, and make it plain on tablets, that he who runs may read it.

kikuyu: Aregi a watho nĩmarikĩire ũragani-inĩ. Niĩ nĩngamaherithia othe.
english: There is cursing, lying, murder, stealing, and committing adultery; they break boundaries, and bloodshed causes bloodshed.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo mĩromo ya mũtumia mũtharia itaataga ũũkĩ, na mĩario yake ĩnyorokete gũkĩra maguta;
english: For the lips of an adulteress drip honey. Her mouth is smoother than oil, TR adds “or shot with an arrow”

kikuyu: Ũtukũ ũcio Ngai nĩakorire Balamu, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Tondũ andũ aya nĩ mokĩte gũgwĩta, thiĩ nao, no wĩke o ũrĩa wiki ngũkwĩra.” Ndigiri ya Balamu
english: and from Bamoth to the valley that is in the field of Moab, to the top of Pisgah, which looks down on the desert.

kikuyu: Mataaro ma Jehova nĩmarũngĩrĩru, nĩ makenagia ngoro. Maathani ma Jehova nĩ macangararu, maheaga maitho ũtheri.
english: Your hand will find out all of your enemies. Your right hand will find out those who hate you.

kikuyu: Amũra Harũni na ariũ ake matungatage marĩ athĩnjĩri-Ngai; mũndũ ũngĩ o wothe ũrĩa ũrĩkuhagĩrĩria harĩa haamũre no nginya ooragwo.” Ningĩ Jehova akĩĩra Musa atĩrĩ,
english: You shall appoint Aaron and his sons, and they shall keep their priesthood, but the stranger who comes near shall be put to death.”

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo Danieli agĩthiĩ kũrĩ Arioku, ũcio mũthamaki aamũrĩte athiĩ akooragithie andũ acio oogĩ a Babuloni, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Ndũkooragithie andũ acio oogĩ a Babuloni. Ndwara kũrĩ mũthamaki, na nĩngũmũtaũrĩra kĩroto gĩake.”
english: Therefore Daniel went in to Arioch, whom the king had appointed to destroy the wise men of Babylon. He went and said this to him: “Don’t destroy the wise men of Babylon. Bring me in before the king, and I will show to the king the interpretation.”

kikuyu: “Nĩ ũndũ ũcio ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ, ũthamaki wa Ngai nĩũkeeherio kũrĩ inyuĩ, ũheo andũ angĩ arĩa magaaciaraga maciaro maguo.
english: “Therefore I tell you, God’s Kingdom will be taken away from you and will be given to a nation producing its fruit.

kikuyu: Gĩkabũ kĩmwe kĩarĩ na ngũyũ njega mũno, ta ngũyũ iria ciambaga kwĩrua; gĩkabũ kĩrĩa kĩngĩ kĩarĩ na ngũyũ njũru mũno, itangĩrĩĩka nĩ ũndũ wa ũrĩa ciathũkĩte.
english: One basket had very good figs, like the figs that are first-ripe; and the other basket had very bad figs, which could not be eaten, they were so bad.

kikuyu: Nake mũtumia ũcio akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Noguo Mwathani, no o na ngui irĩ rungu rwa metha nĩ irĩĩaga rũitĩki rũrĩa rũitagwo nĩ ciana.”
english: But she answered him, “Yes, Lord. Yet even the dogs under the table eat the children’s crumbs.”

kikuyu: “Ririkanai watho wa ndungata yakwa Musa, na irĩra ciakwa cia kũrũmĩrĩrwo, o na mawatho marĩa ndaamũheire kũu Horebu marĩ ma andũ a Isiraeli othe.
english: For they disobeyed your commandments. You gave us as plunder, for captivity, for death, and for a proverb of reproach to all the nations among whom we are dispersed.

kikuyu: Mũhĩrĩga wa Benjamini nĩguo ũgũcookerera. Mũtongoria wa andũ a Benjamini nĩ Abidani mũrũ wa Gideoni.
english: “The tribe of Benjamin: the prince of the children of Benjamin shall be Abidan the son of Gideoni.

kikuyu: Maitĩte thakame ya andũ aku ta maaĩ Jerusalemu guothe, na gũtirĩ mũndũ wa gũthika arĩa makuĩte.
english: Turn us again, God. Cause your face to shine, and we will be saved.

kikuyu: Nacio njiaro cia Dani kũringana na mbarĩ ciao ciarĩ ici: kuuma kũrĩ Shuhamu nĩ mbarĩ ya Ashuhamu. Icio nĩcio ciarĩ mbarĩ ya Dani.
english: These are the sons of Dan after their families: of Shuham, the family of the Shuhamites. These are the families of Dan after their families.

kikuyu: Arata akwa endwa, ti rĩathani rĩerũ ndĩramwandĩkĩra, no nĩ rĩathani rĩrĩa rĩa tene, o rĩrĩa mũtũire narĩo kuuma o kĩambĩrĩria. Rĩathani rĩĩrĩ rĩa tene nĩrĩo ndũmĩrĩri ĩrĩa mũiguĩte. No rĩu nĩ rĩathani rĩerũ ndĩramwandĩkĩra; naguo ũhoro wa ma warĩo wonekaga thĩinĩ wake na thĩinĩ wanyu, tondũ nduma no ĩrathira, naguo ũtheri ũrĩa wa ma nĩũrĩkĩtie kwara.
english: Brothers, I write no new commandment to you, but an old commandment which you had from the beginning. The old commandment is the word which you heard from the beginning.

kikuyu: marĩa maandĩkĩtwo ũũ: “Ũhoro nĩwarĩtio ndũrĩrĩ-inĩ, na Geshemu aroiga ũhoro ũcio nĩ wa ma, atĩ wee na Ayahudi nĩmũrathugunda kũremera mũthamaki mũmũũkĩrĩre, na nĩ ũndũ ũcio nĩmũraaka rũthingo. Ningĩ kũringana na ũhoro ũcio nĩũrenda gũtuĩka mũthamaki wao 7na atĩ nĩ ũthuurĩte anabii a kwanĩrĩra ũhoro ũgũkoniĩ kũu Jerusalemu makiugaga atĩrĩ, ‘Juda kũrĩ na mũthamaki!’ Rĩu-rĩ, ũhoro ũcio nĩũgũkinyĩra mũthamaki; nĩ ũndũ ũcio ũka, twaranĩrie.” Na niĩ ngĩkĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ: “Gũtirĩ ũndũ ta ũcio ũroiga ũrekĩka; nĩ gwĩthugundĩra ũrethugundĩra ũhoro ũcio meciiria-inĩ maku.”
english: 1 Now when it was reported to Sanballat, Tobiah, Geshem the Arabian, and to the rest of our enemies that I had built the wall, and that there was no breach left in it (though even to that time I had not set up the doors in the gates),  “Behold”, from “הִ ͏נֵּה”, means look at, take notice, observe, see, or gaze at. It is often used as an interjection.  A shekel is about

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa tũrumbeta tũu magana matatũ twahuhirwo, Jehova agĩtũma andũ arĩa maarĩ kambĩ- inĩ yothe mahũũrane na hiũ ciao cia njora mũndũ na mũndũ ũrĩa ũngĩ. Nayo mbũtũ ĩyo yothe ĩkĩũrĩra Bethi-Shita ĩrorete Zerera o nginya mũhaka-inĩ wa Abeli-Mehola gũkuhĩ na Tabathu.
english: They blew the three hundred trumpets, and Yahweh set every man’s sword against his fellow and against all the army; and the army fled as far as Beth Shittah toward Zererah, as far as the border of Abel Meholah, by Tabbath.

kikuyu: mũri ũrĩa guoko gwaku kwa ũrĩo kwahaandire, mũriũ ũrĩa wee mwene wĩrereire.
english: The haters of Yahweh would cringe before him, and their punishment would last forever.

kikuyu: akĩrũnda mĩthabibũ yao, na mĩkũyũ, na akiunanga mĩtĩ ya bũrũri wao.
english: He struck their vines and also their fig trees, and shattered the trees of their country.

kikuyu: Kaba indo nini cionekete na njĩra ya ũthingu, gũkĩra uumithio mũnene ũtarĩ wa kĩhooto.
english: A bribe is a precious stone in the eyes of him who gives it; wherever he turns, he prospers.

kikuyu: Tondũ wa wendo mũnene wa Jehova, nĩkĩo tũtaniinĩtwo biũ, nĩgũkorwo tha ciake itirĩ hĩndĩ ithiraga.
english: It is because of Yahweh’s loving kindnesses that we are not consumed, because his mercies don’t fail.

kikuyu: “Ti-itherũ mũthenya wa ituĩro nĩũrooka, naguo ũgaakana ta icua rĩa mwaki. Arĩa othe etĩĩi, na arĩa aaganu, magaatuĩka maragara ma mahuti, naguo mũthenya ũcio wakinya magaacinwo, maniinwo biũ. Gũtirĩ mũri kana rũhonge rwao rũgaatigara,” ũguo nĩguo Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe ekuuga.
english: The book of the words of Tobit, the son of Tobiel, the son of Ananiel, the son of Aduel, the son of Gabael, of the seed of Asiel, of the tribe of Naphtali; or, plunder it.

kikuyu: na icicio cia kwĩrora, na nguo cia gatani ĩrĩa njega, na itambaya, na macuka ma ciande.
english: the hand mirrors, the fine linen garments, the tiaras, and the shawls.

kikuyu: na ndaũragĩre andũ othe ũhoro wa hitho ĩno, ĩrĩa ĩtũire ĩhithĩtwo kuuma o tene, thĩinĩ wa Ngai o we wombire indo ciothe.
english: and to make all men see what is the administration† of the mystery which for ages has been hidden in God, who created all things through Jesus Christ,

kikuyu: Ũhoro ũcio wothe wekĩkire ihinda-inĩ rĩa ta mĩaka magana mana ma mĩrongo ĩtano. “Thuutha wa ũguo, Ngai akĩmahe atiirĩrĩri bũrũri o nginya hĩndĩ ya Samũeli ũrĩa warĩ mũnabii.
english: But as the disciples stood around him, he rose up, and entered into the city. On the next day he went out with Barnabas to Derbe.

kikuyu: Nĩwonete maũndũ maingĩ, no ndũmarũmbũyagia; matũ maku nĩmahingũrĩtwo, no ndũrĩ ũndũ ũiguaga.”
english: You see many things, but don’t observe. His ears are open, but he doesn’t listen.

kikuyu: Moima Pi-Hahirothu, magĩtuĩkanĩria iria-inĩ gatagatĩ, magĩkinya werũ-inĩ, na maarĩkia gũthiĩ rũgendo rwa mĩthenya ĩtatũ werũ-inĩ ũcio wa Ethamu, makĩamba hema ciao Mara.
english: They traveled from before Hahiroth, and crossed through the middle of the sea into the wilderness. They went three days’ journey in the wilderness of Etham, and encamped in Marah.

kikuyu: Akĩmeera ũrĩa wothe kwahanĩte agĩcooka akĩmatũma Jopa. Kĩoneki gĩa Petero
english: Having explained everything to them, he sent them to Joppa.

kikuyu: “ ‘Ningĩ andũ a Javani, na Tubali, na Mesheki nĩmoonjorithanagia nawe; maakũũranagia indo ciaku na ngombo, na indo ciao cia gĩcango.
english: “ ‘ “Javan, Tubal, and Meshech were your traders. They traded the persons of men and vessels of bronze for your merchandise.

kikuyu: ngĩona kĩmwana gatagatĩ ka arĩa matarĩ oogĩ, na ngĩkũũrana gatagatĩ ka aanake, mũndũ mwĩthĩ ũtaarĩ na ũũgĩ wa gũkũũrana maũndũ.
english: I saw among the simple ones. I discerned among the youths a young man void of understanding,

kikuyu: Thigari cia mũcaara iria irĩ kuo ihaana ta njaũ cia gĩcegũ. O nao nĩmakagarũrũka more marĩ hamwe, matikahota gwĩtiiria, nĩgũkorwo mũthenya wa mwanangĩko nĩũkamakorerera, na nĩrĩo ihinda rĩao rĩa kũherithio.
english: Also her hired men in the middle of her are like calves of the stall, for they also are turned back. They have fled away together. They didn’t stand, for the day of their calamity has come on them, the time of their visitation.

kikuyu: Ũcooke wakĩre Jehova Ngai waku kĩgongona kĩrĩa kĩagĩrĩire hau igũrũ rĩa harĩa hambatĩru. Hũthĩra ngũ cia gĩtugĩ kĩa Ashera kĩrĩa ũtemangire, ũndutĩre ndegwa ĩyo ya keerĩ ĩtuĩke igongona rĩa njino.” Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Gideoni akĩoya ndungata ciake ikũmi, agĩĩka o ta ũrĩa Jehova aamwĩrĩte. No rĩrĩ, tondũ nĩetigagĩra andũ a nyũmba yao na andũ a itũũra rĩu-rĩ, eekire maũndũ macio ũtukũ handũ ha mũthenya.
english: Then build an altar to Yahweh your God on the top of this stronghold, in an orderly way, and take the second bull, and offer a burnt offering with the wood of the Asherah which you shall cut down.”

kikuyu: Jehova akĩnjĩĩra atĩrĩ, “Mũrũ wa mũndũ, aya nĩo arũme arĩa marathugunda waganu, na makaheana kĩrĩra kĩa waganu itũũra-inĩ rĩĩrĩ inene.
english: He said to me, “Son of man, these are the men who devise iniquity, and who give wicked counsel in this city;

kikuyu: Inyuĩ arĩa mũgarũraga kĩhooto gĩgatuĩka ũrũrũ, na mũkarangĩrĩria ũthingu thĩ
english: Therefore they will now go captive with the first who go captive. The feasting and lounging will end.

kikuyu: atĩ kũrũũhia kwa arĩa aaganu no gwa kahinda, o nakĩo gĩkeno kĩa andũ arĩa matooĩ Ngai no gĩa kahinda kanini.
english: that the triumphing of the wicked is short, the joy of the godless but for a moment?

kikuyu: mĩhaka ya Akaanani yoimĩte Sidoni ĩrorete Gerari ĩgakinya Gaza, ningĩ ĩkarora Sodomu, na Gomora, na Adima, na Zeboimu, o nginya Lasha.
english: The border of the Canaanites was from Sidon—as you go toward Gerar—to Gaza—as you go toward Sodom, Gomorrah, Admah, and Zeboiim—to Lasha.

kikuyu: Andũ akwa, ririkanai ũrĩa Balaki mũthamaki wa Moabi aamũciirĩire, na ũrĩa Balamu mũrũ wa Beori aamũcookeirie. Ririkanai rũgendo rwanyu kuuma Shitimu nginya Giligali, nĩgeetha mũmenye ciĩko cia ũthingu iria Jehova aaneka.” Nĩ kĩĩ ingĩtwara mbere ya Jehova,
english: He will be our peace when Assyria invades our land and when he marches through our fortresses, then we will raise against him seven shepherds, and eight leaders of men.

kikuyu: Ti-itherũ ndũrĩrĩ ihaana ta itata rĩa maaĩ rĩrĩ ndoo-inĩ; cionagwo ihaana ta karũkũngũ kanini karĩa gatigagwo ratiri-inĩ; we athimaga icigĩrĩra cia iria-inĩ taarĩ rũkũngũ rũhinyu.
english: For this is the will of God, that by well-doing you should put to silence the ignorance of foolish men.

kikuyu: Ĩno nĩyo ndũmĩrĩri ĩrĩa Jehova aaheire Jeremia ũcio mũnabii ĩkoniĩ gũũka kwa Nebukadinezaru mũthamaki wa Babuloni, gũtharĩkĩra bũrũri wa Misiri:
english: The word that Yahweh spoke to Jeremiah the prophet, how that Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon should come and strike the land of Egypt:

kikuyu: Mũndũ mũkĩĩgu aitũrũraga marakara make mothe, no mũndũ mũũgĩ aikaraga amarigĩrĩirie, akahoorera.
english: There is a generation that curses their father, and doesn’t bless their mother.

kikuyu: Niĩ nĩ niĩ Jehova Ngai wanyu; mwĩamũrei na mwĩtherie, tondũ niĩ ndĩ mũtheru. Mũtikanethaahie na kĩũmbe o gĩothe kĩrĩa gĩtambaga thĩ.
english: For I am Yahweh your God. Sanctify yourselves therefore, and be holy; for I am holy. You shall not defile yourselves with any kind of creeping thing that moves on the earth.

kikuyu: hamwe na athamaki na aheani kĩrĩra a gũkũ thĩ arĩa meeyakĩire kũndũ kũrĩa kwanangĩku rĩngĩ,
english: fear came on me, and trembling, which made all my bones shake.

kikuyu: Bũrũri ũrĩa niĩ ndaheire Iburahĩmu na Isaaka, o nawe nĩngakũhe guo, na nĩngaheana bũrũri ũyũ kũrĩ njiaro ciaku iria igooka thuutha waku.”
english: Timna was concubine to Eliphaz, Esau’s son; and she bore to Eliphaz Amalek. These are the descendants of Adah, Esau’s wife.

kikuyu: Nyama ciothe cia igongona rĩu ĩngĩtigara ĩkinyie mũthenya wa gatatũ-rĩ, no nginya ĩcinwo na mwaki.
english: But the bull, and its skin, and its meat, and its dung, he burned with fire outside the camp, as Yahweh commanded Moses.

kikuyu: Mũthenya ũyũ ũngĩ rũciinĩ tene andũ magĩaka kĩgongona na makĩruta maruta ma njino o na maruta ma ũiguano.
english: The Levite, the husband of the woman who was murdered, answered, “I came into Gibeah that belongs to Benjamin, I and my concubine, to spend the night.

kikuyu: Agĩcooka akĩgĩa nda rĩngĩ, agĩciara kahĩĩ kangĩ, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Ihinda rĩĩrĩ nĩ ngũgooca Jehova.” Nĩ ũndũ ũcio agĩgatua Juda. Agĩcooka akĩrũgama kũgĩa ciana.
english: She conceived again, and bore a son. She said, “This time I will praise Yahweh.” Therefore she named him Judah. Then she stopped bearing.

kikuyu: Mawatho marĩa ũheanĩte nĩ ma ũthingu; nĩ ma kwĩhokwo kũna.
english: You have commanded your statutes in righteousness. They are fully trustworthy.

kikuyu: Atũmaga wathani wake ũkinye gũkũ thĩ; kiugo gĩake kĩhanyũkaga narua mũno.
english: He sends out his commandment to the earth. His word runs very swiftly.

kikuyu: Ũũgĩ wa rũhiũ rwake rwa njora nĩũũhũndũrĩte, na ũkaaga kũmũteithĩrĩria mbaara-inĩ.
english: Yes, you turn back the edge of his sword, and haven’t supported him in battle.

kikuyu: “Kũrĩ mũraika wa Kanitha wa Efeso, mwandĩkĩre atĩrĩ: Ici nĩcio ciugo cia ũrĩa ũnyiitĩte njata icio mũgwanja na guoko gwake kwa ũrĩo, ũrĩa ũthiiagĩra mĩtĩ-inĩ ĩyo mũgwanja ya thahabu ya kũigĩrĩra matawa.
english: This is the Revelation of Jesus Christ,† which God gave him to show to his servants the things which must happen soon, which he sent and made known by his angel‡ to his servant, John,

kikuyu: Inyuĩ-rĩ, geragiai gũikarania na mũndũ o wothe na thayũ kũngĩhoteka.
english: If it is possible, as much as it is up to you, be at peace with all men.

kikuyu: Magĩkĩĩra mũtumia ũcio atĩrĩ, “Ithuĩ tũtiretĩkia no ũndũ wa ũrĩa ũratwĩrire; rĩu nĩtwĩiguĩrĩire ithuĩ ene na tũkamenya atĩ mũndũ ũyũ ti-itherũ nĩwe Mũhonokia wa kĩrĩndĩ.” Jesũ Kũhonia Mwana wa Mũnene * 4:18 Ayahudi moigaga atĩ mũtumia nĩangĩateirwo nĩ athuuri eerĩ kana atatũ, no angĩakĩririe hau onekaga arĩ mũndũ mwĩhia biũ. † 4:22 Asamaria maarĩnamaandĩkomamabukuomarĩamatanomaMusa, namatiametĩkĩtie mothe. MeetĩkĩtieMesia, weMũhonokia, akoima kũrĩ Ayahudi.
english: They said to the woman, “Now we believe, not because of your speaking; for we have heard for ourselves, and know that this is indeed the Christ, the Savior of the world.”  “Messiah” (Hebrew) and “Christ” (Greek) both mean “Anointed One”.

kikuyu: Mbeũ nĩkũbutha irabuthĩra tĩĩri-inĩ kũrĩa ihaandĩtwo. Nyũmba cia kũigwo indo nĩnyanange, namo makũmbĩ ma irio makamomorwo, nĩgũkorwo ngano nĩyũmĩte.
english: The seeds rot under their clods. The granaries are laid desolate. The barns are broken down, for the grain has withered.

kikuyu: Nao andũ acio magĩcookia makiuga atĩrĩ, “Kũroaga gũtuĩka ũguo, atĩ tũtiganĩrie Jehova atĩ nĩguo tũtungatĩre ngai ingĩ!
english: The people answered, “Far be it from us that we should forsake Yahweh, to serve other gods;

kikuyu: Mũndũ wa kũngĩ ũrĩa wĩnyiitanĩtie na Jehova ndakanoige atĩrĩ, “Ti-itherũ Jehova nĩakanyamũrania, andigithanie na andũ ake.” O na kana mũndũ ũrĩa mũhakũre arekwo oige atĩrĩ, “Niĩ haana o ta mũtĩ mũũmũ.”
english: Let no foreigner who has joined himself to Yahweh speak, saying, “Yahweh will surely separate me from his people.” Do not let the eunuch say, “Behold, I am a dry tree.”

kikuyu: na gategwa kamwe, na ndũrũme ĩmwe, na gatũrũme kamwe ka mwaka ũmwe, nĩ ũndũ wa iruta rĩa njino;
english: “So they shall put my name on the children of Israel; and I will bless them.”

kikuyu: Guoko gwaku nĩgũkambarario na igũrũ kuonanie ũhootani harĩ thũ ciaku, na amuku aku othe nĩmakaniinwo.
english: Let your hand be lifted up above your adversaries, and let all of your enemies be cut off.

kikuyu: Goocai Ngai, kĩũngano-inĩ kĩrĩa kĩnene; mũgooce Jehova kĩũngano-inĩ gĩa Isiraeli.
english: “Bless God in the congregations, even the Lord in the assembly of Israel!”

kikuyu: na matiacookire kũririkana Jehova Ngai wao, ũrĩa wamahonoketie akamaruta moko-inĩ ma thũ ciao ciothe iria ciarĩ mĩena-inĩ yao yothe.
english: The children of Israel didn’t remember Yahweh their God, who had delivered them out of the hand of all their enemies on every side;

kikuyu: Ĩĩ Jeremia 23:33 ti-itherũ, nĩngũũkĩrĩra anabii acio marathaga mohoro ma irooto cia maheeni,” ũguo nĩguo Jehova ekuuga. “Maheanaga ũhoro wacio, makahĩtithia andũ akwa na maheeni na mwĩtĩĩo wao, no ti niĩ ndĩmatũmĩte kana ngamaatha. Matigunaga andũ aya o na hanini,” ũguo nĩguo Jehova ekuuga. Ndũmĩrĩri Nditũ cia Maheeni, na Anabii a Maheeni
english: Behold, I am against those who prophesy lying dreams,” says Yahweh, “who tell them, and cause my people to err by their lies, and by their vain boasting; yet I didn’t send them or command them. They don’t profit this people at all,” says Yahweh.

kikuyu: na ngoro ĩrĩa ĩthugundaga maũndũ ma waganu, na magũrũ marĩa matengʼeraga gũthiĩ gwĩka ũũru,
english: Come, let’s take our fill of loving until the morning. Let’s solace ourselves with loving.

kikuyu: Mũthenya ũyũ nĩ Jehova ũtũmĩte ũgĩe; nĩtũcanjamũkei, tũkĩũkenere.
english: This is the day that Yahweh has made. We will rejoice and be glad in it!

kikuyu: Atĩrĩrĩ, Wee Ngai thikĩrĩria, na ũtũigue, ũhingũre maitho maku wone ihooru rĩa itũũra inene rĩrĩa rĩĩtanĩtio na Rĩĩtwa rĩaku. Tũtiragũthaitha nĩ ũndũ tũrĩ athingu, no nĩ ũndũ wa tha ciaku nyingĩ.
english: Then one like the appearance of a man touched me again, and he strengthened me.

kikuyu: Mũira mwĩhokeku ndaheenanagia, no mũira wa maheeni aaragia o maheeni.
english: A fool despises his father’s correction, but he who heeds reproof shows prudence.

kikuyu: Wee-rĩ, ũkĩrĩ mũũgĩ gũkĩra Danieli? Anga gũtirĩ hitho ũngĩhithwo?
english: behold, you are wiser than Daniel. There is no secret that is hidden from you.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwomũndũũrĩawothe wĩtũgagĩria nĩakanyiihio, na ũrĩa wothe wĩnyiihagia nĩagatũũgĩrio.”
english: For everyone who exalts himself will be humbled, and whoever humbles himself will be exalted.”

kikuyu: Andũ arĩa magwĩtigĩrĩte maronjookerera, o acio mataũkĩirwo nĩ kĩrĩra gĩaku.
english: Let those who fear you turn to me. They will know your statutes.

kikuyu: Ta thikĩrĩria kĩrĩro kĩa andũ akwa marĩ bũrũri wa kũraya: “Atĩrĩrĩ, githĩ Jehova ndarĩ kũu Zayuni? Mũthamaki wakuo-rĩ, kaĩ atatũũraga kuo?”
english: Behold, the voice of the cry of the daughter of my people from a land that is very far off: “Isn’t Yahweh in Zion? Isn’t her King in her?” “Why have they provoked me to anger with their engraved images, and with foreign idols?”

kikuyu: “Nĩ ũndũ ũcio meere atĩrĩ, ‘Mwathani Jehova ekuuga atĩrĩ: Gũtirĩ kiugo o na kĩmwe gĩakwa gĩgaacooka gũcererwo; ũhoro ũrĩa wothe niĩ ngaaria nĩũkahingio, ũguo nĩguo Mwathani Jehova ekuuga.’ ”
english: “Therefore tell them, ‘The Lord Yahweh says: “None of my words will be deferred any more, but the word which I speak will be performed,” says the Lord Yahweh.’ ”

kikuyu: ũcooke ũcookanĩrĩrie kĩũngano gĩothe kĩa andũ a Isiraeli mũromo-inĩ wa Hema- ya-Gũtũnganwo.”
english: and assemble all the congregation at the door of the Tent of Meeting.”

kikuyu: “ ‘Kũngĩgĩa ngarari-rĩ, athĩnjĩri-Ngai no nginya matuithanagie ciira,* na maũtue kũringana na matuĩro makwa ma ciira. Nĩmarĩmenyagĩrĩra mawatho makwa na kĩrĩra kĩa watho wakwa wa kũrũmĩrĩrwo igũrũ rĩa ciathĩ ciakwa ciothe iria njathane, na maikaragie Thabatũ ciakwa irĩ theru.
english: “ ‘ “In a controversy they shall stand to judge. They shall judge it according to my ordinances. They shall keep my laws and my statutes in all my appointed feasts. They shall make my Sabbaths holy.

kikuyu: Mũtumia wa mũthamaki rĩrĩa aiguire inegene rĩa mũthamaki na andũ acio ake maarĩ igweta agĩtoonya nyũmba ĩyo yarĩ na iruga. Akĩĩra mũthamaki atĩrĩ, “Wee mũthamaki, ũrotũũra nginya tene! Tiga kũmaka o na kana ũtukie gĩthiithi nĩ ũndũ wa ũhoro ũyũ!
english: When Daniel knew that the writing was signed, he went into his house (now his windows were open in his room toward Jerusalem) and he kneeled on his knees three times a day, and prayed, and gave thanks before his God, as he did before.

kikuyu: “Mwathani egũtũũra atũregete nginya tene? Ndarĩ hĩndĩ agacooka gũtũkenera?
english: “Will the Lord reject us forever? Will he be favorable no more?

kikuyu: “ ‘Mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai ndakanethaahie na ũndũ wa gũkuhĩrĩria kĩimba; no rĩrĩ, ũrĩa ũkuĩte angĩkorwo nĩ ithe kana nyina, kana mũriũ kana mwarĩ, kana mũrũ wa nyina kana mwarĩ wa nyina ũtahikĩte-rĩ, hĩndĩ ĩyo no ethaahie.
english: “ ‘ “They shall go in to no dead person to defile themselves; but for father, or for mother, or for son, or for daughter, for brother, or for sister who has had no husband, they may defile themselves.

kikuyu: Jesũ Kũũmĩrĩria Arutwo Ake
english: If you will ask anything in my name, I will do it.

kikuyu: Thikĩrĩriai wega ũhoro wakwa; matũ manyu nĩmakĩigue ũrĩa nguuga.
english: Listen carefully to my speech. Let my declaration be in your ears.

kikuyu: “ ‘Mwathani Jehova ekuuga ũũ: “ ‘Nĩnganiina kĩrĩndĩ kĩa Misiri, hũthĩrĩte guoko kwa Nebukadinezaru, mũthamaki wa Babuloni.
english: “Therefore thus said the Lord Yahweh: ‘Because he is exalted in stature, and he has set his top among the thick branches, and his heart is lifted up in his height,

kikuyu: Tiga kwenda toro, ndũkae gũthĩĩna; ikaraga wĩiguĩte, na nĩũrĩrĩĩaga irio ũkahũũna.
english: Don’t love sleep, lest you come to poverty. Open your eyes, and you shall be satisfied with bread.

kikuyu: na Shefatia, na Hatili, na Pokerethu-Hazebaimu, na Amoni.
english: the children of Shephatiah, the children of Hattil, the children of Pochereth Hazzebaim, and the children of Amon.

kikuyu: “ ‘No rĩrĩ, mũgeni kana mũndũ ũrĩa ũrarĩrĩire kwanyu angĩtonga arĩ gatagatĩ kanyu, nake mũndũ wa bũrũri wanyu athĩĩne na eyendie kũrĩ mũgeni ũrĩa ũtũũranĩtie na inyuĩ, kana kũrĩ mũndũ wa mũhĩrĩga wa mũndũ ũcio mũgeni-rĩ,
english: “ ‘If an alien or temporary resident with you becomes rich, and your brother beside him has grown poor, and sells himself to the stranger or foreigner living among you, or to a member of the stranger’s family,

kikuyu: Petero akĩmũrũmĩrĩra arĩ haraaya o nginya agĩkinya nja ya mũciĩ wa mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai ũcio mũnene. Agĩikarania na thigari kũu agĩota mwaki. Na rĩrĩ, athĩnjĩri-Ngai arĩa anene, o na Kĩama kĩu gĩothe,† magĩcaria ũira wa kũũragithia Jesũ, no mationire. Na andũ aingĩ nĩmarutire ũira wa maheeni, no ũira wao ũkĩaga kũiguana.
english: Peter had followed him from a distance, until he came into the court of the high priest. He was sitting with the officers, and warming himself in the light of the fire.

kikuyu: Mwamenyire ũhoro ũcio kuuma kũrĩ Epafara, ũrĩa twendete, na mũruti wĩra hamwe na ithuĩ o we mũtungatĩri wa Kristũ wa kwĩhokeka ithenya riitũ,
english: rooted and built up in him and established in the faith, even as you were taught, abounding in it in thanksgiving.

kikuyu: Ngoro yakwa ndũkanatoonye ndundu-inĩ yao, ndũkananyiitane na kĩũngano kĩao, nĩgũkorwo nĩmooragĩte andũ marakarĩte, na magatemanga ndegwa magũrũ macithuithie o ũrĩa mangĩendire.
english: My soul, don’t come into their council. My glory, don’t be united to their assembly; for in their anger they killed men. In their self-will they hamstrung cattle.

kikuyu: Matirĩ ũndũ mooĩ, na matirĩ ũndũ mamenyaga; maitho mao nĩmahumbĩre, nĩ ũndũ ũcio matingĩona, na meciiria mao nĩmahingĩtwo makaaga kũmenya maũndũ.
english: They don’t know, neither do they consider, for he has shut their eyes, that they can’t see, and their hearts, that they can’t understand.

kikuyu: Gĩkĩ nĩkĩo gĩcunjĩ kĩa maruta marĩa maarutagĩrwo Jehova macinĩtwo na mwaki, kĩrĩa kĩa- gaĩirwo Harũni na ariũ ake mũthenya ũrĩa maamũrirwo matungatagĩre Jehova marĩ athĩnjĩri-Ngai.
english: This is the consecrated portion of Aaron, and the consecrated portion of his sons, out of the offerings of Yahweh made by fire, in the day when he presented them to minister to Yahweh in the priest’s office;

kikuyu: Nguraro cia gũtihio nĩ mũrata no ciĩhokwo, no imumunyano nyingĩ cia mũgũthũũri ti cia kwĩhokwo.
english: Better is the poor who walks in his integrity than he who is perverse in his ways, and he is rich.

kikuyu: Ithuĩ ithuothe tũrĩ ariũ a mũthuuri ũmwe. Ithuĩ ndungata ciaku tũrĩ andũ ehokeku, tũtirĩ athigaani.” Nake Jusufu akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Aca! Mũũkĩte kũrora bũrũri witũ kũrĩa ũtarĩ mũgitĩre.” No makĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Ndungata ciaku ciarĩ andũ ikũmi na eerĩ, ariũ a mũthuuri ũmwe, ũrĩa ũtũũraga bũrũri wa Kaanani. Ũrĩa mũnini biũ nĩwe ũrĩ na baba rĩu, na ũmwe witũ ndarĩ ho.”
english: Their father, Israel, said to them, “If it must be so, then do this: Take from the choice fruits of the land in your bags, and carry down a present for the man, a little balm, a little honey, spices and myrrh, nuts, and almonds;

kikuyu: Nĩagatũma maũndũ ma maheeni magaacĩre, na nĩakeyona arĩ mũnene gũkĩra andũ arĩa angĩ. Rĩrĩa makaigua ta kũrĩ thayũ nĩguo nake akooragithia andũ aingĩ, o na okĩrĩre Mũnene wa anene. No rĩrĩ, nĩakanangwo, no ti hinya wa mũndũ ũkaamwananga.
english: He will speak words against the Most High, and will wear out the saints of the Most High. He will plan to change the times and the law; and they will be given into his hand until a time and times and half a time.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩo ĩyo agĩũka harĩo, agĩcookeria Ngai ngaatho, na akĩaria ũhoro wa kaana kau kũrĩ arĩa othe meetagĩrĩra kuona gũkũũrwo kwa Jerusalemu. Rĩrĩa Jusufu na Mariamu maarĩkirie gwĩka maũndũ marĩa mothe maathanĩtwo nĩ watho wa Mwathani-rĩ, magĩcooka Galili, makĩinũka itũũra rĩao kũu Nazarethi.
english: Coming up at that very hour, she gave thanks to the Lord, and spoke of him to all those who were looking for redemption in Jerusalem.

kikuyu: Makĩoneka-rĩ, mothe mana nĩmahaanaine; o kũgũrũ kuonekaga ta gũtoonyanĩte na kũgũrũ kũngĩ.
english: 1 Then I looked, and see, in the expanse that was over the head of the cherubim there appeared above them as it were a sapphire† stone, as the appearance of the likeness of a throne.

kikuyu: Ũguo nĩguo gwathiire na mbere gũikara; itu rĩkamĩhumbĩra ũtukũ na rĩkoonagwo rĩhaana ta mwaki.
english: So it was continually. The cloud covered it, and the appearance of fire by night.

kikuyu: Agĩcooka akĩinamĩrĩra rĩngĩ, akĩandĩka thĩ na kĩara. Rĩrĩa maiguire ũguo, makĩambĩrĩria kwehera hau ũmwe kwa ũmwe, maambĩrĩirie na arĩa akũrũ, nginya hagĩtigara o Jesũ na mũtumia ũcio arũngiĩ o hau.
english: Therefore the neighbors and those who saw that he was blind before said, “Isn’t this he who sat and begged?”

kikuyu: “Ũyũ nĩwe mũndũ ũrĩa wagire gũtua Ngai kĩĩhitho gĩake kĩa hinya, no eehokire ũtonga wake mũingĩ, na akĩgĩa hinya nĩ ũndũ wa kũniina andũ arĩa angĩ!” No rĩrĩ, niĩ ndariĩ ta mũtĩ wa mũtamaiyũ, ũrĩa ũrakũra wega ũrĩ thĩinĩ wa nyũmba ya Ngai; ndĩhokete wendo wa Ngai ũrĩa ũtathiraga nginya tene na tene.
english: “Behold, this is the man who didn’t make God his strength, but trusted in the abundance of his riches, and strengthened himself in his wickedness.”

kikuyu: “Gũtirĩ mũndũ ũũĩ ũhoro wa mũthenya ũcio kana ithaa rĩu, o na araika a igũrũ matiũĩ, o na Mũriũ ndooĩ, tiga no Ithe wiki.
english: “But no one knows of that day and hour, not even the angels of heaven,‡ but my Father only.

kikuyu: nĩngahonokia rũũru rwakwa, naruo rũtigaacooka gũtahwo rĩngĩ. Nĩngatuithania ciira gatagatĩ ka ngʼondu ĩmwe na ĩrĩa ĩngĩ.
english: therefore I will save my flock, and they will no more be a prey. I will judge between sheep and sheep.

kikuyu: Niĩ ndaarĩ na ũkũrũ wa mĩaka mĩrongo ĩna hĩndĩ ĩrĩa Musa ndungata ya Jehova aandũmire kuuma Kadeshi-Barinea thiĩ ngathigaane bũrũri ũcio. Nĩndamũcookeirie ũhoro o ta ũrĩa warĩ ngoro-inĩ yakwa,
english: The border went up to Debir from the valley of Achor, and so northward, looking toward Gilgal, that faces the ascent of Adummim, which is on the south side of the river. The border passed along to the waters of En Shemesh, and ended at En Rogel.

kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũrĩmaga mũgũnda wake arĩĩkoragwo na irio nyingĩ, no ũrĩa ũhanyũkanagia na maũndũ hatarĩ arĩhũũnaga o thĩĩna.
english: A servant can’t be corrected by words. Though he understands, yet he will not respond.

kikuyu: “Ũkĩra, ũthiĩ itũũra rĩrĩa inene rĩa Nineve, na ũrĩhunjĩrie ũhoro ũrĩa ngũkwĩra.” Nake Jona agĩathĩkĩra ndũmĩrĩri ya Jehova, agĩthiĩ Nineve. Na rĩrĩ, Nineve rĩarĩ itũũra inene na rĩa bata mũno. Kũrĩthiũrũrũka rĩothe kũngĩaninire mĩthenya ĩtatũ.
english: He prayed to Yahweh, and said, “Please, Yahweh, wasn’t this what I said when I was still in my own country? Therefore I hurried to flee to Tarshish, for I knew that you are a gracious God and merciful, slow to anger, and abundant in loving kindness, and you relent of doing harm.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo ũrĩa wothe wendaga kũhonokia muoyo wake nĩ kũũrwo akoorwo nĩguo, no ũrĩa wothe ũkoorwo nĩ muoyo wake nĩ ũndũ wakwa na nĩ ũndũ wa Ũhoro-ũrĩa-Mwega, nĩakaũhonokia.
english: For whoever wants to save his life will lose it; and whoever will lose his life for my sake and the sake of the Good News will save it.

kikuyu: Wakĩagire kũiga mbeeca ciakwa bengi nĩkĩ, nĩgeetha rĩrĩa ngaacooka ndĩcioye irĩ na uumithio?’
english: Then why didn’t you deposit my money in the bank, and at my coming, I might have earned interest on it?’

kikuyu: Ta rora wone ũrĩa andũ arĩa mekaga ũũru magwĩte thĩ; maikanĩtio thĩ, na matingĩhota gũũkĩra!
english: The wicked plots against the just, and gnashes at him with his teeth.

kikuyu: Ĩtũũraga kĩharũrũka-inĩ kĩa ihiga, na nĩ ho ĩraaraga; rũhĩa-inĩ rwa ihiga iraaya mũno nĩ ho mwĩgitio wayo.
english: On the cliff he dwells and makes his home, on the point of the cliff and the stronghold.

kikuyu: Wee Ngai, o na ndakũra ngĩe mbuĩ, ndũkanandiganĩrie, o nginya nyumbũre ũhoro wa ũhoti waku kũrĩ rũciaro rũrĩa rũrooka, na nyumbũre ũhoro wa hinya waku kũrĩ arĩa othe magooka thuutha.
english: Praise be to Yahweh God, the God of Israel, who alone does marvelous deeds.

kikuyu: Mũtikanareke ngoro cianyu ciũrwo nĩ hinya, kana mwĩtigĩre hĩndĩ ĩrĩa ndeto cia mũhuhu ikaiguuo bũrũri-inĩ; ũhoro ũmwe wa mũhuhu nĩũkaiguuo mwaka ũyũ, na ũngĩ ũiguuo mwaka ũcio ũngĩ ũrũmĩrĩire, mũhuhu ũkoniĩ ndeto cia haaro bũrũri-inĩ, na wa mwathani ũmwe agĩũkĩrĩra mwathani ũrĩa ũngĩ.
english: Don’t let your heart faint. Don’t fear for the news that will be heard in the land. For news will come one year, and after that in another year news will come, and violence in the land, ruler against ruler.

kikuyu: Mawĩra ma moko make nĩ ma kwĩhokeka, na nĩ ma kĩhooto; mataaro make mothe nĩ ma kwĩhokwo. Nĩ marũmu nginya tene na tene, maheanĩtwo na wĩhokeku na ũrũngĩrĩru.
english: The day of unleavened bread came, on which the Passover must be sacrificed.

kikuyu: Ngĩkiuga atĩrĩ, “Niĩ ndikona Jehova rĩngĩ, ndikona Jehova ndĩ bũrũri ũyũ wa arĩa matũũraga muoyo; gũtirĩ hĩndĩ ĩngĩ ngona mũndũ wa thĩ, kana ngorwo hamwe na arĩa marĩ gũkũ thĩ.
english: I said, “I won’t see Yah, Yah in the land of the living. I will see man no more with the inhabitants of the world.

kikuyu: Ndarĩ mũndũ o na ũmwe eetĩkĩririe amanyariire; nĩ ũndũ wao akĩrũithia athamaki, akĩmeera atĩrĩ:
english: He allowed no one to do them wrong. Yes, he reproved kings for their sakes,

kikuyu: arĩ rĩo ihinda rĩrĩa Ayahudi meeyũkĩrĩirie kũrĩ thũ ciao, o na arĩ guo mweri ũrĩa kĩeha kĩao gĩatuĩkire gĩkeno, namo macakaya mao magĩtuĩka mũthenya wa gũkũngũiya. Aamandĩkĩire marũmagie mĩthenya ĩyo ĩrĩ mĩthenya ya ndĩa na ya gĩkeno, na ya kũheana iheo cia irio harĩ mũndũ na ũrĩa ũngĩ, na ya kũhe athĩĩni iheo. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Ayahudi magĩtĩkĩra gũthiĩ na mbere na gũkũngũĩra ũguo maambĩrĩirie, magekaga ũrĩa Moridekai aamandĩkĩire.
english: as the days in which the Jews had rest from their enemies, and the month which was turned to them from sorrow to gladness, and from mourning into a holiday; that they should make them days of feasting and gladness, and of sending presents of food to one another, and gifts to the needy.

kikuyu: “Makenagia mũthamaki na waganu wao, magakenia anene na maheeni mao.
english: They make the king glad with their wickedness, and the princes with their lies.

kikuyu: Nĩarĩtũmaga ũthingu waku ware o ta rũciinĩ, nakĩo kĩhooto gĩa ciira waku kĩare o ta riũa rĩa mũthenya barigici.
english: he will make your righteousness shine out like light, and your justice as the noon day sun.

kikuyu: Nĩmeĩkĩrĩire na magĩĩka maũndũ marĩ magigi maitho-inĩ makwa. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio nĩndamaingatire, o ta ũguo muonete.
english: They were arrogant and committed abomination before me. Therefore I took them away when I saw it.

kikuyu: Johana ehumbaga nguo ciathondeketwo na guoya wa ngamĩĩra, na nĩehotoraga mũcibi wa rũũa njohero, na aarĩĩaga ngigĩ na ũũkĩ wa gĩthaka.
english: But there were some of the scribes sitting there and reasoning in their hearts,

kikuyu: Agĩthiiathiia, agĩturumithia ũthiũ thĩ, akĩhooya, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Baba, kũngĩhoteka-rĩ, njehereria gĩkombe gĩkĩ. No ti ũrĩa niĩ ngwenda, no nĩ ũrĩa wee ũkwenda.”
english: He went forward a little, fell on his face, and prayed, saying, “My Father, if it is possible, let this cup pass away from me; nevertheless, not what I desire, but what you desire.”

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo nĩagaatha araika ake nĩ ũndũ waku, makũgitagĩre mĩthiĩre-inĩ yaku yothe;
english: My eye has also seen my enemies. My ears have heard of the wicked enemies who rise up against me.

kikuyu: “No rĩrĩ, ũmũthĩ ũyũ tũrĩ ngombo, ngombo bũrũri-inĩ ũrĩa waheire maithe maitũ ma tene nĩguo marĩĩage matunda maguo na indo iria ingĩ njega ikũraga kuo.
english: “Behold, we are servants today, and as for the land that you gave to our fathers to eat its fruit and its good, behold, we are servants in it.

kikuyu: Gũthererwo gĩthiithi nĩ mũthamaki nĩ muoyo; ũtugi wake ũtariĩ ta itu rĩa mbura ĩrĩa ĩkũragia irio.*
english: He who justifies the wicked, and he who condemns the righteous, both of them alike are an abomination to Yahweh.

kikuyu: Mũtigacookage rĩngĩ kũhaara mĩgũnda-inĩ yanyu ya mĩthabibũ, o na mũtikahaarage thabibũ iria igũĩte thĩ. Citigagĩriei andũ arĩa athĩĩni na arĩa ageni. Niĩ nĩ niĩ Jehova Ngai wanyu.
english: You shall not glean your vineyard, neither shall you gather the fallen grapes of your vineyard. You shall leave them for the poor and for the foreigner. I am Yahweh your God.

kikuyu: Ũkai na ihenya, inyuĩ ndũrĩrĩ cia kũndũ guothe, mũngane, mwĩcookanĩrĩrie hamwe. Wee Jehova, tũma njamba ciaku cia ita ciikũrũke kuo.
english: Hurry and come, all you surrounding nations, and gather yourselves together.” Cause your mighty ones to come down there, Yahweh.

kikuyu: O rũciinĩ reke njiguage kiugo kĩa wendo waku ũrĩa ũtathiraga, nĩgũkorwo nĩwe ndĩĩhokete. Nyonia njĩra ĩrĩa njagĩrĩirwo nĩkũgera, nĩgũkorwo muoyo wakwa ndĩwerekeirie igũrũ kũrĩ we.
english: Yahweh is gracious, merciful, slow to anger, and of great loving kindness.

kikuyu: Gũtirĩ kĩndũ kĩrĩ nja ya mũndũ kĩngĩmũgwatia ‘thaahu’ nĩ ũndũ wa gũtoonya thĩinĩ wake. No rĩrĩ, kĩrĩa kiumaga thĩinĩ wa mũndũ nĩkĩo kĩmũgwatagia ‘thaahu’.”
english: There is nothing from outside of the man that going into him can defile him; but the things which proceed out of the man are those that defile the man. or, “I AM!”  or, tassel

kikuyu: Kuuma kũrĩ we mwĩrĩ wothe ũnyiitithanagio na ũkohanio hamwe nĩ nga iria iũnyiitithanagia, naguo ũgakũraga na ũkeyakaga guo mwene thĩinĩ wa wendani, o kĩĩga gĩkĩrutaga wĩra wakĩo. Gũtũũra ta Ciana cia Ũtheri Nĩ ũndũ ũcio ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ, na ngamũringĩrĩria thĩinĩ wa Mwathani, atĩ no nginya mũtige gũtũũraga ta ũrĩa andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ matũũraga, marĩ na meciiria ma tũhũ.
english: from whom all the body, being fitted and knit together through that which every joint supplies, according to the working in measure of each individual part, makes the body increase to the building up of itself in love.

kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa wĩkĩte ũguo mũigĩre mũndũ mũũru amũũkĩrĩre; mwena wake wa ũrĩo nĩkũrũgame mũndũ wa kũmũthitanga.
english: He has shown his people the power of his works, in giving them the heritage of the nations.

kikuyu: na Meshezabeli, na Zadoku, na Jadua,
english: Meshezabel, Zadok, Jaddua,

kikuyu: Andũ a bũrũri ũngĩ nĩmagatũũra Ashidodi, na niĩ nĩnganiina mwĩtĩĩo wa Afilisti.
english: Foreigners will dwell in Ashdod, and I will cut off the pride of the Philistines.

kikuyu: Thayũ na kũiguĩrwo tha kũrogĩa na andũ arĩa marũmagĩrĩra wathani ũyũ, o hamwe na andũ a Isiraeli arĩa a Ngai.
english: As many as walk by this rule, peace and mercy be on them, and on God’s Israel.

kikuyu: Ũkai mũthikĩrĩrie inyuothe arĩa mwĩtigĩrĩte Ngai; rekei ndĩmũhe ũhoro wa ũrĩa anjĩkĩire.
english: Come and hear, all you who fear God. I will declare what he has done for my soul.

kikuyu: Kĩyo gĩakwa nĩkĩĩnogetie, nĩgũkorwo thũ ciakwa nĩcitiganĩirie ciugo ciaku.
english: My zeal wears me out, because my enemies ignore your words.

kikuyu: Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe ekuuga ũũ: “O ta ũrĩa ndaatuĩte atĩ nĩngũmũrehere mwanangĩko, na ndiigana kũigua tha rĩrĩa maithe manyu mandakaririe-rĩ,” Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe ekuuga atĩrĩ,
english: For Yahweh of Armies says: “As I thought to do evil to you when your fathers provoked me to wrath,” says Yahweh of Armies, “and I didn’t repent,

kikuyu: Mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai ũrĩa mũitĩrĩrie maguta na akaamũrwo gũcooka ithenya rĩa ithe ta mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai ũrĩa mũnene nĩwe ũkaahoroheria andũ. Nĩakehumbaga nguo iria nyamũre cia gatani 33na ahoroherie Handũ-harĩa-Hatheru-Mũno, na Hema- ya-Gũtũnganwo, na kĩgongona, na athĩnjĩri-Ngai, na andũ othe a mũingĩ wa Isiraeli.
english: The priest, who is anointed and who is consecrated to be priest in his father’s place, shall make the atonement, and shall put on the linen garments, even the holy garments.

kikuyu: “Na rĩrĩa ndenyũrangĩire andũ arĩa ngiri inya mĩgate ĩrĩa mũgwanja-rĩ, nĩ ciondo ciigana muonganirie ciiyũrĩte cienyũ cia mĩgate?” Nao makĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Ciarĩ ciondo mũgwanja.”
english: “When the seven loaves fed the four thousand, how many baskets full of broken pieces did you take up?” They told him, “Seven.”

kikuyu: “Wĩhotore ta mũndũ mũrũme; nĩngũkũũria ciũria, nawe ũkĩnjookerie.
english: “Now brace yourself like a man. I will question you, and you will answer me.

kikuyu: “Jehova ekuuga atĩrĩ: O ta ũrĩa ndaarehithĩirie andũ aya mathĩĩna manene, ũguo noguo ngaamahe ũgaacĩru ũrĩa wothe ndĩmerĩire.
english: For Yahweh says: “Just as I have brought all this great evil on this people, so I will bring on them all the good that I have promised them.

kikuyu: Mũrathi na mĩguĩ ndakeyũmĩrĩria, na mũthigari ũrĩa ũhiũkaga ndakahota kũũra, na ũrĩa ũhaicĩte mbarathi ndakahonokia muoyo wake.
english: He who handles the bow won’t stand. He who is swift of foot won’t escape. He who rides the horse won’t deliver himself.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa Ayahudi moonire kĩrĩndĩ kĩu, makĩnyiitwo nĩ ũiru na makĩaria ciugo njũru cia gũũkĩrĩra ũrĩa Paũlũ oigaga. Nao Paũlũ na Baranaba makĩmacookeria marĩ na ũcamba makĩmeera atĩrĩ, “No nginya tũngĩram- bire kũheana kiugo kĩa Ngai kũrĩ inyuĩ. No rĩrĩ, kuona nĩmũraũrega na mũgetuĩra atĩ mũtiagĩrĩirwo nĩ muoyo wa tene na tene-rĩ, rĩu nĩtũkũgarũrũka tũthiĩ kũrĩ andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ.
english: But when the Jews saw the multitudes, they were filled with jealousy, and contradicted the things which were spoken by Paul, and blasphemed.  TR, NU read “a Savior, Jesus” instead of “salvation”  TR, NU read “to all the people of Israel” instead of “to Israel”

kikuyu: Nowe Gedalia mũrũ wa Ahikamu akĩĩra Johanani mũrũ wa Karea atĩrĩ, “Ndũkae gwĩka ũndũ ta ũcio! Ũhoro ũcio ũraaria ũkoniĩ Ishumaeli ti wa ma.”
english: So Zedekiah the king swore secretly to Jeremiah, saying, “As Yahweh lives, who made our souls, I will not put you to death, neither will I give you into the hand of these men who seek your life.”

kikuyu: Nĩndĩmũheete bũrũri wa Misiri ũtuĩke kĩheo gĩa kĩyo gĩake, tondũ we na mbũtũ ciake cia ita meekire ũguo nĩ ũndũ wakwa, ũguo nĩguo Mwathani Jehova ekuuga.
english: I have given him the land of Egypt as his payment for which he served, because they worked for me,’ says the Lord Yahweh.

kikuyu: O na ithe, kana nyina, kana mũrũ wa nyina, kana mwarĩ wa nyina angĩkua, ndakanethaahie nĩ ũndũ wao, tondũ arĩ na rũũri rwa kwĩyamũrĩra Ngai mũtwe.
english: He shall not make himself unclean for his father, or for his mother, for his brother, or for his sister, when they die, because his separation to God† is on his head.

kikuyu: Nĩngakũhe igĩĩna iria ihithĩtwo nduma-inĩ, ngũhe ũtonga ũrĩa mũhithe,
english: I will give you the treasures of darkness and hidden riches of secret places, that you may know that it is I, Yahweh, who calls you by your name, even the God of Israel.

kikuyu: Mũndũ o wothe angĩkaahutia kĩndũ kĩrĩ na thaahu, arĩ thaahu uumĩte harĩ mũndũ, kana nyamũ ĩrĩ na thaahu, kana kĩndũ o ro gĩothe kĩũru biũ kĩrĩ na thaahu, na acooke arĩe nyama o yothe ya iruta rĩu rĩa ũiguano rĩrutĩirwo Jehova-rĩ, mũndũ ũcio no nginya aingatwo kuuma kũrĩ andũ ao.’ ” Kũgirio kũrĩa Maguta na Thakame Ningĩ Jehova akĩĩra Musa atĩrĩ,
english: He washed the innards and the legs with water; and Moses burned the whole ram on the altar. It was a burnt offering for a pleasant aroma. It was an offering made by fire to Yahweh, as Yahweh commanded Moses.

kikuyu: na mahuru mothe,
english: any kind of raven,

kikuyu: O ta ũrĩa igũrũ rĩraihanĩrĩirie na thĩ, ũguo no taguo wendo wake ũrĩ mũnene kũrĩ arĩa mamwĩtigĩrĩte;
english: For as the heavens are high above the earth, so great is his loving kindness toward those who fear him.

kikuyu: Kũgirio Kũrĩa Thakame Ningĩ Jehova akĩĩra Musa atĩrĩ,
english: 1 Yahweh spoke to Moses, saying,

kikuyu: Ndegera gũtũ na ũhiũhe ũũke ũũhatũre; tuĩka rwaro rwakwa rwa ihiga rwa kũũrĩra, ũtuĩke gĩikaro kĩirigĩre na hinya, ũũhonokie.
english: Bow down your ear to me. Deliver me speedily. Be to me a strong rock, a house of defense to save me.

kikuyu: “O na gũtuĩka Efiraimu nĩakĩte igongona nyingĩ cia kũrutagĩrwo maruta ma kũhoroherio mehia, icio ituĩkĩte igongona cia kwĩhagĩria.
english: Because Ephraim has multiplied altars for sinning, they became for him altars for sinning.

kikuyu: Ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ na ma, amwe marũngiĩ haha matigacama gĩkuũ matonete Mũrũ wa Mũndũ agĩũka na ũthamaki wake.”
english: Most certainly I tell you, there are some standing here who will in no way taste of death until they see the Son of Man coming in his Kingdom.”

kikuyu: Nĩnjũũĩ mĩnyamaro yaku na ũthĩĩni waku, no ũrĩ gĩtonga! Nĩnjũũĩ ũrĩa ũrumagwo nĩ arĩa moigaga atĩ nĩ Ayahudi no ti Ayahudi, no nĩ thunagogi ya Shaitani.
english: I John, your brother and partner with you in the oppression, Kingdom, and perseverance in Christ Jesus, was on the isle that is called Patmos because of God’s Word and the testimony of Jesus Christ.

kikuyu: Moko ma mũndũ ũrĩ kĩyo nĩmo magaathana, no kĩgũũta gĩkaarutithio wĩra wa ũkombo.
english: The hands of the diligent ones shall rule, but laziness ends in slave labor.

kikuyu: Mangʼũrĩ ma mũthamaki nĩ ta mũraramo wa mũrũũthi; ũrĩa ũmũrakaragia nĩ muoyo wake ateaga.
english: The terror of a king is like the roaring of a lion.

kikuyu: Tondũ ũcio mũndũ ũrĩa mũbacĩrĩri no gũkira akiraga ki mahinda ta macio, tondũ nĩ mahinda mooru.
english: you who rejoice in a thing of nothing, who say, ‘Haven’t we taken for ourselves horns by our own strength?’

kikuyu: Kũrathimwo-rĩ, nĩ mũndũ ũrĩa ũgũũka na rĩĩtwa rĩa Jehova. Ithuĩ nĩtwamũrathima tũrĩ thĩinĩ wa nyũmba ya Jehova.
english: Blessed is he who comes in Yahweh’s name! We have blessed you out of Yahweh’s house.

kikuyu: kũngĩkorwo andũ a nyũmba yakwa matirĩ moiga atĩrĩ, ‘Nũũ ũtarĩ warĩa nyama cia Ayubu akahũũna?’
english: if the men of my tent have not said, ‘Who can find one who has not been filled with his meat?’

kikuyu: Narĩo itũũra rĩrĩa inene rĩgĩatũkana njatũ ithatũ, namo matũũra ma ndũrĩrĩ makĩmomoka. Nake Ngai akĩririkana Babuloni itũũra rĩrĩa inene, na akĩrĩhe gĩkombe kĩaiyũrĩtio ndibei ya ũrũrũ wa mangʼũrĩ make.
english: The great city was divided into three parts, and the cities of the nations fell. Babylon the great was remembered in the sight of God, to give to her the cup of the wine of the fierceness of his wrath.

kikuyu: Mũndũ wa mũhuhu nĩoimagia ndeto cia ndundu nja, no mũndũ mwĩhokeku nĩaigaga hitho.
english: One who brings gossip betrays a confidence, but one who is of a trustworthy spirit is one who keeps a secret.

kikuyu: Ha ũhoro ũkoniĩ Dameski: “Hamathu na Aripadi nĩamaku, nĩ ũndũ nĩmaiguĩte ndeto njũru. Nĩmorĩtwo nĩ hinya, makaagaaga ta maaĩ ma iria.
english: Of Damascus: “Hamath and Arpad are confounded, for they have heard evil news. They have melted away. There is sorrow on the sea. It can’t be quiet.

kikuyu: Marigĩrĩrio-inĩ nĩgũkarahũka ũthamaki ũngĩ wa kana ũrĩ hinya ta kĩgera, nĩgũkorwo kĩgera kiunangaga na gĩkahehenja indo ciothe, na o ta ũrĩa kĩgera kiunangaga indo igatuĩka tũcunjĩ ũguo noguo ũkaahehenja na uunange mothamaki macio mangĩ mothe.
english: The fourth kingdom will be strong as iron, because iron breaks in pieces and subdues all things; and as iron that crushes all these, it will break in pieces and crush.

kikuyu: Ngai mwene thayũ aroikara hamwe na inyuĩ inyuothe. Ameni.
english: Now the God of peace be with you all. Amen.

kikuyu: O ũndũ ũmwe athĩnjĩri-Ngai arĩa anene, na arutani a watho, na athuuri o nao makĩmũnyũrũria.
english: Watch and pray, that you don’t enter into temptation. The spirit indeed is willing, but the flesh is weak.”

kikuyu: Narĩo riiko rĩu rĩa kĩgongona rĩiganaine mĩena yothe ĩna, rĩrĩ na ũraihu wa mĩkono ikũmi na ĩĩrĩ,† na wariĩ wa mĩkono ikũmi na ĩĩrĩ.
english: the thresholds, and the closed windows, and the galleries around on their three stories, opposite the threshold, with wood ceilings all around, and from the ground up to the windows, (now the windows were covered),

kikuyu: “Ithuĩ tũrĩ aira a maũndũ marĩa mothe eekire thĩinĩ wa bũrũri wa Ayahudi o na Jerusalemu. Nĩmamũũragire na njĩra ya kũmwamba mũtĩ-igũrũ. No mũthenya wa gatatũ Ngai akĩmũriũkia kuuma kũrĩ arĩa akuũ na agĩtũma onekane kũrĩ andũ.
english: We are witnesses of everything he did both in the country of the Jews and in Jerusalem; whom they also‡ killed, hanging him on a tree.

kikuyu: “Ngai nĩanduĩte mũndũ wa kuunwo thimo nĩ andũ othe, nduĩkĩte mũndũ wa gũtuagĩrwo mata ũthiũ nĩ andũ.
english: “But he has made me a byword of the people. They spit in my face.

kikuyu: Ndũkanametigĩre, nĩgũkorwo ndĩ hamwe nawe, na nĩngakũhonokia,” ũguo nĩguo Jehova ekuuga.
english: Don’t be afraid because of them, for I am with you to rescue you,” says Yahweh.

kikuyu: ‘Jehova nĩagũtuĩte mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai ithenya rĩa Jehoiada nĩguo ũtuĩke mũrũgamĩrĩri wa nyũmba ya Jehova; wee wagĩrĩirwo nĩkuoherera mũgũrũki o wothe ũretua mũnabii ndungu magũrũ na ũmuoherere igera cia ngingo. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio-rĩ, nĩ kĩĩ kĩgirĩtie wee ũkaanie Jeremia ũcio wa kuuma Anathothu, o ũcio wĩtuaga mũnabii gatagatĩ-inĩ kanyu?
english: “Yahweh has made you priest in the place of Jehoiada the priest, that there may be officers in Yahweh’s house, for every man who is crazy and makes himself a prophet, that you should put him in the stocks and in shackles.

kikuyu: Magaakũherithia marĩ na rũthũũro, na magũtunye indo ciaku ciothe iria ũnogeire. Magaagũtiga njaga, ũrĩ ũtheri, nacio thoni cia ũmaraya waku nĩikaguũrio. Naguo ũũra-thoni na ũmaraya waku.
english: They will deal with you in hatred, and will take away all your labor, and will leave you naked and bare. The nakedness of your prostitution will be uncovered, both your lewdness and your prostitution.

kikuyu: Tondũ ũcio nĩngũũranĩte atĩ ti niĩ mwene njĩkaga maũndũ macio, no nĩ wĩhia ũrĩa ũtũũraga
english: For if by the trespass of the one, death reigned through the one; so much more will those who receive the abundance of grace and of the gift of righteousness reign in life through the one, Jesus Christ.

kikuyu: Ciana iroina ngũ, namo maithe mao magaakia mwaki, nao atumia magakanda mũtu, makarugĩra ngai ĩrĩa ĩĩtagwo Mũthamaki-Mũndũ-wa-nja wa kũũrĩa Igũrũ tũmĩgate, na maitĩre ngai ingĩ maruta ma indo cia kũnyuuo nĩguo maathirĩkie ndaakare.
english: The children gather wood, and the fathers kindle the fire, and the women knead the dough, to make cakes to the queen of the sky, and to pour out drink offerings to other gods, that they may provoke me to anger.

kikuyu: We na mbũtũ ciake cia ita, o mbũtũ icio ikĩrĩtie ndũrĩrĩ iria ingĩ ciothe kwaga tha, nĩcio ikaarehwo, iniine bũrũri ũcio. Nĩigacomora hiũ ciao cia njora, ciũkĩrĩre bũrũri wa Misiri na ciyũrie bũrũri ũcio andũ arĩa moragĩtwo.
english: I will deliver him into the hand of the mighty one of the nations. He will surely deal with him. I have driven him out for his wickedness.

kikuyu: Angĩkorwo aametire ‘ngai’, o acio kiugo kĩa Ngai gĩokire kũrĩ o, (na Maandĩko matheru matingĩaga kũhingio)
english: If he called them gods, to whom the word of God came (and the Scripture can’t be broken),

kikuyu: Riitho rĩaku nĩrĩo tawa wa mwĩrĩ waku. Rĩrĩa maitho maku marĩ mega, mwĩrĩ waku wothe o naguo ũkoragwo ũiyũrĩte ũtheri. No rĩrĩa matarĩ mega-rĩ, mwĩrĩ waku o naguo ũkoragwo ũiyũrĩte nduma.
english: The lamp of the body is the eye. Therefore when your eye is good, your whole body is also full of light; but when it is evil, your body also is full of darkness.

kikuyu: Ndiiragĩrĩra, na nĩngũhonokio; ngũtũũra ndĩĩte uuge waku.
english: Hold me up, and I will be safe, and will have respect for your statutes continually.

kikuyu: Nĩatũmĩte kũgĩe thayũ mĩhaka-inĩ yaku, na agakũhũũnia na ngano ĩrĩa njega mũno.
english: He has lifted up the horn of his people, the praise of all his saints, even of the children of Israel, a people near to him. Praise Yah!

kikuyu: Handũ ha ũguo-rĩ, ngũhe Alawii icunjĩ cia ikũmi iria andũ a Isiraeli marehaga irĩ maruta kũrĩ Jehova ituĩke igai rĩao. Nĩkĩo ndoigire igũrũ rĩao atĩrĩ: ‘Matikagĩa na igai thĩinĩ wa andũ a Isiraeli.’ ”
english: For the tithe of the children of Israel, which they offer as a wave offering to Yahweh, I have given to the Levites for an inheritance. Therefore I have said to them, ‘Among the children of Israel they shall have no inheritance.’ ”

kikuyu: Nĩngahumba athĩnjĩri-Ngai akuo ũhonokio, na andũ akuo arĩa aamũre matũũre mainaga nĩ gũkena.
english: They have mouths, but they can’t speak. They have eyes, but they can’t see.

kikuyu: Nĩndĩraigua kĩrĩro ta kĩa mũndũ-wa-nja akĩrũmwo, arĩ na mũcaayo ta wa mũtumia agĩciara mwana wake wa irigithathi: nĩ kĩrĩro kĩa Mwarĩ wa Zayuni agĩteeha, atambũrũkĩtie moko make, akiugaga atĩrĩ, “Wũi-ĩ-iya-wakwa-ĩ! Nĩndĩraringĩka; muoyo wakwa nĩmũneane kũrĩ oragani.”
english: For I have heard a voice as of a woman in travail, the anguish as of her who gives birth to her first child, the voice of the daughter of Zion, who gasps for breath, who spreads her hands, saying, “Woe is me now! For my soul faints before the murderers.”

kikuyu: “ ‘O na angĩaga gũkũũrwo na njĩra ĩmwe ya icio, we na ciana ciake no nginya makaarekererio Mwaka wa Jubilii wakinya, 55nĩgũkorwo andũ a Isiraeli nĩ ndungata ciakwa. Acio nĩ ndungata ciakwa, iria ndaarutire bũrũri wa Misiri. Niĩ nĩ niĩ Jehova Ngai wanyu.
english: If he isn’t redeemed by these means, then he shall be released in the Year of Jubilee: he and his children with him.

kikuyu: Ndũrĩrĩ ciothe nĩikamũtungatĩra we, na mũriũ, o na mwana wa mũriũ, nginya rĩrĩa ihinda rĩa bũrũri wake rĩgaakinya; hĩndĩ ĩyo ndũrĩrĩ nyingĩ na athamaki anene nĩmakamũtua wa kũmatungatagĩra arĩ o.
english: The priests and the prophets and all the people heard Jeremiah speaking these words in Yahweh’s house.

kikuyu: Tha ciaku ikoragwo irĩ njerũ rũciinĩ o rũciinĩ, wĩhokeku waku nĩ mũnene mũno.
english: They are new every morning. Great is your faithfulness.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa mũrĩmi ekũrĩma nĩguo ahaande-rĩ, atũũraga o arĩmaga? Atũũraga aciimbaga na akĩhũũraga harũ?
english: Then the end comes, when he will deliver up the Kingdom to God the Father,

kikuyu: Mũndũ wa mũhuhu nĩoimagia ndeto cia ndundu nja, nĩ ũndũ ũcio wĩthemage mũndũ waragia mũno.
english: He who goes about as a tale-bearer reveals secrets; therefore don’t keep company with him who opens wide his lips.

kikuyu: arĩa maamanyariirire na makĩmahinyĩrĩria mwaka-inĩ ũcio. Nao makĩhinyĩrĩria andũ a Isiraeli othe arĩa maarĩ mwena wa irathĩro wa Rũũĩ rwa Jorodani kũu Gileadi, bũrũri wa Aamori, mĩaka ikũmi na ĩnana. Ningĩ Aamoni makĩringa Rũũĩ rwa Jorodani nĩguo makarũe na andũ a Juda, na andũ a Benjamini, o na nyũmba ya Efiraimu; nakuo Isiraeli gũkĩgĩa na kĩnyariirĩko kĩnene. Ningĩ andũ a Isiraeli magĩkaĩra Jehova makĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Nĩtwĩhĩtie, tũgatiganĩria Ngai witũ, tũgatungatĩra Mabaali.”
english: They troubled and oppressed the children of Israel that year. For eighteen years they oppressed all the children of Israel that were beyond the Jordan in the land of the Amorites, which is in Gilead.

kikuyu: “Naarĩ korwo ciugo ciakwa nĩciandĩkĩtwo, igakĩandĩkwo ibuku-inĩ rĩa gĩkũnjo,
english: “Oh that my words were now written! Oh that they were inscribed in a book!

kikuyu: * 7:86 nĩ ta kilo ĩmwe na nuthu (1.5)
english: one male goat for a sin offering;

kikuyu: Nyoniai mbeeca ĩrĩa ĩhũthagĩrwo ya kũrĩha igooti.” Nao makĩmũtwarĩra dinari,
english: Show me the tax money.” They brought to him a denarius.

kikuyu: 1 “Mũndũ ũciarĩtwo nĩ mũndũ-wa-nja-rĩ, aikaraga o matukũ manini, namo makaiyũrwo nĩ mathĩĩna.
english: If a man dies, will he live again? I would wait all the days of my warfare, until my release should come.

kikuyu: Mĩtĩ ya mũgũnda nĩĩgaciaraga maciaro mayo, nayo mĩgũnda ĩgĩe na magetha mayo; andũ nao nĩmagaikaraga na thayũ thĩinĩ wa bũrũri wao. Nĩmakamenya atĩ niĩ nĩ niĩ Jehova, rĩrĩa ngoinanga mĩtĩ ya macooki kuuma ngingo-inĩ ciao, na ndĩmateithũre kuuma moko-inĩ ma arĩa maamatuire ngombo.
