english: The tree of the field will yield its fruit, and the earth will yield its increase, and they will be secure in their land. Then they will know that I am Yahweh, when I have broken the bars of their yoke, and have delivered them out of the hand of those who made slaves of them.

kikuyu: Ũgaatuĩka ta mũndũ ũkomete iria-inĩ rĩrĩa inene gatagatĩ, o ta mũndũ ũkomete mũthia wa gĩtugĩ kĩa macua.
english: Yes, you will be as he who lies down in the middle of the sea, or as he who lies on top of the rigging:

kikuyu: O na ningĩ matiigana kwĩrira nĩ ũndũ wa mehia ma ũragani wao, kana ũrogi wao, kana ũmaraya wao, o na kana ũici wao.
english: They didn’t repent of their murders, their sorceries,‡ their sexual immorality, or their thefts.

kikuyu: Ciĩranĩro ciaku nĩthuuranĩre o biũ, na ndungata yaku nĩĩciendete.
english: Your promises have been thoroughly tested, and your servant loves them.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo mũthenya wa thĩĩna-rĩ, akaangitĩra ndĩ hau gĩikaro-inĩ gĩake; nĩakahitha kĩĩgunyĩ-inĩ kĩa hema yake, na andũgamie igũrũ wa rwaro rwa ihiga.
english: For his anger is but for a moment.  Sheol is the place of the dead.

kikuyu: Atĩrĩrĩ, wee wĩhokete andũ a Misiri, acio mahaana ta kamũrangi koinĩkangu, karĩa gatheecaga mũndũ guoko, gakamũguraria etiirania nako! Ũguo nĩguo Firaũni mũthamaki wa Misiri ahaana kũrĩ arĩa othe mamwĩhokaga.
english: Behold, you trust in the staff of this bruised reed, even in Egypt, which if a man leans on it, it will go into his hand and pierce it. So is Pharaoh king of Egypt to all who trust in him.

kikuyu: Ngoro yakwa ĩringĩkaga nĩ kwĩrirĩria ũhonokio waku, no niĩ njigĩte mwĩhoko wakwa harĩ kiugo gĩaku.
english: My soul faints for your salvation. I hope in your word.

kikuyu: Magĩthiĩ, maathiiaga na mwena o wothe wa ĩyo ĩna, o kũrĩa akerubi acio merekeirie ũthiũ; magũrũ macio ma ngaari matiagarũrũkaga rĩrĩa akerubi acio megũthiĩ. Akerubi acio maathiiaga na kũrĩa guothe mũtwe werekera, matekũgarũrũka rĩrĩa marathiĩ. Na rĩrĩ, nĩndaiguire magũrũ macio magĩĩtwo atĩrĩ, “Magũrũ marĩa mathiũrũrũkaga.”
english: Behold, the man clothed in linen, who had the inkhorn by his side, reported the matter, saying, “I have done as you have commanded me.”

kikuyu: Ngoro ya mũndũ mũũgĩ nĩyo ĩtongoragia kanua gake, nayo mĩromo yake ĩtũũgagĩria ũtaaro.
english: The heart of the wise instructs his mouth, and adds learning to his lips.

kikuyu: o ta ũrĩa kwandĩkĩtwo atĩrĩ: “Ngai nĩamaheire roho ta wa andũ magonetio nĩ toro, akĩmahe maitho matekuona, na matũ matekũigua, o nginya ũmũthĩ ũyũ.” Nake Daudi ekuuga atĩrĩ: “Metha yao ya kũrĩĩra ĩrotuĩka mũtego, na kĩndũ gĩa kũmagwatia, o na ĩtuĩke ya kũmahĩnga, na irĩhi rĩa ũũru wao.
english: According as it is written, “God gave them a spirit of stupor, eyes that they should not see, and ears that they should not hear, to this very day.”*

kikuyu: Ũtheri nĩwarĩire arĩa athingu na arĩa arũngĩrĩru ngoro makaiyũrwo nĩ gĩkeno.
english: Light is sown for the righteous, and gladness for the upright in heart.

kikuyu: kuuma mũhĩrĩga wa Simeoni, nĩ Shelumieli mũrũ wa Zurishadai;
english: bushels  A shekel is about

kikuyu: Tuganagai na mũiguanagĩre tha, na mũkarekanagĩra mũndũ na ũrĩa ũngĩ, o ta ũrĩa Ngai aamũrekeire mehia manyu thĩinĩ wa Kristũ.
english: And be kind to one another, tender hearted, forgiving each other, just as God also in Christ forgave you.

kikuyu: Jesũ agĩikara thĩ angʼetheire harĩa heekagĩrwo maruta,‡ akĩĩrorera ũrĩa gĩkundi kĩu kĩahothaga. Andũ aingĩ itonga makĩhotha mbeeca nyingĩ. No mũtumia ũmwe wa ndigwa warĩ mũthĩĩni agĩũka akĩhotha tũthendi twĩrĩ tũnini mũno twa gĩcango tũrĩa twarĩ mũigana wa mbeeca ĩmwe.§
english: Jesus sat down opposite the treasury and saw how the multitude cast money into the treasury. Many who were rich cast in much.

kikuyu: Kuuma kũu magĩthiĩ na mbere nginya kũu Biri, gĩthima-inĩ harĩa Jehova erire Musa atĩrĩ, “Cookereria andũ hamwe na nĩngũmahe maaĩ.”
english: From there they traveled to Beer; that is the well of which Yahweh said to Moses, “Gather the people together, and I will give them water.”

kikuyu: Mũrũ wakwa, nĩ kĩĩ kĩngĩtũma ũkenio nĩ mũtumia mũtharia? Nĩ kĩĩ kĩngĩtũma ũhĩmbĩrie gĩthũri kĩa mũtumia wa mũndũ ũngĩ?
english: My son, keep your father’s commandment, and don’t forsake your mother’s teaching.

kikuyu: Aikirie mĩguĩ yake, akĩharagania thũ, agĩciikĩria heni nene, agĩcingatithia.
english: He sent out his arrows, and scattered them. He routed them with great lightning bolts.

kikuyu: Nĩngatũma kĩĩruru kĩa riũa gĩcooke na thuutha makinya ikũmi marĩa gĩikũrũkĩte ngathĩ-inĩ ya Ahazu.’ ” Nĩ ũndũ ũcio ũtheri wa riũa ũgĩcooka na thuutha makinya ikũmi marĩa waikũrũkĩte.
english: Behold, I will cause the shadow on the sundial, which has gone down on the sundial of Ahaz with the sun, to return backward ten steps.” ’ ” So the sun returned ten steps on the sundial on which it had gone down.

kikuyu: Agĩcooka akĩiga thanju icio oonũrĩte mĩtaro-inĩ yothe ĩrĩa yanyuuagĩrwo maaĩ, nĩgeetha ikoragwo irĩ mbere ya mbũri rĩrĩa cioka kũnyua maaĩ. Rĩrĩa ndũũru icio ciakorwo irĩ na mũrukĩ na cioka kũnyua maaĩ-rĩ, 39igakĩhaicanĩra hau mbere ya thanju icio. Thuutha ũcio igaciara tũũri tũrĩ na manyaga, kana maara, kana marooro.
english: He set the rods which he had peeled opposite the flocks in the watering troughs where the flocks came to drink. They conceived when they came to drink.  Joseph means “may he add”.

kikuyu: Angĩgutha kĩndũ-rĩ, nũũ ũngĩhota kũmũgiria? Nũũ ũngĩmũũria atĩrĩ, ‘Nĩ atĩa ũreka?’
english: Behold, he snatches away. Who can hinder him? Who will ask him, ‘What are you doing?’

kikuyu: Gũtiiha kwayo kũmenũkagia ũtheri; maitho mayo nĩ ta mĩrũri ya rũciinĩ gũgĩthererũka.
english: His sneezing flashes out light. His eyes are like the eyelids of the morning.

kikuyu: Nowe Maritha nĩarĩ na mĩhangʼo nĩ ũndũ wa maũndũ marĩa mothe magĩrĩire kũhaarĩrio. Agĩũka harĩ Jesũ, akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Mwathani, kaĩ ũtarona ũrĩa mwarĩ wa maitũ andekereirie wĩra? Mwĩre oke andeithie!” Nake Mwathani akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Maritha, Maritha, nĩũtangĩkĩte, na ũgethĩĩnia na maũndũ maingĩ,
english: But Martha was distracted with much serving, and she came up to him, and said, “Lord, don’t you care that my sister left me to serve alone? Ask her therefore to help me.”

kikuyu: Aathanaga Kĩrĩma kĩa Herimoni, na Saleka, na Bashani guothe, o nginya mũhaka-inĩ wa andũ a Geshuru, na wa Maaka, na nuthu ya Gileadi o nginya mũhaka-inĩ wao na Sihoni mũthamaki wa Heshiboni.
english: and ruled in Mount Hermon, and in Salecah, and in all Bashan, to the border of the Geshurites and the Maacathites, and half Gilead, the border of Sihon king of Heshbon.

kikuyu: Ngai nĩ roho, nao arĩa mamũhooyaga no nginya mamũhooyage na roho, na mamũhooyage na ma.” Nake mũtumia ũcio akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Nĩnjũũĩ atĩ Mesia” (ũrĩa wĩtagwo Kristũ) “nĩarooka. Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa agooka nĩagatũtaarĩria maũndũ mothe.” Nake Jesũ akiuga atĩrĩ, “Niĩ ndĩrakwarĩria nĩ niĩ we.” Arutwo Gũcooka harĩ Jesũ
english: He who keeps his commandments remains in him, and he in him. By this we know that he remains in us, by the Spirit which he gave us.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa andũ othe maarĩkirie kũbatithio, o nake Jesũ nĩabatithirio. Na rĩrĩa aahooyaga, Igũrũ rĩkĩhingũka,
english: Now when all the people were baptized, Jesus also had been baptized and was praying. The sky was opened,

kikuyu: Waganu waku ũhutagia o mũndũ tawe, na ũthingu waku ũkahutia o ciana cia andũ.
english: If they are bound in fetters, and are taken in the cords of afflictions,

kikuyu: Mweri nĩguo ũmenyithanagia hĩndĩ ya imera, narĩo riũa nĩrĩmenyaga hĩndĩ yarĩo ya gũthũa.
english: He appointed the moon for seasons. The sun knows when to set.

kikuyu: mũrũ wa Aminadabu, mũrũ wa Aruni, mũrũ wa Hezironi, mũrũ wa Perezu, mũrũ wa Juda,
english: the son of Amminadab, the son of Aram,§ the son of Hezron, the son of Perez, the son of Judah,

kikuyu: Thuutha wa matukũ meerĩ, nĩagatwarahũra; mũthenya wa ĩtatũ nĩagatũcookereria nĩguo tũtũũre muoyo mbere yake.
english: After two days he will revive us. On the third day he will raise us up, and we will live before him.

kikuyu: Thĩ nĩnjatũku, yatũkĩte ĩkarekania, ningĩ nĩĩrathingitha mũno mũno.
english: The earth is utterly broken. The earth is torn apart. The earth is shaken violently.

kikuyu: Akĩrekereria ithandũkũ rĩa kĩrĩkanĩro rĩrĩa rĩa hinya wake rĩthaamio, riiri ũcio wake akĩũneana moko-inĩ ma thũ.
english: and delivered his strength into captivity, his glory into the adversary’s hand.

kikuyu: ũguo noguo kiugo gĩakwa kĩrĩa kiumaga kanua gakwa gĩgaaikara: Kiugo kĩu gĩtikanjookerera o ro ũguo tũhũ, no nĩgĩkahingia ũrĩa nyendete wĩkwo, na gĩkinyanĩrie muoroto ũrĩa watũmire ndĩgĩtũme.
english: so is my word that goes out of my mouth: it will not return to me void, but it will accomplish that which I please, and it will prosper in the thing I sent it to do.

kikuyu: O na andũ a Asheri matiigana kũingata andũ othe arĩa maatũũraga Ako, kana arĩa matũũraga Sidoni, kana arĩa maatũũraga Ahalabu, kana arĩa maatũũraga Akizibu, kana arĩa maatũũraga Heliba, kana arĩa maatũũraga Afeku, kana arĩa maatũũraga Rehobu,
english: Asher didn’t drive out the inhabitants of Acco, nor the inhabitants of Sidon, nor of Ahlab, nor of Achzib, nor of Helbah, nor of Aphik, nor of Rehob;

kikuyu: Kana mũndũ o wothe ũngĩkorwo na ihiga rĩngĩũragana guoko, nake agũthe mũndũ narĩo, mũndũ ũcio akue-rĩ, ũcio ũgũthanĩte nĩ mũũragani; mũũragani ũcio no nginya ooragwo.
english: “These are the names of the men who shall divide the land to you for inheritance: Eleazar the priest, and Joshua the son of Nun.

kikuyu: kuuma kũrĩ Asirieli, nĩ mbarĩ ya Aasirieli; kuuma kũrĩ Shekemu, nĩ mbarĩ ya Ashekemu;
english: and Asriel, the family of the Asrielites; and Shechem, the family of the Shechemites;

kikuyu: Esiteri ndoimbũrĩte rũrĩrĩ rwake kana kĩhumo kĩa nyũmba yao, tondũ Moridekai nĩamũtaarĩte akamwĩra ndageke ũguo.
english: Esther had not made known her people nor her relatives, because Mordecai had instructed her that she should not make it known.

kikuyu: O ta ũrĩa ndaarũgamĩrĩire makĩmunywo na magĩtharũrio, na makĩngʼaũranio, na makĩniinwo, o na ngĩmarehera mwanangĩko-rĩ, ũguo noguo ngarũgamĩrĩra nĩguo makwo na mahaandwo wega,” nĩguo Jehova ekuuga.
english: It will happen that, like as I have watched over them to pluck up and to break down and to overthrow and to destroy and to afflict, so I will watch over them to build and to plant,” says Yahweh.

kikuyu: O na ningĩ nĩmagire gũcookia ngaatho kũrĩ nyũmba ya Jerubu-Baali (nĩwe Gideoni) nĩ ũndũ wa maũndũ marĩa mothe mega aamekĩire.
english: neither did they show kindness to the house of Jerubbaal, that is, Gideon, according to all the goodness which he had shown to Israel.

kikuyu: Athĩnjĩri-Ngai akuo mahũthagĩra hinya makiuna watho wakwa, na magathaahia indo ciakwa iria nyamũre; matikũũranaga indo iria nyamũre na iria itarĩ nyamũre; marutanaga atĩ gũtirĩ ngũũrani ya indo irĩ thaahu na iria itarĩ thaahu; na mahingaga * 22:6 Aya maarĩ atongoria thĩinĩ wa Isiraeli, no ti athamaki ta thĩinĩ wa 19:1; na 21:12.
english: Her priests have done violence to my law and have profaned my holy things. They have made no distinction between the holy and the common, neither have they caused men to discern between the unclean and the clean, and have hidden their eyes from my Sabbaths. So I am profaned among them.

kikuyu: Iguthũkagĩra itũũra inene, igatengʼerera rũthingo-inĩ. Ikahaica, igatoonya nyũmba, igatoonyera ndirica ta aici.
english: They rush on the city. They run on the wall. They climb up into the houses. They enter in at the windows like thieves.

kikuyu: tondũ mũndũ o wothe ũrĩa ũtungatagĩra Kristũ na njĩra icio nĩakenagia Ngai, o na akoonagwo arĩ mwega nĩ andũ. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio-rĩ, nĩtwĩkĩragei kĩyo gĩa gwĩkaga maũndũ marĩa marehaga ũhoro wa thayũ na magatũma tuonganĩrĩre hinya.
english: For the word of the cross is foolishness to those who are dying, but to us who are being saved it is the power of God.

kikuyu: Magĩcooka makĩrehe aira a maheeni, arĩa maarutire ũira makiuga atĩrĩ, “Mũndũ ũyũ ndatigithagĩria kwaria agĩũkagĩrĩra handũ haha hatheru, o na gũũkĩrĩra watho.
english: On the second time Joseph was made known to his brothers, and Joseph’s family was revealed to Pharaoh.

kikuyu: Ĩĩ mawĩra maku matikĩrĩ manene, Wee Jehova, meciiria maku-rĩ, matikĩrĩ marikĩru!
english: They break your people in pieces, Yahweh, and afflict your heritage.

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa ngwĩciiria ũrĩrĩ wakwa nĩguo ũkũũhooreria, na atĩ gĩtanda gĩakwa no kĩĩniinĩre gũteta-rĩ,
english: So are the paths of all who forget God. The hope of the godless man will perish,

kikuyu: Nĩngatuĩra andũ akwa matuĩro ma ciira, tondũ wa waganu wao wa kũndirika, na wa gũcinĩra ngai ingĩ ũbumba, na kũhooya indo iria methondekeire na moko mao.
english: I will utter my judgments against them concerning all their wickedness, in that they have forsaken me, and have burned incense to other gods, and worshiped the works of their own hands.

kikuyu: Nacio njiaro cia Bela kuuma kũrĩ Aridi na Naamani ciarĩ ici: kuuma kũrĩ Aridi, nĩ mbarĩ ya Aaridi; na kuuma kũrĩ Naamani nĩ mbarĩ ya Anaamani.
english: The sons of Bela were Ard and Naaman: the family of the Ardites; and of Naaman, the family of the Naamites.

kikuyu: Namo nĩmo maarĩ maroho ma ndaimono marĩa maringaga ciama, namo mathiiaga kũrĩ athamaki a thĩ yothe, nĩguo mamacookanĩrĩrie nĩ ũndũ wa mbaara ĩrĩa ĩkaarũo mũthenya ũcio mũnene wa Ngai Mwene-Hinya- Wothe.
english: These will war against the Lamb, and the Lamb will overcome them, for he is Lord of lords and King of kings; and those who are with him are called, chosen, and faithful.”

kikuyu: makĩona arutwo ake amwe makĩrĩa irio na moko maarĩ na thaahu, ũguo nĩ ta kuuga, matethambĩte moko.
english: Now when they saw some of his disciples eating bread with defiled, that is unwashed, hands, they found fault.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo nĩngwarahũra mũrĩithi bũrũri-inĩ ũyũ, ũrĩa ũtakarũmbũiya ngʼondu iria ciũrĩte, kana acarie tũgondu, kana ohe iria ndiihie, o na kana arĩithie iria ngʼima, we no kũrĩa arĩrĩĩaga nyama cia ngʼondu iria noru, agĩcikũũraga mahũngũ.
english: For, behold, I will raise up a shepherd in the land who will not visit those who are cut off, neither will seek those who are scattered, nor heal that which is broken, nor feed that which is sound; but he will eat the meat of the fat sheep, and will tear their hoofs in pieces.

kikuyu: Nĩngũmahurunja ndũrĩrĩ-inĩ iria o ene matooĩ, o na kana maithe mao, njooke ndĩmaingatithie na rũhiũ rwa njora nginya ndĩmaniine biũ.”
english: Even so, I myself didn’t burden you. But you might say that being crafty, I caught you with deception.

kikuyu: Roho agĩcooka akĩnjoya na igũrũ akĩnjeheria, na niĩ ngĩthiĩ ndĩ na marũrũ na marakara ngoro-inĩ, nakuo guoko kwa Jehova kũrĩa kwa hinya kwarĩ igũrũ rĩakwa.
english: So the Spirit lifted me up, and took me away; and I went in bitterness, in the heat of my spirit; and Yahweh’s hand was strong on me.

kikuyu: “ ‘Mũtikahũthagĩre ithimi itarĩ cia kĩhooto rĩrĩa mũgũthima ũraihu, kana ũritũ, kana ũingĩ.
english: “ ‘You shall do no unrighteousness in judgment, in measures of length, of weight, or of quantity.

kikuyu: Jehova aroraga ciana cia ariũ a andũ arĩ o igũrũ, one kana nĩ harĩ ũrĩ na ũmenyo, o na kana nĩ harĩ ũrongoragia Ngai.
english: My soul, you have said to Yahweh, “You are my Lord. Apart from you I have no good thing.”

kikuyu: Theemaga ũũru, na wĩkage wega,
english: Depart from evil, and do good. Live securely forever.

kikuyu: Andũ amwe arĩa maarĩ kuo makĩigua ũũru, makĩũrania atĩrĩ, “Maguta maya maitangwo ũguo nĩkĩ?
english: Pilate again asked him, “Have you no answer? See how many things they testify against you!”

kikuyu: ũwĩre atĩrĩ, ‘Jehova ekuuga ũũ: Nĩngũgũũkĩrĩra. Nĩngũcomora rũhiũ rwakwa rwa njora kuuma njora-inĩ, na ngwehererie andũ arĩa athingu na arĩa aaganu.
english: But I have sent the brothers so that our boasting on your behalf may not be in vain in this respect, that, just as I said, you may be prepared,

kikuyu: Matukũ-inĩ macio andũ nĩmagacaragia gĩkuũ, no matigakĩona; nĩmakeriragĩria gũkua, nakĩo gĩkuũ gĩkamoorĩra.
english: These have the power to shut up the sky, that it may not rain during the days of their prophecy. They have power over the waters, to turn them into blood, and to strike the earth with every plague, as often as they desire.

kikuyu: “Wee wĩhumbĩrĩte na marakara, ũgatũingatithia; ũũraganĩte ũtekũiguanĩra tha.
english: “You have covered us with anger and pursued us. You have killed. You have not pitied.

kikuyu: Heanagai, na inyuĩ nĩmũrĩheagwo na gĩthimi kĩega, gĩkindĩrĩtwo, kĩinainĩtio na gĩgaitĩkĩrĩra, nĩkĩo mũgaitĩrĩrwo nakĩo nguo-inĩ. Nĩgũkorwo gĩthimi kĩrĩa mũthimanagĩra nakĩo, no kĩo mũrĩthimagĩrwo nakĩo.”
english: “Give, and it will be given to you: good measure, pressed down, shaken together, and running over, will be given to you.§ For with the same measure you measure it will be measured back to you.”

kikuyu: Jehova ekuuga atĩrĩ: “O na aakorwo marĩ na arata, na nĩ aingĩ mũno-rĩ, nĩmakaniinwo, meherio. O na aakorwo nĩngũnyariirĩte, wee Juda-rĩ, ndigacooka gũkũnyariira rĩngĩ. * 1:3 Ihuhũkanio na huho nene Kĩrĩkanĩro-inĩ cionanagia hinya na riiri wa Jehova. † 1:4 Bashani, na Karimeli, na Lebanoni kuoĩkaine nĩ ũndũ wa ũnoru na ũruru wakuo.
english: Yahweh says: “Though they are in full strength and likewise many, even so they will be cut down and pass away. Though I have afflicted you, I will afflict you no more.

kikuyu: Maruta ma ngano, o na maruta ma kũnyuuo nĩmatigĩte kũrehwo nyũmba-inĩ ya Jehova. Athĩnjĩri-Ngai nĩmaracakaya, o acio matungatagĩra Jehova.
english: The meal offering and the drink offering are cut off from Yahweh’s house. The priests, Yahweh’s ministers, mourn.

kikuyu: Kũrĩ hĩndĩ niĩ ndaarĩ muoyo itanamenya watho; no hĩndĩ ĩrĩa rĩathani rĩokire, wĩhia ũkĩgĩa muoyo na niĩ ngĩkua.
english: Much more then, being now justified by his blood, we will be saved from God’s wrath through him.

kikuyu: Nĩngũmuonia ũrĩa mũndũ ũrĩa ũũkaga kũrĩ niĩ, na akaigua ciugo ciakwa, na ageeka ũrĩa ciugĩte ahaana.
english: Everyone who comes to me, and hears my words and does them, I will show you who he is like.

kikuyu: na thenge ĩmwe ya igongona rĩa kũhoroherio mehia;
english: The priest shall present them before Yahweh, and shall offer his sin offering and his burnt offering.

kikuyu: Hau nja ya kĩhingo gĩa thĩinĩ, kũu nja-inĩ ya na thĩinĩ, nĩ kwarĩ na tũnyũmba twĩrĩ, kamwe mwena wa kĩhingo gĩa gathigathini kerekeire mwena wa gũthini, na kangĩ mwena wa kĩhingo gĩa gũthini kerekeire mwena wa gathigathini. Nake akĩnjĩĩra atĩrĩ, “Kanyũmba kau karorete mwena wa gũthini- rĩ, nĩ ka athĩnjĩri-Ngai arĩa maikaragia hekarũ,
english: Outside of the inner gate were rooms for the singers in the inner court, which was at the side of the north gate. They faced toward the south. One at the side of the east gate faced toward the north.

kikuyu: Mahe, Wee Jehova; no nĩ kĩĩ ũkũmahe? Mahe nda iria ihunaga, na ũmahe nyondo iria itarĩ iria.
english: Give them—Yahweh what will you give? Give them a miscarrying womb and dry breasts.

kikuyu: Ĩĩ, ũũ nĩguo Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe, Ngai wa Isiraeli, ekuuga: “Mũtikareke anabii na aragũri arĩa marĩ gatagatĩ kanyu mamũheenie. Mũtikanathikĩrĩrie irooto icio inyuĩ mũmaringagĩrĩria mamũrotere.
english: The prophets who have been before me and before you of old prophesied against many countries, and against great kingdoms, of war, of evil, and of pestilence.

kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa waheire baba ũhoro ũcio arogwatwo nĩ kĩrumi, ũrĩa watũmire akene mũno, rĩrĩa aamwĩrire atĩrĩ, “Nĩũciarĩirwo mwana wa kahĩĩ!”
english: Cursed is the man who brought news to my father, saying, “A boy is born to you,” making him very glad.

kikuyu: Nĩũkamũhooya, nake nĩagakũigua, nawe nĩũkahingia mĩĩhĩtwa yaku.
english: You will make your prayer to him, and he will hear you. You will pay your vows.

kikuyu: Paũlũ agĩtoonya thunagogi, na ihinda rĩa mĩeri ĩtatũ akĩaria na ũcamba kuo, akĩgeragia kũringĩrĩria andũ ũhoro wa ũthamaki wa Ngai. No amwe ao makĩũmia ngoro; makĩaga gwĩtĩkia na magĩcambia Njĩra ĩyo mbere ya kĩrĩndĩ. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Paũlũ akĩmeherera. Akĩoya arutwo agĩthiĩ nao nyũmba ĩrĩa yathomithagĩrio andũ ya Turano, akaaranagĩria na andũ o mũthenya arĩ kuo.
english: Crispus, the ruler of the synagogue, believed in the Lord with all his house. Many of the Corinthians, when they heard, believed and were baptized.

kikuyu: Nĩũigĩte wĩhia witũ o mbere yaku, mehia maitũ marĩa mahithe ũkamoimĩria o ũtheri-inĩ hau mbere yaku.
english: You have set our iniquities before you, our secret sins in the light of your presence.

kikuyu: Ndĩramwandĩkĩra marũa maya inyuothe arĩa mũrĩ kũu Roma, o inyuĩ mwendetwo nĩ Ngai, na mũgetwo mũtuĩke andũ aamũre: Wega na thayũ kuuma kũrĩ Ngai Ithe witũ, na kuuma kũrĩ Mwathani Jesũ Kristũ, iroikara na inyuĩ. Paũlũ Kwĩrirĩria Gũceera Roma
english: to those who by perseverance in well-doing seek for glory, honor, and incorruptibility, eternal life;

kikuyu: Atĩrĩrĩ, ndungata yakwa ĩgeekaga maũndũ na ũũgĩ; nĩĩkambarario na yambatĩrio, o na ĩtũũgĩrio mũno.
english: Behold, my servant will deal wisely. He will be exalted and lifted up, and will be very high.

kikuyu: Gũtirĩ mũndũ ũkiraga ageciiria, gũtirĩ mũndũ ũrĩ na ũũgĩ kana ũmenyo oige atĩrĩ, “Nuthu ya ngũ ndahũthĩrire na gwakia mwaki; o na ngĩrugĩra mĩgate makara-inĩ macio, na ngĩcooka ngĩhĩhia nyama na ngĩrĩa. Ti-itherũ no ngĩthondeke kĩndũ kĩrĩ magigi kuuma kũrĩ ngũ iria itigaire? Ti-itherũ no nyinamĩrĩre gĩcunjĩ kĩa mũtĩ?”
english: No one thinks, neither is there knowledge nor understanding to say, “I have burned part of it in the fire. Yes, I have also baked bread on its coals. I have roasted meat and eaten it. Shall I make the rest of it into an abomination? Shall I bow down to a tree trunk?”

kikuyu: “Mũrũ wa mũndũ, arĩria andũ anyu, ũmeere atĩrĩ: ‘Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa ngaarehithia rũhiũ rwa njora rũũkĩrĩre bũrũri, nao andũ a bũrũri ũcio
english: “Son of man, speak to the children of your people, and tell them, ‘When I bring the sword on a land, and the people of the land take a man from among them, and set him for their watchman,

kikuyu: Ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ, nĩngũmũthaitha mwĩmenyagĩrĩre andũ arĩa marehaga nyamũkano na maũndũ ma kũmũhĩngithia, marĩa mareganĩte na ũhoro ũrĩa mũrutĩtwo. Eheragĩrai andũ ta acio.
english: I have therefore my boasting in Christ Jesus in things pertaining to God.

kikuyu: Inĩrai Jehova! Goocai Jehova! Nĩgũkorwo nĩahonokagia mĩoyo ya arĩa abatari, akamĩruta moko-inĩ ma arĩa aaganu.
english: Sing to Yahweh! Praise Yahweh, for he has delivered the soul of the needy from the hand of evildoers.

kikuyu: Nĩndacookeria Ngai ngaatho nĩ ũndũ wa Jesũ Kristũ Mwathani witũ! Nĩ ũndũ ũcio-rĩ, niĩ mwene thĩinĩ wa meciiria makwa ndĩ ngombo ya watho wa Ngai, no ha ũhoro wa mwĩrĩ ũyũ wa wĩhia, niĩ ndĩ ngombo ya watho wa wĩhia.
english: I thank God through Jesus Christ, our Lord! So then with the mind, I myself serve God’s law, but with the flesh, sin’s law.

kikuyu: O ta magũrũ ma gĩthua ũrĩa mathiiaga maniginĩte, noguo thimo ĩhaana ĩkĩario nĩ kanua ka mũndũ mũkĩĩgu.
english: Like the legs of the lame that hang loose, so is a parable in the mouth of fools.

kikuyu: Wee Ngai, o Wee Ngai ũrĩa ũũhonokagia-rĩ, honokia kuumana na ũiti thakame, naruo rũrĩmĩ rwakwa nĩrũkaina ũhoro wa ũthingu waku.
english: Deliver me from the guilt of bloodshed, O God, the God of my salvation. My tongue will sing aloud of your righteousness.

kikuyu: Gĩkuũ kĩa Lazaro Na rĩrĩ, kwarĩ na mũndũ wetagwo Lazaro, nake aarĩ mũrũaru. Aarĩ wa Bethania, itũũra rĩa Mariamu na mwarĩ wa nyina Maritha.
english: He said these things, and after that, he said to them, “Our friend Lazarus has fallen asleep, but I am going so that I may awake him out of sleep.”

kikuyu: “ ‘Mũtikananeane ciana cianyu irutĩrwo Moleku igongona, mũtikanathaahie rĩĩtwa rĩa Ngai wanyu. Niĩ nĩ niĩ Jehova.
english: “ ‘You shall not give any of your children as a sacrifice to Molech. You shall not profane the name of your God. I am Yahweh.

kikuyu: Ithuĩ nĩtũũĩ atĩ Ngai nĩarĩirie Musa, no ha ũhoro wa mũndũ ũyũ-rĩ, tũtiũĩ o na kũrĩa oimĩte.” Nake mũndũ ũcio akĩmacookeria atĩrĩ, “Kaĩ ũcio nĩ ũrirũ-ĩ! Inyuĩ mũtiũĩ kũrĩa oimĩte, na nĩwe ũũhingũrire maitho.
english: We know that God has spoken to Moses. But as for this man, we don’t know where he comes from.”

kikuyu: ngakũhũthĩra kũhehenja mũndũ mũrũme na mũndũ-wa-nja, ngakũhũthĩra kũhehenja mũndũ mũkũrũ na ũrĩa mwĩthĩ, o na ngakũhũthĩra kũhehenja mwanake na mũirĩtu,
english: With you I will break in pieces the chariot and him who rides therein. With you I will break in pieces man and woman. With you I will break in pieces the old man and the youth. With you I will break in pieces the young man and the virgin.

kikuyu: Mũrungu ũrĩa ũtũũrĩte aikarĩire gĩtĩ kĩa ũnene nginya tene, nĩekũmaigua na amaherithie, andũ acio matagarũragĩra mĩthiĩre yao, o acio matetigagĩra Ngai.
english: God, who is enthroned forever, will hear and answer them. Selah. They never change and don’t fear God.

kikuyu: Atumia ake arĩa oogĩ mũno makamũcookagĩria; o nake agecookagĩria na ngoro yake atĩrĩ,
english: Her wise ladies answered her, Yes, she returned answer to herself,

kikuyu: Kĩrĩa mũndũ mwaganu etigagĩra nĩkĩo gĩkaamũkora, na kĩrĩa mũndũ mũthingu eriragĩria nĩkĩo akaaheo.
english: What the wicked fear will overtake them, but the desire of the righteous will be granted.

kikuyu: Nĩndamenyire ndundu yao njũru nĩ ũndũ Jehova nĩanguũrĩirie, nĩgũkorwo o ihinda rĩu nĩany- onirie ũrĩa meekaga.
english: Yahweh gave me knowledge of it, and I knew it. Then you showed me their doings.

kikuyu: Nĩakanjĩta anjĩĩre atĩrĩ, ‘Wee nĩwe Baba, na Mũrungu wakwa, o Rwaro rwa Ihiga, Mũhonokia wakwa.’ Ningĩ nĩngamwamũra atuĩke irigithathi rĩakwa, na ndũme atũũgĩrio gũkĩra athamaki a thĩ.
english: He will call to me, ‘You are my Father, my God, and the rock of my salvation!’

kikuyu: Akĩanĩrĩra na mũgambo mũnene, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Wee wĩ mũrathime gatagatĩ ka atumia othe, nako kaana karĩa ũgaaciara nĩkarathime! No nĩ kĩĩ gĩtũmĩte njĩkwo wega ũũ, atĩ nyina wa Mwathani wakwa no ooke kũnjeerera?
english: She called out with a loud voice and said, “Blessed are you among women, and blessed is the fruit of your womb!

kikuyu: Iburahĩmu agĩtua kaana kau ga kahĩĩ aaciarĩirwo nĩ Sara, Isaaka. Na rĩrĩa mũriũ ũcio wake Isaaka akinyirie thikũ inyanya-rĩ, Iburahĩmu akĩmũruithia, o ta ũrĩa Ngai aamwathĩte.
english: But God came to Abimelech in a dream of the night, and said to him, “Behold, you are a dead man, because of the woman whom you have taken; for she is a man’s wife.”

kikuyu: Nĩahũũnĩtie arĩa ahũtu na maũndũ mega, no arĩa itonga akamaingata matarĩ na kĩndũ.
english: He has filled the hungry with good things. He has sent the rich away empty.

kikuyu: O ta ũrĩa kwandĩkĩtwo atĩrĩ: “Nĩndendire Jakubu, no ngĩrega Esaũ.”
english: For, “Whoever will call on the name of the Lord will be saved.”*

kikuyu: Nĩerekererie rũthĩa kũrĩ mũndũ ũrĩa ũkũmũgũtha, na aiyũrwo nĩ thoni.
english: Let him give his cheek to him who strikes him. Let him be filled full of reproach.

kikuyu: O na ningĩ no ũrĩ na andũ amwe marũmĩtie ũrutani wa andũ arĩa metagwo Anikolai. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, kĩĩrire! Waga gwĩka ũguo, nĩngũũka kũrĩ we ica ikuhĩ, hũũrane nao na rũhiũ rũrũ rũrĩ kanua gakwa.
english: “I know your works, that you are neither cold nor hot. I wish you were cold or hot.

kikuyu: naguo ti Ũhoro-ũrĩa-Mwega o na atĩa. No nĩ kũrĩ andũ maramũhĩngĩĩca makĩenda gũthũkia Ũhoro-ũrĩa-Mwega wa Kristũ. No rĩrĩ, o na kũngĩkorwo nĩ ithuĩ o na kana mũraika uumĩte igũrũ, ũngĩmũhunjĩria ũhoro mwega ũngĩ tiga ũrĩa twamũhunjĩirie-rĩ, mũndũ ũcio aronyiitwo nĩ kĩrumi nginya tene!
english: but there isn’t another “good news.” Only there are some who trouble you and want to pervert the Good News of Christ.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo Johanani mũrũ wa Karea akĩarĩria Gedalia na hitho marĩ kũu Mizipa, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ: “Njĩtĩkĩria thiĩ ngoorage Ishumaeli mũrũ wa Nethania, na gũtirĩ mũndũ ũkũmenya ũhoro ũcio. Nĩ kĩĩ gĩgũtũma ende gũkũruta muoyo, nĩguo atũme Ayahudi aya othe mecookanĩrĩirie harĩwe mahurunjũke, namo matigari ma Juda mathire biũ?”
english: Then Jeremiah said to Zedekiah, “If I declare it to you, will you not surely put me to death? If I give you counsel, you will not listen to me.”

kikuyu: “Nĩndangʼaũranirie amwe anyu ta ũrĩa ndangʼaũranirie Sodomu na Gomora. Mwahaanaga ta gĩcinga kĩraakana kĩrutĩtwo mwaki-inĩ, no rĩrĩ, inyuĩ mũtiigana kũnjookerera,” ũguo nĩguo Jehova ekuuga.
english: Therefore, because you trample on the poor and take taxes from him of wheat, you have built houses of cut stone, but you will not dwell in them. You have planted pleasant vineyards, but you shall not drink their wine.

kikuyu: Ha ũhoro wa ciĩko cia andũ, na ũndũ wa uuge wa mĩromo yaku niĩ ndũũrĩte ndĩrigagĩrĩria njĩra cia andũ arĩa mendete haaro.
english: As for the deeds of men, by the word of your lips, I have kept myself from the ways of the violent.

kikuyu: Mũndũ ũcio mũtugani na ũheanaga o ũguo tũhũ, o ũcio wĩkaga maũndũ make na kĩhooto-rĩ, nĩarĩonaga maũndũ mega.
english: They were glad, and agreed to give him money.

kikuyu: Gũtaũkĩrwo wega nĩ maũndũ gũtũmaga mũndũ endeke, no njĩra ya arĩa matarĩ ehokeku ti hũthũ.
english: A simple man believes everything, but the prudent man carefully considers his ways.

kikuyu: Mũndũ mũtaana egunaga we mwene, no mũndũ ũtarĩ tha erehagĩra thĩĩna we mwene.
english: The merciful man does good to his own soul, but he who is cruel troubles his own flesh.

kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa mũthingu ndarĩ hĩndĩ akaamunywo, no ũrĩa mwaganu ndagatũũra thĩinĩ wa bũrũri.
english: The righteous will never be removed, but the wicked will not dwell in the land.

kikuyu: Akĩũragithia Jakubu, mũrũ wa nyina na Johana na rũhiũ rwa njora. Na rĩrĩa
english: When Peter had come up to Jerusalem, those who were of the circumcision contended with him,

kikuyu: Mĩgũnda nĩmĩthũkie, nayo thĩ ĩkooma; ngano nĩnyanange, ndibei ya mũhihano nĩĩniarĩte, o namo maguta magathira.
english: The field is laid waste. The land mourns, for the grain is destroyed, The new wine has dried up, and the oil languishes.

kikuyu: Mũndũ-wa-nja ũcio angĩkaarega gũũka nawe-rĩ, ndũkoohwo nĩ mwĩhĩtwa ũyũ wakwa; no ndũkanacookie mũrũ wakwa kũu.” Nĩ ũndũ ũcio ndungata ĩyo ĩkĩiga guoko kwayo rungu rwa kĩero kĩa Iburahĩmu ũcio mwathi wayo, na ĩkĩĩhĩta na mwĩhĩtwa harĩ we kũringana na ũhoro ũcio.
english: If the woman isn’t willing to follow you, then you shall be clear from this oath to me. Only you shall not bring my son there again.”

kikuyu: “Ĩra kĩũngano gĩothe atĩrĩ, ‘Eherai kuuma hema-inĩ cia Kora na Dathani na Abiramu.’ ”
english: “Speak to the congregation, saying, ‘Get away from around the tent of Korah, Dathan, and Abiram!’ ”

kikuyu: Ũrĩa ũrĩ gũtũ-rĩ, nĩakĩigue ũrĩa Roho areera makanitha. Ũrĩa wa gũtoorania-rĩ, ndakahutio o na hanini nĩ gĩkuũ kĩrĩa gĩa keerĩ. Ndũmĩrĩri kũrĩ Kanitha wa Perigamo
english: saying,§ “What you see, write in a book and send to the seven assemblies:† to Ephesus, Smyrna, Pergamum, Thyatira, Sardis, Philadelphia, and to Laodicea.”

kikuyu: O na ningĩ no mwĩgũrĩre ngombo kuuma kũrĩ andũ arĩa mararĩrĩire kwanyu na kuuma kũrĩ andũ a mĩhĩrĩga yao arĩa maciarĩirwo bũrũri wanyu, nao matuĩke indo cianyu.
english: Moreover, of the children of the aliens who live among you, of them you may buy, and of their families who are with you, which they have conceived in your land; and they will be your property.

kikuyu: na Hoshea, na Hanania, na Hashubu,
english: Hoshea, Hananiah, Hasshub,

kikuyu: Ũndũ ũcio warĩ wa kũhingia kiugo kĩa Isaia ũrĩa mũnabii kĩrĩa kĩũragia atĩrĩ: “Mwathani, nũũ wĩtĩkĩtie ũhoro witũ, nakuo guoko kwa Mwathani kũguũrĩirio ũ?” Nĩ ũndũ ũcio matingĩetĩkirie, nĩ tondũ Isaia nĩoigĩte handũ hangĩ atĩrĩ: ‡ 12:13 Mĩkĩndũ yatũmagĩrwo gũkũngũĩra ũhootani.
english: that the word of Isaiah the prophet might be fulfilled, which he spoke: “Lord, who has believed our report? To whom has the arm of the Lord been revealed?”*

kikuyu: O ta ũrĩa mũndũ onaga ũthiũ wake maaĩ-inĩ, no taguo ngoro ya mũndũ yonanagia ũrĩa mũndũ atariĩ.
english: Like water reflects a face, so a man’s heart reflects the man.

kikuyu: “Cooka ũkeere Hezekia atĩrĩ, ‘Jehova Ngai wa thoguo Daudi ekuuga ũũ: Nĩnjiguĩte ihooya rĩaku na ngoona maithori maku; nĩngũkuongerera mĩaka ikũmi na ĩtano ya gũtũũra muoyo.
english: “Go, and tell Hezekiah, ‘Yahweh, the God of David your father, says, “I have heard your prayer. I have seen your tears. Behold, I will add fifteen years to your life.

kikuyu: Jusufu akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “No ta ũrĩa ndamwĩra: Inyuĩ mũrĩ athigaani! Na ũũ nĩguo mũkũmenyeka atĩ mũrĩ andũ a ma: Ti-itherũ, o ta ũrĩa Firaũni atũũraga muoyo-rĩ, mũtingiuma kũndũ gũkũ nginya ũrĩa mũnini wanyu oke.
english: May God Almighty give you mercy before the man, that he may release to you your other brother and Benjamin. If I am bereaved of my children, I am bereaved.”

kikuyu: Nĩndĩmaheete kiugo gĩaku, nao andũ a gũkũ thĩ
english: He will glorify me, for he will take from what is mine and will declare it to you.

kikuyu: Roho wa Jehova agĩũka igũrũ rĩake, agĩtuĩka mũtiirĩrĩri bũrũri wa Isiraeli na agĩthiĩ ita-inĩ. Nake Jehova akĩneana Kushani-Rishathaimu mũthamaki wa Aramu moko-inĩ ma Othinieli, nake akĩmũtooria. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio bũrũri ũgĩkorwo na thayũ mĩaka mĩrongo ĩna, nginya rĩrĩa Othinieli mũrũ wa Kenazu aakuire. Ehudu
english: Yahweh’s Spirit came on him, and he judged Israel; and he went out to war, and Yahweh delivered Cushan Rishathaim king of Mesopotamia into his hand. His hand prevailed against Cushan Rishathaim.

kikuyu: kũũragwo kana kuohwo. Rĩrĩa ndĩramenyithirio atĩ kũraarĩ na ithugunda rĩa kũũraga mũndũ ũcio, ndĩramũtũma kũrĩ we o hĩndĩ ĩyo. Ndĩracooka ndĩraatha andũ acio mamũthitangĩte makũrehere thitango ĩrĩa mamũthitangĩire. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio thigari ikĩoya Paũlũ ũtukũ o ta ũrĩa ciaathĩtwo, ikĩmũtwara o nginya Antipatiri. Naguo mũthenya ũyũ ũngĩ igĩtiga thigari iria ciathiiaga ihaicĩte mbarathi ithiĩ nake, nacio igĩcooka nyũmba ciao.
english: When I was told that the Jews lay in wait for the man, I sent him to you immediately, charging his accusers also to bring their accusations against him before you. Farewell.”

kikuyu: ‘Niĩ nĩ niĩ Ngai wa maithe manyu, Ngai wa Iburahĩmu, na Isaaka, o na Jakubu.’ Musa akĩinaina nĩ gwĩtigĩra na ndaigana kũrora.
english: ‘I am the God of your fathers: the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob.’* Moses trembled and dared not look.

kikuyu: Tũgwĩka atĩa? Hatirĩ ngaanja nĩmekũigua atĩ nĩũũkĩte. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio ĩka ũrĩa tũgũkwĩra. Tũrĩ na andũ ana mehĩtĩte mwĩhĩtwa.
english: Now, behold, I go bound by the Spirit to Jerusalem, not knowing what will happen to me there;

kikuyu: rĩrĩa aahaandire matu kũu igũrũ, na akĩrũmia ithima cia kũrĩa kũriku wega,
english: when he established the clouds above, when the springs of the deep became strong,

kikuyu: Maciaro makwa nĩ mega gũkĩra thahabu ĩrĩa therie; uumithio wakwa nĩũkĩrĩte betha ĩrĩa njega.
english: My fruit is better than gold, yes, than fine gold, my yield than choice silver.

kikuyu: Ta cũthĩrĩriai kũũrĩa irĩma-igũrũ, muone magũrũ ma mũndũ ũrĩa ũrarehe ũhoro mwega, ũrĩa wanagĩrĩra thayũ! Wee Juda-rĩ, kũngũĩra ciathĩ ciaku, na ũhingie mĩĩhĩtwa yaku. Andũ aaganu matigagũtharĩkĩra rĩngĩ, nĩgũkorwo nĩmakaniinwo biũ.
english: Behold,§ on the mountains the feet of him who brings good news, who publishes peace! Keep your feasts, Judah! Perform your vows, for the wicked one will no more pass through you. He is utterly cut off.

kikuyu: “ ‘Ningĩ mũngĩthiĩ na mbere na mĩthiĩre ya kũnemera, na mwage kũnjigua, nĩngakĩrĩrĩria kũmũingĩhĩria mahũũra mangĩ maita mũgwanja o ũrĩa mehia manyu magĩrĩirwo nĩ kũherithio.
english: “ ‘If you walk contrary to me, and won’t listen to me, then I will bring seven times more plagues on you according to your sins.

kikuyu: Nyamũ ĩrĩa yarĩ kuo, na rĩu ndĩrĩ ho, nĩyo mũthamaki wa kanana. We nĩ ũmwe wa acio mũgwanja, na rĩu erekeire kwanangwo.
english: The merchants of the earth weep and mourn over her, for no one buys their merchandise any more:

kikuyu: akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Aca, arata akwa; mũtigeke ũndũ ũcio wa waganu.
english: He said, “Please, my brothers, don’t act so wickedly.

kikuyu: Aarĩhĩtwo nĩguo atũme ndĩtigĩre nĩgeetha ndeka ũguo njĩhie, ngĩe na rĩĩtwa rĩũru nao mone njĩra ya kũũmenithia. * 5:15 nĩ ta nuthu kilo * 6:11 Andũ arĩa mataarĩ athĩnjĩri-Ngai matietĩkĩrĩtio gũtoonya Handũ-harĩa-Hatheru-Mũno.
english: Also I shook out my lap, and said, “So may God shake out every man from his house, and from his labor, that doesn’t perform this promise; even may he be shaken out and emptied like this.” All the assembly said, “Amen,” and praised Yahweh. The people did according to this promise.

kikuyu: “Ariũ ake mangĩgatirika watho wakwa na mage kũrũmĩrĩra kĩrĩra gĩakwa,
english: If his children forsake my law, and don’t walk in my ordinances;

kikuyu: O na ũngĩgaacaria thũ ciaku, ndũgaaciona. Andũ arĩa mahũũranaga mbaara nawe magaatuĩka o ta kĩndũ hatarĩ.
english: You will seek them, and won’t find them, even those who contend with you. Those who war against you will be as nothing, as a nonexistent thing.

kikuyu: “Na rĩu ariũ ao maraanyũrũria na rwĩmbo; ngagĩtuĩka wa kuunagwo thimo nĩo.
english: “Now I have become their song. Yes, I am a byword to them.

kikuyu: Nĩũkoima gwaku kũndũ kũraya, o kũu mwena wa gathigathini, wee mũrĩ hamwe na ndũrĩrĩ nyingĩ, othe mahaicĩte mbarathi, marĩ kĩrĩndĩ kĩingĩ, mbũtũ ya ita ĩrĩ hinya.
english: Those who search through the land will pass through; and when anyone sees a man’s bone, then he will set up a sign by it, until the undertakers have buried it in the valley of Hamon Gog.

kikuyu: na kuuma Bethi-Giligali, na kuuma mwena wa Geba na Azimavethu, nĩgũkorwo aini nĩmeyakĩire matũũra gũthiũrũrũkĩria Jerusalemu. Nao athĩnjĩri-Ngai na Alawii marĩkia gwĩtheria, magĩtheria andũ, na ihingo, o na rũthingo.
english: also from Beth Gilgal and out of the fields of Geba and Azmaveth, for the singers had built themselves villages around Jerusalem.

kikuyu: Kũu irĩma-igũrũ cia bũrũri wa Isiraeli nĩkuo ũkaagũa, wee, na mbũtũ ciaku ciothe cia ita, na ndũrĩrĩ iria igaakorwo hamwe nawe. Nĩngakũneana kũrĩ nyoni cia mĩthemba yothe iria irĩĩaga nyama, na kũrĩ nyamũ cia gĩthaka, ũrĩĩo nĩcio.
english: You will fall on the mountains of Israel, you, and all your hordes, and the peoples who are with you. I will give you to the ravenous birds of every sort and to the animals of the field to be devoured.

kikuyu: Kũringana na watho wa Jehova ũrĩa aathĩte Musa, mũndũ o mũndũ nĩagayagĩrwo wĩra wake, na akeerwo kĩrĩa egũkuua. Ũguo nĩguo maatarirwo, o ta ũrĩa Jehova aathĩte Musa.
english: Moses took the redemption money from those who exceeded the number of those who were redeemed by the Levites;

kikuyu: Ngũkwĩra atĩrĩ, ndũkoima kuo ũtarĩhĩte thiirĩ wothe, hatarĩ gathendi ũgũtigia.”
english: I tell you, you will by no means get out of there until you have paid the very last penny.†”

kikuyu: Kuuma o ũnini-inĩ witũ, ngai icio cia mĩhianano cia thoni nĩcio ciananiina maciaro ma mawĩra ma maithe maitũ: ndũũru ciao cia mbũri na cia ngʼombe, aanake ao na airĩtu ao.
english: But the shameful thing has devoured the labor of our fathers from our youth, their flocks and their herds, their sons and their daughters.

kikuyu: Akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Ngoro yakwa nĩĩraigua kĩeha mũno o ta ingĩkua, ikarai haha na mwĩhũge.” Na aathiathia hanini, akĩĩgũithia thĩ, akĩhooya atĩ korwo kwahoteka, ihinda rĩu rĩmweherere.
english: He said to them, “My soul is exceedingly sorrowful, even to death. Stay here and watch.”

kikuyu: “Mĩthiĩre yaku mwene na ciĩko ciaku nĩcio ikũreheire maũndũ macio. Rĩĩrĩ nĩrĩo iherithia rĩaku. Na kaĩ nĩ irũrũ-ĩ! Kaĩ nĩrĩgũtheeca ngoro-ĩ!”
english: “But even in those days,” says Yahweh, “I will not make a full end of you.

kikuyu: niĩ na niĩ nĩngamũũkĩrĩra na marakara na niĩ mwene ngĩrĩrĩrie kũmũherithia maita mangĩ mũgwanja nĩ ũndũ wa mehia manyu.
english: then I will walk contrary to you in wrath. I will also chastise you seven times for your sins.

kikuyu: Mũndũ ũthũire kĩhooto-rĩ, no ahote gwathana? Ũrĩa ũrĩ kĩhooto, o Ũcio kĩhoti-rĩ, no ũmũtue mwĩhia?
english: Should even one who hates justice govern? Will you condemn him who is righteous and mighty,

kikuyu: Ndeto cia mũndũ wa mũhuhu nĩ ta twenyũ twega twa irio; tũmerũkaga tũgathiĩ mwĩrĩ wa mũndũ o thĩinĩ biũ.
english: The words of a whisperer are as dainty morsels, they go down into the innermost parts.

kikuyu: Ikundi cia agendi cia Tema icaragia maaĩ, agendi a wonjoria a Sheba makamacaria marĩ na mwĩhoko.
english: He will deliver you in six troubles; yes, in seven no evil will touch you.

kikuyu: Mũtikaneke maũndũ marĩa andũ a Misiri, kũrĩa mwatũũraga, meekaga, na mũtikaneke maũndũ marĩa mekagwo bũrũri wa Kaanani, kũrĩa ndĩramũtwara. Mũtikarũmĩrĩre mĩthiĩre yao. No nginya mwathĩkagĩre mawatho makwa na mũmenyagĩrĩre gwathĩkĩra kĩrĩra kĩa watho wakwa wa kũrũmĩrĩrwo. Niĩ nĩ niĩ Jehova Ngai wanyu.
english: You shall not do as they do in the land of Egypt, where you lived. You shall not do as they do in the land of Canaan, where I am bringing you. You shall not follow their statutes.

kikuyu: no nĩ kũrĩ Ngai ũrĩ igũrũ ũguũranagĩria maũndũ marĩa marĩ hitho-inĩ. Nĩwe wonetie Mũthamaki Nebukadinezaru ũrĩa gũgaikara matukũ-inĩ ma thuutha. Kĩroto gĩaku na cioneki iria irahĩtũkĩire meciiria-inĩ maku ũkomete ũrĩrĩ iraarĩ ici:
english: but there is a God in heaven who reveals secrets, and he has made known to King Nebuchadnezzar what will be in the latter days. Your dream and the visions of your head on your bed are these:

kikuyu: Matu matumanu nĩmamũhumbĩrĩte, nĩ ũndũ ũcio ndangĩtuona rĩrĩa egũceera kũu igũrũ gacũmbĩrĩ.’
english: Thick clouds are a covering to him, so that he doesn’t see. He walks on the vault of the sky.’

kikuyu: Maithe maitũ nĩmehirie na rĩu matirĩ ho, na ithuĩ nĩ ithuĩ tũraherithio handũ hao.
english: Our fathers sinned, and are no more. We have borne their iniquities.

kikuyu: Kaĩ nĩ ũrĩ ũũgĩ mũingĩ rĩrĩa ũgũcaria wendo-ĩ! Nĩ ũndũ ũcio o na atumia arĩa aaganu mũno no merute na mĩthiĩre yaku.
english: How well you prepare your way to seek love! Therefore you have even taught the wicked women your ways.

kikuyu: Ti-itherũ nĩũiguĩte ũrĩa athamaki a Ashuri mekĩte mabũrũri-inĩ mothe, ũrĩa maamaniinire biũ. Inyuĩ mũgũkĩhonoka atĩa? Na rĩrĩ, ngai cia ndũrĩrĩ iria cianiinirwo nĩ maithe maitũ ma tene: ngai cia Gozani, na cia Harani, na cia Rezefu, na andũ a Edeni arĩa maarĩ Telasaru-rĩ, nĩciamahonokirie?
english: Then Eliakim, Shebna and Joah said to Rabshakeh, “Please speak to your servants in Aramaic, for we understand it. Don’t speak to us in the Jews’ language in the hearing of the people who are on the wall.”

kikuyu: Bũrũri wao ũkĩiyũra ciũra, iria ciatoonyire nginya tũnyũmba twa toro twa anene ao.
english: Their land swarmed with frogs, even in the rooms of their kings.

kikuyu: Matania, na Bakabukia, na Obadia, na Meshulamu, na Talimoni, na Akubu maarĩ aikaria a ihingo arĩa maarangagĩra makũmbĩ marĩa maarĩ ihingo-inĩ.
english: Mattaniah, Bakbukiah, Obadiah, Meshullam, Talmon, and Akkub were gatekeepers keeping the watch at the storehouses of the gates.

kikuyu: Githĩ Jehova ndamatuĩte ũmwe? Matuĩkĩte ake mwĩrĩ na roho. Amatuĩte ũmwe nĩkĩ? Tondũ nĩacaragia rũciaro rwa etigĩri Ngai. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, gitagĩra roho waku, na ũtũũre ũrĩ mwĩhokeku kũrĩ mũtumia ũcio wa ũnini waku.
english: Did he not make you one, although he had the residue of the Spirit? Why one? He sought godly offspring. Therefore take heed to your spirit, and let no one deal treacherously against the wife of his youth.

kikuyu: Andũ othe arĩa maarĩ thĩinĩ wa thunagogi nĩmarakarire mũno rĩrĩa maiguire ũguo.
english: They were all filled with wrath in the synagogue as they heard these things.

kikuyu: Ndimenyaga maũndũ marĩa njĩkaga. Nĩgũkorwo ũrĩa nyendaga gwĩka tiguo njĩkaga, no ũndũ ũrĩa thũire nĩguo njĩkaga.
english: But the free gift isn’t like the trespass. For if by the trespass of the one the many died, much more did the grace of God and the gift by the grace of the one man, Jesus Christ, abound to the many.

kikuyu: Ikaragai thĩinĩ wakwa na niĩ nĩndĩrĩikaraga thĩinĩ wanyu. Gũtirĩ rũhonge rũngĩciara maciaro rũrĩ rwiki; no nginya rũikare thĩinĩ wa mũthabibũ. O na inyuĩ mũtingĩciara maciaro mũtangĩikara thĩinĩ wakwa.
english: Remain in me, and I in you. As the branch can’t bear fruit by itself unless it remains in the vine, so neither can you, unless you remain in me.

kikuyu: Ũhoro ũkoniĩ Kedari, o na mothamaki ma Hazoru, marĩa Nebukadinezaru mũthamaki wa Babuloni aatharĩkĩire: Jehova ekuuga atĩrĩ: “Arahũkai mũtharĩkĩre Kedari, na mũniine andũ acio a mwena wa Irathĩro.
english: Of Kedar, and of the kingdoms of Hazor, which Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon struck, Yahweh says: “Arise, go up to Kedar, and destroy the children of the east.

kikuyu: Mũndũ wa kũgĩa thiritũ na mũici nĩ muoyo wake mwene athũire; ehĩtithagio na ndangĩruta ũira.
english: Whoever is an accomplice of a thief is an enemy of his own soul. He takes an oath, but dares not testify.

kikuyu: (Nĩ ũndũ ũcio mĩthenya ĩyo ĩgĩtwo Purimu kuumana na rĩĩtwa rĩu Puri.) Nĩ ũndũ wa maũndũ mothe marĩa maandĩkĩtwo marũa-inĩ macio, na nĩ ũndũ wa ũrĩa meyoneire na nĩ ũndũ wa maũndũ marĩa maamakorire,
english: Therefore they called these days “Purim”,† from the word “Pur.” Therefore because of all the words of this letter, and of that which they had seen concerning  Purim is the Hebrew plural for pur, which means lot.

kikuyu: Wee nĩwe ndĩrakaĩra, honokia na nĩndĩrĩmenyagĩrĩra mawatho maku.
english: I have called to you. Save me! I will obey your statutes.

kikuyu: Nderi nĩwe ũmĩathaga yũmbũke na igũrũ, na ĩkeyakĩra gĩtara kĩayo igũrũ mũno?
english: Is it at your command that the eagle mounts up, and makes his nest on high?

kikuyu: Hwaĩ-inĩ arutwo arĩa ikũmi na eerĩ magĩũka kũrĩ we makĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Ingata andũ aya nĩguo mathiĩ mĩciĩ ĩrĩa ĩrĩ hakuhĩ o na mĩgũnda-inĩ mageethe irio na gwa gũkoma, tondũ haha tũrĩ nĩ werũ ũtarĩ andũ.” Nake akĩmacookeria atĩrĩ, “Maheei kĩndũ gĩa kũrĩa.” Nao makĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Tũrĩ o na mĩgate ĩtano na thamaki igĩrĩ, no tũthiire tũkagũrĩre andũ aya othe irio.”
english: The day began to wear away; and the twelve came and said to him, “Send the multitude away, that they may go into the surrounding villages and farms and lodge and get food, for we are here in a deserted place.”

kikuyu: atĩ ndingĩĩtĩkĩra kuoya kĩndũ o na kĩmwe gĩaku, o na karũrigi kana kamũkwa ga kĩraatũ, nĩgeetha ndũkanoige atĩrĩ, ‘Nĩ niĩ ndatongirie Aburamu.’
english: that I will not take a thread nor a sandal strap nor anything that is yours, lest you should say, ‘I have made Abram rich.’

kikuyu: Ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ na ma, ũrĩa wothe wamũkagĩra mũndũ ũrĩa ndũmĩte nĩ niĩ mwene aamũkagĩra; na ũrĩa wothe ũnyamũkagĩra, aamũkagĩra ũrĩa wandũmire.”
english: In that day you will know that I am in my Father, and you in me, and I in you.

kikuyu: “Bũrũri wa Zebuluni, o na bũrũri wa Nafitali, njĩra ya gũthiĩ iria-inĩ, gũikũrũkania na Rũũĩ rwa Jorodani, kũu gwĩtagwo Galili-kwa-Ndũrĩrĩ:
english: But Jesus, answering, said to him, “Allow it now, for this is the fitting way for us to fulfill all righteousness.” Then he allowed him.

kikuyu: Rungu rwa wariĩ ũcio, mathagu ma cio maatambũrũkĩtio o rĩmwe rĩerekeire harĩ rĩrĩa rĩngĩ, na o kĩũmbe kĩarĩ na mathagu meerĩ marĩa maakĩhumbĩrĩte mwĩrĩ. * 1:1 No gũkorwo ĩno yarĩ mĩaka ya Ezekieli; Mũlawii aingĩraga ũtungata-inĩ wa ũthĩnjĩri-Ngai aakinyia mĩaka mĩrongo ĩtatũ ( ). † 1:5 Mũigana wa “inya” ũrũgamĩrĩire ũkinyanĩru ta “ciũmbe iria inya irĩ muoyo” ( ) kana “mĩena ĩna ĩrĩa yumaga rũhuho” ( ).
english: Under the expanse, their wings were straight, one toward the other. Each one had two which covered on this side, and each one had two which covered their bodies on that side.  The Hebrew word rendered “God” is “אֱֹלהִ ֑ים” (Elohim).  “Yahweh” is God’s proper Name, sometimes rendered “LORD” (all caps) in other translations.  “Behold”, from “הִ ͏נֵּה”, means look at, take notice, observe, see, or gaze at. It is often used as an interjection.

kikuyu: Maamwĩrire atĩrĩ, “Mahinda ma kũrigĩrĩria-rĩ, nĩgũkagĩa na anyũrũrania arĩa makarũ- magĩrĩra merirĩria mao ene matarĩ ma gwĩtigĩra Ngai.”
english: They said to you, “In the last time there will be mockers, walking after their own ungodly lusts.”

kikuyu: nĩakirĩrĩirie mĩtugo yao ihinda rĩa ta mĩaka mĩrongo ĩna marĩ werũ-inĩ,
english: Even saying these things, they hardly stopped the multitudes from making a sacrifice to them.

kikuyu: Nebukadinezaru agĩatha thigari imwe iria ciarĩ na hinya mũno ita-inĩ rĩake ciohe Shadiraka, na Meshaki, na Abedinego, imaikie icua-inĩ rĩu rĩa mwaki wakanĩte mũno. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio andũ acio, mehumbĩte kanjũ ciao, na thuruarĩ, na iremba, o na nguo icio ingĩ-rĩ, makĩohwo na magĩikio icua-inĩ rĩu rĩa mwaki wakanĩte mũno.
english: Yes and without these they might have fallen by a single breath, being pursued by Justice, and scattered abroad by the breath of your power; but you arranged all things by measure, number, and weight.

kikuyu: no ndirĩ hĩndĩ ngaamwehereria wendo wakwa, o na kana thaahie wĩhokeku wakwa o na rĩ.
english: But I will not completely take my loving kindness from him, nor allow my faithfulness to fail.

kikuyu: Ta kĩmarore! Magĩikara thĩ kana magĩũkĩra, maanyũrũragia na nyĩmbo ciao.
english: You see their sitting down and their rising up. I am their song.

kikuyu: Aagarũrire kũrĩa kwarĩ werũ gũkĩgĩa tũria twa maaĩ, nakuo kũrĩa kwarĩ kũngʼaru agĩtũma gũkunũke ithima;
english: He turns a desert into a pool of water, and a dry land into water springs.

kikuyu: Kuuma ikinya rĩa kũgũrũ nginya mũtwe gũtirĩ handũ hagima: no nguraro theri, na mĩtũnda, na ironda cia ndĩĩra itarĩ thambie, kana ikoohwo, kana ikororoiyo na maguta.
english: For you have forsaken your people, the house of Jacob, because they are filled from the east, with those who practice divination like the Philistines, and they clasp hands with the children of foreigners.

kikuyu: “Ti-itherũ gũtirĩ mũndũ ũũkagĩrĩra mũndũ ũthuthĩkĩte ngoro, hĩndĩ ĩrĩa egũkaya ateithio arĩ mĩnyamaro-inĩ.
english: “However doesn’t one stretch out a hand in his fall? Or in his calamity therefore cry for help?

kikuyu: Nĩmagĩcookerie Jehova ngaatho nĩ ũndũ wa wendo wake ũrĩa ũtathiraga, na mawĩko make ma magegania marĩa eekĩire andũ,
english: Let them praise Yahweh for his loving kindness, for his wonderful deeds to the children of men!

kikuyu: magongona manyu matingenagia.” Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, Jehova ekuuga atĩrĩ: “Nĩngwĩkĩra mĩhĩnga mbere ya andũ aya. Maithe na ariũ ao nĩmakahĩngagwo nĩyo; andũ a itũũra hamwe na arata ao nĩmagakua.”
english: Therefore Yahweh says, “Behold, I will lay stumbling blocks before this people. The fathers and the sons together will stumble against them. The neighbor and his friend will perish.”

kikuyu: Mũthenya ũcio nĩgũgakorwo kĩrĩro kĩnene kũu Jerusalemu, o ta kĩrĩro kĩrĩa kĩarĩ Hadadi-Rimoni* o kũu werũ-inĩ wa Megido.
english: Simon Peter went up, and drew the net to land, full of one hundred fifty-three great fish. Even though there were so many, the net wasn’t torn.

kikuyu: Ũrĩa wothe ũgeragia gũtũũria muoyo wake nĩakoorwo nĩguo; no ũrĩa wothe ũkoorwo nĩ muoyo wake nĩ ũndũ wakwa nĩakaũtũũria.
english: He who seeks his life will lose it; and he who loses his life for my sake will find it.

kikuyu: Nũũ ũhaana ta Jehova Ngai witũ, o Ũcio ũikaragĩra gĩtĩ kĩa ũnene kũu igũrũ,
english: For I reckon that I am not at all behind the very best apostles.

kikuyu: “Ciĩko ciao itingĩmetĩkĩria gũcookerera Ngai wao. Roho wa ũmaraya ũrĩ ngoro-inĩ ciao, nao makaaga kũmenya Jehova.
english: “Yet let no man bring a charge, neither let any man accuse; for your people are like those who bring charges against a priest.

kikuyu: Mũheei o ta ũrĩa araheanaga; mũrĩhei maita meerĩ ma ũrĩa ekĩte. Gĩkombe kĩrĩa aramũringagĩria-rĩ, mũringĩriei kĩo maita meerĩ.
english: Return to her just as she returned, and repay her double as she did, and according to her works. In the cup which she mixed, mix to her double.

kikuyu: nake Jehova akĩmaneana moko-inĩ ma Isiraeli. Nao makĩmahoota makĩmaingatithia o nginya Sidoni ĩrĩa Nene, makĩmakinyia Misirefothu-Aimu, o na makĩmakinyia Gĩtuamba kĩa Mizipa kĩrĩa kĩrĩ mwena wa irathĩro, o nginya hakĩaga mũndũ watigirwo arĩ muoyo.
english: Yahweh said to Joshua, “Don’t fear them, for I have delivered them into your hands. Not a man of them will stand before you.”

kikuyu: Niĩ nĩ niĩ Jehova ũrĩa wamũrutire kuuma bũrũri wa Misiri nduĩke Ngai wanyu; nĩ ũndũ ũcio mwĩtheriei, tondũ niĩ ndĩ mũtheru.
english: For I am Yahweh who brought you up out of the land of Egypt, to be your God. You shall therefore be holy, for I am holy.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo o na thĩ ĩtanombwo, Ngai nĩatũthuurire nĩguo tũtuĩke ake thĩinĩ wa Kristũ, nĩguo tũtuĩke atheru na twage ũcuuke maitho-inĩ make. Nĩ ũndũ wa wendo wake-rĩ,
english: even as he chose us in him before the foundation of the world, that we would be holy and without defect before him in love,

kikuyu: Njohorithia kuuma kĩoho-inĩ gĩakwa, nĩguo ngoocage rĩĩtwa rĩaku. Hĩndĩ ĩyo andũ arĩa athingu nĩmakandigiicĩria, nĩ ũndũ wa ũrĩa ũnjĩkĩte wega.
english: Hurry to answer me, Yahweh. My spirit fails. Don’t hide your face from me, so that I don’t become like those who go down into the pit.

kikuyu: ningĩ ũngĩĩrutĩra kũhũũnia andũ arĩa ahũtu, nao arĩa ahinyĩrĩrie ũmahingĩrie maũndũ marĩa mamabatairie, hĩndĩ ĩyo ũtheri waku nĩũgaathera kũu nduma-inĩ, naguo ũtukũ waku ũgaatuĩka o ta mũthenya barigici.
english: and if you pour out your soul to the hungry, and satisfy the afflicted soul, then your light will rise in darkness, and your obscurity will be as the noonday;

kikuyu: Rĩĩtwa rĩakwa nĩrĩgatũũgĩrio ndũrĩrĩ-inĩ, kuuma riũa rĩkĩratha o nginya riũa rĩgĩthũa. Kũndũ guothe ũbumba nĩũgacinagwo na maruta matheru marutagĩrwo rĩĩtwa rĩakwa, tondũ rĩĩtwa rĩakwa nĩrĩgatũũgĩrio ndũrĩrĩ-inĩ,” ũguo nĩguo Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe ekuuga.
english: For from the rising of the sun even to its going down, my name is great among the nations, and in every place incense will be offered to my name, and a pure offering; for my name is great among the nations,” says Yahweh of Armies.

kikuyu: Neana Alawii acio kũrĩ Harũni na ariũ ake; nĩo andũ a Isiraeli arĩa mekũneanwo kũrĩ we biũ.
english: You shall give the Levites to Aaron and to his sons. They are wholly given to him on the behalf of the children of Israel.

kikuyu: Nũũ ũngĩhota gũtara rũkũngũ rwa Jakubu, kana atare gĩcunjĩ kĩa inya kĩa Isiraeli? Rekei ngue gĩkuũ kĩa arĩa athingu, na marigĩrĩrio makwa-rĩ, marotuĩka ta mao!”
english: Who can count the dust of Jacob, or count the fourth part of Israel? Let me die the death of the righteous! Let my last end be like his!”

kikuyu: Arekagĩrĩria rũheni rwake rungu rwa igũrũ guothe na akarũtũma nginya ituri cia thĩ.
english: He sends it out under the whole sky, and his lightning to the ends of the earth.

kikuyu: Ũtukũ ũcio magĩtũma ithe wao anyue ndibei, na mũirĩtu ũrĩa mũkũrũ agĩtoonya na agĩkoma nake. Nowe Loti ndaamenyire rĩrĩa mũirĩtu aakomire kana rĩrĩa ookĩrire.
english: They made their father drink wine that night: and the firstborn went in, and lay with her father. He didn’t know when she lay down, nor when she arose.

kikuyu: Ririkana ũrĩa mũtũũrĩre wakwa ũrĩ wa gũthira o narua. Kaĩ akĩrĩ ũndũ wa tũhũ watũmire ũmbe andũ othe! Nĩ mũndũ ũrĩkũ ũngĩtũũra muoyo na ndonane na gĩkuũ, kana ehonokie kuuma kũrĩ hinya wa mbĩrĩra?
english: Remember how short my time is, for what vanity you have created all the children of men!

kikuyu: “ ‘Nĩ mũtĩ ũrĩkũ harĩ mĩtĩ ya Edeni ũngĩgerekanio nawe ũhoro-inĩ ũkoniĩ riiri na ũnene? No rĩrĩ, o nawe nĩũgaikũrũkio hamwe na mĩtĩ ya Edeni nginya kũu thĩ kũriku; ũgaakoma gatagatĩ ka andũ arĩa mataruaga, hamwe na arĩa mooragirwo na rũhiũ rwa njora. “ ‘Ũcio nĩwe Firaũni na kĩrĩndĩ gĩake gĩothe, ũguo nĩguo Mwathani Jehova ekuuga.’ ”
english: “ ‘To whom are you thus like in glory and in greatness among the trees of Eden? Yet you will be brought down with the trees of Eden to the lower parts of the earth. You will lie in the middle of the uncircumcised, with those who are slain by the sword. “ ‘This is Pharaoh and all his multitude,’ says the Lord Yahweh.”

kikuyu: ‘Ta thikĩrĩria ndũmĩrĩri ya Jehova, wee mũthamaki wa Juda, o wee ũikarĩire gĩtĩ kĩa ũthamaki kĩa Daudi: wee mwene, na anene aku, o na andũ anyu arĩa matoonyagĩra ihingo-inĩ ici.
english: ‘Hear Yahweh’s word, king of Judah, who sits on David’s throne—you, your servants, and your people who enter in by these gates.

kikuyu: Mũndũ ũcio arĩkoragwo arĩ na thaahu hĩndĩ ĩrĩa angĩkorwo akiura hĩndĩ ciothe, o na kana angĩtiga kuura. Kuura kũu arĩ nakuo kũrĩĩtũmaga athaahe na njĩra ici:
english: This shall be his uncleanness in his discharge: whether his body runs with his discharge, or his body has stopped from his discharge, it is his uncleanness.

kikuyu: Mũmeere atĩrĩ, ‘Ta thikĩrĩriai ndũmĩrĩri ya Jehova, inyuĩ athamaki a Juda, na andũ othe a Juda, na mũndũ o wothe ũrĩa ũtũũraga Jerusalemu, ũrĩa ũtoonyagĩra ihingo-inĩ ici.
english: Tell them, ‘Hear Yahweh’s word, you kings of Judah, all Judah, and all the inhabitants of Jerusalem, that enter in by these gates:

kikuyu: Mũndũ mũkĩĩgu onanagia marakara make o rĩmwe, no mũndũ mũbaarĩrĩri aarumwo ndatindanagĩrĩra.
english: A fool shows his annoyance the same day, but one who overlooks an insult is prudent.

kikuyu: Magĩcooka magĩcuuka mĩtĩ, naguo mũtĩ ũkĩgwĩra Mathia; nĩ ũndũ ũcio akĩongererwo harĩ atũmwo acio ikũmi na ũmwe.
english: Therefore my heart was glad, and my tongue rejoiced. Moreover my flesh also will dwell in hope,

kikuyu: Ndegwa ĩyo na thenge, iria cia iruta rĩa kũhoroherio mehia, iria thakame yacio yatwarĩtwo Handũ-harĩa-Hatheru-Mũno ya kũhorohanĩria, no nginya ciumio nja ya kambĩ; njũa ciacio na nyama, na mahu ciothe icinwo ihĩe biũ.
english: The bull for the sin offering, and the goat for the sin offering, whose blood was brought in to make atonement in the Holy Place, shall be carried outside the camp; and they shall burn their skins, their flesh, and their dung with fire.

kikuyu: Meeri nĩigooka ciumĩte hũgũrũrũ-inĩ cia Kitimu; nĩigatooria Ashuri o na Eberi, no rĩrĩ, o nacio nĩikaanangwo.” Nake Balamu agĩgĩũkĩra, akĩinũka gwake mũciĩ, nake Balaki agĩthiĩ na njĩra ciake.
english: 1 When Balaam saw that it pleased Yahweh to bless Israel, he didn’t go, as at the other times, to use divination, but he set his face toward the wilderness.

kikuyu: Wamaheire Roho waku mwega amarutage. Ndwagire kũmahe mana marĩe, kana ũkĩaga kũmahe maaĩ maanyoota.
english: You gave also your good Spirit to instruct them, and didn’t withhold your manna from their mouth, and gave them water for their thirst.

kikuyu: Gũtirĩ mũndũ ũkaaheana irio cia kũhooreria acio maracakaĩra arĩa makuĩte,† o na akorwo nĩ ithe kana nyina, o na gũtirĩ mũndũ ũkaaheana kĩndũ gĩa kũnyua nĩguo amagirie maithori.
english: Men won’t break bread for them in mourning, to comfort them for the dead. Men won’t give them the cup of consolation to drink for their father or for their mother.

kikuyu: MayanĩmomabũrũrimarĩaEleazaruũrĩamũthĩnjĩri-Ngai, naJoshuamũrũwaNuni, naatongoria a mbarĩ cia mĩhĩrĩga ya Isiraeli maaheanire na njĩra ya gũcuuka mĩtĩ kũu Shilo o mbere ya Jehova itoonyero-inĩ rĩa Hema-ya-Gũtũnganwo. Na nĩ ũndũ ũcio makĩrĩkia kũgaya bũrũri ũcio.
english: These are the inheritances, which Eleazar the priest, Joshua the son of Nun, and the heads of the fathers’ houses of the tribes of the children of Israel, distributed for inheritance by lot in Shiloh before Yahweh, at the door of the Tent of Meeting. So they finished dividing the land.

kikuyu: no nĩĩigagĩra mũthiithũ wa irio hĩndĩ ya riũa, na ĩgacookanĩrĩria irio ciayo hĩndĩ ya magetha.
english: provides her bread in the summer, and gathers her food in the harvest.

kikuyu: Oya nguo ya mũndũ ũrĩa ũngĩrũgamĩrĩra thiirĩ wa mũndũ wa kũngĩ; mĩige ĩrũgamĩrĩre thiirĩ ũcio wa mũndũ wa kũngĩ.
english: Take the garment of one who puts up collateral for a stranger; and hold him in pledge for a wayward woman.

kikuyu: Nĩtũthiiage na mĩthiĩre mĩagĩrĩru, o ĩrĩa yagĩrĩire gũikarwo nayo mũthenya, tũtigatoonye maruga-inĩ mooru marĩ na nyĩmbo njũru na ũrĩĩu, na tũtigatoonye ũtharia-inĩ kana maũndũ ma ũũra-thoni, na tũtikagĩage na njũgitano na kũiguanĩra ũiru. No rĩrĩ, mwĩhumbei Mwathani Jesũ Kristũ, na mũtigatũũre mwĩciiragia o ũrĩa mũngĩhingia merirĩria ma mĩĩrĩ ĩno ya mehia.
english: Let’s walk properly, as in the day; not in reveling and drunkenness, not in sexual promiscuity and lustful acts, and not in strife and jealousy.

kikuyu: Ũgaathiĩ mbĩrĩra-inĩ ũrĩ o na hinya, ũhaana ta itĩĩa cia ngano ĩgethetwo hĩndĩ ya magetha.
english: You will come to your grave in a full age, like a shock of grain comes in its season.

kikuyu: Nĩnjũũĩ atĩ Mũngũũri arĩ muoyo, na atĩ marigĩrĩrio-inĩ nĩwe ũkaarũgama thĩ ĩno.
english: But as for me, I know that my Redeemer lives. In the end, he will stand upon the earth.

kikuyu: Mũtikanamũkombere mbeeca nĩguo acookie na maciaro, kana mũmwenderie irio nĩguo muone uumithio.
english: You shall not lend him your money at interest, nor give him your food for profit.

kikuyu: Ũcio ũrumĩte na ũkamenereria nũũ? Nũũ ũgũthũkĩire na mũgambo, na ũkamwambararĩria maitho na mwĩtĩĩo? Wee nĩũũkĩrĩire Ũrĩa Mũtheru wa Isiraeli!
english: Whom have you defied and blasphemed? Against whom have you exalted your voice and lifted up your eyes on high? Against the Holy One of Israel.

kikuyu: mũrũ wa Mathati, mũrũ wa Lawi, mũrũ wa Meliki, mũrũ wa Janai, mũrũ wa Jusufu,
english: the son of Matthat, the son of Levi, the son of Melchi, the son of Jannai, the son of Joseph,

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo nĩndamenyaga atĩ wee ũrĩ mũremi mũno, nayo ngingo yaku ĩkahaana ta rũga rũmũ rwa kĩgera, naguo thiithi waku ũgatuĩka ta gĩcango. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio ndakwĩrire maũndũ maya o tene; ngĩkwĩra ũhoro wamo mataanekĩka, nĩgeetha ndũkanoige atĩrĩ, ‘Mĩhianano yakwa ya kũhooywo nĩyo ĩĩkĩte maũndũ macio; mũhianano wakwa wa gwacũhio, na ngai yakwa ya kĩgera nĩcio ciathanire ũhoro wamo.’
english: But I said, “I have labored in vain. I have spent my strength in vain for nothing; yet surely the justice due to me is with Yahweh, and my reward with my God.”

kikuyu: no ũthiĩ kũrĩ nyũmba ya baba na kũrĩ mũhĩrĩga wakwa, na ũrutĩre mũriũ wakwa mũtumia kuo.’
english: but you shall go to my father’s house, and to my relatives, and take a wife for my son.’

kikuyu: Nĩakaruta nyamũ ĩyo ya iruta rĩa njino rũũa na amĩtinangie tũcunjĩ.
english: You shall cut it in pieces, and pour oil on it. It is a meal offering.  “Yahweh” is God’s proper Name, sometimes rendered “LORD” (all caps) in other translations.

kikuyu: Mahũũra na nguraro nĩitheragia ũũru wa mũndũ, nacio iboko itheragia o nginya tũturi twa thĩinĩ mũno.
english: Wounding blows cleanse away evil, and beatings purge the innermost parts.

kikuyu: Ngai arokũhe kĩrathimo kĩrĩa aaheire Iburahĩmu hamwe na njiaro ciaku, nĩgeetha wĩgwatĩre bũrũri ũyũ ũratũũra rĩu ũrĩ ta mũgeni, o bũrũri ũyũ Ngai eerĩire Iburahĩmu.”
english: and give you the blessing of Abraham, to you and to your offspring with you, that you may inherit the land where you travel, which God gave to Abraham.”

kikuyu: Ti-itherũ Ngai nĩwe ũteithio wakwa; Mwathani nĩwe ũndiiragĩrĩra.
english: My heart is severely pained within me. The terrors of death have fallen on me.

kikuyu: “Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa Mũrũ wa Mũndũ agooka arĩ na riiri wake, na araika othe marĩ hamwe nake, nĩagaikarĩra gĩtĩ gĩake kĩa ũnene na kĩrĩ riiri wa igũrũ.
english: “But when the Son of Man comes in his glory, and all the holy angels with him, then he will sit on the throne of his glory.

kikuyu: Andũ aku arĩa aamũre nĩmegwatĩire bũrũri ũyũ wamaheire ũgĩtuĩka wao o ihinda inini, no rĩu thũ ciitũ nĩcirangĩrĩirie handũ haku harĩa haamũre.
english: Your holy people possessed it but a little while. Our adversaries have trodden down your sanctuary.

kikuyu: “ ‘Mwathani Jehova ekuuga ũũ: Mũthenya ũrĩa mũtĩ ũcio waikũrũkirio mbĩrĩra-inĩ, nĩndahingire ithima iria ciarĩ ndiku nĩ ũndũ wa kũũcakaĩra; nĩndarũgamirie tũrũũĩ twakuo, namo maaĩ maingĩ makuo makĩhingĩrĩrio. Nĩ ũndũ waguo ngĩhumba Lebanoni nduma, nayo mĩtĩ yothe ya gĩthaka ĩkĩhooha.
english: “When I make the land of Egypt desolate and waste, a land destitute of that of which it was full, when I strike all those who dwell therein, then they will know that I am Yahweh.

kikuyu: Gũtirĩ nyoni ndĩa-nyama yũĩ gacĩra kau kahithe, na gatirĩ konwo nĩ riitho rĩa hũngũ.
english: That path no bird of prey knows, neither has the falcon’s eye seen it.

kikuyu: Ihiga rĩrĩa aaki maaregire nĩrĩo rĩtuĩkĩte ihiga inene rĩa koine;
english: The stone which the builders rejected has become the cornerstone.†

kikuyu: “No nĩnjũũĩ kũrĩa ũikaraga, na ngamenya ũkiumagara na ũgĩcooka, o na ũrĩa ũndakaragĩra.
english: But I know your sitting down, your going out, your coming in, and your raging against me.

kikuyu: Nĩndarakaririo nĩ ũhoro ũcio mũno na ngĩikia indo ciothe cia Tobia nja.
english: It grieved me severely. Therefore I threw all Tobiah’s household stuff out of the room.

kikuyu: nĩatũhingĩirie ithuĩ ciana ciao, na ũndũ wa kũriũkia Jesũ. O ta ũrĩa kwandĩkĩtwo thĩinĩ wa Thaburi ya keerĩ atĩrĩ: “ ‘Wee ũrĩ Mũrũ wakwa; ũmũthĩ nĩndatuĩka Thoguo.’ Naguo ũhoro wa ma wa atĩ Ngai nĩamũriũkirie kuuma kũrĩ arĩa akuũ nĩguo ndakabuthe-rĩ, nĩ warĩtio na ciugo ici: “ ‘Nĩngakũhe irathimo iria theru na iria itarĩ hĩndĩ ingĩaga iria cierĩirwo Daudi.’
english: that God has fulfilled this to us, their children, in that he raised up Jesus. As it is also written in the second psalm, ‘You are my Son. Today I have become your father.’*

kikuyu: Esaũ akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “No kĩreke ngũtigĩre ndungata imwe ciakwa.” Jakubu akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Ũgwĩka ũguo nĩkĩ? Reke niĩ njĩtĩkĩrĩke maitho-inĩ ma mwathi wakwa.” Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Esaũ akĩhũndũka mũthenya o ro ũcio agĩthiĩ arorete Seiru.
english: Only on this condition will we consent to you. If you will be as we are, that every male of you be circumcised,

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo ũmwe wao wetagwo Kaiafa, ũrĩa warĩ mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai mũnene mwaka ũcio, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Inyuĩ mũtirĩ ũndũ mũũĩ o na atĩa!
english: But a certain one of them, Caiaphas, being high priest that year, said to them, “You know nothing at all,

kikuyu: Thĩinĩ wa andũ-a-nja aku arĩa atĩĩku harĩ na aarĩ a athamaki; mũhiki ũcio wa mũthamaki aikarĩte mwena waku wa ũrĩo egemetie na thahabu ya Ofiri.
english: He makes wars cease to the end of the earth. He breaks the bow, and shatters the spear. He burns the chariots in the fire.

kikuyu: ya Biliga, aarĩ Shamua; ya Shemaia, aarĩ Jehonathani;
english: of Bilgah, Shammua; of Shemaiah, Jehonathan;

kikuyu: Aarĩkia kuuga ũguo, agĩturia ndu hamwe nao othe makĩhooya. Nao othe makĩrĩra makĩmũhĩm- bagĩria na makĩmũmumunya.
english: When he had spoken these things, he knelt down and prayed with them all.

kikuyu: Ũũkarange na maguta rũgĩo-inĩ; ũũrehe ũtukanĩtio wega, na ũneane iruta rĩu rĩa mũtu rĩenyũrangĩtwo cienyũ, rĩtuĩke mũtararĩko mwega wa gũkenia Jehova.
english: It shall be made with oil in a griddle. When it is soaked, you shall bring it in. You shall offer the meal offering in baked pieces for a pleasant aroma to Yahweh.

kikuyu: kuuma mũhĩrĩga wa Efiraimu, aarĩ Hoshea mũrũ wa Nuni;
english: Of the tribe of Ephraim, Hoshea the son of Nun.

kikuyu: nĩguo ngaacooka nyũmba-inĩ ya baba na thayũ-rĩ, Jehova nĩagatuĩka Ngai wakwa,
english: Isaac said to Jacob, “Please come near, that I may feel you, my son, whether you are really my son Esau or not.”

kikuyu: Ũũmbi wa Ngai Wonanagia Riiri Wake Thaburi ya Daudi
english: For the Chief Musician. A Psalm by David.

kikuyu: Mbugĩrĩrio ya gĩkeno na ya ũhootani ĩiyũrĩte hema-inĩ cia andũ arĩa athingu, atĩ: “Guoko kwa ũrĩo kwa Jehova nĩ gwĩkĩte maũndũ manene!
english: I will meditate on your precepts, and consider your ways.

kikuyu: Nĩndamahurunjire ndũrĩrĩ-inĩ, na makĩharaganĩrio mabũrũri-inĩ; ndaamatuĩrĩire ciira kũringana na mĩtugo yao na ciĩko ciao. Na kũrĩa guothe maathiire ndũrĩrĩ-inĩ nĩmathaahirie rĩĩtwa rĩakwa itheru, nĩgũkorwo kweragwo atĩrĩ igũrũ rĩao, ‘Aya nĩ andũ a Jehova, no no nginya maangĩeherire bũrũri wake.’
english: I scattered them among the nations, and they were dispersed through the countries. I judged them according to their way and according to their deeds.

kikuyu: “Mwathani, nĩmoragĩte anabii aku na nĩmatharĩtie igongona ciaku; no niĩ nyiki ndigarĩte, nao nĩkũnjaria maranjaria manjũrage”?
english: “Lord, they have killed your prophets. They have broken down your altars. I am left alone, and they seek my life.”*

kikuyu: Mũthenya ũyũ ũngĩ, rũciinĩ tene, andũ acio na Isaaka makĩĩhĩtanĩra mwĩhĩtwa mũndũ na ũrĩa ũngĩ. Isaaka agĩcooka akĩmoimagaria, magĩĩthiĩra, makĩmũtiga na thayũ.
english: They rose up some time in the morning, and swore an oath to one another. Isaac sent them away, and they departed from him in peace.

kikuyu: Nĩmũriganĩirwo nĩ Ngai, Mũhonokia wanyu; nĩmwagĩte kũririkana Rwaro rũu rwa Ihiga, o rũu kĩĩgitĩro kĩanyu.
english: For you have forgotten the God of your salvation, and have not remembered the rock of your strength. Therefore you plant pleasant plants, and set out foreign seedlings.

kikuyu: Alawii othe arũme a ũkũrũ wa mweri ũmwe na makĩria arĩa maatarirwo maarĩ andũ 23,000. Matiataranĩirio hamwe na andũ a Isiraeli arĩa angĩ tondũ matiagaire kĩndũ hamwe na andũ arĩa angĩ.
english: Those who were counted of them were twenty-three thousand, every male from a month old and upward; for they were not counted among the children of Israel, because there was no inheritance given them among the children of Israel.

kikuyu: naguo ũthĩĩni ũgaagũkorerera ta mũitũ, na wagi ũgũkorerere ta mũndũ wĩohete indo cia mbaara.
english: so your poverty will come as a robber and your want as an armed man.

kikuyu: “No mĩaka mĩnini ĩgũthira, thiĩ rũgendo rũrĩa mũndũ athiiaga na ndacooke.
english: For when a few years have come, I will go the way of no return.

kikuyu: Roho wa mũndũ nĩ tawa wa Jehova, ũthuthuuragia o nginya tũturi tũrĩa tũrĩ thĩinĩ mũno.
english: The spirit of man is Yahweh’s lamp, searching all his innermost parts.

kikuyu: Aciirithania aku ngaatũma matuĩke o ta ũrĩa maatariĩ matukũ-inĩ ma tene, na aheani kĩrĩra aku o ta ũrĩa maatariĩ kĩambĩrĩria-inĩ. Thuutha ũcio ũgeetagwo Itũũra Inene rĩa Ũthingu, o na Itũũra Inene Rĩĩhokeku.”
english: I will restore your judges as at the first, and your counselors as at the beginning. Afterward you shall be called ‘The city of righteousness, a faithful town.’

kikuyu: Wee ũngĩgaathĩĩnia airĩtu akwa kana ũmahikĩrĩrie atumia angĩ, o na hangĩgakorwo hatarĩ mũndũ ũngĩ-rĩ, ririkana atĩ Ngai nĩwe mũira gatagatĩ gakwa nawe.” Ningĩ Labani akĩĩra Jakubu atĩrĩ, “Ĩno nĩ hĩba, na gĩkĩ nĩ gĩtugĩ kĩrĩa ndahaanda gatagatĩ gaku na niĩ.
english: If you afflict my daughters, or if you take wives in addition to my daughters,  “Jegar Sahadutha” means “Witness Heap” in Aramaic.  “Galeed” means “Witness Heap” in Hebrew.

kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa wamũrĩte nyũmba yake angĩmĩkũũra-rĩ, no nginya ongerere gĩcunjĩ kĩngĩ gĩa ithano gĩa thogora wayo, nayo nyũmba ĩyo nĩĩgatuĩka yake rĩngĩ.
english: and if you shall reject my statutes, and if your soul abhors my ordinances, so that you will not do all my commandments, but break my covenant,

kikuyu: Kuuma hau mũhaka ũcio ũgĩthiũrũrũka na mwena wa gathigathini, ũgĩkinya Hanathoni, na ũkĩrĩkĩrĩra gĩtuamba-inĩ kĩa Ifita-Eli.
english: The border turned around it on the north to Hannathon; and it ended at the valley of Iphtah El;

kikuyu: “Nĩnengeretwo maũndũ mothe nĩ Baba. Gũtirĩ mũndũ ũũĩ Mũriũ tiga Ithe, na gũtirĩ mũndũ ũũĩ Ithe tiga Mũriũ, na arĩa Mũriũ, endaga kũguũrĩria.
english: All things have been delivered to me by my Father. No one knows the Son, except the Father; neither does anyone know the Father, except the Son and he to whom the Son desires to reveal him.

kikuyu: Bũrũri nĩũracakaya na ũgathirĩrĩkĩra, nakuo kũu Lebanoni nĩgũconorithĩtio na mĩtĩ yakuo ĩkahooha. Sharoni nakuo kũhanĩte ta werũ wa Araba, nakuo Bashani na Karimeli gũgaita mathangũ makuo.
english: Its streams will be turned into pitch, its dust into sulfur, and its land will become burning pitch.

kikuyu: O ihinda-inĩ o rĩu ndaacookereirwo nĩ ũgima wa meciiria makwa-rĩ, norĩo ndaacookeirio gĩtĩĩo gĩakwa na ũkaru wakwa nĩgeetha ũthamaki wakwa ũgĩe na riiri. Ningĩ ataari akwa na andũ arĩa maarĩ igweta makĩĩnyamũkĩra, ngĩcookio gĩtĩ-inĩ gĩakwa kĩa ũnene, na ngĩneneha gũkĩra mbere. Na rĩrĩ, niĩ Nebukadinezaru-rĩ, nĩngũkumia, na ndũũgĩrie, na ngooce Mũthamaki wa igũrũ, nĩgũkorwo maũndũ marĩa mothe ekaga nĩmagĩrĩire, na mĩthiĩre yake yothe nĩ ya kĩhooto. Nao arĩa mathiiaga na mwĩgaatho nĩahotaga kũmanyiihia.
english: At the same time my understanding returned to me; and for the glory of my kingdom, my majesty and brightness returned to me. My counselors and my lords sought me; and I was established in my kingdom, and excellent greatness was added to me.

kikuyu: “Nĩkĩ kĩragiririe maguta macio mendio na mbeeca icio iheo athĩĩni? Nĩmaraiganĩte mũcaara wa mwaka mũgima.”
english: “Why wasn’t this ointment sold for three hundred denarii‡ and given to the poor?”

kikuyu: nĩgeetha Kristũ atũũrage ngoro-inĩ cianyu na ũndũ wa gwĩtĩkia. Na ningĩ nĩhooyaga atĩ inyuĩ mũhaandwo nĩguo mũgĩe na mĩri na mwĩkindĩre thĩinĩ wa wendani,
english: This I say therefore, and testify in the Lord, that you no longer walk as the rest of the Gentiles also walk, in the futility of their mind,

kikuyu: Ũndũ wa mbere ũrĩa Anderea eekire nĩgũcaria mũrũ wa nyina Simoni, na kũmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Nĩtuonete Mesia” (nĩwe Kristũ). Nake akĩmũtwara kũrĩ Jesũ. Nake Jesũ akĩmũrora, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Wee nĩwe Simoni mũrũ wa Johana. Ũgũcooka gwĩtwo Kefa” (na rĩo rĩgĩtaũrwo nĩ kuuga Petero). Jesũ gwĩta Filipu na Nathanieli
english: He first found his own brother, Simon, and said to him, “We have found the Messiah!” (which is, being interpreted, Christ‡).

kikuyu: Nĩtũiguĩte ũhoro wa mwĩtĩĩo wa Moabi: tũkaigua ũhoro wa mwĩtĩĩo wake mũkĩru na mwĩĩkĩrĩro wake, tũkaigua ũhoro wa mwĩtĩĩo wake na rũngʼathio rwake; no rĩrĩ, mwĩgaatho ũcio wake no wa tũhũ. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio andũ a Moabi nĩkũgirĩka maragirĩka, maragirĩka hamwe nĩ ũndũ wa Moabi. Cakayai na mũnyiitwo nĩ kĩeha nĩ ũndũ wa andũ a Kiri-Haresethi.
english: Yet gleanings will be left there, like the shaking of an olive tree, two or three olives in the top of the uppermost bough, four or five in the outermost branches of a fruitful tree,” says Yahweh, the God of Israel.

kikuyu: Nyondo ĩrĩa ĩtũũrĩte ĩhũũraga thĩ yothe-rĩ, kaĩ nĩyunangĩtwo, ĩgathethereka-ĩ! Kaĩ Babuloni nĩgũkirĩte ihooru gatagatĩ-inĩ ka ndũrĩrĩ-ĩ!
english: How the hammer of the whole earth is cut apart and broken! How Babylon has become a desolation among the nations!

kikuyu: “Jaeli mũtumia wa Heberi ũrĩa Mũkeni, arorathimwo gũkĩra atumia arĩa angĩ, o we mũrathime mũno harĩ atumia arĩa matũũraga hema-inĩ.
english: “Jael shall be blessed above women, the wife of Heber the Kenite; blessed shall she be above women in the tent.

kikuyu: Ndaaraga itarĩ toro; haanĩte ta nyoni ĩrĩ o iiki nyũmba igũrũ.
english: I watch, and have become like a sparrow that is alone on the housetop.

kikuyu: Thikĩrĩria ngĩgũkaĩra ũndeithie, Mũthamaki wakwa na Ngai wakwa, nĩgũkorwo nĩwe ndĩrahooya.
english: Have mercy on me, Yahweh, for I am faint. Yahweh, heal me, for my bones are troubled.

kikuyu: Jakubu akĩũria arĩithi acio atĩrĩ, “Ariũ a baba, muumĩte kũ?” Nao makĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Tuumĩte Harani.”
english: She gave him Bilhah her servant as wife, and Jacob went in to her.

kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa wĩgĩĩagĩra na ũũgĩ nĩ ngoro yake mwene endete; ũrĩa ũkenagĩra ũtaũku nĩagaacagĩra.
english: He who gets wisdom loves his own soul. He who keeps understanding shall find good.

kikuyu: ndaahubanĩirio nĩ maaĩ maingĩ, magĩikũrũkĩra igũrũ rĩa mũtwe wakwa, na niĩ ngĩĩciiria ndĩ hakuhĩ kũniinwo.
english: Waters flowed over my head. I said, “I am cut off.”

kikuyu: Ũũ nĩguo Jehova ekuuga, o ũcio watemire njĩra iria-inĩ, na agĩtema gacĩra kũndũ kũrĩa kwarĩ maaĩ marĩ hinya,
english: He burns part of it in the fire. With part of it, he eats meat. He roasts a roast and is satisfied. Yes, he warms himself

kikuyu: Musa nĩaiguire andũ a nyũmba ciothe makĩrĩra, o mũndũ arĩ mũromo-inĩ wa hema yake. Nake Jehovanĩarakariremũnomakĩria, naMusaagĩthĩĩnĩka.
english: Moses heard the people weeping throughout their families, every man at the door of his tent; and Yahweh’s anger burned greatly; and Moses was displeased.

kikuyu: Jehova arĩ hakuhĩ na andũ arĩa othe mamũkayagĩra, arĩa othe mamũkayagĩra na ma. Arĩa othe mamwĩtigagĩra nĩamahingagĩria mabataro mao; nĩaiguaga kĩrĩro kĩao na akamahonokia.
english: Yahweh is near to all those who call on him, to all who call on him in truth.

kikuyu: Ũcio nĩguo ũtaũri wa kĩoneki kĩa ihiga rĩu rĩenjetwo kuuma kĩrĩma-inĩ, no rĩtienjetwo na moko ma andũ, na nĩrĩo rĩathetherire kĩgera, na gĩcango, na rĩũmba, na betha na thahabu igĩtuĩka tũcunjĩ. “Ngai ũcio mũnene nĩamenyithĩtie mũthamaki ũrĩa gũkaahaana thuutha-inĩ. Kĩroto kĩu nĩ kĩa ma, naguo ũtaũri wakĩo nĩ wa kwĩhokeka.”
english: Because you saw that a stone was cut out of the mountain without hands, and that it broke in pieces the iron, the bronze, the clay, the silver, and the gold, the great God has made known to the king what will happen hereafter. The dream is certain, and its interpretation sure.”  “Behold”, from “הִ ͏נֵּה”, means look at, take notice, observe, see, or gaze at. It is often used as an interjection.

kikuyu: Goocai Jehova mũmũnenehie mũrĩ hamwe na niĩ; rekei tũtũũgĩrie rĩĩtwa rĩake tũrĩ hamwe.
english: Oh magnify Yahweh with me. Let’s exalt his name together.

kikuyu: Kuuma mũhĩrĩga wa Asheri maarĩ andũ 41,500.
english: those who were counted of them, of the tribe of Asher, were forty-one thousand five hundred.

kikuyu: Shaitani agĩcookia atĩrĩ, “Kaĩ Ayubu agĩĩtigagĩra Ngai o ro ũguo tũhũ?
english: Let the stars of its twilight be dark. Let it look for light, but have none, neither let it see the eyelids of the morning,

kikuyu: Thiĩi magomano-inĩ ma njĩra mwĩre mũndũ o wothe mũkuona oke iruga- inĩ rĩa kĩhikanio.’ Nĩ ũndũ ũcio ndungata icio ikiumagara, igĩthiĩ njĩra-inĩ, igĩcookanĩrĩria andũ othe arĩa ciahotire kuona, igĩĩta arĩa ega o na arĩa ooru, nayo nyũmba ya kĩhikanĩrio ĩkĩiyũra ageni.
english: Go therefore to the intersections of the highways, and as many as you may find, invite to the wedding feast.’

kikuyu: makĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Angĩkorwo we tiwe Kristũ, na tiwe Elija, o na kana Mũnabii ũrĩa-rĩ, ũkĩbatithanagia nĩkĩ?” Nake Johana akĩmacookeria atĩrĩ, “Niĩ ndĩmũbatithagia na maaĩ. No gatagatĩ-inĩ kanyu nĩ harĩ na ũmwe mũtooĩ.
english: They asked him, “Why then do you baptize if you are not the Christ, nor Elijah, nor the prophet?”

kikuyu: Andũ arĩa magwĩtigĩrĩte marokenaga rĩrĩa maanyona,
english: Those who fear you will see me and be glad, because I have put my hope in your word.

kikuyu: Bathaka ya Jehova ĩrĩambagĩrĩria hwaĩ-inĩ kwaira, mũthenya wa ikũmi na ĩna wa mweri wa mbere.
english: In the first month, on the fourteenth day of the month in the evening, is Yahweh’s Passover.

kikuyu: Maigua ũguo makĩhumbĩra matũ mao, makĩhanyũka harĩ we makiugagĩrĩria na mũgambo mũnene.
english: But they cried out with a loud voice and stopped their ears, then rushed at him with one accord.

kikuyu: no rĩrĩ, nĩathuurire mũhĩrĩga wa Juda, na Kĩrĩma gĩa Zayuni kĩrĩa endete.
english: But chose the tribe of Judah, Mount Zion which he loved.

kikuyu: Jehova, nĩnjũũĩ atĩ mawatho maku nĩ ma ũthingu, na ũnyonetie thĩĩna nĩ ũndũ wa wĩhokeku waku.
english: Yahweh, I know that your judgments are righteous, that in faithfulness you have afflicted me.

kikuyu: Jusufu akĩĩra ariũ a ithe atĩrĩ, “Nĩ niĩ Jusufu! Baba arĩ o muoyo?” No ariũ a ithe matiahotire kũmũcookeria, nĩgũkorwo nĩmamakĩte mũno marĩ mbere yake. Ningĩ Jusufu akĩĩra ariũ a ithe atĩrĩ, “Ta nguhĩrĩriai.” Meeka ũguo-rĩ, akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Nĩ niĩ mũrũ wa thoguo Jusufu, ũrĩa mwendirie Misiri!
