english: “It will happen in that day”, says Yahweh, “that I will cut off your horses from among you and will destroy your chariots.

kikuyu: Inyuĩ ngʼondu ciakwa, o inyuĩ ngʼondu cia rũũru rwakwa-rĩ, inyuĩ mũrĩ andũ, na niĩ ndĩ Ngai wanyu, ũguo nĩguo Mwathani Jehova ekuuga.’ ” Ningĩ kiugo kĩa Jehova nĩkĩanginyĩrĩire, ngĩĩrwo atĩrĩ:
english: You my sheep, the sheep of my pasture, are men, and I am your God,’ says the Lord Yahweh.”

kikuyu: nĩmagakuoya na igũrũ na moko mao nĩgeetha ndũkaringwo kũgũrũ nĩ ihiga.
english: They will bear you up in their hands, so that you won’t dash your foot against a stone.

kikuyu: Maũndũ-inĩ macio mothe matũkorete, wee ũkoretwo ũrĩ na kĩhooto; wĩkĩte maũndũ na wĩhokeku, o rĩrĩa ithuĩ tũhĩtĩtie.
english: However you are just in all that has come on us; for you have dealt truly, but we have done wickedly.

kikuyu: nake Joiada aarĩ ithe wa Jonathani, nake Jonathani aarĩ ithe wa Jadua.
english: Seraiah the son of Hilkiah, the son of Meshullam, the son of Zadok, the son of Meraioth, the son of Ahitub, the ruler of God’s house,

kikuyu: Rũrĩmĩ rwa maheeni nĩkũmena rũmenete arĩa rũtuuragia, nako kanua ga kwĩyendithĩrĩria karehaga mwanangĩko.
english: A lying tongue hates those it hurts; and a flattering mouth works ruin.

kikuyu: Ũkĩrai mũthiĩ, mwehere! Nĩgũkorwo gĩikaro gĩkĩ ti kĩo kĩhurũko kĩanyu, tondũ nĩgĩthaahie, kĩanangĩtwo ũndũ gĩtangĩcookererio.
english: Arise, and depart! For this is not your resting place, because of uncleanness that destroys, even with a grievous destruction.

kikuyu: nake akĩrĩkũnjũrĩra hau mbere yakwa. Narĩo rĩandĩkĩtwo ciugo cia macakaya mĩena yeerĩ, na cia kĩrĩro, o na cia kĩeha.
english: He spread it before me. It was written within and without; and lamentations, mourning, and woe were written in it.

kikuyu: Maũndũ macio magaatuĩka ithaga rĩa kũgemia mũtwe waku, na ta mũgathĩ wa kũgemia ngingo yaku.
english: Honor Yahweh with your substance, with the first fruits of all your increase;

kikuyu: Maya nĩmo marĩĩtwa ma ariũ a Lawi: Nĩ Gerishoni, na Kohathu, na Merari.
english: These were the sons of Levi by their names: Gershon, Kohath, and Merari.

kikuyu: Hwaĩ-inĩ, na rũciinĩ, o na mĩaraho, ngayaga ndĩ mĩnyamaro-inĩ, nake nĩaiguaga mũgambo wakwa.
english: Evening, morning, and at noon, I will cry out in distress. He will hear my voice.

kikuyu: Kĩndũ kĩrĩ Thaahu na Gĩtarĩ Thaahu Na rĩrĩ, Afarisai na arutani amwe a watho arĩa moimĩte Jerusalemu makĩũngana harĩ Jesũ na
english: They worship me in vain, teaching as doctrines the commandments of men.’*

kikuyu: “ ‘Rĩrĩa mũkũgetha irio bũrũri-inĩ wanyu, mũtikanagethe nginya ndeere-inĩ cia mĩgũnda yanyu, o na mũtikanonganie rũitĩki rwa magetha manyu.
english: “ ‘When you reap the harvest of your land, you shall not wholly reap the corners of your field, neither shall you gather the gleanings of your harvest.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo mũndũ agathiĩ kũruta wĩra wake, agakĩruta wĩra o nginya hwaĩ-inĩ.
english: Man goes out to his work, to his labor until the evening.

kikuyu: Angĩ moigaga atĩrĩ, “Tũneanaga ithaka ciitũ na mĩgũnda iitũ ya mĩthabibũ, na mĩciĩ iitũ itũrũgamĩrĩre nĩguo tũheo irio hĩndĩ ya ngʼaragu.”
english: Now Tobiah the Ammonite was by him, and he said, “What they are building, if a fox climbed up it, he would break down their stone wall.”

kikuyu: Nĩaikare arĩ wiki akirĩte, nĩgũkorwo nĩ Jehova ũmũigĩrĩire rĩo.
english: Let him sit alone and keep silence, because he has laid it on him.

kikuyu: Jesũ akĩmacookeria atĩrĩ, “Ndamwĩra atĩ nĩ niĩ we. Akorwo nĩ niĩ mũracaria-rĩ, rekei andũ aya mathiĩ.”
english: Jesus answered, “I told you that I am he. If therefore you seek me, let these go their way,”

kikuyu: “Nĩkĩĩgĩgũtũmawonekĩhutikĩrĩakĩrĩriithorĩamũrũwathoguo, noũkaagakũrũmbũiyamũgogo ũrĩa ũrĩ riitho rĩaku we mwene?
english: Why do you see the speck of chaff that is in your brother’s eye, but don’t consider the beam that is in your own eye?

kikuyu: Nayo mĩaka mũgwanja ya bũthi kũu Misiri ĩgĩthira,
english: The seven years of plenty, that were in the land of Egypt, came to an end.

kikuyu: Gũtirĩ ihĩtia o na rĩmwe rĩa marĩa aaneka rĩgaacooka kũririkanwo. Tondũ wa maũndũ ma ũthingu marĩa ekĩte-rĩ, nĩegũtũũra muoyo.
english: None of his transgressions that he has committed will be remembered against him. In his righteousness that he has done, he shall live.

kikuyu: Mũndũ o wothe angĩrĩa maguta ma nyamũ ĩrutĩirwo Jehova iruta rĩa gũcinwo na mwaki, no nginya aingatwo kuuma kũrĩ andũ ao. Na kũrĩa guothe mũgaatũũra-rĩ, mũtikanarĩe thakame ya mũthemba o na ũrĩkũ, ĩrĩ ya nyoni kana ya nyamũ.
english: For whoever eats the fat of the animal which men offer as an offering made by fire to Yahweh, even the soul who eats it shall be cut off from his people.

kikuyu: Ũkoiga atĩrĩ, “Hĩ, kaĩ nĩndathũũrire gũtaarwo-ĩ!
english: and say, “How I have hated instruction, and my heart despised reproof.

kikuyu: Ũhoro ũcio ũgĩtũma atuuo nĩ mũthingu kũrĩ njiarwa na njiarwa nginya tene. Nao makĩrakaria Jehova hau maaĩ-inĩ ma Meriba, nake Musa agĩkinyĩrĩrwo nĩ thĩĩna nĩ ũndũ wao;
english: That was credited to him for righteousness, for all generations to come.

kikuyu: Nĩmũkuona atĩ mũndũ atuagwo mũthingu nĩ ũndũ wa ũrĩa ekaga, no ti ũndũ wa wĩtĩkio wiki.
english: You see then that by works a man is justified, and not only by faith.

kikuyu: “Harũni nĩakarehe ndegwa ĩyo ĩtuĩke ya iruta rĩa mehia make mwene, nĩguo ehoroherie we mwene na nyũmba yake, na nĩagathĩnja ndegwa ĩyo nĩ ũndũ wa iruta rĩa kũhoroherio mehia make mwene.
english: “Aaron shall present the bull of the sin offering, which is for himself, and shall make atonement for himself and for his house, and shall kill the bull of the sin offering which is for himself.

kikuyu: Aburamu agĩcookithia indo ciothe iria ciatahĩtwo, na Loti mũndũ ũcio wao, na indo ciake ciothe, hamwe na andũ-a-nja na andũ arĩa angĩ.
english: In the fourth generation they will come here again, for the iniquity of the Amorite is not yet full.”  The word translated “Lord” is “Adonai”.

kikuyu: Nĩmũkahoorerio ngoro rĩrĩa mũkoona mĩtugo yao na ciĩko ciao, nĩgũkorwo nĩmũkamenya atĩ gũtirĩ ũndũ o na ũmwe njĩkĩte kuo ũtarĩ na gĩtũmi, ũguo nĩguo Mwathani Jehova ekuuga.”
english: Therefore you shall no more see false visions nor practice divination. I will deliver my people out of your hand. Then you will know that I am Yahweh.’ ”

kikuyu: “Ningĩ nĩ kwĩrĩtwo atĩrĩ, ‘Ũrĩa wothe ũngĩtigana na mũtumia wake no nginya amũhe marũa ma kuonanianĩmatigananake.’
english: “It was also said, ‘Whoever shall put away his wife, let him give her a writing of divorce,’*

kikuyu: mũthenya wa kũhuhwo karumbeta na mbugĩrĩrio ya mbaara ya gũũkĩrĩra matũũra marĩa mairigĩirwo na hinya, na gũũkĩrĩra mĩthiringo ĩrĩa mĩraihu na igũrũ.
english: a day of the trumpet and alarm against the fortified cities and against the high battlements.

kikuyu: o na gũtuĩka ndangĩhota kũũrekia na aũigaga kanua gake-rĩ,
english: though he spare it, and will not let it go, but keep it still within his mouth,

kikuyu: Moima Punoni, magĩthiĩ makĩamba hema ciao Obothu.
english: They traveled from Punon, and encamped in Oboth.

kikuyu: Taara mũndũ mũũgĩ na nĩegũkĩrĩrĩria kũũhĩga; mũndũ mũthingu mũrute, na nĩekuongerera ũũgĩ wake.
english: They are all plain to him who understands, right to those who find knowledge.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo o ta ũrĩa mũtuagĩra andũ arĩa angĩ ciira, noguo na inyuĩ mũgaatuĩrwo ciira, na gĩthimi kĩrĩa mũthimanagĩra nakĩo, no kĩo mũrĩthimagĩrwo nakĩo.
english: For with whatever judgment you judge, you will be judged; and with whatever measure you measure, it will be measured to you.

kikuyu: Mũthenya wa mũgwanja, mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai ũcio nĩakarora ũhere ũcio o rĩngĩ, na angĩkorwo ndũthegeete ngoothi-inĩ na ndũkuoneka ũrikĩire gũkĩra ngoothi-rĩ, mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai ũcio nĩagatua atĩ mũndũ ũcio ndarĩ na thaahu. No no nginya athambie nguo ciake, na nĩagatuĩka ndarĩ na thaahu. No, ũhere ũcio, ũngĩthegea ngoothi-inĩ thuutha wa mũndũ ũcio gũtuuo atĩ ndarĩ na thaahu-rĩ,
english: On the seventh day, the priest shall examine the itch; and behold, if the itch hasn’t spread in the skin, and its appearance isn’t deeper than the skin, then the priest shall pronounce him clean. He shall wash his clothes and be clean.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo kĩũmbe kĩmwe gĩa ciũmbe iria inya irĩ muoyo-rĩ, gĩkĩnengera araika acio mũgwanja mbakũri mũgwanja cia thahabu, ciyũrĩtio mangʼũrĩ ma Ngai ũrĩa ũtũũraga muoyo tene na tene.
english: I heard the altar saying, “Yes, Lord God, the Almighty, true and righteous are your judgments.”

kikuyu: Ti atĩ nĩ kĩheo ndĩracaria kuuma kũrĩ inyuĩ, no ndĩracaria kĩrĩa kĩngĩigwo mũthiithũ-inĩ wanyu.
english: Brothers, be imitators together of me, and note those who walk this way, even as you have us for an example.

kikuyu: Tũũrai mwĩhokete Jehova nginya tene, nĩgũkorwo Jehova, o we Jehova, nĩwe Rwaro rwa Ihiga rwa tene na tene.
english: For indeed we who are in this tent do groan, being burdened, not that we desire to be unclothed, but that we desire to be

kikuyu: Mũtikamaathĩkagĩre o hĩndĩ ĩrĩa maramuona atĩ nĩguo mamwendage; no ĩkagai ũguo ta mũrĩ ngombo cia Kristũ, mũgĩĩkaga maũndũ marĩa Ngai endaga mekwo mwĩendeire na ngoro cianyu.
english: not in the way of service only when eyes are on you, as men pleasers, but as servants of Christ, doing the will of God from the heart,

kikuyu: Naguoũhorowakĩonekigĩkĩgĩotheharĩinyuĩũtuĩkĩtekĩndũgĩatũhũ, ũgatuĩkataciugoirĩibuku- inĩ rĩa gĩkũnjo, rĩtumĩtwo na rĩkahũũrwo mũhũũri. Na mũngĩnengera mũndũ ũngĩhota gũthoma ibuku rĩu rĩa gĩkũnjo na mũmwĩre atĩrĩ, “Twagũthaitha, tũthomere ibuku rĩĩrĩ,” nake akaamũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Ndingĩhota gũthoma tondũ nĩitume na rĩkahũũrwo mũhũũri.”
english: For who among men knows the things of a man except the spirit of the man which is in him? Even so, no one knows the things of God except God’s Spirit.

kikuyu: Makinya makwa nĩmarũmĩtie njĩra ciaku; magũrũ makwa matirĩ matenderũka.
english: My steps have held fast to your paths. My feet have not slipped.

kikuyu: Othe nĩ itharia, maakanaga ta riiko rĩa kũrugĩra mĩgate, rĩrĩa mũrugi wa mĩgate atabataragio nĩ gwakĩrĩria kuuma gũkanda mũtu nginya ũimbe.
english: They are all adulterers. They are burning like an oven that the baker stops stirring, from the kneading of the dough, until it is leavened.

kikuyu: Athamaki acio nĩ Sihoni wa Aamori, ũrĩa wathamakaga Heshiboni. Aathanaga kuuma itũũra rĩa Aroeri rĩrĩa rĩrĩ rũteere-inĩ rwa mũkuru wa Arinoni, o na kuuma itũũra rĩrĩa rĩarĩ gatagatĩ ka mũkuru ũcio o na nuthu ya Gileadi nginya Rũũĩ rwa Jaboku, rũrĩa rũrĩ mũhaka-inĩ wa Aamoni.
english: Sihon king of the Amorites, who lived in Heshbon, and ruled from Aroer, which is on the edge of the valley of the Arnon, and the middle of the valley, and half Gilead, even to the river Jabbok, the border of the children of Ammon;

kikuyu: no ciandĩkirwo nĩ ũndũ witũ, ithuĩ arĩa Ngai agaatua athingu, o ithuĩ arĩa twĩtĩkĩtie Ngai ũrĩa wariũkirie Jesũ Mwathani witũ kuuma kũrĩ arĩa akuũ.
english: but for our sake also, to whom it will be accounted, who believe in him who raised Jesus our Lord from the dead,

kikuyu: Ndagũthaitha ndũkae kuuma haha nginya njooke na ndeehe iruta rĩakwa na ndĩrĩige mbere yaku.” Nake Jehova akiuga atĩrĩ, “Nĩngweterera nginya ũcooke.”
english: When I blow the trumpet, I and all who are with me, then blow the trumpets also on every side of all the camp, and shout, ‘For Yahweh and for Gideon!’ ”

kikuyu: “Nĩnjiguĩte maũndũ maingĩ ta macio; inyuothe mũrĩ ahoorerania a kũnyamarania!
english: “I have heard many such things. You are all miserable comforters!

kikuyu: Ndũkanagerere njĩra ya andũ arĩa aaganu, kana ũthiĩre njĩra ya andũ arĩa ooru.
english: knowing that the putting off of my tent comes swiftly, even as our Lord Jesus Christ made clear to me.

kikuyu: Irathimo cia thoguo nĩ makĩria ma irathimo cia irĩma iria cia tene, na nĩ makĩria ma ũtonga wa tũrĩma tũrĩa tũkũrũ. Ici ciothe nĩirekwo ihurũke mũtwe-inĩ wa Jusufu,
english: So Joseph died, being one hundred ten years old, and they embalmed him, and he was put in a coffin in Egypt.

kikuyu: Ngoro ĩrĩ na thayũ ĩheaga mwĩrĩ muoyo, no ũiru ũbuthagia mahĩndĩ.
english: The life of the body is a heart at peace, but envy rots the bones.

kikuyu: Ũrĩ thĩinĩ wa ũnene waku thiĩ ũhaicĩte mbarathi, ũkĩhootanaga nĩ ũndũ wa ũhoro wa ma, na wa kwĩnyiihia, o na wa ũthingu; reke guoko gwaku kwa ũrĩo kuonanie ciĩko cia kũmakania.
english: In your majesty ride on victoriously on behalf of truth, humility, and righteousness. Let your right hand display awesome deeds.

kikuyu: Nĩũthingithĩtie bũrũri na ũkawatũra; thinga mĩatũka yaguo, nĩgũkorwo nĩũrenyenya.
english: From the end of the earth, I will call to you when my heart is overwhelmed. Lead me to the rock that is higher than I.

kikuyu: Mũthenya ũcio, Joshua agĩtunyana itũũra rĩa Makeda; akĩniina mũthamaki na itũũra rĩu na rũhiũ rwanjora, naakĩniinaandũothe arĩa maarĩ thĩinĩwarĩobiũ. Ndaatigiriemũndũo naũmwe arĩ muoyo; na agĩĩka mũthamaki wa Makeda o ta ũrĩa eekire mũthamaki wa Jeriko. Gũtoorio kwa Matũũra ma Mwena wa Gũthini Ningĩ Joshua na Isiraeli rĩothe arĩa maarĩ hamwe nake makiuma kũu Makeda magĩthiĩ nginya itũũra rĩa Libina na makĩrĩtharĩkĩra. Nake Jehova akĩneana itũũra rĩu o narĩo na mũthamaki warĩo
english: Joshua took Makkedah on that day, and struck it with the edge of the sword, with its king. He utterly destroyed it and all the souls who were in it. He left no one remaining. He did to the king of Makkedah as he had done to the king of Jericho.

kikuyu: Jeshua nĩwe warĩ ithe wa Joiakimu, nake Joiakimu aarĩ ithe wa Eliashibu, nake Eliashibu aarĩ ithe wa Joiada,
english: Of the priests: Jedaiah the son of Joiarib, Jachin,

kikuyu: Nacio ikĩĩrwo itikeehie nyeki ya thĩ, kana mũmera o wothe o na kana mũtĩ, o tiga andũ arĩa matarĩ na rũũri rwa Ngai ithiithi ciao.
english: These are the two olive trees and the two lamp stands, standing before the Lord of the earth.

kikuyu: na mũthamaki wa Afeku, ũmwe, na mũthamaki wa Lasharoni, ũmwe,
english: the king of Aphek, one; the king of Lassharon, one;

kikuyu: Gwĩtigĩra Jehova nĩ ũndũ mũtheru, gũtũũraga nginya tene. Matua ma Jehova nĩ ma ma, na mothe nĩ ma kĩhooto.
english: You will make them as a fiery furnace in the time of your anger. Yahweh will swallow them up in his wrath. The fire shall devour them.

kikuyu: Atĩrĩrĩ, wee bũrũri, bũrũri, bũrũri ũyũ, ta thikĩrĩria ndũmĩrĩri ya Jehova!
english: O earth, earth, earth, hear Yahweh’s word!

kikuyu: Nĩmacookereire mehia ma maithe mao ma tene, arĩa maaregire kũigua ndeto ciakwa. Nĩmarũmĩrĩire ngai ingĩ nĩgeetha macitungatĩre. Nyũmba cierĩ, ya Isiraeli na ya Juda, nĩithũkĩtie kĩrĩkanĩro kĩrĩa ndaarĩkanĩire na maithe mao ma tene. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, Jehova ekuuga atĩrĩ: ‘Nĩngũmarehithĩria mwanangĩko matangĩhota kũũrĩra. O na mangĩkaangaĩra, ndikamathikĩrĩria.
english: But Yahweh is the true God. He is the living God, and an everlasting King. At his wrath, the earth trembles. The nations aren’t able to withstand his indignation.

kikuyu: Nawe Pashuri, na arĩa othe matũũraga gwaku nyũmba, nĩ mũgaatahwo mũtwarwo Babuloni. Kũu nĩkuo ũgaakuĩra na ũthikwo o kuo, wee hamwe na arata aku othe arĩa ũrathagĩra ũhoro wa maheeni.’ ” Mateta ma Jeremia
english: You, Pashhur, and all who dwell in your house will go into captivity. You will come to Babylon, and there you will die,  “Magormissabib” means “surrounded by terror”

kikuyu: Moima Zalimona, magĩthiĩ makĩamba hema ciao Punoni.
english: They traveled from Zalmonah, and encamped in Punon.

kikuyu: Maitho ma Jehova nĩmamenyagĩrĩra ũmenyo, no nĩathaahagia ciugo cia ũrĩa ũtarĩ mwĩhokeku.
english: Yahweh’s eyes watch over knowledge, but he frustrates the words of the unfaithful.

kikuyu: Ciarĩkia kũmwamba mũtĩ- igũrũ, cikĩgayana nguo ciake na njĩra ya gũcicuukĩra mĩtĩ.
english: Peter said to him, “Even if I must die with you, I will not deny you.” All of the disciples also said likewise.

kikuyu: Ciana ciake irotuĩka njara-rũhĩ cia gũcangacanga; iroingatwo ciume mĩciĩ yao ĩyo mĩanangĩku.
english: The fear of Yahweh is the beginning of wisdom. All those who do his work have a good understanding. His praise endures forever!

kikuyu: Wee Jehova, kaĩ mawĩra maku nĩ maingĩ-ĩ! Mothe ũmarutĩte na ũũgĩ; nayo thĩ ĩiyũrĩte ciũmbe ciaku.
english: Yahweh, how many are your works! In wisdom, you have made them all. The earth is full of your riches.

kikuyu: “Na rĩrĩ, roho mũũru woima thĩinĩ wa mũndũ, ũtuĩkanagĩria werũ-inĩ ũgĩcaria ũhurũko, na ũkaaga.
english: “When an unclean spirit has gone out of a man, he passes through waterless places seeking rest, and doesn’t find it.

kikuyu: Ĩĩ, ti-itherũ Jehova, nĩwe twetagĩrĩra o tũgĩthiiagĩra njĩra ya mawatho maku; ngoro ciitũ ciĩriragĩria rĩĩtwa rĩaku na ngumo yaku.
english: We are pressed on every side, yet not crushed; perplexed, yet not to despair;

kikuyu: Balamu agĩcookeria Ngai atĩrĩ, “Balaki mũrũ wa Ziporu, mũthamaki wa Moabi, nĩwe ũrandeheire ndũmĩrĩri ĩno:
english: The children of Israel traveled, and encamped in Oboth.

kikuyu: Ayahudi maigua ũguo makĩambĩrĩria kũngʼongʼora nĩ ũndũ wake tondũ nĩoigĩte atĩrĩ, “Niĩ nĩ niĩ mũgate ũrĩa waikũrũkire kuuma igũrũ.”
english: The Jews therefore murmured concerning him, because he said, “I am the bread which came down out of heaven.”

kikuyu: Igai rĩu rĩao rĩarĩ na matũũra maya: Birishiba (kana Shiba), na Molada,
english: They had for their inheritance Beersheba (or Sheba), Moladah,

kikuyu: Aatoonyire nyũmba ya Ngai, na arĩ hamwe na arĩa maarĩ nake, makĩrĩa mĩgate ĩrĩa yamũrĩirwo Ngai ĩrĩa mateetĩkĩrĩtio kũrĩa nĩ watho, tiga no athĩnjĩri-Ngai meetĩkĩrĩtio kũmĩrĩa.
english: how he entered into God’s house and ate the show bread, which was not lawful for him to eat, nor for those who were with him, but only for the priests?*

kikuyu: kũu nja-inĩ cia nyũmba ya Jehova, o thĩinĩ waku, wee Jerusalemu. Goocai Jehova.
english: namely, that God was in Christ reconciling the world to himself, not reckoning to them their trespasses, and having committed to us the word of reconciliation.

kikuyu: Ciathiũrũrũkĩirie na mĩena yothe ta njũkĩ, no ikĩhora narua o ta mwaki wa mĩigua; tondũ wa kwĩhoka rĩĩtwa rĩa Jehova ngĩciananga.
english: For we are not commending ourselves to you again, but speak as giving you occasion of boasting on our behalf, that you may have something to answer those who boast in appearance and not in heart.

kikuyu: Maaĩ macio makĩambatĩra mũno igũrũ wa thĩ, nacio irĩma ciothe iria ndaaya na igũrũ gũkũ mũhuro wa matu guothe ikĩhumbĩrwo nĩmo.
english: Of every living thing of all flesh, you shall bring two of every sort into the ship, to keep them alive with you. They shall be male and female.

kikuyu: Ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ na ma: Harĩ andũ arĩa manaciarwo nĩ andũ-a-nja, gũtirĩ kuoneka mũndũ mũnene gũkĩra Johana Mũbatithania; no rĩrĩ mũndũ ũrĩa mũnini mũno ũthamaki-inĩ wa igũrũ-rĩ, nĩ mũnene kũmũkĩra.
english: Most certainly I tell you, among those who are born of women there has not arisen anyone greater than John the Baptizer; yet he who is least in the Kingdom of Heaven is greater than he.

kikuyu: Mũrogĩa na wega na thayũ iria ciumĩte kũrĩ Ngai Ithe witũ, o na kũrĩ Mwathani Jesũ Kristũ,
english: But not even Titus, who was with me, being a Greek, was compelled to be circumcised.

kikuyu: Maitho makwa nĩmaraaga hinya nĩ gũtũũra njũthĩrĩirie kĩĩranĩro gĩaku; ngakĩũria atĩrĩ, “Ũgaakĩĩhooreria rĩ?”
english: My eyes fail for your word. I say, “When will you comfort me?”

kikuyu: “Ndaagire gũkua ngĩciarwo nĩkĩ? Ndaagire gũkua ngiuma nda nĩkĩ?
english: The old lion perishes for lack of prey. The cubs of the lioness are scattered abroad.

kikuyu: Matukũ ma mũndũ nĩ matare; nĩũtuĩte mũigana wa mĩeri yake, na nĩũmũigĩire mĩhaka ĩrĩa ataangĩkĩra. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio tiga kũmũrora, tigana nake, nginya arĩĩkie mĩthenya yake ta mũthũkũmi.
english: For your iniquity teaches your mouth, and you choose the language of the crafty.

kikuyu: “Mũrarĩra mũkooria atĩrĩ, ‘Wee, rũhiũ rwa njora rwa Jehova-rĩ, ũgũthiĩ na mbere nginya rĩ ũngĩkaahurũka?
english: “ ‘You sword of Yahweh, how long will it be before you are quiet? Put yourself back into your scabbard; rest, and be still.’

kikuyu: “ ‘Gĩcunjĩ gĩa ikũmi kĩa indo ciothe cia kuuma mũgũnda, arĩ ngano ya mũgũnda kana maciaro ma mĩtĩ, nĩ kĩa Jehova; gĩcunjĩ kĩu kĩamũrĩirwo Jehova.
english: “ ‘All the tithe of the land, whether of the seed of the land or of the fruit of the trees, is Yahweh’s. It is holy to Yahweh.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo andũ a thiritũ ya arĩa matooĩ Ngai matũũraga marĩ thaata, na mwaki nĩũgaacina hema cia arĩa mendete mahaki.
english: For the company of the godless will be barren, and fire will consume the tents of bribery.

kikuyu: “Arĩria andũ a Isiraeli, ũmeere atĩrĩ: ‘Mũndũ mũrũme kana mũndũ-wa-nja angĩenda kwĩhĩta mwĩhĩtwa wa mwanya, ũrĩ mwĩhĩtwa wa kwĩyamũrĩra Jehova atuĩke Mũnaziri,*
english: “Speak to the children of Israel, and tell them: ‘When either man or woman shall make a special vow, the vow of a Nazirite, to separate himself to Yahweh,

kikuyu: Ndĩciiragia ũhoro ũyũ ngamaka mũno; ngethithimũkwo nĩ mwĩrĩ, ngambĩrĩria kũinaina.
english: When I remember, I am troubled. Horror takes hold of my flesh.

kikuyu: Mũtikambate tondũ Jehova ndarĩ hamwe na inyuĩ. Nĩmũkũhootwo nĩ thũ cianyu,
english: Don’t go up, for Yahweh isn’t among you; that way you won’t be struck down before your enemies.

kikuyu: Thĩ na merirĩria mayo nĩithiraga, no mũndũ ũrĩa wĩkaga ũrĩa Ngai endaga atũũraga nginya tene. Kwĩmenyerera Mũmena-Kristũ
english: The world is passing away with its lusts, but he who does God’s will remains forever.

kikuyu: Kũrathimwo-rĩ, nĩ mũndũ ũrĩa wĩkaga maũndũ macio, o mũndũ ũrĩa ũrũmagia maũndũ macio mũno, na ũrĩa ũmenyagĩrĩra thabatũ atekũmĩthaahia, na akagirĩrĩria guoko gwake gwĩka ũũru.”
english: Blessed is the man who does this, and the son of man who holds it fast; who keeps the Sabbath without profaning it and keeps his hand from doing any evil.”

kikuyu: Mũndũ angĩrathima mũndũ wa itũũra rĩake rũciinĩ tene anĩrĩire-rĩ, ũndũ ũcio ũngĩtuĩka taarĩ kĩrumi.
english: Blessed is the man who always fears; but one who hardens his heart falls into trouble.

kikuyu: “Ngirĩrĩria hanini, na nĩngũkuonia atĩ harĩ maũndũ mangĩ mangiugwo ma gũciirĩrĩra Ngai.
english: “Bear with me a little, and I will show you; for I still have something to say on God’s behalf.

kikuyu: Agĩcooka agĩturia ndu, akĩanĩrĩra, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Mwathani, ndũkamoorie rĩĩhia rĩĩrĩ.” Na aarĩkia kuuga ũguo, agĩkoma.
english: He kneeled down and cried with a loud voice, “Lord, don’t hold this sin against them!” When he had said this, he fell asleep.

kikuyu: Baranaba na Paũlũ Gũtũmwo Na rĩrĩ, kanitha-inĩ wa Antiokia nĩ kwarĩ na anabii na arutani: nao nĩo Baranaba, na Simeoni ũrĩa wetagwo na rĩĩtwa rĩngĩ Nigeri, na Lukio ũrĩa woimĩte Kurene, na Manaeni (ũrĩa wareranĩirio
english: Now Paul and his company set sail from Paphos and came to Perga in Pamphylia. John departed from them and returned to Jerusalem.

kikuyu: Ũũgĩ nĩ ũikaraga ngoro-inĩ ya ũrĩa ũũĩ gũkũũrana maũndũ, o na gatagatĩ ka irimũ nĩwĩmenyithanagia.
english: Wisdom rests in the heart of one who has understanding, and is even made known in the inward part of fools.

kikuyu: O rĩmwe akĩambĩrĩria kuona, nake akĩrũmĩrĩra Jesũ, akĩgoocaga Ngai. Rĩrĩa andũ othe moonire ũguo, o nao makĩgooca Ngai.
english: Immediately he received his sight and followed him, glorifying God. All the people, when they saw it, praised God.

kikuyu: Makĩria ya macio mothe, rangagĩra ngoro yaku, nĩgũkorwo nĩyo gĩthima kĩrĩa gĩtherũkaga muoyo.
english: Keep your heart with all diligence, for out of it is the wellspring of life.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ciothe makoragwo marĩ ataana, na makombanagĩra matekwenda uumithio; ciana ciao nĩ ikaarathimwo.
english: All day long he deals graciously, and lends. His offspring is blessed.

kikuyu: Moherere ngoro-inĩ yaku nginya tene; makundĩke marigiicĩrie ngingo yaku. Rĩrĩa ũgũthiĩ-rĩ, nĩmarĩgũtongoragia; wakoma-rĩ, nĩmarĩkũmenyagĩrĩra; na rĩrĩa warahũka-rĩ, nĩmarĩkwaragĩria.
english: With persuasive words, she led him astray. With the flattering of her lips, she seduced him.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩmwe tũgĩthiĩ harĩa haahooyagĩrwo Ngai, nĩtwatũngirwo nĩ mũirĩtu warĩ ngombo na nĩarĩ na roho wa kũragũra. Nake nĩatũmaga ene we meonere mbeeca nyingĩ mũno nĩ ũndũ wa ũragũri wake
english: Now while Paul waited for them at Athens, his spirit was provoked within him as he saw the city full of idols.  TR reads “And the Jews who were unpersuaded, becoming envious and taking along” instead of “But the unpersuaded Jews took along”  The word for “brothers” here and where the context allows may be also correctly translated “brothers and sisters” or “siblings.”

kikuyu: Andũ arĩa makũũrĩtwo nĩ Jehova nĩmakiuge ũguo: o acio akũũrĩte kuuma guoko-inĩ gwa thũ,
english: Let the redeemed by Yahweh say so, whom he has redeemed from the hand of the adversary,

kikuyu: Jehova ekuuga atĩrĩ: “Mũndũ ũrĩa mũũgĩ-rĩ, nĩatige kwĩgaatha nĩ ũndũ wa ũũgĩ wake, kana mũndũ ũrĩa ũrĩ na hinya egaathe nĩ ũndũ wa hinya ũrĩa arĩ naguo, o na kana gĩtonga kĩĩgathe nĩ ũndũ wa ũtonga wakĩo,
english: Yahweh says, “Don’t let the wise man glory in his wisdom. Don’t let the mighty man glory in his might. Don’t let the rich man glory in his riches.

kikuyu: “Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, ũũ nĩguo ngũgwĩka wee Isiraeli, na tondũ nĩngũgwĩka ũguo-rĩ, wĩhaarĩrie gũtũngana na Ngai waku, wee Isiraeli.”
english: For I know how many are your offenses, and how great are your sins— you who afflict the just, who take a bribe, and who turn away the needy in the courts.

kikuyu: Atũmaga magũrũ makwa magĩe ihenya ta ma thwariga; aahotithagia kũrũgama kũndũ kũrĩa kũraihu.
english: He makes my feet like deer’s feet, and sets me on my high places.

kikuyu: “No rĩrĩ, ndũkareke mũndũ arehe thitango, mũndũ ndagathitange ũrĩa ũngĩ, nĩgũkorwo andũ anyu mahaana andũ arĩa mathitangaga mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai.
english: Don’t seek preeminence from the Lord, nor the seat of honor from the king.

kikuyu: Jehova, o we Ngai ũcio Mwene-Hinya, nĩaraaria, ageeta thĩ yothe kuuma irathĩro rĩa riũa nginya kũrĩa rĩthũagĩra.
english: The Mighty One, God, Yahweh, speaks, and calls the earth from sunrise to sunset.

kikuyu: Ũthingu nĩgũtũũgĩria ũtũũgagĩria rũrĩrĩ, no mehia nĩ gĩconoko harĩ andũ o othe.
english: Righteousness exalts a nation, but sin is a disgrace to any people.

kikuyu: “Mathagu ma nyaga mabatabataga nĩ gũkena, no matingĩgerekanio na mathagu na njoya cia njũũ.
english: “The wings of the ostrich wave proudly, but are they the feathers and plumage of love?

kikuyu: no Firaũni na ita rĩake akĩmahubĩkanĩria kũu Iria-inĩ Itune; Wendo wake nĩ wa tene na tene.
english: but overthrew Pharaoh and his army in the Red Sea, for his loving kindness endures forever;

kikuyu: Sisera akiuga atĩrĩ, “Ndĩ mũnyootu, ndagũthaitha he maaĩ nyue.” Jaeli agĩkunũra mbũthũ ya iria, akĩmũhe anyue, agĩcooka akĩmũhumbĩra.
english: He said to her, “Please give me a little water to drink; for I am thirsty.” She opened a container of milk, and gave him a drink, and covered him.

kikuyu: Tondũ ũcio makĩambata magĩthiĩ kũrĩ mũnene wa nyũmba ya Jusufu, makĩmwarĩria marĩ hau mũromo-inĩ wa nyũmba.
english: If you are honest men, then let one of your brothers be bound in your prison; but you go, carry grain for the famine of your houses.

kikuyu: Jesũ agĩcookia atĩrĩ, “Rĩathani rĩrĩa rĩa bata mũno nĩ rĩĩrĩ: ‘Thikĩrĩria, inyuĩ Isiraeli, Mwathani Ngai witũ, Mwathani nĩ ũmwe.
english: Jesus answered, “The greatest is: ‘Hear, Israel, the Lord our God, the Lord is one.

kikuyu: Tondũ rĩrĩa wee mũndũ ũyũ, ũtuagĩra arĩa angĩ ciira na ũgacooka ũgeeka maũndũ o macio mekaga-rĩ, wee ũgwĩciiria nĩũkahonoka ciira wa Ngai?
english: Do you think this, O man who judges those who practice such things, and do the same, that you will escape the judgment of God?

kikuyu: nĩguo kũngĩhoteka, ngaariũkio nyume kũrĩ arĩa akuũ. Kwĩrutanĩria Gũkinya Mũthia
english: and that every tongue should confess that Jesus Christ is Lord, to the glory of God the Father.

kikuyu: “Hĩndĩ ĩyo Moabi nĩagaconoka nĩ ũndũ wa ngai ĩrĩa ĩtagwo Kemoshu, o ta ũrĩa nyũmba ya Isiraeli yaconokire, rĩrĩa meehokete Betheli.
english: For I have sworn by myself,” says Yahweh, “that Bozrah will become an astonishment, a reproach, a waste, and a curse. All its cities will be perpetual wastes.”

kikuyu: Jesũ Kũhonia Mũndũ Wakuĩte Ciĩga Nake agĩtoonya gatarũ, akĩringa iria, agĩkinya itũũra rĩa kwao.
english: 1 He entered into a boat and crossed over, and came into his own city.

kikuyu: “Inyuĩ andũ aya, mũkoragwo mũkiuga atĩa rĩrĩa mũkwaria thimo ĩno igũrũ rĩa bũrũri wa Isiraeli: * 17:10 Rũrũ rwarĩ rũhuho rwa mwena wa irathĩro, na rwakoragwo rũrĩ rũhiũ; nĩruomagia irio irĩ mĩgũnda na ruoĩkaine ta “Kahamsini”. Maandĩko-inĩ rwarũgamĩrĩire mbũtũ cia Babuloni.
english: “What do you mean, that you use this proverb concerning the land of Israel, saying, ‘The fathers have eaten sour grapes, and the children’s teeth are set on edge’?  or, seed

kikuyu: “Mũtigathũkie thĩ, kana iria, o na kana mĩtĩ, nginya tũkorwo tũrĩkĩtie gwĩkĩra ndungata cia Ngai witũ rũũri ithiithi-inĩ ciao.” Ningĩ ngĩigua mũigana wa arĩa meekĩrirwo rũũri, nao maarĩ: 144,000 kuuma mĩhĩrĩga yothe ya Isiraeli.
english: Another angel came and stood over the altar, having a golden censer. Much incense was given to him, that he should add it to the prayers of all the saints on the golden altar which was before the throne.

kikuyu: Mũthiro o ta ũcio nĩũkaniina mbarathi na nyũmbũ, na ngamĩĩra na ndigiri, na nyamũ ciothe iria igaakorwo kambĩ-inĩ icio. Ningĩ-rĩ, matigari ma ndũrĩrĩ icio ciothe iria cianatharĩkĩra Jerusalemu makaambataga mwaka o mwaka kũhooya Mũthamaki, o we Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe, na gũkũngũĩra Gĩathĩ gĩa Ithũnũ.
english: A plague like this will fall on the horse, on the mule, on the camel, on the donkey, and on all the animals that will be in those camps.

kikuyu: Mwana mũkĩĩgu arehagĩra ithe kĩeha, na marũrũ kũrĩ nyina ũrĩa wamũciarire.
english: A foolish son brings grief to his father, and bitterness to her who bore him.

kikuyu: Ngoro ciao nĩ cia maheeni, na rĩu no nginya marĩhio wĩhia wao. Jehova nĩakamomora igongona ciao, na athũkangie mahiga macio mao maamũre ma kũhooyagwo. Ningĩ nĩmakoiga atĩrĩ, “Tũtirĩ na mũthamaki, tondũ tũtiigana gũtĩĩa Jehova. No o na korwo twarĩ na mũthamaki-rĩ, nĩ atĩa angĩahotire gũtwĩkĩra?”
english: Their heart is divided. Now they will be found guilty. He will demolish their altars. He will destroy their sacred stones.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo Maandĩko marĩa matheru moigĩte atĩa? “Iburahĩmu nĩetĩkirie Ngai, naguo ũndũ ũcio ũgĩtũma atuuo mũthingu.”
english: For what does the Scripture say? “Abraham believed God, and it was accounted to him for righteousness.”*

kikuyu: Nacio thigari igĩtuma thũmbĩ ya mĩigua, ikĩmwĩkĩra mũtwe, na ikĩmũhumba kanjũ ya rangi wa ndathi, 3igathiiaga harĩ we o ihinda kwa ihinda, ikamũnyũrũragia ikiugaga atĩrĩ, “Ngeithi, mũthamaki wa Ayahudi!” Nacio ikamũgũthaga ũthiũ na hĩ.
english: The soldiers twisted thorns into a crown and put it on his head, and dressed him in a purple garment.

kikuyu: Nĩngangʼaũrania itĩ cia ũnene, na hehenje hinya wa mothamaki ma thĩ ngʼeni. Nĩngakurumania ngaari cia ita, na atwarithia a cio; na ndungumanie mbarathi hamwe na ahaici a cio, o mũndũ arũndwo na rũhiũ rwa njora rwa mũrũ wa nyina.
english: I will overthrow the throne of kingdoms. I will destroy the strength of the kingdoms of the nations. I will overthrow the chariots and those who ride in them. The horses and their riders will come down, everyone by the sword of his brother.

kikuyu: “Atĩrĩrĩ, nĩngamũtũmĩra mũnabii Elija mbere ya mũthenya ũcio mũnene na wa kũmakania wa Jehova ũtanakinya.
english: Now your judgments are many and true, that you should deal with me according to my sins and the sins of my fathers, because we didn’t keep your commandments, for we didn’t walk in truth before you.

kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũtarĩ ũũgĩ ndangĩmamenya, mũndũ mũkĩĩgu ndangĩkũũrana ũndũ ũyũ,
english: They kill the widow and the alien, and murder the fatherless.

kikuyu: Roho akĩĩra Filipu atĩrĩ, “Thiĩ ngaari-inĩ ĩĩrĩa ya mbarathi ũikare hakuhĩ nayo.”
english: The Spirit said to Philip, “Go near, and join yourself to this chariot.”

kikuyu: Nĩngeheria marĩĩtwa ma Baali kuuma mĩromo-inĩ yake; marĩĩtwa macio matikagwetwo rĩngĩ.
english: Humble your soul greatly, for the punishment of the ungodly man is fire and the worm.

kikuyu: nĩ ũndũ ũcio ngĩkĩgũra mũgũnda ũcio warĩ Anathothu kuuma kũrĩ Hanameli mũrũ wa mama, na ngĩmũthimĩra cekeri ikũmi na mũgwanja† cia betha.
english: I bought the field that was in Anathoth of Hanamel my uncle’s son, and weighed

kikuyu: o na angĩhĩngwo, ndangĩgũa, nĩgũkorwo Jehova amũnyiitagĩrĩra na guoko gwake.
english: Though he stumble, he shall not fall, for Yahweh holds him up with his hand.

kikuyu: Niĩ ndĩ na hinya wa gwĩteithia, kuona atĩ rĩu nĩndunyĩtwo ũhootani?
english: He takes the wise in their own craftiness; the counsel of the cunning is carried headlong.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo arũgamaga guoko-inĩ kwa ũrĩo kwa mũndũ ũrĩa mũbatari, nĩguo ahonokagie muoyo wake harĩ andũ arĩa mangĩmũtua nĩ mwĩhia.
english: For he will stand at the right hand of the needy, to save him from those who judge his soul.

kikuyu: nĩgeetha ngoro yakwa ĩkũinĩre na ndĩgakire. Wee Jehova Ngai wakwa, ngũtũũra ngũcookagĩria ngaatho nginya tene.
english: I am forgotten from their hearts like a dead man. I am like broken pottery.

kikuyu: kuuma ariũ a Jusufu: kuuma Efiraimu, nĩ Elishama mũrũ wa Amihudu; kuuma Manase, nĩ Gamalieli mũrũ wa Pedazuru;
english: He shall not alter it, nor exchange it, a good for a bad, or a bad for a good. If he shall at all exchange animal for animal, then both it and that for which it is exchanged shall be holy.

kikuyu: Atĩrĩrĩ Wee Jehova, ithuĩ na athamaki aitũ, na anene aitũ na maithe maitũ tũiyũrĩtwo nĩ thoni nĩ ũndũ wa mehia marĩa tũkwĩhĩirie.
english: 1 In the third year of the reign of King Belshazzar, a vision appeared to me, even to me, Daniel, after that which appeared to me at the first.

kikuyu: “Nĩnjũũĩ nĩakariũka hĩndĩ ya iriũkĩro mũthenya wa mũthia.” Nake Jesũ akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Niĩ nĩ niĩ kũriũka na nĩ niĩ muoyo. Nake ũrĩa wothe ũnjĩtĩkĩtie, o na aakua, nĩagatũũra muoyo;
english: Most certainly I tell you, unless a grain of wheat falls into the earth and dies, it remains by itself alone. But if it dies, it bears much fruit.

kikuyu: Musa akĩĩra Harũni atĩrĩ, “Ũka kĩgongona-inĩ ũrute igongona rĩaku rĩa kũhoroherio mehia na iruta rĩaku rĩa njino, wĩhoroherie wee mwene na ũhoroherie andũ aya; ũcooke ũrute igongona rĩa indo iria andũ marehete ũmahoroherie, o ta ũrĩa Jehova aathanĩte.” Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Harũni agĩũka kĩgongona-inĩ, agĩthĩnja gacaũ ka iruta rĩa kũhoroherio mehia, nĩ ũndũ wake mwene.
english: Moses said to Aaron, “Draw near to the altar, and offer your sin offering, and your burnt offering, and make atonement for yourself, and for the people; and offer the offering of the people, and make atonement for them, as Yahweh commanded.”

kikuyu: “Ndawa nini tu ya kũimbia mĩgate nĩĩtũmaga mũtu ũrĩa mũkande nayo wothe ũimbe.”
english: Let’s not be weary in doing good, for we will reap in due season if we don’t give up.

kikuyu: Ngũmũtũũria muoyo matukũ maingĩ nginya aiganie, na ndĩmuonagie ũhonokio wakwa.”
english: I will satisfy him with long life, and show him my salvation.”

kikuyu: “Jusufu nĩ mũthabibũ ũrĩa ũciaraga, mũthabibũ ũrĩa ũciaraga ũrĩ hakuhĩ na gĩthima, ũrĩa honge ciaguo itambagĩrĩra rũthingo.
english: Joseph lived in Egypt, he, and his father’s house. Joseph lived one hundred ten years.

kikuyu: Ngũ iria ciatigara agathondeka ngai, mũhianano wake wa kũhooya; akaũinamĩrĩra na akaũturĩria maru. Akaũhooya, akoiga atĩrĩ, “Honokia; wee nĩwe ngai yakwa.”
english: The rest of it he makes into a god, even his engraved image. He bows down to it and worships, and prays to it, and says, “Deliver me, for you are my god!”

kikuyu: na rĩrĩ, o hĩndĩ ĩyo rũhuho rũnene rwahurutana ruumĩte werũ-inĩ, na ruo rwahũũra nyũmba ĩyo koine ciayo inya, yamomoka, yamag- wĩra maakua; na no niĩ nyiki ndahonoka, ndagĩũka gũkũrehere ũhoro!” Nake Ayubu aigua-ũhoro ũcio, akĩrũgama, agĩtembũranga nguo yake ya igũrũ, na akĩenjwo mũtwe. Agĩcooka akĩĩgũithia thĩ, akĩhooya Ngai,
english: and behold, there came a great wind from the wilderness, and struck the four corners of the house, and it fell on the young men, and they are dead. I alone have escaped to tell you.”

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo kũrĩ mwĩrĩ o ũmwe na Roho o ũmwe, o ta ũrĩa mwetĩirwo kĩĩrĩgĩrĩro o kĩmwe rĩrĩa mwetirwo, 5nake Mwathani no ũmwe, na wĩtĩkio ũmwe, na ũbatithio o ũmwe;
english: There is one body and one Spirit, even as you also were called in one hope of your calling,

kikuyu: Igwatanĩtio hamwe irĩ nũmu; inyiitanĩte hamwe ũndũ itangĩtigithũkana.
english: They are joined to one another. They stick together, so that they can’t be pulled apart.

kikuyu: ũmeere atĩrĩ, ‘Jehova Mwene- Hinya-Wothe ekuuga atĩrĩ: Nĩngahehenja rũrĩrĩ rũrũ, o na itũũra rĩĩrĩ, o ta ũrĩa mũndũ oragaga nyũngũ ya mũũmbi na ndĩcookaga gũthondekeka ĩtuĩke ngʼima rĩngĩ. Magaathika arĩa makuĩte kũu Tofethi nginya kwage handũ ha gũthikanwo.
english: and shall tell them, ‘Yahweh of Armies says: “Even so I will break this people and this city as one breaks a potter’s vessel, that can’t be made whole again. They will bury in Topheth until there is no place to bury.

kikuyu: Wee Jehova, nĩndakũhooya hĩndĩ ĩrĩa ya gwĩtĩkĩrĩka nĩwe; Wee Ngai, nĩ ũndũ wa wendo waku mũnene, ũnjĩtĩke na ũhonokio waku wa ma.
english: Let my accusers be disappointed and consumed. Let them be covered with disgrace and scorn who want to harm me.

kikuyu: Andũ makĩrĩa irio cia araika; akĩmahe irio iria ciothe mangĩahotire kũrĩa.
english: Man ate the bread of angels. He sent them food to the full.

kikuyu: Ũguo nĩguo Ngai aarĩhirie Abimeleku nĩ ũndũ wa waganu ũrĩa ekĩte ithe, wa kũũraga ariũ-a-ithe mĩrongo mũgwanja. Ningĩ Ngai akĩrĩhia andũ a Shekemu nĩ ũndũ wa waganu wao wothe. Kĩrumi kĩa Jothamu mũrũ wa Jerubu-Baali gĩkĩmacookerera.
english: Thus God repaid the wickedness of Abimelech, which he did to his father in killing his seventy brothers;

kikuyu: Nũũ ũngĩmenya kana Ngai aahota kwĩricũkwo, aigue tha atigane na marakara make mahiũ nĩgeetha tũtikaniinwo.”
english: God said to Jonah, “Is it right for you to be angry about the vine?” He said, “I am right to be angry, even to death.”

kikuyu: Okaga kuuma mwena wa gathigathini arĩ na riiri ukengeete ta wa thahabu; Ngai okaga arĩ na ũkaru wa gwĩtigĩrwo.
english: Have you entered the storehouses of the snow, or have you seen the storehouses of the hail,

kikuyu: Anabii ao moigaga atĩrĩ, “Mũtikarathe ũhoro. Tigagai kũratha ũhoro ũkoniĩ maũndũ maya; † 1:13 Lakishi rĩarĩ itũũra rĩmwe rĩa marĩa manene mũno ma Juda.
english: Therefore I will make Samaria like a rubble heap of the field, like places for planting vineyards; and I will pour down its stones into the valley, and I will uncover its foundations.

kikuyu: Tondũ ũcio ngoro ciitũ nĩiringĩkĩte; na tondũ wa maũndũ macio maitho maitũ makaira,
english: For this our heart is faint. For these things our eyes are dim:

kikuyu: Ũrĩa tũngĩmeeka nĩ atĩrĩ: Nĩ tũkũreka matũũre muoyo, nĩgeetha tũtikanacookererwo nĩ marakara nĩ ũndũ wa kuuna mwĩhĩtwa ũrĩa twehĩtire nĩ ũndũ wao.”
english: We will do this to them, and let them live; lest wrath be on us, because of the oath which we swore to them.”

kikuyu: “Ta twĩre ũrĩa twagĩrĩirwo nĩ kũmwĩra; tũtingĩhota gwĩciirĩra nĩ ũndũ wa nduma iitũ.
english: “What is the way to the dwelling of light? As for darkness, where is its place,

kikuyu: nĩgeetha mwage ũcuuke na mũtuĩke atheru, mũtuĩke ciana cia Ngai itarĩ na kaũũgũ mũrĩ thĩinĩ wa rũciaro rũrũ ruogomu na rwaganu, na rĩrĩa mũrĩ hamwe nao inyuĩ mũkengage ta njata thĩ yothe,
english: that you may become blameless and harmless, children of God without defect in the middle of a crooked and perverse generation, among whom you are seen as lights in the world,

kikuyu: ikĩrĩa kĩndũ gĩothe kĩarĩ kĩĩruru bũrũri-inĩ wao, na ikĩrĩa maciaro ma tĩĩri wao. Ningĩ akĩũraga marigithathi mothe ma bũrũri wao, maciaro ma mbere ma ũciari wao wothe.
english: They ate up every plant in their land, and ate up the fruit of their ground.

kikuyu: Rũhuho rwa mwena wa Irathĩro rũmuoyaga akabuĩria; rũmweheragia kũrĩa atũũraga.
english: Seeing it is hidden from the eyes of all living, and kept close from the birds of the sky.

kikuyu: Mũndũ mũkoroku aarahũraga ngarari, no mũndũ wĩhokete Jehova nĩakagaacĩra.
english: One who is greedy stirs up strife; but one who trusts in Yahweh will prosper.

kikuyu: Agĩcooka agĩtoonya gatarũ kaumaarĩ, naruorũhuhorũgĩtuĩka. Naomakĩgegamũno, 52tondũmatiataũkĩirwonĩũhorowamĩgate ĩyo; ngoro ciao ciarĩ o nyũmũ.
english: He got into the boat with them; and the wind ceased, and they were very amazed among themselves, and marveled;

kikuyu: o nake nĩakanyua ndibei ĩyo ya marakara ma Ngai, ĩrĩa ĩitĩrĩirwo gĩkombe-inĩ kĩa mangʼũrĩ make ĩtatukanĩtio na kĩndũ. Nake nĩakanyariirwo na mwaki wa ũbiriti mbere ya araika arĩa atheru na mbere ya Gatũrũme.
english: he also will drink of the wine of the wrath of God, which is prepared unmixed in the cup of his anger. He will be tormented with fire and sulfur in the presence of the holy angels and in the presence of the Lamb.

kikuyu: Humuta, na Kiriathu- Ariba (na no kuo Hebironi), na Zioru; mothe maarĩ matũũra kenda na tũtũũra twamo.
english: Humtah, Kiriath Arba (also called Hebron), and Zior; nine cities with their villages.

kikuyu: nĩmũgookĩrĩra mũthamaki wa Babuloni na kĩnyũrũri, mũmwĩre atĩrĩ: Kaĩ mũhinyanĩrĩria nĩakinyĩte mũthia-ĩ! Kaĩ marakara make mahiũ nĩmathirĩte-ĩ!
english: that you will take up this parable against the king of Babylon, and say, “How the oppressor has ceased! The golden city has ceased!”

kikuyu: “Na mũndũ ũrĩa wothe ũgaatũma kamwe ga twana tũtũ tũnjĩtĩkĩtie kehie, wega nĩ mũndũ ũcio ohererwo ihiga rĩa gĩthĩi ngingo na aikio thĩinĩ wa iria.
english: “Whoever will cause one of these little ones who believe in me to stumble, it would be better for him if he were thrown into the sea with a millstone hung around his neck.

kikuyu: “Jehova ekuuga atĩrĩ: ‘Mwaragia ũhoro ũkoniĩ kũndũ gũkũ, mũkoiga atĩrĩ, “Nĩ kũndũ kũnũhe gũtarĩ kĩndũ, na gũtarĩ andũ kana nyamũ.” No thĩinĩ wa matũũra ma Juda na njĩra-inĩ iria nene cia Jerusalemu, o kũu gũthaamĩtwo gũkaaga andũ kana nyamũ-rĩ, nĩgũgacooka kũiguuo
english: Yahweh says: “Yet again there will be heard in this place, about which you say, ‘It is waste, without man and without animal, even in the cities of Judah, and in the streets of Jerusalem, that are desolate, without man and without inhabitant and without animal,’

kikuyu: Anabiiaku, weeIsiraeli-rĩ, matariĩ ta mbwe irĩ kũndũ kũrĩa kwanangĩku.
english: Israel, your prophets have been like foxes in the waste places.

kikuyu: Jehova aanjĩĩrire atĩrĩ: Mũrũ wa mũndũ, nĩũgũtuĩra Ohola na Oholiba ciira? Nĩ ũndũ ũcio marũithie nĩ ũndũ wa mĩtugo yao ĩrĩ magigi,
english: Yahweh said moreover to me: “Son of man, will you judge Oholah and Oholibah? Then declare to them their abominations.

kikuyu: Nĩnganeana mbakũri ĩyo moko-inĩ ma arĩa makũnyariiraga, o arĩa maakwĩrire atĩrĩ, ‘Wĩgũithie thĩ, nĩguo tũkũgerere igũrũ.’ Nawe ũgĩkoma, mũgongo waku ũgĩtuĩka taarĩ thĩ, na ũkĩũtua taarĩ njĩra nĩguo andũ acio makũgeragĩre igũrũ.”
english: I will put it into the hand of those who afflict you, who have said to your soul, ‘Bow down, that we may walk over you;’ and you have laid your back as the ground, like a street to those who walk over.”

kikuyu: Akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Aathi akwa, ndamũthaitha ũkai mũtoonye nyũmba ya ndungata yanyu. No mwĩthambe magũrũ na mũraarĩrĩre, na rũciinĩ tene mũrooke gũthiĩ na mbere na rũgendo rwanyu.” No-o makĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Aca, ithuĩ tũkũraara nja kĩhaaro-inĩ.”
english: and he said, “See now, my lords, please come into your servant’s house, stay all night, wash your feet, and you can rise up early, and go on your way.” They said, “No, but we will stay in the street all night.”

kikuyu: akĩmooria atĩrĩ, “Nĩ kĩĩ mũkũũhe ingĩmũneana kũrĩ inyuĩ?” Nao makĩmũtarĩra icunjĩ cia betha mĩrongo ĩtatũ, makĩmũnengera.
english: and said, “What are you willing to give me if I deliver him to you?” So they weighed out for him thirty pieces of silver.

kikuyu: O na gũtuĩka mĩario yake nĩ ya gũkenania, ndũkanamwĩtĩkie, nĩgũkorwo ngoro yake ĩiyũrĩte maũndũ mũgwanja marĩ magigi.
english: When his speech is charming, don’t believe him, for there are seven abominations in his heart.

kikuyu: Ũrĩa Mũthingu, o we Ngai, nĩonaga nyũmba ya ũrĩa mwaganu, na akarehithĩria ũcio mwaganu mwanangĩko.
english: The Righteous One considers the house of the wicked, and brings the wicked to ruin.

kikuyu: maitho ma mwĩtĩĩo, na rũrĩmĩ rũrĩa rũheenanagia, na moko marĩa maitaga thakame ya andũ matehĩtie,
english: I have perfumed my bed with myrrh, aloes, and cinnamon.

kikuyu: “O nawe Firaũni-rĩ, nĩũkoinangwo na ũkomio gatagatĩ-inĩ ka arĩa mataruĩte, o hamwe na arĩa maanooragwo na rũhiũ rwa njora.
english: “But you will be broken among the uncircumcised, and will lie with those who are slain by the sword.

kikuyu: Tondũ gĩcukĩro kĩu gĩtiarĩ kĩega gĩa gũikarwo hĩndĩ ya heho-rĩ, andũ aingĩ magĩtua itua tũthiĩ na mbere, twĩhokete gũkinya Foinike, tũikare kuo hĩndĩ ĩyo ya heho. Gĩkĩ kĩarĩ gĩcukĩro kĩa meeri gĩa Kirete, nakĩo kĩangʼetheire mwena wa gũthini wa ithũĩro, o ũndũ ũmwe na mwena wa gathigathini wa ithũĩro. Kĩhuhũkanio Rĩrĩa rũhuho rumĩte mwena wa gũthini rwambĩrĩirie kũhurutana kahora-rĩ, magĩĩciiria nĩmona kĩrĩa mendaga, na nĩ ũndũ ũcio makĩohora marikabu magĩthiĩ magereire hũgũrũrũ-inĩ cia Kirete.
english: Because the haven was not suitable to winter in, the majority advised going to sea from there, if by any means they could reach Phoenix and winter there, which is a port of Crete, looking southwest and northwest.

kikuyu: no rĩrĩ, matimenyaga atĩ nĩndirikanaga ciĩko icio ciao njũru ciothe. Mehia mao nĩmamarigiicĩirie; makoragwo mbere yakwa hĩndĩ ciothe.
english: They don’t consider in their hearts that I remember all their wickedness. Now their own deeds have engulfed them. They are before my face.

kikuyu: Ciana cianyu nĩcio igaacooka ithenya rĩa maithe manyu; ũkaamatua anene bũrũri-inĩ guothe.
english: Your sons will take the place of your fathers. You shall make them princes in all the earth.

kikuyu: Nĩamaheire ngerekano ĩngĩ, akĩmeera atĩrĩ: “Ũthamaki wa igũrũ ũtariĩ ta mbeũ ya karatarĩ, ĩrĩa mũndũ oire akĩhaanda mũgũnda wake.
english: Immediately Jesus stretched out his hand, took hold of him, and said to him, “You of little faith, why did you doubt?”

kikuyu: Nikodemo, ũrĩa wathiĩte kũrĩ Jesũ mbere ĩyo na aarĩ ũmwe wa gĩkundi kĩao, akĩũria atĩrĩ,
english: But I don’t seek my own glory. There is one who seeks and judges.

kikuyu: Kĩambĩrĩria-inĩ Ũhoro aarĩ o kuo, nake Ũhoro aatũũrĩte na Ngai, na Ũhoro aarĩ Ngai.
english: In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.

kikuyu: rĩu no taguo o nao matuĩkĩte aremi, nĩgeetha o nao rĩu maiguĩrwo tha nĩ ũndũ wa ũrĩa Ngai amũiguĩrĩire tha.
english: even so these also have now been disobedient, that by the mercy shown to you they may also obtain mercy.

kikuyu: Kĩoneki kĩa Obadia. Mwathani Jehova ekuuga ũũ ũhoro ũkoniĩ Edomu: Nĩtũiguĩte ũhoro uumĩte kũrĩ Jehova. Mũtũmwo nĩatũmirwo kũrĩ ndũrĩrĩ oige atĩrĩ, “Arahũkai, tũthiĩ tũmũũkĩrĩre, tũrũe nake”:
english: The vision of Obadiah. This is what the Lord† Yahweh‡ says about Edom. We have heard news from Yahweh, and an ambassador is sent among the nations, saying, “Arise, and let’s rise up against her in battle.

kikuyu: Atĩrĩrĩ, ti-itherũ Ngai nĩakaarĩria andũ aya na tũnua twa andũ maaragia na mwarĩrie wa andũ a kũngĩ na thiomi ngʼeni,
english: Whether then it is I or they, so we preach, and so you believed.

kikuyu: “Ningĩ mũthuuri ũcio agĩthiĩ kũrĩ mũriũ ũcio ũngĩ akĩmwĩra o ũguo. Nake agĩcookia akiuga, ‘Ĩĩ baba, nĩngũthiĩ,’ no ndaathiire.
english: He came to the second, and said the same thing. He answered, ‘I’m going, sir,’ but he didn’t go.

kikuyu: Ici nĩcio cioneki iria ndonire rĩrĩa ndaakomete ũrĩrĩ-inĩ wakwa: Ndarorire ngĩona hau mbere yakwa harũngiĩ mũtĩ ũrĩ bũrũri gatagatĩ. Naguo warĩ mũraihu mũno.
english: These were the visions of my head on my bed: I saw, and behold, a tree in the middle of the earth; and its height was great.

kikuyu: Ngĩmacookeria atĩrĩ, “Ngai wa Igũrũ nĩwe ũgũtũma tũgaacĩre. Ithuĩ ndungata ciake nĩtũk- wambĩrĩria gwaka rĩngĩ, no ha ũhoro wanyu-rĩ, mũtirĩ na igai gũkũ Jerusalemu, kana kĩhooto, kana ũndũ wa tene wa kũmũnyiitithania narĩo.”
english: Then I answered them, and said to them, “The God of heaven will prosper us. Therefore we, his servants, will arise and build; but you have no portion, nor right, nor memorial in Jerusalem.”

kikuyu: Nĩngamekĩra hinya ndĩmomĩrĩrie thĩinĩ wa Jehova, na thĩinĩ wa rĩĩtwa rĩake nĩmagetwara,” ũguo nĩguo Jehova ekuuga.
english: I will strengthen them in Yahweh. They will walk up and down in his name,” says Yahweh.

kikuyu: Kuuma ndĩ o mũnini, Wee Ngai, ũtũũrĩte ũndutaga, na nginyagia ũmũthĩ no nyumbũraga ciĩko ciaku cia magegania.
english: His name endures forever. His name continues as long as the sun. Men shall be blessed by him. All nations will call him blessed.

kikuyu: “Mũrũ wa mũndũ, ratha ũhoro, uuge atĩrĩ: ‘Mwathani Jehova ekuuga ũũ: “ ‘Girĩkai, muuge atĩrĩ, “Kaĩ mũthenya ũcio ũrĩ na haaro-ĩ!”
english: “Son of man, set your face against Pharaoh king of Egypt, and prophesy against him and against all Egypt.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo o ta ũrĩa Jona aarĩ kĩmenyithia kũrĩ andũ a Nineve, ũguo noguo Mũrũ wa Mũndũ egũkorwo arĩ kĩmenyithia kũrĩ rũciaro rũrũ.
english: For even as Jonah became a sign to the Ninevites, so the Son of Man will also be to this generation.

kikuyu: Gũtirĩ mũndũ wahotire kũmũcookeria, na kuuma mũthenya ũcio gũtirĩ mũndũ wacookire kũgeria kũmũũria ciũria ingĩ.
english: No one was able to answer him a word, neither did any man dare ask him any more questions from that day forward.

kikuyu: Nĩmaingatirwo kuuma kũrĩ mũingĩ, makiugĩrĩrio ta maarĩ aici.
english: They are driven out from among men. They cry after them as after a thief,

kikuyu: Jehova aahandire mĩthingi ya thĩ na ũũgĩ, na akĩrũmia igũrũ na ũmenyo wake;
english: We have the more sure word of prophecy; and you do well that you heed it as to a lamp shining in a dark place, until the day dawns and the morning star arises in your hearts,

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo nĩ ma nĩambirwo mũtharaba-inĩ atarĩ na hinya, no nĩatũũraga muoyo nĩ ũndũ wa hinya wa Ngai. O ta ũguo-rĩ, o na ithuĩ tũtirĩ hinya tũrĩ thĩinĩ wake, no nĩ ũndũ wa hinya wa Ngai nĩtũtũũraga muoyo thĩinĩ wake nĩguo tũmũtungatage.
english: who risked their own necks for my life, to whom not only I give thanks, but also all the assemblies of the Gentiles.

kikuyu: Ũhaataga andũ, ũkameeheria, magakoma toro wa gĩkuũ; andũ mahaana ta nyeki ĩrĩa ĩringũkaga rũciinĩ:
english: You sweep them away as they sleep. In the morning they sprout like new grass.

kikuyu: mũtikoomie ngoro cianyu ta ũrĩa mwekire mũrĩ Meriba, o na ta ũrĩa mwekire mũthenya ũrĩa mwarĩ Masa kũu werũ-inĩ,
english: No, but you yourselves do wrong and defraud, and that against your brothers.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo na ũndũ wa watho nĩndakuĩrĩire watho ũcio nĩguo ndũũre muoyo ndĩ wa Ngai.
english: For I through the law died to the law, that I might live to God.

kikuyu: “Kaĩ mũrĩ na haaro-ĩ, inyuĩ arutani a watho na Afarisai, hinga ici! Mwakagĩra anabii mbĩrĩra na mũkagemia mbĩrĩra cia arĩa athingu.
english: “Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you build the tombs of the prophets and decorate the tombs of the righteous,

kikuyu: Agĩcooka akĩhũgũkĩra arutwo ake, akĩmeera marĩ oiki atĩrĩ, “Kũrathimwo nĩ maitho marĩa marona kĩrĩa mũrona.
english: “He who is not with me is against me. He who doesn’t gather with me scatters.

kikuyu: “ ‘Kũrĩ na mĩthenya ĩtandatũ rĩrĩa mũngĩruta wĩra, no mũthenya wa mũgwanja nĩ Thabatũ ya kũhurũka, mũthenya wa kĩũngano gĩtheru. Mũtikanarute wĩra o na ũrĩkũ; kũrĩa guothe mũgaatũũra, ĩyo nĩ Thabatũ ya Jehova. Bathaka na Mĩgate ĩtarĩ Mĩĩkĩre Ndawa ya Kũimbia
english: “ ‘Six days shall work be done, but on the seventh day is a Sabbath of solemn rest, a holy convocation; you shall do no kind of work. It is a Sabbath to Yahweh in all your dwellings.

kikuyu: Namo mabũrũri mothe magĩũka Misiri kũrĩ Jusufu kũgũra ngano, tondũ ngʼaragu ĩyo yarĩ nene mũno thĩ yothe.
english: All countries came into Egypt, to Joseph, to buy grain, because the famine was severe in all the earth.

kikuyu: Ngai wakwa ekuuga atĩrĩ, “Mũndũ ũrĩa mwaganu-rĩ, ndarĩ hĩndĩ angĩgĩa na thayũ.”
english: “There is no peace”, says my God, “for the wicked.”

kikuyu: Ciana ciake irotuĩka cia ngoriai, na mũtumia wake atuĩke wa ndigwa.
english: He has sent redemption to his people. He has ordained his covenant forever. His name is holy and awesome!

kikuyu: “Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe, o Wee Ngai wa Isiraeli, ekuuga atĩrĩ, ‘Ta thikĩrĩriai! Nĩngũrehithĩria itũũra rĩĩrĩ inene, o na tũtũũra tũrĩa tũrĩrigiicĩirie tuothe mwanangĩko ũrĩa wothe ngwetete igũrũ rĩao, nĩ ũndũ nĩmoomirie ngingo, makĩaga gũthikĩrĩria ciugo ciakwa.’ ” Rĩrĩa Pashuri ũrĩa mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai, mũrũ wa Imeri, o ũcio warĩ mũnene wa arĩa angĩ kũu hekarũ- inĩ ya Jehova, aiguire Jeremia akĩratha maũndũ macio-rĩ,
english: “Yahweh of Armies, the God of Israel says, ‘Behold, I will bring on this city and on all its towns all the evil that I have pronounced against it, because they have made their neck stiff, that they may not hear my words.’ ”

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo athĩnjĩri-Ngai na anabii makĩĩra anene acio na andũ othe atĩrĩ, “Mũndũ ũyũ agĩrĩire atuĩrwo kũũragwo, tondũ nĩarathĩte maũndũ mooru makoniĩ itũũra rĩĩrĩ inene. Nĩmũmeiguĩrĩire na matũ manyu!”
english: Then the priests and the prophets spoke to the princes and to all the people, saying, “This man is worthy of death, for he has prophesied against this city, as you have heard with your ears.”

kikuyu: “Rĩrĩa anene a Isiraeli maatongoria, rĩrĩa andũ meheana meyendeire-rĩ, goocai Jehova!
english: “Because the leaders took the lead in Israel, because the people offered themselves willingly, be blessed, Yahweh!

kikuyu: Nuhu agĩeterera matukũ mangĩ mũgwanja, agĩcooka akĩrekereria ndutura ĩyo yume nja rĩngĩ, no hĩndĩ ĩno ndĩigana kũmũcookerera. Na rĩrĩ, mũthenya wa mbere, mweri wa mbere wa mwaka ũrĩa Nuhu akinyirie mĩaka magana matandatũ na ũmwe-rĩ, mũthenya ũcio nĩguo maaĩ maahũire biũ gũkũ thĩ. Nake Nuhu akĩeheria kĩrĩa kĩagitĩte thabina, agĩkĩona atĩ thĩ nĩyaniarĩte.
english: God said, “This is the token of the covenant which I make between me and you and every living creature that is with you, for perpetual generations:

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo Jehova nĩathuurĩte Zayuni, akerirĩria gũtuĩke gĩikaro gĩake, akoiga atĩrĩ:
english: Your name, Yahweh, endures forever; your renown, Yahweh, throughout all generations.

kikuyu: ‘Githĩ ti gwetha mareetha na makagayana indo cia ndaho: mũirĩtu ũmwe kana eerĩ harĩ o mũndũ, na nguo cia marangi maingĩ itahĩirwo Sisera, nguo cia marangi maingĩ iria ngʼemie, nguo iria ngʼemie mũno cia ngingo yakwa, icio ciothe nĩ cia gũtahwo?’
english: ‘Have they not found, have they not divided the plunder? A lady, two ladies to every man; to Sisera a plunder of dyed garments, a plunder of dyed garments embroidered, of dyed garments embroidered on both sides, on the necks of the plunder?’

kikuyu: na rĩrĩa kĩrĩndĩ kĩu kĩamenyire atĩ Harũni nĩakuĩte, andũ othe a nyũmba ya Isiraeli makĩmũcakaĩra mĩthenya mĩrongo ĩtatũ.
english: When all the congregation saw that Aaron was dead, they wept for Aaron thirty days, even all the house of Israel.

kikuyu: Arũme othe a ũkũrũ wa kuuma mĩaka mĩrongo ĩtatũ nginya mĩaka mĩrongo ĩtano arĩa mookire kũruta wĩra wa gũtungata na gũkuua Hema-ya-Gũtũnganwo, 48mũigana wao warĩ andũ 8,580.
english: you shall take five shekels apiece for each one; according to the shekel§ of the sanctuary you shall take them (the shekel is twenty gerahs†);

kikuyu: Nĩngananga matũũra marĩa manene ma bũrũri wanyu, na tharũrie ciĩhitho iria nũmu cianyu ciothe.
english: I will cut off the cities of your land and will tear down all your strongholds.

kikuyu: Kuona atĩ indo ciothe nĩikaanangwo na njĩra ĩyo-rĩ, inyuĩ mũkĩagĩrĩirwo gũkorwo mũrĩ andũ a mũthemba ũrĩkũ? Inyuĩ mwagĩrĩirwo nĩgũtũũraga mũtũũrĩre mũtheru na wa kwĩyamũrĩra Ngai,
english: searching for who or what kind of time the Spirit of Christ which was in them pointed to when he predicted the sufferings of Christ and the glories that would follow them.

kikuyu: “ ‘Nyũngũ ya rĩũmba ĩrĩa mũndũ ũcio angĩhutia, no nginya yũragwo, na kĩndũ gĩothe kĩa mbaũ no nginya gĩkamũrwo na maaĩ.
english: “ ‘The earthen vessel, which he who has the discharge touches, shall be broken; and every vessel of wood shall be rinsed in water.

kikuyu: Aathani maanyariiraga hatarĩ gĩtũmi, no ngoro yakwa ĩinainagio nĩ kiugo gĩaku.
english: Princes have persecuted me without a cause, but my heart stands in awe of your words.

kikuyu: Thikĩrĩriai mũigue kĩrĩro kĩa arĩithi, o na kĩgirĩko kĩa atongoria a rũũru rwa mbũri, nĩ ũndũ Jehova nĩaranangithia ũrĩithio wao.
english: A voice of the cry of the shepherds, and the wailing of the leader of the flock, for Yahweh destroys their pasture.

kikuyu: Rora mĩnyamaro yakwa o na gũtangĩka gwakwa, ũkĩndekere mehia makwa mothe.
english: Consider my affliction and my travail. Forgive all my sins.

kikuyu: “Mũrũ wa mũndũ, tema njĩra igĩrĩ cia kũgererwo nĩ rũhiũ rwa njora rwa mũthamaki wa Babuloni, cierĩ ciumĩte bũrũri o ũmwe. Haanda rũũri harĩa njĩra yahũkĩire ĩrorete itũũra inene.
english: “Also, you son of man, appoint two ways, that the sword of the king of Babylon may come. They both will come out of one land, and mark out a place. Mark it out at the head of the way to the city.

kikuyu: Ngai eerire Iburahĩmu atĩrĩ: ‘Njiaro ciaku igaatũũra irĩ ngʼeni bũrũri ũtarĩ wacio, kũrĩa igaatuuo ngombo na inyariirwo ihinda rĩa mĩaka magana mana.’ Ningĩ Ngai akiuga atĩrĩ, ‘No nĩngaherithia rũrĩrĩ rũu igaatungata irĩ ngombo; na thuutha ũcio-rĩ, nĩikoima bũrũri ũcio, nacio nĩikaahooya irĩ kũndũ gũkũ.’
english: God spoke in this way: that his offspring would live as aliens in a strange land, and that they would be enslaved and mistreated for four hundred years.

kikuyu: Tigagai gũthĩĩnĩka ngoro-inĩ cianyu nĩ ũndũ wa kĩrĩa mũrĩrĩĩaga kana mũrĩnyuuaga; mũtigatangĩkage nĩ ũndũ wa ũguo.
english: “Don’t seek what you will eat or what you will drink; neither be anxious.  or, Hell  An assarion was a small copper coin worth about an hour’s wages for an agricultural laborer.  A cubit is the length from the tip of the middle finger to the elbow on a man’s arm, or about

kikuyu: Jesũ aarĩkia kuuga ũguo, agĩtangĩka ngoro na akiumbũra atĩrĩ, “Ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ na ma, ũmwe wanyu nĩekũngunyanĩra.” Nao arutwo ake makĩambĩrĩria kũrorana marigĩtwo nũũ gatagatĩ-inĩ kao aaragia ũhoro wake.
english: One who has my commandments and keeps them, that person is one who loves me. One who loves me will be loved by my Father, and I will love him, and will reveal myself to him.”

kikuyu: Debora agĩtũmanĩra Baraka mũrũ wa Abinoamu kuuma Kedeshi ya Nafitali, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Jehova Ngai wa Isiraeli nĩaragwatha agakwĩra atĩrĩ, ‘Thiĩ, woe andũ 10,000 a kuuma Nafitali na Zebuluni, na ũmatongorie nginya Kĩrĩma-inĩ kĩa Taboru.
english: She sent and called Barak the son of Abinoam out of Kedesh Naphtali, and said to him, “Hasn’t Yahweh, the God of Israel, commanded, ‘Go and lead the way to  A cubit is the length from the tip of the middle finger to the elbow on a man’s arm, or about

kikuyu: Kĩndũ gĩothe kĩrĩ mĩhũmũ nĩkĩgooce Jehova. Goocai Jehova.
english: Let everything that has breath praise Yah! Praise Yah!

kikuyu: Twana twacio twagĩraga mwĩrĩ, tũgakũra tũrĩ na hinya o kũu gĩthaka-inĩ; tũkinyaga ihinda rĩa kwehera na tũticookaga.
english: Their young ones become strong. They grow up in the open field. They go out, and don’t return again.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo mwĩrĩ nĩwĩriragĩria maũndũ marĩa matangĩendwo nĩ Roho, nake Roho nĩareganaga na maũndũ marĩa meriragĩrio nĩ mwĩrĩ. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Roho na mwĩrĩ itũũraga irũaga, na nĩkĩo mũtahotaga gwĩka ũrĩa mwendaga gwĩka. No rĩrĩ, mũngĩtongorio nĩ Roho, inyuĩ mũtingĩkorwo mũgĩathwo nĩ watho. Na rĩrĩ, maũndũ marĩa mekagwo nĩ mwĩrĩ nĩmoĩo, namo nĩ: ũmaraya, na meciiria mooru, na ũmaramari,
english: For the flesh lusts against the Spirit, and the Spirit against the flesh; and these are contrary to one another, that you may not do the things that you desire.

kikuyu: Andũ arĩa metigĩrĩte Jehova nĩmoige atĩrĩ, “Wendo wake nĩ wa tene na tene.”
english: but in everything commending ourselves as servants of God: in great endurance, in afflictions, in hardships, in distresses,

kikuyu: “ ‘Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa megũtoonya ihingo-inĩ cia nja ya na thĩinĩ, marĩĩhumbaga nguo cia gatani; matikanehumbe nguo o na ĩmwe ya guoya wa ngʼondu hĩndĩ ĩrĩa megũtungata ihingo-inĩ cia nja ĩrĩa ya na thĩinĩ, kana kũu hekarũ thĩinĩ.
english: It shall be the prince’s part to give the burnt offerings, the meal offerings, and the drink offerings, in the feasts, and on the new moons, and on the Sabbaths, in  A cubit is the length from the tip of the middle finger to the elbow on a man’s arm, or about

kikuyu: Mĩguĩ yaku ĩrĩa mĩũgĩ nĩĩtheece thũ cia mũthamaki o ngoro-inĩ; ndũrĩrĩ nĩigwe magũrũ-inĩ maku.
english: Your arrows are sharp. The nations fall under you, with arrows in the heart of the king’s enemies.

kikuyu: “Acio eerĩ-rĩ, nũũ wekire ũrĩa ithe eendaga?” Magĩcookia atĩrĩ, “Nĩ ũrĩa wa mbere.” Nake Jesũ akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ na ma, etia mbeeca cia igooti na maraya nĩmaratoonya ũthamaki-inĩ wa Ngai mbere yanyu.
english: Which of the two did the will of his father?” They said to him, “The first.” Jesus said to them, “Most certainly I tell you that the tax collectors and the prostitutes are entering into God’s Kingdom before you.

kikuyu: makĩmũkururia, makĩmumia nja ya itũũra, na makĩambĩrĩria kũmũhũũra na mahiga nyuguto. Nao, aira makĩiga nguo ciao magũrũ-inĩ ma mwanake wetagwo Saũlũ. Rĩrĩa maamũhũũraga na mahiga nyuguto, Stefano nĩahooire Ngai, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Mwathani Jesũ, amũkĩra roho wakwa.”
english: They threw him out of the city and stoned him. The witnesses placed their garments at the feet of a young man named Saul.

kikuyu: Johana nĩoimbũraga ũhoro wake. Aanagĩrĩra akoiga atĩrĩ, “Ũyũ nĩwe ũrĩa ndaaragia ũhoro wake ngiuga atĩrĩ, ‘Ũrĩa ũgũũka thuutha wakwa nĩ mũnene kũngĩra, tondũ aarĩ mbere yakwa.’ ” Na kuuma ũiyũrĩrĩru wa ũtugi wake-rĩ, ithuothe nĩtwamũkĩrĩte irathimo nyingĩ mũno.
english: John testified about him. He cried out, saying, “This was he of whom I said, ‘He who comes after me has surpassed me, for he was before me.’ ”

kikuyu: Nĩakaruta maguta mothe ma ndegwa ĩyo ya igongona rĩa kũhoroherio mehia, namo nĩmo rũambũ rũrĩa ruothe rũhumbĩire nyama cia nda na rũrĩa rũnyiitaine nacio,
english: He shall take all the fat of the bull of the sin offering from it: the fat that covers the innards, and all the fat that is on the innards,

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Jesũ oonire gĩkundi kĩa andũ arĩa maamũthiũrũrũkĩirie, agĩathana maringe mũrĩmo ũrĩa ũngĩ wa iria.
english: Now when Jesus saw great multitudes around him, he gave the order to depart to the other side.

kikuyu: O na ingĩtuĩka ndĩrahungurĩra mũkuru ũrĩ nduma ta ya gĩkuũ, ndirĩ ũũru ingĩĩtigĩra, nĩgũkorwo ũrĩ hamwe na niĩ;
english: Our fathers trusted in you. They trusted, and you delivered them.

kikuyu: Mwathani Jehova ekuuga atĩrĩ, Niĩ mwene nĩ niĩ ngerĩithagĩria ngʼondu ciakwa, na ndũme ciarahage kũu.
english: I myself will be the shepherd of my sheep, and I will cause them to lie down,” says the Lord Yahweh.

kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo acio makaahooha o narua o ta ũrĩa nyeki ĩhoohaga, na makue narua o ta ũrĩa riya rĩhoohaga rĩamunywo.
english: For they shall soon be cut down like the grass, and wither like the green herb.

kikuyu: mũrũ wa Melea, mũrũ wa Mena, mũrũ wa Matatha, mũrũ wa Nathani, mũrũ wa Daudi,
english: the son of Melea, the son of Menan, the son of Mattatha, the son of Nathan, the son of David,

kikuyu: “No rĩrĩ, mũriũ ũcio angĩkorwo arĩ na mwana ũrĩa wonaga mehia macio mothe ithe ekaga, na o na gũtuĩka nĩamoonaga, we ndekaga maũndũ macio mooru:
english: Fire has gone out of its branches. It has devoured its fruit, so that there is in it no strong branch to be a scepter to rule.’

kikuyu: “ ‘Maya nĩmo maarĩ mehia ma mwarĩ wa nyũkwa Sodomu: We marĩ na airĩtu ake maarĩ andũ etĩĩi, a kwĩhũũnĩrĩria, na matiarũmbũyagia ũhoro wa andũ arĩa angĩ; matiateithirie andũ arĩa athĩĩni o na arĩa abatari.
english: “ ‘ “Behold, this was the iniquity of your sister Sodom: pride, fullness of bread, and prosperous ease was in her and in her daughters. She also didn’t strengthen the hand of the poor and needy.

kikuyu: Magĩcooka magĩĩta kĩrĩndĩ gĩothe hamwe mũthenya wa mbere wa mweri wa keerĩ. Andũ makĩonania rũruka rwao kũringana na mbarĩ na nyũmba ciao, nao arũme arĩa maakinyĩtie mĩaka mĩrongo ĩĩrĩ na makĩria makĩandĩkwo, o mũndũ o mũndũ na rĩĩtwa rĩake,
english: But if he dedicates his field after the Jubilee, then the priest shall reckon to him the money according to the years that remain to the Year of Jubilee; and an abatement shall be made from your valuation.

kikuyu: na Obali, na Abimaeli, na Sheba,
english: Obal, Abimael, Sheba,

kikuyu: Irura-rĩ, no rĩkũre handũ hatarĩ itomboya? Ithanjĩ-rĩ, no rĩtheereme handũ hatarĩ maaĩ?
english: “Can the papyrus grow up without mire? Can the rushes grow without water?

kikuyu: Agĩtuĩkanĩria Suriata na Kilikia, agĩĩkagĩra makanitha hinya.
english: He went through Syria and Cilicia, strengthening the assemblies.

kikuyu: Matũũra macio hamwe na tũtũũra twamo nĩmo maatuĩkire igai rĩa mũhĩrĩga wa Gadi, kũringana na ũrĩa mbarĩ ciao ciatariĩ.
english: This is the inheritance of the children of Gad according to their families, the cities and its villages.

kikuyu: Atĩrĩrĩ! Jehova nĩarooka oimĩte gĩikaro gĩake; araikũrũka arangĩrĩrie kũrĩa gũtũũgĩru gũkũ thĩ.
english: For behold,§ Yahweh comes out of his place, and will come down and tread on the high places of the earth.

kikuyu: “Niĩ nĩngagũtua rũrĩrĩ rũnene, na nĩngakũrathima; nĩngatũma rĩĩtwa rĩaku rĩgĩe igweta, nawe nĩũgatuĩka kĩrathimo.
english: Abram was very rich in livestock, in silver, and in gold.

kikuyu: Nĩmethaahirie o ene nĩ gwĩka ũguo meekire; na ũndũ wa ciĩko ciao makĩhũũra ũmaraya. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Jehova akĩrakarĩra andũ ake, na agĩthũũra igai rĩake mũno.
english: Thus they were defiled with their works, and prostituted themselves in their deeds.

kikuyu: Kuuma njiaro-inĩ cia Gadi: Arũme othe arĩa maakinyĩtie mĩaka mĩrongo ĩĩrĩ na makĩria, arĩa mangĩahotire gũthiĩ ita-inĩ, makĩandĩkwo o mũndũ o mũndũ na rĩĩtwa rĩake, kũringana na maandĩko ma mbarĩ ciao na nyũmba ciao.
english: In the Year of Jubilee the field shall return to him from whom it was bought, even to him to whom the possession of the land belongs.

kikuyu: Nyũmba ĩno ĩtanĩtio na Rĩĩtwa rĩakwa mũmĩtuĩte ngurunga ya atunyani? No niĩ nĩndĩĩoneire maũndũ macio! Ũguo nĩguo Jehova ekuuga.
english: They have healed the hurt of the daughter of my people slightly, saying, “Peace, peace,” when there is no peace.

kikuyu: “Ti-itherũ Mũrungu ndangĩrega mũndũ ũtarĩ ũcuuke, kana ekĩre moko ma arĩa mekaga ũũru hinya. Na rĩrĩ, no arĩiyũria kanua gaku mĩtheko, nayo mĩromo yaku yanĩrĩre nĩ gũkena.
english: Though I am righteous, my own mouth will condemn me. Though I am blameless, it will prove me perverse.

kikuyu: O na ningĩ mũgeithie Filologo, na Julia, na Nerea na mwarĩ wa nyina, na Olumpa, na arĩa othe aamũre makoragwo hamwe nao.
english: But I write the more boldly to you in part as reminding you, because of the grace that was given to me by God,

kikuyu: Nĩngameta ndĩmacookanĩrĩrie. Ti-itherũ nĩngamakũũra; nao nĩmakaingĩha o ta ũrĩa maarĩ mbere.
english: I will signal for them and gather them, for I have redeemed them. They will increase as they were before.

kikuyu: O ta ũrĩa kwandĩkĩtwo atĩrĩ: “Gũtirĩ mũndũ mũthingu, gũtirĩ o na ũmwe;
english: As it is written, “There is no one righteous; no, not one.

kikuyu: “No rĩrĩ, ingĩthiĩ mwena wa irathĩro, we ndarĩ kuo; ingĩthiĩ mwena wa ithũĩro, ndiramuona.
english: “If I go east, he is not there. If I go west, I can’t find him.

kikuyu: Nĩmathondekire gacaũ marĩ kũu Horebu, makĩhooya mũhianano ũtweketio kuuma kũrĩ kĩgera.
english: They made a calf in Horeb, and worshiped a molten image.

kikuyu: Andũ arĩa othe maanyonaga nĩmanyũrũragia; makaanuma makĩinainagia mĩtwe, makoiga atĩrĩ:
english: All those who see me mock me. They insult me with their lips. They shake their heads, saying,

kikuyu: Ũguo nĩguo mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai akaahoroheria mũndũ ũcio mehia hau mbere ya Jehova, nake nĩakarekerwo ũndũ o wothe wa marĩa ekĩte wa gũtũma atuĩke mũhĩtia.” Iruta rĩa Njino Nake Jehova akĩĩra Musa atĩrĩ,
english: “ ‘If he can’t afford a lamb, then he shall bring his trespass offering for that in which he has sinned, two turtledoves, or two young pigeons, to Yahweh; one for a sin offering, and the other for a burnt offering.

kikuyu: Ũũ nĩguo Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe ekuuga: “Mũtigathikĩrĩrie ũrĩa anabii acio maramũrathĩra; nĩ ciĩrĩgĩrĩro cia tũhũ mamũheaga. Maaragia ũhoro wa cioneki ciumĩte meciiria-inĩ mao ene, no ti kuuma kanua ka Jehova.
english: Yahweh of Armies says, “Don’t listen to the words of the prophets who prophesy to you. They teach you vanity. They speak a vision of their own heart, and not out of the mouth of Yahweh.

kikuyu: O na gũtuĩka nĩmeyendetie gatagatĩ-inĩ ka ndũrĩrĩ, rĩu nĩngũmacookanĩrĩria hamwe. Mekwambĩrĩria kũhinyara, mahinyĩrĩirio nĩ mũthamaki ũrĩa ũrĩ hinya.
english: But although they sold themselves among the nations, I will now gather them; and they begin to waste away because of the oppression of the king of mighty ones.

kikuyu: Hĩ! Kaĩ nĩmakanangwo o rĩmwe-ĩ! Makaaniinwo biũ nĩ imakania!
english: How they are suddenly destroyed! They are completely swept away with terrors.

kikuyu: Mwathani Jehova Ngai waku ũrĩa ũgitagĩra andũ ake, ekuuga ũũ: “Atĩrĩrĩ, nĩngwehereirie gĩkombe kĩrĩa gĩatũmire ũtũgũge, ngakĩruta guoko-inĩ gwaku; o na ngakwehereria mbakũri ya mangʼũrĩ makwa, na ndũgacooka kũmĩnyuĩra rĩngĩ.
english: Your Lord Yahweh, your God who pleads the cause of his people, says, “Behold, I have taken out of your hand the cup of staggering, even the bowl of the cup of my wrath. You will not drink it any more.

kikuyu: Mũthenya ũrĩa waciarirwo-rĩ, ndwatinirio rũrĩra, o na kana ũgĩthambio na maaĩ nĩguo ũthere, o na kana ũkĩhakwo cumbĩ, kana ũgĩthaywo na nguo cia mwana.
english: Therefore speak to them and tell them, ‘The Lord Yahweh says: “Every man of the house of Israel who takes his idols into his heart and puts the stumbling block of his iniquity before his face then comes to the prophet, I Yahweh will answer him there according to the multitude of his idols,

kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo ndĩ na ariũ a baba atano. Reke athiĩ akamahe ũhoro, nĩguo matikanooke gũkũ kũrĩ na ruo rũnene ũũ.’
english: Likewise, even as it was in the days of Lot: they ate, they drank, they bought, they sold, they planted, they built;

kikuyu: no niĩ nĩndĩmũũĩ, tondũ nyumĩte kũrĩ we, na nĩwe wandũmire.”
english: He who sent me is with me. The Father hasn’t left me alone, for I always do the things that are pleasing to him.”

kikuyu: Ambatai irĩma-inĩ mũgĩĩre mbaũ, mũũke mũnjakĩre nyũmba, nĩgeetha niĩ ndĩmĩkenagĩre na nduĩke wa gũtĩĩagwo,” ũguo nĩguo Jehova ekuuga.
english: Go up to the mountain, bring wood, and build the house. I will take pleasure in it, and I will be glorified,” says Yahweh.

kikuyu: Mũigana wa matukũ maitũ-rĩ, nĩ mĩaka mĩrongo mũgwanja, kana mĩrongo ĩnana, tũngĩkorwo tũrĩ na hinya; no rĩrĩ, mũigana wamo no thĩĩna na kĩeha, nĩgũkorwo mathiraga o narua, na ithuĩ tũgathiĩ, tũkabuĩria.
english: The days of our years are seventy, or even by reason of strength eighty years; yet their pride is but labor and sorrow, for it passes quickly, and we fly away.

kikuyu: Nawe, ũrĩ ta mwathi wa ariũ a thoguo-rĩ, nĩndakũhe rũgongo rwa bũrũri ũrĩa ndaatunyire Aamori na rũhiũ rwakwa rwa njora na ũta wakwa.” Ningĩ Jakubu agĩtũmanĩra ariũ ake, akĩmeera atĩrĩ: “Cookanĩrĩrai haha nĩgeetha ndĩmwĩre ũrĩa gũgekĩka kũrĩ inyuĩ matukũ-inĩ marĩa magooka.
english: Moreover I have given to you one portion above your brothers, which I took out of the hand of the Amorite with my sword and with my bow.”

kikuyu: o acio aacookanĩrĩirie amarutĩte mabũrũri-inĩ, kuuma mwena wa irathĩro na wa ithũĩro, na kuuma mwena wa gathigathini na wa gũthini.
english: and gathered out of the lands, from the east and from the west, from the north and from the south.

kikuyu: Ĩ kũngĩkorwo amwe ao nĩmagire kwĩhokeka, kwaga kwĩhokeka kwao, nĩkũngĩatũmire Ngai aage gũtuĩka mwĩhokeku?
english: For what if some were without faith? Will their lack of faith nullify the faithfulness of God?

kikuyu: Jesũ akĩĩra mũndũ-wa-nja ũcio atĩrĩ, “Gwĩtĩkia gwaku nĩ gwakũhonokia; thiĩ na thayũ.”
english: He said to the woman, “Your faith has saved you. Go in peace.”

kikuyu: Jehova akiumĩrĩra Isaaka, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Ndũgaikũrũke ũthiĩ Misiri; tũũra bũrũri o ũrĩa ndĩrĩkwĩra ũtũũre.
english: Yahweh appeared to him, and said, “Don’t go down into Egypt. Live in the land I will tell you about.

kikuyu: O na ingĩtuĩka njĩkaga ũrĩa itekwenda gwĩka-rĩ, nĩnjĩtĩkĩrĩte atĩ watho nĩ mwega.
english: The gift is not as through one who sinned; for the judgment came by one to condemnation, but the free gift followed many trespasses to justification.

kikuyu: O na inyuĩ-rĩ, mwarĩ akuũ nĩ ũndũ wa ũremi wanyu na mehia manyu,
english: Paul, an apostle of Christ† Jesus through the will of God, to the saints who are at Ephesus, and the faithful in Christ Jesus:

kikuyu: Thuutha wa Musa gũtũma andũ magathigaane Jazeri, andũ a Isiraeli magĩtunyana tũtũũra tũrĩa twarigiicĩirieitũũrarĩu, namakĩingataAamori arĩamaarĩ kuo.
english: Moses sent to spy out Jazer. They took its villages, and drove out the Amorites who were there.

kikuyu: nĩ ũndũ wa mũgambo wa thũ, nĩ ũndũ wa gũkũũrĩrwo maitho nĩ andũ arĩa aaganu; tondũ o mandehagĩra o mathĩĩna, na makaanyũrũria marakarĩte.
english: because of the voice of the enemy, because of the oppression of the wicked. For they bring suffering on me. In anger they hold a grudge against me.

kikuyu: Jesũ agĩcooka akĩmooria atĩrĩ, “Andũ moigaga Kristũ nĩ mũriũ wa Daudi nĩ ũndũ wa kĩĩ?
english: He said to them, “Why do they say that the Christ is David’s son?

kikuyu: “No mũngĩkaaga gwĩka ũguo, nĩmũkehĩria Jehova; na mũmenye na ma atĩ mehia manyu nĩmakamũcookerera.
english: everything that may withstand the fire, you shall make to go through the fire, and it shall be clean; nevertheless it shall be purified with the water for impurity. All that doesn’t withstand the fire you shall make to go through the water.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo ũteithio wa Misiri nĩ wa tũhũ na ndũrĩ kĩguni. Nĩ ũndũ bũrũri ũcio ndĩwĩtĩte Rahabu ũrĩa Ũtarĩ-ũndũ-Ekaga. * 30:1 Andũ a Juda nĩmathondekete ngwatanĩro na andũ a Misiri.
english: So then neither he who plants is anything, nor he who waters, but God who gives the increase.

kikuyu: Mũndũ ũcio oraga tondũ we nĩ wa kwandĩkwo, na ndarũmbũyanagia na ũhoro wa ngʼondu.
english: Now Jesus had spoken of his death, but they thought that he spoke of taking rest in sleep.

kikuyu: Hau Jesũ aambĩirwo nĩ haarĩ na mũgũnda, na thĩinĩ wa mũgũnda ũcio nĩ kwarĩ na mbĩrĩra njerũ, ĩrĩa ĩtakoretwo ĩigĩtwo mwĩrĩ wa mũndũ o na ũmwe.
english: Now in the place where he was crucified there was a garden. In the garden was a new tomb in which no man had ever yet been laid.

kikuyu: Nĩngacaria iria ciũrĩte, na njookie iria itururĩte. Nĩngooha iria ndiihie nacio iria hinyaru ndĩciĩkĩre hinya, no iria noru na iria irĩ na hinya-rĩ, nĩkũniina ngaaciniina. Nĩngarĩithagia rũũru rũu na kĩhooto.
english: “I will seek that which was lost, and will bring back that which was driven away, and will bind up that which was broken, and will strengthen that which was sick; but I will destroy the fat and the strong. I will feed them in justice.” ’

kikuyu: Mĩrũũthi ĩrĩa mĩĩthĩ ĩraramaga ĩkĩhĩta, na ĩgacaria irio ciayo kuuma harĩ Mũrungu.
english: The young lions roar after their prey, and seek their food from God.

kikuyu: Mwĩhũgagei na mũhooyage nĩgeetha mũtikagwe magerio-inĩ. Ngoro nĩyenda, no mwĩrĩ ndũrĩ na hinya.”
english: Watch and pray, that you may not enter into temptation. The spirit indeed is willing, but the flesh is weak.”

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo Mũthamaki Nebukadinezaru akĩĩgũithia, agĩturumithia ũthiũ thĩ mbere ya Danieli, na akĩmũhe gĩtĩĩo kĩnene, na agĩathana atĩ arutĩrwo igongona na ũbumba.
english: Then King Nebuchadnezzar fell on his face, worshiped Daniel, and commanded that they should offer an offering and sweet odors to him.

kikuyu: Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe nĩehĩtĩte, akoiga atĩrĩ, “Ti-itherũ, o ũrĩa thugundĩte, ũguo noguo gũgaatuĩka, na ũrĩa nduĩte, ũguo noguo gũgaikara.
english: Yahweh of Armies has sworn, saying, “Surely, as I have thought, so shall it happen; and as I have purposed, so shall it stand:

kikuyu: Mahiga ma ratiri mataiganainie ũritũ, o na ithimi itaiganĩte: cierĩ nĩ indo irĩ magigi harĩ Jehova.
english: Differing weights and differing measures, both of them alike are an abomination to Yahweh.

kikuyu: Maũgemagia na betha na thahabu; maũhũũragĩrĩra na mĩcumarĩ na nyondo ndũkae kwenyenya.
english: For he was crucified through weakness, yet he lives through the power of God. For we also are weak in him, but we will live with him through the power of God toward you.

kikuyu: Njehereria njono iria ndĩĩtigagĩra, nĩgũkorwo mawatho maku nĩ mega.
english: Take away my disgrace that I dread, for your ordinances are good.

kikuyu: “Nakĩo gĩtonga gĩkĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, ‘Iburahĩmu, baba, acio ti aiganu; mũndũ angĩthiĩ kũrĩ o oimĩte kũrĩ arĩa akuũ, no merire.’
english: It will be the same way in the day that the Son of Man is revealed.

kikuyu: Jakubu akĩrakario nĩ Rakeli akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Kaarĩ niĩ ndĩ ithenya rĩa Ngai ũrĩa ũkũimĩte ciana?”
english: Jacob’s anger burned against Rachel, and he said, “Am I in God’s place, who has withheld from you the fruit of the womb?”

kikuyu: Sisera akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Rũgama mũrango-inĩ wa hema, na hangĩũka mũndũ akũũrie atĩrĩ, ‘Kũrĩ mũndũ ũrĩ gũkũ?’ uuge atĩrĩ, ‘Aca.’ ” No Jaeli ũcio mũtumia wa Heberi akĩoya higĩ ya hema na nyondo, agĩthiĩ acemete harĩa Sisera aakomete anogete mũno. Akĩhũũrĩrĩra higĩ ĩyo thikĩrĩrio-inĩ, ĩgĩtũrĩkania nginya igĩtoonyerera thĩ, nake agĩkua.
english: He said to her, “Stand in the door of the tent, and if any man comes and inquires of you, and says, ‘Is there any man here?’ you shall say, ‘No.’ ”

kikuyu: “ ‘Macio nĩmo mawatho marĩa makoniĩ nyamũ, na nyoni, na kĩndũ gĩothe kĩrĩ muoyo kĩrĩa gĩthiiagĩra maaĩ-inĩ, na kĩũmbe o ro gĩothe kĩrĩa gĩtambaga thĩ. No nginya mũkũũranage nyamũ iria irĩ thaahu na iria itarĩ thaahu, na mũkũũranage ciũmbe iria irĩ muoyo ingĩrĩĩo na iria itangĩrĩĩo.’ ”
english: “ ‘This is the law of the animal, and of the bird, and of every living creature that moves in the waters, and of every creature that creeps on the earth,

kikuyu: Agĩcooka akiuga atĩrĩ, “Nũũ ũngĩerire Iburahĩmu atĩ Sara nĩakongithia ciana? No rĩrĩ, nĩndĩmũciarĩire kaana ga kahĩĩ ũkũrũ-inĩ wake.” Hagari na Ishumaeli Kũingatwo
english: Now therefore, restore the man’s wife. For he is a prophet, and he will pray for you, and you will live. If you don’t restore her, know for sure that you will die, you, and all who are yours.”

kikuyu: Thũ ĩyo nĩyegwatĩire igĩĩna-inĩ ciake ciothe; nake akĩĩonera ndũrĩrĩ iria itooĩ Ngai igĩtoonya handũ harĩa hake haamũre: o arĩa wee wathanĩte matikanatoonye kĩũngano-inĩ gĩaku.*
english: The adversary has spread out his hand on all her pleasant things; for she has seen that the nations have entered into her sanctuary, concerning whom you commanded that they should not enter into your assembly.

kikuyu: Mwĩnyiihanagĩriei mũndũ na ũrĩa ũngĩ nĩ ũndũ wa ũrĩa mũtĩĩte Kristũ. Atumia na Athuuri ao
