kikuyu: Andũ aingĩ arĩa maaragũraga, magĩcookanĩrĩria mabuku mao na makĩmacinĩra mbere ya andũ othe. Rĩrĩa maatarire thogora wa mabuku macio, magĩkinyia durakima ngiri mĩrongo ĩtano.
english: 1 While Apollos was at Corinth, Paul, having passed through the upper country, came to Ephesus and found certain disciples.

kikuyu: Akĩmaneana kũrĩ ndũrĩrĩ, nĩguo maathagwo nĩ thũ ciao.
english: He gave them into the hand of the nations. Those who hated them ruled over them.

kikuyu: Mũrũ wa Mũndũ nĩegũthiĩ o ta ũrĩa ũhoro wake wandĩkĩtwo. No mũndũ ũrĩa ũgũkunyanĩra Mũrũ wa Mũndũ kaĩ arĩ na haaro-ĩ! Nĩ kaba mũndũ ũcio atangĩaciarirwo.”
english: “He also who had received the one talent came and said, ‘Lord, I knew you that you are a hard man, reaping where you didn’t sow, and gathering where you didn’t scatter.

kikuyu: Labani aarĩkia kũigua ũhoro wa Jakubu, mũriũ wa mwarĩ wa nyina, akĩhiũha agĩthiĩ kũmũtũnga. Akĩmũhĩmbĩria akĩmũmumunya, na akĩmũtwara mũciĩ, na arĩ kũu mũciĩ Jakubu akĩhe Labani ũhoro ũcio wothe. Nake Labani akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Ti-itherũ, ũrĩ wa mũthiimo wakwa na thakame yakwa.” Jakubu Kũhikia Lea na Rakeli Thuutha wa Jakubu gũikara nake mweri mũgima,
english: When Laban heard the news of Jacob, his sister’s son, he ran to meet Jacob, and embraced him, and kissed him, and brought him to his house. Jacob told Laban all these things.

kikuyu: Ehokeire marũa macio Elasa mũrũ wa Shafani, na Gemaria mũrũ wa Hilikia, arĩa Zedekia mũthamaki wa Juda aatũmire kũrĩ Nebukadinezaru mũthamaki wa Babuloni. Marũa macio moigĩte atĩrĩ:
english: by the hand of Elasah the son of Shaphan and Gemariah the son of Hilkiah, (whom Zedekiah king of Judah sent to Babylon to Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon). It said:

kikuyu: Ũcio nĩguo ũndũ ũrĩa Ngai agayagĩra arĩa aaganu, igai rĩrĩa maathĩrĩirwo nĩ Mũrungu.”
english: This is the portion of a wicked man from God, the heritage appointed to him by God.”

kikuyu: namo magũrũ manyu mũmahaarĩrie gũthiĩ kũhunjia Ũhoro-ũrĩa-Mwega wa thayũ.
english: and having fitted your feet with the preparation of the Good News of peace,

kikuyu: Ũrĩa mũnene thĩinĩ wanyu nĩatuĩkage ndungata yanyu.
english: Many false prophets will arise and will lead many astray.

kikuyu: “Mũndũ wetagwo Anania nĩookire kũnyona, na aarĩ mũndũ weamũrĩire Ngai na ũndũ wa kũrũmia watho, na nĩaheetwo gĩtĩĩo nĩ Ayahudi othe arĩa maatũũraga kũu.
english: When it was day, some of the Jews banded together and bound themselves under a curse, saying that they would neither eat nor drink until they had killed Paul.

kikuyu: Nĩakaiga ngʼondu mwena wake wa ũrĩo, na mbũri aciige mwena wake wa ũmotho.
english: He will set the sheep on his right hand, but the goats on the left.

kikuyu: Nĩũgakorwo ũrĩ ũndũ mwega harĩ ndungata iria mwathi wacio agaakora ciĩiguĩte rĩrĩa agacooka. Ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ na ma, nĩakehotora acitungatĩre, aciikarie metha-inĩ, na acooke acirehere irio irĩe.
english: Blessed are those servants whom the lord will find watching when he comes. Most certainly I tell you that he will dress himself, make them recline, and will come and serve them.

kikuyu: Wee Mũrungu, ngũkayagĩra tondũ nĩũnjĩtĩkaga; ndegera gũtũ na ũigue ihooya rĩakwa.
english: I have called on you, for you will answer me, God. Turn your ear to me. Hear my speech.

kikuyu: Harĩa yagerera ĩtigaga mũhari ũkũhenia; mũndũ no eciirie atĩ ndia ĩrĩ na mbuĩ.
english: He makes a path shine after him. One would think the deep had white hair.

kikuyu: “Ũndũ ũyũ nĩ wa kũrũmĩrĩrwo thĩinĩ wa watho ũrĩa Jehova aathanĩte: Ĩra andũ a Isiraeli makũrehere moori ndune ĩtarĩ kaũũgũ kana kameni, na ĩtarĩ yekĩrwo icooki.
english: “This is the statute of the law which Yahweh has commanded. Tell the children of Israel to bring you a red heifer without spot, in which is no defect, and which was never yoked.

kikuyu: Andũ arĩa atongu na arĩa athĩĩni harĩ ũndũ ũmwe ũmanyiitithanĩtie; Jehova nĩwe Mũmbi wao othe.
english: The rich and the poor have this in common: Yahweh is the maker of them all.

kikuyu: Mĩtwe yothe nĩmĩenje na nderu ciothe cikenjwo; guoko guothe nĩgũteme, na njohero o yothe ĩhumbĩtwo na nguo ya ikũnia.
english: For every head is bald, and every beard clipped. There are cuttings on all the hands, and sackcloth on the waist.

kikuyu: Mũcaayo wa arĩa moohetwo njeera ũrogũkinyĩra; nĩ ũndũ wa hinya wa guoko gwaku gitĩra arĩa matuĩrĩirwo gũkua.
english: And when a hard battle had been fought, and Judas and his company, by the help of God, had good success, the nomads being overcome implored Judas to grant them friendship, promising to give him livestock, and to help ‡his people in all other ways.

kikuyu: Mũthenya ũkinyagĩria mũthenya ũrĩa ũngĩ ũhoro; naguo ũtukũ ũkamenyithia ũtukũ ũrĩa ũngĩ ũhoro wa riiri ũcio.
english: Day after day they pour out speech, and night after night they display knowledge.

kikuyu: “Tondũ wa kũhinyĩrĩrio kwa andũ arĩa matarĩ hinya, na gũcaaya kwa arĩa abatari-rĩ, rĩu nĩngwarahũka,” ũguo nĩguo Jehova ekuuga. “Nĩngũmagitĩra kuuma kũrĩ andũ arĩa mamacambagia.”
english: he who doesn’t lend out his money for usury, nor take a bribe against the innocent. He who does these things shall never be shaken.

kikuyu: Kaĩ Mũrungu ariganĩirwo nĩkũiguanĩra tha? Kaĩ marakara make matũmĩte atige gũtugana?”
english: Has God forgotten to be gracious? Has he, in anger, withheld his compassion?” Selah.

kikuyu: Mũtongoria mũnene wa Alawii kũu Jerusalemu aarĩ Uzi mũrũ wa Bani, mũrũ wa Hashabia, mũrũ wa Matania, mũrũ wa Mika. Uzi aarĩ ũmwe wa rũciaro rwa Asafu, arĩa maarĩ aini arĩa maarũgamĩrĩire wĩra wa nyũmba ya Ngai.
english: The overseer also of the Levites at Jerusalem was Uzzi the son of Bani, the son of Hashabiah, the son of Mattaniah, the son of Mica, of the sons of Asaph, the singers responsible for the service of God’s house.

kikuyu: JehovaMwene-Hinya-Wotheekuugaũũ: “Matukũ-inĩmacioandũikũmikuumathiomi-inĩciothe na ndũrĩrĩ-inĩ ciothe nĩmakanyiita Mũyahudi ũmwe, marũmie gĩcũrĩ gĩa kanjũ yake na mamwĩre atĩrĩ, ‘Reke tũthiĩ nawe, tondũ nĩtũiguĩte atĩ Ngai arĩ hamwe na inyuĩ.’ ”
english: Yahweh of Armies says: “In those days, ten men out of all the languages of the nations will take hold of the skirt of him who is a Jew, saying, ‘We will go with you, for we have heard that God is with you.’ ”

kikuyu: Rĩithia andũ aku na mũthĩgi waku, nĩo rũũru rwa igai rĩaku, rũrĩa rwĩrĩithagia rũrĩ rwiki mũtitũ-inĩ, kũrĩa kũrĩ ũrĩithio mũnoru. Reke irĩe o kũu Bashani na Gileadi, o ta ũrĩa ciarĩĩaga matukũ ma tene.
english: Shepherd your people with your staff, the flock of your heritage, who dwell by themselves in a forest. Let them feed in the middle of fertile pasture land, in Bashan and Gilead, as in the days of old.

kikuyu: “No rĩrĩ, andũ a Isiraeli magaatuĩka ta mũthanga wa hũgũrũrũ-inĩ cia iria, ũrĩa ũtangĩthimĩka kana ũtarĩke. O handũ harĩa merĩirwo atĩrĩ, ‘Inyuĩ mũtirĩ andũ akwa’, no ho mageetwo ‘ariũ a Ngai ũrĩa ũrĩ muoyo’. Nao andũ a Juda na andũ a Isiraeli nĩmagacookanĩrĩra rĩngĩ, na nĩmagathuura mũndũ ũmwe amatongorie, nao nĩmakoima bũrũri ũcio, nĩgũkorwo mũthenya wa Jezireeli nĩũgaatuĩka mũthenya mũnene.
english: Yet the number of the children of Israel will be as the sand of the sea, which can’t be measured or counted; and it will come to pass that, in the place where it was said to them, ‘You are not my people,’ they will be called ‘sons of the living God.’

kikuyu: Meciiria ma mũndũ mwaganu nĩ kĩndũ kĩrĩ magigi harĩ Jehova, no meciiria ma andũ arĩa atheru nĩmamũkenagia.
english: Yahweh detests the thoughts of the wicked, but the thoughts of the pure are pleasing.

kikuyu: Nĩngũũkĩra thiĩ kũrĩ baba ngamwĩre atĩrĩ: Baba, nĩnjĩhĩirie Ngai na ngakwĩhĩria.
english: I will get up and go to my father, and will tell him, “Father, I have sinned against heaven and in your sight.

kikuyu: Mũraika wa Mwathani akĩmoimĩrĩra, naguo riiri wa Mwathani ũkĩmatherera hau maarĩ, nao makĩnyiitwo nĩ guoya. No mũraika ũcio akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Tigai gwĩtigĩra. Ndamũrehera ũhoro mwega na wa gĩkeno kĩnene, ũrĩa ũgaatuĩka wa andũ othe.
english: Behold, an angel of the Lord stood by them, and the glory of the Lord shone around them, and they were terrified.

kikuyu: Mwathiĩ- rĩ, hunjagiai ndũmĩrĩri ĩno: ‘Ũthamaki wa igũrũ nĩũkuhĩrĩirie.’
english: As these went their way, Jesus began to say to the multitudes concerning John, “What did you go out into the wilderness to see? A reed shaken by the wind?

kikuyu: O mũthenya Moridekai nĩaceerangaga hau nja ya nyũmba ĩyo yatũũragwo nĩ andũ-a-nja, nĩguo amenye ũrĩa Esiteri aikaraga, na ũrĩa aathiiaga na mbere.
english: Mordecai walked every day in front of the court of the women’s house, to find out how Esther was doing, and what would become of her.

kikuyu: Mĩaka yothe ĩrĩa Sethi aatũũrire muoyo yarĩ mĩaka magana kenda na ikũmi na ĩĩrĩ, agĩcooka agĩkua.
english: All of the days of Seth were nine hundred twelve years, then he died.

kikuyu: Gideoni akua-rĩ, andũ a Isiraeli o rĩngĩ nĩmahũũrire ũmaraya na Mabaali. Makĩrũgamia Baali- Berithu‡ ĩtuĩke ngai yao,
english: As soon as Gideon was dead, the children of Israel turned again and played the prostitute following the Baals, and made Baal Berith their god.

kikuyu: Magĩita thakame ĩteehĩtie, thakame ya aanake na airĩtu ao, arĩa maarutire marĩ magongona kũrĩ mĩhianano ya Kaanani, naguo bũrũri ũgĩthaahio nĩ thakame yao.
english: They shed innocent blood, even the blood of their sons and of their daughters, whom they sacrificed to the idols of Canaan. The land was polluted with blood.

kikuyu: Othe mahiũhĩte ta riiko rĩa kũrugĩra mĩgate; moragaga aathani ao. Athamaki ao othe maniinagwo, na gũtirĩ o na ũmwe wao ũngayagĩra.
english: They are all hot as an oven, and devour their judges. All their kings have fallen. There is no one among them who calls to me.

kikuyu: na tũnyũmba twa athĩnjĩri-Ngai rĩarĩ rĩa wariĩ wa mĩkono mĩrongo ĩĩrĩ gũthiũrũrũkĩria hekarũ yothe. Nĩ haarĩ na matoonyero mĩena † 40:43 nĩ ta centimita
english: Between the rooms was a width of twenty cubits around the house on every side.

kikuyu: Ndakahota kũũrĩra nduma; rũrĩrĩmbĩ nĩrũkahoohia thuuna ciake, nayo mĩhũmũ ya kanua ka Ngai nĩĩkamweheria.
english: He will not depart out of darkness. The flame will dry up his branches. He will go away by the breath of God’s mouth.

kikuyu: Ndindaga njonokete mũthenya wothe, ngahumbwo nĩ thoni ũthiũ wothe,
english: All day long my dishonor is before me, and shame covers my face,

kikuyu: Nayo thuriya yake ĩrĩa yaikaraga Shekemu, nĩyamũciarĩire mwana wa kahĩĩ, ũrĩa aatuire Abimeleku.
english: His concubine who was in Shechem also bore him a son, and he named him Abimelech.

kikuyu: Ũnene wa Moridekai Na rĩrĩ, Mũthamaki Ahasuerusu nĩeetagia igooti mabũrũri marĩa mothe aathanaga, o nginya icigĩrĩra ciakuo cia kũraya.
english: 1 King Ahasuerus laid a tribute on the land and on the islands of the sea.

kikuyu: Gĩcunjĩ kĩrĩa kĩarũmĩrĩire gĩacookereirio nĩ Refaia mũrũ wa Huri ũrĩa warĩ mwathi wa nuthu ya gĩcigo kĩmwe kĩa Jerusalemu.
english: Next to them, Rephaiah the son of Hur, the ruler of half the district of Jerusalem, made repairs.

kikuyu: O na ningĩ ũndũ ũngĩ manjĩkĩte nĩ ũyũ: Ihinda o rĩu nĩmathaahirie handũ-hakwa- harĩa-haamũre, na magĩthũkia Thabatũ ciakwa.
english: Moreover this they have done to me: they have defiled my sanctuary in the same day, and have profaned my Sabbaths.

kikuyu: Ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ, gũkĩrĩ na uumithio ũrĩkũ mũndũ angiuga atĩ arĩ na wĩtĩkio, no ndarĩ na ciĩko? Wĩtĩkio ũcio wakĩmũhonokia?
english: What good is it, my brothers, if a man says he has faith, but has no works? Can faith save him?

kikuyu: “Mĩhianano ya kũhooywo ĩrĩa ĩrĩ mahiga-inĩ manyoroku* kũu ituamba-inĩ nĩyo rũgai rwanyu; ĩĩ, mĩhianano ĩyo nĩyo igai rĩanyu. Ĩĩ, ĩyo nĩyo ũitĩire magongona maku ma kĩndũ gĩa kũnyuuo, na ũkamĩrutĩra maruta ma mũtu. Niĩ-rĩ, ingĩhota atĩa gũkirĩrĩria maũndũ ta macio?
english: “Isn’t this the fast that I have chosen: to release the bonds of wickedness, to undo the straps of the yoke, to let the oppressed go free, and that you break every yoke?

kikuyu: Mĩaka Ngiri Ĩmwe Ningĩ ngĩona mũraika agĩikũrũka oimĩte igũrũ, arĩ na kĩhingũro kĩa Irima-rĩrĩa-Rĩtarĩ-Gĩturi, na anyiitĩte mũnyororo mũnene guoko-inĩ gwake. Nake akĩnyiita ndamathia ĩrĩa, na nĩyo nyoka ĩrĩa ya tene, na noyo mũcukani kana Shaitani, akĩmĩoha mĩaka ngiri ĩmwe.
english: the fifth sardonyx, the sixth sardius, the seventh chrysolite, the eighth beryl, the ninth topaz, the tenth chrysoprase, the eleventh jacinth, and the twelfth amethyst.

kikuyu: Ngai ndaigana kũrega andũ ake arĩa aamenyete o mbere. Kaĩ mũtooĩ ũrĩa Maandĩko moigĩte harĩa handĩkĩtwo ũhoro wa Elija, ũrĩa aathaithire Ngai nĩ ũndũ wa andũ a Isiraeli, akiuga atĩrĩ:
english: God didn’t reject his people, whom he foreknew. Or don’t you know what the Scripture says about Elijah? How he pleads with God against Israel:

kikuyu: Agĩkaana ũhoro ũcio o rĩngĩ, na akĩĩhĩta, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Niĩ ndiũĩ mũndũ ũcio!”
english: Again he denied it with an oath, “I don’t know the man.”

kikuyu: Wakorwo ũrĩ na ũndũ wa kuuga-rĩ, njĩĩra; aria, nĩgũkorwo ingĩenda woneke ndwĩhĩtie. No akorwo ti ũguo-rĩ, wee gĩthikĩrĩrie; kira ki, na nĩngũkũruta ũũgĩ.”
english: If you have anything to say, answer me. Speak, for I desire to justify you.

kikuyu: (Nao andũ Benjamini makĩigua atĩ andũ a Isiraeli nĩmambatĩte nginya Mizipa.) Hĩndĩ ĩyo andũ a Isiraeli makiuga atĩrĩ, “Ta twĩre ũrĩa ũndũ ũcio mũũru wekĩkire.” Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Mũlawii ũcio mũthuuri wa mũtumia ũrĩa woragĩtwo akiuga atĩrĩ, “Niĩ na thuriya yakwa tuokire Gibea ya andũ a Benjamini tũraarĩrĩre kuo.
english: (Now the children of Benjamin heard that the children of Israel had gone up to Mizpah.) The children of Israel said, “Tell us, how did this wickedness happen?”

kikuyu: “Nĩ ũndũ Jehova ekuuga atĩrĩ: “ ‘Kĩronda gĩaku gĩtingĩhona, nguraro ciaku itirĩ kĩhonia.
english: For Yahweh says, “Your hurt is incurable. Your wound is grievous.

kikuyu: Nĩagaathana thingo ciothe ikũrũrwo mwena wa na thĩinĩ, namo makũrũro macio mateo kĩara-inĩ na kũu nja ya itũũra. Ningĩ moe mahiga mangĩ make namo mathenya macio mamomoretwo, na macooke moe rĩũmba rĩerũ mathinge nyũmba ĩyo narĩo.
english: If his hair has fallen off from the front part of his head, his forehead is bald. He is clean.

kikuyu: Acio nĩo ariũ a Hamu kũringana na mĩhĩrĩga yao, na thiomi ciao, marĩ ngʼongo ciao, na mabũrũri- inĩ mao. Ashemu
english: These are the sons of Ham, after their families, according to their languages, in their lands and their nations.

kikuyu: Nayo ĩkĩringa ciama nene na morirũ, o na ĩgĩtũma mwaki ũikũrũke uume igũrũ ũũke thĩ andũ makĩonaga.
english: He performs great signs, even making fire come down out of the sky to the earth in the sight of people.

kikuyu: Nacio ithima cia kũrĩa kũriku na ihingo cia mũiyũro wa igũrũ ikĩhingwo, nayo mbura ĩgĩtiga kuura.
english: You shall take seven pairs of every clean animal with you, the male and his female. Of the animals that are not clean, take two, the male and his female.

kikuyu: Ngũkwĩra atĩrĩ na ma, twaragia ũrĩa tũũĩ, na tũkoimbũra ũrĩa tuonete, no inyuĩ, mũtiĩtĩkagĩra ũira witũ.
english: But he who hates his brother is in the darkness, and walks in the darkness, and doesn’t know where he is going, because the darkness has blinded his eyes.

kikuyu: Mũndũ mũthĩĩni na mũndũ mũhinyanĩrĩria harĩ ũndũ ũmwe ũmahaananĩtie: Jehova nĩwe ũtũmaga maitho mao eerĩ moone.
english: There is a generation, oh how lofty are their eyes! Their eyelids are lifted up.

kikuyu: nĩ ũndũ ũcio, inyuĩ arĩithi, iguai kiugo kĩa Jehova:
english: therefore, you shepherds, hear Yahweh’s word!”

kikuyu: na ngoro yake ndĩandathimire nĩ ũndũ wa kũmũiguithia ũrugarĩ na guoya wa ngʼondu ciakwa-rĩ,
english: if his heart hasn’t blessed me, if he hasn’t been warmed with my sheep’s fleece;

kikuyu: Andũ magana matatũ makĩnyua maaĩ na hĩ ciao. Acio angĩ othe magĩturia ndu makĩnyua maaĩ.
english: The number of those who lapped, putting their hand to their mouth, was three hundred men; but all the rest of the people bowed down on their knees to drink water.

kikuyu: Jehova nĩaherithĩtie mũno, no ndandekereirie ngue.
english: Open my eyes, that I may see wondrous things out of your law.

kikuyu: Ihinda rĩu nĩngamũcookanĩrĩria; hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩngamũcookia mũciĩ. Nĩngatũma mũgĩe igweta na ndĩmũgoocithie gatagatĩ-inĩ ka ndũrĩrĩ ciothe cia thĩ, hĩndĩ ĩrĩa ngaatũma mũgaacĩre rĩngĩ, o mũkĩĩonagĩra na maitho manyu,” ũguo nĩguo Jehova ekuuga.
english: At that time I will bring you in, and at that time I will gather you; for I will give you honor and praise among all the peoples of the earth when I restore your fortunes before your eyes, says Yahweh.  “Behold”, from “הִ ͏נֵּה”, means look at, take notice, observe, see, or gaze at. It is often used as an interjection.

kikuyu: Nakĩo Kĩrĩma gĩa Seiru nĩngatũma kĩanangwo gĩkire ihooru, na njũrage arĩa othe mokaga kuo na makoimagara.
english: Thus I will make Mount Seir an astonishment and a desolation. I will cut off from it him who passes through and him who returns.

kikuyu: Githĩ mũtiugaga atĩ, ‘Nĩ mĩeri ĩna ĩtigarĩte magetha makinye’? Ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ, hingũrai maitho manyu muone mĩgũnda! Magetha nĩmakinyu.
english: Don’t you say, ‘There are yet four months until the harvest’? Behold, I tell you, lift up your eyes and look at the fields, that they are white for harvest already.

kikuyu: Nũũ ũtangĩgwĩtigĩra, Wee mũthamaki wa ndũrĩrĩ? Wee nowe wagĩrĩire gwĩtigĩrwo. Thĩinĩ wa andũ arĩa othe oogĩ a ndũrĩrĩ, o na mothamaki-inĩ mao mothe, gũtirĩ ũngĩ ũhaana tawe.
english: Now I pray to God that you do no evil; not that we may appear approved, but that you may do that which is honorable, though we may seem to have failed.

kikuyu: “Mwĩhaarĩrie mũthiũrũrũkĩrie Babuloni, inyuothe arĩa mũgeetaga ũta. Rĩrathei! Mũtigatigie mĩguĩ, nĩgũkorwo nĩrĩĩhĩirie Jehova.
english: Set yourselves in array against Babylon all around, all you who bend the bow; shoot at her. Spare no arrows, for she has sinned against Yahweh.

kikuyu: “Niĩ ndĩ na ũira mũnene gũkĩra ũcio wa Johana. Nĩgũkorwo wĩra ũrĩa Baba aaheete ndĩĩkie, na noguo ndĩraruta, nĩguo uumbũraga atĩ Baba nĩwe ũndũmĩte. Nake Baba ũrĩa wandũmire nĩaheanĩte ũira ũngoniĩ we mwene. Inyuĩ mũtirĩ mwaigua mũgambo wake kana mũkona ũrĩa atariĩ,
english: But the testimony which I have is greater than that of John; for the works which the Father gave me to accomplish, the very works that I do, testify about me, that the Father has sent me.

kikuyu: “Nĩ ũndũ ũcio mũndũ amocaga ta kĩndũ kĩbuthĩte, agathira ta nguo ĩrĩĩtwo nĩ memenyi.
english: though I am decaying like a rotten thing, like a garment that is moth-eaten.

kikuyu: Kũu nĩkuo maatithirie maraarĩrĩre. Nao magĩthiĩ magĩikara thĩ kĩhaaro-inĩ kĩnene gĩa itũũra rĩu inene, no gũtirĩ mũndũ o na ũmwe wamanyiitire ũgeni mararĩrĩre gwake.
english: They went over there, to go in to stay in Gibeah. He went in, and sat down in the street of the city; for there was no one who took them into his house to stay.

kikuyu: Njehererai, inyuĩ eeki ũũru, nĩguo ngĩrũmie maathani ma Ngai wakwa!
english: Depart from me, you evildoers, that I may keep the commandments of my God.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ maaiguire ũguo magĩkena na magĩtĩĩa kiugo kĩa Mwathani, nao andũ arĩa othe maathĩrĩirio muoyo-inĩ wa tene na tene magĩĩtĩkia.
english: As the Gentiles heard this, they were glad and glorified the word of God. As many as were appointed to eternal life believed.

kikuyu: Atĩrĩrĩ, nyuuaga maaĩ ma itangi rĩaku we mwene, maaĩ marĩa mathereraga moimĩte gĩthima-inĩ gĩaku mwene. Nĩ kwagĩrĩire ithima ciaku ciunagĩre maaĩ barabara-inĩ, kana tũrũũĩ twaku tuunagĩre ihaaro-inĩ cia mũingĩ?
english: Therefore his calamity will come suddenly. He will be broken suddenly, and that without remedy.

kikuyu: Tũtigacooka mĩciĩ iitũ o nginya hĩndĩ ĩrĩa Mũisiraeli o wothe akaamũkĩra igai rĩake.
english: But all the girls, who have not known man by lying with him, keep alive for yourselves.

kikuyu: Rongoriai maũndũ marĩa mega, no ti marĩa mooru, nĩguo mũtũũre muoyo. Nake Jehova Ngai Mwene-Hinya-Wothe nĩarĩkoragwo hamwe na inyuĩ, o ta ũrĩa mũtũire muugaga.
english: For, behold, I will raise up against you a nation, house of Israel,” says Yahweh, the God of Armies; “and they will afflict you from the entrance of Hamath to the brook of the Arabah.”

kikuyu: Roho aheanaga muoyo; mwĩrĩ ndũrĩ kĩene. Ciugo iria ndamwarĩria nĩ roho na nĩcio muoyo. No nĩ kũrĩ amwe anyu matarĩ maretĩkia.” Nĩgũkorwo kuuma o kĩambĩrĩria Jesũ nĩooĩ arĩa matetĩkĩtie, na ũrĩa ũkaamũkunyanĩra.
english: It is the spirit who gives life. The flesh profits nothing. The words that I speak to you are spirit, and are life.

kikuyu: Hau nĩho akaarehe maruta make harĩ Jehova, namo nĩ: gatũrũme ka mwaka ũmwe gatarĩ na kaũũgũ gatuĩke ka iruta rĩa njino, na kamwatĩ ka mwaka ũmwe karĩ igongona rĩa kũhoroheria mehia, na ndũrũme ĩmwe ĩtarĩ na kaũũgũ ĩrĩ iruta rĩa ũiguano, 15hamwe na maruta ma mũtu na maruta ma kũnyuuo, na gĩkabũkĩa mĩgate ĩtarĩ na ndawaya kũimbia, na keki cia mũtu ũrĩa mũhinyu mũno ũtukanĩtio na maguta, na tũmĩgate tũhũthũ tũhakĩtwo maguta.
english: and he shall offer his offering to Yahweh: one male lamb a year old without defect for a burnt offering, one ewe lamb a year old without defect for a sin offering, one ram without defect for peace offerings,  or, seed  The Hebrew word rendered “God” is “אֱֹלהִ ֑ים” (Elohim).

kikuyu: O na gũtuĩka ndĩraria ũrĩa kũrĩ, ndĩratuuo mũheenania; o na gũtuĩka ndirĩ na mahĩtia, mũguĩ wake ũngurarĩtie kĩronda gĩtangĩhona.’ Nĩ mũndũ ũrĩkũ ũhaana ta Ayubu, mũndũ ũnyuuaga kĩnyũrũri taarĩ maaĩ aranyua?
english: Notwithstanding my right I am considered a liar. My wound is incurable, though I am without disobedience.’

kikuyu: Makĩhooya mĩhianano yao, ĩrĩa yatuĩkire mũtego kũrĩ o.
english: They served their idols, which became a snare to them.

kikuyu: Marakara mao mahiũ mũno, na mangʼũrĩ mao matarĩ tha, maronyiitwo nĩ kĩrumi. Ngaamaharagania thĩinĩ wa Jakubu na ndĩmahurunjĩre thĩinĩ wa Isiraeli.
english: Cursed be their anger, for it was fierce; and their wrath, for it was cruel. I will divide them in Jacob, and scatter them in Israel.

kikuyu: Ngoro yakwa nĩ Jehova ĩrĩĩrahaga; arĩa anyamaarĩku marĩiguaga ũguo magakena.
english: My soul shall boast in Yahweh. The humble shall hear of it and be glad.

kikuyu: Rũũĩ rwa mbere rwĩtagwo Pishoni; na nĩruo rũthiũrũrũkaga bũrũri wothe wa Havila, kũrĩa kũrĩ thahabu.
english: The name of the first is Pishon: it flows through the whole land of Havilah, where there is gold;

kikuyu: Nĩndagũcookeria ngaatho na ndakũgooca, o Wee Ngai wa maithe makwa: Nĩũũheete ũũgĩ o na ũhoti, ũkaamenyithia ũhoro ũrĩa tũkũhooete, ũgatũma tũmenye kĩroto kĩa mũthamaki.” Danieli Gũtaũra Kĩroto
english: I thank you and praise you, O God of my fathers, who have given me wisdom and might, and have now made known to me what we desired of you; for you have made known to us the king’s matter.”

kikuyu: Petero agĩcookia atĩrĩ, “Mũndũ ũyũ, niĩ ndiũĩ ũrĩa ũroiga!” Na hĩndĩ o ĩyo oigaga ũguo, ngũkũ ĩgĩkũga.
english: But Peter said, “Man, I don’t know what you are talking about!” Immediately, while he was still speaking, a rooster crowed.

kikuyu: Tondũ-rĩ, ũhoro wothe wa watho ũhingagio nĩ rĩathani rĩĩrĩ rĩmwe: “Enda mũndũ ũrĩa ũngĩ o ta ũrĩa wĩendete wee mwene.” No rĩrĩ, mũngĩrũmana mũkĩrĩĩanaga-rĩ, mwĩmenyererei mũtikaniinane inyuĩ ene. Mũtũũrĩre wa Gũtongorio nĩ Roho Nĩ ũndũ ũcio nguuga atĩrĩ, thiiagai mũtongoretio nĩ Roho, na mũtirĩhingagia merirĩria ma mwĩrĩ.
english: But far be it from me to boast except in the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ, through which the world has been crucified to me, and I to the world.

kikuyu: Igongona rĩa mũndũ mwaganu nĩ kĩndũ kĩrĩ magigi: ĩ rĩtikĩrĩ magigi makĩria hĩndĩ ĩrĩa rĩrehetwo na ũhinga ngoro-inĩ!
english: The sacrifice of the wicked is an abomination— how much more, when he brings it with a wicked mind!

kikuyu: Arutwo ake aingĩ maigua ũguo makiuga atĩrĩ, “Ũyũ nĩ ũrutani mũritũ mũno, nũũ ũngĩhota kũwĩtĩkĩra?”
english: Therefore many of his disciples, when they heard this, said, “This is a hard saying! Who can listen to it?”

kikuyu: Ndirĩ ndaaria na hitho, ndĩ handũ bũrũri-inĩ ũrĩ nduma; ndirĩ ndeera njiaro cia Jakubu atĩrĩ, ‘Nongoriai o ũguo tũhũ.’ Niĩ Jehova, njaragia ũhoro wa ma; nyumbũraga maũndũ marĩa magĩrĩire.
english: I have not spoken in secret, in a place of the land of darkness. I didn’t say to the offspring of Jacob, ‘Seek me in vain.’ I, Yahweh, speak righteousness. I declare things that are right.

kikuyu: “Mũndũ ũrĩa ũheaga andũ a itũũra rĩake njoohi, akamatahĩria mondo-inĩ ya rũũa ya ndibei nginya makarĩĩo, nĩgeetha erorere njaga yao, kaĩ arĩ na haaro-ĩ!
english: “Woe to him who gives his neighbor drink, pouring your inflaming wine until they are drunk, so that you may gaze at their naked bodies!

kikuyu: Njerekera na ũnjiguĩre tha, o ta ũrĩa wĩkaga andũ arĩa mendete rĩĩtwa rĩaku.
english: Turn to me, and have mercy on me, as you always do to those who love your name.

kikuyu: Nĩmeyohanirie na Baali ya Peoru, na makĩrĩa magongona maarutĩirwo ngai itarĩ muoyo;
english: They joined themselves also to Baal Peor, and ate the sacrifices of the dead.

kikuyu: na nĩgeetha thĩinĩ wa mwĩrĩ ũcio ũmwe, amaiguithanie na Ngai na ũndũ wa mũtharaba, ũrĩa aahũthĩrire kũniina rũmena rwao.
english: and might reconcile them both in one body to God through the cross, having killed the hostility through it.

kikuyu: Johana eekagĩra Nguo ya guoya wa ngamĩĩra, na nĩeyohaga mũcibi wa rũũa njohero. Irio ciake ciarĩ ngigĩ na ũũkĩ wa gĩthaka. Nao andũ nĩmathiiaga kũrĩ we moimĩte Jerusalemu, na mĩena yothe ya Judea, na bũrũri wothe ũrĩa wariganĩtie na Jorodani.
english: Gathering together all the chief priests and scribes of the people, he asked them where the Christ would be born.

kikuyu: “Mũndũ aahota gũkorwo nĩ mũthingu, na ekaga maũndũ ma kĩhooto na marũngĩrĩru.
english: “ ‘Now when she saw that she had waited, and her hope was lost, then she took another of her cubs, and made him a young lion.

kikuyu: Mĩrumbĩ ya ciũmbe iria irĩ muoyo nĩĩgatũũra muoyo kũrĩa guothe rũũĩ rũu rũgaathereragĩra. Nĩgũkaagĩa thamaki nyingĩ, tondũ maaĩ macio nĩkuo mathereragĩra, magatũma maaĩ macio marĩ na cumbĩ magĩre; nĩ ũndũ ũcio kũrĩa rũũĩ rũu rũthereragĩra, ciũmbe ciothe nĩigatũũra muoyo.
english: “ ‘ “But when the people of the land come before Yahweh in the appointed feasts, he who enters by the way of the north gate to worship shall go out by the way of the south gate; and he who enters by the way of the south gate shall go out by the way of the north gate. He shall not return by the way of the gate by which he came in, but shall go out straight before him.

kikuyu: “Nĩ ũndũ ũcio ĩra andũ a nyũmba ya Isiraeli atĩrĩ, ‘Mwathani Jehova ekuuga ũũ: Ndigwĩka maũndũ macio nĩ ũndũ wanyu, inyuĩ andũ a nyũmba ya Isiraeli, no ngũmeeka nĩ ũndũ wa rĩĩtwa rĩakwa itheru, rĩrĩa mũthaahĩtie ndũrĩrĩ-inĩ kũrĩa mũthiĩte.
english: “Therefore tell the house of Israel, ‘The Lord Yahweh says: “I don’t do this for your sake, house of Israel, but for my holy name, which you have profaned among the nations where you went.

kikuyu: Ũũ nĩguo aathaga ndũrĩrĩ na akaheana irio nyingĩ.
english: For by these he judges the people. He gives food in abundance.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo Simoni Petero, tondũ aarĩ na rũhiũ rwa njora, akĩrũcomora na agĩtinia gũtũ kwa ũrĩo kwa ndungata ya mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai ũrĩa mũnene. (Ndungata ĩyo yetagwo Maliku.) Nake Jesũ agĩatha Petero atĩrĩ, “Cookia rũhiũ rwaku rwa njora! Gĩkombe kĩrĩa Baba aaheete-rĩ, nĩngwaga gũkĩnyuĩra?” Jesũ Gũtwarwo kwa Anasi
english: Simon Peter therefore, having a sword, drew it, struck the high priest’s servant, and cut off his right ear. The servant’s name was Malchus.

kikuyu: Mũtikanamũrĩhie uumithio o na ũrĩkũ, no mwĩtigagĩre Ngai wanyu, nĩgeetha mũndũ wa bũrũri wanyu ahote gũtũũrania na inyuĩ.
english: Take no interest from him or profit; but fear your God, that your brother may live among you.

kikuyu: (Othe makĩĩranĩra atĩ nĩmegũtigana na atumia ao, na o mũndũ akĩruta ndũrũme kuuma rũũru-inĩ rwake nĩ ũndũ wa iruta rĩa mahĩtia mao.)
english: He took the captains over hundreds, and the Carites, and the guard, and all the people of the land; and they brought down the king from Yahweh’s house, and came by the way of the gate of the guard to the king’s house. He sat on the throne of the kings.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo andũ a Rubeni, na a Gadi, na a nuthu ya Manase magĩcookeria anene a mbarĩ cia Isiraeli makĩmeera atĩrĩ:
english: They gave them Shechem with its pasture lands in the hill country of Ephraim, the city of refuge for the man slayer, and Gezer with its pasture lands,

kikuyu: nakuo gũgĩkorwo nĩ kwarĩ na mũtumia wonjetio nĩ ngoma thũku mĩaka ikũmi na ĩnana. Atũire ainamĩrĩire atekũhota gwĩtiira o na hanini.
english: Behold, there was a woman who had a spirit of infirmity eighteen years. She was bent over and could in no way straighten herself up.

kikuyu: Ngai ndahingagĩrĩria marakara make; o na arĩa maateithagĩrĩria Rahabu nĩmamũinamagĩrĩra.
english: “God will not withdraw his anger. The helpers of Rahab stoop under him.

kikuyu: Makĩringa mũrĩmo ũrĩa ũngĩ wa iria, magĩkinya rũgongo rwa Agerasi rũrĩa rũngʼethanĩire na Galili.
english: But what did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I tell you, and much more than a prophet.

kikuyu: Mĩri yakwa ĩgataamba ĩkinye maaĩ-inĩ, narĩo ireera rĩkoragwo honge-inĩ ciakwa ũtukũ wothe.
english: My root is spread out to the waters. The dew lies all night on my branch.

kikuyu: Kĩgũũta kĩĩonaga kĩrĩ kĩũgĩ maitho-inĩ makĩo, gũkĩra andũ mũgwanja arĩa macookagia ũhoro na ũũgĩ.
english: as also in all of his letters, speaking in them of these things. In those, there are some things that are hard to understand, which the ignorant and unsettled twist, as they also do to the other Scriptures, to their own destruction.

kikuyu: Tondũ ũcio-rĩ, nĩngatũma mũtahwo mũtwarwo mbere ya Dameski,” ũguo nĩguo Jehova ekuuga, ũrĩa wĩtagwo Ngai Mwene-Hinya-Wothe.
english: Therefore I will cause you to go into captivity beyond Damascus,” says Yahweh, whose name is the God of Armies.

kikuyu: “ ‘Kĩũmbe gĩothe kĩrĩa gĩtambaga thĩ nĩgĩthũũre; ti gĩa kũrĩĩo.
english: “ ‘Every creeping thing that creeps on the earth is an abomination. It shall not be eaten.

kikuyu: Andũ aingĩ makĩmũkaania, makĩmwĩra akire, nowe agĩkĩrĩrĩria kwanĩrĩra, akiugaga atĩrĩ, “Mũrũ wa Daudi, njiguĩra tha!” Nake Jesũ akĩrũgama, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Mwĩtei.” Nĩ ũndũ ũcio magĩĩta mũndũ ũcio warĩ mũtumumu, makĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Wĩyũmĩrĩrie! Rũgama! Nĩaragwĩta.”
english: Many rebuked him, that he should be quiet, but he cried out much more, “You son of David, have mercy on me!”

kikuyu: Wee Jehova nĩũrĩtũmenyagĩrĩra wega, na ũtũgitagĩre kuuma kũrĩ andũ ta acio nginya tene.
english: Oh that the salvation of Israel would come out of Zion! When Yahweh restores the fortunes of his people, then Jacob shall rejoice, and Israel shall be glad.

kikuyu: Ihinda rĩhĩtũku mũndũ wetagwo Theuda nĩeyumĩririe agĩĩtuuaga atĩ we nĩ mũndũ ma, na akĩgĩa na arũmĩrĩri ta magana mana. Nĩooragirwo, nao arũmĩrĩri aake othe makĩhurunjwo na ũhoro ũcio wothe ũgĩtuĩka wa tũhũ.
english: For before these days Theudas rose up, making himself out to be somebody; to whom a number of men, about four hundred, joined themselves. He was slain; and all, as many as obeyed him, were dispersed and came to nothing.

kikuyu: Mũndũ-wa-nja mũtaana nĩegĩagĩra gĩtĩĩo, no arũme arĩa matarĩ tha no ũtonga magĩaga naguo.
english: A gracious woman obtains honor, but violent men obtain riches.

kikuyu: hakĩrĩ ũguo-rĩ, wee ũrutaga andũ arĩa angĩ- rĩ, ndũngĩkĩĩruta wee mwene? Wee ũhunjagia atĩ andũ matikaiye-rĩ, wee nĩũiyaga?
english: But now apart from the law, a righteousness of God has been revealed, being testified by the law and the prophets;

kikuyu: “Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Mwathani Jehova ekuuga ũũ: Kuona atĩ wee nĩũriganĩirwo nĩ niĩ na ũkanjikia na thuutha waku, no nginya ũcookererwo nĩ maciaro ma ũũra-thoni waku na ũmaraya waku.”
english: “Therefore the Lord Yahweh says: ‘Because you have forgotten me and cast me behind your back, therefore you also bear your lewdness and your prostitution.’ ”

kikuyu: Ũcio nĩwe ũrĩa wathuurire andũ amwe matuĩke atũmwo, na angĩ matuĩke anabii, na angĩ matuĩke ahunjia, na arĩa angĩ matuĩke arĩithi a ngoro cia andũ o na arutani, 12nĩguo ahaarĩrie andũ a Ngai nĩ ũndũ wa wĩra wa gũtungata, nĩgeetha mwĩrĩ wa Kristũ wongererwo hinya,
english: He gave some to be apostles; and some, prophets; and some, evangelists; and some, shepherds† and teachers;

kikuyu: “Ningĩ nĩakeera arĩa marĩ mwena wake wa ũmotho atĩrĩ, ‘Njehererai, inyuĩ mũrumĩtwo, mũthiĩ mwaki-inĩ wa tene na tene ũrĩa ũthondekeirwo mũcukani na araika ake.
english: Then he will say also to those on the left hand, ‘Depart from me, you cursed, into the eternal fire which is prepared for the devil and his angels;

kikuyu: kũrĩ Egiloni mũthamaki wa Moabi. Na rĩrĩ, Ehudu nĩathondekete rũhiũ rwa njora rũũgĩ mĩena yeerĩ, ta rwa buti ĩmwe na nuthu† kũraiha rũrĩa oohereire njohero-inĩ yake mwena wa ũrĩo nguo-inĩ thĩinĩ. Nake agĩtwarĩra Egiloni mũthamaki wa Moabi igooti, na rĩrĩ, Egiloni aarĩ mũndũ mũnoru mũno.
english: Ehud made himself a sword which had two edges, a cubit† in length; and he wore it under his clothing on his right thigh.

kikuyu: Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe arĩ hamwe na ithuĩ; Ngai wa Jakubu nĩwe kĩirigo giitũ kĩa hinya. * 45:8 Nguo nĩciaitagĩrĩrio maguta manungi wega.
english: Yahweh of Armies is with us. The God of Jacob is our refuge. Selah.

kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa wothe ũkaanyiitwo mũgwate agaathecwo atũrĩkanio; nao arĩa othe makaanyiitwo makooragwo na rũhiũ rwa njora.
english: Yet you shall be brought down to Sheol,§ to the depths of the pit.

kikuyu: O na nĩ kaba mũrutage cairi ĩmwe kuuma itĩĩa-inĩ, mũmĩrekie thĩ nĩ ũndũ wake, mũmũtigĩre nĩguo oke akĩmĩhaaraga, no mũtikamũtetie.” Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Ruthu akĩhaara kũu mũgũnda ũcio nginya o hwaĩ-inĩ. Agĩcooka akĩhũũra cairi ĩrĩa aahaarĩte, ĩgĩkinyia mũigana wa eba ĩmwe.†
english: Ruth said, “Don’t urge me to leave you, and to return from following you, for where you go, I will go; and where you stay, I will stay. Your people will be my people, and your God§ my God.

kikuyu: Rũciinĩ gwakĩa-rĩ, andũ acio makiumagario hamwe na ndigiri ciao.
english: As soon as the morning was light, the men were sent away, they and their donkeys.

kikuyu: Mariamu Mũmagidali na Mariamu ũcio ũngĩ maikarĩte hau mangʼetheire mbĩrĩra. Arangĩri Mbĩrĩra-inĩ Na mũthenya ũyũ ũngĩ ũrĩa ũrũmagĩrĩra Mũthenya wa Ihaarĩria, athĩnjĩri-Ngai arĩa anene na Afarisai magĩthiĩ kwa Pilato.
english: and said, “This man said, ‘I am able to destroy the temple of God, and to build it in three days.’ ”

kikuyu: mũrũ wa Joshua, mũrũ wa Eliezeri, mũrũ wa Jorimu, mũrũ wa Mathati, mũrũ wa Lawi,
english: the son of Jose, the son of Eliezer, the son of Jorim, the son of Matthat, the son of Levi,

kikuyu: “Jehova ekuuga atĩrĩ: “ ‘Nĩtũiguĩte mũkayũrũrũko wa kũmakania, wa itua-nda, no ti wa thayũ.
english: For Yahweh says: “We have heard a voice of trembling; a voice of fear, and not of peace.

kikuyu: Maricani kana ngenyekenyi iria cia goro itiagĩrĩire kũgwetanio naguo; thogora wa ũũgĩ ũkĩrĩte wa ruru ĩrĩa ndune. * 28:4 Enji a marima magĩcaria tũhiga twa goro maikaragĩra itĩ ciacunjuuragio na mĩkanda.
english: No mention will be made of coral or of crystal. Yes, the price of wisdom is above rubies.

kikuyu: Mwaki ũngĩaga ngũ no kũhora ũhoraga; ngarari cingĩaga mũhuhu no gũthira ithiraga.
english: For lack of wood a fire goes out. Without gossip, a quarrel dies down.

kikuyu: Ũguo ti kuuga atĩ kiugo kĩa Ngai nĩkĩagĩte kũhinga. Nĩgũkorwo ti andũ othe arĩa maciarĩirwo Isiraeli marĩ andũ a Ngai a ma.
english: But it is not as though the word of God has come to nothing. For they are not all Israel that are of Israel.

kikuyu: Makiuga atĩrĩ, “Nĩarahonokagia andũ arĩa angĩ, no ndangĩhota kwĩhonokia we mwene! Mũthamaki wa Isiraeli! Nĩakiume rĩu mũtharaba-inĩ, na ithuĩ nĩtũkũmwĩtĩkia.
english: Again, a second time he went away and prayed, saying, “My Father, if this cup can’t pass away from me unless I drink it, your desire be done.”

kikuyu: Manjathamagĩria tũnua twao makoiga atĩrĩ, “Haiya! Haiya! Maitho maitũ nĩmeyoneire ũguo.”
english: Yes, they opened their mouth wide against me. They said, “Aha! Aha! Our eye has seen it!”

kikuyu: Atĩrĩ, eheria guoko gwaku, gũikare kũraya na niĩ, na ũtige kũũmakia na imakania ciaku. Ningĩ ũnjĩte na nĩngwĩtĩka, kana ũkĩreke njarie, nawe ũnjookerie. Nĩ mahĩtia na mehia maigana niĩ njĩkĩte? Nyonia ihĩtia na rĩĩhia rĩakwa.
english: His sons come to honor, and he doesn’t know it. They are brought low, but he doesn’t perceive it of them.

kikuyu: Kĩũngano kĩu no nginya kĩgitĩre mũndũ ũcio ũthitangĩirwo kũũragana kuuma kũrĩ mũrĩhanĩria wa thakame, na kĩmũcookie itũũra rĩu inene rĩa kũũrĩrwo rĩrĩa oorĩire. Nake no nginya aikare kuo o nginya rĩrĩa mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai ũrĩa mũnene agaakua, o ũcio waitĩrĩirio maguta marĩa maamũre.
english: The congregation shall deliver the man slayer out of the hand of the avenger of blood, and the congregation shall restore him to his city of refuge, where he had fled. He shall dwell therein until the death of the high priest, who was anointed with the holy oil.

kikuyu: Thuutha wa thĩĩna na mawĩra maritũ, Juda nĩatwarĩtwo ithaamĩrio. Atũũraga gatagatĩ ka ndũrĩrĩ; agakĩaga handũ ha kũhurũka. Arĩa othe mamũingatithagia nĩmamũkinyĩrĩire arĩ hatĩka-inĩ ciake.
english: He has cut off all the horn of Israel in fierce anger. He has drawn back his right hand from before the enemy. He has burned up Jacob like a flaming fire, which devours all around.

kikuyu: Ciugo iteciirĩtio itheecaga ta rũhiũ rwa njora, no rũrĩmĩ rwa mũndũ ũrĩa mũũgĩ nĩkũhonia rũhonagia.
english: There is one who speaks rashly like the piercing of a sword, but the tongue of the wise heals.

kikuyu: Jehova akĩmacookeria atĩrĩ, “Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa andũ a Misiri na Aamori, na Aamoni, na Afilisti,
english: 1 Now Jephthah the Gileadite was a mighty man of valor. He was the son of a prostitute. Gilead became the father of Jephthah.

kikuyu: “Ihinda rĩa magetha nĩrĩhĩtũku, nayo hĩndĩ ya riũa ĩgathira, na tũtihonoketio.”
english: “The harvest is past. The summer has ended, and we are not saved.”

kikuyu: Ngai eekire ũguo nĩgeetha andũ mamũmaathe na hihi mamũhambatĩrie mamuone, o na gũtuĩka ndarĩ haraaya na o ũmwe witũ.
english: that they should seek the Lord, if perhaps they might reach out for him and find him, though he is not far from each one of us.

kikuyu: Namo maũndũ macio meekĩkire nĩgeetha Maandĩko mahinge marĩa moigĩte atĩrĩ, “Gũtirĩ ihĩndĩ o na rĩmwe rĩake rĩkoinwo.”
english: For these things happened that the Scripture might be fulfilled, “A bone of him will not be broken.”*

kikuyu: Andũ a Sheba, na a Dedani, na onjorithia a Tarishishi na matũũra mothe makuo magaakũũria atĩrĩ, “Ũũkĩte gũtaha indo? Ũnganĩtie kĩrĩndĩ gĩaku mũũke mũtunyane indo, na mũkuue betha na thahabu, na mũkuue mahiũ na indo, o na mũtahe indo nyingĩ mũno?” ’
english: Yes, all the people of the land will bury them; and they will become famous in the day that I will be glorified,” says the Lord Yahweh.

kikuyu: no rĩrĩ, nĩagaciirithia arĩa abatari arĩ na ũthingu, nao arĩa athĩĩni a gũkũ thĩ amatuĩre ciira na kĩhooto. Nĩakahũũra thĩ na rũthanju rwa kanua gake, na oorage andũ arĩa aaganu na mĩhũmũ ya mĩromo yake.
english: but he will judge the poor with righteousness, and decide with equity for the humble of the earth. He will strike the earth with the rod of his mouth; and with the breath of his lips he will kill the wicked.

kikuyu: Mũtikanareke ndeto njũru ciume tũnua-inĩ twanyu, tiga o iria cia gwaka andũ arĩa angĩ kũringana na mabata mao, nĩguo igunage arĩa marĩciiguaga. Ningĩ mũtikanaiguithie Roho Mũtheru wa Ngai ũũru, ũrĩa wamwĩkĩrire rũũri o nginya rĩrĩa mũthenya ũrĩa wa gũkũũrwo ũgaakinya.
english: Let no corrupt speech proceed out of your mouth, but only what is good for building others up as the need may be, that it may give grace to those who hear.

kikuyu: “Bũrũri wa Misiri ũhaana moori njega mũno, no rĩrĩ, nĩ harĩ mbogo kanyarare ĩrooka kũmĩũkĩrĩra, yumĩte mwena wa gathigathini.
english: “Egypt is a very beautiful heifer; but destruction out of the north has come.

kikuyu: Akĩanĩrĩra na mũgambo mũnene, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Ũrenda atĩa na niĩ, wee Jesũ, Mũrũ wa Ngai-Ũrĩa-Ũrĩ-Igũrũ-Mũno? Ndagũthaitha na Ngai ndũkaanyariire!”
english: and crying out with a loud voice, he said, “What have I to do with you, Jesus, you Son of the Most High God? I adjure you by God, don’t torment me.”

kikuyu: Marigĩrĩrio-rĩ, mũtumia ũcio nake agĩkua.
english: After them all, the woman died.

kikuyu: O rĩngĩ Jesũ akĩigĩrĩra mũndũ ũcio moko maitho. Hĩndĩ ĩyo maitho make makĩhingũka, agĩcooka kuona na akĩona indo ciothe wega.
english: Then again he laid his hands on his eyes. He looked intently, and was restored, and saw everyone clearly.

kikuyu: Maciaro ma andũ arĩa athingu nĩ mũtĩ wa muoyo, nake mũndũ ũrĩa mũũgĩ nĩaguucagĩrĩria ngoro cia andũ.
english: The fruit of the righteous is a tree of life. He who is wise wins souls.

kikuyu: Niĩ mwene, kana ariũ a baba, kana andũ akwa, kana arangĩri arĩa maarĩ hamwe na niĩ gũtirĩ warutaga nguo; o mũndũ aakoragwo na indo ciake cia mbaara o na agĩthiĩ gũtaha maaĩ.
english: So neither I, nor my brothers, nor my servants, nor the men of the guard who followed me took off our clothes. Everyone took his weapon to the water.

kikuyu: Thũ ciaku nĩikahumbwo thoni, nacio hema cia arĩa aaganu itigacooka kuonwo.”
english: “It is all the same. Therefore I say he destroys the blameless and the wicked.

kikuyu: Tũhocio twa njibe * 40:42 nĩ ta mita robo ithatũ (0.75)
english: The hooks, a hand width long, were fastened within all around. The meat of the offering was on the tables.

kikuyu: Nĩwaũthereirie mũgũnda, naguo ũkĩgwata, na ũkĩiyũra bũrũri wothe.
english: There shall be no strange god in you, neither shall you worship any foreign god.

kikuyu: “Ũrĩĩtiga kwaria mĩario ĩno rĩ? Gĩa na ũũgĩ nĩguo o na ithuĩ tũhote kwaria. Nĩ kĩĩ kĩratũma ũtuone tũhaana ta ngʼombe,
english: Surely there are mockers with me. My eye dwells on their provocation.

kikuyu: Ariũ aku mahiũhĩte magĩcookaga kũrĩ we, nao arĩa makwanangĩte nĩmarakũũrĩra.
english: Therefore “ ‘Come out from among them, and be separate,’ says the Lord. ‘Touch no unclean thing. I will receive you.*

kikuyu: Wee Ngai, ta rora ngo iitũ; tiirĩra ũrĩa waku ũitĩrĩirie maguta gĩthiithi.
english: All nations you have made will come and worship before you, Lord. They shall glorify your name.

kikuyu: Egiloni akĩgĩĩra andũ a Amoni na Amaleki,* makĩũngana hamwe nake magĩtharĩkĩra andũ a Isiraeli, na makĩmatunya Itũũra rĩrĩa Inene rĩa Mĩkĩndũ.
english: They abandoned Yahweh, and served Baal and the Ashtaroth.

kikuyu: O na kũngĩtuĩka atĩ andũ arĩa aaganu no manjohe na mĩhĩndo-rĩ, ndingĩriganĩrwo nĩ watho waku.
english: The ropes of the wicked bind me, but I won’t forget your law.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo Hamani mũrũ wa Hamedatha, ũrĩa Mũagagi, o we thũ ya Ayahudi othe, nĩathugundĩte gũũkĩrĩra Ayahudi amaniine, na agĩcuuka puri (ũguo nĩ kuuga agĩcuuka mĩtĩ) nĩguo monũhwo na maniinwo. No rĩrĩa ũndũ ũcio wa hitho wamenyekire nĩ mũthamaki, akĩruta watho mwandĩke wa kuuga atĩ ũũru ũcio Hamani aathugundĩire Ayahudi ũmũcookerere we mwene, o na atĩ we na ariũ ake macuurio mĩtĩ-igũrũ.
english: because Haman the son of Hammedatha, the Agagite, the enemy of all the Jews, had plotted against the Jews to destroy them, and had cast “Pur”, that is the lot, to consume them and to destroy them;

kikuyu: “ ‘Ĩno nĩyo mĩtabarĩre ya iruta rĩa ũiguano rĩrĩa mũndũ angĩneana kũrĩ Jehova:
english: He shall take off his garments, and put on other garments, and carry the ashes outside the camp to a clean place.

kikuyu: Ndaigua ũguo ngĩũkĩra, ngĩĩhũgũra mĩena yothe. Ngĩigua toro ũcio warĩ mwega mũno harĩ niĩ.
english: On this I awakened, and saw; and my sleep was sweet to me.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo mĩtugo ya andũ acio ndĩrĩ kĩene: matemaga mũtĩ mũtitũ, ũkarutĩrwo wĩra nĩ bundi, akawacũhia na ithanwa rĩake.
english: seeing that you seek a proof of Christ who speaks in me who is not weak, but is powerful in you.

kikuyu: Macio nĩmo maarĩ maaĩ ma Meriba, kũrĩa andũ a Isiraeli maatetanirie na Jehova na kũrĩa eyonanirie atĩ nĩ mũtheru thĩinĩ wao. Edomu Kũgiria Isiraeli Njĩra
english: Whoever touches a dead person, the body of a man who has died, and doesn’t purify himself, defiles Yahweh’s tabernacle; and that soul shall be cut off from Israel; because the water for impurity was not sprinkled on him, he shall be unclean. His uncleanness is yet on him.

kikuyu: Twoima kũu tũkĩambĩrĩria rũgendo rĩngĩ, tũgereire mwena ũrĩa ũtaarĩ rũhuho wa Kuporo, tondũ huho cioimaga na mwena ũrĩa twathiiaga.
english: Putting to sea from there, we sailed under the lee of Cyprus, because the winds were contrary.

kikuyu: Kĩririkano kĩa mũndũ mũthingu gĩgatuĩka kĩrathimo, no rĩĩtwa rĩa mũndũ mwaganu nĩrĩkabutha.
english: When a wicked man dies, hope perishes, and expectation of power comes to nothing.

kikuyu: Ndũũru cia mbũri na ngʼombe igaakoma kuo, hamwe na ciũmbe cia mĩthemba yothe. Ndundu cia werũ na ndundu iria ngʼambi nĩikomba itugĩ-inĩ ciakuo. Kayũ ka mĩgambo yacio gakaiguuagwo ndirica-inĩ, hĩba cia mahiga igaakorwo mĩrango-inĩ, nayo mĩgamba ya mĩtarakwa ĩtigwo ũtheri.
english: Herds will lie down in the middle of her, all kinds of animals. Both the pelican and the porcupine will lodge in its capitals. Their calls will echo through the windows. Desolation will be in the thresholds, for he has laid bare the cedar beams.

kikuyu: Andũ arĩa atongu nĩ mathaga arĩa athĩĩni, na ũrĩa ũkombaga nĩ ndungata ya mũmũkoomberi.
english: The violence of the wicked will drive them away, because they refuse to do what is right.

kikuyu: Nĩwe ũtũmaga rũhuho rũtuĩke arekio ake, nacio nĩnĩmbĩ cia mwaki agacitua ndungata ciake.
english: Seek Yahweh and his strength. Seek his face forever more.

kikuyu: Jehova aroiga atĩrĩ, “O na rĩu-rĩ, njokererai na ngoro cianyu ciothe, mwĩhingĩte kũrĩa irio, mũkĩrĩraga na mũgĩcakayaga.”
english: “Yet even now,” says Yahweh, “turn to me with all your heart, and with fasting, and with weeping, and with mourning.”

kikuyu: Igũrũrĩamĩtweyaciũmbeicioirĩmuoyonĩhaarĩkĩndũkĩaraganukĩahaanagatawariĩwakengaga ta mbarabu, na warĩ wa kũmakania.
english: Over the head of the living creature there was the likeness of an expanse, like an awesome crystal to look at, stretched out over their heads above.

kikuyu: Abimeleku agĩcooka agĩĩta Iburahĩmu na akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Nĩ atĩa ũguo ũtwĩkĩte? Nĩ atĩa ngwĩhĩirie, nĩguo ũndeherere niĩ na ũthamaki wakwa ũũru mũnene ũguo? Ũnjĩkĩte maũndũ mataagĩrĩire gwĩkwo.” Ningĩ Abimeleku akĩũria Iburahĩmu atĩrĩ, “Nĩ kĩĩ gĩatũmire wĩke ũguo?”
english: Then Abimelech called Abraham, and said to him, “What have you done to us? How have I sinned against you, that you have brought on me and on my kingdom a great sin? You have done deeds to me that ought not to be done!”  Zoar means “little”.

kikuyu: Njathamĩtie kanua gakwa ngahũma nĩ ũndũ wa kwĩrirĩria maathani maku.
english: I opened my mouth wide and panted, for I longed for your commandments.

kikuyu: Nĩndathiire ũtukũ ngereire Kĩhingo gĩa Kĩanda,† njerekeire Gĩthima kĩa Ndamathia na Kĩhingo gĩa Kĩara-inĩ,‡ ngĩroraga thingo cia Jerusalemu iria ciamomoretwo, na ihingo ciarĩo iria ciacinĩtwo ikaniinwo nĩ mwaki. Ningĩ ngĩthiĩ o na mbere njerekeire Kĩhingo gĩa Gĩthima na Karia-inĩ ka Mũthamaki, no hatiarĩ handũ haiganu ha kũhĩtũkĩrwo nĩ mbarathi yakwa;
english: I went out by night by the valley gate toward the jackal’s well, then to the dung gate; and I inspected the walls of Jerusalem, which were broken down, and its gates were consumed with fire.

kikuyu: Wendani wanyu nĩũkoragwo nĩ ũrĩa ũtarĩ ũhinga. Thũũragai ũndũ ũrĩa mũũru; naguo ũndũ ũrĩa mwega mũkĩũrũmagie.
english: Let love be without hypocrisy. Abhor that which is evil. Cling to that which is good.

kikuyu: Mũthenya ũcio nĩgũkagĩa njĩra njariĩ yumĩte bũrũri wa Misiri ĩgakinya Ashuri. Andũ a Ashuri nĩmagathiiaga Misiri, na o andũ a Misiri mathiiage Ashuri. Andũ a Misiri na andũ a Ashuri nĩmakahooyaga hamwe.
english: In that day there will be a highway out of Egypt to Assyria, and the Assyrian shall come into Egypt, and the Egyptian into Assyria; and the Egyptians will worship with the Assyrians.

kikuyu: Nĩekĩte maũndũ ma hinya na guoko gwake; na arĩa etĩĩi aharaganagia meciiria ma ngoro ciao.
english: He has shown strength with his arm. He has scattered the proud in the imagination of their hearts.

kikuyu: Itugĩ iria itiirĩrĩire igũrũ nĩ ithingithaga,
english: They make oil within the walls of these men. They tread wine presses, and suffer thirst.

kikuyu: na mũthamaki wa Tiriza, ũmwe; athamaki acio othe maarĩ mĩrongo ĩtatũ na ũmwe.
english: the king of Tirzah, one: all the kings thirty-one.

kikuyu: Gĩthima gĩaku kĩrorathimwo, nawe ũkenagĩre mũtumia wa ũnini waku.
english: a heart that devises wicked schemes, feet that are swift in running to mischief,

kikuyu: nĩendirie gĩthaka gĩake na akĩrehe mbeeca icio, agĩciiga magũrũ-inĩ ma atũmwo.
english: having a field, sold it and brought the money and laid it at the apostles’ feet.

kikuyu: Nacio ikĩaga kũheo ũhoti wa kũmooraga, o tiga wa kũmaherithia mĩeri ĩtano. Naruo ruo rũrĩa maiguire rwarĩ o ta ruo rwa kangʼaurũ karathĩte mũndũ.
english: If anyone desires to harm them, fire proceeds out of their mouth and devours their enemies. If anyone desires to harm them, he must be killed in this way.

kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũkoora nĩ ũndũ wa iguoyohia, akaagũa irima, nake ũrĩa ũkoimĩra irima rĩu-rĩ, akaagwatio nĩ mũtego ũcio. Ndirica cia mũiyũro wa maaĩ cia igũrũ nĩhiingũre, nacio itina cia thĩ igathingitha.
english: It will happen that he who flees from the noise of the fear will fall into the pit; and he who comes up out of the middle of the pit will be taken in the snare; for the windows on high are opened, and the foundations of the earth tremble.

kikuyu: nao athiritũ ao maarĩ: Shebania, na Hodia, na Kelita, na Pelaia, na Hanani,
english: and their brothers, Shebaniah, Hodiah, Kelita, Pelaiah, Hanan,

kikuyu: Andũ aingĩ nĩmagatherio, mathirwo nĩ gĩko merũhio, taarĩ gũkumuthwo makumuthĩtwo magathera, no arĩa aaganu nĩmagakĩrĩrĩria kwagana, na gũtirĩ o na ũmwe wao ũkaamenya gĩtũmi kĩa maũndũ macio, no arĩa oogĩ nĩmakamamenya.
english: Many will purify themselves, and make themselves white, and be refined, but the wicked will do wickedly; and none of the wicked will understand, but those who are wise will understand.

kikuyu: Githĩ ũcio wanyũũmbire kũu nda ya maitũ to we wamoombire? Githĩ tiwe watũthondekire tũrĩ kũu nda cia aa maitũ?
english: Didn’t he who made me in the womb make him? Didn’t one fashion us in the womb?

kikuyu: Kĩhingo gĩa Kĩara-inĩ gĩacookereirio nĩ Malikija mũrũ wa Rekabu, mwathi wa gĩcigo kĩa Bethi- Hakeremu. Agĩgĩaka rĩngĩ na agĩĩkĩra mĩrango, na igera cia kũmĩhinga na icuuma ciakĩo.
english: Malchijah the son of Rechab, the ruler of the district of Beth Haccherem, repaired the dung gate. He built it, and set up its doors, its bolts, and its bars.

kikuyu: Nĩnyenjete ithima mabũrũri ma kũngĩ na nganyua maaĩ kuo. Nĩhũithĩtie tũrũũĩ twa Misiri na makinya ma magũrũ makwa.’
english: I have dug and drunk water, and with the sole of my feet I will dry up all the rivers of Egypt.”

kikuyu: O nawe mwĩmenyerere, tondũ nĩakararirie ndũmĩrĩri iitũ na hinya.
english: which at the right time he will show, who is the blessed and only Ruler, the King of kings and Lord of lords.

kikuyu: Wee kĩrimũ gĩkĩ, nĩ ũira ũrakĩenda wa kuonania atĩ wĩtĩkio ũtarĩ na ciĩko nĩ wa tũhũ?
english: But do you want to know, vain man, that faith apart from works is dead?

kikuyu: O ta ũrĩa mwanake ahikagia mũirĩtu, ũguo noguo ariũ aku magaakũhikia; na o ta ũrĩa mũhikania akenagĩrĩra mũhiki wake, ũguo noguo Ngai waku agaagũkenerera.
english: I looked, and there was no one to help; and I wondered that there was no one to uphold. Therefore my own arm brought salvation to me. My own wrath upheld me.

kikuyu: Agaagwĩka atĩa, na nĩ atĩa ũngĩ makĩria ya ũguo, wee rũrĩmĩ rũrũ rũheenanagia?
english: Jerusalem is built as a city that is compact together,

kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, nĩnguunanga icooki rĩao riume ngingo-inĩ yaku, na nduange mĩnyororo ĩrĩa woheetwo nayo.”
english: Now I will break his yoke from off you, and will burst your bonds apart.”

kikuyu: nĩgeetha mũtobokie makinya manyu thakame-inĩ ya thũ cianyu, o nacio nĩmĩ cia ngui cianyu igĩe na igai rĩacio.”
english: that you may crush them, dipping your foot in blood, that the tongues of your dogs may have their portion from your enemies.”

kikuyu: O na korwo mũndũ enda gũkararania na Ngai-rĩ, ndangĩhota kũmũcookeria kĩũria o na kĩmwe harĩ ciũria ngiri.
english: If he is pleased to contend with him, he can’t answer him one time in a thousand.

kikuyu: Inyuĩ ene nĩmũũĩ atĩ ndaarutaga wĩra na moko makwa nĩ ũndũ wa mabata makwa niĩ mwene, na mabata ma andũ arĩa ndakoragwo nao.
english: But when they perceived that he was a Jew, all with one voice for a time of about two hours cried out, “Great is Artemis of the Ephesians!”

kikuyu: O ta ũrĩa aathĩtwo nĩ Jehova-rĩ, Harũni ũrĩa mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai akĩambata Kĩrĩma kĩa Horu, harĩa aakuĩrĩire mũthenya wa mbere wa mweri wa gatano, mwaka-inĩ wa mĩrongo ĩna kuuma andũ a Isiraeli moima bũrũri wa Misiri.
english: Aaron the priest went up into Mount Hor at the commandment of Yahweh and died there, in the fortieth year after the children of Israel had come out of the land of Egypt, in the fifth month, on the first day of the month.

kikuyu: Ũyũ nĩguo mũigana wa andũ arĩa Nebukadinezaru aatahire akĩmatwara bũrũri ũngĩ: mwaka-inĩ wa mũgwanja aatahire Ayahudi 3,023; * 52:21 nĩ ta mita
english: This is the number of the people whom Nebuchadnezzar carried away captive: in the seventh year, three thousand twenty-three Jews;

kikuyu: “Atĩrĩrĩ, nĩngagũtua kĩhũũri kĩa ngano, kĩhũũri kĩerũ na kĩũgĩ, kĩna magego maingĩ. Nĩũkahũũra irĩma na ũcihehenje, natuo tũrĩma ũtũtue mũũngũ.
english: Behold, I have made you into a new sharp threshing instrument with teeth. You will thresh the mountains, and beat them small, and will make the hills like chaff.

kikuyu: Ĩra mũtitũ ũcio wa mwena wa gũthini atĩrĩ: ‘Igua kiugo kĩa Jehova. Ũũ nĩguo Mwathani Jehova ekuuga: Ndĩ hakuhĩ gwakia mwaki thĩinĩ waku, naguo nĩũkaniina mĩtĩ yaku yothe, ĩrĩa mĩigũ nginya ĩrĩa mĩũmũ. Rũrĩrĩmbĩ rũu rũtikahoreka, na ũthiũ o wothe kuuma * 20:29 Bama nĩ kuuga kũndũ kũrĩa gũtũũgĩru. † 20:40 Nyũmba yothe ya Isiraeli yarũgamĩrĩire Ũthamaki wa Gũthini hamwe na wa Gathigathini.
english: Tell the forest of the south, ‘Hear Yahweh’s word: The Lord Yahweh says, “Behold, I will kindle a fire in you, and it will devour every green tree in you, and every dry tree. The burning flame will not be quenched, and all faces from the south to the north will be burned by it.

kikuyu: Rũmagiai irĩra ciakwa cia watho na mũcirũmagĩrĩre. Niĩ nĩ niĩ Jehova, ũrĩa ũmũtheragia.
english: Even as Jannes and Jambres opposed Moses, so these also oppose the truth, men corrupted in mind, who concerning the faith are rejected.

kikuyu: Nacio mbarĩ cia Amerari (nao nĩo Alawii arĩa angĩ maatigaire) makĩheo: kuuma mũhĩrĩga wa Zebuluni, nĩ itũũra rĩa Jokineamu, na Karita,
english: To the families of the children of Merari, the rest of the Levites, out of the tribe of Zebulun, Jokneam with its pasture lands, Kartah with its pasture lands,

kikuyu: Handũ ha ũguo-rĩ, mũthigari ũmwe agĩtheeca Jesũ mbaru na itimũ, na o rĩmwe hakiura thakame na maaĩ. Nake mũndũ ũrĩa wonire maũndũ macio nĩarutĩte ũira, naguo ũira wake nĩ wa ma. We nĩooĩ atĩ aaragia ũhoro wa ma, na oimbũraga nĩgeetha o na inyuĩ mwĩtĩkie.
english: However, one of the soldiers pierced his side with a spear, and immediately blood and water came out.  “the sixth hour” would have been 6:00 a.m. according to the Roman timekeeping system, or noon for the Jewish timekeeping system in use, then.

kikuyu: Ũtukũ ũmwe-rĩ, o ũmwe witũ akĩroota kĩroto, na o kĩroto kĩarĩ na ũtaũri wakĩo mwanya. Na rĩrĩ, kũu njeera twarĩ na mwanake Mũhibirania, ndungata ya mũnene wa arangĩri. Na ithuĩ tũkĩmwĩra irooto ciitũ, nake agĩtũtaũrĩra; akĩhe o mũndũ ũtaũri wa kĩroto gĩake.
english: We dreamed a dream in one night, he and I. Each man dreamed according to the interpretation of his dream.

kikuyu: Ũtukũ ndirikanaga rĩĩtwa rĩaku, ngakĩrũmia watho waku Wee Jehova.
english: I have remembered your name, Yahweh, in the night, and I obey your law.

kikuyu: Mũtĩ wa gatano wagwĩrĩire mũhĩrĩga wa Asheri, kũringana na ũrĩa mbarĩ ciao ciatariĩ.
english: The fifth lot came out for the tribe of the children of Asher according to their families.

kikuyu: Jakubu akĩĩra Jusufu atĩrĩ, “Ngai Mwene-Hinya-Wothe nĩanyumĩrĩire ndĩ Luzu bũrũri-inĩ wa Kaanani, na akĩndathima,
english: Jacob said to Joseph, “God Almighty appeared to me at Luz in the land of Canaan, and blessed me,

kikuyu: Andũ aku nĩo magaacookereria kũrĩa kwaanangĩtwo o tene, na maakĩrĩre mĩthingi ĩrĩa ya tene mũno; nawe ũgeetagwo Mũcookereria wa Thingo iria Momoku, na wĩtagwo Mũcookereria wa Njĩra cia Itũũro.
english: Those who will be of you will build the old waste places. You will raise up the foundations of many generations. You will be called Repairer of the Breach, Restorer of Paths with Dwellings.

kikuyu: Mathĩĩna ma ngoro yakwa nĩmaingĩhĩte; niinĩra ruo rũrĩa ndĩ naruo.
english: The troubles of my heart are enlarged. Oh bring me out of my distresses.

kikuyu: Mũirĩtu ũrĩa mũkũrũ agĩciara kahĩĩ na agĩgatua Moabi, na nĩwe ithe wa andũ a Moabi gwata ũmũthĩ.
english: The firstborn bore a son, and named him Moab. He is the father of the Moabites to this day.

kikuyu: Mũngĩgaakora nyamũ ĩkuĩte, kana ĩkooragwo nĩ nyamũ cia gĩthaka, maguta mayo no mahũthĩrwo na ũndũ ũngĩ o wothe, no mũtikanamarĩe.
english: The fat of that which dies of itself, and the fat of that which is torn of animals, may be used for any other service, but you shall in no way eat of it.

kikuyu: Hĩ! Kaĩ itũũra inene rĩrĩa rĩĩhokeku nĩrĩtuĩkĩte ta mũmaraya-ĩ! Hĩndĩ ĩmwe rĩaiyũrĩte ũtuanĩri ciira wa kĩhooto; ũthingu nĩguo watũũraga thĩinĩ warĩo, no rĩu nĩ oragani matũũraga kuo!
english: How the faithful city has become a prostitute! She was full of justice. Righteousness lodged in her, but now there are murderers.

kikuyu: “Ĩno nĩyo ndungata yakwa ĩrĩa ndĩĩthuurĩire, ĩrĩa nyendete, na nĩyo ĩkenagia ngoro yakwa; nĩngamĩitĩrĩria Roho wakwa, nayo nĩĩkamenyithia ndũrĩrĩ ciira wa kĩhooto.
english: For John came neither eating nor drinking, and they say, ‘He has a demon.’

kikuyu: Nĩndĩrĩathĩkagĩra kĩrĩra kĩa watho waku; ndũgakĩndiganĩrie o biũ. Gwathĩkĩra Kiugo kĩa Jehova
english: For my brothers’ and companions’ sakes, I will now say, “Peace be within you.”

kikuyu: Matũũra marĩa mũkaahe Alawii kuuma kũrĩ bũrũri ũcio andũ a Isiraeli makegwatĩra, makaaheanwo kũringana na ũrĩa igai rĩa o mũhĩrĩga rĩigana: Oyai matũũra maingĩ kuuma kũrĩ mũhĩrĩga ũrĩa ũrĩ na matũũra maingĩ, na muoe matũũra manini kuuma kũrĩ mũhĩrĩga ũrĩa ũrĩ matũũra manini.” Ningĩ Jehova akĩĩra Musa atĩrĩ:
english: Concerning the cities which you shall give of the possession of the children of  A cubit is the length from the tip of the middle finger to the elbow on a man’s arm, or about

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa arutwo acio eerĩ maiguire oiga ũguo, makĩrũmĩrĩra Jesũ.
english: The two disciples heard him speak, and they followed Jesus.

kikuyu: Jakubu akĩhaanda gĩtugĩ igũrũ rĩa mbĩrĩra yake, na nginya ũmũthĩ ũyũ gĩtugĩ kĩu nĩkĩo kĩonanagia mbĩrĩra ya Rakeli.
english: These are the sons of Seir the Horite, the inhabitants of the land: Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah,

kikuyu: akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Thiĩi itũũra rĩĩrĩa rĩrĩ mbere yanyu, na mwatoonya o ũguo, nĩmũkuona njaũ ya ndigiri yohetwo hau, nayo ndĩrĩ yakuua mũndũ. Mĩohorei mũmĩrehe. Na mũngĩũrio nĩ mũndũ atĩrĩ, ‘Mũreka ũguo nĩkĩ?’ Mwĩrei atĩrĩ, ‘Mwathani nĩabatarĩtio nĩyo na nĩekũmĩcookia haha o narua.’ ” Nao magĩthiĩ magĩkora njaũ ya ndigiri yohetwo mũromo-inĩ handũ nja, hakuhĩ na njĩra. Na rĩrĩa maamĩohoraga-rĩ,
english: and said to them, “Go your way into the village that is opposite you. Immediately as you enter into it, you will find a young donkey tied, on which no one has sat. Untie him and bring him.

kikuyu: “Niĩ ndiagĩrĩire o na hanini; ndaakĩhota atĩa gũgũcookeria? Nĩndehumbĩra kanua na guoko.
english: “Behold, I am of small account. What will I answer you? I lay my hand on my mouth.

kikuyu: O ta ũrĩa nduba ĩĩkaga rĩrĩa ĩtunyĩtwo twana twayo-rĩ, ngaamatharĩkĩra na ndĩmatarũrange. Ngaamarĩa ta mũrũũthi, ndĩmatambuure o ta ũrĩa nyamũ ya gĩthaka ĩĩkaga.
english: Ephraim, what have I to do any more with idols? I answer, and will take care of him. I am like a green cypress tree; from me your fruit is found.”

kikuyu: ũkaamacookeria, ũmeere atĩrĩ, ‘Mwathani Jehova ekuuga ũũ: Nĩnguoya gathanju kau ka Jusufu na mĩhĩrĩga ya Isiraeli ĩyo ĩkuruhanĩte nake, o kau karĩ guoko-inĩ kwa Efiraimu, ndĩkoohanie hamwe na gathanju ka Juda, tũtuĩke rũthanju rũmwe, natuo tũtuĩke rũthanju rũmwe guoko-inĩ gwakwa.’
english: For in my jealousy and in the fire of my wrath I have spoken. Surely in that day there will be a great shaking in the land of Israel,

kikuyu: na ndirahũgĩ igĩrĩ kana tũtutuura twĩrĩ, o iria angĩhota kũruta, ĩmwe ĩtuĩke ya iruta rĩa kũhoroherio mehia, na ĩrĩa ĩngĩ ya iruta rĩa njino.
english: If it spreads in the skin, then the priest shall pronounce him unclean. It is a plague.

kikuyu: Andũ a Juda o nao nĩmakarũĩra kũu Jerusalemu. Ũtonga wa ndũrĩrĩ iria irigiicĩirie kũu nĩũkonganio, naguo nĩ ũtonga mũingĩ wa thahabu, na wa betha na wa nguo.
english: Judah also will fight at Jerusalem; and the wealth of all the surrounding nations will be gathered together: gold, silver, and clothing, in great abundance.

kikuyu: Mahiga ma ratiri mataiganainie ũritũ nĩ kĩndũ kĩrĩ magigi harĩ Jehova, nacio ratiri itarĩ cia ma itimũkenagia.
english: Yahweh detests differing weights, and dishonest scales are not pleasing.

kikuyu: Mũthenya ũcio nĩũrooka! Ti-itherũ nĩũgakinya, ũguo nĩguo Mwathani Jehova ekuuga. Ũcio nĩguo mũthenya ũrĩa njarĩtie ũhoro waguo.
english: Behold, it comes, and it will be done,” says the Lord Yahweh. “This is the day about which I have spoken.

kikuyu: “Na rĩrĩ, Ngai witũ, ĩtĩkĩra kũigua mahooya na mathaithana ma ndungata ĩno yaku. Nĩ ũndũ wa rĩĩtwa rĩaku Wee Mwathani, iguĩra handũ haku hatheru tha, o hau hakirĩte ihooru.
english: For how can the servant of this my lord talk with this my lord? For as for me, immediately there remained no strength in me. There was no breath left in me.”

kikuyu: Ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ, mũrogĩa na thayũ, na wendani hamwe na wĩtĩkio ciumĩte kũrĩ Ngai Ithe witũ o na kuuma kũrĩ Mwathani witũ Jesũ Kristũ.
english: Peace be to the brothers, and love with faith, from God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.

kikuyu: Nĩthũire arĩa mahooyaga ngai cia mĩhianano ĩtarĩ kĩene; niĩ ndĩhokete o Jehova.
english: By Yahweh’s word, the heavens were made: all their army by the breath of his mouth.

kikuyu: Kũrathimwo-rĩ, nĩ arĩa mahũũtagĩra na makanyootera ũthingu, nĩgũkorwo nĩmakahũũnio.
english: Blessed are those who hunger and thirst for righteousness, for they shall be filled.

kikuyu: nĩ ũndũ ndũigana kũhinga mĩrango ya nda ya maitũ, na ndũigana kũgirĩrĩria maitho makwa kuona thĩĩna.
english: The roaring of the lion, and the voice of the fierce lion, the teeth of the young lions, are broken.

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio ndũmĩrĩri ya Jehova ĩndeeheire o kũrumwo na kũnyararwo mũthenya wothe. No ingiuga atĩrĩ, “Ndigũcooka kũmũgweta kana kwaria ngĩgwetaga rĩĩtwa rĩake,” ndũmĩrĩri yake ĩrĩa ĩrĩ ngoro-inĩ yakwa ĩhaana ta mwaki, o ta mwaki ũhingĩrĩirio mahĩndĩ-inĩ makwa. Nĩnogetio nĩkũmĩiga thĩinĩ wakwa, na ti-itherũ ndingĩhota.
english: If I say that I will not make mention of him, or speak any more in his name, then there is in my heart as it were a burning fire shut up in my bones. I am weary with holding it in. I can’t.

kikuyu: Jesũ ehũgũra akĩmoona mamumĩte thuutha, akĩmooria atĩrĩ, “Nĩ atĩa mũrenda?” Nao makĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Rabii” (ũguo nĩ kuuga Mũrutani), “ũikaraga kũ?” Nake akĩmacookeria atĩrĩ, “Ũkai na nĩmũkuona.” Nĩ ũndũ ũcio magĩthiĩ makĩona kũrĩa aikaraga, na magĩikara nake mũthenya ũcio. Na kwarĩ ta thaa ikũmi. Na rĩrĩ, ũmwe wa arutwo acio eerĩ arĩa maiguĩte ũrĩa Johana oigĩte na makĩrũmĩrĩra Jesũ aarĩ Anderea, mũrũ wa nyina na Simoni Petero.
english: Jesus turned and saw them following, and said to them, “What are you looking for?” They said to him, “Rabbi” (which is to say, being interpreted, Teacher), “where are you staying?”

kikuyu: Hau nĩho Iburahĩmu na mũtumia wake Sara maathikirwo, na no ho Isaaka na Rebeka mũtumia wake maathikirwo, na no ho ndaathikire Lea.
english: There they buried Abraham and Sarah, his wife. There they buried Isaac and Rebekah, his wife, and there I buried Leah:

kikuyu: ngĩo icio cia andũ acio makuĩte nĩ ũndũ wa mehia mao. Ngĩo icio nĩiturwo ituĩke mabati ma kũhumbĩra kĩgongona, nĩ ũndũ nĩciarehetwo mbere ya Jehova, na igatuĩka nyamũre. Nacio nĩ ituĩke kĩmenyithia harĩ andũ a Isiraeli.” Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Eleazaru ũrĩa mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai akĩoya ngĩo icio cia gĩcango ciarehetwo nĩ andũ acio maacinĩtwo, nake agĩciturithia ituĩke cia kũhumbĩra kĩgongona,
english: even the censers of those who sinned against their own lives. Let them be beaten into plates for a covering of the altar, for they offered them before Yahweh. Therefore they are holy. They shall be a sign to the children of Israel.”

kikuyu: Tiga kũũhitha ũthiũ waku rĩrĩa ndĩ mĩnyamaro-inĩ. Inamĩrĩria gũtũ gwaku ũnjigue; ndagũkaĩra, njĩtĩka o narua.
english: Don’t hide your face from me in the day of my distress. Turn your ear to me. Answer me quickly in the day when I call.

kikuyu: Nĩgeetha njarahũre mangʼũrĩ na ndĩĩrĩhĩrie-rĩ, ndaitire thakame yarĩo rwaro-inĩ rwa ihiga itheri, nĩguo ndĩgathikwo.
english: That it may cause wrath to come up to take vengeance, I have set her blood on the bare rock, that it should not be covered.”

kikuyu: Wee na arũmĩrĩri aku othe mũnganĩte hamwe mũũkĩrĩre Jehova. Harũni nũũ atĩ nĩguo mũnugune nĩ ũndũ wake?”
english: Only Luke is with me. Take Mark and bring him with you, for he is useful to me for service.

kikuyu: Nda yayo nĩ ta rũgĩo rũrĩ na mageca; ĩtigaga ngururo ndaka-inĩ ta cia ngaari iria ikonyoraga ngano.
english: His undersides are like sharp potsherds, leaving a trail in the mud like a threshing sledge.

kikuyu: Ũguo noguo rĩrĩa mũkoona maũndũ macio mothe, mũkaamenya atĩ ihinda rĩrĩ hakuhĩ, rĩrĩ o ta mũrango-inĩ.
english: Even so you also, when you see all these things, know that he is near, even at the doors.

kikuyu: Mũrangĩri wa njeera akĩarahũka, na rĩrĩa aarorire akĩona mĩrango ya njeera ĩrĩ mĩhingũku, akĩruta rũhiũ rwake rwa njora akĩenda kwĩyũraga tondũ eeciiririe atĩ andũ arĩa moohetwo nĩmoorĩte.* No Paũlũ akĩanĩrĩra, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Ndũkeegere ngero! Ithuothe tũrĩ ho!” Nake mũrangĩri ũcio wa njeera agĩĩtia matawa, agĩcooka akĩhanyũka thĩinĩ akĩinainaga, akĩĩgũithia mbere ya Paũlũ na Sila.
english: The jailer, being roused out of sleep and seeing the prison doors open, drew his sword and was about to kill himself, supposing that the prisoners had escaped.

kikuyu: Ti-itherũ muoyo waguo ũmaga ũgathira, naho hau tĩĩri-inĩ ũcio hagakũra mĩtĩ ĩngĩ.
english: If it is a matter of strength, behold, he is mighty! If of justice, ‘Who,’ says he, ‘will summon me?’

kikuyu: Afarisai amwe maarĩ gĩkundi-inĩ kĩu makĩĩra Jesũ atĩrĩ, “Mũrutani, kaania arutwo aku!”
english: Some of the Pharisees from the multitude said to him, “Teacher, rebuke your disciples!”

kikuyu: O makwĩroreire, tũrĩkia rũthingo, ũgererie mĩrigo yaku ho.
english: Dig through the wall in their sight, and carry your baggage out that way.

kikuyu: Mũndũ nĩaiguaga gĩkeno agĩcookia ũhoro ũrĩa wagĩrĩire, nakĩo kiugo kĩarĩtio ihinda rĩrĩa rĩagĩrĩire, kaĩ nĩ kĩega-ĩ!
english: Joy comes to a man with the reply of his mouth. How good is a word at the right time!

kikuyu: Mũndũ mũthingu nĩathũire ũhoro ũtarĩ wa ma, no mũndũ mwaganu ekaga maũndũ ma thoni na ma njono.
english: A righteous man hates lies, but a wicked man brings shame and disgrace.

kikuyu: Nuthu ya igai rĩa andũ arĩa maarũĩte mbaara-inĩ ciarĩ: ngʼondu 337,500,
english: The half, which was the portion of those who went out to war, was in number three hundred thirty-seven thousand five hundred sheep;

kikuyu: Rĩu-rĩ, gũtirĩ mũndũ ũngĩhota kũrora riũa, rĩcangararĩte kũu igũrũ thuutha wakuo kũhaatwo nĩ rũhuho gũgathera.
english: Surely you know, for you were born then, and the number of your days is great!

kikuyu: Thĩ nĩĩkanangĩka ĩkire ihooru nĩ ũndũ wa andũ arĩa matũũraga kuo, marĩ maciaro ma ciĩko ciao.
english: Yet the land will be desolate because of those who dwell therein, for the fruit of their doings.

kikuyu: Menyagĩrĩra niĩ ta kĩũma kĩa riitho rĩaku; hitha kĩĩruru-inĩ kĩa mathagu maku
english: Keep me as the apple of your eye. Hide me under the shadow of your wings,

kikuyu: hĩndĩ ĩyo rũhiũ rwa njora rũu mwĩtigĩrĩte, nĩrũkamũhĩta kuo, na ngʼaragu ĩyo ĩramũmakia nĩĩkamũrũmĩrĩra nginya bũrũri wa Misiri, na kũu nĩkuo mũgaakuĩra.
english: then it will happen that the sword, which you fear, will overtake you there in the land of Egypt; and the famine, about which you are afraid, will follow close behind you there in Egypt; and you will die there.

kikuyu: Gĩai na hinya na mwĩyũmĩrĩrie ngoro, inyuĩ inyuothe arĩa mwĩhokete Jehova.
english: Be strong, and let your heart take courage, all you who hope in Yahweh.

kikuyu: Mũrungu-rĩ, njĩra ciake nĩnginyanĩru; kiugo kĩa Jehova gĩtirĩ ihĩtia. We nĩwe ngo ya arĩa othe mehithaga harĩ we. Nĩ ũndũ-rĩ, nũũ Mũrungu tiga Jehova? Na nũũ Rwaro rwa Ihiga tiga Ngai witũ? Nĩ Mũrungu ũũheaga hinya na agatũma njĩra yakwa ĩkinyanĩre.
english: As for God, his way is perfect. Yahweh’s word is tried. He is a shield to all those who take refuge in him.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo Danieli (na nowe wĩtagwo Beliteshazaru) akĩamba gũtangĩka mũno kwa ihinda, na akĩmaka mũno meciiria-inĩ make. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio mũthamaki akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Beliteshazaru, tiga kũmakio nĩ kĩroto kĩu kana ũtaũri wakĩo.” Beliteshazaru agĩcookia atĩrĩ, “Mwathi wakwa, naarĩ korwo kĩroto kĩu kĩerekererio thũ ciaku, o na ũtaũri wakĩo wathĩrĩrio andũ arĩa magũthũire!
english: Then Daniel, whose name was Belteshazzar, was stricken mute for a while, and his thoughts troubled him. The king answered, “Belteshazzar, don’t let the dream or the interpretation, trouble you.” Belteshazzar answered, “My lord, may the dream be for those who hate you, and its interpretation to your adversaries.

kikuyu: na angĩkorwo nĩatuĩrĩire matũũra ma Sodomu na Gomora ciira na ũndũ wa kũmacina magĩtuĩka mũhu, na akĩmatua kĩonereria kĩa ũrĩa gũgaatuĩka kũrĩ andũ arĩa matetĩkĩtie Ngai;
english: In this you greatly rejoice, though now for a little while, if need be, you have been grieved in various trials,

kikuyu: Andũ acio othe marĩ hamwe maarĩ 42,360, 67gũtatarĩtwo ndungata ciao cia arũme na cia andũ- a-nja 7, 337; ningĩ nĩ maarĩ na aini arũme na andũ-a-nja 245.
english: The whole assembly together was forty-two thousand three hundred sixty,

kikuyu: Agĩcooka akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Ũrĩa wothe wamũkagĩra kaana gaka thĩinĩ wa rĩĩtwa rĩakwa, nĩ niĩ aamũkagĩra; na ũrĩa ũnyamũkagĩra ti niĩ aamũkagĩra, no nĩ ũrĩa wandũmire. Nĩgũkorwo ũrĩa mũnini gatagatĩ-inĩ kanyu inyuothe, ũcio nĩwe mũnene kũmũkĩra.”
english: and said to them, “Whoever receives this little child in my name receives me. Whoever receives me receives him who sent me. For whoever is least among you all, this one will be great.”

kikuyu: Nakuo kũgũrũ gwaku kũngĩtũma wĩhie, gũtinie. Nĩ kaba ũtoonye muoyo-inĩ ũrĩ gĩthua, gũkĩra ũikio Jehanamu ũrĩ na magũrũ meerĩ.
english: If your foot causes you to stumble, cut it off. It is better for you to enter into life lame, rather than having your two feet to be cast into Gehenna, § into the fire that will never be quenched—

kikuyu: Nĩmũtũũgĩrĩtie ihooero rĩa Moleku, na njata ya ngai yanyu ĩrĩa ĩĩtagwo Refani, mĩhianano ĩrĩa mwathondekire ya kũhooyaga. Tondũ ũcio, nĩngatũma mũtahwo mũtwarwo’ kũhĩtũka Babuloni.
english: You took up the tabernacle of Moloch, the star of your god Rephan, the figures which you made to worship, so I will carry you away* beyond Babylon.’

kikuyu: Isaaka agĩaka kĩgongona kũu, na agĩkaĩra rĩĩtwa rĩa Jehova. Akĩamba hema yake o kũu, nacio ndungata ciake ikĩenja gĩthima o kuo.
english: He built an altar there, and called on Yahweh’s name, and pitched his tent there. There Isaac’s servants dug a well.

kikuyu: Mahe ndũmĩrĩri matwarĩre anene ao, ũmeere atĩrĩ, ‘Ũũ nĩguo Mwene-Hinya-Wothe, Ngai wa Isiraeli, ekuuga: “Ĩrai anene anyu atĩrĩ:
english: You shall tell them, “Yahweh says: ‘If you will not listen to me, to walk in my law which I have set before you,

kikuyu: Adamu agĩĩta mũtumia wake Hawa, tondũ nĩwe watuĩkire nyina wa arĩa othe matũũraga muoyo. Ngai kũingata Adamu na Hawa kuuma Edeni
english: Adah gave birth to Jabal, who was the father of those who dwell in tents and have livestock.

kikuyu: Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe, Ngai wa Isiraeli, ekuuga atĩrĩ: Inyuĩ na atumia anyu nĩmuonanĩtie na ciĩko cianyu ũrĩa mweranĩire rĩrĩa mwoigire atĩrĩ, ‘Gũtirĩ nganja, ithuĩ no nginya tũhingie mĩĩhĩtwa ĩrĩa twehĩtire atĩ nĩtũrĩcinagĩra Mũthamaki-Mũndũ- wa-nja wa kũũrĩa Igũrũ ũbumba, na nĩtũrĩmũrutagĩra maruta ma indo cia kũnyuuo.’ “Thiĩi na mbere, ĩkai ũrĩa mweranĩire! Hingiai mĩĩhĩtwa yanyu! No rĩrĩ, iguai ndũmĩrĩri ya Jehova, inyuĩAyahudiothearĩamũtũũragabũrũriwaMisiri: Jehovaekuugaatĩrĩ, ‘Nĩndĩhĩtĩtenarĩĩtwarĩakwa inene, ngoiga gũtirĩ mũndũ o na ũmwe wa kuuma Juda ũtũũraga handũ o hothe thĩinĩ wa bũrũri wa Misiri ũgaacooka kũgweta rĩĩtwa rĩakwa kana ehĩte akiugaga atĩrĩ, “Ti-itherũ o ta ũrĩa Mwathani Jehova atũũraga muoyo.”
english: Yahweh of Armies, the God of Israel, says, ‘You and your wives have both spoken with your mouths, and with your hands have fulfilled it, saying, “We will surely perform our vows that we have vowed, to burn incense to the queen of the sky, and to pour out drink offerings to her.” “ ‘Establish then your vows, and perform your vows.’

kikuyu: Hĩ, kaĩ ũrĩa ũragia ithe atĩrĩ, ‘Nĩ kĩĩ kĩu wagĩire?’ kana akooria nyina atĩrĩ, ‘Nĩ kĩĩ kĩu waciara?’ Kaĩ arĩ na haaro-ĩ!
english: I declare the end from the beginning, and from ancient times things that are not yet done. I say: My counsel will stand, and I will do all that I please.

kikuyu: Maũndũ macio mathondeketwo o rĩu, na ti ma tene; ndũrĩ waigua ũhoro ũcio, waũigua ũmũthĩ. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio ndũngiuga atĩrĩ, ‘Maũndũ macio nĩndamenyete ũhoro wamo.’
english: Yahweh, the Redeemer of Israel, and his Holy One, says to him whom man despises, to him whom the nation abhors, to a servant of rulers: “Kings shall see and rise up, princes, and they shall worship, because of Yahweh who is faithful, even the Holy One of Israel, who has chosen you.”

kikuyu: Acio moimĩte Isiraeli! Njaũ ĩno nĩ mũturi ũmĩthondekete; yo ti Ngai. Njaũ ĩyo ya Samaria-rĩ, nĩĩkoinangwo icunjĩ.
english: For, behold, when they flee destruction, Egypt will gather them up. Memphis will bury them. Nettles will possess their pleasant things of silver. Thorns will be in their tents.

kikuyu: Nĩũhũthũ ngamĩĩra ĩhungurĩre irima rĩa cindano, gũkĩra gĩtonga gũtoonya ũthamaki-inĩ wa Ngai.” Nao arĩa maiguire ũhoro ũcio makĩũria atĩrĩ, “Nũũ ũngĩkĩhota kũhonoka?”
english: For it is easier for a camel to enter in through a needle’s eye than for a rich man to enter into God’s Kingdom.”

kikuyu: Girĩkai, nĩgũkorwo mũthenya wa Jehova nĩũkuhĩrĩirie; ũgooka ũtariĩ ta mwanangĩko uumĩte kũrĩ Mwene-Hinya-Wothe. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio nĩngatũma igũrũ rĩinaine; nayo thĩ nĩĩgaathingitha, yehere gĩikaro kĩayo,
english: Wail, for Yahweh’s day is at hand! It will come as destruction from the Almighty.

kikuyu: marĩa Jehova aaheire Musa arĩ Kĩrĩma-inĩ gĩa Sinai, mũthenya ũrĩa aathire andũ a Isiraeli marehagĩre Jehova maruta mao kũu Werũ-inĩ wa Sinai.
english: which Yahweh commanded Moses in Mount Sinai in the day that he commanded the children of Israel to offer their offerings to Yahweh, in the wilderness of Sinai.

kikuyu: “Wee Jehova, ririkana ũrĩa ngoretwo ngĩthiĩ na mĩthiĩre ya kwĩhokeka mbere yaku, na ngeheana na ngoro yakwa yothe, na ngeeka ũrĩa kwagĩrĩire maitho-inĩ maku.” Nake Hezekia akĩrĩra mũno.
english: and said, “Remember now, Yahweh, I beg you, how I have walked before you in truth and with a perfect heart, and have done that which is good in your sight.” Then Hezekiah wept bitterly.

kikuyu: No Juda agĩthiĩ harĩ we, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Ndagũthaitha mwathi wakwa, ĩtĩkĩria ndungata yaku yarĩrie mwathi wakwa. Ndũkarakarĩre ndungata yaku o na gũtuĩka ũiganaine na Firaũni we mwene.
english: Then Judah came near to him, and said, “Oh, my lord, please let your servant speak a word in my lord’s ears, and don’t let your anger burn against your servant; for you are even as Pharaoh.

kikuyu: mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai ũcio nĩakoima nja ya nyũmba ĩyo na acooke amĩhinge gwa kahinda ka mĩthenya mũgwanja.
english: “When a man or a woman has bright spots in the skin of the body, even white bright spots,

kikuyu: Andũ a Gadi nĩmakire Diboni, na Atarothu, na Aroeri,
english: The children of Gad built Dibon, Ataroth, Aroer,

kikuyu: Aya nĩo Alawii arĩa maatarirwo kũringana na mbarĩ ciao: kuuma kũrĩ Gerishoni, nĩ mbarĩ ya Agerishoni; kuuma kũrĩ Kohathu, nĩ mbarĩ ya Akohathu; kuuma kũrĩ Merari, nĩ mbarĩ ya Amerari.
english: These are those who were counted of the Levites after their families: of Gershon, the family of the Gershonites; of Kohath, the family of the Kohathites; of Merari, the family of the Merarites.

kikuyu: Ngai nĩatuĩte o mbere atĩ nĩtũgaatuĩka ciana ciake nĩ ũndũ wa Jesũ Kristũ, kũringana na ũrĩa eendete na ũrĩa oonire arĩ wega,
english: having predestined us for adoption as children through Jesus Christ to himself, according to the good pleasure of his desire,

kikuyu: Rĩĩaga irio icio o ta ũrĩa ũngĩrĩa mũgate wa cairi; ũũruge andũ makĩonaga, ũhũthĩrĩte mwaki wakĩtio na mai ma mũndũ.”
english: You shall eat it as barley cakes, and you shall bake it in their sight with dung that comes out of man.”

kikuyu: O ta ũrĩa gĩkerenge kĩiyũraga nyoni, ũguo nĩguo nyũmba ciao ciyũrĩte ũheenania; matongete na makagĩa na hinya,
english: As a cage is full of birds, so are their houses full of deceit. Therefore they have become great, and grew rich.

kikuyu: Rĩra na ũgirĩke, wee mũrũ wa mũndũ, nĩgũkorwo nĩrũũkĩrĩire andũ akwa, o na rũgookĩrĩra athamaki othe a Isiraeli. Nĩmaneanĩtwo rũhiũ-inĩ rwa njora marĩ hamwe na andũ akwa. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio wĩhũũre gĩthũri.
english: For if the readiness is there, it is acceptable according to what you have, not according to what you don’t have.

kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũmenyagĩrĩra kanua gake nĩ muoyo wake amenyagĩrĩra, no ũrĩa ũhiũhaga kwaria nĩakanangĩka.
english: He who guards his mouth guards his soul. One who opens wide his lips comes to ruin.

kikuyu: Nako kaana kau gagĩkũra, gakĩgĩa na hinya; gakĩiyũrwo nĩ ũũgĩ, naguo wega wa Ngai warĩ hamwe nako. Jesũ arĩ Kamwana thĩinĩ wa Hekarũ
english: The child was growing, and was becoming strong in spirit, being filled with wisdom, and the grace of God was upon him.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo thĩ nĩĩkaiyũrwo nĩ ũmenyo wa riiri wa Jehova, o ta ũrĩa maaĩ maiyũrĩte iria-inĩ.
english: For the earth will be filled with the knowledge of Yahweh’s glory, as the waters cover the sea.

kikuyu: Rwĩmbo rwa Kwĩhoka Jehova Thaburi ya Daudi
english: For the Chief Musician. By David.

kikuyu: mũrũ wa Simeoni, mũrũ wa Juda, mũrũ wa Jusufu, mũrũ wa Jonamu, mũrũ wa Eliakimu,
english: the son of Simeon, the son of Judah, the son of Joseph, the son of Jonan, the son of Eliakim,

kikuyu: We na atumia acio eerĩ a ariũ ake makiumagara kuuma kũu maatũũraga, makĩnyiita njĩra nĩguo macooke bũrũri wa Juda. Nake Naomi akĩĩra atumia acio eerĩ a ariũ ake atĩrĩ, “Cookai, o mũndũ athiĩ mũciĩ kwa nyina.† Jehova aromwĩka maũndũ mega, o ta ũrĩa o na inyuĩ mwekire acio maakuire, o na niĩ mwene.
