english: She went out of the place where she was, and her two daughters-in-law with her. They went on the way to return to the land of Judah.

kikuyu: Ariũ a ithe na Jusufu Gũthiĩ Misiri Rĩrĩa Jakubu aamenyire atĩ Misiri nĩ kwarĩ na ngano, akĩĩra ariũ ake atĩrĩ, “Nĩ kĩĩ gĩtũmĩte mũikare o ro ũguo mũcũthanĩrĩirie?”
english: 1 Now Jacob saw that there was grain in Egypt, and Jacob said to his sons, “Why do you look at one another?”

kikuyu: tondũ nĩ ũndũ mwega hĩndĩ ĩrĩa ũkũmaiga ngoro-inĩ yaku, na ũkoragwo namo mothe mĩromo-inĩ yaku.
english: For it is a pleasant thing if you keep them within you, if all of them are ready on your lips.

kikuyu: Mũhĩrĩga ũrĩa mũnini wa Benjamini nĩguo ũcio ũmatongoretie, kĩrĩndĩ kĩnene kĩa athamaki a Juda kĩrĩ ho, na makarũmĩrĩrwo nĩ athamaki a Zebuluni na a Nafitali.
english: There is little Benjamin, their ruler, the princes of Judah, their council, the princes of Zebulun, and the princes of Naphtali.

kikuyu: “Ngerekano ĩyo yugĩte atĩrĩ: Mbeũ icio nĩ ũhoro wa Ngai.
english: “Now the parable is this: The seed is the word of God.

kikuyu: Nĩngaingĩhia maciaro ma mĩtĩ na maciaro ma mũgũnda, nĩgeetha mũtikanacooke kũnyararwo mũrĩ thĩinĩ wa ndũrĩrĩ tondũ wa ngʼaragu.
english: I will multiply the fruit of the tree and the increase of the field, that you may receive no more the reproach of famine among the nations.

kikuyu: Tondũ ũcio no tũthiĩ na atongoria amwe anyu nĩgeetha mathiĩ magathitangĩre mũndũ ũcio kuo, angĩkorwo nĩ harĩ ũndũ mũũru ekĩte.”
english: “Let them therefore”, he said, “that are in power among you go down with me, and if there is anything wrong in the man, let them accuse him.”  TR adds “but the commanding officer, Lysias, came by and with great violence took him out of our hands,”  TR adds “commanding his accusers to come to you.”

kikuyu: Wee Jehova, nĩwĩoneire ũguo; tiga gũkira. Wee Mwathani-rĩ, ndũgaikare haraaya na niĩ.
english: You have seen it, Yahweh. Don’t keep silent. Lord, don’t be far from me.

kikuyu: Mwĩcũraniei nĩguo mũtikogomie kĩhooto; njookererai, nĩgũkorwo wĩhokeku wakwa nĩguo ũraarũithio. Nĩ kũrĩ wĩhia ũrĩ mĩromo-inĩ yakwa? Anga kanua gakwa gatingĩhota gũkũũrana maũndũ ma rũmena?
english: Please return. Let there be no injustice. Yes, return again. My cause is righteous.

kikuyu: o ta ũrĩa mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai ũrĩa mũnene na Kĩama gĩothe gĩa athuuri o nao mangĩheana ũira ũcio. O na nĩmaheire marũa ndwarĩre andũ ao kũu Dameski, na niĩ ngĩthiĩ kuo nganyiite andũ acio ndĩmarehe Jerusalemu nĩguo maherithio.
english: as also the high priest and all the council of the elders testify, from whom also I received letters to the brothers, and traveled to Damascus to bring them also who were there to Jerusalem in bonds to be punished.

kikuyu: Nacio njiaro cia Isakaru kũringana na mbarĩ ciao ciarĩ ici: kuuma kũrĩ Tola, nĩ mbarĩ ya Atola; kuuma kũrĩ Puva nĩ mbarĩ ya Apuva;
english: The sons of Issachar after their families: of Tola, the family of the Tolaites; of Puvah, the family of the Punites; 24of Jashub, the family of the Jashubites; of Shimron, the family of the Shimronites.

kikuyu: no ngĩĩra mũthamaki atĩrĩ, “Mũthamaki arotũũra nginya tene! Gĩthiithi gĩakwa kĩngĩaga gũtuka atĩa hĩndĩ ĩrĩa itũũra inene rĩrĩa maithe makwa mathikĩtwo rĩkirĩte ihooru, na ihingo ciarĩo igacinwo ikaniinwo na mwaki?”
english: I said to the king, “Let the king live forever! Why shouldn’t my face be sad, when the city, the place of my fathers’ tombs, lies waste, and its gates have been consumed with fire?”

kikuyu: Ciugo ciaku ciataũrwo nĩirehaga ũtheri; nĩciũhĩgagia andũ arĩa matarĩ oogĩ.
english: The entrance of your words gives light. It gives understanding to the simple.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa Herode aamenyire atĩ nĩaheenirio nĩ Oogĩ-a-Njata, akĩrakara mũno, akĩruta watho wa kũũraga tũhĩĩ tuothe kũu Bethilehemu na kũrĩa gũkuhĩ nakuo, tũrĩa twarĩ na ũkũrũ wa mĩaka ĩĩrĩ gũcooka na thĩ, kũringana na ihinda rĩrĩa eerĩtwo nĩ Oogĩ-a-Njata.
english: Then Herod, when he saw that he was mocked by the wise men, was exceedingly angry, and sent out and killed all the male children who were in Bethlehem and in all the surrounding countryside, from two years old and under, according to the exact time which he had learned from the wise men.

kikuyu: Mũthamaki nĩathuure anene mabũrũri-inĩ mothe marĩa aathanaga marehe airĩtu acio othe ciĩrorerwa nyũmba- inĩ ĩrĩa ĩtũũragwonĩ andũ-a-njathĩinĩ wa nyũmba ya ũthamaki kũu gĩikaro kĩrĩa kĩirigĩre gĩa Shushani. Nao nĩmamenyagĩrĩrwo nĩ Hegai, ndungata ĩrĩa hakũre ya mũthamaki ĩrĩa yarũgamagĩrĩra andũ-a- nja acio; ningĩ maheo indo cia gwĩthakaria nacio.
english: Let the king appoint officers in all the provinces of his kingdom, that they may gather together all the beautiful young virgins to the citadel of Susa, to the women’s house, to the custody of Hegai the king’s eunuch, keeper of the women. Let cosmetics be given them;

kikuyu: Rũmena rũrehaga nyamũkano, no wendani ũhumbagĩra ũũru wothe.
english: One who despises his neighbor is void of wisdom, but a man of understanding holds his peace.

kikuyu: nake amũkinyĩrie wega wake na oige atĩrĩ, ‘Mũhonokie ndagatuĩke wa gũikũrũka mbĩrĩra-inĩ; nĩnyonete gĩa kũmũkũũra nakĩo,’
english: then God is gracious to him, and says, ‘Deliver him from going down to the pit, I have found a ransom.’

kikuyu: Mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai nĩakahoroheria kĩrĩndĩ kĩu gĩothe kĩa andũ a Isiraeli, na nĩmakarekerwo, nĩgũkorwo ũndũ ũcio ndwarĩ wa rũtũrĩko, na nĩ ũndũ wa kũrutĩra Jehova iruta rĩa gũcinwo na mwaki rĩa mahĩtia mao, na iruta rĩa kũhoroherio mehia.
english: Since the Amalekite and the Canaanite dwell in the valley, tomorrow turn and go into the wilderness by the way to the Red Sea.”

kikuyu: akĩmahe mabũrũri ma ndũrĩrĩ, nao magĩtuĩka a kũgaya kĩrĩa andũ angĩ maanogeire,
english: He gave them the lands of the nations. They took the labor of the peoples in possession,

kikuyu: Nĩmwageithio nĩ Timotheo, ũrĩa tũrutaga wĩra nake, o na Lukio, na Jasoni na Sosipatero, arĩa a mũhĩrĩga witũ.
english: But, as it is written, “They will see, to whom no news of him came. They who haven’t heard will understand.”*

kikuyu: Ndaamũheire igai gũkũ, o na ikinya rĩmwe. No Ngai nĩamwĩrire atĩ we hamwe na njiaro ciake nĩmakegwatĩra bũrũri ũyũ, o na gũtuĩka hĩndĩ ĩyo Iburahĩmu ndaarĩ na mwana.
english: He gave him no inheritance in it, no, not so much as to set his foot on. He promised that he would give it to him for a possession, and to his offspring after him, when he still had no child.

kikuyu: “ ‘Mũndũ angĩkaaruta nyamũ rũũru-inĩ rwa mbũri, ĩrĩ ya kũrutĩra Jehova iruta rĩa ũiguano, nĩakaruta ndũrũme kana mwatĩ itarĩ na kaũũgũ.
english: “ ‘If his offering for a sacrifice of peace offerings to Yahweh is from the flock, either male or female, he shall offer it without defect.

kikuyu: Wũi-ĩiya-wakwa, nĩ ũndũ wa itihia rĩrĩa ndiihĩtio-ĩ! Ironda ciakwa itingĩhona! No ngĩĩcookeria ũhoro ngiuga atĩrĩ, “Ũyũ nĩ mũrimũ wakwa, na no nginya ndĩũkirĩrĩrie.”
english: Woe is me because of my injury! My wound is serious; but I said, “Truly this is my grief, and I must bear it.”

kikuyu: Mũtare matukũ mĩrongo ĩtano kuuma mũthenya ũrĩa ũrũmĩrĩire Thabatũ ya kũhingithia mũgwanja, mũcooke mũrutĩre Jehova iruta rĩa mũtu wa nganoyamũgethano.
english: The next day after the seventh Sabbath you shall count fifty days; and you shall offer a new meal offering to Yahweh.

kikuyu: Njiaro cia Merari, kũringana na ũrĩa mbarĩ ciao ciatariĩ, ciaheirwo matũũra ikũmi na meerĩ kuuma mũhĩrĩga wa Rubeni, na wa Gadi, na wa Zebuluni. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio andũ a Isiraeli magĩkĩhe Alawii matũũra macio na kũndũ kũrĩa kwamathiũrũrũkĩirie kũrĩ ũrĩithio wao o ta ũrĩa Jehova aathanĩte na kanua ka Musa.
english: The children of Merari according to their families had twelve cities out of the tribe of Reuben, out of the tribe of Gad, and out of the tribe of Zebulun.

kikuyu: Honokia arĩa maratwarĩrĩrio gĩkuũ-inĩ; girĩrĩria arĩa maratũgũũga merekeire gĩthĩnjĩro.
english: Rescue those who are being led away to death! Indeed, hold back those who are staggering to the slaughter!

kikuyu: Kũu ndũnyũ-inĩ yaku-rĩ, nĩmoonjorithanagia nawe nguo iria thaka, na itambaya cia bururu, na nguo iria ngʼemie, na mĩgeka ya marangi maingĩ ĩtumĩtwo na ndigi njogothe ikundĩkĩtwo ikanyiitio.
english: These were your traders in choice wares, in wrappings of blue and embroidered work, and in cedar chests of rich clothing bound with cords, among your merchandise.

kikuyu: “Arĩria andũ a Isiraeli, ũmeere atĩrĩ: ‘Niĩ nĩ niĩ Jehova Ngai wanyu.
english: “Speak to the children of Israel, and say to them, ‘I am Yahweh your God.

kikuyu: Athingithagia thĩ ĩkoima handũ hayo, na akainainia itugĩ ciayo.
english: He shakes the earth out of its place. Its pillars tremble.

kikuyu: Roho Mũtheru we mwene nĩwe mũira hamwe na maroho maitũ atĩ ithuĩ tũrĩ ciana cia Ngai.
english: But if what I don’t desire, that I do, I consent to the law that it is good.

kikuyu: “ ‘Ningĩ andũ a Dedani nĩo mwonjorithanagia nao matandĩko ma mbarathi.
english: “ ‘ “Dedan was your merchant in precious saddle blankets for riding.

kikuyu: O na ningĩ nĩngatũma mũriũ wa ndungata ĩyo ya mũndũ-wa-nja atuĩke rũrĩrĩ rũnene, tondũ nĩ rũciaro rwaku.”
english: He spoke to Ephron in the audience of the people of the land, saying, “But if you will, please hear me. I will give the price of the field. Take it from me, and I will bury my dead there.”

kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũrĩcinaga moori ĩyo no nginya athambie nguo ciake na ethambe mwĩrĩ na maaĩ, na o nake atiinde arĩ na thaahu nginya hwaĩ-inĩ.
english: He who burns her shall wash his clothes in water, and bathe his flesh in water, and shall be unclean until the evening.

kikuyu: Inyuĩ mwĩhokaga rũhiũ rwanyu rwa njora, na mũgeeka maũndũ marĩ magigi, na o
english: You stand on your sword, you work abomination, and every one of you defiles his neighbor’s wife. So should you possess the land?” ’

kikuyu: Lea na ciana ciake makĩrũmĩrĩra, magĩũka na makĩinamĩrĩra. Marigĩrĩrio, Jusufu na Rakeli magĩũka, o nao makĩinamĩrĩra.
english: The sons of Jacob came in from the field when they heard it. The men were grieved, and they were very angry, because he had done folly in Israel in lying with Jacob’s daughter, a thing that ought not to be done.

kikuyu: Ũcio ũtaigana gũcaĩra Mũriũ wake mwene, no akĩmũneana nĩ ũndũ witũ ithuothe, na rĩrĩ, agĩtũheete ũcio-rĩ, angĩkĩrega atĩa gũtũtaanahĩra na indo iria ingĩ ciothe?
english: He who didn’t spare his own Son, but delivered him up for us all, how would he not also with him freely give us all things?

kikuyu: Hinya wa andũ ethĩ nĩguo riiri wao, nacio mbuĩ nĩcio riiri wa arĩa akũrũ.
english: The glory of young men is their strength. The splendor of old men is their gray hair.

kikuyu: Ũngĩcookerera ũcio Mwene-Hinya-Wothe, nĩegũkwamũkĩra; ũngĩeheria waganu kũraya na hema yaku,
english: One dies in his full strength, being wholly at ease and quiet.

kikuyu: Nĩngananga mĩhianano yanyu ya gwacũhio, na nyanange mahiga manyu marĩa maamũre mũrĩ namo; mũtigacooka kũinamĩrĩra wĩra wa moko manyu rĩngĩ.
english: I will cut off your engraved images and your pillars from among you; and you shall no more worship the work of your hands.

kikuyu: Jehova nĩakoimagara athiĩ o ta njamba ĩrĩ hinya, na aarahũre kĩyo gĩake ta njamba ya ita; akaanĩrĩra na oigĩrĩrie mbugĩrĩrio ya mbaara, na nĩagatooria thũ ciake.
english: Yahweh will go out like a mighty man. He will stir up zeal like a man of war. He will raise a war cry. Yes, he will shout aloud. He will triumph over his enemies.

kikuyu: Magũrũ make maikũrũkaga gĩkuũ-inĩ; makinya make mathiiaga merekeire o mbĩrĩra.
english: Her feet go down to death. Her steps lead straight to Sheol.†

kikuyu: Meciiragia atĩ irooto icio meranaga mũndũ na ũrĩa ũngĩ nĩigũtũma andũ akwa mariganĩrwo nĩ rĩĩtwa rĩakwa, o ta ũrĩa maithe mao mariganĩirwo nĩ rĩĩtwa rĩakwa makĩhooya Baali.
english: They intend to cause my people to forget my name by their dreams which they each tell his neighbor, as their fathers forgot my name because of Baal.

kikuyu: Andũ arĩa athingu nĩo makaagaya bũrũri, na matũũre kuo nginya tene.
english: The righteous shall inherit the land, and live in it forever.

kikuyu: “Mũngiuga atĩrĩ, ‘Ĩ nĩtũthingatane nake, kuona atĩ nĩwe kĩhumo gĩa thĩĩna ũyũ,’
english: If you say, ‘How we will persecute him!’ because the root of the matter is found in me,

kikuyu: tondũ nĩmamũtuire thũ ciao rĩrĩa maamũheenirie ũhoro-inĩ wa kũhoera kũu Peori na wa mwarĩ wa nyina wao Kozibi, mwarĩ wa mũtongoria Mũmidiani, mũndũ-wa-nja ũrĩa woragirwo rĩrĩa mũthiro wokire nĩ ũndũ wa Peori.” Na rĩrĩ, thuutha wa mũthiro ũcio Jehova akĩĩra Musa na Eleazaru mũrũ wa Harũni, ũrĩa mũthĩnjĩri- Ngai atĩrĩ,
english: for they harassed you with their wiles, wherein they have deceived you in the matter of Peor, and in the incident regarding Cozbi, the daughter of the prince of Midian, their sister, who was slain on the day of the plague in the matter of Peor.”

kikuyu: no ũrĩa wothe ũnjiguaga arĩtũũraga na thayũ, na atũũre ahooreire, atarĩ ũgwati angĩĩtigĩra.”
english: But whoever listens to me will dwell securely, and will be at ease, without fear of harm.”

kikuyu: mũrurumo wa mbaara nĩũgokĩrĩra andũ aku. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio ciikaro ciaku iria nũmu nĩikaanangwo, o ta ũrĩa Shalimani aanangire Bethi-Arabeli mũthenya ũrĩa wa mbaara, rĩrĩa manyina ma twana maatungumanirio thĩ hamwe na twana twao.
english: Therefore a battle roar will arise among your people, and all your fortresses will be destroyed, as Shalman destroyed Beth Arbel in the day of battle. The mother was dashed in pieces with her children.

kikuyu: “Andũ makayaga nĩ icooki rĩa kũhinyĩrĩrio; mathaithanaga mateithũrwo guoko-inĩ kwa ũcio ũrĩ hinya mũingĩ. No gũtirĩ mũndũ uugaga atĩrĩ, ‘Ngai ũrĩa wanyũũmbire arĩ ha, ũrĩa ũheaga mũndũ nyĩmbo ũtukũ,
english: then he shows them their work, and their transgressions, that they have behaved themselves proudly.

kikuyu: Geithaniai mũndũ na ũrĩa ũngĩ na ngeithi theru cia kĩmumunyano. Makanitha mothe ma Kristũ nĩmamũgeithia.
english: that I should be a servant of Christ Jesus to the Gentiles, serving as a priest of the Good News of God, that the offering up of the Gentiles might be made acceptable, sanctified by the Holy Spirit.

kikuyu: Kũu njĩra-inĩ andũ nĩ kũrĩra mararĩra nĩ kwaga ndibei, gĩkeno gĩothe gĩtuĩkĩte kĩeha, nacio ikeno ciothe igathira thĩ.
english: There is a crying in the streets because of the wine. All joy is darkened. The mirth of the land is gone.

kikuyu: Amuku ake matuĩkĩte nĩo mamwathaga; thũ ciake nĩcio igaacĩire. Jehova nĩamũrehithĩirie kĩeha nĩ ũndũ wa mehia make maingĩ. Ciana ciake itahĩtwo igatwarwo bũrũri ũngĩ, igatuĩka mĩgwate ya thũ.
english: Her adversaries have become the head. Her enemies prosper; for Yahweh† has afflicted her for the multitude of her transgressions. Her young children have gone into captivity before the adversary.

kikuyu: 1 Mũndũ ũrĩa ũtendaga gũtũũrania na andũ arũmagĩrĩra o merirĩria make; ningĩ areganaga na matua mothe ma ũũgĩ ũrĩa mũkinyanĩru.
english: The lot settles disputes, and keeps strong ones apart.

kikuyu: Ndĩrĩthiiaga ndĩ na wĩyathi, nĩgũkorwo nĩmaathaga mawatho maku.
english: I will walk in liberty, for I have sought your precepts.

kikuyu: Gũtirĩ hĩndĩ ũtheri wa tawa ũgacooka kwara thĩinĩ waku o na rĩ. O naguo mũgambo wa mũhikania na wa mũhiki ndũrĩ hĩndĩ ũgaacooka kũiguuo thĩinĩ waku o na rĩ. Onjoria aku nĩo maarĩ andũ marĩ igweta gũkũ thĩ.
english: The light of a lamp will shine no more at all in you. The voice of the bridegroom and of the bride will be heard no more at all in you, for your merchants were the princes of the earth; for with your sorcery all the nations were deceived.

kikuyu: Gĩthaku kĩu kĩarĩ na wariĩ wa mĩkono mĩrongo ĩĩrĩ,‡ na mĩkono ikũmi na ĩĩrĩ§ kuuma mbere nginya thuutha. Ningĩ hau nĩ haarĩ na ngathĩ cia kwambata ho, na nĩ haarĩ na itugĩ, o kĩmwe mwena ũmwe wa itingĩ icio.
english: The length of the porch was twenty cubits and the width eleven cubits, even by the steps by which they went up to it. There were pillars by the posts, one on this side, and another on that side.

kikuyu: Atongoria aku othe nĩmorĩte marĩ hamwe; maatahirwo o na hatarĩ mũguĩ ũikĩtio. Andũ aku othe arĩa maakorereirwo moohirwo hamwe, o acio mooraga thũ ĩrĩ o kũraya. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio ngiuga atĩrĩ, “Njeherera; reke ndĩre ngĩgirĩkaga. Ndũkagerie kũũhooreria igũrũ rĩa mwanangĩko ũrĩa ũkorete andũ akwa.”
english: All your rulers fled away together. They were bound by the archers. All who were found by you were bound together. They fled far away.

kikuyu: “Na rĩu-rĩ, Wee Ngai witũ, Mũrungu mũnene ũrĩ hinya na wa gwĩtigĩrwo, o wee ũhingagia kĩrĩkanĩro gĩaku kĩa wendo, ndũkareke moritũ maya moneke arĩ ũndũ mũnini maitho-inĩ maku, moritũ maya matũkorete, na magakora athamaki aitũ na atongoria aitũ, na magakora athĩnjĩri- Ngai na anabii aitũ, na magakora maithe maitũ na andũ aku othe, kuuma matukũ ma athamaki a Ashuri nginya ũmũthĩ.
english: Now therefore, our God, the great, the mighty, and the awesome God, who keeps covenant and loving kindness, don’t let all the travail seem little before you that has come on us, on our kings, on our princes, on our priests, on our prophets, on our fathers, and on all your people, since the time of the kings of Assyria to this day.

kikuyu: “Andũ arĩa angĩ, nĩo athĩnjĩri-Ngai, na Alawii, na aikaria a ihingo, na aini, o na ndungata cia hekarũ na arĩa othe meyamũranĩtie na andũ arĩa maariganĩtie nao nĩguo maathĩkĩre Watho wa Ngai, marĩ hamwe na atumia ao, na aanake, na airĩtu ao othe arĩa mangĩhota gũtaũkĩrwo nĩ maũndũ,
english: The rest of the people, the priests, the Levites, the gatekeepers, the singers, the temple servants, and all those who had separated themselves from the peoples of the lands to the law of God, their wives, their sons, and their daughters—everyone who had knowledge and understanding—

kikuyu: “Anĩrĩrai na mũhunjanĩrie ũhoro ndũrĩrĩ-inĩ, haandai kĩbebero na mũhunjanĩrie ũhoro wakĩo; mũtikamahithe ũndũ, no mũmeere atĩrĩ, ‘Babuloni nĩgũgũtunyanwo; Beli nĩĩgũconorithio, nayo Merodaki ĩiyũrwo nĩ kĩmako. Mĩhianano yakuo nĩĩgaconorithio, na mĩhianano yao nĩĩkaiyũrwo nĩ guoya.’
english: “Declare among the nations and publish, and set up a standard; publish, and don’t conceal; say, ‘Babylon has been taken, Bel is disappointed, Merodach is dismayed! Her images are disappointed. Her idols are dismayed.’

kikuyu: Moima werũ ũcio wa Sini, magĩthiĩ makĩamba hema ciao Dofika.
english: They traveled from the wilderness of Sin, and encamped in Dophkah.

kikuyu: Ũgaacooka uume kũndũ kũu ũigĩrĩire moko mũtwe, nĩgũkorwo Jehova nĩaregete acio wĩhokete, o matigagũteithia.
english: You will also leave that place with your hands on your head; for Yahweh has rejected those in whom you trust, and you won’t prosper with them.

kikuyu: Watho wothe na ũrutani wa Anabii ũhingĩtio nĩ maathani macio meerĩ.” Kristũ nĩ Mũrũ wa Ũ? Na hĩndĩ ĩyo Afarisai monganĩte hamwe-rĩ, Jesũ akĩmooria atĩrĩ,
english: The whole law and the prophets depend on these two commandments.”

kikuyu: Mũirĩtu ũrĩa mũnini o nake agĩciara kahĩĩ, nake agĩgatua Beni-Ami, na nĩwe ithe wa Aamoni arĩa marĩ kuo nginya ũmũthĩ.
english: The younger also bore a son, and called his name Ben Ammi. He is the father of the children of Ammon to this day.

kikuyu: nao maainaga rwĩmbo rwa Musa ũrĩa ndungata ya Ngai,* na rwĩmbo rwa Gatũrũme, makoiga atĩrĩ: “Maũndũ maku marĩa wĩkĩte nĩ manene na ma kũgegania, Wee Mwathani Ngai Mwene-Hinya-Wothe. Njĩra ciaku nĩ cia kĩhooto na nĩ cia ma, Wee Mũthamaki wa mĩndĩ na mĩndĩ.
english: The second angel poured out his bowl into the sea, and it became blood as of a dead man. Every living thing in the sea died.

kikuyu: Nĩngahurunja andũ a Misiri ndũrĩrĩ-inĩ, na ndĩmaharaganĩrie mabũrũri-inĩ.
english: I will scatter the Egyptians among the nations, and will disperse them through the countries.

kikuyu: Inyuĩ mũtiũĩ atĩ nĩ kaba mũndũ ũmwe akue nĩ ũndũ wa kĩrĩndĩ, handũ ha rũrĩrĩ ruothe rũthire.”
english: nor do you consider that it is advantageous for us that one man should die for the people, and that the whole nation not perish.”

kikuyu: Ciĩko ciothe njũru nĩ mehia, no nĩ kũrĩ rĩĩhia rĩtangĩtwara mũndũ gĩkuũ-inĩ.
english: But you, beloved, remember the words which have been spoken before by the apostles of our Lord Jesus Christ.

kikuyu: Cikĩmũtuĩra mata, cikĩoya kamũrangi kau, na cikĩmũhũũra nako mũtwe.
english: When they had sung a hymn, they went out to the Mount of Olives.

kikuyu: “Nĩ mũndũ ũrĩkũ wanyu ũngĩkorwo arĩ na ndungata ya kũmũrĩmĩra na mũraũ kana ya kũmũrĩithĩria ngʼondu, na ndungata ĩyo yoimĩra mũgũnda amĩĩre atĩrĩ, ‘Ũka ũikare thĩ ũrĩe?’
english: But who is there among you, having a servant plowing or keeping sheep, that will say when he comes in from the field,  “Mammon” refers to riches or a false god of wealth.  or, ‘Come immediately and sit down at the table’?

kikuyu: Zila o nake aarĩ na mũriũ, nake eetagwo Tubali-Kaini, na nĩwe waturaga indo cia mĩthemba yothe cia gĩcango na cia kĩgera. Tubali-Kaini aarĩ na mwarĩ wa nyina wetagwo Naama.
english: After Methuselah’s birth, Enoch walked with God for three hundred years, and became the father of more sons and daughters.

kikuyu: Nĩngakũnengera cabi cia ũthamaki wa igũrũ; na kĩrĩa gĩothe ũkooha gũkũ thĩ nĩgĩkoohwo igũrũ, na kĩrĩa gĩothe ũkoohora gũkũ thĩ, nĩgĩkoohorwo igũrũ.”
english: I will give to you the keys of the Kingdom of Heaven, and whatever you bind on earth will have been bound in heaven; and whatever you release on earth will have been released in heaven.”

kikuyu: Ndũkareke mũndũ ũrĩa mũhinyĩrĩrie acooke na thuutha aconokete; andũ arĩa athĩĩni na arĩa abatari nĩmagooce rĩĩtwa rĩaku.
english: Don’t let the oppressed return ashamed. Let the poor and needy praise your name.

kikuyu: Angĩ nao makoiga atĩrĩ, “Ũyũ nĩ Elija.” Angĩ nao makoiga atĩrĩ, “Ũyũ nĩ mũnabii ta ũmwe wa arĩa a tene.”
english: But others said, “He is Elijah.” Others said, “He is a prophet, or like one of the prophets.”

kikuyu: “Nĩnganeana Zedekia mũthamaki wa Juda na anene ake kũrĩ thũ ciao iria ciendaga kũmaruta muoyo, ndĩmaneane kũrĩ mbũtũ cia ita cia mũthamaki wa Babuloni, icio ciambĩte kũmweherera.
english: “I will give Zedekiah king of Judah and his princes into the hands of their enemies, into the hands of those who seek their life and into the hands of the king of Babylon’s army, who has gone away from you.

kikuyu: nao aya nĩo maarĩ a njiaro cia Beria: kuuma kũrĩ Heberi, nĩ mbarĩ ya Aheberi; na kuuma kũrĩ Malikieli, nĩ mbarĩ ya Amalikieli.
english: Of the sons of Beriah: of Heber, the family of the Heberites; of Malchiel, the family of the Malchielites.

kikuyu: “Arahũkai mũtharĩkĩre rũrĩrĩ rũu rũtũũrĩte rũgaacĩire na rũhooreire, o rũu rũtũũrĩte rũrĩ na thayũ, rũrĩrĩ rũteehingagĩra na ihingo kana rũkeirigĩra na igera; andũ aruo matũũraga marĩ oiki,” ũguo nĩguo Jehova ekuuga.
english: Arise! Go up to a nation that is at ease, that dwells without care,” says Yahweh; “that has neither gates nor bars, that dwells alone.

kikuyu: Ikundi cia kambĩ ya Juda nĩcio cioimagarire mbere, irũmĩrĩire bendera yao. Nake Nahashoni mũrũ wa Aminadabu nĩwe warĩ mũtongoria wao.
english: “ ‘If a foreigner lives among you and desires to keep the Passover to Yahweh, then he shall do so according to the statute of the Passover, and according to its ordinance. You shall have one statute, both for the foreigner and for him who is born in the land.’ ”

kikuyu: Musa na Harũni makiuma hau kĩungano kĩarĩ, magĩthiĩ nginya o itoonyero-inĩ rĩa Hema-ya- Gũtũnganwo makĩĩgũithia na magĩturumithia mothiũ mao thĩ, naguo riiri wa Jehova ũkĩmoimĩrĩra.
english: Moses and Aaron went from the presence of the assembly to the door of the Tent of Meeting, and fell on their faces. Yahweh’s glory appeared to them.

kikuyu: Angĩkorwo mwene no ende gũkũũra nyamũ ĩyo, no nginya ongerere gĩcunjĩ kĩngĩ gĩa ithano harĩ thogora ũrĩa ũtuĩtwo.
english: I am Yahweh your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt, that you should not be their slaves. I have broken the bars of your yoke, and made you walk upright.

kikuyu: Mwana mũkĩĩgu nĩ mwanangĩko harĩ ithe, na mũtumia wa haaro ahaana ta maaĩ maratata mategũtigithĩria.
english: A foolish son is the calamity of his father. A wife’s quarrels are a continual dripping.

kikuyu: We mwene tiwe warĩ ũtheri ũcio, no okire arĩ o ta mũira tu nĩgeetha oimbũre ũhoro wa ũtheri ũcio.
english: He said to them, “Now draw some out, and take it to the ruler of the feast.” So they took it.

kikuyu: Andũ arĩa maanyariiraga nĩ aingĩ, no ndirĩ ndahutatĩra kĩrĩra gĩaku.
english: Many are my persecutors and my adversaries. I haven’t swerved from your testimonies.

kikuyu: “Ningĩ Mũthamaki nĩakeera arĩa marĩ mwena wake wa ũrĩo atĩrĩ, ‘Ũkai, inyuĩ mũrathimĩtwo nĩ Baba; oyai igai rĩa ũthamaki ũrĩa mwathondekeirwo kuuma rĩrĩa thĩ yombirwo.
english: Then the King will tell those on his right hand, ‘Come, blessed of my Father, inherit the Kingdom prepared for you from the foundation of the world;  A talent is about

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo nao andũ a Babuloni nĩmagacooka gũkũ na matharĩkĩre itũũra rĩĩrĩ inene, marĩtunyane na marĩcine na mwaki.’
english: The Chaldeans will come again, and fight against this city. They will take it and burn it with fire.” ’

kikuyu: Nacio iria ciagũire rũnyanjara-inĩ nĩ andũ arĩa maiguaga ũhoro wa Ngai, na makawamũkĩra na gĩkeno, no makaaga mĩri. Metĩkagia o gwa kahinda kanini, no magerio moka makaũtiganĩria.
english: Those on the rock are they who, when they hear, receive the word with joy; but these have no root. They believe for a while, then fall away in time of temptation.

kikuyu: Mũndũ mũritũhĩre nĩ thakame ya mũndũ ooragĩte, egũtũũra ehithaga nginya gũkua gwake; mũndũ o na ũrĩkũ ndakanamũteithĩrĩrie.
english: Correct your son, and he will give you peace; yes, he will bring delight to your soul.

kikuyu: Nĩagatambũrũkia guoko gwake okĩrĩre andũ a gathigathini na aniine Ashuri, atige Nineve gũkirĩte ihooru, na kũũmagarĩte o ta werũ.
english: He will stretch out his hand against the north, destroy Assyria, and will make Nineveh a desolation, as dry as the wilderness.

kikuyu: Andũ a Misiri mangĩkaaga gũthiĩ kũnyiitanĩra na arĩa angĩ-rĩ, kwao gũtikoira mbura. Jehova nĩakamarehera mũthiro ũrĩa ahũũraga ndũrĩrĩ naguo iria itaambataga gũkũngũĩra Gĩathĩ kĩu gĩa Ithũnũ.
english: If the family of Egypt doesn’t go up and doesn’t come, neither will it rain on them. This will be the plague with which Yahweh will strike the nations that don’t go up to keep the feast of booths.

kikuyu: Agĩcooka akĩorota arutwo ake, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Aya nĩo maitũ na ariũ a maitũ.
english: He stretched out his hand toward his disciples, and said, “Behold, my mother and my brothers!

kikuyu: “ ‘Iruta o rĩothe rĩa mũtu rĩrĩa mũrĩrehagĩra Jehova no nginya rĩthondekagwo rĩtarĩ na ndawa ya kũimbia, nĩgũkorwo mũtigaacina ndawa ya kũimbia kana ũũkĩ mũkĩrutĩra Jehova iruta rĩa gũcinwo na mwaki.
english: The priest shall burn it on the altar: it is the food of the offering made by fire to Yahweh.

kikuyu: Maarĩire o nginya makĩhũũna, nĩgũkorwo aamaheire kĩrĩa merirĩirie kũrĩa. No matanarĩkia kũrĩa irio icio meriragĩria, o irĩ tũnua-inĩ twao-rĩ,
english: So they ate, and were well filled. He gave them their own desire.

kikuyu: O na ũngĩnjĩĩra atĩrĩ, “Ithuĩ twĩhokete Jehova Ngai witũ,” githĩ ũcio tiwe Hezekia aatharĩirie igongona na kũndũ kũrĩa gũtũũgĩru, na akĩĩra andũ a Juda na a Jerusalemu atĩrĩ, “No nginya mũhooyagĩre kĩgongona-inĩ gĩkĩ”?
english: But if you tell me, ‘We trust in Yahweh our God,’ isn’t that he whose high places and whose altars Hezekiah has taken away, and has said to Judah and to Jerusalem, ‘You shall worship before this altar’?”

kikuyu: Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe, o we Ngai wa Isiraeli, ekuuga atĩrĩ: “Mwarĩ wa Babuloni atariĩ ta kĩhuhĩro kĩa ngano hĩndĩ ĩrĩa yaraganĩtio ĩkĩrangĩrĩrio; kahinda gake ga kũgethwo nĩgegũkinya o narua.”
english: For Yahweh of Armies, the God of Israel says: “The daughter of Babylon is like a threshing floor at the time when it is trodden. Yet a little while, and the time of harvest comes for her.”

kikuyu: Mũndũ ũcio akĩmũcookeria, akiuga atĩrĩ, “ ‘Endaga Mwathani Ngai waku na ngoro yaku yothe, na muoyo waku wothe, na hinya waku wothe, o na meciiria maku mothe’; na ‘Endaga mũndũ wa itũũra rĩanyu o ta ũrĩa wĩyendete wee mwene.’ ”
english: It came to pass, as he said these things, a certain woman out of the multitude lifted up her voice and said to him, “Blessed is the womb that bore you, and the breasts which nursed you!”

kikuyu: Ũndũ ũcio noguo ũtũmaga mũrutage magooti, nĩgũkorwo arĩa maathanaga nĩ ndungata cia Ngai, na maheanaga mahinda mao mothe nĩ ũndũ wa wĩra ũcio kĩũmbe.
english: He who observes the day, observes it to the Lord; and he who does not observe the day, to the Lord he does not observe it. He who eats, eats to the Lord, for he gives

kikuyu: Tondũ wa ũguo-rĩ, mbura ya maguna-ngʼombe nĩĩgirĩtio yure, o na ĩrĩa ya gũkũria irio nayo ĩkaaga kuura. No rĩrĩ, wee nĩũmĩtie gĩthiithi ta kĩa mũmaraya, ũkaaga thoni o na hanini.
english: Therefore the showers have been withheld and there has been no latter rain; yet you have had a prostitute’s forehead and you refused to be ashamed.

kikuyu: Nayo ndigiri ĩkĩũria Balamu atĩrĩ, “Githĩ niĩ ti niĩ ndigiri yaku, ĩrĩa ũtũire ũhaicaga nginya ũmũthĩ? Nĩmenyerete gũgwĩka ta ũũ?” Balamu akiuga atĩrĩ, “Aca.” Nake Jehova akĩhingũra maitho ma Balamu, nake akĩona mũraika wa Jehova arũngiĩ njĩra-inĩ acomorete rũhiũ rwa njora. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio akĩinamĩrĩra na akĩĩgũithia, agĩturumithia ũthiũ wake thĩ.
english: The donkey said to Balaam, “Am I not your donkey, on which you have ridden all your life long until today? Was I ever in the habit of doing so to you?” He said, “No.”

kikuyu: na ningĩ wĩre andũ arĩa mohetwo atĩrĩ, ‘Thiĩi, nĩmuohorwo,’ nao arĩa maikarĩte nduma-inĩ ũmeere atĩrĩ, ‘Umĩrai ũtheri-inĩ!’ “Makaarĩithagio mĩkĩra-inĩ ya njĩra, na moonage gĩa kũrĩa tũrĩma-inĩ tuothe tũrĩa tũtarĩ kĩndũ.
english: I now rejoice, not that you were grieved, but that you were grieved to repentance. For you were grieved in a godly way, that you might suffer loss by us in nothing.

kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo mũndũ mũthingu o na angĩgũa maita mũgwanja, agũũaga o agĩũkagĩra, no andũ arĩa aaganu marũndagwo nĩ mũtino.
english: for a righteous man falls seven times and rises up again, but the wicked are overthrown by calamity.

kikuyu: “Inyuĩ muugĩte atĩrĩ, ‘Gũtungatĩra Ngai nĩ ũndũ wa tũhũ. Nĩ uumithio ũrĩkũ tuonire nĩkũhingia kwenda gwake, na gũthiiaga mbere ya Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe ta andũ maracakaya? No rĩrĩ, andũ etĩĩi nĩo twĩtaga arathime. Ti-itherũ arĩa aaganu nĩo magaacagĩra, na arĩa makararagia Ngai matiherithagio.’ ” Nao arĩa meetigĩrĩte Jehova nĩmaranĩirie o mũndũ na ũrĩa ũngĩ, nake Jehova agĩthikĩrĩria na akĩigua ũhoro ũcio. Ibuku rĩa kĩririkano nĩrĩandĩkirwo ũhoro wa andũ acio metigĩrĩte Jehova na magatĩĩa rĩĩtwa rĩake, rĩkĩigwo hau mbere yake.
english: But she said, “It has been given to me for a gift more than the wages.” I didn’t believe her, and I asked her to return it to the owners; and I was ashamed of her. But she answered and said to me, “Where are your alms and your righteous deeds? Behold, you and all your works are known.‡”

kikuyu: Jehova ekuuga atĩrĩ, “Andũ arĩa aaganu matikagĩa na thayũ o na rĩ.”
english: “There is no peace”, says Yahweh, “for the wicked.”

kikuyu: “Mũrũ wa mũndũ, nĩ thimo ĩrĩkũ ĩno yunanagwo thĩinĩ wa bũrũri wa Isiraeli, ĩno yugaga atĩrĩ: ‘Matukũ nĩmathiraga, nakĩo kĩoneki o gĩothe gĩgatuĩka gĩa tũhũ’?
english: “Son of man, what is this proverb that you have in the land of Israel, saying, ‘The days are prolonged, and every vision fails’?

kikuyu: no rĩrĩ, tũtithereraga rĩrĩa kwara, na hĩndĩ ya ũrugarĩ tũkahũa mĩtaro-inĩ yatuo.
english: “Behold, happy is the man whom God corrects. Therefore do not despise the chastening of the Almighty.

kikuyu: na icũhĩ cia matũ, na mĩrĩnga ya moko, na taama wa kwĩhumbĩra ũthiũ,
english: the earrings, the bracelets, the veils,

kikuyu: Mũndũ angĩkũhatĩrĩria ũthiĩ itĩĩna rĩmwe-rĩ, thiĩ nake matĩĩna meerĩ.
english: Whoever compels you to go one mile, go with him two.

kikuyu: nĩguo agitĩre roho wake kuuma gĩkuũ-inĩ, na agitĩre muoyo wake ndũkaniinwo na rũhiũ rwa njora. Ningĩ mũndũ no arũithio na gũkomio ũrĩrĩ wa ruo, akorwo na thĩĩna wa mahĩndĩ ũtathiraga,
english: He keeps back his soul from the pit, and his life from perishing by the sword.

kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa mwaganu nĩakoona ũguo na arakare, nĩakahagarania magego make, acooke abuĩrie; merirĩria ma arĩa aaganu matigaakinyanĩra.
english: As the truth of Christ is in me, no one will stop me from this boasting in the regions of Achaia.

kikuyu: Wara wa Andũ a Gibeoni Na rĩrĩa athamaki othe arĩa maarĩ mwena wa ithũĩro wa Rũũĩ rwa Jorodani maiguire maũndũ macio, na arĩa maarĩ bũrũri ũrĩa ũrĩ irĩma, na arĩa maarĩ ituamba-ini ciikũrũko cia irĩma cia mwena wa ithũĩro, na kũu hũgũrũrũ-inĩ cia Iria rĩrĩa Inene guothe o nginya Lebanoni (nĩo athamaki a andũ a Ahiti, na Aamori, na Akaanani, na Aperizi, na Ahivi, na Ajebusi),
english: 1 When all the kings who were beyond the Jordan, in the hill country, and in the lowland, and on all the shore of the great sea in front of Lebanon, the Hittite, the Amorite, the Canaanite, the Perizzite, the Hivite, and the Jebusite, heard of it

kikuyu: Kũrathimwo-rĩ, nĩ arĩa macaragia thayũ, nĩgũkorwo nĩmagetwo ariũ a Ngai.
english: Blessed are the peacemakers, for they shall be called children of God.

kikuyu: “Arakũrũithia na agagũthitanga nĩ ũndũ wa wĩtigĩri Ngai waku?
english: Is it for your piety that he reproves you, that he enters with you into judgment?

kikuyu: Rũhonge o ruothe rũrĩ thĩinĩ wakwa rũtaciaraga nĩarũtemaga akarweheria. Naruo rũhonge o ruothe rũrĩa rũciaraga nĩarũceehaga nĩguo rũciare maciaro maingĩ makĩria.
english: Every branch in me that doesn’t bear fruit, he takes away. Every branch that bears fruit, he prunes, that it may bear more fruit.

kikuyu: Hakĩrĩ ũguo-rĩ, angĩkorwo Demeterio na andũ acio angĩ marutaga wĩra nake marĩ na mateta igũrũ rĩa mũndũ o wothe, maciirĩro nĩmahingũre na aciirithania marĩ ho. No mamathitange.
english: If therefore Demetrius and the craftsmen who are with him have a matter against anyone, the courts are open and there are proconsuls. Let them press charges against one another.

kikuyu: Mũcirutanĩrie na thenge ĩmwe ĩrĩ igongona rĩa kũhoroherio mehia, yongererwo harĩ igongona rĩa njino rĩa hĩndĩ ciothe, hamwe na iruta rĩa mũtu na iruta rĩao rĩa kũnyuuo.
english: Set him before Eleazar the priest, and before all the congregation; and commission him in their sight.

kikuyu: “Nĩmamakĩte, na matirĩ na ũndũ ũngĩ mangiuga; nĩmarigĩtwo nĩ atĩa mangiuga.
english: “They are amazed. They answer no more. They don’t have a word to say.

kikuyu: Wega wa Mwathani witũ Jesũ Kristũ ũrogĩa na maroho manyu, inyuĩ ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ. Ameni.
english: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit, brothers. Amen.

kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo nĩcio itũũragia arĩa maciĩgwatĩire muoyo, na igekĩra mwĩrĩ wao wothe ũgima.
english: For they are life to those who find them, and health to their whole body.

kikuyu: “Ingĩkorwo ndaanakena nĩ thũ yakwa kuona mũtino, kana ngĩmĩthekerera rĩrĩa thĩĩna wamĩkora,
english: “If I have rejoiced at the destruction of him who hated me, or lifted up myself when evil found him

kikuyu: Paũlũ wa Baranaba makĩhunjia ũhoro mwega itũũra-inĩ rĩu na magĩtũma andũ aingĩ matuĩke arutwo. Magĩcooka makĩhũndũka magĩthiĩ Lisitera, na Ikonia, na Antiokia,
english: When they had preached the Good News to that city and had made many disciples, they returned to Lystra, Iconium, and Antioch,

kikuyu: “Tondũ ũcio nĩngamaruta maamenye, ihinda-inĩ rĩĩrĩ nĩguo ngaamaruta ũhoro wa ũhoti na hinya wakwa. Hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩmakamenya atĩ rĩĩtwa rĩakwa nĩ Jehova.
english: “Therefore behold, I will cause them to know, this once I will cause them to know my hand and my might. Then they will know that my name is Yahweh.”

kikuyu: ti na ũndũ wa ciĩko iria mekĩte, no nĩ ũndũ wa wendi wa ũcio wĩtanaga, nĩerire Rebeka atĩrĩ, “Ũrĩa mũkũrũ nĩagatungatĩra ũrĩa mũnini.”
english: For there is no distinction between Jew and Greek; for the same Lord is Lord of all, and is rich to all who call on him.

kikuyu: Nĩngarehithĩria andũ a Elamu huho iria inya ciumaga mĩena ĩrĩa ĩna ya igũrũ; ngaamahurunjĩra na kũu huho icio inya ciumaga, na gũtirĩ rũrĩrĩ o na rũmwe andũ acio aingate a Elamu matakoorĩra.
english: I will bring on Elam the four winds from the four quarters of the sky, and will scatter them toward all those winds. There will be no nation where the outcasts of Elam will not come.

kikuyu: Njookeria gĩkeno kĩrĩa kĩa ũhonokio waku, na ũũhe roho wa kũndũũria ngwathĩkagĩra.
english: Restore to me the joy of your salvation. Uphold me with a willing spirit.

kikuyu: ngĩkĩĩririkania ũrĩa ndainaga ũtukũ, ngoro yakwa ĩgĩtarania maũndũ, naguo roho wakwa ũkĩyũũria atĩrĩ:
english: I remember my song in the night. I consider in my own heart; my spirit diligently inquires:

kikuyu: Niĩ nĩngatũma rũrĩmĩ rwaku rũnyiitane na karakara nĩguo ũkire wage kũmanegenia, o na akorwo o nĩ andũ a nyũmba ya ũremi. No rĩrĩa ngaakwarĩria-rĩ, nĩngakũhingũra kanua, nawe nĩũkameera ũũ, ‘Mwathani Jehova ekuuga atĩrĩ.’ Ũrĩa wothe ũngĩthikĩrĩria nĩathikĩrĩrie, nake ũrĩa wothe ũkũrega nĩarege; nĩgũkorwo o nĩ andũ a nyũmba ya ũremi.
english: I will make your tongue stick to the roof of your mouth so that you will be mute and will not be able to correct them, for they are a rebellious house.

kikuyu: Cakayai ta mũirĩtu gathirange ehumbĩte nguo ya ikũnia, akĩrĩrĩra mũthuuriwe wa wĩthĩ wake.
english: Mourn like a virgin dressed in sackcloth for the husband of her youth!

kikuyu: “Mũrimũ mũũru nĩũmũnyiitĩte; ndarĩ hĩndĩ agokĩra hau akomete.”
english: Yahweh‡ will command his loving kindness in the daytime. In the night his song shall be with me: a prayer to the God of my life.

kikuyu: O hĩndĩ ĩyo Jesũ akĩaragia-rĩ, hagĩũka andũ moimĩte kwa Jairũ ũrĩa warĩ mũnene wa thunagogi, makĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Mwarĩguo nĩakua. Ũgũgĩthĩĩnia mũrutani rĩngĩ nĩkĩ?” Nake Jesũ akĩaga gũthikĩrĩria ũguo moigaga, akĩĩra mũnene ũcio wa thunagogi atĩrĩ, “Wee ĩtĩkia na ndũgetigĩre.”
english: While he was still speaking, people came from the synagogue ruler’s house, saying, “Your daughter is dead. Why bother the Teacher any more?”

kikuyu: Inyuĩ o na no mũcuukĩre mwana wa ngoriai mĩtĩ, na mwendie mũrata wanyu.
english: Yes, you would even cast lots for the fatherless, and make merchandise of your friend.

kikuyu: Mĩĩtheemage, na ndũkanamĩgerere; mĩhutatĩre, na wĩthiĩre na njĩra yaku.
english: Yes, I will make every effort that you may always be able to remember these things even after my departure.

kikuyu: “Nĩ ũndũ ũcio-rĩ, ndigũkira; ngwaria nĩ ũndũ wa ũrĩa roho wakwa ũrĩ na ruo, ndĩtetere nĩ ũndũ wa ũrĩa ngoro yakwa ĩrĩ na marũrũ.
english: What is my strength, that I should wait? What is my end, that I should be patient?

kikuyu: Niĩ ndatariĩ ta gatũrũme kahooreri koimagarĩtio gagathĩnjwo; ndiamenyaga atĩ nĩmathugundĩte kũnjĩka ũũru, makiugaga atĩrĩ, “Nĩtwanangei mũtĩ ũyũ na maciaro maguo; nĩtũmũniinei oime bũrũri-inĩ wa arĩa marĩ muoyo, nĩgeetha rĩĩtwa rĩake rĩtikanacooke kũririkanwo o rĩ, o rĩ.” No rĩrĩ, Wee Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe, o wee ũtuaga ciira na kĩhooto na ũkoiruuria maũndũ ma ngoro na ma meciiria-rĩ, reke ndĩĩonere wee mwene ũkĩndĩhĩria harĩ andũ aya, nĩgũkorwo wee nĩwe ndekereirie maũndũ makwa mothe.
english: But I was like a gentle lamb that is led to the slaughter. I didn’t know that they had devised plans against me, saying, “Let’s destroy the tree with its fruit, and let’s cut him off from the land of the living, that his name may be no more remembered.”

kikuyu: Kĩroto kĩa Nebukadinezaru Na rĩrĩ, mwaka-inĩ wa keerĩ wa ũthamaki wake-rĩ, Nebukadinezaru nĩarootire irooto; agĩtangĩka meciiria na akĩũrwo nĩ toro. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio mũthamaki agĩĩta ago na aragũri, na arogi na arori a njata nĩguo moke mamwĩre ũrĩa aarootete. Rĩrĩa mookire makĩrũgama mbere ya mũthamaki,
english: 1 In the second year of the reign of Nebuchadnezzar, Nebuchadnezzar  The word translated “Lord” is “Adonai.”  The Hebrew word rendered “God” is “אֱֹלהִ ֑ים” (Elohim).  or, seed

kikuyu: Akĩmũtua mwathi wa indo cia nyũmba yake, na mũrori wa indo iria ciothe aarĩ nacio,
english: He made him lord of his house, and ruler of all of his possessions,

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo thũ ciaku, Wee Jehova, ti-itherũ thũ ciaku nĩigathira; arĩa othe mekaga ũũru nĩmakaharaganio.
english: He who implanted the ear, won’t he hear? He who formed the eye, won’t he see?

kikuyu: na Mika, na Rehobu, na Hashabia,
english: Mica, Rehob, Hashabiah,

kikuyu: Atũmaga mũtumia thaata atũũre gwake mũciĩ, agatuĩka nyina wa ciana, agakenaga. Goocai Jehova. Rĩrĩa Isiraeli moimire bũrũri wa Misiri, nyũmba ya Jakubu ĩkiuma kũrĩ andũ a rũthiomi rũngĩ-rĩ,
english: When I was present with you and was in need, I wasn’t a burden on anyone, for the brothers, when they came from Macedonia, supplied the measure of my need. In everything I kept myself from being burdensome to you, and I will continue to do so.

kikuyu: Wee ũtũũraga ũrigiicĩirio nĩ maheeni, nao aheenania acio nĩmaregete kũmenyana na niĩ,” ũguo nĩguo Jehova ekuuga.
english: Your habitation is in the middle of deceit. Through deceit, they refuse to know me,” says Yahweh.

kikuyu: Jehova nĩarĩgũtoongoragia hĩndĩ ciothe, na nĩarĩkũhingagĩria mabataro maku ũrĩ bũrũri-inĩ mũũmũ, o na mahĩndĩ maku amekagĩre hinya. Ũkahaana ta mũgũnda unithĩirio maaĩ, ũtuĩke ta gĩthima kĩrĩa gĩtahũaga.
english: and Yahweh will guide you continually, satisfy your soul in dry places, and make your bones strong. You will be like a watered garden, and like a spring of water whose waters don’t fail.

kikuyu: Naarĩ korwo andũ aya me rungu rwa wathani wakwa! Hĩndĩ ĩyo niĩ no ndĩmweherie. No njĩĩre Abimeleku atĩrĩ, ‘Ĩta ita rĩaku rĩothe!’ ” Rĩrĩa Zebuli ũcio mwathi wa itũũra aiguire ũrĩa Gaali mũrũ wa Ebedi oigĩte-rĩ, akĩrakario nĩ ũhoro ũcio mũno.
english: I wish that this people were under my hand! Then I would remove Abimelech.”  “Beer” is Hebrew for “well”, i.e., a village named for its well.

kikuyu: na mũira ũtarĩ wa ma ũrĩa ũthothoraga ndeto cia maheeni, o na mũndũ ũrĩa ũrehaga nyamũkano gatagatĩ ka andũ a nyina ũmwe. Gũkaanio ũtharia
english: For my husband isn’t at home. He has gone on a long journey.

kikuyu: JEHOVA, nĩwe ũtũũrĩte ũrĩ gĩikaro giitũ kuuma njiarwa na njiarwa.
english: Lord,‡ you have been our dwelling place for all generations.

kikuyu: Ndĩ na hinya ũmũthĩ o ta ũrĩa ndarĩ naguo mũthenya ũrĩa Musa aandũmire; ndĩ na hinya wa gũthiĩ mbaara-inĩ o ta ũrĩa ndaarĩ naguo hĩndĩ ĩyo, hinya wa gũthiĩ rũgendo kana wa kũinũka mũciĩ.
english: and the border went out to the side of Ekron northward; and the border extended to Shikkeron, and passed along to Mount Baalah, and went out at Jabneel; and the goings out of the border were at the sea.

kikuyu: nao andũ maarĩ 16,000.
english: and sixteen thousand persons),

kikuyu: Gĩkuũ na Mwanangĩko itiiganagia, na o namo maitho ma mũndũ matiiganagia.
english: Sheol† and Abaddon are never satisfied; and a man’s eyes are never satisfied.

kikuyu: Matigacooka gũtahwo nĩ ndũrĩrĩ, kana kũrĩĩo nĩ nyamũ cia gĩthaka. Magaatũũraga na thayũ, na gũtirĩ mũndũ ũkaamamakagia.
english: They will no more be a prey to the nations, neither will the animals of the earth devour them; but they will dwell securely, and no one will make them afraid.

kikuyu: Jusufu akĩoya mwĩrĩ ũcio, akĩwoha na taama mũtheru wa gatani,
english: Now the chief priests, the elders, and the whole council sought false testimony against Jesus, that they might put him to death,

kikuyu: Manase akaarĩa Efiraimu, nake Efiraimu arĩe Manase: nao marĩ hamwe nĩmagookĩrĩra Juda. * 9:8 Jakubu, Isiraeli, Efiraimu na Samaria ciothe nĩ kuuga Ũthamaki wa Gathigathini; itũũra inene rĩaguo rĩarĩ Samaria. † 9:11 Thũ cia Rezini ciarĩ andũ a Ashuri arĩa marĩ hamwe na andũ a Suriata na Afilisti maatharĩkĩire Isiraeli.
english: They will pass through it, very distressed and hungry. It will happen that when they are hungry, they will worry, and curse their king and their God. They will turn their faces upward,

kikuyu: Jehova ekuuga atĩrĩ, “Ha ũhoro wa anabii acio mahĩtithagia andũ akwa, mũndũ aamahe irio, maanagĩrĩra makiugaga ‘thayũ,’ no angĩmaima irio, makahaarĩria mbaara ya kũmũũkĩrĩra.
english: Indeed all the nations may walk in the name of their gods, but we will walk in the name of Yahweh our God forever and ever.

kikuyu: Amwe maatuĩkire andũ akĩĩgu nĩ ũndũ wa mĩthiĩre yao ya ũremi, o na nĩ ũndũ wa waganu wao magĩtuĩka a gũthĩĩnĩka.
english: Fools are afflicted because of their disobedience, and because of their iniquities.

kikuyu: “Ũcooke ũũrage gatigithũ kau, acio mũgaathiĩ nao makĩonaga,
english: “Then you shall break the container in the sight of the men who go with you,

kikuyu: Mũthĩgi wa andũ arĩa aaganu ndũgũtũũra wathanaga bũrũri ũrĩa ũgaĩirwo andũ arĩa athingu, nĩguo andũ acio athingu matikae gũtambũrũkia moko mao meeke ũũru.
english: Behold, children are a heritage of Yahweh. The fruit of the womb is his reward.

kikuyu: Nacio ikĩĩrana atĩrĩ, “Nĩtũtigei kũmĩtarũra; rekei tũmĩcuukĩre mĩtĩ nĩguo tũmenye ũrĩa ũgũthiĩ nayo.” Gwekĩkire ũguo nĩguo Maandĩko mahinge marĩa moigĩte atĩrĩ, “Maagayanire nguo ciakwa, na magĩcuukĩra kanjũ yakwa mĩtĩ.” Ũguo nĩguo thigari ciekire.
english: Then they said to one another, “Let’s not tear it, but cast lots for it to decide whose it will be,” that the Scripture might be fulfilled, which says, “They parted my garments among them. They cast lots for my clothing.”* Therefore the soldiers did these things.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo ngiuga atĩrĩ: “Hĩ! Mwathani Jehova, ti-itherũ nĩ ta ũkĩheenetie andũ aya biũ, o na ũkaheenia Jerusalemu, nĩ ũndũ wa kũmeera atĩrĩ, ‘Nĩmũkaagĩa na thayũ,’ o rĩrĩa tũigĩrĩirwo rũhiũ rwa njora mĩmero.”
english: “Go up on her walls, and destroy, but don’t make a full end. Take away her branches, for they are not Yahweh’s.

kikuyu: tondũ nĩ kũgerera harĩ Jesũ Kristũ watho wa Roho ũrĩa ũheaga muoyo wanjohorire kuuma kũrĩ wĩhia na gĩkuũ.
english: For the law of the Spirit of life in Christ Jesus made me free from the law of sin and of death.

kikuyu: Marakara mahiũ ma Jehova matigaathira, o nginya rĩrĩa akaahingia muoroto wa ngoro yake biũ. Matukũ-inĩ marĩa magooka-rĩ, nĩmũgataũkĩrwo nĩ ũhoro ũcio wega.
english: The fierce anger of Yahweh will not return until he has accomplished, and until he has performed the intentions of his heart. In the latter days you will understand it.”

kikuyu: ũmeere atĩrĩ, ‘Mwathani Jehova ekuuga ũũ: Nĩngaruta andũ a Isiraeli kuuma ndũrĩrĩ-inĩ kũrĩa maathiĩte. Nĩngamacookanĩrĩria ndĩmarute kũrĩa guothe marĩ, na ndĩmacookie bũrũri wao kĩũmbe.
english: I will call for a sword against him to all my mountains,” says the Lord Yahweh.

kikuyu: nĩngahũgũkĩra mũndũ ũcio, hamwe na andũ a nyũmba yake, na ndĩmaingate kuuma kũrĩ andũ ao, we mwene na arĩa othe mamũrũmagĩrĩra na kũhũũra ũmaraya na Moleku.
english: holding a form of godliness but having denied its power. Turn away from these, also.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo maambataga kuo na mahiũ mao na hema ciao mahaana ta ngigĩ kũingĩha. Andũ na ngamĩĩra ciao matingĩatarĩkire; magĩtharĩkĩra bũrũri ũcio nĩgeetha mawanange biũ.
english: So he brought down the people to the water; and Yahweh said to Gideon, “Everyone who laps of the water with his tongue, like a dog laps, you shall set him by himself; likewise everyone who bows down on his knees to drink.”

kikuyu: Ti-itherũ inegene rĩa ũhooi mĩhianano kũu tũrĩma-inĩ o na kũu irĩma-inĩ nĩ rĩa maheeni; ti-itherũ, ũhonokio wa Isiraeli ũrĩ na Jehova Ngai witũ.
english: Truly help from the hills, the tumult on the mountains, is in vain. Truly the salvation of Israel is in Yahweh our God.

kikuyu: Andũ acio mekaga maũndũ mooru kaĩ matamenyaga,
english: Their sorrows shall be multiplied who give gifts to another god.

kikuyu: Tũnyiitithanie tũtuĩke rũthanju rũmwe nĩguo tũtuĩke rũthanju rũmwe guoko-inĩ gwaku.
english: “ ‘The Lord Yahweh says: “Are you he of whom I spoke in old time by my servants the prophets of Israel, who prophesied in those days for years that I would bring you against them?

kikuyu: Mũndũ wa marakara mahiũ no nginya aherithio; ũngĩmũteithũra rĩmwe no nginya ũkaamũteithũra rĩngĩ.
english: A hot-tempered man must pay the penalty, for if you rescue him, you must do it again.

kikuyu: Bũrũri wothe wa Isiraeli nĩwagararĩte watho waku na ũgakũhutatĩra, na ũkarega gũgwathĩkĩra. “Nĩ ũndũ ũcio nĩtũkinyĩrĩirwo nĩ irumi na tũgacookererwo nĩ mwĩhĩtwa ũrĩa wandĩkĩtwo watho- inĩ wa Musa, ndungata ya Ngai, tondũ nĩtũkwĩhĩirie. Na rĩu nĩũhingĩtie ciugo iria waririe cia gũtũũkĩrĩra ithuĩ na anene aitũ na ũndũ wa gũtũrehithĩria mwanangĩko mũnene. Gũkũ thĩ guothe
english: “I watched at that time because of the voice of the arrogant words which the horn spoke. I watched even until the animal was slain, and its body destroyed, and it was given to be burned with fire.

kikuyu: “Edomu gũgaatuĩka kũndũ gwa kũmakania; arĩa othe makaahĩtũkĩra kuo nĩmakamaka na makũnyũrũrie, nĩ ũndũ wa ũrĩa gũgaakorwo kwanangĩtwo.
english: “Edom will become an astonishment. Everyone who passes by it will be astonished, and will hiss at all its plagues.

kikuyu: Rĩĩrĩ nĩrĩo itũũra rĩrĩa rĩratũire na gĩkeno, o na rĩtarĩ na ũgwati. Rĩrĩa rĩeĩĩraga atĩrĩ, “Niĩ no niĩ, na gũtirĩ ũngĩ tiga niĩ.”
english: This is the joyous city that lived carelessly, that said in her heart, “I am, and there is no one besides me.” How she has become a desolation, a place for animals to lie down in! Everyone who passes by her will hiss and shake their fists.

kikuyu: Nĩngumbũra rĩĩtwa rĩaku kũrĩ ariũ a baba; ndĩrĩkũgoocaga ndĩ kĩũngano-inĩ.
english: I will declare your name to my brothers. Among the assembly, I will praise you.

kikuyu: Mĩromo ĩrĩa ĩheenanagia nĩ kĩndũ kĩrĩ magigi harĩ Jehova,
english: Lying lips are an abomination to Yahweh, but those who do the truth are his delight.

kikuyu: “Jehova nĩarathimĩte mwathi wakwa mũno, na nĩ atongete mũno. Nĩamũheete ngʼondu na ngʼombe, betha na thahabu, ndungata cia arũme na cia andũ-a-nja, na ngamĩĩra o na ndigiri. Nake Sara mũtumia wa mwathi wakwa nĩamũciarĩire kĩmwana arĩ o mũkũrũ, nake nĩakĩheete indo ciake ciothe. Nake mwathi wakwa agĩtũma ndĩĩhĩte akĩnjĩĩra atĩrĩ, ‘Ndũkanethere mũriũ wakwa mũtumia kuuma kũrĩ airĩtu a Akaanani, a gũkũ bũrũri ũyũ ndũũraga,
english: Yahweh has blessed my master greatly. He has become great. Yahweh has given him flocks and herds, silver and gold, male servants and female servants, and camels and donkeys.

kikuyu: “ ‘Na rĩrĩ, woire ariũ na aarĩ aku arĩa wanjiarĩire, ũkĩmaruta igongona matuĩke ta irio cia mĩhianano ĩyo. Ũhũũri waku wa ũmaraya-rĩ, kaĩ ũtaarĩ mũiganu?
english: “ ‘ “Moreover you have taken your sons and your daughters, whom you have borne to me, and you have sacrificed these to them to be devoured. Was your prostitution a small matter,

kikuyu: O rĩngĩ akĩgĩa nda na rĩrĩa aaciarire kahĩĩ, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Ihinda rĩĩrĩ mũthuuri wakwa no ekwĩohania na niĩ tondũ nĩndĩmũciarĩire ihĩĩ ithatũ.” Nĩ ũndũ ũcio agĩgatua Lawi.
english: She conceived again, and bore a son. She said, “Now this time my husband will be joined to me, because I have borne him three sons.” Therefore his name was called Levi.

kikuyu: “Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, ikaragai mwĩiguĩte tondũ mũtiũĩ rĩrĩa mwene mũciĩ agacooka, kana agooka hwaĩ-inĩ, kana ũtukũ gatagatĩ, kana ngũkũ ĩkĩgamba, o na kana gũgĩthererũka.
english: Watch therefore, for you don’t know when the lord of the house is coming—whether at evening, or at midnight, or when the rooster crows, or in the morning;

kikuyu: “Wee, mũthamaki-rĩ, Nĩwonire mũrangĩri, mũndũ mũtheru, agĩikũrũka oimĩte matu-inĩ, akiugaga atĩrĩ, ‘Temai mũtĩ ũcio na mũwanange, no mũtige gĩthukĩ kĩohetwo na kĩgera na gĩcango, kũu nyeki-inĩ ya gĩthaka nayo mĩri yakĩo ĩtigwo tĩĩri-inĩ. Nake nĩaihũgagio nĩ ime rĩa matu-inĩ; nĩaikarage ta nyamũ cia gĩthaka ihinda rĩa mĩaka mũgwanja.’
english: “Whereas the king saw a holy watcher coming down from the sky and saying, ‘Cut down the tree, and destroy it; nevertheless leave the stump of its roots in the earth, even with a band of iron and bronze, in the tender grass of the field, and let it be wet with the dew of the sky. Let his portion be with the animals of the field, until seven times pass over him.’

kikuyu: Thuutha wa maũndũ macio mothe gwĩkĩka, Paũlũ agĩtua itua rĩa gũthiĩ Jerusalemu, atuĩkanĩirie Makedonia na Akaia. Akiuga atĩrĩ, “Ndaarĩkia gũkinya kũu, no nginya ngaacooka thiĩ Roma.”
english: Now after these things had ended, Paul determined in the Spirit, when he had passed through Macedonia and Achaia, to go to Jerusalem, saying, “After I have been there, I must also see Rome.”

kikuyu: Aakorwo nĩ mũndũ wa kuuma mĩaka ĩtano nginya mĩrongo ĩĩrĩ, ũgaatua irĩhi rĩa mũndũ mũrũme rĩtuĩke nĩ cekeri mĩrongo ĩĩrĩ,§ na irĩhi rĩa mũndũ-wa-nja nĩ cekeri ikũmi.*
english: If the person is from five years old to twenty years old, then your valuation shall be for a male twenty shekels, and for a female ten shekels.

kikuyu: Tũnyũmba twakĩo twa arangĩri, na thingo ciakĩo, na gĩthaku gĩakĩo nĩciaiganainie ithimo na icio ingĩ. Itoonyero rĩu na gĩthaku kĩarĩo ciarĩ na mĩanya mĩena yothe. Itoonyero rĩu rĩarĩ na ũraihu wa mĩkono mĩrongo ĩtano, na wariĩ wa mĩkono mĩrongo ĩĩrĩ na ĩtano.
english: with its lodges, its posts, and its arches, according to these measurements. There were windows in it and in its arches all around. It was fifty cubits long, and twenty-five cubits wide.

kikuyu: o nayo mĩmera ĩrĩa ĩrĩ hũgũrũrũ-inĩ cia Rũũĩ rwa Nili, gĩtwe-inĩ kĩa rũũĩ rũu, nĩĩkahooha. Indo iria ciothe ĩhaandĩtwo mĩgũnda ĩrĩa ĩrĩ gũkuhĩ na Rũũĩ rwa Nili nĩikooma biũ, na ciũmbũrwo, na itigacooka kuoneka kuo rĩngĩ.
english: When he sees a troop, horsemen in pairs, a troop of donkeys, a troop of camels, he shall listen diligently with great attentiveness.”

kikuyu: Wee Mwathani, wendo waku mũnene ũrĩa wa tene ũkĩrĩ kũ, ũrĩa wehĩtire, ũkĩĩrĩra Daudi na ũndũ wa wĩhokeku waku?
english: Lord, where are your former loving kindnesses, which you swore to David in your faithfulness?

kikuyu: Nayo ndogo ya kĩnyariirĩko kĩao ĩgũtũũra yambataga nginya tene na tene. Gũtirĩ kĩhurũko mũthenya kana ũtukũ kũrĩ arĩa mahooyaga nyamũ ĩyo na mũhiano wayo, kana kũrĩ mũndũ o wothe ũrĩa wĩkĩrĩtwo rũũri rwa rĩĩtwa rĩayo.”
english: The smoke of their torment goes up forever and ever. They have no rest day and night, those who worship the beast and his image, and whoever receives the mark of his name.

kikuyu: Mũthenya ũcio Jehova nĩakamaaũra mathaga mao: nĩmo bangiri, na rũtamĩ rwa kuoha njuĩrĩ, na mĩgathĩ ĩrĩa ĩĩhacĩte ta mweri,
english: In that day the Lord will take away the beauty of their anklets, the headbands, the crescent necklaces,

kikuyu: na gategwa kamwe, na ndũrũme ĩmwe, na gatũrũme kamwe ka mwaka ũmwe, nĩ ũndũ wa iruta rĩa njino;
english: one young bull, one ram, one male lamb a year old, for a burnt offering;

kikuyu: Ithuothe twarĩ andũ Rĩrĩa maarĩire makĩhũũna, maacookire magĩikia ngano iria-inĩ nĩgeetha marikabu ĩhũthe.
english: In all, we were two hundred seventy-six souls on the ship.

kikuyu: Matikanahikie atumia a ndigwa, kana mahikie atumia arĩa matiganĩte na athuuri ao; no rĩrĩ, no mahikie airĩtu gathirange a rũciaro rwa andũ a Isiraeli, kana atumia a ndigwa a athĩnjĩri- Ngai. No nginya marutage andũ akwa ũtiganu wa maũndũ marĩa matheru na marĩa ma ndũire, na mamoonagie gũkũũrana maũndũ marĩa marĩ thaahu na marĩa matarĩ thaahu.
english: They shall not take for their wives a widow, or her who is put away; but they shall take virgins of the offspring of the house of Israel, or a widow who is the widow of a priest.

kikuyu: “Ingĩkorwo nĩnyimĩte ndungata ciakwa cia arũme na cia andũ-a-nja kĩhooto rĩrĩa manateta nĩ ũndũ wakwa-rĩ,
english: “If I have despised the cause of my male servant or of my female servant, when they contended with me,

kikuyu: “ ‘Wee mũtumia ũyũ mũtharia! Wee wendaga ageni handũ ha mũthuuriguo!
english: “ ‘ “Adulterous wife, who takes strangers instead of her husband!

kikuyu: Akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Ũũ nĩguo ndamwĩrire hĩndĩ ĩrĩa ndaarĩ na inyuĩ atĩ: Maũndũ mothe marĩa mandĩkĩtwo ũhoro wakwa Watho-inĩ wa Musa, na Maandĩko-inĩ ma Anabii, na Thaburi, no nginya mahingio.”
english: He said to them, “This is what I told you while I was still with you, that all things which are written in the law of Moses, the prophets, and the psalms concerning me must be fulfilled.”

kikuyu: Akĩmĩikia Irima rĩu-Rĩtarĩ- Gĩturi, akĩmĩhingĩrĩria kuo, na akĩrĩcinĩrĩra mũhũri, nĩguo amĩgirĩrĩrie ndĩkanacooke kũheenia andũ a ndũrĩrĩ rĩngĩ nginya mĩaka ĩyo ngiri ĩmwe ĩthire. Thuutha wa ũguo nĩĩkarekererio ihinda inini.
english: There will be no curse any more. The throne of God and of the Lamb will be in it, and his servants will serve him.

kikuyu: Nayo ngumo yake ĩkĩhunja na ihenya bũrũri wothe wa Galili. Na maarĩkia kuuma thunagogi, magĩthiĩ na Jakubu na Johana mũciĩ gwa Simoni na Anderea. Na rĩrĩ, nyina wa mũtumia wa Simoni aarĩ gĩtanda-inĩ arĩ mũrũaru mũrimũ wa kũhiũha mwĩrĩ, nao makĩĩra Jesũ ũhoro wake. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Jesũ agĩthiĩ harĩa aarĩ, akĩmũnyiita guoko akĩmũũkĩria. Mũrimũ ũcio wa kũhiũha mwĩrĩ ũkĩmũtiga na akĩambĩrĩria kũmatungatĩra.
english: The report of him went out immediately into all the region of Galilee and its surrounding area.  “Behold”, from “ἰδοὺ”, means look at, take notice, observe, see, or gaze at. It is often used as an interjection.

kikuyu: Tondũ wa mehia maitũ, magetha maguo maingĩ mathiiaga kũrĩ athamaki arĩa ũigĩte igũrũ riitũ. Maathaga mĩĩrĩ iitũ na ngʼombe ciitũ o ũrĩa mekwenda. Tũrĩ ahinyĩrĩrie mũno. Kĩrĩko kĩa Andũ
english: It yields much increase to the kings whom you have set over us because of our sins. Also they have power over our bodies and over our livestock, at their pleasure, and we are in great distress.

kikuyu: Thĩ nĩkuo kuumaga irio, no na kũu thĩinĩ mũhuro kũgarũrũkĩte gũkahaana ta gũcinĩtwo na mwaki;
english: As for the earth, out of it comes bread. Underneath it is turned up as it were by fire.

kikuyu: Inyuĩ nĩmũũĩ ũrĩa gwekĩkire kũu Judea guothe, kwambĩrĩria Galili thuutha wa ũbatithio ũrĩa Johana aahunjagia,
english: you yourselves know what happened, which was proclaimed throughout all Judea, beginning from Galilee, after the baptism which John preached;

kikuyu: Ihinda rĩothe rĩa kwĩyamũra gwake arĩkoragwo aamũrĩirwo Jehova.
english: All the days of his separation he is holy to Yahweh.

kikuyu: Gĩcunjĩ gĩa ithatũ kĩa andũ a gũkũ thĩ nĩkĩoragirwo nĩ mĩthiro ĩyo ĩtatũ ya mwaki, na ndogo, na ũbiriti iria cioimaga tũnua-inĩ twacio.
english: By these three plagues, one third of mankind was killed: by the fire, the smoke, and the sulfur, which proceeded out of their mouths.

kikuyu: Nĩũũhonoketie ngaaga gũtharĩkĩrwo nĩ andũ; ũnduĩte mũtongoria wa ndũrĩrĩ; andũ itooĩ nĩmandungatagĩra.
english: You have delivered me from the strivings of the people. You have made me the head of the nations. A people whom I have not known shall serve me.

kikuyu: Mĩaka yothe ĩrĩa Adamu aatũũrire muoyo yarĩ mĩaka magana kenda na mĩrongo ĩtatũ, agĩcooka agĩkua.
english: All the days that Adam lived were nine hundred thirty years, then he died.

kikuyu: “Niĩ-rĩ, mũngĩngerekania na ũ kana nũũ mũngĩnjiganania nake? Mũngĩĩhaanania na ũ nĩguo tũgerekanio nake?
english: “To whom will you compare me, and consider my equal, and compare me, as if we were the same?

kikuyu: O na kũrĩ ũguo-rĩ, Juda, mwarĩ wa nyina ũcio ũtaarĩ mwĩhokeku, ndaanjookereire na ngoro yake yothe, no aanjookereire na ũhinga,” ũguo nĩguo Jehova ekuuga.
english: For pass over to the islands of Kittim, and see. Send to Kedar, and consider diligently, and see if there has been such a thing.

kikuyu: Nĩmakaaria ũhoro wakwa, moige atĩrĩ, ‘Ũthingu na hinya cionekaga harĩ Jehova o we wiki.’ ” Andũ arĩa othe maarakarire makĩmũũkĩrĩra, nĩmagooka harĩ we maconorithio. No njiaro ciothe cia Isiraeli nĩikoonekaga irĩ na ũthingu nĩ ũndũ wa Jehova, na nĩigakena mũno.
english: They will say of me, ‘There is righteousness and strength only in Yahweh.’ ” Even to him will men come. All those who raged against him will be disappointed.

kikuyu: Gegearai na mũmake, mwĩtuei atumumu na mwage kuona; mũnyue mũrĩĩo, no mũtikarĩĩo nĩ ndibei, ningĩ mũtũgũũge, no mũtigatũgũũgio nĩ njoohi.
english: For we are God’s fellow workers. You are God’s farming, God’s building.

kikuyu: Gũgĩkinya mũthenya wa mĩrongo ĩĩrĩ na mũgwanja wa mweri wa keerĩ-rĩ, thĩ nĩyaniarĩte biũ. Ningĩ Ngai akĩĩra Nuhu atĩrĩ,
english: When I bring a cloud over the earth, that the rainbow will be seen in the cloud,

kikuyu: “Ũndũ ũyũ nĩguo ũkoniĩ Alawii: Atĩrĩ, arũme othe arĩa mahingĩtie mĩaka mĩrongo ĩĩrĩ na ĩtano na makĩria nĩo marĩũkaga kũruta wĩra Hema-inĩ-ya- Gũtũnganwo,
english: “This is what is assigned to the Levites: from twenty-five years old and upward they shall go in to wait on the service in the work of the Tent of Meeting;

kikuyu: naguo mweri na njata ciathage ũtukũ; Wendo wake nĩ wa tene na tene.
english: the moon and stars to rule by night, for his loving kindness endures forever;

kikuyu: Ta thikĩrĩriai mũigue arĩa moorĩte na arĩa metharĩte makoima bũrũri wa Babuloni, makiumbũra marĩ kũu Zayuni ũrĩa Jehova Ngai witũ erĩhĩirie, akerĩhĩria nĩ ũndũ wa hekarũ yake.
english: Listen to those who flee and escape out of the land of Babylon, to declare in Zion the vengeance of Yahweh our God, the vengeance of his temple.

kikuyu: Watho wa Jehova nĩ mũkinyanĩru, ũriũkagia ngoro ya mũndũ. Kĩrĩra kĩa Jehova nĩ gĩa kwĩhokeka, nĩkĩũhĩgagia mũndũ ũrĩa ũtarĩ mũũgĩ.
english: For the king trusts in Yahweh. Through the loving kindness of the Most High, he shall not be moved.

kikuyu: Mũrũ wakwa tũũria matua mega na ũkũũrani maũndũ, ndũkanareke ciehere maitho-inĩ maku;
english: For no prophecy ever came by the will of man, but holy men of God spoke, being moved by the Holy Spirit.

kikuyu: † 43:16 nĩ ta mita ithathatũ na robo (6.25) ‡ 43:17 nĩ ta mita mũgwanja na robo (7:25) § 43:17 nĩ ta robo ya mita (0.25)
english: Facing the length of one hundred cubits† was the north door, and the width was fifty cubits.

kikuyu: Jehova nĩ mwega kũrĩ arĩa mamwĩhokaga, kũrĩ ngoro ĩyo ĩmũrongoragia;
english: Yahweh is good to those who wait for him, to the soul who seeks him.

kikuyu: “Mũrũ wa mũndũ, githĩ andũ a nyũmba ya Isiraeli o acio aremi matiakũũririe atĩrĩ, ‘Nĩ atĩa ũreka?’
english: “Son of man, hasn’t the house of Israel, the rebellious house, said to you, ‘What are you doing?’

kikuyu: Twareka athiĩ na mbere ũguo, andũ othe nĩmekũmwĩtĩkia, nao Aroma moke meyoere hekarũ iitũ, o na rũrĩrĩ rwitũ.”
english: If we leave him alone like this, everyone will believe in him, and the Romans will come and take away both our place and our nation.”

kikuyu: O ta ũrĩa mwĩrĩ ũtarĩ na roho ũrĩ mũkuũ-rĩ, ũguo noguo wĩtĩkio ũtarĩ ciĩko ũrĩ mũkuũ.
english: For as the body apart from the spirit is dead, even so faith apart from works is dead.

kikuyu: nĩguo ngoragwo na ũndũ wa gũcookeria andũ arĩa maanyũrũragia, nĩgũkorwo niĩ ndĩhokete kiugo gĩaku.
english: So I will have an answer for him who reproaches me, for I trust in your word.

kikuyu: Ndakaĩire Jehova ndĩ mĩnyamaro-inĩ, nake akĩnjĩtĩka na akĩndeithũra.
english: in beatings, in imprisonments, in riots, in labors, in watchings, in fastings,

kikuyu: Nowe Joashu agĩcookeria kĩrĩndĩ kĩu kĩamũrigiicĩirie kĩ na ũũru atĩrĩ, “Anga nĩmũkwenda kũrũĩrĩra Baali? Mũrageria kũmũhonokia? Mũndũ ũrĩa ũkũmũrũĩrĩra, ũcio ekũũragwo gũtanakĩa! Angĩkorwo Baali nĩ ngai-rĩ, no ahote kwĩrũĩrĩra rĩrĩa mũndũ angiunanga kĩgongona gĩake.” Nĩ ũndũ ũcio mũthenya ũcio magĩtua Gideoni “Jerubu-Baali,” makiuga atĩrĩ, “Baali nĩarekwo arũe nake we mwene,” nĩ ũndũ nĩoinangĩte kĩgongona kĩa Baali. Na rĩrĩ, Amidiani othe, na Amaleki, na andũ angĩ a mwena wa irathĩro magĩcookanĩrĩria mbũtũ ciao cia ita, makĩringa mũrĩmo wa Jorodani, na makĩamba hema ciao Kĩanda-inĩ kĩa Jezireeli.
english: Joash said to all who stood against him, “Will you contend for Baal? Or will you save him? He who will contend for him, let him be put to death by morning! If he is a god, let him contend for himself, because someone has broken down his altar!”

kikuyu: Ũriũngariũngaga nĩkĩ, ũkĩgarũraga mĩthiĩre yaku?
english: Why do you go about so much to change your ways? You will be ashamed of Egypt also, as you were ashamed of Assyria.

kikuyu: Ngũĩ ndĩgaacooka kũiguuo bũrũri-inĩ waku, o na kana mĩhaka-inĩ ya bũrũri waku kũgĩe ũũnũhi kana mwanangĩko, no nĩũgeeta thingo ciaku Ũhonokio, na ihingo ciaku ũciĩte Ũgooci.
english: Violence shall no more be heard in your land, nor desolation or destruction within your borders; but you will call your walls Salvation, and your gates Praise.

kikuyu: Mũndũ o wothe ũngĩkoma kana arĩĩre irio nyũmba ĩyo, no nginya athambie nguo ciake.
english: “The garment also that the plague of leprosy is in, whether it is a woolen garment, or a linen garment;

kikuyu: tondũ mũthenya ũcio nĩmũkahoroherio, nĩguo mũtherio. Hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩmũgatherio kuuma kũrĩ mehia manyu mothe mũrĩ hau mbere ya Jehova.
english: for on this day shall atonement be made for you, to cleanse you. You shall be clean from all your sins before Yahweh.

kikuyu: Inyuĩ irĩma ici, iguai thitango ya Jehova; thikĩrĩriai inyuĩ mĩthingi ya thĩ ĩrĩa ĩtũũraga tene na tene. Nĩgũkorwo Jehova arĩ na ciira na andũ ake, nĩarathitanga andũ a Isiraeli.
english: Hear, you mountains, Yahweh’s indictment, and you enduring foundations of the earth; for Yahweh has a case against his people, and he will contend with Israel.

kikuyu: Githĩ Isiraeli ndatuĩkĩte kĩndũ gĩa gũthekererwo nĩwe? Kaĩ aanyiitirwo arĩ gatagatĩ ka aici, atĩ nĩkĩo ũinagia kĩongo nĩkũmũnyarara, rĩrĩa rĩothe ũkwaria ũhoro wake?
english: For wasn’t Israel a derision to you? Was he found among thieves? For as often as you speak of him, you shake your head.

kikuyu: Mwĩnyiitĩrei bũrũri ũcio ũtuĩke wanyu na mũtũũre kuo, nĩgũkorwo nĩ ndĩmũheete bũrũri ũcio ũtuĩke wanyu.
english: You shall take possession of the land, and dwell therein; for I have given the land to you to possess it.

kikuyu: “ ‘Mũtikanandirike, mũhooyage mĩhianano kana mwĩthondekere ngai cia kĩgera gĩtweketio. Niĩ nĩ niĩ Jehova Ngai wanyu.
english: “ ‘Don’t turn to idols, nor make molten gods for yourselves. I am Yahweh your God.

kikuyu: Ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ, ngwenda mũmenye atĩ Ũhoro-ũrĩa-Mwega ndamũhunjĩirie ti ũhoro woimanĩte na mũndũ.
english: But I make known to you, brothers, concerning the Good News which was preached by me, that it is not according to man.

kikuyu: O na nĩ maarĩ na tũthamaki tũnini tũtarĩ tũingĩ; agĩcookia ngaatho nĩ ũndũ watuo, na akĩĩra arutwo magaĩre andũ. Nao makĩrĩa makĩhũũna. Thuutha ũcio arutwo makĩũngania cienyũ iria ciatigarĩte, ikĩiyũra ciondo mũgwanja.
english: They also had a few small fish. Having blessed them, he said to serve these also.  NU ends Jesus’ direct quote and question after “latrine”, ending the verse with “Thus he declared all foods clean.

kikuyu: “Atĩrĩrĩ, ihinda rĩa ciumia mĩrongo mũgwanja nĩrĩo rĩtuĩrĩirwo andũ aku, na rĩgatuĩrwo itũũra rĩaku itheru, nĩgeetha hagĩe na ihinda rĩa kũniina ũmaramari, na rĩa gũtigana na mehia, na rĩa kũhooroheria waganu, nĩguo ũhoro wa ũthingu ũrĩa ũtathiraga ũgĩe, nakĩo kĩoneki na ũrathi ciĩkĩrwo mũhũũri, nake ũrĩa mũtheru mũno aitĩrĩrio maguta.
english: “Seventy weeks are decreed on your people and on your holy city, to finish disobedience, to make an end of sins, to make reconciliation for iniquity, to bring in everlasting righteousness, to seal up vision and prophecy, and to anoint the most holy.

kikuyu: Mũtĩ wa kana wagwĩrĩire mũhĩrĩga wa Isakaru kũringana na ũrĩa mbarĩ ciao ciatariĩ.
english: The fourth lot came out for Issachar, even for the children of Issachar according to their families.

kikuyu: Nĩakaminjaminja thakame ĩmwe na kĩara gĩake igũrũ rĩakĩo maita mũgwanja, agĩtherie na akĩamũre nĩguo gĩthirwo nĩ thaahu wa andũ a Isiraeli.
english: He shall sprinkle some of the blood on it with his finger seven times, and cleanse it, and make it holy from the uncleanness of the children of Israel.

kikuyu: Ũtũmĩte andũ aku moke marume Jehova. Na ũkoiga atĩrĩ, ‘Nĩ ũndũ wa ngaari ciakwa nyingĩ cia ita, nĩnyambatĩte ngakinya tũcũmbĩrĩ twa irĩma, o kũu igũrũ mũno Lebanoni. Nĩndemete mĩtarakwa yakuo ĩrĩa mĩraihu mũno, o na mĩthengera ĩrĩa mĩega mũno. Nĩnginyĩte tũcũmbĩrĩ twakuo kũrĩa kũraya mũno, o kũrĩa kũrĩ na mĩtitũ ĩrĩa mĩega mũno.
english: By your servants, you have defied the Lord, and have said, “With the multitude of my chariots I have come up to the height of the mountains, to the innermost parts of Lebanon. I will cut down its tall cedars and its choice cypress trees. I will enter into its farthest height, the forest of its fruitful field.

kikuyu: Meere ũũ, “Jehova ekuuga atĩrĩ: * 9:17 Gĩtũmi kĩa acakai a kũrĩhwo kĩarĩ gĩa gũteithia arĩa makuĩrĩirwo marĩre na macakae.
english: Speak, “Yahweh says, “ ‘The dead bodies of men will fall as dung on the open field, and as the handful after the harvester. No one will gather them.’ ”

kikuyu: na Alameleki, na Amadi, na Mishali. Mwena wa ithũĩro mũhaka wahutĩtie Karimeli na Shihoru-Libunathu.
english: Allammelech, Amad, Mishal. It reached to Carmel westward, and to Shihorlibnath.

kikuyu: Wĩtĩkio wa mũndũ ũmwe-rĩ, nĩũmwĩtĩkĩrĩtie kũrĩa irio cia mĩthemba yothe, no mũndũ ũngĩ, ũrĩa ũrĩ wĩtĩkio mũhũthũ, arĩĩaga o mĩthemba ya nyeni.
english: One man has faith to eat all things, but he who is weak eats only vegetables.

kikuyu: Rĩra, wee mũthithinda, nĩgũkorwo mũtarakwa nĩũgwĩte; mĩtĩ ĩrĩa mĩrũngarũ nĩmĩanange! Rĩrai inyuĩ mĩgandi ya Bashani; nĩgũkorwo mĩtĩ ya mũtitũ ũrĩa mũtumanu nĩĩtemetwo.
english: Wail, cypress tree, for the cedar has fallen, because the stately ones are destroyed. Wail, you oaks of Bashan, for the strong forest has come down.

kikuyu: na mũthamaki wa Kedeshi, ũmwe, na mũthamaki wa Jokineamu itũũra rĩ kũu Karimeli, ũmwe,
english: the king of Kedesh, one; the king of Jokneam in Carmel, one;

kikuyu: Bũrũri ũcio ũgaatuĩka wa matigari ma nyũmba ya Juda; kũu nĩkuo makoona ũrĩithio. Hwaĩ-inĩ magaakomaga nyũmba cia Ashikeloni. Nake Jehova Ngai wao nĩakamamenyerera, na atũme magaacĩre rĩngĩ. Ũrathi wa Gũũkĩrĩra Moabi na Amoni
english: I said, “Just fear me. Receive correction,” so that her dwelling won’t be cut off, according to all that I have appointed concerning her. But they rose early and corrupted all their doings.

kikuyu: Nĩmagĩcookerie Jehova ngaatho nĩ ũndũ wa wendo wake ũrĩa ũtathiraga, na mawĩko make ma magegania marĩa eekĩire andũ.
english: Let them praise Yahweh for his loving kindness, for his wonderful deeds for the children of men!

kikuyu: ngurunga ĩrĩa ĩrĩ gĩthaka- inĩ kĩa Makipela hakuhĩ na Mamure kũu Kaanani, ĩrĩa Iburahĩmu aagũranĩirie na gĩthaka kĩu, ĩrĩ handũ ha gũthikana, kuuma kũrĩ Efironi ũrĩa Mũhiti.
english: in the cave that is in the field of Machpelah, which is before Mamre, in the land of Canaan, which Abraham bought with the field from Ephron the Hittite as a burial place.

kikuyu: Jusufu agĩkĩigithia ngano nyingĩ mũno, o ta mũthanga wa iria-inĩ; yarĩ nyingĩ mũno, o nginya agĩtiga kũiga maandĩko ma mũigana wayo tondũ ndĩngĩathimĩkire.
english: Joseph laid up grain as the sand of the sea, very much, until he stopped counting, for it was without number.

kikuyu: Ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ, aca! No rĩrĩ, mwaga kwĩrira, o na inyuĩ inyuothe mũkaaniinwo o tao.”
english: I tell you, no, but, unless you repent, you will all perish in the same way.”

kikuyu: Nowe Jesũ akĩĩhũgũra akĩmakaania,
english: But he turned and rebuked them, “You don’t know of what kind of spirit you are.

kikuyu: Maaĩ ma kũu Nimurimu nĩmahũĩte, nayo nyeki nĩĩhoohete; namo mahuti mothe maigũ nĩmomĩte, na gũtirĩ kĩndũ kĩĩruru gĩtigaire. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio ũtonga ũrĩa magĩte naguo na makeigĩra, maũkuuĩte makaũringia mũrĩmo wa karũũĩ karĩa karĩ mĩtĩ hũgũrũrũ-inĩ ciako.
english: We have heard of the pride of Moab, that he is very proud; even of his arrogance, his pride, and his wrath. His boastings are nothing.

kikuyu: Andũ aingĩ nĩmathiire kũrĩ Johana akamabatithie, nake akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Inyuĩ rũciaro rũrũ rwa nduĩra! Nũũ ũmũtaarĩte mũũrĩre marakara marĩa makiriĩ gũũka?
english: He said therefore to the multitudes who went out to be baptized by him, “You offspring of vipers, who warned you to flee from the wrath to come?

kikuyu: Aatũmire Musa ndungata yake, na Harũni, ũrĩa aathurĩte. Nao makĩringa ciama ciake gatagatĩ-inĩ kao, magĩĩka magegania make bũrũri-inĩ wa Hamu.
english: He sent Moses, his servant, and Aaron, whom he had chosen.

kikuyu: Makĩnuguna marĩ hema-inĩ ciao, na makĩaga gwathĩkĩra Jehova. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio akĩoya guoko na igũrũ, akĩĩhĩta atĩ nĩakamangʼaũranĩria kũu werũ-inĩ,
english: but murmured in their tents, and didn’t listen to Yahweh’s voice.

kikuyu: ngĩheaga andũ arĩa manyendete ũtonga, na ngaiyũragia igĩĩna ciao.
english: that I may give wealth to those who love me. I fill their treasuries.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo Jehova Ngai nĩ riũa na ngo; Jehova nĩarĩtũtugaga na agatwĩka maũndũ mega; arĩa mĩthiĩre yao ĩtarĩ ũcuuke ndarĩ ũndũ mwega angĩmaima.
english: Teach me your way, Yahweh. I will walk in your truth. Make my heart undivided to fear your name.

kikuyu: Mogwati maya meerĩ nĩmo magũkorete; nũũ ũgaagĩkũhooreria? Mogwati macio nĩ mwanangĩko na ũniinani,
english: These two things have happened to you— who will grieve with you?— desolation and destruction, and famine and the sword. How can I comfort you?

kikuyu: Andũ othe arĩa maamũiguire makĩgega makĩũria atĩrĩ, “Githĩ mũndũ ũyũ tiwe ũraagithĩtie andũ thayũ kũu Jerusalemu gatagatĩ- inĩ ka arĩa makayagĩra rĩĩtwa rĩu? Na githĩ ndokĩte gũkũ nĩgeetha amanyiite amatware marĩ ohe kũrĩ athĩnjĩri-Ngai arĩa anene?”
english: All who heard him were amazed, and said, “Isn’t this he who in Jerusalem made havoc of those who called on this name? And he had come here intending to bring them bound before the chief priests!”

kikuyu: Agĩtũma igwe thĩinĩ wa kambĩ yao, igĩthiũrũrũkĩria hema ciao.
english: He let them fall in the middle of their camp, around their habitations.

kikuyu: Ũroaga gwĩka ũndũ ta ũcio, wa kũũraganĩria arĩa athingu hamwe na arĩa aaganu, arĩa athingu na arĩa aaganu ũmataranĩrie ũndũ ũmwe. Ũroaga gwĩka ũndũ ta ũcio! Mũtuanĩri ciira wa thĩ yothe-rĩ, githĩ ndagĩrĩirwo nĩ gwĩka ũrĩa kwagĩrĩire?” Nake Jehova akiuga atĩrĩ, “Ingĩkora andũ mĩrongo ĩtano athingu kũu itũũra rĩu inene rĩa Sodomu- rĩ, nĩngũhonokia kũndũ kũu guothe nĩ ũndũ wao.” Ningĩ Iburahĩmu akĩaria rĩngĩ, akiuga atĩrĩ: “Rĩu tondũ nĩngĩĩũmĩrĩirie nginya ndaaria na Mwathani, o na gũtuĩka ndĩ o rũkũngũ na mũhu-rĩ, atĩrĩ,
english: May it be far from you to do things like that, to kill the righteous with the wicked, so that the righteous should be like the wicked. May that be far from you. Shouldn’t the Judge of all the earth do right?” ,14  NU puts the phrase “not of works, but of him who calls” at the beginning of verse

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo nĩwe wombire ciĩga ciakwa cia na thĩinĩ; wanyiitithanirie ndĩ o nda ya maitũ.
english: Surely the righteous will give thanks to your name. The upright will dwell in your presence.

kikuyu: nĩcio ikũreheire maũndũ macio, nĩgũkorwo nĩwerirĩirie ndũrĩrĩ icio, na ũgĩĩthaahia na mĩhianano yacio.
english: These things will be done to you because you have played the prostitute after the nations, and because you are polluted with their idols.

kikuyu: Nũũ ũngiuga atĩrĩ, “Nĩnjigĩte ngoro yakwa ĩrĩ theru; ndĩ mũtheru, na ndirĩ na mehia”?
english: Who can say, “I have made my heart pure. I am clean and without sin”?

kikuyu: Matũũra mothe marĩa maaheirwo mbarĩ cia Amerari, nao nĩo Alawii arĩa maatigaire, maarĩ ikũmi na meerĩ. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio matũũra ma Alawii thĩinĩ wa bũrũri ũrĩa wegwatĩirwo nĩ andũ a Isiraeli maarĩ mĩrongo ĩna na manana marĩ mothe, hamwe na ithaka ciamo cia ũrĩithio.
english: All these were the cities of the children of Merari according to their families, even the rest of the families of the Levites. Their lot was twelve cities.

kikuyu: Agĩteania nguo yake ya igũrũ, agĩũkĩra na ihenya agĩthiĩ harĩ Jesũ.
english: He, casting away his cloak, sprang up, and came to Jesus.

kikuyu: Ngĩkinya kũrĩ arĩa maatahĩtwo, arĩa maatũũraga kũu Teli-Abibu, gũkuhĩ na Rũũĩ rwa Kebari, na ngĩikarania nao kwa ihinda rĩa mĩthenya mũgwanja, makĩte mũno. Isiraeli Gũkaanio
english: Then I came to them of the captivity at Tel Aviv who lived by the river Chebar, and to where they lived; and I sat there overwhelmed among them seven days.

kikuyu: Wee wĩhumbĩrĩte na itu nĩgeetha gũtikagĩe na ihooya rĩngĩgũkinyĩra.
english: You have covered yourself with a cloud, so that no prayer can pass through.

kikuyu: o na nduma ndĩrĩ hĩndĩ ĩngĩgũtumanĩra; ũtukũ ũkwara ta mũthenya, nĩgũkorwo nduma kũrĩ we nĩ ta ũtheri.
english: I know that Yahweh will maintain the cause of the afflicted, and justice for the needy.

kikuyu: mwĩrutei gwĩkaga wega! Thingatagai ciira wa kĩhooto, na mũkoomĩrĩria arĩa ahinyĩrĩrie. Tuagai ciira wa ciana cia ngoriai na kĩhooto, na mũthaithanagĩrĩre mũtumia wa ndigwa.”
english: Learn to do well. Seek justice. Relieve the oppressed. Defend the fatherless. Plead for the widow.”

kikuyu: andũ arĩa Jehova arathimĩte nĩmakagaya bũrũri, no arĩa arumĩte nĩmakaniinwo.
english: For such as are blessed by him shall inherit the land. Those who are cursed by him shall be cut off.

kikuyu: Kũrathimwo-rĩ, nĩ arĩa atheru ngoro, nĩgũkorwo nĩmakona Ngai.
english: Blessed are the pure in heart, for they shall see God.

kikuyu: “ ‘Daudi ndungata yakwa nĩwe ũgaatuĩka mũthamaki wao, nao othe magĩe na mũrĩithi ũmwe. Nĩmakarũmĩrĩra mawatho makwa na mamenyerere watho wakwa wa kũrũmĩrĩrwo.
english: “ ‘ “My servant David will be king over them. They all will have one shepherd. They will also walk in my ordinances and observe my statutes, and do them.

kikuyu: Ithuothe nĩtũhĩngĩcagwo nĩ maũndũ maingĩ. Mũndũ angĩkorwo ndarĩ na mahĩtia thĩinĩ wa maũndũ marĩa aragia-rĩ, ũcio nĩ mũkinyanĩru kũna, na nĩahotete kũriĩra mwĩrĩ wake wothe.
english: For we all stumble in many things. Anyone who doesn’t stumble in word is a perfect person, able to bridle the whole body also. ); or (2) Literally, “pride” or “arrogance” —possibly a reference to a large aquatic creature (

kikuyu: Irĩra iria ciothe ũheetwo no gũkũnogia ikũnogetie! Ago aku arĩa maroraga igũrũ matu-inĩ nĩmeyumĩrie, acio matuĩragia njata na makaratha mohoro o mweri, o mweri-rĩ, nĩmagĩkũhonokie kuuma kũrĩ maũndũ marĩa megũgũkorerera.
english: You are wearied in the multitude of your counsels. Now let the astrologers, the stargazers, and the monthly prognosticators stand up and save you from the things that will happen to you.

kikuyu: mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai nĩakarora kĩronda kĩu, na kĩngĩoneka kĩrikĩire gũkĩra ngoothi, nayo njuĩrĩ yaho ĩkorwo nĩ ya rangi wa ngoikoni, na ĩkorwo ĩrĩ njeke, mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai nĩagatua atĩ mũndũ ũcio arĩ na thaahu; ũcio nĩ ũhere, nĩ mũrimũ wa mangũ ma mũtwe kana ma kĩreru. No hĩndĩ ĩrĩa mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai angĩrora kĩronda kĩa mũthemba ũcio, na gĩkorwo ti kĩrikĩru gũkĩra ngoothi, na hatirĩ njuĩrĩ njirũ thĩinĩ wakĩo, hĩndĩ ĩyo mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai nĩakaiga mũndũ ũcio mũrũaru handũ mwanya mĩthenya mũgwanja.
english: then the priest shall examine the plague; and behold, if its appearance is deeper than the skin, and the hair in it is yellow and thin, then the priest shall pronounce him unclean. It is an itch. It is leprosy of the head or of the beard.

kikuyu: Jehova nĩehĩtire na mwĩhĩtwa harĩ Daudi, mwĩhĩtwa atangĩĩricũkwo, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ: “Nĩngaikarĩria ũmwe wa rũciaro rwaku Gĩtĩ-inĩ gĩaku kĩa ũnene,
english: Sihon king of the Amorites, Og king of Bashan, and all the kingdoms of Canaan—

kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo ngoro ciao ithugundaga o haaro, na mĩromo yao yaragia ũhoro wa kũrehere andũ thĩĩna.
english: for their hearts plot violence and their lips talk about mischief.
