kikuyu: Mwĩohei indo ciothe cia Ngai cia mbaara nĩguo mũhote gwĩtiiria mawara ma mũcukani.
english: Put on the whole armor of God, that you may be able to stand against the wiles of the devil.

kikuyu: Ihooya rĩa Hwaĩ-inĩ Thaburi ya Daudi
english: Then I wept much, because no one was found worthy to open the book or to look in it.

kikuyu: Nĩngũhingĩria Jehova mĩĩhĩtwa ĩrĩa ndehĩtire, andũ ake othe marĩ ho.
english: I will pay my vows to Yahweh, yes, in the presence of all his people.

kikuyu: O na ningĩ andũ matiakagia tawa magacooka kũũkunĩkĩrana mbakũri; handũ ha ũguo, maũigagĩrĩra igũrũ handũ haguo, naguo ũkamũrĩkĩra andũ othe arĩa marĩ thĩinĩ wa nyũmba.
english: “The land of Zebulun and the land of Naphtali, toward the sea, beyond the Jordan, Galilee of the Gentiles,

kikuyu: “Niĩ mwene ngiuga atĩrĩ, “ ‘Ingĩakenire atĩa ngĩmũtua ta ariũ akwa, na ndĩmũhe bũrũri mwega, igai rĩrĩa rĩega rĩkĩrĩte magai ma ndũrĩrĩ ciothe.’ Ndeciiragia mũrĩnjĩtaga ‘Baba’ na mwage kũũhutatĩra nĩguo mũtige kũnũmĩrĩra. No rĩrĩ, o ta mũtumia ũtarĩ mwĩhokeku harĩ mũthuuriwe, ũguo noguo mũkoretwo mũtarĩ ehokeku harĩ niĩ, inyuĩ nyũmba ya Isiraeli,” ũguo nĩguo Jehova ekuuga.
english: “But I said, ‘How I desire to put you among the children, and give you a pleasant land, a goodly heritage of the armies of the nations!’ and I said, ‘You shall call me “My Father”, and shall not turn away from following me.’

kikuyu: Kuuma hĩndĩ ĩyo Judasi akĩambĩrĩria gũcaria mweke wa kũmũneana. Gĩathĩ kĩa Bathaka
english: From that time he sought opportunity to betray him.

kikuyu: Nũũ waheire ngoro ũũgĩ, kana akĩhe meciiria ũmenyo?
english: Who has put wisdom in the inward parts? Or who has given understanding to the mind?

kikuyu: Baraka agĩũka aingatithĩtie Sisera, nake Jaeli agĩthiĩ kũmũtũnga. Akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Ũka, nĩngũkuonia mũndũ ũrĩa ũracaria.” Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Baraka agĩtoonya hamwe na Jaeli, na agĩkora Sisera akomete thĩ arĩ mũkuũ nayo higĩ ĩmũtũrĩkanĩtie thikĩrĩrio.
english: Behold, as Barak pursued Sisera, Jael came out to meet him, and said to him, “Come, and I will show you the man whom you seek.” He came to her; and behold, Sisera lay dead, and the tent peg was in his temples.

kikuyu: nuthu ĩrĩa yaheirwo kĩrĩndĩ ciarĩ ngʼondu 337,500, 44nacio ngʼombe ciarĩ 36,000, 45nacio ndigiri ciarĩ 30,500,
english: (now the congregation’s half was three hundred thirty-seven thousand five hundred sheep,

kikuyu: Andũ a Isiraeli acio makĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Ithuĩ tũkũgerera o njĩra ĩrĩa nene, na ithuĩ ene kana mahiũ maitũ tũngĩnyua maaĩ manyu-rĩ, nĩtũkũrĩha. Tũrenda o tũtuĩkanie tũthiĩte na magũrũ tu hatarĩ ũndũ ũngĩ.” * 20:14 Andũ a Edomu maarĩ njiaro cia Esaũ.
english: The clean person shall sprinkle on the unclean on the third day, and on the seventh day. On the seventh day, he shall purify him. He shall wash his clothes and bathe himself in water, and shall be clean at evening.

kikuyu: Andũ arĩa arũngĩrĩru monaga ũguo magakena, no arĩa othe aaganu nĩgũkirio makiragio.
english: The upright will see it, and be glad. All the wicked will shut their mouths.

kikuyu: tũrĩ atheru na athingu mbere yake matukũ-inĩ maitũ mothe.
english: in holiness and righteousness before him all the days of our life.

kikuyu: “O na kũrĩ we, wee Juda, nĩwathĩrĩirio ihinda rĩa magetha. “O hĩndĩ ĩrĩa ndaacookeria andũ akwa maũndũ ma kũmaguna,
english: “Ephraim is like an easily deceived dove, without understanding. They call to Egypt. They go to Assyria.

kikuyu: Andũ a thĩ acio angĩ, arĩa matooragirwo nĩ mĩthiro ĩyo, matiigana kwĩrira mehia ma wĩra wa moko mao; matiigana gũtiga kũhooya ndaimono, na mĩhianano ya thahabu, na ya betha, na ya gĩcango, na ya mahiga, na ya mĩtĩ, o mĩhianano ĩrĩa ĩtangĩhota kuona, kana ĩigue, kana ĩĩtware.
english: The rest of mankind, who were not killed with these plagues, didn’t repent of the works of their hands, that they wouldn’t worship demons, and the idols of gold, and of silver, and of brass, and of stone, and of wood, which can’t see, hear, or walk.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo Mwathani atwathĩte agatwĩra atĩrĩ: “ ‘Nĩndĩmũtuĩte ũtheri wa gũtherera andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ, nĩguo mũtware ũhoro wa ũhonokio nginya ituri-inĩ cia thĩ.’ ”
english: For so has the Lord commanded us, saying, ‘I have set you as a light for the Gentiles, that you should bring salvation to the uttermost parts of the earth.’ ”*

kikuyu: Andũ magaatũgũũgaga kuuma iria-inĩ rĩmwe nginya rĩrĩa rĩngĩ, na morũũrage kuuma gathigathini nginya irathĩro, magĩcaria kiugo kĩa Jehova, no matigakĩona.
english: There are certain Jews whom you have appointed over the affairs of the province of Babylon: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego. These men, O king, have not respected you. They don’t serve your gods, and don’t worship the golden image which you have set up.”

kikuyu: Andũ arĩa etĩĩi maanyũrũragia matekwĩgirĩrĩria, no nditiganagĩria watho waku.
english: The arrogant mock me excessively, but I don’t swerve from your law.

kikuyu: Agĩthiĩ na Petero, na ariũ arĩa eerĩ a Zebedi, nake akĩambĩrĩria kũigua kĩeha na gũthĩĩnĩka ngoro.
english: He took with him Peter and the two sons of Zebedee, and began to be sorrowful and severely troubled.

kikuyu: (No rĩrĩ, andũ a Dani nĩmaremirwo nĩ kwĩgwatĩra bũrũri wao; nĩ ũndũ ũcio makĩambata magĩtharĩkĩra itũũra rĩa Leshemu, makĩrĩtunyana na makĩũraga andũ arĩa maatũũraga kuo na rũhiũ rwa njora, narĩo rĩgĩgĩtuĩka rĩao. Nao magĩtũũra kũu Leshemu. Makĩrĩtua Dani o ta ũrĩa ithe wao eetagwo.)
english: The border of the children of Dan went out beyond them; for the children of Dan went up and fought against Leshem, and took it, and struck it with the edge of the sword, and possessed it, and lived therein, and called Leshem, Dan, after the name of Dan their forefather.

kikuyu: O mũndũ aanagĩrĩra akoiga atĩrĩ, “Ũnganai ndĩmũrehere ndibei! Nĩtũnyue njoohi nginya tũrĩĩo! Naruo rũciũ rũkaahaana o ta ũmũthĩ, kana rwagĩre mũno makĩria.”
english: “Come,” they say, “I will get wine, and we will fill ourselves with strong drink; and tomorrow will be as today, great beyond measure.”

kikuyu: Menyai atĩ Jehova nĩeyamũrĩire arĩa mamwendete; Jehova nĩarĩiguaga rĩrĩa ndamũkaĩra.
english: No one in heaven above, or on the earth, or under the earth, was able to open the book or to look in it.

kikuyu: “Nao othe, o mũndũ akĩambĩrĩria kũheana ciĩgwatio. Wa mbere akiuga atĩrĩ, ‘No hĩndĩ ndĩragũrire mũgũnda na no nginya thiĩ ngawone. Thiĩ ũmwĩre andekere.’
english: They all as one began to make excuses. “The first said to him, ‘I have bought a field, and I must go and see it. Please have me excused.’

kikuyu: Wee Jehova, ndũkandiganĩrie; Wee Ngai wakwa, ndũgaikare haraihu na niĩ.
english: Don’t forsake me, Yahweh. My God, don’t be far from me.

kikuyu: Akĩinamĩrĩra, na akĩrora thĩinĩ, akĩona mataama ma gatani marĩ maige thĩ, no ndaatoonyire. Nake Simoni Petero, ũrĩa warĩ thuutha wake, agĩkinya na agĩtoonya mbĩrĩra thĩinĩ. Akĩona mataama ma
english: Stooping and looking in, he saw the linen cloths lying there; yet he didn’t enter in.

kikuyu: Nĩngũkena mũno thĩinĩ wa Mwathani nĩkuona atĩ marigĩrĩrio-inĩ nĩmũrurumũkĩtie ũhoro wanyu wa kwĩĩnjiiria. Ti-itherũ nĩmũreĩnjiiragia, no mũtirarĩ na mweke wa kuonania ũguo.
english: that I may know him and the power of his resurrection, and the fellowship of his sufferings, becoming conformed to his death,

kikuyu: Ũũ nĩguo Jehova ekuuga: ‘Nĩngũneana Firaũni Hofira mũthamaki wa bũrũri wa Misiri kũrĩ thũ ciake iria icaragia kũmũruta muoyo, o ta ũrĩa ndaaneanire Zedekia mũthamaki wa Juda moko-inĩ ma Nebukadinezaru mũthamaki wa Babuloni, thũ ĩrĩa yacaragia kũmũruta muoyo.’ ”
english: Yahweh says, ‘Behold, I will give Pharaoh Hophra king of Egypt into the hand of his enemies and into the hand of those who seek his life, just as I gave Zedekiah king of Judah into the hand of Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon, who was his enemy and sought his life.’ ”

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo mĩaka ngiri harĩwe no ta mũthenya wa ira ũrĩa ũrahĩtũkire, kana ta ituanĩra rĩmwe rĩa arangĩri a ũtukũ.
english: For a thousand years in your sight are just like yesterday when it is past, like a watch in the night.

kikuyu: Mũrata aheenagia mũratawe, na gũtirĩ mũndũ o na ũmwe waragia ũhoro wa ma. Maarutĩte nĩmĩ ciao kwaria ndeto cia maheeni; menogagia na mĩhangʼo o ya kwĩhia.
english: Friends deceive each other, and will not speak the truth. They have taught their tongue to speak lies. They weary themselves committing iniquity.

kikuyu: Nĩikahĩngwo rĩngĩ na rĩngĩ; ikaagũanĩra o mũndũ igũrũ rĩa ũrĩa ũngĩ. Ikoiga atĩrĩ, ‘Ũkĩrai, nĩtũcookei kũrĩ andũ aitũ, kũrĩa twaciarĩirwo, tweherere rũhiũ rũrũ rwa njora rwa mũtũhatĩrĩria.’
english: He made many to stumble. Yes, they fell on one another. They said, ‘Arise! Let’s go again to our own people, and to the land of our birth, from the oppressing sword.’

kikuyu: “Ta njiguai, inyuĩ andũ arĩa mũũĩ ũhoro wa ũthingu, o inyuĩ andũ arĩa mũrũmĩtie watho wakwa ngoro-inĩ cianyu: Mũtigetigĩre kũmenwo nĩ andũ, o na kana mũmakio nĩ irumi iria mamũrumaga nacio.
english: “Listen to me, you who know righteousness, the people in whose heart is my law. Don’t fear the reproach of men, and don’t be dismayed at their insults.

kikuyu: “ ‘Ici nĩcio ciathĩ iria ciathanĩtwo cia Jehova, nĩcio ciũngano iria theru, iria mũrĩhunjanagĩria mahinda ma cio makinya.
english: “ ‘These are the set feasts of Yahweh, even holy convocations, which you shall proclaim in their appointed season.

kikuyu: tiga ũkorirwo nĩũtũregeete biũ, ũgagĩtũrakarĩra o mũno makĩria.
english: But you have utterly rejected us. You are very angry against us.

kikuyu: na Rabithu, na Kishioni, na Ebezu,
english: Rabbith, Kishion, Ebez,

kikuyu: Nyũmba-inĩ ya mũthamaki kũu Shushani, Ayahudi nĩmooragire na makĩniina andũ magana matano. Ningĩ nĩmooragire Parishandatha, na Dalifoni, na Asipatha,
english: For how can I endure to see the evil that would come to my people? How can I endure to see the destruction of my relatives?”

kikuyu: nĩguo ũhote kũnyiita thĩ na mĩthia yaguo, nao arĩa aaganu maribaribwo moime kuo?
english: that it might take hold of the ends of the earth, and shake the wicked out of it?

kikuyu: Maitho ma andũ othe nĩwe macũthĩrĩirie, na nĩũmaheaga irio ciao hĩndĩ ĩrĩa yagĩrĩire.
english: The eyes of all wait for you. You give them their food in due season.

kikuyu: gũtiarĩ kahinga o na kamwe ga gĩthaka koonekete thĩ kana mũmera wa gĩthaka ũkamera. Nĩ tondũ Jehova Ngai ndoirĩtie mbura thĩ na gũtiarĩ mũndũ wa kũrĩma mũgũnda,
english: No plant of the field was yet in the earth, and no herb of the field had yet sprung up; for Yahweh God had not caused it to rain on the earth. There was not a man to till the ground,

kikuyu: O na ningĩ ngĩmeera ũrĩa guoko kwa ũtugi kwa Ngai wakwa kwarĩ hamwe na niĩ, na ũrĩa mũthamaki anjĩĩrĩte. Nao magĩcookia atĩrĩ, “Nĩtwambĩrĩriei gwaka rĩngĩ.” Nĩ ũndũ ũcio makĩambĩrĩria wĩra ũcio mwega. No rĩrĩa Sanibalati ũrĩa Mũhoroni na Tobia mũnene ũrĩa Mũamoni na Geshemu ũrĩa Mũarabu maiguire ũhoro ũcio, magĩtũnyũrũria na magĩtũthekerera, makĩũria atĩrĩ, “Nĩ atĩa ũũ mũreka? Kaĩ mũkũremera mũthamaki?”
english: I told them about the hand of my God which was good on me, and also about the king’s words that he had spoken to me. They said, “Let’s rise up and build.” So they strengthened their hands for the good work.

kikuyu: Gĩkĩ nĩkĩo kĩarĩ kĩama gĩa keerĩ kĩrĩa Jesũ aaringire kũu Galili, oimĩte Judea.
english: This is again the second sign that Jesus did, having come out of Judea into Galilee.

kikuyu: Ti-itherũ, ũndũ ũyũ noguo ũgaatuĩka wa kũũhonokia, nĩgũkorwo mũndũ ũtooĩ Ngai ndangĩũmĩrĩria gũũka mbere yake!
english: This also will be my salvation, that a godless man will not come before him.

kikuyu: Ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ na ma, kũrĩa guothe Ũhoro-ũyũ-Mwega ũkaahunjio thĩinĩ wa thĩ yothe-rĩ, ũndũ ũcio eeka nĩũkeeragwo andũ nĩgeetha aririkanagwo.” Judasi Gwĩtĩkĩra Gũkunyanĩra Jesũ
english: Most certainly I tell you, wherever this Good News is preached in the whole world, what this woman has done will also be spoken of as a memorial of her.”

kikuyu: Mũndũ o wothe ũngĩconoka nĩ ũndũ wakwa na aconoke nĩ ũndũ wa ciugo ciakwa hĩndĩ ĩno ya rũciaro rũrũ rwa ũtharia na rwa mehia, o nake Mũrũ wa Mũndũ nĩakamũconokera rĩrĩa agaacooka arĩ na riiri wa Ithe, arĩ na araika arĩa atheru.”
english: For whoever will be ashamed of me and of my words in this adulterous and sinful generation, the Son of Man also will be ashamed of him when he comes in his Father’s glory with the holy angels.”  The word translated “generation” here (genea) could also be translated “people”, “race”, or “family”.

kikuyu: ngĩhooya ũũ: “Wee Ngai wakwa, nĩngũigua thoni mũno na ngaconoka na ndingĩhota gũgũtiirĩra gĩthiithi, Wee Ngai wakwa, tondũ mehia maitũ nĩ maingĩ makaraiha gũkĩra ũraihu witũ na ũũru witũ, ũgakinya o kũu igũrũ.
english: But it was so, that in the twenty-third year of King Jehoash the priests had not repaired the damage to the house.

kikuyu: O na agĩthima mwena wa gũthini; mũigana waho warĩ mĩkono magana matano ũthimĩtwo na rũthanju rũu rwa gũthima. Ningĩ agĩcooka agĩthima mwena wa ithũĩro; mũigana waho warĩ mĩkono magana matano ũthimĩtwo na rũthanju rũu rwa gũthima.
english: It was made with cherubim and palm trees. A palm tree was between cherub and cherub, and every cherub had two faces,

kikuyu: Bũrũri ũcio nĩũkagĩa na kĩrĩro, o mbarĩ o mbarĩ ĩrĩre ĩrĩ iiki, na atumia ao marĩ oiki: mbarĩ ya nyũmba ya Daudi ĩrĩre ĩrĩ iiki na atumia ao marĩ oiki, mbarĩ ya nyũmba ya Nathani ĩrĩre ĩrĩ iiki, na atumia ao marĩ oiki,
english: Jesus said to them, “Come and eat breakfast!” None of the disciples dared inquire of him, “Who are you?” knowing that it was the Lord.

kikuyu: Ngĩgwa thĩ, na ngĩigua mũgambo mũnene ũkĩnjarĩria, ũkĩnjĩĩra atĩrĩ, ‘Saũlũ! Saũlũ! Ũũnyariiraga nĩkĩ?’
english: I fell to the ground and heard a voice saying to me, ‘Saul, Saul, why are you persecuting me?’

kikuyu: Nĩakaigĩrĩra moko make meerĩ igũrũ rĩa mũtwe wa thenge ĩyo ĩrĩ muoyo, na oimbũrĩre hau igũrũ rĩayo waganu wothe na ũremi wa andũ a Isiraeli, oimbũre mehia mao mothe, amacookererie igũrũ rĩa mũtwe wayo. Nĩakarekereria thenge ĩyo ĩĩthiĩre werũ-inĩ ĩmenyereirwo nĩ mũndũ ũrĩa ũthuurĩirwo wĩra ũcio.
english: Aaron shall lay both his hands on the head of the live goat, and confess over him all the iniquities of the children of Israel, and all their transgressions, even all their sins; and he shall put them on the head of the goat, and shall send him away into the wilderness by the hand of a man who is ready.

kikuyu: Wee Jehova, o Wee Ngai witũ, nĩ harĩ aathani angĩ manatwatha tiga wee, no rĩrĩ, rĩĩtwa rĩaku riiki norĩo tũtĩĩte. Na rĩrĩ, acio rĩu nĩmakuĩte, matigacooka gũtũũra muoyo; maroho macio matikariũka. Wee nĩwamaherithirie ũkĩmaananga; ningĩ ũgĩtũma mariganĩre biũ.
english: It will happen in that day that a great trumpet will be blown; and those who were ready to perish in the land of Assyria, and those who were outcasts in the land of Egypt, shall come; and they will worship Yahweh in the holy mountain at Jerusalem.

kikuyu: Nĩũũhurĩtie, ũkaandindĩka mbere ya rũhuho; ũũnyugutanĩtie kĩhuhũkanio-inĩ.
english: You lift me up to the wind, and drive me with it. You dissolve me in the storm.

kikuyu: na aarĩkia kũrĩa irio, akĩgĩa na hinya. Saũlũ arĩ Dameski na Jerusalemu Saũlũ agĩikara mĩthenya ĩigana ũna marĩ hamwe na arutwo kũu Dameski. Na hĩndĩ ĩyo akĩambĩrĩria kũhunjia thunagogi-inĩ atĩ Jesũ nĩ Mũrũ wa Ngai.
english: He took food and was strengthened. Saul stayed several days with the disciples who were at Damascus.

kikuyu: “Ngaamaikũrũkia ta tũgondu tũgĩthiĩ gũthĩnjwo, o na ta ndũrũme irĩ hamwe na thenge.
english: “I will bring them down like lambs to the slaughter, like rams with male goats.

kikuyu: Nĩngũtũma ũtũũre ũririkanagwo nĩ njiarwa na njiarwa; nĩ ũndũ ũcio ndũrĩrĩ igaatũũra igũkumagia tene na tene.
english: I will make your name to be remembered in all generations. Therefore the peoples shall give you thanks forever and ever.

kikuyu: Jehova ekuuga atĩrĩ “Tiga gwĩtigĩra, wee Jakubu ndungata yakwa, nĩgũkorwo ndĩ hamwe nawe. o na ingĩkaniinũkia ndũrĩrĩ ciothe kũrĩa ndarekire mũtwarwo mũrĩ atahe-rĩ, wee ndigakũniina biũ, no rĩrĩ, nĩngakũherithia na kĩhooto; ndigakũreka ũthiĩ o ũguo ũtaherithĩtio.”
english: Don’t be afraid, O Jacob my servant,” says Yahweh, “for I am with you; for I will make a full end of all the nations where I have driven you, but I will not make a full end of you, but I will correct you in measure, and will in no way leave you unpunished.”

kikuyu: Mĩbango ya ngoro ya mũndũ nĩ mĩingĩ, no muoroto wa Jehova noguo ũkaahinga.
english: There are many plans in a man’s heart, but Yahweh’s counsel will prevail.

kikuyu: Kĩhingũro kĩa nyũmba ya Daudi ngaakĩigĩrĩra kĩande-inĩ gĩake; na rĩrĩ, kĩrĩa gĩothe akaahingũra gũtirĩ mũndũ ũkaahota kũhinga, na kĩrĩa gĩothe akaahinga gũtirĩ mũndũ ũkaahota kũhingũra.
english: I will lay the key of David’s house on his shoulder. He will open, and no one will shut. He will shut, and no one will open.

kikuyu: Jehova nĩakaniina Babuloni; nĩagakiria inegene rĩu inene rĩakuo. Makũmbĩ ma thũ makaahũũyũka taarĩ mũrurumo wa maaĩ maingĩ. Mũrurumo wa mĩgambo yao nĩĩkaiguuagwo.
english: For Yahweh lays Babylon waste, and destroys out of her the great voice! Their waves roar like many waters. The noise of their voice is uttered.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa aiganĩire nĩ ũndũ wa kũingĩhia indo, mĩnyamaro nĩĩkamũnyiita; ũkĩa nĩũkamũkora na nditi nene.
english: In the fullness of his sufficiency, distress will overtake him. The hand of everyone who is in misery will come on him.

kikuyu: Mũndũ o wothe ũkaaruta wĩra mũthenya ũcio nĩngamũniina kuuma thĩinĩ wa andũ ao.
english: It shall be eaten on the same day; you shall leave none of it until the morning. I am Yahweh.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ya kwamũrwo kwa rũthingo rwa Jerusalemu, Alawii nĩmacaririo kuuma kũrĩa guothe maarĩ nĩguo moke Jerusalemu makũngũĩre kwamũrwo kũu marĩ na gĩkeno, na makĩinaga nyĩmbo cia gũcookia ngaatho, makĩinaga na thaani iria ihũũrithanagio, na inanda cia kĩnũbi, na cia mũgeeto.
english: At the dedication of the wall of Jerusalem, they sought the Levites out of all their places, to bring them to Jerusalem to keep the dedication with gladness, both with giving thanks and with singing, with cymbals, stringed instruments, and with harps.

kikuyu: Wee Jehova-rĩ, wathanaga nginya tene; gĩtĩ gĩaku kĩa ũnene gĩtũũraga kuuma njiarwa nginya njiarwa. * 5:16 Thũmbĩ yonanagia riiri na gĩtĩĩo ( , 3).
english: Our pursuers were swifter than the eagles of the sky. They chased us on the mountains. They set an ambush for us in the wilderness.

kikuyu: Othe nĩmagarũrũkĩte, makoora, othe hamwe magatuĩka kĩndũ gĩtarĩ bata; gũtirĩ mũndũ wĩkaga wega, gũtirĩ o na ũmwe.” * 2:25 Kũrua kwa arũme kwarĩ rũũri rwa kĩrĩkanĩro kĩrĩa Ngai aathondekire na andũ a Isiraeli.
english: They have all turned away. They have together become unprofitable. There is no one who does good, no, not so much as one.”*

kikuyu: “Atĩrĩrĩ, nĩngũmba igũrũ rĩerũ o na thĩ njerũ. Maũndũ marĩa ma tene matigacooka kũririkanwo, o na kana meciirio. No rĩrĩ, canjamũkai na mũkenere indo iria ngũmba tene na tene, nĩgũkorwo nĩngũmba itũũra rĩa Jerusalemu rĩtuĩke rĩa gũkenerwo, na andũ a rĩo matuĩke a gũkenania.
english: “For, behold, I create new heavens and a new earth; and the former things will not be remembered, nor come into mind.

kikuyu: angĩkorwo no njĩĩre ũhoro wa kũbutha atĩrĩ, ‘Wee nĩwe baba,’ na njĩĩre kĩgunyũ atĩrĩ, ‘Wee nĩwe maitũ’ kana ‘Nĩwe mwarĩ wa maitũ-rĩ’,
english: He breaks me with breach on breach. He runs at me like a giant.

kikuyu: Irio cionekete na njĩra ya kũheenania irĩ cama harĩ mũndũ, no thuutha kanua gake kaiyũraga o mũthanga.
english: Fraudulent food is sweet to a man, but afterwards his mouth is filled with gravel.

kikuyu: Ndũkareke arĩa maathũire tũhũ mangũũrĩre maitho, mangenerere; kana acio maamenete hatarĩ gĩtũmi moinanĩre maitho.
english: Don’t let those who are my enemies wrongfully rejoice over me; neither let those who hate me without a cause wink their eyes.

kikuyu: Akĩrora na igũrũ, akĩhũmũka na hinya, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Efatha!” (ũguo nĩ kuuga, “Hingũka!”).
english: Looking up to heaven, he sighed, and said to him, “Ephphatha!” that is, “Be opened!”

kikuyu: Andũ nĩ kũũra mooraga maigua mũrurumo wa mũgambo waku; warahũka-rĩ, ndũrĩrĩ nĩihurunjũkaga.
english: Their slain will also be cast out, and the stench of their dead bodies will come up. The mountains will melt in their blood.

kikuyu: Makiuga atĩrĩ, ‘Andũ arĩa marigirie kwandĩkwo-rĩ, marutire wĩra o ithaa rĩmwe, na wamaiganania na ithuĩ arĩa twatinda wĩra na mĩnoga na kũhĩa nĩ riũa.’
english: saying, ‘These last have spent one hour, and you have made them equal to us who have borne the burden of the day and the scorching heat!’

kikuyu: Inyuĩ mũkaanjaria, no mũtikanyona; na kũrĩa ngaakorwo, mũtikahota gũkinya.”
english: Jesus answered them, “Most certainly I tell you, everyone who commits sin is the bondservant of sin.

kikuyu: Kũrĩ mũndũ wĩtuaga gĩtonga, no ndarĩ kĩndũ; kũrĩ ũngĩ wĩtuaga mũthĩĩni, no arĩ na ũtonga mũnene.
english: There are some who pretend to be rich, yet have nothing. There are some who pretend to be poor, yet have great wealth.

kikuyu: na mwĩhumbe mũndũ ũrĩa mwerũ, ũrĩa wombirwo ahaanaine na Ngai, arĩ na ũthingu na ũtheru ũrĩa wa ma.
english: But as the assembly is subject to Christ, so let the wives also be to their own husbands in everything.

kikuyu: na Elitoladi, na Bethulu, na Horoma,
english: Eltolad, Bethul, Hormah,

kikuyu: Wee Jehova, mũndũ akĩrĩ kĩ nĩguo ũmũmenyagĩrĩre, nake mũrũ wa mũndũ akĩrĩ kĩ nĩgeetha ũmũrũmbũyagie?
english: Great is Yahweh, and greatly to be praised! His greatness is unsearchable.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo ndũmĩrĩri ya Jehova ĩgĩkinyĩra Aburamu, akĩĩrwo atĩrĩ, “Aca, Eliezeri tiwe ũgaakũgaya, no mũrũguo ũrĩa ũkoima mũthiimo waku nĩwe ũgaakũgaya.”
english: You adulterers and adulteresses, don’t you know that friendship with the world is hostility toward God? Whoever therefore wants to be a friend of the world makes himself an enemy of God.

kikuyu: Mwahota kũũria atĩrĩ, “Tũkaarĩa kĩ mwaka wa mũgwanja aakorwo tũtikahaanda kana tũgethe?”
english: If you said, “What shall we eat the seventh year? Behold, we shall not sow, nor gather in our increase;”

kikuyu: Inyuĩ mũkaagĩa nda cia maragara, mũciare rũũa rwa ngano; nayo mĩhũmũ yanyu ĩtuĩkĩte mwaki wa kũmũcina.
english: But the pelican and the porcupine will possess it. The owl and the raven will dwell in it. He will stretch the line of confusion over it, and the plumb line of emptiness.

kikuyu: Arata akwa nyendete mũno, maya nĩ marũa makwa ma keerĩ kũrĩ inyuĩ. Na thĩinĩ wamo meerĩ ndĩmaandĩkĩte marĩ ma kũmũririkania mwarahũke mũgĩe na meciiria marĩa magĩrĩru.
english: Peter, an apostle of Jesus Christ,† to the chosen ones who are living as foreigners in the Dispersion in Pontus, Galatia, Cappadocia, Asia, and Bithynia,

kikuyu: Mwathani, ririkana ũrĩa ndungata yaku ĩnyũrũrĩtio, na ũrĩa nyũmĩrĩirie irumi iria numĩtwo nĩ ndũrĩrĩ ciothe ngoro-inĩ yakwa,
english: Remember, Lord, the reproach of your servants, how I bear in my heart the taunts of all the mighty peoples,

kikuyu: “Mehia makwa mohanĩtio, magatuĩka icooki. Moko make mamogothanĩtie hamwe. Magagĩĩkĩrwo ngingo-inĩ yakwa, nake Mwathani agakĩĩniina hinya. Akaaneana moko-inĩ ma arĩa itangĩhota gwĩtiiria.
english: “The yoke of my transgressions is bound by his hand. They are knit together. They have come up on my neck. He made my strength fail. The Lord‡ has delivered me into their hands, against whom I am not able to stand.

kikuyu: Mũthenya wa gwaka thingo ciaku nĩũgakinya, o guo mũthenya wa kwaramia mĩhaka yaku.
english: A day to build your walls! In that day, he will extend your boundary.

kikuyu: Rutagai wĩra na kĩyo, na mũreke ngoro cianyu ihĩahĩage, mũgĩtungatagĩra Mwathani.
english: not lagging in diligence, fervent in spirit, serving the Lord,

kikuyu: Aathuurire Daudi ndungata yake na akĩmũruta ciugũ-inĩ cia ngʼondu;
english: He also chose David his servant, and took him from the sheepfolds;

kikuyu: Ũrĩa ũrĩ gũtũ-rĩ, nĩakĩigue ũrĩa Roho areera makanitha. Ũrĩa ũgaatoorania-rĩ, nĩngamũhe mana mamwe marĩa mahithe. O na ningĩ nĩngamũhe ihiga rĩerũ rĩandĩkĩtwo rĩĩtwa rĩerũ, na gũtarĩ ũngĩ ũrĩũĩ o tiga ũcio ũkaamũkĩra ihiga rĩu. Ndũmĩrĩri kũrĩ Kanitha wa Thuatira
english: Because you say, ‘I am rich, and have gotten riches, and have need of nothing,’ and don’t know that you are the wretched one, miserable, poor, blind, and naked;

kikuyu: Angĩkorwo acio nĩ anabii marĩ na ndũmĩrĩri kuuma kũrĩ Jehova-rĩ, nĩmagĩthaitheJehovaMwene-Hinya-WothenĩguoindoiriaitigarĩtegũkũthĩinĩwanyũmbayaJehova, o na thĩinĩ wa nyũmba ya mũthamaki wa Juda, o na kũu Jerusalemu itigatwarwo Babuloni. Nĩ ũndũ ũũ nĩguo Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe oigĩte ũhoro ũkoniĩ itugĩ cia hekarũ, na karia ka maaĩ, indo cia kũigĩrĩra iria ingĩ, na indo ciothe cia nyũmba ingĩkorwo itigaire gũkũ itũũra-inĩ rĩĩrĩ inene,
english: “Micah the Morashtite prophesied in the days of Hezekiah king of Judah; and he spoke to all the people of Judah, saying, ‘Yahweh of Armies says: “ ‘Zion will be plowed as a field, and Jerusalem will become heaps, and the mountain of the house as the high places of a forest.’

kikuyu: Gĩtarũrũ gĩake akĩnyiitĩte na guoko, na nĩagatheria kĩhuhĩro gĩake, na acookanĩrĩrie ngano yake ikũmbĩ, acooke acine mahuti na mwaki ũtangĩhoreka.” Kũbatithio kwa Jesũ
english: Being warned in a dream not to return to Herod, they went back to their own country another way.

kikuyu: Mĩtugo-inĩ yaku yothe ĩrĩ magigi ĩrĩa wekire, na ũhũũri-inĩ waku wa ũmaraya, ndwaririkanire matukũ ma ũnini waku, rĩrĩa warĩ njaga na ũtaarĩ na nguo, o rĩrĩa wegaragaragia thakame-inĩ yaku.
english: In all your abominations and your prostitution you have not remembered the days of your youth, when you were naked and bare, and were wallowing in your blood.

kikuyu: Kĩrĩa gĩothe gĩkaahutia nyama icio gĩgaatuĩka gĩtheru, na thakame o yothe ĩkaaminjũkĩra nguo no nginya ũkaamĩthambĩria handũ hatheru.
english: Whatever shall touch its flesh shall be holy. When there is any of its blood sprinkled on a garment, you shall wash that on which it was sprinkled in a holy place.

kikuyu: Mũriũ wake wa mũtumia ũrĩa warĩ ngombo aaciarĩtwo na njĩra ya ũndũire; no mũriũ ũrĩa waciarĩtwo nĩ mũtumia ũcio ũtaarĩ ngombo aaciarĩtwo na ũndũ wa kũhingia kĩĩranĩro.
english: However, the son by the servant was born according to the flesh, but the son by the free woman was born through promise.

kikuyu: nĩguo marũmagie mataaro make, na maathĩkagĩre mawatho make. Goocai Jehova.
english: that they might keep his statutes, and observe his laws. Praise Yah!

kikuyu: Ndarekaga njookererwo nĩ mĩhũmũ, no nĩahatagĩrĩria na mathĩĩna.
english: “ ‘Why, then, have you brought me out of the womb? I wish I had given up the spirit, and no eye had seen me.

kikuyu: Kũrĩ mũndũ ũheanaga na ũtaana, nake agakĩrĩrĩria kũgĩa na indo; ũngĩ atuĩkaga mũkarĩ mũno, no agathiĩ o na mbere gũthĩĩna.
english: There is one who scatters, and increases yet more. There is one who withholds more than is appropriate, but gains poverty.

kikuyu: Mũndũ angĩĩguna nakĩ angĩĩgwatĩra thĩ yothe, no orwo nĩ muoyo wake?
english: For what does it profit a man to gain the whole world and forfeit his life?

kikuyu: “Ũyũ nowe Musa ũrĩa maaregete makiuga atĩrĩ, ‘Nũũ ũgũtuĩte mwathi witũ na mũtũciirithia?’ Ngai we mwene nĩamũtũmire atuĩke mũnene na mũkũũri wao, na ũndũ wa mũraika ũrĩa wa- muumĩrĩire kĩhinga-inĩ.
english: “This Moses whom they refused, saying, ‘Who made you a ruler and a judge?’—God has sent him as both a ruler and a deliverer by the hand of the angel who appeared to him in the bush.

kikuyu: Mwahooya ũndũ o wothe thĩinĩ wa rĩĩtwa rĩakwa, nĩndĩrĩwĩkaga. Jesũ Kwĩranĩra Roho Mũtheru
english: You are my friends if you do whatever I command you.

kikuyu: Ndĩ na hinya wa gũkũgera ngero; no rĩrĩ, ũtukũ wa ira Ngai wa thoguo aranjĩĩrire atĩrĩ, ‘Menya wĩre Jakubu ũndũ, ũrĩ mwega kana ũrĩ mũũru.’
english: It is in the power of my hand to hurt you, but the God of your father spoke to me last night, saying, ‘Be careful that you don’t speak to Jacob either good or bad.’

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo akerubi acio, marĩ na magũrũ ma ngaari mĩena-inĩ, magĩtambũrũkia mathagu mao, naguo riiri wa Ngai wa Isiraeli warĩ igũrũ rĩao.
english: Then the cherubim lifted up their wings, and the wheels were beside them. The glory of the God of Israel was over them above.

kikuyu: “Mũtigeciirie atĩ njũkĩte kũrehe thayũ gũkũ thĩ. Ndiokire kũrehe thayũ, no ndookire kũrehe rũhiũ rwa njora.
english: “Don’t think that I came to send peace on the earth. I didn’t come to send peace, but a sword.

kikuyu: Jehova ekuuga atĩrĩ, “Nĩmwarĩtie maũndũ mooru ma kũnjambia. “No mũũragia atĩrĩ, ‘Tũgũcambĩtie atĩa?’
english: But when it came to my house, it began to cry, and I said to her, “Where did this kid come from? Is it stolen? Give it back to the owners; for it is not lawful to eat anything that is stolen.”

kikuyu: Ithuĩ tuumĩte kũrĩ Ngai, nake mũndũ o wothe ũrĩa ũũĩ Ngai nĩatũthikagĩrĩria; no ũrĩa wothe ũtoimĩte kũrĩ Ngai ndatũthikagĩrĩria. Ũguo nĩguo tũkũũranaga Roho ũrĩa wa ma, na roho ũrĩa wa maheeni. Wendani wa Ngai na Witũ
english: We are of God. He who knows God listens to us. He who is not of God doesn’t listen to us. By this we know the spirit of truth, and the spirit of error.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo ũgenyũ wa arĩa matarĩ ũũgĩ nĩũkamooraga, nakuo kwĩgangara kwa arĩa akĩĩgu nĩgũkamaniina;
english: For the backsliding of the simple will kill them. The careless ease of fools will destroy them.

kikuyu: Nĩngamũtambũrũkĩria wabu wakwa, nake agwatio nĩ mũtego wakwa. Nĩngamũrehe Babuloni ndĩmũciirithĩrie kuo, tondũ nĩagire kwĩhokeka harĩ niĩ.
english: I will spread my net on him, and he will be taken in my snare. I will bring him to Babylon, and will enter into judgment with him there for his trespass that he has trespassed against me.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo Isaia mũrũ wa Amozu agĩtũma ndũmĩrĩri kũrĩ Hezekia, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ: “Ũũ nĩguo Jehova Ngai wa Isiraeli ekuuga: Tondũ nĩũũhooete ũhoro wĩgiĩ Senakeribu mũthamaki wa Ashuri- rĩ,
english: Then Isaiah the son of Amoz sent to Hezekiah, saying, “Yahweh, the God of Israel says, ‘Because you have prayed to me against Sennacherib king of Assyria,

kikuyu: Agĩtũma marũa kũndũ guothe ũthamaki-inĩ ũcio, o bũrũri na mwandĩkĩre waguo na o rũrĩrĩ na mwarĩrie waruo, akĩanĩrĩrithia na thiomi cia andũ othe atĩ mũthuuri o wothe nĩwe mwathi wa mũciĩ wake.
english: for he sent letters into all the king’s provinces, into every province according to its writing, and to every people in their language, that every man should rule his own house, speaking in the language of his own people.

kikuyu: Thĩinĩ wake kwarĩ muoyo, naguo muoyo ũcio nĩguo warĩ ũtheri wa andũ.
english: Jesus said to her, “Woman, what does that have to do with you and me? My hour has not yet come.”

kikuyu: Thaani iria nene ikũmi na igĩrĩ cia thahabu ciyũrĩtio ũbumba ciarĩ na ũritũ wa cekeri ikũmi o ĩmwe, kũringana na gĩthimo gĩa cekeri ya harĩa haamũre. Thaani icio nene cia thahabu ciothe ciarĩ na ũritũ wa cekeri igana rĩa mĩrongo ĩĩrĩ.*
english: the twelve golden ladles, full of incense, weighing ten shekels apiece, according to the shekel of the sanctuary; all the gold of the ladles weighed one hundred twenty shekels;

kikuyu: Acio othe-rĩ, matiĩciiragia, no nĩ akĩĩgu; mataaragwo nĩ mĩhianano ĩtarĩ kĩene o ĩrĩa ĩthondeketwo na mĩtĩ.
english: For we can do nothing against the truth, but for the truth.

kikuyu: Nĩaririkanaga kĩrĩkanĩro gĩake nginya tene, nĩkĩo kiugo kĩrĩa aathanire, nĩ ũndũ wa njiarwa ngiri,
english: He has remembered his covenant forever, the word which he commanded to a thousand generations,

kikuyu: Nyamũ ĩyo ndonire yahaanaga ta ngarĩ, no magũrũ mayo maatariĩ ta ma nduba, nako kanua kayo kahaanaga ta ka mũrũũthi. Nayo ndamathia ĩyo ĩkĩhe nyamũ ĩyo hinya wayo na gĩtĩ kĩayo kĩa ũnene o na wathani mũnene.
english: The beast which I saw was like a leopard, and his feet were like those of a bear, and his mouth like the mouth of a lion. The dragon gave him his power, his throne, and great authority.

kikuyu: Niĩ ha ũhoro wa maũndũ makoniĩ ndini ya Ayahudi, nĩ ndaathiĩte mbere o na gũkĩra Ayahudi aingĩ a riika rĩakwa na ndaarĩ na kĩyo kĩnene gĩa kũrũmia mĩtugo ya maithe maitũ. No rĩrĩ, Ngai ũrĩa wanyamũrire kuuma ndaciarwo, na akĩnjĩta nĩ ũndũ wa wega wake-rĩ, nĩ oonire arĩ wega,
english: I advanced in the Jews’ religion beyond many of my own age among my countrymen, being more exceedingly zealous for the traditions of my fathers.

kikuyu: Ti-itherũ Mwathani Jehova ndarĩ ũndũ angĩĩka atamenyithĩtie anabii, ndungata ciake, ũrĩa atuĩte gwĩka.
english: “I also have withheld the rain from you, when there were yet three months to the harvest; and I caused it to rain on one city, and caused it not to rain on another city. One field was rained on, and the field where it didn’t rain withered.

kikuyu: Wee Jehova, ngoro yakwa ndĩmĩroretie na igũrũ kũrĩ we;
english: To you, Yahweh, I lift up my soul.

kikuyu: Kũrua kana kwaga kũrua gũtirĩ bata; kĩrĩa kĩrĩ bata nĩ mũndũ agarũrũke atuĩke kĩũmbe kĩerũ.
english: For in Christ Jesus neither is circumcision anything, nor uncircumcision, but a new creation.

kikuyu: Kuuma matukũ ma Johana Mũbatithania gwata rĩu, ũthamaki wa igũrũ nĩũkoretwo ũkĩĩhatĩrĩria gũtheerema, nao andũ arĩa mehatagĩrĩria nĩo mawĩgwatagĩra na hinya.
english: From the days of John the Baptizer until now, the Kingdom of Heaven suffers violence, and the violent take it by force.† -4, except with Judas Iscariot replaced by Matthias (

kikuyu: “Nĩmakoorĩrĩria njĩra ya gũthiĩ Zayuni, na magarũrũke, merekeirie mothiũ mao kuo. Nĩmagooka meeohanie na Jehova, na marĩkanĩre kĩrĩkanĩro gĩa gũtũũra tene na tene kĩrĩa gĩtagacooka kũriganĩra.
english: They will inquire concerning Zion with their faces turned toward it, saying, ‘Come, and join yourselves to Yahweh in an everlasting covenant that will not be forgotten.’

kikuyu: na okĩrĩre mũthiringo o wothe mũraihu na igũrũ na rũthingo o ruothe rwakĩtwo rwa hinya,
english: for every lofty tower, for every fortified wall,

kikuyu: Ngaakũrehera gĩcango handũ ha thahabu, na ngũrehere betha handũ ha kĩgera. Handũ ha mbaũ ngaakũrehera gĩcango, na ngũrehere kĩgera handũ ha mahiga. Nĩngatũma thayũ ũtuĩke mũnene waku, naguo ũthingu ũtuĩke mwathi waku.
english: For bronze I will bring gold; for iron I will bring silver; for wood, bronze, and for stones, iron. I will also make peace your governor, and righteousness your ruler.

kikuyu: “Mũhianano mũicũhie nĩ wa bata ũrĩkũ mũndũ aarĩkia kũwacũhia? O naguo mũhianano wa gũtwekio ũrĩa ũtũmaga andũ maheeneke nĩ wa bata ũrĩkũ? Nĩgũkorwo ũrĩa ũũthondekaga ehokaga kĩrĩa ombĩte; athondekaga mĩhianano ĩtaaragia.
english: “What value does the engraved image have, that its maker has engraved it; the molten image, even the teacher of lies, that he who fashions its form trusts in it, to make mute idols?

kikuyu: Mũthenya wa kenda, Abidani mũrũ wa Gideoni, mũtongoria wa andũ a Benjamini, nake akĩrehe maruta make.
english: On the ninth day Abidan the son of Gideoni, prince of the children of Benjamin,

kikuyu: o acio matigaga kũrũmĩrĩra Jehova magacooka na thuutha, na maticaragia Jehova, o na kana makamũrongooria.
english: those who have turned back from following Yahweh, and those who haven’t sought Yahweh nor inquired after him.

kikuyu: “Gĩai na hinya mũno; mũrũmie na mwathĩkagĩre watho wothe ũrĩa wandĩkĩtwo Ibuku-inĩ rĩa watho wa Musa, mũtekũgarũrũka na mwena wa ũrĩo kana wa ũmotho.
english: “Therefore be very courageous to keep and to do all that is written in the book of the law of Moses, that you not turn away from it to the right hand or to the left;

kikuyu: Jehova nĩagacookeria Jakubu riiri wake, ũhaane ta riiri wa Isiraeli, o na aakorwo aanangi nĩmamonũhĩte, na makaananga mĩthabibũ yao.
english: For Yahweh restores the excellency of Jacob as the excellency of Israel, for the destroyers have destroyed them and ruined their vine branches.

kikuyu: Mũcaayo wa arĩa marakua nĩũraiguuo ũkiuma itũũra inene, nayo mĩoyo ya arĩa agurarie no gũkaya ĩrakaya ĩkĩenda ũteithio. No Ngai gũtirĩ mũndũ aratuĩra ciira nĩ ũndũ wa gwĩka ũũru.
english: From out of the populous city, men groan. The soul of the wounded cries out, yet God doesn’t regard the folly.

kikuyu: “Maturubarĩ nĩmagwĩte thĩ, no ithuĩ tũgwaka rĩngĩ na mahiga maicũhie; mĩtĩ ya mĩkũyũ nĩmĩteme, no ithenya rĩayo tũgwaka na mĩtarakwa.” No rĩrĩ, Jehova nĩaheete andũ arĩa marĩ muku na Rezini hinya mamookĩrĩre, na akaarahũra thũ ciao.†
english: But may the God of all grace, who called you to his eternal glory by Christ Jesus, after you have suffered a little while, perfect, establish, strengthen, and settle you.

kikuyu: “Wee thiĩ na mbere na ciama ciaku cia ũgo, na ũrogi waku mũingĩ ũrĩa ũtũire wĩkaga kuuma ũrĩ mũnini. No gũkorwo hihi nĩũkona kĩguni kĩa mo, kana hihi ũtũme kũgĩe na itua-nda inene.
english: “Stand now with your enchantments and with the multitude of your sorceries, in which you have labored from your youth, as if you might profit, as if you might prevail.

kikuyu: “Nĩngũtua Jerusalemu ta gĩkombe kĩa ndibei gĩa gũtũma arĩa othe magũthiũrũrũkĩirie matũgũũge. Juda o ũndũ ũmwe na Jerusalemu nĩgũkarigiicĩrio nĩ thũ.
english: “Behold, I will make Jerusalem a cup of reeling to all the surrounding peoples, and it will also be on Judah in the siege against Jerusalem.

kikuyu: Kaanani nĩwe warĩ ithe wa Sidoni irigithathi rĩake, na Ahiti, 16na Ajebusi, na Aamori, na Agirigashi, 17na Ahivi, na Ariki, na Asiniti,
english: Canaan became the father of Sidon (his firstborn), Heth,

kikuyu: Mũndũ o wothe angĩkũhooya kĩndũ mũhe, na mũndũ o wothe angĩoya kĩndũ gĩaku, ndũkamwĩtie.
english: Give to everyone who asks you, and don’t ask him who takes away your goods to give them back again.

kikuyu: Akĩrĩra mĩthenya ĩyo yothe mũgwanja ya iruga rĩu, na mũthenya wa mũgwanja Samusoni * 14:1 Timina rĩarĩ itũũra thĩinĩ wa Dani ( ); Afilisti nĩo maikaraga kuo gũtanatunyanwo nĩ andũ a Isiraeli. † 14:5 Ihinda rĩĩrĩ nĩ gwakoragwo na mĩrũũthi mĩingĩ mwena wa gũthini wa Palestina.
english: She wept before him the seven days, while their feast lasted; and on the seventh day, he told her, because she pressed him severely; and she told the riddle to the children of her people.

kikuyu: Gĩkeno na gũcanjamũka nĩithirĩte kũu mĩgũnda-inĩ ya matunda, o na kũu mĩgũnda-inĩ ya Moabi. Nĩndũmĩte ndibei yage ihihĩro-inĩ ciayo; gũtirĩ mũndũ ũhihaga thabibũ kuo arĩ na mbugĩrĩrio ya gĩkeno. O na gũtuĩka nĩ kũrĩ na mbugĩrĩrio, ti mbugĩrĩrio cia gĩkeno.
english: Gladness and joy is taken away from the fruitful field and from the land of Moab. I have caused wine to cease from the wine presses. No one will tread with shouting. The shouting will be no shouting.

kikuyu: “ ‘ “Jehova arokũrathima, * 6:2 Mũndũ nĩangĩehĩtire mwĩhĩtwa wa gũtuĩka Mũnaziri kwa ihinda, kana mũtũũrĩre wake wothe. Aciari nĩmangĩehĩtire mwĩhĩtwa handũ ha ciana ciao. Mũnaziri ndetĩkĩrĩtio kũnyua kĩndũ kĩgagatu, kwenja njuĩrĩ kana nderu, o na kana kũhutia kĩndũ gĩkuũ.
english: He shall make the woman drink the water of bitterness that causes the curse; and the water that causes the curse shall enter into her and become bitter.

kikuyu: Jehova ekuuga atĩrĩ, “Mũthenya ũcio-rĩ, nĩngacookia ũhoro; nĩngacookeria matu ũhoro, namo matu macookerie thĩ ũhoro;
english: She will rest upon him as a mighty stone of trial. He won’t hesitate to cast her from him.

kikuyu: Mũthenya ũcio-rĩ, nĩngaherithia ataagarari othe a hingĩro, o acio maiyũragia hekarũ ya ngai ciao na haaro na maheeni.”
english: In that day, I will punish all those who leap over the threshold, who fill their master’s house with violence and deceit.

kikuyu: Anania na Safira Na rĩrĩ, mũndũ wetagwo Anania, marĩ hamwe na mũtumia wake Safira o nao nĩmendirie gĩcunjĩ gĩa gĩthaka kĩao.
english: Ananias, hearing these words, fell down and died. Great fear came on all who heard these things.

kikuyu: (Nĩmonete Tirofimo ũrĩa Mũefeso marĩ na Paũlũ kũu itũũra-inĩ, no magĩĩciiria atĩ Paũlũ nĩamũtoonyetie hekarũ-inĩ.)
english: For they had seen Trophimus the Ephesian with him in the city, and they supposed that Paul had brought him into the temple.

kikuyu: Wee-rĩ, ndũnaamumunya, no mũndũ-wa-nja ũyũ, kuuma rĩrĩa ndonyire ndarĩ aratigithĩria kũmumunya nyarĩrĩ ciakwa.
english: You gave me no kiss, but she, since the time I came in, has not ceased to kiss my feet.

kikuyu: mũndũ ũcio no arĩkoragwo arĩ na kĩhooto gĩa gũkũũrwo thuutha wa kwĩyendia. Mũndũ wa nyũmba yao no amũkũũre:
english: after he is sold he may be redeemed. One of his brothers may redeem him;

kikuyu: Icio nĩcio ciarĩ mbarĩ cia Simeoni; nao maarĩ andũ 22,200.
english: These are the families of the Simeonites, twenty-two thousand two hundred.

kikuyu: Githĩ tũnyoni twĩrĩ tũtiendagio thendi ĩmwe? No gũtirĩ o na kamwe gatuo kangĩgũa thĩ Ithe wanyu atetĩkĩrĩte.
english: “Aren’t two sparrows sold for an assarion coin?‡ Not one of them falls to the ground apart from your Father’s will.

kikuyu: “Ndakwambatirie ngĩkũruta bũrũri wa Misiri, na ngĩgũtongoria mĩaka mĩrongo ĩna kũu werũ-inĩ, nĩguo ngũhe bũrũri wa Aamori. Ningĩ nĩndarahũrire anabii kuuma kũrĩ ariũ aku, na ngĩarahũra Anaziri kuuma kũrĩ aanake aku. Githĩ ũcio ti ũhoro wa ma, inyuĩ andũ a Isiraeli?” ũguo nĩguo Jehova ekuuga.
english: Also I brought you up out of the land of Egypt and led you forty years in the wilderness, to possess the land of the Amorite.

kikuyu: “Ĩra andũ a Isiraeli makũngũyagĩre Gĩathĩ-kĩa-Bathaka ihinda rĩrĩa rĩtuĩtwo.
english: “Let the children of Israel keep the Passover in its appointed season.

kikuyu: Nĩakaruta maguta mothe o ta ũrĩa maguta marutagwo kuuma iruta rĩa ũiguano, nake mũthĩnjĩri- Ngai nĩakamacinĩra kĩgongona-inĩ igũrũ matuĩke mũtararĩko mwega wa gũkenia Jehova. Ũguo nĩguo mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai akaahoroheria mũndũ ũcio, nake nĩakarekerwo.
english: All its fat he shall take away, like the fat is taken away from the sacrifice of peace offerings; and the priest shall burn it on the altar for a pleasant aroma to Yahweh; and the priest shall make atonement for him, and he will be forgiven.

kikuyu: Ngoro yakwa ĩroaga ũcuuke ũhoro-inĩ wa kĩrĩra kĩa watho waku, nĩguo ndikanaconorithio. Ihooya rĩa kũũria Ũhonokio
english: Let my heart be blameless toward your decrees, that I may not be disappointed. KAPF

kikuyu: Makaahandaga ngano, makagetha mĩigua; makeenogia na matione kĩndũ. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio-rĩ, conokerai magetha manyu, tondũ wa marakara mahiũ ma Jehova.”
english: 1 Yahweh said to me, “Go, and buy yourself a linen belt, and put it on your waist, and don’t put it in water.”

kikuyu: Wee Ngai, mĩĩhĩtwa ĩrĩa ndĩĩhĩtĩte ndĩ o nayo, nĩndĩrĩkũrutagĩra maruta ma gũgũcookeria ngaatho.
english: Your vows are on me, God. I will give thank offerings to you.

kikuyu: Isaaka agĩthaitha Jehova nĩ ũndũ wa mũtumia wake tondũ aarĩ thaata. Nake Jehova akĩigua mahooya make, na Rebeka mũtumia wake akĩgĩa nda.
english: Isaac entreated Yahweh for his wife, because she was barren. Yahweh was entreated by him, and Rebekah his wife conceived.

kikuyu: Tũrũndĩtwo rũkũngũ-inĩ; mĩĩrĩ iitũ ĩkĩnyiitanĩte na thĩ.
english: For our soul is bowed down to the dust. Our body clings to the earth.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo mũngĩtũũra kũringana na ũrĩa mwĩrĩ ũyũ wĩhagia wendaga-rĩ, nĩmũgakua, no mũngĩũraga ciĩko icio njũru cia mwĩrĩ na hinya wa Roho-rĩ, nĩmũgatũũra muoyo,
english: Did then that which is good become death to me? May it never be! But sin, that it might be shown to be sin, was producing death in me through that which is good; that through the commandment sin might become exceedingly sinful.

kikuyu: Pilato akĩmooria atĩrĩ, “Nĩ mũkwenda ndĩmuohorere mũthamaki wa Ayahudi?”
english: Pilate answered them, saying, “Do you want me to release to you the King of the Jews?”

kikuyu: Maũndũ macio aamaaririe rĩrĩa aarutanaga thunagogi-inĩ kũu Kaperinaumu. Arutwo Aingĩ Gũtiganĩria Jesũ
english: He said these things in the synagogue, as he taught in Capernaum.

kikuyu: “Ĩra andũ a Isiraeli atĩrĩ, ‘Mũndũ angĩkua na ndatige mwana wa kahĩĩ, mwarĩ nĩaheo igai rĩake.
english: You shall speak to the children of Israel, saying, ‘If a man dies, and has no son, then you shall cause his inheritance to pass to his daughter.

kikuyu: gĩĩke atĩrĩ, mũrũ wakwa, nĩguo wĩteithũre, kuona atĩ nĩũgwĩte moko-inĩ ma mũndũ wa itũũra rĩaku: Thiĩ wĩnyiihie wee mwene; mũthaithe mũno akuohere.
english: Do this now, my son, and deliver yourself, since you have come into the hand of your neighbor. Go, humble yourself. Press your plea with your neighbor.

kikuyu: Nĩngagũtongoria na nĩngaragania irĩma; nĩngoinanga ihingo cia gĩcango, na ndinangie mĩgĩĩko ya kĩgera.
english: “I will go before you and make the rough places smooth. I will break the doors of bronze in pieces and cut apart the bars of iron.

kikuyu: Ariũ ao nĩmatoonyire, makĩĩgwatĩra bũrũri ũcio. Ũgĩtooria andũ a Kaanani arĩa maatũũraga bũrũri ũcio mbere yao; ũkĩneana andũ a Kaanani kũrĩ o, hamwe na athamaki ao na andũ a bũrũri ũcio, ũkĩmaneana moko-inĩ mao meke nao o ũrĩa mangĩendire.
english: “So the children went in and possessed the land; and you subdued before them the inhabitants of the land, the Canaanites, and gave them into their hands, with their kings and the peoples of the land, that they might do with them as they pleased.

kikuyu: Andũ arĩa othe mehagia matarĩ watho-inĩ makaaniinwo matarĩ watho-inĩ, nao arĩa othe mehagia marĩ rungu rwa watho magaatuĩrwo ciira kũringana na watho ũcio.
english: For as many as have sinned without the law will also perish without the law. As many as have sinned under the law will be judged by the law.

kikuyu: Rĩu- rĩ, mũngĩĩhaarĩria nĩgeetha rĩrĩa mũrĩigua mũgambo wa coro, na mĩtũrirũ, na thethe, na kĩnanda gĩa kĩnũbi, na kĩnanda kĩa mũgeeto, na mĩtũrirũ ĩrĩa mĩariĩ, na ũini wa mĩthemba yothe, mwĩgũithie thĩ, mũhooe mũhianano ũcio niĩ thondekithĩtie, nĩ wega mũno. No rĩrĩ, mũngĩrega kũhooya mũhianano * 3:1 nĩ ta mita
english: Now if you are ready whenever you hear the sound of the horn, flute, zither, lyre, harp, pipe, and all kinds of music to fall down and worship the image which I have made, good; but if you don’t worship, you shall be cast the same hour into the middle of a burning fiery furnace.  A cubit is the length from the tip of the middle finger to the elbow on a man’s arm, or about

kikuyu: Tondũ nĩmũũĩ wega na ma atĩ: gũtirĩ mũhũũri-maraya, kana mũndũ mwaganu, o na kana mũndũ mũkoroku; mũndũ ũrĩa wĩkaga ũguo nĩ mũhooi mĩhianano, andũ ta acio gũtirĩ ũrĩ na igai thĩinĩ wa ũthamaki wa Kristũ na wa Ngai.
english: Know this for sure, that no sexually immoral person, nor unclean person, nor covetous man (who is an idolater), has any inheritance in the Kingdom of Christ and God.

kikuyu: Nĩngwenda mũmenye atĩ ndireeka ũndũ ũyũ nĩ ũndũ wanyu, ũguo nĩguo Mwathani Jehova ekuuga. Iguai thoni, na mũnyararithio nĩ ũndũ wa mĩtugo yanyu, inyuĩ andũ a nyũmba ya Isiraeli!
english: I don’t do this for your sake,” says the Lord Yahweh. “Let it be known to you. Be ashamed and confounded for your ways, house of Israel.”

kikuyu: Tondũ-rĩ, Mũyahudi ti ũrĩa wonanagia atĩ nĩ Mũyahudi na igũrũ, nakuo kũrua to kũrĩa gwa kuoneka mwĩrĩ-inĩ.
english: For he is not a Jew who is one outwardly, neither is that circumcision which is outward in the flesh;

kikuyu: Mũthamaki wa Babuloni nĩaiguĩte ũhoro wao, namo moko make makoorwo nĩ hinya. Nĩanyiitĩtwo nĩ ruo rũnene ngoro-inĩ yake, na ruo ta rwa mũndũ-wa-nja akĩrũmwo.
english: The king of Babylon has heard the news of them, and his hands become feeble. Anguish has taken hold of him, pains as of a woman in labor.

kikuyu: Nĩndehingĩte kũrĩa irio njega; kanua gakwa gatiacamire nyama kana ndibei; ningĩ ndiehakaga maguta o na hanini, o nginya ciumia icio ithatũ igĩthira.
english: A mighty king will stand up, who will rule with great dominion, and do according to his will.

kikuyu: Mũnene ũcio wa arangĩri nĩanyiitire Seraia mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai ũrĩa mũnene, na Zefania mũthĩnjĩri- Ngai ũrĩa wa keerĩ harĩ we, o na andũ arĩa atatũ maarangagĩra mũrango.
english: The captain of the guard took Seraiah the chief priest, and Zephaniah the second priest, and the three keepers of the threshold,

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo kuuma rĩrĩa thĩ yombirwo, maũndũ ma Ngai marĩa matonekaga, na nĩmo hinya wake wa tene na tene, o na ũngai wake, nĩmarĩkĩtie kuoneka o wega, na makamenyeka nĩ ũndũ wa indo iria ciombirwo, nĩgeetha andũ matikagĩe na kĩĩgwatio.
english: a corrector of the foolish, a teacher of babies, having in the law the form of knowledge and of the truth.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo andũ amwe arĩa ũhoro wao wagũtuĩrwo ciira waandĩkĩtwo o tene nĩmetoonyereirie na hitho thĩinĩ wanyu. Acio nĩ andũ matetigagĩra Ngai, na nĩmagarũraga Wega wa Ngai witũ ũtuĩke wa kũmetĩkĩria kũhũũra ũmaraya, na magakaanaga Jesũ Kristũ, o we wiki Mũnene na Mwathani witũ.
english: For there are certain men who crept in secretly, even those who were long ago written about for this condemnation: ungodly men, turning the grace of our God into indecency, and denying our only Master, God, and Lord, Jesus Christ.

kikuyu: Njiaro ciao nĩikaagĩa igweta ndũrĩrĩ-inĩ, na ciana ciao imenywo nĩ andũ a ndũrĩrĩ. Arĩa othe makaamoona nĩmagetĩkĩra atĩ acio nĩo andũ arĩa Jehova arathimĩte.”
english: Their offspring will be known among the nations, and their offspring among the peoples. All who see them will acknowledge them, that they are the offspring which Yahweh has blessed.”

kikuyu: 1 Rĩrĩa waikara thĩ kũrĩĩanĩra na mwathi wa bũrũri-rĩ,
english: 1 When you sit to eat with a ruler, consider diligently what is before you;

kikuyu: Nĩakamateithũra kuuma kũrĩ arĩa mamahinyagĩrĩria na kuuma haaro-inĩ, nĩgũkorwo thakame yao ĩrĩ bata mũno maitho-inĩ make.
english: He will redeem their soul from oppression and violence. Their blood will be precious in his sight.

kikuyu: Mũhaka ũcio ũgĩcooka ũkĩrigiicũka werekeire Rama, ũgĩthiĩ nginya itũũra inene rĩrĩa rĩirige na hinya rĩa Turo, ũgĩcooka ũkĩrigiicũka ũgĩkinya Hosa, na ũkiumĩrĩra iria rĩrĩa rĩrĩ gũkuhĩ na Akizibu,
english: The border turned to Ramah, to the fortified city of Tyre; and the border turned to Hosah. It ended at the sea by the region of Achzib;

kikuyu: NakeJesũagĩũkĩra, agĩkaaniarũhuhonaakĩĩramakũmbĩmaciomairiaatĩrĩ, “Kira! Naũhoorere!” Naruo rũhuho rũgĩtiga kũhurutana, gũkĩhoorera biũ.
english: He awoke and rebuked the wind, and said to the sea, “Peace! Be still!” The wind ceased and there was a great calm.

kikuyu: nake ũmwe wanyu amwĩre atĩrĩ, “Thiĩ na wega; gĩa na ũrugarĩ na ũrĩe wega,” no ndarĩ ũndũ aamũteithia naguo mabataro-inĩ make ma mwĩrĩ- rĩ, ũndũ ũcio ũkĩrĩ na uumithio ũrĩkũ?
english: and one of you tells them, “Go in peace. Be warmed and filled;” yet you didn’t give them the things the body needs, what good is it?

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo itũũra rĩothe rĩkiumagara rĩgatũnge Jesũ. Na hĩndĩ ĩrĩa maamuonire, makĩmũthaitha oime rũgongo rwao.
english: Behold, all the city came out to meet Jesus. When they saw him, they begged that he would depart from their borders.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo Jehova agĩtambũrũkia guoko, akĩĩhutia kanua, akĩnjĩĩra atĩrĩ, “Rĩu nĩndekĩra ndũmĩrĩri yakwa kanua gaku.
english: Then Yahweh stretched out his hand and touched my mouth. Then Yahweh said to me, “Behold, I have put my words in your mouth.

kikuyu: Hingũrĩra mĩthemba yothe ya ciũmbe iria irĩ muoyo icio ũrĩ nacio: irĩ nyoni, na nyamũ, na ciũmbe iria ithiiagĩra thĩ, nĩgeetha ciongerereke thĩ, na iciarane cingĩhe gũkũ thĩ.” Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Nuhu akiuma thabina hamwe na ariũ ake, na mũtumia wake, na atumia a ariũ ake.
english: God said to Noah, “This is the token of the covenant which I have established between me and all flesh that is on the earth.”

kikuyu: o we ũkũhũũnagia na maũndũ mega nĩgeetha waanake waku werũhagio o ta wa nderi.*
english: He laid the foundations of the earth, that it should not be moved forever.  or, angels

kikuyu: Nĩaguũrĩirio nĩ Roho Mũtheru atĩ ndagakua atoneete Kristũ ũrĩa wa Mwathani. Nake agĩtoonya thĩinĩ wa hekarũ atongoretio nĩ Roho. Na rĩrĩa aciari a kaana kau ti Jesũ, maagatwarire hekarũ thĩinĩ nĩguo mahingie ũrĩa mũtugo wa watho watuĩte-rĩ,
english: Now in the sixth month, the angel Gabriel was sent from God to a city of Galilee named Nazareth,

kikuyu: Mũira mwĩhokeku nĩahonokagia mĩoyo, no mũira wa maheeni nĩ mũheenania.
english: A truthful witness saves souls, but a false witness is deceitful.

kikuyu: We Ũhoro aarĩ gũkũ thĩ, no o na gũtuĩka thĩ yombirwo na ũndũ wake-rĩ, andũ a thĩ matiigana kũmũmenya.
english: He was in the world, and the world was made through him, and the world didn’t recognize him.

kikuyu: ũrĩa waguucĩrĩirie ngaari cia ita o na mbarathi, na mbũtũ cia ita, hamwe na anene a cio, nao magĩkoma hau na maticooke kwarahũka rĩngĩ, makĩhora biũ ta rũtambĩ rũhoretio.
english: who brings out the chariot and horse, the army and the mighty man (they lie down together, they shall not rise; they are extinct, they are quenched like a wick) says:

kikuyu: Mĩaka ĩngĩkorwo ĩtigarĩte mĩingĩ, mũkongerera thogora, na mĩaka ĩngĩkorwo ĩtigarĩte mĩnini, mũkanyiihia thogora, nĩ ũndũ kĩrĩa arakwenderia nĩ mũigana wa magetha.
english: According to the length of the years you shall increase its price, and according to the shortness of the years you shall diminish its price; for he is selling the number of the crops to you.

kikuyu: Ndakanethaahie nĩ ũndũ wa athoni ake, na nĩ ũndũ ũcio anyiitwo nĩ thaahu.
english: He shall not defile himself, being a chief man among his people, to profane himself.

kikuyu: Kũrathimwo-rĩ, nĩ arĩa ahooreri, nĩgũkorwo nĩmakagaya thĩ.
english: Blessed are the gentle, for they shall inherit the earth.†*

kikuyu: Nĩaguũragia maũndũ marĩa mariku ma nduma, na nduma nene ya gĩkuũ akamiumĩria ũtheri-inĩ.
english: Then call, and I will answer, or let me speak, and you answer me.

kikuyu: Ngĩĩaga na ũtaũku nĩ ũndũ wa kĩrĩra gĩaku; nĩ ũndũ ũcio nĩthũire njĩra o yothe njũru. Ũtheri ũrĩa Uumanaga na Watho wa Jehova
english: Through your precepts, I get understanding; therefore I hate every false way. NUN

kikuyu: Mũthamaki ũcio ndangĩtunya andũ acio magai mao na njĩra ya kũmaingata mehere indo-inĩ ciao. Nĩakahe ariũ ake magai mao kuuma indo-inĩ ciake mwene, nĩguo gũtikanagĩe mũndũ o na ũrĩkũ gatagatĩ-inĩ ka andũ akwa ũkeeherio indo-inĩ ciake.’ ”
english: “ ‘The Lord Yahweh says: “In the first month, on the first day of the month, you shall take a young bull without defect, and you shall cleanse the sanctuary.

kikuyu: Gũtirĩ ciama tũringagĩrwo mahinda maya; anabii nĩmathirĩte, na gũtirĩ witũ ũũĩ nĩ nginya rĩ gũgũikara ũguo.
english: But I will declare this forever: I will sing praises to the God of Jacob.

kikuyu: Tuĩriai bũrũri ũcio ũrĩa ũtariĩ, muone kana andũ akuo marĩ na hinya kana matirĩ hinya, o na kana nĩ aingĩ kana nĩ anini. Ningĩ mũmenye bũrũri ũcio matũũraga ũhaana atĩa? Nĩ mwega kana nĩ mũũru? Matũũraga matũũra ma mũthemba ũrĩkũ? Nĩ mairigĩre na thingo cia hinya kana ti mairigĩre?
english: ‘Yahweh is slow to anger, and abundant in loving kindness, forgiving iniquity and disobedience; and he will by no means clear the guilty, visiting the iniquity of the fathers on the children, on the third and on the fourth generation.’

kikuyu: Thuutha wa Rakeli gũciara Jusufu, Jakubu akĩĩra Labani atĩrĩ, “Ndekereria ndĩthiĩre, nĩguo njooke bũrũri witũ.
english: Laban caught up with Jacob. Now Jacob had pitched his tent in the  teraphim were household idols that may have been associated with inheritance rights to the household property.

kikuyu: Ũrwoi nĩ hinya, inyuĩ arĩmi, na mũgirĩke, inyuĩ arĩa mũkũragia mĩthabibũ; mwĩthikĩrei nĩ ũndũ wa ngano na cairi, tondũ magetha ma mĩgũnda nĩmanangĩtwo.
english: Be confounded, you farmers! Wail, you vineyard keepers, for the wheat and for the barley; for the harvest of the field has perished.

kikuyu: Arata akwa, mũtikanerĩhĩrie, no tigagĩriai mangʼũrĩ ma Ngai handũ, nĩgũkorwo nĩ kwandĩkĩtwo atĩrĩ: “Kũrĩhanĩria nĩ gwakwa; nĩ niĩ ngaarĩhanĩria,” ũguo nĩguo Mwathani ekuuga.
english: Don’t seek revenge yourselves, beloved, but give place to God’s wrath. For it is written, “Vengeance belongs to me; I will repay, says the Lord.”*

kikuyu: Andũ a Misiri nĩmakenire moima kuo, tondũ nĩmanyiitĩtwo nĩ guoya wa andũ acio a Isiraeli.
english: Egypt was glad when they departed, for the fear of them had fallen on them.

kikuyu: Magĩcooka magĩikara thĩ kũrĩa irio ciao, magĩtiira maitho makĩona gĩkundi kĩnene kĩa Aishu- maeli gĩgĩũka kiumĩte Gileadi. Ngamĩĩra ciao ciakuuithĩtio mahuti manungi wega, na ũbani, na ũũkĩ- wa-ngoma ũrĩa wĩtagwo manemane, na maikũrũkĩte mathiĩ matware indo icio Misiri.
english: They sat down to eat bread, and they lifted up their eyes and looked, and saw a caravan of Ishmaelites coming from Gilead, with their camels bearing spices and balm and myrrh, going to carry it down to Egypt.

kikuyu: Moima Iria Itune, magĩthiĩ makĩamba hema ciao werũ-inĩ wa Sini.
english: They traveled from the Red Sea, and encamped in the wilderness of Sin.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo Ngai akĩmũhingũra maitho, nake akĩona gĩthima kĩa maaĩ. Agĩthiĩ ho, akĩiyũria rũũa maaĩ, na akĩhe kĩmwana kĩu kĩnyue.
english: So Abraham returned to his young men, and they rose up and went together to Beersheba. Abraham lived at Beersheba.

kikuyu: “Mũrũ wa mũndũ, atĩrĩ, ndĩ hakuhĩ gũkwehereria kĩrĩa gĩkenagia maitho maku mũno na igũtha o rĩmwe. No rĩrĩ, ndũgacakae kana ũrĩre, o na kana ũite maithori.
english: therefore the Lord Yahweh says, “Behold, I will stretch out my hand on the Philistines, and I will cut off the Cherethites, and destroy the remnant of the sea coast.

kikuyu: Mũthenya wa mũthia, na ũrĩa warĩ mũnene wa Gĩathĩ kĩu-rĩ, Jesũ akĩrũgama, akĩanĩrĩra na mũgambo mũnene, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Mũndũ ũrĩa ũnyootiĩ-rĩ, nĩoke harĩ niĩ anyue.
english: I know that you are Abraham’s offspring, yet you seek to kill me, because my word finds no place in you.

kikuyu: “Jehova ekuuga atĩrĩ: Rũgama nja ya nyũmba ya Jehova warĩrie andũ othe a matũũra ma Juda, acio mokaga kũhoera thĩinĩ wa nyũmba ĩno ya Jehova. Mahe ũhoro ũrĩa wothe ngũgwatha; ndũgatige o na kiugo kĩmwe.
english: “Yahweh says: ‘Stand in the court of Yahweh’s house, and speak to all the cities of Judah which come to worship in Yahweh’s house, all the words that I command you to speak to them. Don’t omit a word.

kikuyu: Maũndũ ma ũhinya nĩmakũrĩte magatuĩka rũthanju rwa kũherithia waganu; gũtirĩ andũ magaatigara, gũtirĩ o na ũmwe wa kĩrĩndĩ kĩu; gũtirĩ ũtonga, na gũtirĩ kĩndũ kĩa bata gĩgaatigara.
english: Seventy men of the elders of the house of Israel stood before them. In the middle of them Jaazaniah the son of Shaphan stood, every man with his censer in his hand; and the smell of the cloud of incense went up.

kikuyu: Jehova ekuuga atĩrĩ: Mwĩmenyererei mũtikanakuue mũrigo mũthenya wa Thabatũ, kana mũũtoonyerie ihingo-inĩ cia Jerusalemu.
english: Yahweh says, “Be careful, and bear no burden on the Sabbath day, nor bring it in by the gates of Jerusalem.

kikuyu: Ihooya rĩakwa rĩrokinya harĩwe; inamĩrĩria gũtũ gwaku ũigue gũkaya gwakwa.
english: I indeed declare, “Love stands firm forever. You established the heavens. Your faithfulness is in them.”  Rahab is a reference to Egypt.  Sheol is the place of the dead.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo Jehova nĩakahuuhĩra ngi mĩrũri, aciĩte kuuma tũrũũĩ-inĩ twa kũraya twa bũrũri wa Misiri, na eete njũkĩ iria irĩ bũrũri wa Ashuri.
english: It will happen in that day that Yahweh will whistle for the fly that is in the uttermost part of the rivers of Egypt, and for the bee that is in the land of Assyria.

kikuyu: Hatiarĩ o na ũmwe wa arĩa matarĩtwo nĩ Musa na Harũni ũrĩa mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai, hĩndĩ ĩrĩa maatarĩire andũ a Isiraeli werũ- inĩ wa Sinai. Nĩ ũndũ Jehova nĩeerĩte andũ a Isiraeli atĩ ti-itherũ no nginya makuĩre werũ-inĩ, na gũtirĩ o na ũmwe wao watigarire, tiga no Kalebu mũrũ wa Jefune, na Joshua mũrũ wa Nuni.
english: But among these there was not a man of them who were counted by Moses and Aaron the priest, who counted the children of Israel in the wilderness of Sinai.

kikuyu: Ũtukũ ũcio Ngai agĩĩka o ro ũguo. No rũũa rwiki rwarĩ rũmũ; nakuo thĩ guothe kwarĩ kũhumbĩre nĩ ime.
english: God did so that night; for it was dry on the fleece only, and there was dew on all the ground.

kikuyu: Gĩa na ũũgĩ, gĩa na kũmenya maũndũ; ndũkanariganĩrwo nĩ ciugo ciakwa, kana wahũke kuuma harĩ cio.
english: Yes, and for this very cause adding on your part all diligence, in your faith supply moral excellence; and in moral excellence, knowledge;

kikuyu: Jehova aikaraga haraaya na andũ arĩa aaganu, no nĩaiguaga mahooya ma andũ arĩa athingu.
english: Yahweh is far from the wicked, but he hears the prayer of the righteous.

kikuyu: Nĩmarutaga wĩra wa gũtungatĩra Ngai wao, na wĩra wa gwĩtheria, o ta ũrĩa aini na aikaria a ihingo meekaga, kũringana na ũrĩa gwathanĩtwo nĩ Daudi na mũriũ Solomoni.
english: They performed the duty of their God and the duty of the purification, and so did the singers and the gatekeepers, according to the commandment of David and of Solomon his son.

kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũrũmagia watho nĩ mwana ũrĩ na gũkũũrana, no mwana ũrĩa ũthiiaga na thiritũ ya arĩa akoroku aconorithagia ithe.
english: Whoever keeps the law is a wise son; but he who is a companion of gluttons shames his father.

kikuyu: Gũtirĩ ũũgĩ, kana ũmenyi, kana kĩrĩra o na kĩrĩkũ kĩngĩhota gũũkĩrĩra Jehova.
english: There is no wisdom nor understanding nor counsel against Yahweh.

kikuyu: Tiga kwĩhoka mũndũ, ũrĩa mĩhũmũ yake ĩrĩ o maniũrũ-inĩ make. Nĩ ũndũ-rĩ, akĩrĩ wa bata ũrĩkũ?
english: Stop trusting in man, whose breath is in his nostrils; for of what account is he?

kikuyu: Amerari maatarirwo kũringana na mbarĩ na nyũmba ciao.
english: But those things that are reported of him, and of his uncleanness and impiety, are written in the chronicles of the kings.

kikuyu: Wee ũtũmĩte tũtuĩke ta gĩko, na ta mahuti gatagatĩ ka ndũrĩrĩ.
english: You have made us an off-scouring and refuse in the middle of the peoples.

kikuyu: Ngoro ciitũ iticookete na thuutha; makinya maitũ matiumĩte njĩra-inĩ yaku. No ũtũhehenjete, na ũgatũtua imamo cia mbwe, na ũgatũhumbĩra na nduma nene.
english: Our heart has not turned back, neither have our steps strayed from your path,

kikuyu: Mwathani Jehova, Ngai Mwene-Hinya-Wothe, ekuuga atĩrĩ: “Ta iguai ũhoro ũyũ, na inyuĩ mũrute ũira wa gũũkĩrĩra nyũmba ya Jakubu.
english: “Listen, and testify against the house of Jacob,” says the Lord Yahweh, the God of Armies.

kikuyu: no thuutha wa kahinda o kanini akĩehera, akĩaga gũkorwo ho rĩngĩ; o na gũtuĩka nĩndamũcaririe, ndiigana kũmuona.
english: But he passed away, and behold, he was not. Yes, I sought him, but he could not be found.

kikuyu: Marangagĩrĩria mĩtwe ya arĩa athĩĩni taarĩ rũkũngũ rwa thĩ, na makaagithia kĩhooto arĩa ahinyĩrĩrie.
english: but I will send a fire on the wall of Gaza, and it will devour its palaces.

kikuyu: Gũtirĩ mũndũ o na ũmwe wao ũgaatigara, tondũ nĩngarehithĩria andũ a Anathothu mwanangĩko mwaka-inĩ ũrĩa makaaherithio.’ ”
english: There will be no remnant to them, for I will bring evil on the men of Anathoth, even the year of their visitation.’ ”

kikuyu: “Oya rũthanju rwaku, nawe na mũrũ wa nyũkwa Harũni mũcokanĩrĩrie kĩrĩndĩ kĩu hamwe. Arĩria rwaro rũrũ rwa ihiga makwĩroreire, na nĩrũkuuma maaĩ. Nawe ũrutĩre kĩrĩndĩ maaĩ rwaro-inĩ rũrũ rwa ihiga, nĩgeetha kĩnyue hamwe na mahiũ ma kĩo.” Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Musa akĩoya rũthanju akĩrũruta hau mbere ya Jehova, o ta ũrĩa Jehova aamwathĩte. Nake Musa na Harũni magĩcokanĩrĩria kĩrĩndĩ kĩu hamwe hau mbere ya rwaro rwa ihiga, na Musa akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Ta thikĩrĩriai, inyuĩ aremi aya, no nginya tũmũrutĩre maaĩ kuuma rwaro-inĩ rũrũ rwa ihiga?”
english: “Take the rod, and assemble the congregation, you, and Aaron your brother, and speak to the rock before their eyes, that it pour out its water. You shall bring water to them out of the rock; so you shall give the congregation and their livestock drink.”

kikuyu: Maya nĩmo marĩĩtwa ma ariũ a Isiraeli (Jakubu na njiaro ciake) arĩa maathiire Misiri: Rubeni irigithathi rĩa Jakubu.
english: So now it wasn’t you who sent me here, but God, and he has made me a father to Pharaoh, lord of all his house, and ruler over all the land of Egypt.

kikuyu: Ũndũ ũyũ nĩũbataire ũũgĩ. Mũndũ o wothe angĩkorwo arĩ na ũmenyo-rĩ, nĩeke ithabu rĩa namba ya nyamũ ĩyo, nĩgũkorwo nĩ namba ya mũndũ. Nayo namba yake nĩ 666.
english: Another angel came out from the altar, he who has power over fire, and he called with a great voice to him who had the sharp sickle, saying, “Send your sharp sickle and gather the clusters of the vine of the earth, for the earth’s grapes are fully ripe!”

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo gũtirĩ ngũũrani gatagatĩ ka Mũyahudi na mũndũ-wa-Ndũrĩrĩ: tondũ Mwathani o ũcio nĩwe Mwathani wa andũ othe, na nĩarathimaga mũno arĩa othe mamũkayagĩra;
english: Now if their fall is the riches of the world, and their loss the riches of the Gentiles, how much more their fullness!

kikuyu: Mwaka-inĩ wa ikũmi na ũmwe, mũthenya wa mũgwanja wa mweri wa mbere, kiugo kĩa Jehova nĩkĩanginyĩrĩire, ngĩĩrwo atĩrĩ:
english: In the eleventh year, in the first month, in the seventh day of the month, Yahweh’s word came to me, saying,

kikuyu: Ũkĩra ũthiĩ Padani-Aramu, kwa nyũmba ya Bethueli ithe wa nyũkwa. Wĩcarĩrie mũtumia kuumakũrĩairĩtu a Labani, mũrũwa nyinananyũkwa.
english: Arise, go to Paddan Aram, to the house of Bethuel your mother’s father. Take a wife from there from the daughters of Laban, your mother’s brother.

kikuyu: “ ‘Mũndũ ũcio ũkuura angĩhutia mũndũ atethambĩte moko na maaĩ, mũndũ ũcio ahutia no nginya athambie nguo ciake na ethambe na maaĩ, na atiinde arĩ na thaahu o nginya hwaĩ-inĩ.
english: “ ‘Whomever he who has the discharge touches, without having rinsed his hands in water, he shall wash his clothes, and bathe himself in water, and be unclean until the evening.

kikuyu: Maranĩrĩra, makoigĩrĩria nĩ gũkena; kuuma mwena wa ithũĩro marakũngũĩra ũnene wa Jehova. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio inyuĩ andũ a kuuma mwena wa irathĩro gocithiai Jehova; nenehiai rĩĩtwa rĩa Jehova Ngai wa Isiraeli, kũu icigĩrĩra-inĩ cia iria.
english: These shall lift up their voice. They will shout for the majesty of Yahweh. They cry aloud from the sea.

kikuyu: Geithiai Turufena na Turufosa, andũ-a-nja acio marutaga wĩra na hinya thĩinĩ wa Mwathani. Na mũgeithie mũrata wakwa Perisi, mũndũ-wa-nja ũngĩ o nake ũrutĩte wĩra na hinya mũno thĩinĩ wa Mwathani.
english: Again, Isaiah says, “There will be the root of Jesse, he who arises to rule over the Gentiles; in him the Gentiles will hope.”*

kikuyu: Mũtikanarute wĩra mũthenya ũcio, tondũ nĩguo Mũthenya wa Kũhoroherio Mehia, hĩndĩ ĩrĩa mũrahoroherio mehia mbere ya Jehova-Ngai wanyu.
english: Whether it is a cow or ewe, you shall not kill it and its young both in one day.

kikuyu: Gĩkundi gĩa keerĩ kĩa aini gĩgĩthiĩ kĩerekeire mwena wa ũmotho. Na niĩ ngĩmarũmĩrĩra kũu rũthingo igũrũ hamwe na nuthu ya andũ, tũkĩhĩtũkĩra Mũthiringo wa Mariiko ma Mwaki o nginya Rũthingo-inĩ Rwariĩ,
english: The other company of those who gave thanks went to meet them, and I after them, with the half of the people on the wall above the tower of the furnaces, even to the wide wall,

kikuyu: ‘Ayubu aaragia atarĩ na ũmenyo; ciugo ciake nĩciagĩte ũũgĩ.’
english: 1 Moreover Elihu answered,

kikuyu: Kũrathimwo-rĩ, nĩ mũndũ ũrĩa Jehova atatuaga mwĩhia, na ũrĩa ũtarĩ ũhinga ngoro. Rĩrĩa ndakirĩte ki, mahĩndĩ makwa nĩmahinyarire nĩ ũndũ wa gũcaaya gwakwa mũthenya wothe.
english: Give thanks to Yahweh with the lyre. Sing praises to him with the harp of ten strings.

kikuyu: Niĩ nĩ niĩ Jehova Ngai wanyu, ũrĩa wamũrutire bũrũri wa Misiri nĩguo nduĩke Ngai wanyu. Niĩ nĩ niĩ Jehova Ngai wanyu.’ ”
english: Moses said, “Why now do you disobey the commandment of Yahweh, since it shall not prosper?

kikuyu: Nĩndathiire Jerusalemu, na thuutha wa gũikara kuo mĩthenya ĩtatũ-rĩ,
english: So I came to Jerusalem, and was there three days.

kikuyu: Nowe Jesũ akiuga atĩrĩ, “Nĩ harĩ mũndũ waahutia, tondũ nĩndaigua hinya woima thĩinĩ wakwa.”
english: You didn’t anoint my head with oil, but she has anointed my feet with ointment.

kikuyu: Kĩhingo gĩa Kĩanda gĩacookereirio nĩ Hanuni hamwe na andũ arĩa maatũũraga Zanoa. Magĩgĩaka rĩngĩ na magĩĩkĩra mĩrango, na igera cia kũmĩhinga na icuuma ciakĩo, ningĩ magĩcookereria rũthingo ũraihu wa mĩkono 1,000* o nginya Kĩhingo gĩa Kĩara-inĩ.
english: Hanun and the inhabitants of Zanoah repaired the valley gate. They built it, and set up its doors, its bolts, and its bars, and one thousand cubits† of the wall to the dung gate.

kikuyu: Thuutha ũcio nĩndacookire ngĩthiĩ Suriata na Kilikia.
english: Then I came to the regions of Syria and Cilicia.

kikuyu: Nĩnguuga o rĩngĩ atĩrĩ: Mũndũ ndakae kuona ta ndĩ kĩrimũ. No angĩkorwo nĩguo mũkuona-rĩ, kĩnyamũkĩrei o ta ũrĩa mũngĩamũkĩra kĩrimũ, nĩguo ndĩĩrahe o hanini.
english: Greet one another with a holy kiss. The assemblies of Christ greet you.

kikuyu: ‘Kũrĩ andũ moimĩte bũrũri wa Misiri makaiyũra bũrũri ũyũ wothe. Ndagũthaitha ũũke ũnumĩre o manyiitwo nĩ kĩrumi, no gũkorwo no hũũrane nao ndĩmaingate moime bũrũri ũyũ!’ ” No Ngai akĩĩra Balamu atĩrĩ, “Ndũgũthiĩ nao. Ndũkarume andũ acio, tondũ nĩ andũ arathime.”
english: They traveled from Oboth, and encamped at Iyeabarim, in the wilderness which is before Moab, toward the sunrise.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa aamoonire, akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Thiĩi mũkeyonanie kũrĩ athĩnjĩri-Ngai.” Na rĩrĩa maathiiaga-rĩ, magĩtherio. Rĩrĩa ũmwe wao onire nĩahonio, akĩhũndũka akĩgooca Ngai na mũgambo mũnene,
english: When he saw them, he said to them, “Go and show yourselves to the priests.” As they went, they were cleansed.

kikuyu: Isiraeli nĩariganĩirwo nĩ ũrĩa wamũmbire, na agaaka nyũmba cia ũnene; Juda nĩairigĩire matũũra maingĩ na thingo cia hinya. No nĩngarehere matũũra mao manene mwaki ũrĩa ũgaacina ciikaro ciao iria ciirigĩtwo na hinya.”
english: For Israel has forgotten his Maker and built palaces; and Judah has multiplied fortified cities; but I will send a fire on his cities, and it will devour its fortresses.”

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩmũrĩhikagia airĩtu aitũ na nĩtũrĩhikagia airĩtu anyu. Nĩtũgũtũũrania na inyuĩ na tũtuĩke andũ amwe. No angĩkorwo mũtigwĩtĩkĩra kũrua-rĩ, tũkuoya mwarĩ witũ twĩthiĩre.”
english: They traveled from Bethel. There was still some distance to come to Ephrath, and Rachel travailed. She had hard labor.

kikuyu: no inyuĩ mũtiĩtĩkagia tondũ mũtirĩ ngʼondu ciakwa.
english: But you don’t believe, because you are not of my sheep, as I told you.

kikuyu: Mũndũ o na ũrĩkũ ũrĩa ũtakeima irio mũthenya ũcio nĩakaingatwo kuuma kũrĩ andũ ao.
english: “When you sacrifice a sacrifice of thanksgiving to Yahweh, you shall sacrifice it so that you may be accepted.

kikuyu: ‘Wee Debora, ũkĩra! Ĩ ndũgĩũkĩre! Ũkĩra, ũkĩra, ũkũye rwĩmbo! Arahũka wee Baraka! Wee mũrũ wa Abinoamu-rĩ, wĩnyiitĩre mĩgwate yaku.’
english: Yahweh’s angel appeared to him, and said to him, “Yahweh is with you, you mighty man of valor!”

kikuyu: “ ‘No mũndũ ũrĩa ũkehia na rũtũrĩko akĩendaga, arĩ wa gũciarĩrwo kwanyu kana arĩ mũgeni, ũcio nĩarumĩte Jehova, na mũndũ ũcio no nginya aingatwo kuuma kũrĩ andũ ao. Nĩ ũndũ nĩ amenete kiugo kĩa Jehova na akoina maathani make, mũndũ ũcio ti-itherũ no nginya aingatwo biũ naguo wĩhia wake nĩũkamũcokerera.’ ” Mũthũkia wa Thabatũ Kũũragwo
english: surely you shall not come into the land concerning which I swore that I would make you dwell therein, except Caleb the son of Jephunneh, and Joshua the son of Nun.

kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo Ngai no ũmwe, na nĩwe ũrĩĩtuaga andũ arĩa maruaga athingu, na ũndũ wa gwĩtĩkia, na arĩa mataruaga nao amatuuage athingu na ũndũ wa gwĩtĩkia o ro ũguo.
english: since indeed there is one God who will justify the circumcised by faith and the uncircumcised through faith.

kikuyu: Etiiranagia na rũrenda rwake, naruo rũgatuĩka; akenyiitĩrĩra harĩ ruo, no rũtingĩmũtiirĩrĩra.
english: Though I were righteous, yet I wouldn’t answer him. I would make supplication to my judge.

kikuyu: Niĩ ndĩ gatagatĩ-inĩ ka mĩrũũthi; ngomete gatagatĩ ka nyamũ ndĩĩani, na nĩo andũ arĩa magego mao mahaana ta matimũ na mĩguĩ, o acio nĩmĩ ciao ihaana ta hiũ cia njora njũgĩ.
english: In God, I praise his word. In God, I put my trust. I will not be afraid. What can flesh do to me?

kikuyu: “Gũtua ciira gwakwa na kĩhooto-rĩ, githĩ no ũkũmenererie? Githĩ no ũndue mwĩhia nĩgeetha wee ũtuĩke ndwĩhĩtie?
english: Will you even annul my judgment? Will you condemn me, that you may be justified?

kikuyu: Mĩaka ikũmi na ĩna yathira nĩndambatire rĩngĩ Jerusalemu, na ihinda rĩĩrĩ tũgĩthiĩ hamwe na Baranaba o na tũgĩthiĩ na Tito.
english: Paul, an apostle—not from men, nor through man, but through Jesus Christ,† and God the Father, who raised him from the dead—

kikuyu: O na gũtikanagĩe mĩario ya ũũra-thoni, o na kana mĩario ya ũrimũ, o na kana mĩario ya itherũ rĩtarĩ rĩega. Mĩario ta ĩyo ndĩagĩrĩire kũgwetagwo; nĩ kaba gũcookagĩria Ngai ngaatho.
english: nor filthiness, nor foolish talking, nor jesting, which are not appropriate, but rather giving of thanks.

kikuyu: Ndathũkirie wĩra wothe wa moko manyu na mbaa, na mbuu na mbura ya mbembe,* no inyuĩ mũtiigana kũnjookerera,’ ũguo nĩguo Jehova ekuuga.
english: I struck you with blight, mildew, and hail in all the work of your hands; yet you didn’t turn to me,’ says Yahweh.

kikuyu: Wee Jehova nyonagĩrĩria njĩra yaku, na ũngeragĩrie njĩra ĩrĩa nũngarũ, nĩ ũndũ wa thũ ciakwa.
english: Yahweh will give strength to his people. Yahweh will bless his people with peace.

kikuyu: o na gũtuĩka rũciinĩ nĩĩringũkaga, hwaĩ-inĩ ĩhoohaga, ĩkooma.
english: In the morning it sprouts and springs up. By evening, it is withered and dry.

kikuyu: Wee Jehova, njeheria moko-inĩ ma andũ arĩa aaganu;
english: Don’t incline my heart to any evil thing, to practice deeds of wickedness with men who work iniquity.

kikuyu: nĩwe wombire igũrũ na ũndũ wa ũmenyo wake, Wendo wake nĩ wa tene na tene.
english: to him who by understanding made the heavens, for his loving kindness endures forever;

kikuyu: ciana cia ndungata ciake nĩigakũgaya, nao arĩa mendete rĩĩtwa rĩake nĩmagatũũra kuo.
english: The children also of his servants shall inherit it. Those who love his name shall dwell therein.

kikuyu: Ndaabataragio nĩ ũira wa mũndũ, ũkoniĩ mũndũ ũrĩa ũngĩ, nĩgũkorwo nĩamenyaga kĩrĩa kĩrĩ thĩinĩ wa mũndũ.
english: and because he didn’t need for anyone to testify concerning man; for he himself knew what was in man.

kikuyu: Wee nĩũgaacihuha, naruo rũhuho rũcigũrũkie, nakĩo kĩhuhũkanio gĩcihurunje. No rĩrĩ, wee nĩũgakenera Jehova, na wĩrahagĩre Ũrĩa Mũtheru wa Isiraeli.
english: You will winnow them, and the wind will carry them away, and the whirlwind will scatter them. You will rejoice in Yahweh. You will glory in the Holy One of Israel.

kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo ngoro-inĩ yaku nĩũũĩ atĩ mahinda maingĩ wee mwene wanaruma andũ arĩa angĩ.
english: She makes for herself carpets of tapestry. Her clothing is fine linen and purple.

kikuyu: Mũthamaki nĩoĩ maũndũ maya, na no ndĩmwarĩrie itegwĩtigĩra. Nĩ njũũĩ atĩ gũtirĩ ũndũ o na ũmwe wa maũndũ maya aagĩte kũmenya, tondũ matiekĩirwo hitho-inĩ.
english: For the king knows of these things, to whom also I speak freely. For I am persuaded that none of these things is hidden from him, for this has not been done in a corner.

kikuyu: Kaĩ ciugo ciaku irĩ mũrĩo ngĩcicama-ĩ, irĩ mũrĩo gũkĩra ũũkĩ ũrĩ kanua-inĩ gakwa!
english: How sweet are your promises to my taste, more than honey to my mouth!

kikuyu: Kũrathimwo-rĩ, nĩ arĩa marĩ na kĩeha, nĩgũkorwo nĩmakaniinĩrwo kĩeha.
english: Blessed are those who mourn, for they shall be comforted.*

kikuyu: Andũ othe arĩa magaĩirwo gũikara kambĩ ya Rubeni, kũringana na ikundi ciao-rĩ, nĩ andũ 151,450. Acio nĩo magaatuĩka a keerĩ kuumagara.
english: “All who were counted of the camp of Reuben were one hundred fifty-one thousand four hundred fifty, according to their armies. They shall set out second.

kikuyu: Kuuma kũu ũgakĩrania ũrorete na mwena wa kĩharũrũka kĩa gũthini kĩa Luzu (na norĩo Betheli) na ũgaikũrũka nginya Atarothu-Adari gũkuhĩ na kĩrĩma kĩa mwena wa gũthini wa itũũra rĩa Bethi-Horoni ya Mũhuro.
english: From there it passed along eastward to Gath Hepher, to Ethkazin; and it went out at Rimmon which stretches to Neah.

kikuyu: “ ‘Marikabu cia Tarishishi nĩcio igũkuuagĩra indo ciaku cia wonjoria. Ũiyũrĩrĩirio mĩrigo mĩritũ o kũu iria gatagatĩ.
english: “ ‘ “The ships of Tarshish were your caravans for your merchandise. You were replenished and made very glorious in the heart of the seas.

kikuyu: Wee Jehova, wendo waku ũrĩa ũtathiraga ũronginyĩra, ũhonokio waku ũnginyĩre kũringana na kĩĩranĩro gĩaku;
english: Let your loving kindness also come to me, Yahweh, your salvation, according to your word.

kikuyu: Irĩĩaga tĩĩri nĩ ũrũme; ndĩngĩrũgama ĩkindĩirie hĩndĩ ĩrĩa karumbeta kagamba.
english: Shall any take him when he is on the watch, or pierce through his nose with a snare?

kikuyu: Bũrũri nĩũrathingitha na ũkenyogonda nĩ ruo, nĩgũkorwo ũndũ ũrĩa Jehova aciirĩire gwĩka Babuloni nĩ mũrũmu: wa kũharagania bũrũri wa Babuloni nĩguo kwage mũndũ ũngĩtũũra kuo.
english: The land trembles and is in pain; for the purposes of Yahweh against Babylon stand, to make the land of Babylon a desolation, without inhabitant.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa mũcemanio wathirire, Ayahudi aingĩ na andũ arĩa meegarũrĩte magatuĩka Ayahudi makĩrũmĩrĩra Paũlũ na Baranaba, nao makĩmaarĩria na makĩmaringĩrĩria mathiĩ na mbere gũikara wega-inĩ wa Ngai.
english: Now when the synagogue broke up, many of the Jews and of the devout proselytes followed Paul and Barnabas; who, speaking to them, urged them to continue in the grace of God.

kikuyu: Andũ aingĩ nĩmarĩkĩtie kwandĩka ũhoro wa maũndũ marĩa mahingĩtio gatagatĩ-inĩ gaitũ,
english: Since many have undertaken to set in order a narrative concerning those matters which have been fulfilled among us,

kikuyu: Mũtĩ ũcio wa kũigĩrĩrwo matawa wathondeketwo ũũ: wathondeketwo na thahabu ya gũturwo, kuuma gĩtina-inĩ kĩaguo o nginya kũrĩa mahũa maguo maarĩ. Mũhianĩre wa mũtĩ ũcio wa kũigĩrĩrwo matawa wathondeketwo o ta ũrĩa mũhianĩre waguo wonetio Musa nĩ Jehova. Kwamũra Alawii Nake Jehova akĩĩra Musa atĩrĩ:
english: This was the workmanship of the lamp stand, beaten work of gold. From its base to its flowers, it was beaten work. He made the lamp stand according to the pattern which Yahweh had shown Moses.

kikuyu: “O na ningĩ korwo ndarehithia mũthiro bũrũri ũcio, na niĩ ndĩũitĩrĩrie mangʼũrĩ makwa na ũndũ wa gũitithia thakame, na njũragithie andũ akuo na mahiũ mao,
english: You have profaned me among my people for handfuls of barley and for pieces of bread, to kill the souls who should not die and to save the souls alive who should not live, by your lying to my people who listen to lies.’

kikuyu: “Na rĩrĩ, niĩ nĩngamũhehenja o ta ũrĩa ngaari ĩiyũrĩte ngano ĩhehenjaga.
english: Behold,‡ I will crush you in your place, as a cart crushes that is full of grain.

kikuyu: “Nũũ wakũiguithirie guoya na ũkĩmwĩtigĩra, ũũ atĩ ũkoretwo ũkĩaga gũtuĩka mwĩhokeku kũrĩ niĩ, na ũkaaga gũcooka kũndirikana, o na kana wĩcũũranie ũhoro ũcio na ngoro yaku? Githĩ ti tondũ ngoretwo ngirĩte ki ihinda iraihu, nawe ũkaaga kũnjĩtigĩra?
english: “Whom have you dreaded and feared, so that you lie, and have not remembered me, nor laid it to your heart? Haven’t I held my peace for a long time, and you don’t fear me?

kikuyu: nĩguo ũigue thoni na ũconoke nĩ ũndũ wa maũndũ macio mothe wekire nĩ ũndũ wa kũmahe ũhurũko. Nao aarĩ acio a maitũguo, Sodomu marĩ na airĩtu ake, na Samaria marĩ na airĩtu ake-rĩ, nĩmagacooka o ta ũrĩa maarĩ mbere; nawe ũrĩ na airĩtu aku nĩmũgacooka o ta ũrĩa mwarĩ mbere.
english: that you may bear your own shame, and may be ashamed because of all that you have done, in that you are a comfort to them.

kikuyu: na ũrĩa wamũrutire bũrũri wa Misiri nduĩke Ngai wanyu. Niĩ nĩ niĩ Jehova.”
english: who brought you out of the land of Egypt, to be your God. I am Yahweh.”
