Na rĩrĩ, wee Mũthamaki, Ayahudi maathitangĩte nĩ ũndũ wa kĩĩrĩgĩrĩro kĩu. Nĩ kĩĩ kĩngĩtũma mũndũ o na ũrĩkũ wanyu atue atĩ nĩ ũndũ ũtangĩĩtĩkio, atĩ Ngai nĩariũkagia arĩa akuũ?
english: When he had come, the Jews who had come down from Jerusalem stood around him, bringing against him many and grievous charges which they could not prove,

kikuyu: Kĩndũ gĩothe kĩarĩ muoyo na gĩathiiaga gũkũ thĩ gĩgĩkua, nyoni, na mahiũ na nyamũ cia gĩthaka, na ciũmbe ciothe iria ciahunjĩte thĩ, o na andũ othe.
english: Take with you some of all food that is eaten, and gather it to yourself; and it will be for food for you, and for them.”

kikuyu: Jehova, ririkana tha ciaku nyingĩ o na wendo waku, nĩgũkorwo nĩ cia kuuma o tene.
english: Now my head will be lifted up above my enemies around me. I will offer sacrifices of joy in his tent. I will sing, yes, I will sing praises to Yahweh.

kikuyu: Harĩ o ndegwa icio ikũmi na ithatũ-rĩ, haragĩriai iruta rĩa mũtu icunjĩ ithatũ cia ikũmi cia ebaĩmwe‡ ya mũtu mũhinyumũnoũtukanĩtiona maguta; harĩo ndũrũmeĩmwe ya icio igĩrĩ, irutanĩrio na icunjĩ igĩrĩ cia ikũmi;§
english: because in the strife of the congregation, you rebelled against my word in the wilderness of Zin, to honor me as holy at the waters before their eyes.” (These are the waters of Meribah of Kadesh in the wilderness of Zin.)

kikuyu: Nĩwathĩnjire ciana ciakwa ũgĩciruta igongona harĩ mĩhianano ĩyo.
english: that you have slain my children and delivered them up, in causing them to pass through the fire to them?

kikuyu: “ ‘Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, ũũ nĩguo Mwathani Jehova ekuuga: Tondũ wa ciugo cianyu cia maheeni na cioneki cia kũheenania-rĩ, nĩngũmũũkĩrĩra, ũguo nĩguo Mwathani Jehova ekuuga.’
english: “ ‘Therefore the Lord Yahweh says: “Because you have spoken falsehood and seen lies, therefore, behold, I am against you,” says the Lord Yahweh.

kikuyu: Inyuĩ andũ arĩa mũtũũraga Shafiri, thiĩi mũrĩ njaga na mũconokete. Andũ arĩa matũũraga Zaanani matingiuma itũũra rĩao. * 1:1 Rĩĩtwa Mika rĩgĩtaũrwo nĩ kuuga “Nũũ ũhaana ta Jehova”.
english: Pass on, inhabitant of Shaphir, in nakedness and shame. The inhabitant of Zaanan won’t come out. The wailing of Beth Ezel will take from you his protection.

kikuyu: Heei ciana cianyu ũhoro ũcio, na mũreke ciana cianyu ciĩre ciana ciacio, nacio ciana ciacio ikeera rũciaro rũrĩa rũkaarũmĩrĩra.
english: Tell your children about it, and have your children tell their children, and their children, another generation.

kikuyu: Kũrigania nake, Baruku mũrũ wa Zabai agĩcookereria gĩcunjĩ kĩngĩ arĩ na kĩyo, kuuma koine- inĩ nginya itoonyero rĩa nyũmba ya Eliashibu mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai ũrĩa mũnene.
english: After him, Baruch the son of Zabbai earnestly repaired another portion, from the turning of the wall to the door of the house of Eliashib the high priest.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩĩrĩamaakũnyiitire na moko mao-rĩ, nĩwatũkangire, na ũgĩtarũranga ciande ciao; hĩndĩ ĩrĩa meetiiranirie nawe-rĩ, nĩwoinĩkangire, nayo mĩgongo yao ĩkĩgonyoka.
english: When they took hold of you by your hand, you broke and tore all their shoulders. When they leaned on you, you broke and paralyzed all of their thighs.”

kikuyu: Naho hau hakiumĩra nyamũ inya ciarĩ nene kuuma iria-inĩ rĩu, na gũtirĩ yahanaga ĩrĩa ĩngĩ.
english: Four great animals came up from the sea, different from one another.

kikuyu: Inyuĩ nĩmũũĩ atĩ ndirĩ ndatithia kũhunjia ũndũ o wothe ũngĩmũguna no ndĩmũrutagĩra mbere ya andũ othe na ngathiiaga o nyũmba o nyũmba. Na ngoimbũrĩra Ayahudi o hamwe na Ayunani atĩ no nginya magarũrũke kũrĩ Ngai na ũndũ wa kwĩrira na magĩe na wĩtĩkio thĩinĩ wa Mwathani witũ Jesũ.
english: 1 After the uproar had ceased, Paul sent for the disciples, took leave of them, and departed to go into Macedonia.

kikuyu: O rĩngĩ agĩceera Kana ya Galili, kũrĩa aagarũrĩte maaĩ magatuĩka ndibei. Na kũu Kaperinaumu nĩ kwarĩ na mũndũ ũmwe mũnene warĩ na mũriũ mũrũaru. Rĩrĩa mũndũ ũcio aaiguire atĩ Jesũ nĩoimĩte Judea agooka Galili, agĩthiĩ kũrĩ we, akĩmũthaitha athiĩ akahonie mũriũ, ũrĩa warĩ hakuhĩ gũkua.
english: Jesus came therefore again to Cana of Galilee, where he made the water into wine. There was a certain nobleman whose son was sick at Capernaum.

kikuyu: Johana nginya hĩndĩ ĩrĩa Jesũ oirwo akĩambata akĩehera harĩ ithuĩ. Nĩgũkorwo mũndũ ũmwe wa aya no nginya atuĩke mũira hamwe na ithuĩ ũhoro-inĩ wa kũriũka gwake.” Nĩ ũndũ ũcio makĩrũgamia andũ eerĩ, Jusufu ũrĩa wetagwo Barasaba‡ (na no oĩkaine ta Jusito), na Mathia.
english: him, being delivered up by the determined counsel and foreknowledge of God, you have taken by the hand of lawless men, crucified and killed;

kikuyu: 1 Na rĩrĩ, mũthenya ũcio andũ-a-nja mũgwanja makaanyiita mũndũ mũrũme ũmwe, mamwĩre atĩrĩ, “Tũrĩrĩĩaga irio ciitũ ithuĩ ene,
english: when the Lord shall have washed away the filth of the daughters of Zion, and shall have purged the blood of Jerusalem from within it, by the spirit of justice and by the spirit of burning.

kikuyu: Jesũ nĩendete Maritha na mwarĩ wa
english: Now Jesus loved Martha, and her sister, and Lazarus.

kikuyu: Gĩcango kĩrĩa kĩarutirwo itugĩ-inĩ icio cierĩ, na Karia kau, na ndegwa iria ikũmi na igĩrĩ cia gĩcango iria ciarĩ rungu rwako, na metha, iria Mũthamaki Solomoni aathondekete cia hekarũ ya Jehova, gĩtingĩathimĩkire nĩ kũingĩha.
english: They took the two pillars, the one sea, and the twelve bronze bulls that were under the bases, which King Solomon had made for Yahweh’s house. The bronze of all these vessels was without weight.

kikuyu: Hĩ, riiri wake ndũngĩmũmakia? Mũtingĩnyiitwo nĩ guoya nĩ ũndũ wake?
english: Won’t his majesty make you afraid and his dread fall on you?

kikuyu: “Nũũ wanahe Ngai kĩndũ, atĩ nĩguo Ngai acooke amũrĩhe kĩndũ kĩu?”
english: “Or who has first given to him, and it will be repaid to him again?”*

kikuyu: Agĩthiĩ na mbere kuuga atĩrĩ, “Ngũmwĩra na ma, gũtirĩ mũnabii wĩtĩkagĩrwo itũũra-inĩ rĩake.
english: He said, “Most certainly I tell you, no prophet is acceptable in his hometown.

kikuyu: Andũ a Isiraeli makĩĩra Gideoni atĩrĩ, “Tũthamakĩre, wee, na mũrũguo, na mũrũ wa mũrũguo o nake, nĩgũkorwo wee nĩũtũhonoketie, ũgatũruta moko-inĩ ma Midiani.”
english: Then the men of Israel said to Gideon, “Rule over us, both you, your son, and your son’s son also; for you have saved us out of the hand of Midian.”

kikuyu: Ũhoro wa kũrua nĩ ũrĩ kĩguni ũngĩrũmia ũrĩa watho uugĩte, no ũngĩagarara watho ũcio-rĩ, ũhoro ũcio waku wa kũrua ũtuĩkĩte ta ũtaruĩte.*
english: For circumcision indeed profits, if you are a doer of the law, but if you are a transgressor of the law, your circumcision has become uncircumcision.

kikuyu: Mũngĩathĩkĩramaathanimakwa-rĩ, nĩmũrĩikaragawendo-inĩwakwaotaũrĩaniĩnjathĩkĩire maathani ma Baba na ngaikara wendo-inĩ wake.
english: If you keep my commandments, you will remain in my love, even as I have kept my Father’s commandments and remain in his love.

kikuyu: Labani akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Ti mũtugo witũ gũkũ kũheana mũirĩtu ũrĩa mũnini ahike mbere ya ũrĩa mũkũrũ:
english: Laban said, “It is not done so in our place, to give the younger before the firstborn.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa aahingũrire Irima-rĩtarĩ-Gĩturi-rĩ, rĩkiuma ndogo yahaanaga ta ndogo ya icua inene. Narĩo riũa na matu igĩĩkĩrwo nduma nĩ ndogo ĩyo yoimaga Irima-rĩu-rĩtarĩ-Gĩturi. Na thĩinĩ wa ndogo ĩyo gũkiuma ngigĩ, igĩũka thĩ, nacio ikĩheo ũhoti ũtariĩ ta wa tũngʼaurũ twa thĩ.
english: Leave out the court which is outside of the temple, and don’t measure it, for it has been given to the nations. They will tread the holy city under foot for forty-two months.

kikuyu: “Githĩ Ngai ndarĩ o kũu igũrũ o igũrũ? O na ta kĩone ũrĩa njata iria irĩ igũrũ mũno itũũgĩrĩte! No rĩrĩ, wee ũroiga atĩrĩ, ‘Mũrungu nĩ atĩa angĩmenya? No atuanĩre ciira nduma-inĩ ta ĩno?
english: “Isn’t God in the heights of heaven? See the height of the stars, how high they are!

kikuyu: Mũndũ ũtarĩ ũũgĩ etĩkagia ũndũ o wothe, no mũndũ mũbaarĩrĩri nĩacũũranagia ũhoro wa makinya make.
english: All the days of the afflicted are wretched, but one who has a cheerful heart enjoys a continual feast.

kikuyu: Mũhĩrĩga wa Nafitali nĩguo ũgũcookerera. Mũtongoria wa andũ a Nafitali nĩ Ahira mũrũ wa Enani.
english: “The tribe of Naphtali: the prince of the children of Naphtali shall be Ahira the son of Enan.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo nĩ Jehova Ngai witũ we mwene watũrutire bũrũri wa Misiri hamwe na maithe maitũ, tũkiuma bũrũri ũcio wa ũkombo, na akĩringa ciama icio nene ũguo na tũgĩciona na maitho maitũ. Nĩaatũgitĩrire rũgendo-inĩ rwitũ ruothe kũrĩa twagereire, o na tũrĩ gatagatĩ ka ndũrĩrĩ ciothe kũrĩa twatuĩkanĩirie. Nake Jehova nĩaingatire ndũrĩrĩ icio ciothe ciehere mbere iitũ, o hamwe na Aamori arĩa maatũũraga bũrũri-inĩ ũcio. Tondũ ũcio o na ithuĩ tũrĩtungatagĩra Jehova, nĩ ũndũ nĩwe Ngai witũ.” Nake Joshua akĩĩra andũ acio atĩrĩ, “Mũtingĩhota gũtungatĩra Jehova. Jehova nĩ Ngai Mũtheru; we nĩ Ngai ũrĩ ũiru. Ndakamũrekera ũremi wanyu o na kana mehia manyu.
english: for it is Yahweh our God who brought us and our fathers up out of the land of Egypt, from the house of bondage, and who did those great signs in our sight, and preserved us in all the way in which we went, and among all the peoples through the middle of whom we passed.

kikuyu: Nĩngakũũria ũtuĩke wakwa na wĩhokeku, nawe nĩũgetĩkĩra Jehova.”
english: Don’t abuse a servant who works faithfully, or a hireling who gives you his life.

kikuyu: mũkinyia-ũhoro agĩkora Ayubu, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Ndegwa nĩkũrĩma ikũrĩmaga na mĩraũ, nacio ndigiri ikũrĩĩaga o hau hakuhĩ,
english: that a messenger came to Job, and said, “The oxen were plowing, and the donkeys feeding beside them,

kikuyu: Guoko gwaku nĩgũkĩĩhaarĩrie kũndeithia, nĩgũkorwo mawatho maku nĩmo niĩ ndĩthuurĩire.
english: Let your hand be ready to help me, for I have chosen your precepts.

kikuyu: Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe ekuuga atĩrĩ, “Ndũrĩrĩ ciothe nĩikamwĩtaga andũ arathime, nĩgũkorwo bũrũri wanyu ũgaakorwo ũrĩ wa gĩkeno.”
english: and sent the work back to the owners. They on their part paid her wages, and also gave her a kid.

kikuyu: mũrũ wa Matathia, mũrũ wa Amosi, mũrũ wa Nahumu, mũrũ wa Esili, mũrũ wa Nagai,
english: the son of Mattathias, the son of Amos, the son of Nahum, the son of Esli, the son of Naggai,

kikuyu: Atongoria a Juda nĩndameerire mahaice rũthingo-igũrũ. Ningĩ ngĩhe ikundi igĩrĩ nene cia aini wĩra wa gũcookia ngaatho. Gĩkundi kĩmwe gĩgĩthiĩ mwena wa ũrĩo kĩrĩ rũthingo-igũrũ, kĩerekeire Kĩhingo gĩa Kĩara-inĩ.
english: Then I brought the princes of Judah up on the wall, and appointed two great companies who gave thanks and went in procession. One went on the right hand on the wall toward the dung gate;

kikuyu: Aca gũtitariĩ ta ũguo, ũhoro ũyũ nĩguo warĩtio nĩ mũnabii Joeli, akiuga atĩrĩ:
english: But this is what has been spoken through the prophet Joel:

kikuyu: Andũ arĩa akuũ matigoocaga Jehova, acio maikũrũkaga kũu ũkiri-inĩ;
english: I will offer to you the sacrifice of thanksgiving, and will call on Yahweh’s name.

kikuyu: Atĩrĩrĩ, wee thũ yakwa-rĩ, tiga kũngenerera! O na ingĩgũa gũtirĩ hĩndĩ itagookĩra! O na ingĩikara nduma-inĩ-rĩ, Jehova nĩwe ũrĩtuĩkaga ũtheri wakwa.
english: Don’t rejoice against me, my enemy. When I fall, I will arise. When I sit in darkness, Yahweh will be a light to me.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo arutwo makĩmenya atĩ aamaheaga ũhoro wa Johana Mũbatithania. Kũhonia Kamwana kaarĩ na Ndaimono
english: Then the disciples understood that he spoke to them of John the Baptizer.

kikuyu: Mũndũ o wothe ũrĩa wĩkaga ũũru nĩakarĩhwo kũringana na ũũru wake, na gũtirĩ gũtĩĩra mũndũ maũthĩ.
english: But he who does wrong will receive again for the wrong that he has done, and there is no partiality.

kikuyu: Nĩndakũgemirie na mathaga: ngĩgwĩkĩra bangiri moko, na mũgathĩ ngingo,
english: Tell those who plaster it with whitewash that it will fall. There will be an overflowing shower; and you, great hailstones, will fall. A stormy wind will tear it.

kikuyu: Ndũkanagĩe ũrata na mũndũ ũrĩa ũrĩ marakara mahiũ, ndũkanagĩe thiritũ na mũndũ ũrĩa ũhiũhaga kũrakara,
english: Don’t befriend a hot-tempered man. Don’t associate with one who harbors anger,

kikuyu: “Atĩrĩrĩ, nĩngakũnyiihia maitho-inĩ ma ndũrĩrĩ; nawe nĩũkameneka biũ.
english: Behold,§ I have made you small among the nations. You are greatly despised.

kikuyu: Akĩĩgũithia thĩ magũrũ-inĩ ma Jesũ, akĩmũcookeria ngaatho. Mũndũ ũcio aarĩ Mũsamaria.
english: He fell on his face at Jesus’ feet, giving him thanks; and he was a Samaritan.

kikuyu: na Baala, na Iimu, na Ezemu,
english: Baalah, Iim, Ezem,

kikuyu: Nayo ngumo ya maũndũ macio igĩkinyĩra andũ a kanitha ũrĩa warĩ kũu Jerusalemu; nao magĩtũma Baranaba athiĩ Antiokia. Rĩrĩa aakinyire na akĩona ũira wa wega wa Ngai, agĩkena na akĩmomĩrĩria marĩ othe, atĩ maikarage maathĩkĩire Mwathani na ngoro ciao ciothe.
english: The report concerning them came to the ears of the assembly which was in  The word for “brothers” here and where context allows may also be correctly translated “brothers and sisters” or “siblings.”  A Hellenist is someone who keeps Greek customs and culture.

kikuyu: Maakũũranirie Riiri wa Ngai na mũhianano wa ndegwa ĩrĩa ĩrĩĩaga nyeki.
english: Thus they exchanged their glory for an image of a bull that eats grass.

kikuyu: Karĩa kaambire gũciarwo kaarĩ gatune na mwĩrĩ wako wothe wahaanaga ta nguo ya maguoya; tondũ ũcio magĩgatua Esaũ.
english: The first came out red all over, like a hairy garment. They named him Esau.

kikuyu: O na angĩnjũraga-rĩ, niĩ nowe ndĩrĩĩhokaga; na ti-itherũ nĩ ngatetera mĩthiĩre yakwa hau mbere yake.
english: Behold, he will kill me. I have no hope. Nevertheless, I will maintain my ways before him.

kikuyu: O na gũtuĩka nĩmehĩtaga makoigaga atĩrĩ, ‘Ti-itherũ o ta ũrĩa Jehova atũũraga muoyo,’ kwĩhĩta kwao no kwa maheeni.”
english: Though they say, ‘As Yahweh lives,’ surely they swear falsely.”

kikuyu: 1 Nake Paũlũ agĩkũũrĩra athuuri a Kĩama kĩu maitho akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Ariũ a Baba, nĩndungatĩire Ngai ndĩ na thamiri ĩtarĩ ũcuuke nginya mũthenya wa ũmũthĩ.” Nake Anania mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai ũrĩa mũnene, agĩatha andũ arĩa maarũgamĩte hakuhĩ na Paũlũ mamũringe kanua.
english: 1 Paul, looking steadfastly at the council, said, “Brothers, I have lived before God in all good conscience until today.”

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo andũ a Makedonia na a Akaia nĩmoonire arĩ wega kũhotha nĩguo mateithie andũ arĩa athĩĩni a thiritũ ya andũ arĩa aamũre kũu Jerusalemu.
english: For it has been the good pleasure of Macedonia and Achaia to make a certain contribution for the poor among the saints who are at Jerusalem.

kikuyu: Angĩ nao matariĩ ta mbeũ iria ciagũire mĩigua-inĩ. Acio nĩ arĩa maiguaga ũhoro,
english: As he was entering into the boat, he who had been possessed by demons begged him that he might be with him.

kikuyu: Ikundi icio cierĩ cia aini cia gũcookia ngaatho igĩikara handũ ha cio thĩinĩ wa nyũmba ya Ngai, o na niĩ ngĩĩka o ũguo, hamwe na nuthu ya anene,
english: So the two companies of those who gave thanks in God’s house stood, and I and the half of the rulers with me;

kikuyu: Samaria na mũthamaki wakuo magaathererio ta karũhonge karĩ igũrũ wa maaĩ, maniinwo.
english: My people are determined to turn from me. Though they call to the Most High, he certainly won’t exalt them.

kikuyu: “Na rĩrĩ, ta thaithai Ngai nĩguo atũtuge. Rĩrĩa mũkũrehe maruta ta macio-rĩ, no amwĩtĩkĩre?” Ũguo nĩguo Jehova Mwene Hinya Wothe ekũũria.
english: “Now, please entreat the favor of God,‡ that he may be gracious to us. With this, will he accept any of you?” says Yahweh of Armies.

kikuyu: Akĩũria ũcio warĩ mũnene wa arangĩria mũthamaki atĩrĩ, “Nĩ kĩĩ gĩatũma mũthamaki atue itua rĩũru ũguo?” Nake Arioku agĩtaarĩria Danieli ũrĩa ũhoro ũcio wothe watariĩ.
english: At the end of ten days, their faces appeared fairer and they were fatter in flesh than all the youths who ate of the king’s delicacies.

kikuyu: Ũtheri ũcio nĩũtheraga nduma- inĩ, nayo nduma ndĩigana kũũhoota.
english: His mother said to the servants, “Whatever he says to you, do it.”

kikuyu: “Ĩra andũ a Isiraeli ũũ: ‘Rĩrĩa mũndũ o na ũrĩkũ angĩĩhia atekwenda, na eke ũndũ ũrĩa mũkananie thĩinĩ wa maathani mothe ma Jehova:
english: “Speak to the children of Israel, saying, ‘If anyone sins unintentionally, in any of the things which Yahweh has commanded not to be done, and does any one of them,

kikuyu: na kwarĩ na mairia o kahwaĩ-inĩ gũgĩtukatuka, gũkĩambĩrĩria kũgĩa nduma ya ũtukũ. Na rĩrĩ, hagĩũka mũndũ-wa-nja kũmũtũnga, wehumbĩte nguo ta mũmaraya, arĩ na wara ngoro-inĩ.
english: in the twilight, in the evening of the day, in the middle of the night and in the darkness.

kikuyu: Thũ yaku ĩngĩkorwo ĩhũtiĩ-rĩ, mĩhe irio ĩrĩe; ĩngĩkorwo ĩnyootiĩ-rĩ, mĩhe maaĩ ĩnyue.
english: If your enemy is hungry, give him food to eat. If he is thirsty, give him water to drink;

kikuyu: Namo maroho macio magĩcookanĩrĩria athamaki handũ harĩa hetagwo na Kĩhibirania, Arimage- doni. Nake mũraika wa mũgwanja akĩonoreria mbakũri yake rĩera-inĩ, na rĩrĩ, hekarũ thĩinĩ gũkiuma mũgambo mũnene uumĩte gĩtĩ-inĩ kĩa ũnene, ũkiuga atĩrĩ, “Ũhoro ũcio nĩwarĩĩka!”
english: The ten horns which you saw, they and the beast will hate the prostitute, will make her desolate, will strip her naked, will eat her flesh, and will burn her utterly with fire.

kikuyu: Githĩ Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe ndatuĩte atĩ wĩra wa andũ no ngũ cia gwakĩrĩria mwaki, na atĩ ndũrĩrĩ ciĩnogagia o tũhũ?
english: Behold, isn’t it from Yahweh of Armies that the peoples labor for the fire, and the nations weary themselves for vanity?

kikuyu: Nĩngweheria matigari ma Juda macio matuĩte atĩ nĩmegũthiĩ bũrũri wa Misiri magatũũre kuo. Othe magaathirĩra bũrũri wa Misiri; makooragwo na rũhiũ rwa njora, kana makue nĩ ngʼaragu, kuuma ũrĩa mũnini nginya ũrĩa mũnene, othe makaaniinwo na rũhiũ rwa njora kana ngʼaragu. Magaatuĩka o ta kĩndũ kĩrume na kĩndũ kĩrĩ magigi, o na kĩndũ gĩa kũmenwo na kĩa njono;
english: I will take the remnant of Judah that have set their faces to go into the land of Egypt to live there, and they will all be consumed. They will fall in the land of Egypt. They will be consumed by the sword and by the famine. They will die, from the least even to the greatest, by the sword and by the famine. They will be an object of horror, an astonishment, a curse, and a reproach.

kikuyu: Hwaĩ-inĩ ũcio, andũ aingĩ arĩa maarĩ na ndaimono makĩrehwo kũrĩ we, nake akĩingata maroho macio na kuuga o kiugo, na akĩhonia arĩa othe maarĩ arũaru.
english: By their fruits you will know them. Do you gather grapes from thorns or figs from thistles?

kikuyu: Harũni agĩĩka o ta ũguo; akĩbanga matawa macio amaroretie mwena ũrĩa ũtheri wamo watheragĩra hau mbere ya mũtĩ ũcio maaigagĩrĩrwo, o ta ũrĩa Jehova aathĩte Musa gwĩkwo.
english: Aaron did so. He lit its lamps to light the area in front of the lamp stand, as Yahweh commanded Moses.

kikuyu: O ta ũrĩa kwandĩkĩtwo atĩrĩ, “Rĩĩtwa rĩa Ngai nĩrĩcambagio thĩinĩ wa andũ- a-Ndũrĩrĩ nĩ ũndũ wanyu.”
english: For “the name of God is blasphemed among the Gentiles because of you,”* just as it is written.

kikuyu: Manyiitĩte mĩguĩ na matimũ; nĩ andũ ooru matarĩ tha. Mũrurumo wao magĩũka mahaicĩte mbarathi, nĩ ta wa ndiihũ cia iria rĩrĩa inene; maroka mehaarĩirie ta andũ moka mbaara nĩguo magũtharĩkĩre, wee Mwarĩ wa Zayuni.”
english: They take hold of bow and spear. They are cruel, and have no mercy. Their voice roars like the sea, and they ride on horses, everyone set in array, as a man to the battle, against you, daughter of Zion.”

kikuyu: Oohagĩria andũ njĩra-inĩ o ta njangiri, na nĩatũmaga kuongerereke andũ matarĩ ehokeku kĩrĩndĩ-inĩ.
english: Yes, she lies in wait like a robber, and increases the unfaithful among men.

kikuyu: nayo thĩ ĩcookerie ngano, na ndibei ya mũhihano, na maguta ũhoro, nacio nĩigacookeria Jezireeli ũhoro.
english: For wisdom is according to her name. She isn’t manifest to many.

kikuyu: We aaneanirwo oragwo nĩ ũndũ wa mehia maitũ, na agĩcooka akĩriũkio nĩgeetha tũtuuo athingu.
english: who was delivered up for our trespasses, and was raised for our justification.

kikuyu: Nyũmba ya Harũni nĩyuuge atĩrĩ, “Wendo wake nĩ wa tene na tene.”
english: We give no occasion of stumbling in anything, that our service may not be blamed,

kikuyu: Indo iria aarutire ciarĩ thaani ĩmwe ya betha ya ũritũ wa cekeri igana rĩa mĩrongo ĩtatũ na mbakũri ĩmwe ya betha ya kũminjaminjĩria ya ũritũ wa cekeri mĩrongo mũgwanja, cierĩ kũringana na gĩthimo gĩa cekeri ya harĩa haamũre, na o ĩmwe ĩiyũrĩtio mũtu ũrĩa mũhinyu mũno ũtukanĩtio na maguta, ũrĩ iruta rĩa mũtu; 56na thaani ĩmwe nene ya thahabu ya ũritũ wa cekeri ikũmi, ĩiyũrĩtio ũbumba;
english: gave his offering: one silver platter, the weight of which was one hundred thirty shekels, one silver bowl of seventy shekels, according to the shekel of the sanctuary, both of them full of fine flour mixed with oil for a meal offering;

kikuyu: Tiirai maitho manyu na igũrũ, na mũrore thĩ ĩrĩa ĩrĩ mũhuro; igũrũ rĩkaabuĩria o ta ndogo, nayo thĩ ĩthire o ta ũrĩa nguo ĩthiraga, nao atũũri ao makuage o ũguo ta ngi. No rĩrĩ, ũhonokio wakwa nĩwagũtũũra nginya tene, naguo ũthingu wakwa ndũrĩ hĩndĩ ũgaathira.
english: Lift up your eyes to the heavens, and look at the earth beneath; for the heavens will vanish away like smoke, and the earth will wear out like a garment. Its inhabitants will die in the same way, but my salvation will be forever, and my righteousness will not be abolished.

kikuyu: Thũmbĩ ya ũnene* nĩĩgwĩte, ĩgatuuma mũtwe, kaĩ tũrĩ na haaro-ĩ, nĩgũkorwo nĩtwĩhĩtie!
english: The crown has fallen from our head. Woe to us, for we have sinned!

kikuyu: Wee ũkoretwo ũrĩ rĩũrĩro rĩa athĩĩni, na rĩũrĩro rĩa abatari hĩndĩ ya mĩnyamaro yao, handũ ha kũũrĩra hĩndĩ ya kĩhuhũkanio, o na ta kĩĩruru gĩa kwĩyũa riũa. Nĩgũkorwo mĩhũmũ ya arĩa matarĩ tha ĩhaana kĩhuhũkanio kĩrĩa kĩgũthaga rũthingo na nditi,
english: For you have been a stronghold to the poor, a stronghold to the needy in his distress, a refuge from the storm, a shade from the heat, when the blast of the dreaded ones is like a storm against the wall.

kikuyu: Kĩrimũ kĩĩraga na ngoro atĩrĩ, “Gũtirĩ Ngai.” Nao amaramari, ciĩko ciao irĩ magigi; gũtirĩ mũndũ o na ũmwe wĩkaga wega.
english: Why do you boast of mischief, mighty man? God’s loving kindness endures continually.

kikuyu: Atĩrĩrĩ, inyuĩ kũndũ kũrĩa kwanangĩtwo kwa Jerusalemu, ambĩrĩriai kũina nyĩmbo cia gĩkeno, nĩgũkorwo Jehova nĩahooreretie andũ ake, na agakũũra Jerusalemu.
english: They made his grave with the wicked, and with a rich man in his death, although he had done no violence, nor was any deceit in his mouth.

kikuyu: Atĩrĩrĩ, inyuĩ andũ arĩa mũtũũraga thĩ, iguoyohia, na irima, o na mũtego nĩcio imwetereire.
english: Fear, the pit, and the snare are on you who inhabit the earth.

kikuyu: Wee wĩrahagĩra watho-rĩ, nĩũconorithagia Ngai na ũndũ wa kwagarara watho ũcio?
english: You who glory in the law, do you dishonor God by disobeying the law?

kikuyu: Nĩngakenaga ngĩmeeka wega, na ti-itherũ nĩngamahaanda gũkũ bũrũri ũyũ ngwatĩirie na ngoro yakwa yothe o na muoyo wakwa wothe.
english: Yes, I will rejoice over them to do them good, and I will plant them in this land assuredly with my whole heart and with my whole soul.”

kikuyu: Kuuma ndaaciarwo ndaikirio o harĩwe; kuuma ndoima nda ya maitũ ũkoretwo ũrĩ Mũrungu wakwa.
english: I was thrown on you from my mother’s womb. You are my God since my mother bore me.

kikuyu: Ngai mwene thayũ nĩarĩhehenja Shaitani o narua, na inyuĩ mũmũrangĩrĩrie rungu rwa magũrũ manyu. Wega wa Mwathani witũ Jesũ Kristũ ũroikara na inyuĩ.
english: yes, making it my aim to preach the Good News, not where Christ was already named, that I might not build on another’s foundation.

kikuyu: “Atĩrĩrĩ, inyuĩ andũ a rũciaro rũrũ, mwĩcũraniei wega ũhoro wa ndũmĩrĩri ĩno ya Jehova: “Kaĩ nduĩkĩte ta werũ harĩ Isiraeli, kana ta bũrũri ũrĩ nduma nene? Nĩ kĩĩ gĩtũmaga andũ akwa moige atĩrĩ, ‘Ithuĩ tũrĩĩkaga ũrĩa tũkwenda; tũtigagũcookerera rĩngĩ’?
english: Generation, consider Yahweh’s word. Have I been a wilderness to Israel? Or a land of thick darkness? Why do my people say, ‘We have broken loose. We will come to you no more’?

kikuyu: Ndakanatoonye handũ harĩ kĩimba. Ndakanethaahie o na aakorwo nĩ ũndũ wa ithe kana nyina,
english: He must not go in to any dead body, or defile himself for his father or for his mother.

kikuyu: Jehova ekuuga atĩrĩ: “Tigai kwĩruta mĩthiĩre ya ndũrĩrĩ, kana mũguoyohio nĩ morirũ ma kũũrĩa igũrũ, o na gũtuĩka ndũrĩrĩ icio nĩiguoyohetio nĩmo.
english: I have warned previously, and I warn again, as when I was present the second time, so now, being absent, I write to those who have sinned before now and to all the rest that if I come again, I will not spare,

kikuyu: atĩ mũndũ ũrĩa mwaganu nĩ aiguagĩrwo tha mũthenya wa mũtino, atĩ nĩahonokagio mũthenya wa mangʼũrĩ?
english: that the evil man is reserved to the day of calamity, that they are led out to the day of wrath?

kikuyu: Ĩtĩkĩra tha ciaku inginyĩre nĩguo ndũũre muoyo, nĩgũkorwo watho waku nĩguo ngenagĩra.
english: Let your tender mercies come to me, that I may live; for your law is my delight.

kikuyu: Nĩwahũũrire ũmaraya na andũ a Misiri arĩa mwariganĩtie nao, o acio maiyũrĩtwo nĩ merirĩria ma mwĩrĩ, ũgĩtũma ndakare nĩ ũndũ waku gũkĩrĩrĩria gũtharia. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio ngĩgũtambũrũkĩria guoko ngĩgũũkĩrĩra, ngĩnyiihia bũrũri waku; nĩndakũneanire moko-inĩ ma thũ ciaku, o acio airĩtu a Afilisti, arĩa maamakirio nĩ mĩtugo yaku ya ũũra-thoni.
english: You have also committed sexual immorality with the Egyptians, your neighbors, great of flesh; and have multiplied your prostitution, to provoke me to anger.

kikuyu: kana mwĩhĩte na thĩ, nĩgũkorwo nĩkuo gĩturwa gĩa kũigĩrĩra magũrũ make; o na kana mwĩhĩte na Jerusalemu, nĩgũkorwo nĩrĩo itũũra inene rĩa Mũthamaki mũnene. Na ndũkanehĩte na mũtwe waku, nĩgũkorwo ndũngĩhota gũtũma rũcuĩrĩ o na rũmwe rwerũhe kana rũire.
english: nor by the earth, for it is the footstool of his feet; nor by Jerusalem, for it is the city of the great King.

kikuyu: Mũthenya o mũthenya, marĩ hekarũ-inĩ o na marĩ mĩciĩ-inĩ, matiigana gũtiga kũrutana na kũhunjia ũhoro ũcio mwega atĩ Jesũ nĩwe Kristũ.
english: Every day, in the temple and at home, they never stopped teaching and preaching Jesus, the Christ.

kikuyu: “Ũũ nĩguo Jehova ekuuga: “ ‘Nĩngacookia ũgaacĩru wa Jakubu hema-inĩ ciake, nacio ciikaro ciake ndĩciiguĩre tha; itũũra rĩu inene nĩrĩgaakwo rĩngĩ o kũu rĩanangĩirwo, nayo nyũmba ya ũthamaki nĩĩkarũgama harĩa hamĩagĩrĩire.
english: Yahweh says: “Behold, I will reverse the captivity of Jacob’s tents, and have compassion on his dwelling places. The city will be built on its own hill, and the palace will be inhabited in its own place.

kikuyu: Ũtariĩ ta kĩndũ gĩa kũhahũra nyamũ gĩthecereirwo mũtĩ mũgũnda-inĩ wa marenge, mĩhianano yao ndĩngĩhota kwaria; ningĩ no nginya ĩkuuo, tondũ ndĩngĩhota gwĩtwara. Mũtikanamĩĩtigĩre; ndĩngĩhota gwĩka ũũru o na kana gwĩka wega o na ũrĩkũ.”
english: Examine your own selves, whether you are in the faith. Test your own selves. Or don’t you know about your own selves, that Jesus Christ is in you?—unless indeed you are disqualified.

kikuyu: Nyamũ ĩyo ĩgĩathana na ũhoti wothe wa nyamũ ĩrĩa ya mbere ithenya rĩayo, ĩgĩtũma thĩ na arĩa othe matũũraga kuo mahooyage nyamũ ĩyo ya mbere, o ĩyo yahonete iringa rĩayo rĩa kĩũrago.
english: He exercises all the authority of the first beast in his presence. He makes the earth and those who dwell in it to worship the first beast, whose fatal wound was healed.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo ndarehagĩra andũ mĩnyamaro, kana akarehere ciana cia andũ kĩeha akĩendaga. Rĩrĩa andũ a bũrũri arĩa othe mohetwo maarangĩrĩrio na makinya-rĩ,
english: For he does not afflict willingly, nor grieve the children of men.

kikuyu: Ariũ a Ezeri maarĩ: Bilihani, na Zaavani, na Akani.
english: These are the children of Ezer: Bilhan, Zaavan, and Akan.

kikuyu: hĩndĩ ĩyo kĩũngano kĩu no nginya kĩmũtuithanie ciira na mũrĩhanĩria wa thakame kũringana na mawatho maya.
english: then the congregation shall judge between the striker and the avenger of blood according to these ordinances.

kikuyu: inai na tũrumbeta, na mũgambo wa coro: anĩrĩrai na gĩkeno mũrĩ mbere ya Jehova, o ũcio Mũthamaki.
english: Moses and Aaron were among his priests, Samuel was among those who call on his name. They called on Yahweh, and he answered them.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo mũcaara wa mehia nĩ gĩkuũ, no kĩheo kĩrĩa Ngai aheanaga nĩ muoyo wa tene na tene thĩinĩ wa Kristũ Jesũ Mwathani witũ.
english: Now it was not written that it was accounted to him for his sake alone,

kikuyu: kũrĩ ũrĩa wĩtheragia, ũrĩĩonanagia ũrĩ mũtheru, no kũrĩ ũrĩa ũrĩ mĩthiĩre mĩogomu, ũrĩĩonanagia ũrĩ mũmũhutatĩri.
english: With the pure, you will show yourself pure. With the crooked you will show yourself shrewd.

kikuyu: no-o mamuona agereire maaĩ igũrũ magĩĩciiria nĩ ngoma moonaga. Magĩkaya
english: but they, when they saw him walking on the sea, supposed that it was a ghost, and cried out;

kikuyu: Mbeũ iria ingĩ ikĩgũa mĩigua-inĩ, nayo mĩigua ĩgĩkũra, ĩgĩthararia mbeũ icio ikĩaga gũciara.
english: Jesus withdrew to the sea with his disciples; and a great multitude followed him from Galilee, from Judea,

kikuyu: thũ cia mũndũ igaakorwo irĩ andũ a nyũmba yake kĩũmbe.’
english: A man’s foes will be those of his own household.*

kikuyu: Maya nĩmo maarĩ maruta ma atongoria a andũ a Isiraeli nĩ ũndũ wa kwamũrwo gwa kĩgongona rĩrĩa gĩaitagĩrĩrio maguta: ciarĩ thaani cia betha ikũmi na igĩrĩ na mbakũri cia betha cia kũminjam- injĩria ikũmi na igĩrĩ na thaani nene cia thahabu ikũmi na igĩrĩ.
english: This was the dedication offering of the altar, on the day when it was anointed, by the princes of Israel: twelve silver platters, twelve silver bowls, twelve golden ladles;

kikuyu: kuuma mũhĩrĩga wa Juda, aarĩ Kalebu mũrũ wa Jefune;
english: Of the tribe of Judah, Caleb the son of Jephunneh.

kikuyu: “Kũrathimwo-rĩ, nĩ inyuĩ hĩndĩ ĩrĩa andũ mekũmũruma, na makamũnyariira, na makamũigĩrĩra maũndũ mothe mooru nĩ ũndũ wakwa.
english: “Blessed are you when people reproach you, persecute you, and say all kinds of evil against you falsely, for my sake.

kikuyu: Ngai wa andũ a Isiraeli nĩathuurire maithe maitũ; agĩtũma andũ acio matheereme hĩndĩ ĩrĩa maatũũraga Misiri, na akĩmaruta bũrũri ũcio na hinya mũnene,
english: Yet he didn’t leave himself without witness, in that he did good and gave you‡ rains from the sky and fruitful seasons, filling our hearts with food and gladness.”

kikuyu: Ndũgatuĩke mũndũ wa kũiga kĩndũ gĩa kũrũgamĩrĩra thiirĩ, kana ũtuĩke wee nĩwe wa kũrũgamagĩrĩra mathiirĩ ma andũ angĩ;
english: Don’t you be one of those who strike hands, of those who are collateral for debts.

kikuyu: Ndũratũmwo kũrĩ andũ a mwarĩrie ũtooĩ kana rũthiomi rũritũ, no nĩ kũrĩ andũ a nyũmba ya Isiraeli;
english: For you are not sent to a people of a strange speech and of a hard language, but to the house of Israel—

kikuyu: o na amehererie mũndũ ũrĩa njamba na mũrũi wa mbaara, na mũtuanĩri ciira na mũnabii, na mũragũri na mũthuuri wa kĩama,
english: the mighty man, the man of war, the judge, the prophet, the diviner, the elder,

kikuyu: Ndĩ hamwe nawe na nĩngakũhonokia,’ ũguo nĩguo Jehova ekuuga. ‘O na ingĩniina ndũrĩrĩ ciothe cia kũrĩa guothe ngũharaganĩirie, wee ndigakũniina biũ. No nĩngakũherithia na kĩhooto; ndikaareka ũthiĩ o ro ũguo itakũherithĩtie.’
english: For I am with you, says Yahweh, to save you; for I will make a full end of all the nations where I have scattered you, but I will not make a full end of you; but I will correct you in measure, and will in no way leave you unpunished.”

kikuyu: Haandai bendera kũrigania na thingo cia Babuloni! Ongererai agitĩri, igai arangĩri, mũhaarĩrie andũ a kũhithũkĩra njĩra-inĩ! Jehova nĩekũhingia ũrĩa aciirĩire gwĩka, ahingie uuge wake wa kũrũmĩrĩrwo, wa gũũkĩrĩra andũ a Babuloni.
english: Now in the fifth month, in the tenth day of the month, which was the nineteenth year of King Nebuchadnezzar, king of Babylon, Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard, who stood before the king of Babylon, came into Jerusalem.

kikuyu: “Inyuĩ mũhaicaga ndigiri njerũ, mũikarĩire matandĩko manyu, na inyuĩ mũthiiaga na njĩra, cũraniai,
english: I said to you, “I am Yahweh your God. You shall not fear the gods of the Amorites, in whose land you dwell.” But you have not listened to my voice.’ ”

kikuyu: Ndũrĩrĩ nĩikoona na iconoke, itunyĩtwo hinya wacio wothe. Nĩikenyiita tũnua twacio, na ihingare matũ ciage kũigua.
english: The nations will see and be ashamed of all their might. They will lay their hand on their mouth. Their ears will be deaf.

kikuyu: Ti-itherũ, ũhoro wa Zayuni ũkĩario nĩgũkagwetagwo atĩrĩ, “Ũyũ o na ũũrĩa maaciarĩirwo kuo, na Ũrĩa-ũrĩ-Igũrũ-Mũno we mwene nĩagatũma gũtũũre kũrĩ kũrũmu.”
english: set apart among the dead, like the slain who lie in the grave, whom you remember no more. They are cut off from your hand.

kikuyu: Mbeeca icio cia gũkũũra andũ a Isiraeli acio makĩrĩte-rĩ, cinengere Harũni na ariũ ake.” Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Musa akĩũngania mbeeca cia gũkũũra andũ acio maakĩrĩte mũigana wa andũ arĩa maakũũrĩtwo nĩ Alawii.
english: and you shall give the money, with which their remainder is redeemed, to Aaron and to his sons.”

kikuyu: Maingatithie ũrĩ na marakara, na ũmaniine mathire gũkũ mũhuro wa igũrũ rĩa Jehova.
english: You will pursue them in anger, and destroy them from under the heavens of Yahweh.

kikuyu: Mũndũ nĩakenagĩra maciaro mega ma kuuma kanua gake, no mũndũ ũrĩa ũtarĩ mwĩhokeku eeriragĩria haaro.
english: By the fruit of his lips, a man enjoys good things, but the unfaithful crave violence.

kikuyu: Mũhĩrĩga wa Gadi nĩguo ũgũcookerera. Mũtongoria wa andũ a Gadi nĩ Eliasafu mũrũ wa Deueli.
english: “The tribe of Gad: the prince of the children of Gad shall be Eliasaph the son of Reuel.

kikuyu: Mũndũ o wothe ũrĩ muoyo-rĩ, atetaga nĩkĩ rĩrĩa ekũherithio nĩ ũndũ wa mehia make?
english: Why should a living man complain, a man for the punishment of his sins?

kikuyu: Wee nĩũũĩ ũrĩa Ngai aathaga matu, na ũrĩa atũmaga rũheni rwake rũhenũke?
english: Do you know how God controls them, and causes the lightning of his cloud to shine?

kikuyu: Mũhaka ũcio ũkĩambata ũrorete na mwena wa ithũĩro ũgĩkinya Marala, ũkĩhutia Dabeshethu na ũgĩtambũrũka ũgĩkinya karũũĩ karĩa kaarĩ hakuhĩ na Jokineamu.
english: Their border went up westward, even to Maralah, and reached to Dabbesheth. It reached to the brook that is before Jokneam.

kikuyu: Rekei tũhikanagie; mũtũheage airĩtu anyu na inyuĩ tũkamũheaga airĩtu aitũ.
english: Make marriages with us. Give your daughters to us, and take our daughters for yourselves.

kikuyu: Tondũ nĩũndakarĩire na rũngʼathio rwaku nĩrũkinyĩire matũ makwa, nĩngũgwĩkĩra gĩcũhĩ gĩakwa iniũrũ, na matamu ndĩmoohe kanua-inĩ gaku, na nĩngatũma ũcookere njĩra o ĩyo wokĩire.
english: Because of your raging against me, and because your arrogance has come up into my ears, therefore I will put my hook in your nose and my bridle in your lips, and I will turn you back by the way by which you came.

kikuyu: acooke aũtwarĩre ariũ a Harũni acio athĩnjĩri-Ngai. Mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai nĩakarũma ngundi ya mũtu ũcio mũhinyu mũno ũrĩ na maguta hamwe na ũbani ũcio wothe, na aũcinĩre igũrũ rĩa kĩgongona ũtuĩke gĩcunjĩ gĩa kĩririkano, ũrĩ iruta rĩa gũcinwo na mwaki, rĩrĩ na mũtararĩko mwega wa gũkenia Jehova.
english: He shall bring it to Aaron’s sons, the priests. He shall take his handful of its fine flour, and of its oil, with all its frankincense, and the priest shall burn its memorial on the altar, an offering made by fire, of a pleasant aroma to Yahweh.

kikuyu: Keriothu nĩgũgũtunyanwo, nacio ciĩhitho iria cia hinya kuo itahwo. Mũthenya ũcio-rĩ, ngoro cia njamba iria irĩ hinya cia Moabi igaatuĩka ta ngoro ya mũndũ-wa-nja akĩrũmwo.
english: Kerioth is taken, and the strongholds are seized. The heart of the mighty men of Moab at that day will be as the heart of a woman in her pangs.

kikuyu: Rũgamai mũkĩra-inĩ wa barabara mwĩrorere, inyuĩ arĩa mũtũũraga Aroeri. Hooyai kĩrĩra harĩ mũndũ ũcio ũrethara, o na harĩ mũndũ-wa-nja ũcio ũroora, mũmoorie atĩrĩ, ‘Kaĩ gwĩkĩkĩte atĩa?’
english: Inhabitant of Aroer, stand by the way and watch. Ask him who flees, and her who escapes; say, ‘What has been done?’

kikuyu: Magaatũũra kuo marĩ agitĩre, na nĩmagaka nyũmba, na mahaande mĩgũnda ya mĩthabibũ; nao magaatũũra marĩ agitĩre rĩrĩa ngaarehe iherithia kũrĩ andũ arĩa othe mariganĩtie nao arĩa maamacambagia. Hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩmakamenya atĩ niĩ nĩ niĩ Jehova Ngai wao.’ ” Na rĩrĩ, mwaka-inĩ wa ikũmi, mweri wa ikũmi, mũthenya wa ikũmi na ĩĩrĩ wa mweri wa ikũmi, kiugo kĩa Jehova nĩkĩanginyĩrĩire, ngĩĩrwo atĩrĩ:
english: They will dwell in it securely. Yes, they will build houses, plant vineyards, and will dwell securely when I have executed judgments on all those around them who have treated them with contempt. Then they will know that I am Yahweh their God.” ’ ”

kikuyu: Maarĩkia kuuma gatarũ-inĩ, o rĩmwe andũ makĩmenya Jesũ.
english: When they had come out of the boat, immediately the people recognized him,

kikuyu: Maathani maku mothe nĩ ma kwĩhokwo; ndeithia, nĩgũkorwo andũ maanyariiraga hatarĩ gĩtũmi.
english: All of your commandments are faithful. They persecute me wrongfully. Help me!

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ o ĩyo ngĩĩra andũ atĩrĩ, “Reke o mũndũ na mũteithia wake maikarage Jerusalemu thĩinĩ ũtukũ, nĩgeetha matũtungatagĩre ta arangĩri ũtukũ, na mũthenya makaruta wĩra.”
english: Likewise at the same time I said to the people, “Let everyone with his servant lodge within Jerusalem, that in the night they may be a guard to us, and may labor in the day.”

kikuyu: “Mũgambo wa kĩrĩro nĩũraiguuo kũu Babuloni; nĩ mũgambo wa kũniinanwo ũraiguuo kũu bũrũri wa andũ a Babuloni.
english: “The sound of a cry comes from Babylon, and of great destruction from the land of the Chaldeans!

kikuyu: Agĩcooka akĩmahe ngerekano ĩno, akĩmooria atĩrĩ, “Mũtumumu no atongorie mũtumumu ũrĩa ũngĩ? Githĩ eerĩ to magũe irima?
english: He spoke a parable to them. “Can the blind guide the blind? Won’t they both fall into a pit?

kikuyu: Tondũ wa ũguo ũũgĩ wa Ngai uugĩte atĩrĩ, ‘Nĩngamatũmĩra anabii na atũmwo, moorage amwe ao na manyariire arĩa angĩ.’ Nĩ ũndũ ũcio rũciaro rũrũ nĩrũgaciirithĩrio thakame ya anabii othe ĩrĩa yanaitwo kuuma o kĩambĩrĩria gĩa thĩ,
english: Therefore also the wisdom of God said, ‘I will send to them prophets and apostles; and some of them they will kill and persecute,

kikuyu: Matukũ marĩa magooka-rĩ, Jakubu nĩakagĩa na mĩri, Isiraeli agaathundũka arute kĩro, aiyũrie thĩ yothe maciaro.
english: In days to come, Jacob will take root. Israel will blossom and bud. They will fill the surface of the world with fruit.

kikuyu: O ihinda-inĩ rĩu arutwo ake magĩũka, na makĩgega maakora akĩaranĩria na mũtumia. No gũtirĩ o na ũmwe woririe atĩrĩ, “Nĩ kĩĩ ũrenda?” kana akĩũria, “Ũraaranĩria nake nĩkĩ?”
english: Just then, his disciples came. They marveled that he was speaking with a woman; yet no one said, “What are you looking for?” or, “Why do you speak with her?”

kikuyu: “Wee nawe nĩngũtuĩte mũgeria wa cuuma, nao andũ akwa matariĩ ta kĩgera, nĩguo wee ũrorage na ũthimage mĩthiĩre yao. Nĩ aremi momĩtie ngoro, mathiiaga magĩcambanagia. Mahaana o ta gĩcango na kĩgera; othe mekaga maũndũ mathũku mũno.
english: “I have made you a tester of metals and a fortress among my people, that you may know and try their way.

kikuyu: Nĩtwĩtĩkĩtie na tũkamenya atĩ wee nĩwe ũrĩa Mũtheru wa Ngai.”
english: We have come to believe and know that you are the Christ, the Son of the living God.”

kikuyu: Mĩromo yakwa nĩrĩanagĩrĩra nĩ gũkena rĩrĩa ngũina ngũgooce, o niĩ, ũrĩa Wee ũkũũrĩte.
english: My lips shall shout for joy! My soul, which you have redeemed, sings praises to you!

kikuyu: Jesũ agĩcooka akĩĩra mũndũ ũcio wamwĩtĩte atĩrĩ, “Waruga iruga rĩa mũthenya kana rĩa hwaĩ-inĩ- rĩ, ndũgetage o arata aku kana ariũ a thoguo, kana andũ anyu, o na kana andũ arĩa atongu a itũũra rĩanyu; ũngĩka ũguo-rĩ, nao nĩmagagwĩta na nĩ ũndũ ũcio makũrĩhe ũguo ũmekĩte. No hĩndĩ ĩrĩa waruga iruga ĩtaga athĩĩni, na cionje, na ithua, na atumumu,
english: He also said to the one who had invited him, “When you make a dinner or a supper, don’t call your friends, nor your brothers, nor your kinsmen, nor rich neighbors, or perhaps they might also return the favor, and pay you back.

kikuyu: “Nake agĩcookia atĩrĩ, ‘Aca, tondũ mũkĩmunya itindiĩ rĩu, mwahota kũrĩmunyanĩria na ngano.
english: He said, “Come!” Peter stepped down from the boat and walked on the waters to come to Jesus.

kikuyu: Andũ arĩa othe ehia thĩinĩ wa andũ akwa makooragwo na rũhiũ rwa njora, o arĩa othe moigaga atĩrĩ, ‘Tũtingĩkorererwo nĩ mũtino, kana ũtũtũnge.’ Gũcookio kwa Isiraeli
english: For the violence done to your brother Jacob, shame will cover you, and you will be cut off forever.

kikuyu: Mũgũnda wa ĩĩka ikũmi wa mĩthabibũ ũkaaruta bathi ĩmwe* ya ndibei, nayo homeri ĩmwe† ya mbegũ yume eba ĩmwe‡ ya ngano.”
english: Make the heart of this people fat. Make their ears heavy, and shut their eyes; lest they see with their eyes, hear with their ears, understand with their heart, and turn again, and be healed.”

kikuyu: Wee igũrũ, anĩrĩra nĩ gũkena; nawe thĩ ũcanjamũke, na inyuĩ irĩma-rĩ, inai rwĩmbo! Nĩgũkorwo Jehova nĩahooreretie andũ ake, nao andũ ake arĩa anyariire akamaiguĩra tha. No rĩrĩ, Zayuni oigire atĩrĩ, “Jehova nĩandiganĩirie, Mwathani nĩariganĩirwo nĩ niĩ.”
english: Therefore we have been comforted. In our comfort we rejoiced the more exceedingly for the joy of Titus, because his spirit has been refreshed by you all.

kikuyu: Niĩ nĩnjĩtĩkĩtie ũũ: atĩ nĩngeyonera wega wa Jehova ndĩ bũrũri wa arĩa marĩ muoyo.
english: I am still confident of this: I will see the goodness of Yahweh in the land of the living.

kikuyu: “Oya Alawii ũmeherie kuuma kũrĩ andũ arĩa angĩ a Isiraeli, ũmatherie mathirwo nĩ thaahu.
english: “Take the Levites from among the children of Israel, and cleanse them.

kikuyu: Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe nĩakamarathima akiugaga atĩrĩ, “Kũrathimwo-rĩ, nĩ andũ akwa a Misiri, o na Aashuri arĩa ndeyũmbĩire, na Isiraeli igai rĩakwa.”
english: because Yahweh of Armies has blessed them, saying, “Blessed be Egypt my people, Assyria the work of my hands, and Israel my inheritance.”

kikuyu: Kuuma kũu thĩinĩ wa mbĩrĩra, atongoriaarĩamarĩhinyanĩmakaaragiaũhoroũkoniĩbũrũriwaMisirinaarĩamarĩngwatanĩronaguo, moige atĩrĩ, ‘Nĩmaikũrũkĩtio, na makometio hamwe na arĩa mataruĩte, o hamwe na arĩa maanooragwo na rũhiũ rwa njora.’
english: The strong among the mighty will speak to him out of the middle of Sheol† with those who help him. They have gone down. The uncircumcised lie still, slain by the sword.

kikuyu: Wee mwene mũrĩ na andũ aku-rĩ, mũgũkĩĩninithia nĩkĩ na rũhiũ rwa njora, na ngʼaragu, o na mũthiro, o icio Jehova atuĩrĩire rũrĩrĩ o ruothe rũrĩa rũtagatungatĩra mũthamaki wa Babuloni?
english: Now therefore amend your ways and your doings, and obey Yahweh your God’s voice; then Yahweh will relent from the evil that he has pronounced against you.

kikuyu: no ũndikirie irima rĩa gĩcoro nĩgeetha o na nguo ciakwa iithũũre.
english: yet you will plunge me in the ditch. My own clothes will abhor me.

kikuyu: Nginyagia rĩu mũtirĩ mwahooya ũndũ thĩinĩ wa rĩĩtwa rĩakwa. Hooyai na nĩmũkũheo, nĩguo gĩkeno kĩanyu gĩkinyanĩre.
english: Until now, you have asked nothing in my name. Ask, and you will receive, that your joy may be made full.

kikuyu: “Ambata, ũthiĩ Lebanoni, ũkanĩrĩre kuo, ũreke mũgambo waku ũiguĩke kũu Bashani, na ũkayũrũrũke kuuma Abarimu, nĩgũkorwo athiritũ aku othe nĩahehenje.
english: “Go up to Lebanon, and cry out. Lift up your voice in Bashan, and cry from Abarim; for all your lovers have been destroyed.

kikuyu: Nĩngũkũhonokia kuuma kũrĩ andũ anyu na kuuma kũrĩ andũ-a- Ndũrĩrĩ arĩa ndĩragũtũma kũrĩ o,
english: When therefore they had come together here, I didn’t delay, but on the next day sat on the judgment seat and commanded the man to be brought.

kikuyu: Andũ aingĩ nĩmathiire na mbere kũngana harĩ Jesũ moimĩte matũũra-inĩ, na rĩrĩa andũ maingĩhire mũno akĩmahe ũhoro na ngerekano, akĩmeera atĩrĩ,
english: When a great multitude came together and people from every city were coming to him, he spoke by a parable:

kikuyu: nake Ramu aarĩ ithe wa Aminadabu, nake Aminadabu aarĩ ithe wa Nahashoni, nake Nahashoni aarĩ ithe wa Salimoni,
english: Ram became the father of Amminadab. Amminadab became the father of Nahshon. Nahshon became the father of Salmon.

kikuyu: Tondũ wa maũndũ macio, mangʼũrĩ ma Ngai nĩmarooka.
english: Let your speech always be with grace, seasoned with salt, that you may know how you ought to answer each one.

kikuyu: Nowe Herode rĩrĩa aiguire ũhoro ũcio, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Johana, mũndũ ũrĩa ndaatinithirie mũtwe-rĩ, nĩariũkĩte kuuma kũrĩ arĩa akuũ!”
english: But Herod, when he heard this, said, “This is John, whom I beheaded. He has risen from the dead.”

kikuyu: Jehova, Mũhonokia wanyu, o Ũrĩa Mũtheru wa Isiraeli, ekuuga atĩrĩ, “Nĩngatũma mbũtũ ya ita yũkĩrĩre Babuloni nĩ ũndũ wanyu, na ndehe andũ othe a Babuloni marĩ mĩgwate, maikũrũkĩtio na marikabu iria makenagio nĩcio.
english: He cuts down cedars for himself, and takes the cypress and the oak, and strengthens for himself one among the trees of the forest. He plants a cypress tree, and the rain nourishes it.

kikuyu: ndũmĩrĩri ya mũndũ ũrĩa ũiguaga ciugo cia Ngai, ũrĩa ũrĩ na ũmenyo uumĩte kũrĩ Ũrĩa-ũrĩ-Igũrũ-Mũno, ũrĩa wonaga kĩoneki kuuma kũrĩ Mwene-Hinya-Wothe, ũrĩa ũgũũaga agakoma thĩ, namo maitho make makahingũka, ekuuga ũũ:
english: Yahweh met Balaam, and put a word in his mouth, and said, “Return to Balak, and say this.”

kikuyu: Agethi a thabibũ mangĩũka kũrĩ we-rĩ, githĩ to matigie tũthabibũ tũnini? Aici mangĩũka ũtukũ-rĩ, githĩ matingĩiya o indo iria imabatarĩtie? No rĩrĩ, niĩ nĩngaguũria Esaũ nguo atigwo njaga, nĩngaguũria ciĩhitho ciake, nĩguo ndakanacooke kuona gwa kwĩhitha. Ciana ciake, na andũ a nyũmba yao, na andũ a itũũra rĩake nĩmakaniinwo, na o nake ndagatigara.
english: For, behold, I will stir up and cause to come up against Babylon a company of great nations from the north country; and they will set themselves in array against her. She will be taken from there. Their arrows will be as of an expert mighty man. None of them will return in vain.

kikuyu: Ariũ a Biliha ndungata ya mũirĩtu ya Rakeli maarĩ: Dani na Nafitali.
english: The sons of Bilhah (Rachel’s servant): Dan and Naphtali.

kikuyu: Angĩkorwo mũciĩ ũrĩa njĩrĩgĩrĩire no mbĩrĩra, angĩkorwo ingĩara ũrĩrĩ wakwa o nduma-inĩ-rĩ,
english: If I look for Sheol† as my house, if I have spread my couch in the darkness,  Sheol is the place of the dead.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo maitho makwa nĩmonete ũhonokio waku,
english: The angel said to her, “Don’t be afraid, Mary, for you have found favor with God.

kikuyu: Angĩkorwo harĩ na ũndũ ũngĩ mũngĩenda kuuga makĩria ma ũguo, ũcio no ũciirĩirwo kĩama-inĩ kĩa watho.
english: But if you seek anything about other matters, it will be settled in the regular assembly.

kikuyu: Nacio igĩeterera o nginya igĩconoka, no rĩrĩa aagire kũhingũra mĩrango ya nyũmba, ikĩoya hingũro ikĩhingũra mĩrango. Na hau ikĩona mũnene wao agũĩte thĩ arĩ mũkuũ. Na rĩrĩ, hĩndĩ ĩyo cietereire ihingũrĩrwo-rĩ, Ehudu akĩĩyũrĩra. Akĩhĩtũkĩra mĩhianano-inĩ akĩũrĩra Seira.
english: They waited until they were ashamed; and behold, he didn’t open the doors of the upper room. Therefore they took the key and opened them, and behold, their lord had fallen down dead on the floor.

kikuyu: akĩniina ndũrĩrĩ mũgwanja kũu Kaanani naguo bũrũri wacio akĩũhe andũ aake ũtuĩke igai rĩao.
english: But some Jews from Antioch and Iconium came there, and having persuaded the multitudes, they stoned Paul and dragged him out of the city, supposing that he was dead.

kikuyu: Nĩamwarĩirie na ũhooreri, na akĩmũhe gĩtĩ gĩa kũmũtũgĩria gũkĩra athamaki acio angĩ maarĩ nao kũu Babuloni. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Jekonia akĩruta nguo ciake cia njeera, na agĩtũũra arĩĩanagĩra irio na mũthamaki matukũ mothe marĩa aatũũrire muoyo.
english: He spoke kindly to him, and set his throne above the throne of the kings who were with him in Babylon,

kikuyu: Ũngĩka ũrĩa kwagĩrĩire-rĩ, no wage gwĩtĩkĩrĩka? No ũngĩaga gwĩka ũrĩa kwagĩrĩire-rĩ, mehia makuoheirie mũrango-inĩ waku makĩenda gũgwatha; no wee no nginya ũmatoorie.”
english: If you do well, won’t it be lifted up? If you don’t do well, sin crouches at the door. Its desire is for you, but you are to rule over it.”

kikuyu: “Mũndũ aahota kũguna Mũrungu? O na mũndũ mũũgĩ-rĩ, akĩhota kũmũguna? Nĩ gĩkeno kĩrĩkũ ũthingu waku ũngĩrehere Mwene-Hinya-Wothe? Nĩ uumithio ũrĩkũ angĩgĩa naguo angĩkorwo mĩthiĩre yaku ndĩrĩ ũcuuke?
english: “Can a man be profitable to God? Surely he who is wise is profitable to himself.

kikuyu: O na angĩkorwo Iburahĩmu ndatũũĩ, nake Isiraeli nĩatũkaanĩte-rĩ, Wee nĩwe Ithe witũ; Wee Jehova-rĩ, nĩwe Ithe witũ, narĩo rĩĩtwa rĩaku nĩ Mũkũũri witũ kuuma tene.
english: For you are our Father, though Abraham doesn’t know us, and Israel does not acknowledge us. You, Yahweh, are our Father. Our Redeemer from everlasting is your name.

kikuyu: Wee Mũrungu, thuthuuria, ũmenye ngoro yakwa; ngeria ũmenye meciiria makwa.
english: Search me, God, and know my heart. Try me, and know my thoughts.

kikuyu: Tondũ ũcio Jesũ, o akĩrutanaga kũu hekarũ-inĩ, akĩanĩrĩra, akiuga atĩrĩ; “Ĩĩ nĩguo, nĩmũnjũũĩ, na mũkamenya kũrĩa nyumĩte. Ndiũkĩte gũkũ nĩ ũndũ wakwa niĩ mwene, no ũrĩa wandũmire nĩ wa ma. Inyuĩ mũtimũũĩ,
english: Jesus therefore said to them, “When you have lifted up the Son of Man, then you will know that I am he, and I do nothing of myself, but as my Father taught me, I say these things.

kikuyu: “ ‘Mũndũ angĩkeendia nyũmba ĩrĩ itũũra-inĩ inene rĩirigĩre, nĩarĩkoragwo arĩ na kĩhooto gĩa kũmĩkũũra ihinda rĩa mwaka mũgima kuuma amĩendia. Thĩinĩ wa ihinda rĩu no amĩkũũre.
english: “ ‘If a man sells a dwelling house in a walled city, then he may redeem it within a whole year after it has been sold. For a full year he shall have the right of redemption.

kikuyu: Ĩĩ-ni, o na nĩngũkũũria, wee mũrata wakwa wa ma, wee tũrutithanagia wĩra ta tuohetwo icooki rĩmwe, teithagia atumia acio, arĩa maarũĩrĩire Ũhoro-ũrĩa-Mwega marĩ hamwe na niĩ, o na Klementi na andũ arĩa angĩ marutithanagia wĩra hamwe na niĩ, arĩa marĩĩtwa mao mandĩkĩtwo ibuku-inĩ rĩa muoyo.
english: Yes, I beg you also, true partner, help these women, for they labored with me in the Good News with Clement also, and the rest of my fellow workers, whose names are in the book of life.

kikuyu: nake Nebukadinezaru akĩmooria atĩrĩ, “Nĩ ũhoro wa ma wee Shadiraka, na Meshaki, na Abedinego, atĩ mũtitungatagĩra ngai ciakwa, o na kana mũkahooya mũhianano wa thahabu ũcio handithĩtie?
english: Nebuchadnezzar answered them, “Is it true, Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego, that you don’t serve my gods and you don’t worship the golden image which I have set up?

kikuyu: Magĩĩta Baranaba Zeu,* nake Paũlũ makĩmwĩta Herime tondũ nĩwe warĩ mwaria ũrĩa mũnene.
english: All the multitude kept silence, and they listened to Barnabas and Paul reporting what signs and wonders God had done among the nations through them.

kikuyu: “Mũndũ ũtarĩ na thaahu acooke ahakũre mũhu ũcio wa moori ĩyo na aũige handũ hatarĩ na thaahu nja ya kambĩ. Mũhu ũcio ũigagwo nĩ kĩrĩndĩ kĩa andũ a Isiraeli, nĩgeetha ũhũthagĩrwo maaĩ-inĩ ma kũniina thaahu; ũcio nĩguo wa gũtheranagia kuuma mehia-inĩ.
english: “A man who is clean shall gather up the ashes of the heifer, and lay them up outside of the camp in a clean place; and it shall be kept for the congregation of the children of Israel for use in water for cleansing impurity. It is a sin offering.

kikuyu: Maitho makwa nĩmeyoneire gũtoorio gwa thũ ciakwa; matũ makwa nĩmeiguĩrĩire ũrĩa thũ ciakwa icio njaganu itooretio.
english: Yahweh knows the thoughts of man, that they are futile.

kikuyu: Athamaki nĩmakenagĩra mĩromo ĩrĩa yaragia ma; nĩ marĩ bata na mũndũ waragia ũhoro wa ma.
english: Whoever rewards evil for good, evil shall not depart from his house.

kikuyu: namo nĩ ndungata ĩtuĩkĩte mũthamaki, na mũndũ mũkĩĩgu ahũũnĩte irio,
english: For a servant when he is king, a fool when he is filled with food,

kikuyu: Andũ a mũhĩrĩga wa Rubeni, na wa Gadi, na nuthu ya Manase makĩringa mũrĩmo, meeohete indo cia mbaara, marĩ mbere ya andũ a Isiraeli, o ta ũrĩa Musa aamaathĩte.
english: Now therefore, please swear to me by Yahweh, since I have dealt kindly with you, that you also will deal kindly with my father’s house, and give me a true sign;

kikuyu: Petero akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Mbeeca ciaku irothiranĩra hamwe nawe, nĩgũkorwo wĩciirĩtie no ũgũre kĩheo kĩa Ngai na mbeeca.
english: But Peter said to him, “May your silver perish with you, because you thought you could obtain the gift of God with money!

kikuyu: Wee Jehova-rĩ, gũkaya gwakwa kũrokinya harĩwe; he gũtaũkĩrwo kũringana na kiugo gĩaku.
english: Let my cry come before you, Yahweh. Give me understanding according to your word.

kikuyu: O na thuutha wa Jesũ kũringa ciama icio ciothe makĩonaga-rĩ, matiigana kũmwĩtĩkia.
english: But though he had done so many signs before them, yet they didn’t believe in him,

kikuyu: nake nĩagathamakĩra nyũmba ya Jakubu nginya tene; ũthamaki wake ndũgaathira o na rĩ.”
english: and he will reign over the house of Jacob forever. There will be no end to his Kingdom.”

kikuyu: Jehova, o we Ngai wa Isiraeli, arogoocwo, kuuma tene wa tene nginya tene na tene. Ameni, na ningĩ Ameni. Ibuku rĩa Keerĩ
english: Blessed be Yahweh, the God of Israel, from everlasting and to everlasting! Amen and amen. BOOK

kikuyu: Ũrute Harũni nguo ciake, ũcihumbe mũriũ Eleazaru, nĩ ũndũ Harũni nĩegũkua, na egũkuĩra o ro kũu.”
english: and strip Aaron of his garments, and put them on Eleazar his son. Aaron shall be gathered, and shall die there.”

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa ciũmbe icio ciathiĩ, o namo magũrũ ma cio magathiĩ; rĩrĩa ciũmbe icio ciarũgama, o namo makarũgama; na rĩrĩa ciũmbe icio ciambata na igũrũ ciumĩte thĩ, magũrũ macio makambatania nacio, tondũ roho wa ciũmbe icio irĩ muoyo warĩ thĩinĩ wa magũrũ macio.
english: When those went, these went. When those stood, these stood. When those were lifted up from the earth, the wheels were lifted up beside them; for the spirit of the living creature was in the wheels.

kikuyu: Nacio irio icio ciagĩrĩire kũigwo irĩ mũthiithũ wa bũrũri, ikaahũthĩrwo hĩndĩ ĩrĩa ya mĩaka mũgwanja ya ngʼaragu ĩrĩa ĩkaagĩa Misiri, nĩgeetha bũrũri ndũkanathũkio nĩ ngʼaragu ĩyo.”
english: The food will be to supply the land against the seven years of famine, which will be in the land of Egypt; so that the land will not perish through the famine.”

kikuyu: O gĩtugĩ kĩmwe gĩacio kĩarĩ kĩa mĩkono ikũmi na ĩnana* kũraiha, na mĩkono ikũmi na ĩĩrĩ† gũthiũrũrũka; na o kĩmwe gĩacio kĩarĩ na ũtungu wa ciara inya, na kĩarĩ na irima gatagatĩ.
english: As for the pillars, the height of the one pillar was eighteen cubits;† and a line of twelve cubits encircled it; and its thickness was four fingers. It was hollow.

kikuyu: Ũhoro ũcio ũkĩoneka ũrĩ mwega nĩ Hamoru na mũriũ Shekemu.
english: As her soul was departing (for she died), she named him Benoni,† but his father named him Benjamin.‡

kikuyu: Mĩrũũthi no yage gĩa kũrĩa ĩhũte, no arĩa marongoragia Jehova gũtirĩ kĩndũ kĩega maagaga.
english: The young lions do lack, and suffer hunger, but those who seek Yahweh shall not lack any good thing.

kikuyu: Areeta igũrũ na thĩ nĩguo ituĩke aira agĩciirithia andũ ake:
english: He calls to the heavens above, to the earth, that he may judge his people:

kikuyu: na Aarivadi, na Azemari, na Ahamathi. Thuutha ũcio mĩhĩrĩga ya Kaanani ĩkĩhurunjũka,
english: the Arvadites, the Zemarites, and the Hamathites. Afterward the families of the Canaanites were spread abroad.

kikuyu: Hĩ anga mũndũ ũcio wĩtagwo Jekonia, nĩ nyũngũ nguũ na nyarare, na kĩndũ gĩtarĩ mũndũ ũngĩkĩenda? Nĩ kĩĩ gĩgũtũma we hamwe na ciana ciake manyugutwo, na maikio bũrũri matooĩ?
english: Is this man Coniah a despised broken vessel? Is he a vessel in which no one delights? Why are they cast out, he and his offspring, and cast into a land which they don’t know?

kikuyu: Agĩcooka akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Ngoro yakwa ĩrĩ na kĩeha mũno, o hakuhĩ gũkua. Ikarai haha mwĩiguĩte hamwe na niĩ.”
english: Then Jesus said to them, “My soul is exceedingly sorrowful, even to death. Stay here and watch with me.”

kikuyu: O ta ũrĩa riũa na ũrugarĩ iniinaga maaĩ ma tharunji-rĩ, ũguo noguo mbĩrĩra ĩhuragia arĩa mehĩtie.
english: Drought and heat consume the snow waters, so does Sheol† those who have sinned.

kikuyu: ‘Nĩigatwarwo Babuloni, na igaikara kũu nginya mũthenya ũrĩa ngooka gũcigĩĩra,’ ũguo nĩguo Jehova ekuuga. ‘Hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩngacirehe ndĩcicookie gũkũ.’ ”
english: Then Jehoiakim the king sent Elnathan the son of Achbor and certain men with him into Egypt.

kikuyu: Inyuĩ ene nĩmwĩoneire maũndũ marĩa mothe Jehova Ngai wanyu ekĩte ndũrĩrĩ ici ciothe Nĩ ũndũ wanyu, nĩgũkorwo nĩ Jehova Ngai wanyu wamũrũĩrĩire.
english: You have seen all that Yahweh your God has done to all these nations because of you; for it is Yahweh your God who has fought for you.

kikuyu: “Elamu arĩ o kũu, na kĩrĩndĩ gĩake gĩothe kĩrigiicĩirie mbĩrĩra yake. Othe nĩmoragĩtwo, makoor- agwo na rũhiũ rwa njora. Arĩa othe maiyũrĩtie bũrũri wa arĩa marĩ muoyo guoya maaharũrũkirio thĩ kũrĩa kũriku matarĩ aruu. Maikaraga maconokete marĩ hamwe na arĩa maharũrũkĩtio irima.
english: “There is Elam and all her multitude around her grave; all of them slain, fallen by the sword, who have gone down uncircumcised into the lower parts of the earth, who caused their terror in the land of the living, and have borne their shame with those who go down to the pit.

kikuyu: Thuutha wa ũguo Joshua agĩakĩra Jehova, o we Ngai wa Isiraeli, kĩgongona o kũu kĩrĩma-inĩ kĩa Ebali,
english: Then Joshua built an altar to Yahweh, the God of Israel, on Mount Ebal,

kikuyu: amu Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe nĩagooka * 29:1 Arieli nĩ rĩĩtwa rĩngĩ rĩa Jerusalemu.
english: Then he appeared to over five hundred brothers at once, most of whom remain until now, but some have also fallen asleep.

kikuyu: Ndeciiririe ũhoro wa matukũ ma tene, ngĩririkana mĩaka o ĩrĩa yarĩ ya tene;
english: I have considered the days of old, the years of ancient times.

kikuyu: Niĩ mwene nĩthuurĩte Alawii athiritũ anyu kuuma thĩinĩ
english: For I am already being offered, and the time of my departure has come.

kikuyu: Ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ, ũguo noguo kũgĩaga gĩkeno kĩnene igũrũ nĩ ũndũ wa mũndũ ũmwe mwĩhia wĩrirĩte, gũkĩra ũrĩa gũkenagwo nĩ ũndũ wa andũ mĩrongo kenda na kenda athingu arĩa matabatarĩtio nĩ kwĩrira.
english: He spoke a parable to those who were invited, when he noticed how they chose the best seats, and said to them,

kikuyu: Ndarĩ haha; nĩariũkĩte! Ririkanai ũrĩa aamwĩrire hĩndĩ ĩrĩa aarĩ na inyuĩ Galili atĩ:
english: He isn’t here, but is risen. Remember what he told you when he was still in Galilee,

kikuyu: Ingĩ nacio ikĩgũa tĩĩri-inĩ mũnoru. Ikĩmera, igĩkũra na igĩciara maciaro, imwe ikiumia maita mĩrongo ĩtatũ, ingĩ maita mĩrongo ĩtandatũ, o na ingĩ maita igana.”
english: from Jerusalem, from Idumaea, beyond the Jordan, and those from around Tyre and Sidon. A great multitude, hearing what great things he did, came to him.

kikuyu: Atĩrĩrĩ, wee mwarĩ wa Galimu, uga mbu! Wee Laisha, thikĩrĩria! Nawe Anathothu, wũi-ĩ!
english: Cry aloud with your voice, daughter of Gallim! Listen, Laishah! You poor Anathoth!

kikuyu: “Nĩ ũndũ ũcio-rĩ, mũndũ o wothe ũiguaga ciugo ici ciakwa na ageeka ũrĩa ciugĩte, ahaana ta mũndũ mũũgĩ ũrĩa wakire nyũmba yake igũrũ rĩa rwaro rwa ihiga.
english: “Everyone therefore who hears these words of mine and does them, I will liken him to a wise man who built his house on the rock.

kikuyu: “Ĩtĩkĩra indo icio kuuma kũrĩ o, nĩguo ihũthĩrwo wĩra-inĩ wa Hema-ya-Gũtũnganwo. Ũciheane kũrĩ Alawii o ta ũrĩa wĩra wa mũndũ o mũndũ ũkũbatara.” Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Musa akĩoya ngaari icio na ndegwa icio agĩciheana kũrĩ Alawii.
english: “Accept these from them, that they may be used in doing the service of the Tent of Meeting; and you shall give them to the Levites, to every man according to his service.”

kikuyu: Ngaamarĩhia maita meerĩ nĩ ũndũ wa waganu wao na mehia mao, nĩ ũndũ nĩmathaahĩtie bũrũri wakwa na mĩhaano ĩtarĩ muoyo mĩmeneku mũno, na makaiyũria igai rĩakwa na mĩhianano ĩrĩ magigi.”
english: First I will recompense their iniquity and their sin double, because they have polluted my land with the carcasses of their detestable things, and have filled my inheritance with their abominations.”

kikuyu: Jehova, o we Ngai wa igũrũ ũrĩa wandutire kuuma nyũmba ya baba na kuuma bũrũri ũrĩa ndaciarĩirwo na akĩnjarĩria na akĩnjĩĩra na mwĩhĩtwa atĩrĩ, ‘Rũciaro rwaku nĩngarũhe bũrũri ũyũ’ we nĩwe ũgaatũma mũraika wake mbere yaku nĩguo ũkoonera mũrũ wakwa mũtumia kuuma kuo.
english: Yahweh, the God of heaven—who took me from my father’s house, and from the land of my birth, who spoke to me, and who swore to me, saying, ‘I will give this land to your offspring—he will send his angel before you, and you shall take a wife for my son from there.

kikuyu: Wee ũhonokagia andũ arĩa enyiihia, no arĩa marĩ maitho ma mwĩtĩĩo ũkamaconorithia.
english: For you will save the afflicted people, but the arrogant eyes you will bring down.

kikuyu: Nĩmũũragĩte andũ aingĩ itũũra-inĩ rĩĩrĩ inene, na mũkaiyũria ciimba njĩra-inĩ ciarĩo.
english: You have multiplied your slain in this city, and you have filled its streets with the slain.”

kikuyu: ‘Nĩ ũndũ ũcio nĩmũgatuĩka andũ akwa, na niĩ nduĩke Ngai wanyu.’ ”
english: “You shall be my people, and I will be your God.

kikuyu: O mũndũ agaatuĩka ta handũ ha kwĩgitia rũhuho, na ha kwĩyũa mbura ya kĩhuhũkanio, o na atuĩke ta tũrũũĩ twa maaĩ tũgũtherera werũ-inĩ, o na ta kĩĩruru kĩa rwaro rũnene rwa ihiga bũrũri-inĩ ũrĩ na nyoota.
english: A man shall be as a hiding place from the wind, and a covert from the storm, as streams of water in a dry place, as the shade of a large rock in a weary land.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo guoko gwaku nĩ kwanditũhagĩra mũthenya na ũtukũ; hinya wakwa wathirire o ta ũrĩa ime rĩthiraga hĩndĩ ya riũa.
english: For Yahweh’s word is right. All his work is done in faithfulness.

kikuyu: Makĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Twĩre wĩkaga maũndũ maya na ũhoti ũrĩkũ. Nũũ wakũheire ũhoti ũcio?” Nake akĩmacookeria atĩrĩ, “O na niĩ nĩngũmũũria kĩũria.
english: They asked him, “Tell us: by what authority do you do these things? Or who is giving you this authority?”

kikuyu: nake eekire ũguo nĩgeetha onanie ũthingu wake mahinda-inĩ maya, na kũmenyeke atĩ we nĩ mũthingu, na nĩwe ũtuaga andũ arĩa metĩkĩtie Jesũ athingu.
english: to demonstrate his righteousness at this present time, that he might himself be just and the justifier of him who has faith in Jesus.

kikuyu: Wathani waku warĩ o kũu iria gatagatĩ; aaki aku nĩo maatũmire ũthaka waku ũkinyanĩre.
english: They will destroy the walls of Tyre, and break down her towers. I will also scrape her dust from her, and make her a bare rock.

kikuyu: Matiaririkanire ũhoti wake: mũthenya ũrĩa aamakũũrire kuuma kũrĩ arĩa maamahinyagĩrĩria,
english: They didn’t remember his hand, nor the day when he redeemed them from the adversary;

kikuyu: Kuuma mũthenya ũcio gũthiĩ na mbere, andũ a nyũmba ya Isiraeli nĩmakamenya atĩ niĩ nĩ niĩ Jehova Ngai wao.
english: So the house of Israel will know that I am Yahweh their God, from that day and forward.

kikuyu: Ĩngĩaga gũkũũrwo mwaka ũmwe ũtanathira, nyũmba ĩyo ĩrĩ itũũra-inĩ inene rĩirigĩre nĩĩgatuĩka ya mũmĩgũri na njiaro ciake nginya tene. Nyũmba ĩyo ndĩgaacookerio mwene Mwaka wa Jubilii. No nyũmba cia tũtũũra-inĩ tũrĩa tũtarĩ tũirigĩre, irĩtuagwo ta mĩgũnda ya kũndũ gũtarĩ kũirige. Icio no ikũũrwo, na nĩ igaacookio Mwaka wa Jubilii.
english: If it isn’t redeemed within the space of a full year, then the house that is in the walled city shall be made sure in perpetuity to him who bought it,

kikuyu: na igaragaro-inĩ cia mĩkuru ĩrĩa ithiĩte o nginya kũu Ari, na ĩgatwarana na mũhaka wa Moabi.”
english: how our fathers went down into Egypt, and we lived in Egypt a long time. The Egyptians mistreated us and our fathers.

kikuyu: “Naarĩ korwo ndaheo ũndũ ũrĩa ndĩrahooya, korwo Ngai aahe ũndũ ũrĩa ndĩrerirĩria,
english: “But as for me, I would seek God. I would commit my cause to God,

kikuyu: Ehĩtũkĩri othe nĩmatahĩte indo ciake; atuĩkĩte wa kũnyararwo nĩ andũ arĩa mariganĩtie nake.
english: All who pass by the way rob him. He has become a reproach to his neighbors.

kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa mwega nĩatugagwo nĩ Jehova, no mũndũ mwara Jehova amũtuaga mũndũ mwĩhia.
english: A good man shall obtain favor from Yahweh, but he will condemn a man of wicked plans.

kikuyu: “Cooka mũciĩ ũkoige ũrĩa wothe Ngai agwĩkĩire.” Nĩ ũndũ ũcio mũndũ ũcio agĩthiĩ akĩĩra andũ othe a itũũra ũrĩa Jesũ aamwĩkĩire. Kairĩtu Gaakuĩte na Mũtumia warĩ Mũrũaru Na rĩrĩ, Jesũ aacooka kuuma mũrĩmo ũũrĩa ũngĩ wa iria, andũ aingĩ nĩmamwamũkĩrire tondũ nĩmamwetereire.
english: Now when the Pharisee who had invited him saw it, he said to himself, “This man, if he were a prophet, would have perceived who and what kind of woman this is who touches him, that she is a sinner.”

kikuyu: nacio njata cia igũrũ ikĩgũa thĩ, o ta ũrĩa ngũyũ iria thaatu ciitĩkaga kuuma mũkũyũ-inĩ rĩrĩa wainainio nĩ rũhuho rũnene.*
english: The stars of the sky fell to the earth, like a fig tree dropping its unripe figs when it is shaken by a great wind.

kikuyu: Moimbũraga mehia mao, nake akamabatithĩria Rũũĩ-inĩ rwa Jorodani. Na rĩrĩa oonire Afarisai na Asadukai* aingĩ magĩũka hau nĩgeetha amabatithie, akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Inyuĩ rũciaro rũrũ rwa nduĩra! Nũũ ũmũheete ũhoro atĩ mũũrĩre marakara marĩa makiriĩ gũũka?
english: ‘You Bethlehem, land of Judah, are in no way least among the princes of Judah; for out of you shall come a governor who shall shepherd my people, Israel.’ ”*

kikuyu: “Atĩrĩrĩ, mũnjĩtaga ‘Mwathani, Mwathani,’ nĩkĩ, na mũtiĩkaga ũrĩa ndĩmwĩraga?
english: “Why do you call me, ‘Lord, Lord,’ and don’t do the things which I say?

kikuyu: Tiga andũ a Benjamini-rĩ, Isiraeli nĩmacookanĩrĩirie thigari
english: The men of Israel, besides Benjamin, were counted four hundred thousand men who drew sword. All these were men of war.

kikuyu: “Ciana ciakwa, ngũkorwo na inyuĩ o gwa kahinda kanini. Nĩmũkanjaria, na o ta ũrĩa ndeerire Ayahudi, no taguo ngũmwĩra: Kũrĩa ndĩrathiĩ, inyuĩ mũtingĩhota gũũka.
english: Little children, I will be with you a little while longer. You will seek me, and as I said to the Jews, ‘Where I am going, you can’t come,’ so now I tell you.

kikuyu: Nĩũũĩ mawatho ma igũrũ? No ũhote kũhaanda wathani wa Ngai ũrũme gũkũ thĩ?
english: Do you know the laws of the heavens? Can you establish its dominion over the earth?

kikuyu: Itĩĩna rĩa kuuma itoonyero rĩa kĩhingo nginya mũthia ũrĩa ũngĩ wa gĩthaku kĩarĩo warĩ wa mĩkono mĩrongo ĩtano.‡
english: From the forefront of the gate at the entrance to the forefront of the inner porch of the gate were fifty cubits.

kikuyu: “Na rĩrĩ, ndaarĩkia kũnengera Baruku wa Neria marũa macio ma kĩrĩkanĩro gĩa kuonania nũũ mwene gĩthaka, ngĩhooya Jehova ngiuga ũũ:
english: Yahweh says: “Refrain your voice from weeping, and your eyes from tears, for your work will be rewarded,” says Yahweh. “They will come again from the land of the enemy.

kikuyu: “Nĩ ũndũ ũcio nĩndĩrarĩrĩra Moabi, nĩ Moabi guothe ndĩrarĩrĩra, ndĩracakaĩra andũ a Kiri-Haresethi.
english: Therefore I will wail for Moab. Yes, I will cry out for all Moab. They will mourn for the men of Kir Heres.

kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo nĩarangagĩra njĩra cia arĩa marũmagĩrĩra kĩhooto, na akagitĩra njĩra ya andũ ake arĩa ehokeku.
english: It will be health to your body, and nourishment to your bones.

kikuyu: Jakubu akĩamũrania tũũri tũu kuuma rũũru-inĩ rwa Labani, na tũgetindia, no agatũma icio ingĩ ingʼethere iria ciarĩ na maroro na iria njirũ cia Labani. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio agĩĩthondekera ndũũru ciake mwene, na ndaacituranĩrire na cia Labani.
english: Jacob separated the lambs, and set the faces of the flocks toward the streaked and all the black in Laban’s flock. He put his own droves apart, and didn’t put them into Laban’s flock.

kikuyu: Ririkanai maũndũ marĩa mahĩtũkĩte, marĩa ma tene; niĩ nĩ niĩ Mũrungu, na gũtirĩ ũngĩ tiga niĩ; niĩ nĩ niĩ Ngai, na gũtirĩ ũngĩ ũhaana ta niĩ.
english: Remember the former things of old; for I am God, and there is no other. I am God, and there is none like me.

kikuyu: “Cookanĩrĩrai hamwe, mũũke; ũnganai, inyuĩ arĩa mũũrĩte kuuma kũrĩ ndũrĩrĩ. Kwaga ũmenyi-rĩ, nĩ andũ arĩa makuuaga mĩhianano ya kũhooywo ya mĩtĩ, na makahooya ngai itangĩhota kũmahonokia.
english: “Assemble yourselves and come. Draw near together, you who have escaped from the nations. Those have no knowledge who carry the wood of their engraved image, and pray to a god that can’t save.

kikuyu: “ ‘No rĩrĩ, nĩngarĩrehera ũgima wa mwĩrĩ na ndĩrĩhonie. Ningĩ nĩngahonia andũ akwa na ndũme matũũre makenagĩra thayũ mũingĩ na ũgitĩri.
english: behold, I will bring it health and healing, and I will cure them; and I will reveal to them abundance of peace and truth.

kikuyu: no rĩrĩ, andũ arĩa mehokaga Jehova nĩmakerũhĩrio hinya wao. Makombũkaga na mathagu o ta nderi; magaatengʼera na matinoge, magaathiiaga na magũrũ na matiũrwo nĩ hinya.
english: but those who wait for Yahweh will renew their strength. They will mount up with wings like eagles.

kikuyu: Ndungata ĩyo nĩyanyararirwo na ĩkĩregwo nĩ andũ, aarĩ mũndũ wa kĩeha kĩingĩ na wonete thĩĩna. O ta mũndũ ũrĩa andũ mahithaga mothiũ mao matikamuone-rĩ, aanyararirwo, na ithuĩ tũtiigana kũmũtĩĩa.
english: He was despised and rejected by men, a man of suffering and acquainted with disease. He was despised as one from whom men hide their face; and we didn’t respect him.

kikuyu: o na mũtikanahe mũcukani kamweke.
english: and don’t give place‡ to the devil.

kikuyu: “Akorwo ũrĩ mũndũ ũrĩ na ũtaũku-rĩ, igua ũhoro ũyũ;
english: then he opens the ears of men, and seals their instruction,

kikuyu: O nao andũ a Zebuluni matiigana kũingata andũ a Kaanani arĩa maatũũraga kũu Kitironi kana Nahaloli, arĩa maatigaire gatagatĩ kao; no nĩmamarutithirie wĩra na hinya.
english: Zebulun didn’t drive out the inhabitants of Kitron, nor the inhabitants of Nahalol; but the Canaanites lived among them, and became subject to forced labor.

kikuyu: Nĩngatũma matuĩke andũ a gũthũũrwo nĩ mothamaki mothe ma gũkũ thĩ, nĩ ũndũ wa ũrĩa Manase mũrũ wa Hezekia mũthamaki wa Juda eekire gũkũ itũũra-inĩ rĩa Jerusalemu.
english: I will cause them to be tossed back and forth among all the kingdoms of the earth, because of Manasseh, the son of Hezekiah, king of Judah, for that which he did in Jerusalem.

kikuyu: Jesũ akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “ ‘Endaga Mwathani Ngai waku na ngoro yaku yothe, na muoyo waku wothe, na meciiria maku mothe.’
english: Jesus said to him, “ ‘You shall love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your soul, and with all your mind.’*

kikuyu: Ũgacooka ũkarigiicũka ũrorete na mwena wa irathĩro, ũgakinya Bethi-Dagoni, ũkahutia Zebuluni na Gĩtuamba kĩa Ifita-Eli, ũgacooka ũkerekera mwena wa gathigathini ũgakinya Bethi-Emeki na Neieli, ũhĩtũkĩire Kabuli mwena wa ũmotho.
english: It turned toward the sunrise to Beth Dagon, and reached to Zebulun, and to the valley of Iphtah El northward to Beth Emek and Neiel. It went out to Cabul on the left hand,

kikuyu: Nyũmba ya mũndũ mwaganu nĩĩkanangwo, no hema ya mũndũ mũrũngĩrĩru nĩĩkagaacĩra.
english: Sheol† and Abaddon are before Yahweh— how much more then the hearts of the children of men!

kikuyu: Agĩthiĩ na mbere, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Ũmũthĩ rĩrĩa nginyire gĩthima-inĩ kĩa maaĩ, njugire atĩrĩ, ‘Wee Jehova Ngai wa Iburahĩmu mwathi wakwa, ũngĩenda, ndagũthaitha, gaacĩrithia rũgendo rũrũ njũkĩte.
english: I came today to the spring, and said, ‘Yahweh, the God of my master Abraham, if now you do prosper my way which I go—

kikuyu: no nginya njeterere, nĩ ũndũ rĩu nĩmakirĩte, tondũ atĩ rĩu marũgamĩte hau matarĩ na macookio?
english: Shall I wait, because they don’t speak, because they stand still, and answer no more?

kikuyu: Andũ arĩa athingu monaga andũ arĩa matarĩ ehokeku ta kĩndũ kĩrĩ magigi; nao andũ arĩa aaganu moonaga andũ arĩa arũngĩrĩru ta kĩndũ kĩrĩ magigi.
english: A dishonest man detests the righteous, and the upright in their ways detest the wicked.

kikuyu: na Hazoru-Gada, na Heshimoni, na Bethi-Peletu,
english: Hazar Gaddah, Heshmon, Beth Pelet,

kikuyu: Nĩtũtuĩkĩte ciana cia ngoriai na ciana itarĩ na maithe, nao manyina maitũ magatuĩka ta atumia a ndigwa.
english: We are orphans and fatherless. Our mothers are as widows.

kikuyu: Maya nĩmo marĩĩtwa ma atongoria acio: kuuma mũhĩrĩga wa Rubeni, aarĩ Shamua mũrũ wa Zakuri;
english: These were their names: Of the tribe of Reuben, Shammua the son of Zaccur.

kikuyu: Jakubu Kwĩhaarĩria Gũcemania na Esaũ
english: 1 Jacob went on his way, and the angels of God met him.

kikuyu: Nacio ngʼombe icio mũgwanja hĩnju na njongʼi iria cioimĩrire thuutha nĩ mĩaka mũgwanja, na no taguo magira marĩa mũgwanja matarĩ ngano macinĩtwo nĩ rũhuho rwa mwena wa irathĩro: ĩyo nĩ mĩaka mũgwanja ya ngʼaragu.
english: The seven thin and ugly cattle that came up after them are seven years, and also the seven empty heads of grain blasted with the east wind; they will be seven years of famine.

kikuyu: Hwaĩ-inĩ ũcio, mũthuuri ũmwe mũkũrũ wa kuuma bũrũri ũrĩa ũrĩ irĩma wa Efiraimu, watũũraga Gibea, (andũ a kũu maarĩ Abenjamini), agĩũka oimĩte wĩra mĩgũnda-inĩ. Na rĩrĩa aarorire akĩona mũgendi ũcio hau kĩhaaro-inĩ gĩa itũũra inene, mũthuuri ũcio akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Wathiĩ kũ na uumĩte kũ?”
english: Behold, an old man came from his work out of the field at evening. Now the man was from the hill country of Ephraim, and he lived in Gibeah; but the men of the place were Benjamites.

kikuyu: Gĩkonde gĩakwa nĩkĩgarũrũkĩte, gĩgathita na gĩkoonũka; mwĩrĩ wakwa ũhiũhĩte nĩ ũrugarĩ.
english: My skin grows black and peels from me. My bones are burned with heat.

kikuyu: Ihuhĩro cia ngano na ihihĩro cia ndibei itikahũũnia andũ, o na gũtigakorwo na ndibei ya mũhihano.
english: The threshing floor and the wine press won’t feed them, and the new wine will fail her.

kikuyu: nao arĩa othe maũiguire makĩgegio mũno nĩ maũndũ marĩa arĩithi acio maameeraga.
english: Zacharias said to the angel, “How can I be sure of this? For I am an old man, and my wife is well advanced in years.”

kikuyu: Nĩagathĩnjĩra nyoni ĩmwe ya icio igũrũ rĩa nyũngũ ya rĩũmba ĩrĩ na maaĩ matahĩtwo o hĩndĩ ĩyo.
english: The priest shall examine the plague, and isolate the plague seven days.

kikuyu: (Aaragia ũhoro wa Judasi mũrũ wa Simoni Isikariota, tondũ o na aakorwo aarĩ ũmwe wa acio ikũmi na eerĩ, nĩwe warĩ amũkunyanĩre thuutha ũcio.)
english: Now he spoke of Judas, the son of Simon Iscariot, for it was he who would betray him, being one of the twelve.

kikuyu: ũkahaana ta njagĩ ĩrĩa ĩmenyerete gũikara werũ-inĩ, ĩrĩa ĩnuhanuhaga rĩrĩa ĩkwenda kũhaicwo; rĩrĩa ĩrĩ na mũrukĩ-rĩ, nũũ ũngĩhota kũmĩrigĩrĩria? Njamba iria ingĩmĩenda, itingĩĩnogia ikĩmĩcaria; mweri wayo wakinya, nĩikamĩona.
english: a wild donkey used to the wilderness, that sniffs the wind in her craving. When she is in heat, who can turn her away? All those who seek her will not weary themselves. In her month, they will find her.

kikuyu: Ikĩambĩrĩria kũmũnyũrũria, ikĩmwĩraga atĩrĩ, “Ũkĩrĩ mũhoro, wee mũthamaki wa Ayahudi?”
english: They began to salute him, “Hail, King of the Jews!”

kikuyu: “Meere atĩrĩ: ‘Mwathani Jehova ekuuga ũũ: Ti-itherũ o ta ũrĩa niĩ ndũũraga muoyo, acio matigaire kũu kwanangĩku makaaniinwo na rũhiũ rwa njora, nao arĩa marĩ mĩgũnda-inĩ ngaa- maneana kũrĩ nyamũ cia gĩthaka imatambuurange, nao arĩa marĩ ciĩhitho-inĩ nũmu na ngurunga- inĩ makooragwo nĩ mũthiro.
english: “You shall tell them, ‘The Lord Yahweh says: “As I live, surely those who are in the waste places will fall by the sword. I will give whoever is in the open field to the animals to be devoured, and those who are in the strongholds and in the caves will die of the pestilence.

kikuyu: nacio ngʼombe ciarĩ 36,000 na kuuma harĩ icio Jehova akĩrutĩrwo ngʼombe 72;
english: The cattle were thirty-six thousand, of which Yahweh’s tribute was seventy-two.

kikuyu: Icio nĩcio ciana iria ciaciarĩirwo Jakubu nĩ Zilipa, ũrĩa Labani aaheete mwarĩ Lea; ciothe ciarĩ ciana ikũmi na ithathatũ.
english: These are the sons of Zilpah, whom Laban gave to Leah, his daughter, and these she bore to Jacob, even sixteen souls.

kikuyu: Maakaire mateithio, no hatiarĩ na wa kũmahonokia, maakaĩire Jehova no ndaametĩkire.
english: They cried, but there was no one to save; even to Yahweh, but he didn’t answer them.

kikuyu: na Joana mũtumia wa Husa, ũrĩa warĩ mũrori wa indo cia Herode; na Susana; na angĩ aingĩ. Atumia aya nĩmamũtungatagĩra na indo ciao ene.
english: and Joanna, the wife of Chuzas, Herod’s steward; Susanna; and many others who served them† from their possessions.

kikuyu: Rĩrĩa maiguire ũhoro ũcio, andũ arĩa maatũũraga mũthiringo-inĩ wa Shekemu magĩtoonya kĩĩhitho-inĩ kĩa hinya kĩa hekarũ ya Eli-Berithu. Rĩrĩa Abimeleku aiguire atĩ nĩmacookanĩrĩire kuo- rĩ,
english: When all the men of the tower of Shechem heard of it, they entered into the stronghold of the house of Elberith.

kikuyu: O ũguo, noguo athuuri magĩrĩirwo nĩ kwenda atumia ao o taarĩ mĩĩrĩ yao ene. Mũndũ ũrĩa wendete mũtumia wake nĩwe mwene wĩyendete.
english: Even so husbands also ought to love their own wives as their own bodies. He who loves his own wife loves himself.

kikuyu: Thahabu yothe ĩrĩa yoimire kũrĩ anene a ikundi cia o ngiri, ngiri na anene a ikundi cia o igana, igana ĩrĩa Musa na Eleazaru maatwarĩire Jehova kĩrĩ kĩheo, yarĩ ya ũritũ wa cekeri 16,750.*
english: All the gold of the wave offering that they offered up to Yahweh, of the captains of thousands, and of the captains of hundreds, was sixteen thousand seven hundred fifty shekels.†

kikuyu: Githĩ ũyũ ti ũrĩa bundi wa mbaũ? Githĩ ũyũ ti mũrũ wa Mariamu, o we mũrũ wa nyina na Jakubu na Jusufu na Judasi na Simoni. Na aarĩ a nyina githĩ matirĩ gũkũ hamwe na ithuĩ?” Nao makĩmũrakarĩra.
english: Isn’t this the carpenter, the son of Mary and brother of James, Joses, Judah, and Simon? Aren’t his sisters here with us?” So they were offended at him.

kikuyu: Mũndũ o wothe angĩũka kũrĩ inyuĩ arĩ na ũrutani ũngĩ tiga ũcio-rĩ, mũtikanamũnyiite ũgeni o na kana mũmwamũkĩre kwanyu mũciĩ.
english: But these speak evil of whatever things they don’t know. They are destroyed in these things that they understand naturally, like the creatures without reason.

kikuyu: Jehova arogũtiirĩra gĩthiithi gĩake, na akũhe thayũ.” ’
english: Yahweh lift up his face toward you, and give you peace.’

kikuyu: Watho ũcio wa mũthamaki warĩ wa ihenya mũno, narĩo icua rĩkahiũha mũno, ũũ atĩ nĩnĩmbĩ cia mwaki ũcio cioragire thigari iria cianyiitĩte Shadiraka, na Meshaki, na Abedinego,
english: Because the whole world before you is as a grain in a balance, and as a drop of dew that comes down upon the earth in the morning.

kikuyu: Gĩkundi kĩu kĩa andũ gĩkĩmakaania, gĩkĩmeera makire, no-o magĩkĩrĩrĩria kwanĩrĩra, makiugaga atĩrĩ, “Mwathani, Mũrũ wa Daudi, tũiguĩre tha!” Nake Jesũ akĩrũgama, akĩmeeta. Akĩmooria atĩrĩ, “Mũrenda ndĩmwĩkĩre atĩa?”
english: The multitude rebuked them, telling them that they should be quiet, but they cried out even more, “Lord, have mercy on us, you son of David!”

kikuyu: O rĩngĩ matigari ma nyũmba ya Juda nĩmagaikũrũkia mĩri na thĩ, na maciare maciaro na igũrũ.
english: The remnant that is escaped of the house of Judah will again take root downward, and bear fruit upward.

kikuyu: Korwo aagĩa na muoroto wa kweheria roho wake na mĩhũmũ-rĩ,
english: For God speaks once, yes twice, though man pays no attention.

kikuyu: nĩguo makũngũyagĩre mũthenya wa ikũmi na ĩna, na wa ikũmi na ĩtano wa mweri wa Adari o mwaka,
english: to enjoin them that they should keep the fourteenth and fifteenth days of the month Adar yearly,

kikuyu: Ndũkareke maitho maku mone toro, kana ũreke imone ciaku icũnge.
