english: Give no sleep to your eyes, nor slumber to your eyelids.

kikuyu: Wee Mwathani, tumũra mĩromo yakwa, nako kanua gakwa koimbũre ũgooci waku.
english: Lord, open my lips. My mouth will declare your praise.

kikuyu: Athamaki acio othe hamwe na mabũrũri mao-rĩ, Joshua aamahootire na itharĩkĩra rĩmwe, nĩ ũndũ Jehova, Ngai wa Isiraeli nĩwe warũagĩrĩra Isiraeli. Nake Joshua agĩcooka hamwe na andũ othe a Isiraeli kambĩ-inĩ kũu Giligali.
english: Joshua took all these kings and their land at one time because Yahweh, the God of Israel, fought for Israel.

kikuyu: Nĩmagĩtũũre marĩ maku wiki, matigatuĩke maku na mũndũ wa kũngĩ o na rĩ.
english: arrogant eyes, a lying tongue, hands that shed innocent blood,

kikuyu: no rĩrĩ, nĩakaniina Nineve na kĩguũ kĩnene kĩa maaĩ; nĩakaingata thũ ciake nginya nduma-inĩ.
english: But Nineveh has been from of old like a pool of water, yet they flee away. “Stop! Stop!” they cry, but no one looks back.

kikuyu: Aakinya kuo akĩhuha karumbeta kũu bũrũri ũrĩa ũrĩ irĩma wa Efiraimu, nao andũ a Isiraeli magĩikũrũkania nake moimĩte irĩma-inĩ, amatongoretie.
english: When he had come, he blew a trumpet in the hill country of Ephraim; and the children of Israel went down with him from the hill country, and he led them.

kikuyu: na mũcookagĩrie Ngai o we Ithe witũ ngaatho maũndũ-inĩ mothe, thĩinĩ wa rĩĩtwa rĩa Mwathani witũ Jesũ Kristũ.
english: giving thanks always concerning all things in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ to God, even the Father;

kikuyu: agaatũũraga matũũra maanangĩku, na aikarage nyũmba itatũũragwo nĩ mũndũ, nyũmba itigairie o kũmomoka ituĩke ihumbu cia mahiga.
english: He has lived in desolate cities, in houses which no one inhabited, which were ready to become heaps.

kikuyu: Ndaigua mũgambo wa ngeithi ciaku wakinya matũ-inĩ makwa, nako kaana karĩa karĩ nda yakwa kambarara nĩ ũndũ wa gũkena.
english: For behold, when the voice of your greeting came into my ears, the baby leaped in my womb for joy!

kikuyu: Harĩ rũrĩrĩ rũtharĩkĩire bũrũri wakwa, rũrĩrĩ rũrĩ hinya na rũtangĩtarĩka; rũrĩ na magego mahaana ta ma mũrũũthi, magego manene ta ma mũrũũthi wa mũgoma. Nĩ rwanangĩte mĩthabibũ yakwa, na rũgathũkia mĩkũyũ yakwa. Nĩrũmĩũnũrĩte makoni rũkamate, rũgatiga honge ciayo irĩ nyũnũre.
english: For a nation has come up on my land, strong, and without number. His teeth are the teeth of a lion, and he has the fangs of a lioness.

kikuyu: Niĩ ndingĩcihutia; irio ta icio no itũme njire ngoro.
english: but man is born to trouble, as the sparks fly upward.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo ũrĩa ũtarĩ ũthũ na ithuĩ nĩ ũmwe witũ.
english: For whoever is not against us is on our side.

kikuyu: Magĩthiĩ, magĩkinya handũ heetagwo Gologotha (ũguo nĩ kuuga Handũ hahaana ta Ihĩndĩ rĩa Mũtwe).
english: But Peter answered him, “Even if all will be made to stumble because of you, I will never be made to stumble.”

kikuyu: Jehova akĩĩra Musa atĩrĩ,
english: Yahweh spoke to Moses, saying,

kikuyu: Kuuma njiaro-inĩ cia Isakaru: Arũme othe arĩa maakinyĩtie mĩaka mĩrongo ĩĩrĩ na makĩria, arĩa mangĩahotire gũthiĩ ita-inĩ, makĩandĩkwo o mũndũ o mũndũ na rĩĩtwa rĩake, kũringana na maandĩko ma mbarĩ ciao na nyũmba ciao.
english: Of the children of Issachar, their generations, by their families, by their fathers’ houses, according to the number of the names, from twenty years old and upward, all who were able to go out to war:  “Yahweh” is God’s proper Name, sometimes rendered “LORD” (all caps) in other translations.  or, chiefs, or, leaders

kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩ ngoro ya ũremi ndagaacagĩra; nake mũndũ ũrĩa ũrĩ rũrĩmĩ rwa kũheenania agũũaga thĩĩna-inĩ.
english: A man’s stomach is filled with the fruit of his mouth. With the harvest of his lips he is satisfied.

kikuyu: “Marutaga andũ magongona, na makamumunya mĩhianano ya njaũ.” Nĩ ũndũ ũcio makahaana ta kĩbii kĩa rũciinĩ, na ta ime rĩa kĩrooko rĩrĩa rĩcookaga gũthira, na ta mũũngũ ũrĩa ũmbũragwo kuuma kĩhuhĩro-inĩ kĩa ngano, o na ta ndogo ĩgĩtoga yumĩire ndirica-inĩ.
english: Assyria can’t save us. We won’t ride on horses; neither will we say any more to the work of our hands, ‘Our gods!’ for in you the fatherless finds mercy.”

kikuyu: Akumbatagĩria rũheni na moko make, na akarwatha rũringe kĩrĩa kĩorotetwo.
english: He covers his hands with the lightning, and commands it to strike the mark.

kikuyu: Nĩngonania morirũ kũu igũrũ, o na gũkũ thĩ, namo nĩ ma thakame, na ma mwaki, o na ma ndogo ĩkwambata na igũrũ.
english: Therefore, being a prophet, and knowing that God had sworn with an oath to him that of the fruit of his body, according to the flesh, he would raise up the Christ§ to sit on his throne,

kikuyu: nĩatũtũũrĩtie muoyo na akagirĩrĩria magũrũ maitũ matirũke.
english: who preserves our life among the living, and doesn’t allow our feet to be moved.

kikuyu: Ndaacũthĩrĩria thĩinĩ wakĩo ngĩona nyamũ cia thĩ iria irĩ magũrũ mana, na nyamũ cia gĩthaka, na nyamũ iria itambaga thĩ na nyoni cia rĩera-inĩ.
english: When I had looked intently at it, I considered, and saw the four-footed animals of the earth, wild animals, creeping things, and birds of the sky.

kikuyu: Icio nĩ ciũmbe irĩ muoyo iria ndoonete irĩ rungu rwa Ngai wa Isiraeli hau Rũũĩ-inĩ rwa Kebari, na niĩ ngĩmenya atĩ maarĩ akerubi.
english: This is the living creature that I saw under the God of Israel by the river Chebar; and I knew that they were cherubim.

kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũhaandaga maũndũ ma gũkenia merirĩria ma mwĩrĩ-rĩ, ũcio akaagetha kwanangĩka kũrĩa kuumanaga na maũndũ macio. No ũrĩa ũhaandaga maũndũ ma gũkenia Roho-rĩ, ũcio akaagetha muoyo wa tene na tene ũrĩa uumanaga na Roho. Ningĩ tũtikanogio nĩ gwĩka wega, nĩgũkorwo ihinda rĩrĩa rĩagĩrĩru
english: For he who sows to his own flesh will from the flesh reap corruption. But he who sows to the Spirit will from the Spirit reap eternal life.

kikuyu: Maaĩ macio makĩambatĩra na makĩongerereka mũno gũkũ thĩ, nayo thabina ĩkĩreera maaĩ igũrũ.
english: But I will establish my covenant with you. You shall come into the ship, you, your sons, your wife, and your sons’ wives with you.

kikuyu: na Bethi-Lebaothu, na Sharuheni; mothe maarĩ matũũra ikũmi na matatũ na tũtũũra twamo.
english: Beth Lebaoth, and Sharuhen; thirteen cities with their villages;

kikuyu: Kũu-rĩ, mĩgwate o nayo nĩĩkenagĩra kwaraha kwayo; nĩĩtigĩte kũigua kĩgũthũko kĩa nyabaara ya ngombo.
english: Behold, he puts no trust in his servants. He charges his angels with error.

kikuyu: Inĩrai Jehova rwĩmbo rwerũ; inĩrai Jehova, inyuĩ andũ a thĩ yothe.
english: Corinthians 4:18 ways which are in Christ, even as I teach everywhere in every assembly.

kikuyu: Mĩario ya Ayubu Ningĩ Ayubu agĩcookia atĩrĩ:
english: 1 Then Job answered,

kikuyu: “ ‘Ndũkanahikie mwarĩ wa nyina na mũtumia waku atuĩke mũiru wa mũtumia waku, ũkomage nake mũtumia waku arĩ muoyo.
english: “ ‘You shall not take a wife in addition to her sister, to be a rival, to uncover her nakedness, while her sister is still alive.

kikuyu: na njiaro cia Adonikamu ciarĩ Nao athĩnjĩri-Ngai maarĩ: njiaro cia Jedaia (iria cioimĩte harĩ nyũmba ya Jeshua) ciarĩ Nao Alawii maarĩ: a njiaro cia Jeshua (iria cioimĩte harĩ Kadimieli, rũciaro-inĩ rwa Hodavia) ciarĩ 74. Nao aini maarĩ: njiaro cia Asafu ciarĩ 148. Nao aikaria a ihingo maarĩ: njiaro cia Shalumu, na Ateri, na Talimoni, na Akubu, na Hatita, na Shobai ciarĩ 138.
english: For there were many in Judah sworn to him because he was the son-in-law of Shecaniah the son of Arah; and his son Jehohanan had taken the daughter of Meshullam the son of Berechiah as wife.

kikuyu: Ũndũ ũyũ ũkooneka mũthenya ũrĩa Ngai, na ũndũ wa Jesũ Kristũ agaatuĩra ciira meciiria ma hitho marĩa marĩ ngoro-inĩ cia andũ, o ta ũrĩa Ũhoro-ũrĩa-Mwega uugĩte, ũrĩa niĩ hunjagia. Ayahudi na Watho
english: in the day when God will judge the secrets of men, according to my Good News, by Jesus Christ.

kikuyu: Wee-rĩ, ũgũtuĩra mũrũ kana mwarĩ wa Ithe wanyu ciira nĩkĩ? Kana ũkũmena mũrũ kana mwarĩ wa ithe wanyu nĩkĩ? Nĩgũkorwo ithuothe nĩtũkarũgama mbere ya gĩtĩ gĩa ciira kĩa Ngai.
english: Now I beg you, brothers,‡ through the name of our Lord, Jesus Christ, that you all speak the same thing, and that there be no divisions among you, but that you be perfected together in the same mind and in the same judgment.

kikuyu: Mũndũ angĩhinga matũ ndakaigue kĩrĩro kĩa arĩa athĩĩni-rĩ, o nake agaakaya na ndone wa kũmwĩtĩka.
english: Whoever stops his ears at the cry of the poor, he will also cry out, but shall not be heard.

kikuyu: “Inyuĩ nĩmwatũmanire kũrĩ Johana, nake nĩaheanire ũira wa ma.
english: You have sent to John, and he has testified to the truth.

kikuyu: O na ningĩ ndaimono ikiuma andũ aingĩ, ikĩanagĩrĩra atĩrĩ, “Wee nĩwe Mũrũ wa Ngai!” Nowe agĩcikaania na agĩcigiria ciarie, tondũ nĩcioĩ atĩ nĩwe Kristũ. Na gwakĩa-rĩ, Jesũ akiumagara agĩthiĩ kũndũ gũtaarĩ andũ. Nao andũ makĩmũcaria, na rĩrĩa maakinyire harĩa aarĩ, makĩgeria kũmũgirĩrĩria ndakoime kũu amatige.
english: Demons also came out of many, crying out and saying, “You are the Christ, the Son of God!” Rebuking them, he didn’t allow them to speak, because they knew that he was the Christ.

kikuyu: “No rĩrĩ, nĩngacookia ũgaacĩru wa Moabi matukũ-inĩ marĩa magooka,” ũguo nĩguo Jehova ekuuga. Hau nĩho mũthia wa ituĩro rĩa Moabi.
english: “Yet I will reverse the captivity of Moab in the latter days,” says Yahweh. Thus far is the judgment of Moab.

kikuyu: Marothario ibuku-inĩ rĩa muoyo, mage kwandĩkwo mũtaratara-inĩ wa andũ arĩa athingu.
english: Let them be blotted out of the book of life, and not be written with the righteous.

kikuyu: Maitho makwa nĩmakamarora na ndĩmeeke maũndũ mega, na nĩngamacookia gũkũ bũrũri ũyũ. Nĩngamarũgamia na ndikamamomora; nĩngamahaanda na ndigacooka kũmamunya.
english: For I will set my eyes on them for good, and I will bring them again to this land. I will build them, and not pull them down. I will plant them, and not pluck them up.

kikuyu: Kũrigania nao, andũ a Tekoa magĩcookereria gĩcunjĩ kĩngĩ kuuma mũthiringo mũnene ũrĩa wacomokete na igũrũ nginya rũthingo rwa Ofeli.
english: After him the Tekoites repaired another portion, opposite the great tower that stands out, and to the wall of Ophel.

kikuyu: Isaia oigĩte atĩrĩ, “Thondekai ngũmba ya ngũyũ, mũmĩigĩrĩre ihũha-inĩ, na nĩekũhona.”
english: Now Isaiah had said, “Let them take a cake of figs, and lay it for a poultice on the boil, and he shall recover.”

kikuyu: rĩrĩa haroimĩrire ngʼombe mũgwanja njega na noru kuuma rũũĩ, na iraitĩka kũrĩa kũu ithanjĩ-inĩ.
english: and behold, seven fat and sleek cattle came up out of the river. They fed in the marsh grass;

kikuyu: Magongona marĩa Ngai etĩkagĩra nĩ roho ũrĩa mũthuthĩku; ngoro ĩrĩa thuthĩku na ĩkahera-rĩ, Wee Ngai ndũngĩmĩrega.
english: The sacrifices of God are a broken spirit. O God, you will not despise a broken and contrite heart.

kikuyu: Agĩtũma kũgĩe nduma naguo bũrũri ũcio ũgĩtumana, tondũ-rĩ, githĩ ti ciugo ciake maaremeire?
english: He sent darkness, and made it dark. They didn’t rebel against his words.

kikuyu: Matukũ make maronyiiha; ũtongoria wake ũrooywo nĩ mũndũ ũngĩ.
english: They are established forever and ever. They are done in truth and uprightness.

kikuyu: Mũndũ o wothe ũthikagĩrĩria kiugo no ndekaga ũrĩa kiugĩte-rĩ, ahaana ta mũndũ ũrĩa wĩroraga ũthiũ na gĩcicio,
english: For if anyone is a hearer of the word and not a doer, he is like a man looking at his natural face in a mirror;

kikuyu: AkĩũriaJehovaatĩrĩ, “ ndungata yaku thĩĩna ũyũ? Nĩ atĩa njĩkĩte ũkaaga gũgũkenia nĩguo ũnjigĩrĩre mũrigo wa andũ aya othe?
english: Moses said to Yahweh, “Why have you treated your servant so badly? Why haven’t I found favor in your sight, that you lay the burden of all this people on me?

kikuyu: Nĩnginyĩte hũgũrũrũ-inĩ cia mwanangĩko, ndĩ o gatagatĩ-inĩ ga kĩũngano gĩothe.”
english: in whose heart is perverseness, who devises evil continually, who always sows discord.

kikuyu: Thiĩi Kaline mũkeyonere kuo, ningĩ muume kuo mũthiĩ nginya Hamathu, itũũra rĩu inene, mũcooke mũikũrũke mũkinye Gathu kwa Afilisti. Anga acio nĩega magakĩra mothamaki manyu meerĩ? Bũrũri wao-rĩ, nĩ mũnene gũkĩra wanyu?
english: Go to Calneh, and see. From there go to Hamath the great. Then go down to Gath of the Philistines. Are they better than these kingdoms? Is their border greater than your border?

kikuyu: “ ‘Na rĩrĩ, ũyũ nĩguo watho wa Mũnaziri ihinda rĩake rĩa kwĩyamũrĩra Ngai rĩathira. Nĩakarehwo itoonyero-inĩ rĩa Hema-ya-Gũtũnganwo.
english: “ ‘This is the law of the Nazirite: when the days of his separation are fulfilled, he shall be brought to the door of the Tent of Meeting,

kikuyu: “Kaĩ ũtaiguĩte? Kuuma tene nĩndamũrĩte ũndũ ũcio. Matukũ-inĩ macio ma tene nĩndabangĩte ũndũ ũcio: rĩu nĩndĩũhingĩtie, atĩ wee nĩũtũmĩte matũũra manene marĩa mairigĩre na thingo cia hinya matuĩke hĩba cia mahiga.
english: “ ‘Have you not heard how I have done it long ago, and formed it in ancient times? Now I have brought it to pass, that it should be yours to destroy fortified cities, turning them into ruinous heaps.

kikuyu: Nĩwe watũciarire nĩ kwenda na ũndũ wa ũhoro-ũrĩa-wa-ma, nĩguo tũtuĩke ta maciaro ma mbere ma indo ciothe iria ombire. Gũthikĩrĩria na Gwĩka
english: Of his own will he gave birth to us by the word of truth, that we should be a kind of first fruits of his creatures.

kikuyu: Aca! No gĩkundi gĩkĩ kĩa andũ matooĩ watho-rĩ, nĩkĩnyiite nĩ kĩrumi.”
english: Jesus answered, “I don’t have a demon, but I honor my Father and you dishonor me.

kikuyu: “ ‘Niĩ no niĩ igai rĩa athĩnjĩri-Ngai. Mũtikanamagaĩre igai gũkũ bũrũri wa Isiraeli; Nĩ niĩ ngũtuĩka igai rĩao.
english: “ ‘They shall have an inheritance: I am their inheritance; and you shall give them no possession in Israel. I am their possession.

kikuyu: Mũtikanaheenanie mũndũ na ũrĩa ũngĩ, no mwĩtigagĩre Ngai wanyu. Niĩ nĩ niĩ Jehova Ngai wanyu.
english: You shall not wrong one another, but you shall fear your God; for I am Yahweh your God.

kikuyu: Riũa rĩu rĩaku rĩtigacooka gũthũa rĩngĩ, o na kana mweri waku ũtukĩrwo rĩngĩ; nĩgũkorwo Jehova nĩwe ũgaatuĩka ũtheri wa gũgũtherera nginya tene, na matukũ maku ma kĩeha nĩmagathira.
english: Your sun will not go down any more, nor will your moon withdraw itself; for Yahweh will be your everlasting light, and the days of your mourning will end.

kikuyu: nginya ũrĩa oigĩte ũgĩkinyanĩra, nginya kiugo kĩa Jehova gĩkĩonania atĩ aarĩ mũndũ wa ma.
english: until the time that his word happened, and Yahweh’s word proved him true.

kikuyu: Andũ arĩa matarĩ ehokeku nĩmakarĩhwo kũna nĩ ũndũ wa mĩthiĩre yao, nake mũndũ mwega aheo kĩheo kũringana na mĩthiĩre yake.
english: The heart of one who has understanding seeks knowledge, but the mouths of fools feed on folly.

kikuyu: Ũcooke uuge atĩrĩ, ‘Wee Jehova, nĩugĩte nĩũkũniina kũndũ gũkũ, nĩgeetha gũtikanatũũrwo nĩ mũndũ, o na kana nyamũ; gũgũtũũra gũkirĩte ihooru nginya tene.’
english: and say, ‘Yahweh, you have spoken concerning this place, to cut it off, that no one will dwell in it, neither man nor animal, but that it will be desolate forever.’

kikuyu: Thuutha wa kũrĩkanĩra kĩrĩĩko kĩu kũu Birishiba, Abimeleku na Fikolu mũnene wa mbũtũ ciake cia ita, magĩcooka bũrũri wa Afilisti. Nake Iburahĩmu akĩhaanda mũtĩ wa mũbinde kũu Birishiba, na agĩkaĩra rĩĩtwa rĩa Jehova, o we Mũrungu Ũrĩa-Ũtũũraga-Tene-na-Tene, arĩ handũ hau. Nake Iburahĩmu agĩikara bũrũri-inĩ ũcio wa Afilisti ihinda iraaya ma.
english: So they made a covenant at Beersheba. Abimelech rose up with Phicol, the captain of his army, and they returned into the land of the Philistines.

kikuyu: Acio nĩo andũ arĩa maatarirwo nĩ Musa na Harũni na atongoria arĩa ikũmi na eerĩ a Isiraeli, o ũmwe wao arũgamĩrĩire nyũmba yake.
english: These are those who were counted, whom Moses and Aaron counted, and the twelve men who were princes of Israel, each one for his fathers’ house.

kikuyu: Nĩngamacookia matũũre Jerusalemu; nao magaatuĩka andũ akwa na niĩ nduĩke Ngai wao wa kwĩhokeka na mũthingu.”
english: I will bring them, and they will dwell within Jerusalem. They will be my people, and I will be their God, in truth and in righteousness.”

kikuyu: Mũthamaki arotũũra hĩndĩ ĩrĩa yothe riũa rĩgũtũũra, na rĩrĩa rĩothe mweri ũgũtũũra nginya njiarwa na njiarwa.
english: They shall fear you while the sun endures; and as long as the moon, throughout all generations.

kikuyu: Wee Ngai-rĩ, onania ũhoti waku; tuonie hinya waku, Wee Ngai, ta ũrĩa waneka.
english: Your God has commanded your strength. Strengthen, God, that which you have done for us.

kikuyu: Simoni Petero akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Mwathani nĩ kũ ũgũthiĩ?” Nake Jesũ akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Kũrĩa ngũthiĩ, mũtingĩhota kũnũmĩrĩra rĩu, no nĩmũrĩnũmĩrĩra thuutha-inĩ.”
english: Simon Peter said to him, “Lord, where are you going?” Jesus answered, “Where I am going, you can’t follow now, but you will follow afterwards.”

kikuyu: Kĩndũ o gĩothe gĩtũũraga maaĩ-inĩ na gĩtirĩ mathagu manyiitaine kana ngaracũ mwĩrĩ, nĩciagũthũũrwo nĩ inyuĩ biũ.
english: Whatever has no fins nor scales in the waters is an abomination to you.

kikuyu: Kaĩ mũriganĩirwo nĩ maũndũ ma waganu marĩa mekirwo nĩ maithe manyu, na athamaki na atumia-athamaki a Juda, na maũndũ ma waganumarĩainyuĩnaatumiaanyumwekirebũrũri-inĩwaJudananjĩra-inĩciaJerusalemu?
english: Have you forgotten the wickedness of your fathers, the wickedness of the kings of Judah, the wickedness of their wives, your own wickedness, and the wickedness of your wives which they committed in the land of Judah and in the streets of Jerusalem?

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa ũhoro ũcio ũgaakinya bũrũri wa Misiri, andũ akuo nĩmakaagĩa na ruo rũnene mũno maigua ũhoro ũcio wa Turo.
english: When the report comes to Egypt, they will be in anguish at the report of Tyre.

kikuyu: “ ‘No mũngĩkaaga kũingata atũũri a bũrũri ũcio-rĩ, arĩa mũgeetĩkĩria matigwo magaatuĩka ngaracũ cia kũmũtũraga maitho, o na matuĩke mĩigua ya kũmũtheecaga mbaru. Nao nĩmakamũthĩĩ- nagia kũu bũrũri ũcio mũgaatũũra. Na rĩrĩ, ũndũ ũrĩa ndaciirĩire kũmeka-rĩ, ũtuĩke nĩguo ngaamwĩka inyuĩ.’ ”
english: “But if you do not drive out the inhabitants of the land from before you, then those you let remain of them will be like pricks in your eyes and thorns in your sides. They will harass you in the land in which you dwell.

kikuyu: “Inyuĩ nĩ inyuĩ ũtheri wa thĩ. Itũũra inene rĩakĩtwo kĩrĩma igũrũ rĩtingĩhithĩka.
english: “You are the light of the world. A city located on a hill can’t be hidden.

kikuyu: Nĩnjũũĩ ciĩko ciaku, o na wĩra waku mũritũ, o na ũkirĩrĩria waku. Nĩnjũũĩ atĩ ndũngĩkirĩrĩria andũ arĩa aaganu, na atĩ andũ arĩa moigaga nĩ atũmwo no ti atũmwo nĩwamageririe, na ũkĩmenya atĩ nĩ a maheeni.
english: 1 “To the angel of the assembly in Ephesus write: “He who holds the seven stars in his right hand, he who walks among the seven golden lamp stands says these things:  “Christ” means “Anointed One”.  or, messenger (here and wherever angel is mentioned)

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo athĩnjĩri-Ngai arĩa anene na athuuri a kĩrĩndĩ makĩgomana kwa mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai ũrĩa mũnene, wetagwo Kaiafa,
english: Then the chief priests, the scribes, and the elders of the people were gathered together in the court of the high priest, who was called Caiaphas.

kikuyu: “Ta thaa ithatũ nĩoimagarire na akĩona andũ angĩ marũngiĩ thoko-inĩ matarĩ ũndũ meekaga.
english: He went out about the third hour,‡ and saw others standing idle in the marketplace.

kikuyu: Nĩngamahuha ta mahuti makĩhuhwo na gĩtarũrũ ihingo-inĩ cia itũũra inene rĩa bũrũri. Nĩngarehe gĩkuũ na mwanangĩko kũrĩ andũ akwa, nĩgũkorwo nĩmagĩte kũgarũrĩra mĩthiĩre yao.
english: I have winnowed them with a fan in the gates of the land. I have bereaved them of children. I have destroyed my people. They didn’t return from their ways.

kikuyu: Mũndũ o wothe angĩkorwo aciirĩire rũmena na athinĩkanie ũrĩa ũngĩ, kana amũikĩrie kĩndũ akĩendaga nake mũgũthwo akue,
english: Of the tribe of the children of Simeon, Shemuel the son of Ammihud.

kikuyu: agĩcooka akĩĩra mũrutwo ũcio atĩrĩ, “Ũcio maitũguo.” Kuuma hĩndĩ ĩyo mũrutwo ũcio akĩoya nyina wa Jesũ, agĩthiĩ gũtũũra mũciĩ kwa mũrutwo ũcio. Gũkua kwa Jesũ
english: Then he said to the disciple, “Behold, your mother!” From that hour, the disciple took her to his own home.

kikuyu: Goocai Jehova gũkũ thĩ, inyuĩ ciũmbe cia iria-inĩ, na ũriku wothe wa maria marĩa manene,
english: to execute vengeance on the nations, and punishments on the peoples;

kikuyu: Honokia, nĩgũkorwo ndĩ waku, na nĩĩmaathĩte mawatho maku.
english: I am yours. Save me, for I have sought your precepts.

kikuyu: Roho wa Jehova agĩũka igũrũ rĩake na hinya nginya agĩtarũranga mũrũũthi ũcio na moko matheri o ta ũrĩa angĩtarũranga koori. No ndaigana kwĩra ithe o na kana nyina ũrĩa ekĩte. Nake agĩcooka agĩikũrũka makĩaranĩria na mũndũ-wa-nja ũcio, na akĩmwenda.
english: Yahweh’s Spirit came mightily on him, and he tore him as he would have torn a young goat with his bare hands, but he didn’t tell his father or his mother what he had done.

kikuyu: Nĩmakarũa nawe no matigaakũhoota, nĩgũkorwo ndĩ hamwe nawe na nĩndĩrĩkũhonokagia,” ũguo nĩguo Jehova ekuuga.
english: They will fight against you, but they will not prevail against you; for I am with you”, says Yahweh, “to rescue you.”

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa aiyũrĩtie nda yake-rĩ, nĩguo Ngai akaarekereria marakara make mahiũ mamũkore na amuurĩrie mahũũra make.
english: When he is about to fill his belly, God will cast the fierceness of his wrath on him. It will rain on him while he is eating.

kikuyu: Mũndũ mwaganu erũagĩrĩra na ũrũme, no mũndũ mũrũngĩrĩru nĩeciiragia ũhoro wa mĩthiĩre yake.
english: A wicked man hardens his face; but as for the upright, he establishes his ways.

kikuyu: (we ũrĩa wombire njata iria ciĩtagwo Kĩrĩmĩra na Karaũ, o we ũrĩa ũgarũraga nduma ndumanu ĩgatuĩka rũciinĩ, na agatumania mũthenya ũgatuĩka ũtukũ, ũrĩa wĩtaga maaĩ ma iria rĩrĩa inene akamaitũrũra gũkũ thĩ-rĩ: Jehova nĩrĩo rĩĩtwa rĩake;
english: “The Lord Yahweh has sworn by himself,” says Yahweh, the God of Armies: “I abhor the pride of Jacob, and detest his fortresses. Therefore I will deliver up the city with all that is in it.

kikuyu: Marakara ma Jehova ma Gũũkĩrĩra Isiraeli
english: The sorrows of a travailing woman will come on him.  The word translated “Lord” is “Adonai.”

kikuyu: Ũndũ ũrĩa ndeetigagĩra nĩũngorete; ũndũ ũrĩa wamakagia mũno nĩguo ũnginyĩrĩire.
english: For the thing which I fear comes on me, that which I am afraid of comes to me.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo mũthenya ũcio nĩũkuhĩrĩirie, mũthenya wa Jehova nĩũkuhĩrĩirie, mũthenya ũrĩ na matu, mũthenya ũrĩ na mũthiro harĩ ndũrĩrĩ. * 29:11 Ihinda rĩa mĩaka mĩrongo ĩna rĩmwe rĩonanagia ihinda iraihu na iritũ (4:6).
english: Speak and say, ‘The Lord Yahweh says: “Behold, I am against you, Pharaoh king of Egypt, the great monster that lies in the middle of his rivers, that has said, ‘My river is my own, and I have made it for myself.’

kikuyu: nĩguo atuĩre o mũndũ ciira, na aiguithie kĩhooto andũ othe arĩa matetigĩrĩĩte Ngai, nĩ ũndũ wa ciĩko ciothe cia kũremera Ngai, iria maaneka mategwĩtigĩra Ngai, o na nĩ ũndũ wa ciugo ciothe njũru iria ehia arĩa matamwĩtigĩrĩte manaaria cia gũũkĩrĩra Ngai.”
english: to execute judgment on all, and to convict all the ungodly of all their works of ungodliness which they have done in an ungodly way, and of all the hard things which ungodly sinners have spoken against him.”

kikuyu: Balamu agĩcookeria Balaki atĩrĩ, “Githĩ ndierire andũ arĩa wandũmĩire atĩrĩ,
english: He answered and said, “Must I not take heed to speak that which Yahweh puts in my mouth?”

kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Isiraeli akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Thiĩ ũkarore kana ariũ a thoguo marĩ o ho o wega, o ũndũ ũmwe na mbũri, na ũnjookerie ũhoro.” Nake akĩmũtũma kuuma Gĩtuamba-inĩ kĩa Hebironi. Rĩrĩa Jusufu aakinyire Shekemu,
english: He said to him, “Go now, see whether it is well with your brothers, and well with the flock; and bring me word again.” So he sent him out of the valley of Hebron, and he came to Shechem.

kikuyu: Mũndũ wa haaro aheenagĩrĩria mũndũ wa itũũra rĩake, akamũtongoria njĩra-inĩ ĩtarĩ njega.
english: A man of violence entices his neighbor, and leads him in a way that is not good.

kikuyu: na makĩmũhingĩra nĩ ũndũ gũtiamenyekaga ũrĩa agĩrĩirwo nĩ gwĩkwo. Nake Jehova akĩĩra Musa atĩrĩ, “Mũndũ ũcio no nginya akue. Kĩũngano gĩothe kĩa Isiraeli no nginya kĩmũhũũre na mahiga nyuguto nja ya kambĩ.” Nĩ ũndũ ũcio kĩũngano kĩu gĩkĩmũtwara nja ya kambĩ gĩkĩmũhũũra na mahiga nyuguto agĩkua, o ta ũrĩa Jehova aathĩte Musa. Ciohe cia Ndigi Nguo-inĩ Nake Jehova akĩĩra Musa atĩrĩ,
english: After the number of the days in which you spied out the land, even forty days, for every day a year, you will bear your iniquities, even forty years, and you will know my alienation.’

kikuyu: Musa akĩnengera Eleazaru ũrĩa mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai gĩcunjĩ kĩa Jehova, o ta ũrĩa Jehova aathĩte Musa.
english: Moses gave the tribute, which was Yahweh’s wave offering, to Eleazar the priest, as Yahweh commanded Moses.

kikuyu: Mahinda mamwe itu rĩu rĩaikaraga igũrũ rĩa Hema-ĩyo-Nyamũre o matukũ manini; no Jehova aathana makaamba hema, na ningĩ aathana, makoimagara magathiĩ.
english: Sometimes the cloud was a few days on the tabernacle; then according to the commandment of Yahweh they remained encamped, and according to the commandment of Yahweh they traveled.

kikuyu: Jusufu agĩcookeria Firaũni atĩrĩ, “Niĩ mwene ndingĩhota, no Ngai nĩekũhe Firaũni macookio marĩa arenda.”
english: Joseph answered Pharaoh, saying, “It isn’t in me. God will give Pharaoh an answer of peace.”

kikuyu: ya Maluki, aarĩ Jonathani; ya Shebania, aarĩ Jusufu;
english: and their brothers, mighty men of valor, one hundred twenty-eight; and their overseer was Zabdiel, the son of Haggedolim.

kikuyu: Isaaka akĩmarugithĩria iruga, nao makĩrĩa na makĩnyua.
english: He made them a feast, and they ate and drank.

kikuyu: “Atĩrĩrĩ, andũ aya mombũkĩte na igũrũ ta matu, o na kana ta ndutura igĩcooka itara-inĩ ciacio nĩ a?
english: “Who are these who fly as a cloud, and as the doves to their windows?

kikuyu: meciiria marĩa mehagia marĩ ũthũ na Ngai. Meciiria macio matiathĩkagĩra watho wa Ngai, o na matingĩhota gwĩka ũguo.
english: because the mind of the flesh is hostile toward God, for it is not subject to God’s law, neither indeed can it be.

kikuyu: “Mũrũ wa mũndũ, andũ acio matũũraga kũu kwanangĩku bũrũri-inĩ wa Isiraeli maroiga atĩrĩ, ‘Iburahĩmu aarĩ o mũndũ ũmwe, no nĩegwatĩire bũrũri ũyũ. No ithuĩ tũrĩ aingĩ; ti-itherũ bũrũri ũyũ nĩ ithuĩ tũheetwo ũtuĩke igai riitũ.’ Nĩ ũndũ ũcio meere atĩrĩ, ‘Mwathani Jehova ekuuga ũũ: Kuona atĩ inyuĩ mũrĩĩaga nyama irĩ o na thakame, na mũkainamĩrĩra mĩhianano yanyu, na mũgaitithia thakame-rĩ, inyuĩ no mũkĩĩgwatĩre bũrũri ũyũ ũtuĩke wanyu?
english: “Son of man, those who inhabit the waste places in the land of Israel speak, saying, ‘Abraham was one, and he inherited the land; but we are many. The land is given us for inheritance.’

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo ngĩmooria atĩrĩ: Gĩtũmi gĩa kũndũ kũu gũtũũgĩru mũthiiaga nĩ kĩĩ?’ ” (Nakuo gwĩtagwo Bama* nginya ũmũthĩ.) Gũtuĩrwo Ciira na Gũcookererio
english: Then I said to them, ‘What does the high place where you go mean?’ So its name is called Bamah† to this day.” ’

kikuyu: Andũ arĩa oogĩ magayaga gĩtĩĩo, no andũ arĩa akĩĩgu magayaga njono.
english: The wise will inherit glory, but shame will be the promotion of fools.

kikuyu: Thiiagĩrai njĩra ĩrĩ kũraya nake, mũtigakuhĩrĩrie mũrango wa nyũmba yake,
english: Remove your way far from her. Don’t come near the door of her house,

kikuyu: Mwathani ũrĩa ũhatagĩrĩria andũ arĩa athĩĩni ahaana ta mbura ya rũhuho ĩrĩa ĩtatigagia irio.
english: A needy man who oppresses the poor is like a driving rain which leaves no crops.

kikuyu: Andũ othe a gũkũ thĩ amonaga ta matarĩ bata. Nake ekaga ũrĩa ekwenda na mbũtũ cia kũu igũrũ, na andũ arĩa matũũraga gũkũ thĩ. Na gũtirĩ mũndũ ũngĩhota kũgirĩrĩria guoko gwake, kana amũũrie atĩrĩ: “Nĩatĩa ũguo weka?”
english: All the inhabitants of the earth are reputed as nothing; and he does according to his will in the army of heaven, and among the inhabitants of the earth; and no one can stop his hand, or ask him, “What are you doing?”

kikuyu: o ta ũrĩa irathĩro kũraihanĩrĩirie na ithũĩro-rĩ, ũguo no taguo atwehereirie mehia maitũ magatũraihĩrĩria.
english: As far as the east is from the west, so far has he removed our transgressions from us.

kikuyu: O ta ũrĩa tũrĩ na ciĩga nyingĩ thĩinĩ wa mwĩrĩ ũmwe, nacio ciĩga ici ciothe itirutaga wĩra mũthemba ũmwe-rĩ,
english: For even as we have many members in one body, and all the members don’t have the same function,

kikuyu: Andũ arĩa athingu nĩmamenyagĩrĩra kĩhooto kĩa arĩa athĩĩni, no arĩa aaganu matirũmbũyagia kĩhooto kĩao.
english: “Two things I have asked of you. Don’t deny me before I die.

kikuyu: Maithori makwa matuĩkĩte nĩmo irio ciakwa mũthenya o na ũtukũ, nao andũ manjũũragia mũthenya wothe atĩrĩ,
english: For they didn’t get the land in possession by their own sword, neither did their own arm save them; but your right hand, your arm, and the light of your face, because you were favorable to them.

kikuyu: Magĩcina matũũra mothe kũrĩa andũ a Midiani maatũũrĩte, o ũndũ ũmwe na kambĩ ciao.
english: this is the burnt offering of every Sabbath, in addition to the continual burnt offering and its drink offering.

kikuyu: Igaacũnaga rũkũngũ ta nyoka, ta ciũmbe iria ikurumaga thĩ. Nĩikoimĩra kuuma imamo-inĩ ciacio ikĩinainaga; nĩigacookerere Jehova Ngai witũ na guoya, na nĩigagwĩtigĩra. Nĩ Ngai ũrĩkũ ũhaana tawe, ũrĩa ũrekagĩra matigari ma igai rĩake mehia na ũremi wao? Ndũtũũraga ũrakarĩte nginya tene, no nĩũkenagio nĩ kũiguanĩra tha.
english: They will lick the dust like a serpent. Like crawling things of the earth, they will come trembling out of their dens. They will come with fear to Yahweh our God, and will be afraid because of you.

kikuyu: Mũrũ wa Palu arĩ Eliabu,
english: The son of Pallu: Eliab.

kikuyu: Zeba na Zalimuna makĩĩra Gideoni atĩrĩ, “Ũkĩra, ũtũũrage arĩ we. Tondũ o ta ũrĩa mũndũ atariĩ noguo hinya wake ũigana.” Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Gideoni agĩũkĩra, akĩmooraga, na akĩruta irengeeri iria ciagemetie ngingo cia ngamĩĩra ciao, agĩthiĩ nacio. Ebodi ya Gideoni
english: Then Zebah and Zalmunna said, “You rise and fall on us; for as the man is, so is his strength.” Gideon arose, and killed Zebah and Zalmunna, and took the crescents that were on their camels’ necks.

kikuyu: irio ciake nĩikarũra irĩ nda yake; igaatuĩka ũrũrũ wa nduĩra thĩinĩ wake.
english: yet his food in his bowels is turned. It is cobra venom within him.

kikuyu: Mbeeca nĩ cia bata ũrĩkũ irĩ guoko-inĩ kwa mũndũ mũkĩĩgu, na ndangĩhota kũgũra ũũgĩ, na ndarĩ ngoro ya kwenda gũtaũkĩrwo?
english: A man’s gift makes room for him, and brings him before great men.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo Zedekia akĩĩra Jeremia atĩrĩ, “Ndũkareke mũndũ o na ũrĩkũ amenye ũhoro wa mĩario ĩno, ndũkae kũũragwo.
english: Then Zedekiah said to Jeremiah, “Let no man know of these words, and you won’t die.

kikuyu: Handũ hau hagaatuĩka haamũre thĩinĩ wa bũrũri nĩ ũndũ wa athĩnjĩri- Ngai, arĩa matungataga handũ-harĩa-haamũre, arĩa marĩĩkuhagĩrĩria ho nĩguo matungatagĩre Jehova. No ho hagaatuĩka ha nyũmba ciao o na hatuĩke harĩa hatheru ha handũ-harĩa-haamũre.
english: It is a holy portion of the land; it shall be for the priests, the ministers of the sanctuary, who come near to minister to Yahweh. It shall be a place for their houses and a holy place for the sanctuary.

kikuyu: akĩmatũma kũrĩ Mwathani makamũũrie atĩrĩ, “Wee nĩwe ũrĩa werĩtwo nĩagooka kana tweterere mũndũ ũngĩ?”
english: All the multitude sought to touch him, for power came out of him and healed them all.

kikuyu: Niĩ ndĩ mũndũ wa thayũ; no rĩrĩa ndaaria-rĩ, o mendaga o mbaara.
english: Peace be within your walls, and prosperity within your palaces.

kikuyu: Nyama ciacio nĩ ciaku, o ta ũrĩa gĩthũri kĩa igongona rĩa gũthũngũthio na kĩero kĩa ũrĩo irĩ ciaku.
english: Their meat shall be yours, as the wave offering breast and as the right thigh, it shall be yours.

kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũregaga gũtaarwo anyiitagwo nĩ thĩĩna na thoni, no mũndũ ũrĩa wĩtĩkagĩra gũkaanio atuĩkaga wa gũtĩĩo.
english: The simple inherit folly, but the prudent are crowned with knowledge.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa ngaakorwo ndĩmacooketie kuuma ndũrĩrĩ-inĩ, na ngaamongania kuuma mabũrũri-inĩ ma thũ ciao, nĩngeyonania atĩ ndĩ mũtheru kũgerera harĩo maitho-inĩ ma ndũrĩrĩ nyingĩ.
english: when I have brought them back from the peoples, gathered them out of their enemies’ lands, and am shown holy among them in the sight of many nations.

kikuyu: Ndingĩathĩkĩire arutani akwa, kana thikĩrĩrie ataarani akwa.
english: who winks with his eyes, who signals with his feet, who motions with his fingers,

kikuyu: Ĩra ũũgĩ atĩrĩ, “Wee nĩwe mwarĩ wa maitũ,” naguo ũtaũku ũwĩtage mũndũ wa nyũmba yanyu;
english: Tell wisdom, “You are my sister.” Call understanding your relative,

kikuyu: Atongoragia andũ arĩa menyiihagia na njĩra ĩrĩa yagĩrĩire, na akamarutaga njĩra yake.
english: Don’t hide your face from me. Don’t put your servant away in anger. You have been my help. Don’t abandon me, neither forsake me, God of my salvation.

kikuyu: nĩgeetha mũtuĩke ariũ a Ithe wanyu ũrĩa ũrĩ igũrũ. Atũmaga riũa rĩake rĩrathĩre arĩa ooru na arĩa ega, na akoiria mbura kũrĩ arĩa athingu na kũrĩ arĩa matarĩ athingu.
english: that you may be children of your Father who is in heaven. For he makes his sun to rise on the evil and the good, and sends rain on the just and the unjust.

kikuyu: Andũ ngiri no magũe mwena waku, nao ngiri ikũmi magũe guoko-inĩ gwaku kwa ũrĩo, no ndũngĩkuhĩrĩrio nĩguo.
english: though the wicked spring up as the grass, and all the evildoers flourish, they will be destroyed forever.

kikuyu: Gwĩtheema ngũĩ kũrehagĩra mũndũ gĩtĩĩo, no mũndũ o wothe mũkĩĩgu ahiũhaga kũũgitana.
english: The foolishness of man subverts his way; his heart rages against Yahweh.

kikuyu: Ũtukũ gatagatĩ, kĩndũ gĩkĩhahũra Boazu, nake egarũra, agĩkora nĩ mũndũ-wa-nja wakomete magũrũ-inĩ make.
english: Naomi said to her two daughters-in-law, “Go, return each of you to her mother’s house. May Yahweh deal kindly with you, as you have dealt with the dead and with me.

kikuyu: Atĩrĩrĩ, mangʼũrĩ ma Jehova nĩmagatuthũka mahaana ta kĩhuhũkanio; ti-itherũ makaahaana ta rũhuho rũkũhurutana mũno, igũrũ rĩa andũ arĩa aaganu.
english: Behold, Yahweh’s storm, his wrath, has gone out, a sweeping storm; it will burst on the head of the wicked.

kikuyu: Menyererai gũtikagĩe mũndũ o na ũmwe wanyu ũkwaga gũkinyĩrwo nĩ Wega wa Ngai, na mũtigetĩkĩrie ũndũ mũũru ũhaana ta mũri wa mũtĩ mũrũrũ ũkũre thĩinĩ wanyu, ũcooke kũrehe thĩĩna na ũthaahie andũ aingĩ.
english: To the pure, all things are pure, but to those who are defiled and unbelieving, nothing is pure; but both their mind and their conscience are defiled.

kikuyu: Ndĩrakora atĩ athitangĩirwo maũndũ makoniĩ watho wao, no hatirarĩ ũndũ acuukĩirwo ũngĩratũmire atuĩrwo
english: I found him to be accused about questions of their law, but not to be charged with anything worthy of death or of imprisonment.

kikuyu: Nĩwahũũrire ũmaraya na andũ a Ashuri o nao, nĩ ũndũ ndũngĩaiganirie; o na thuutha ũcio-rĩ, ndwaiganirie. Ningĩ ũgĩkĩrĩrĩria gũtharia o nginya ũgĩkinya Babuloni, bũrũri wa onjoria, no-o na weka ũguo ndwaiganirie.
english: You have played the prostitute also with the Assyrians, because you were insatiable; yes, you have played the prostitute with them, and yet you weren’t satisfied.

kikuyu: Ta thikĩrĩria, wee mwarĩ ũyũ, wĩcũũranie na ũigue: Riganĩrwo nĩ andũ anyu o na nyũmba ya thoguo.
english: “Be still, and know that I am God. I will be exalted among the nations. I will be exalted in the earth.”

kikuyu: Rũithia nyamũ ĩrĩa ĩrĩ ithanjĩ-inĩ, ũkĩrũithie rũũru rwa ndegwa rũrĩa rũrĩ thĩinĩ wa tũcaũ twa ndũrĩrĩ. Ĩkwĩnyiihĩrie, na ĩkũrehere icunjĩ cia betha.
english: Rebuke the wild animal of the reeds, the multitude of the bulls with the calves of the peoples. Trample under foot the bars of silver. Scatter the nations who delight in war.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo andũ othe no kũrĩra maarĩraga magĩcakayaga nĩ ũndũ wa mwana ũcio. Jesũ akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Tigai kũrĩra, ti mũkuũ, nĩ gũkoma akomete.” Nao makĩmũthekerera, tondũ nĩmooĩ atĩ aarĩ mũkuũ.
english: All were weeping and mourning her, but he said, “Don’t weep. She isn’t dead, but sleeping.”

kikuyu: no inyuĩ nĩndĩmũũĩ. Ngamenya atĩ inyuĩ mũtirĩ na wendo wa Ngai ngoro-inĩ cianyu.
english: But I know you, that you don’t have God’s love in yourselves.

kikuyu: Ngĩmenya atĩ rĩathani o rĩu rĩarĩ rĩa gũtũma mũndũ atũũre muoyo-rĩ, norĩo rĩarehire gĩkuũ.
english: For if while we were enemies, we were reconciled to God through the death of his Son, much more, being reconciled, we will be saved by his life.

kikuyu: Maitho makwa marĩnyũrũrũkagia maithori mategũtigithĩria, magaikara o makĩnyũrũrũkaga,
english: My eye pours down and doesn’t cease, without any intermission,

kikuyu: Mũrurumo wa Babuloni gũgĩtahwo nĩũgathingithia thĩ; kayũ ga kĩrĩro gĩakuo nĩgakaiguuo ndũrĩrĩ-inĩ.
english: The earth trembles at the noise of the taking of Babylon.

kikuyu: Nĩndaririe rĩmwe, no ndirĩ na macookio; o na ngĩaria rĩa keerĩ, no ndirĩ na ũndũ ũngĩ nguuga.” Ningĩ Jehova akĩarĩria Ayubu arĩ kĩhuhũkanio-inĩ, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ:
english: I have spoken once, and I will not answer; Yes, twice, but I will proceed no further.”

kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũhithaga mehia make ndagaacagĩra, no ũrĩa ũmoimbũraga na agatigana namo nĩaiguagĩrwo tha.
english: The poor man and the oppressor have this in common: Yahweh gives sight to the eyes of both.

kikuyu: “Gĩcigo kĩu kĩa mwanya mũkaarutĩra Jehova-rĩ, gĩgaakorwo kĩrĩ na ũraihu wa mĩkono 25,000, na wariĩ wa mĩkono 10,000.†
english: It will happen that every living creature which swarms, in every place where the rivers come, will live. Then there will be a very great multitude of fish; for these waters have come there, and the waters of the sea will be healed, and everything will live wherever the river comes.

kikuyu: Jehova nĩatuire itua rĩa kũmomora rũthingo rũrĩa rũthiũrũrũkĩirie Mwarĩ wa Zayuni. Nĩatambũrũkirie rũrigi rwa gũthima,
english: Yes, when I cry, and call for help, he shuts out my prayer.

kikuyu: Kũrathimwo-rĩ, nĩ mũndũ ũrĩa ũteithio wake uumaga kũrĩ Mũrungu wa Jakubu, ũrĩa mwĩhoko wake ũrĩ harĩ Jehova Ngai wake,
english: Great is our Lord, and mighty in power. His understanding is infinite.

kikuyu: Anene mangĩigua atĩ nĩtwaranĩirie nawe, nao moke kũrĩ we makũũrie atĩrĩ, ‘Twĩre ũrĩa wĩrĩte mũthamaki, na ũrĩa nake mũthamaki akwĩrĩte; ndũgatũhithe ũndũ kana tũkũũrage,’ 26hĩndĩ ĩyo nawe ũmeere atĩrĩ, ‘Nĩgũthaitha ndĩrathaithaga mũthamaki ndakae kũnjookia nyũmba-inĩ kwa Jonathani, ndigakuĩre kuo.’ ”
english: But if the princes hear that I have talked with you, and they come to you, and tell you, ‘Declare to us now what you have said to the king; don’t hide it from us, and we will not put you to death; also tell us what the king said to you;’

kikuyu: Nyanagĩrĩra ũhoro wa ũthingu ndĩ kĩũngano-inĩ kĩnene; o ta ũrĩa ũũĩ, Wee Jehova, niĩ ndihingaga mĩromo yakwa.
english: Yes, my own familiar friend, in whom I trusted, who ate bread with me, has lifted up his heel against me.

kikuyu: Ũgũũta ũrehaga toro mũnene, na mũndũ gĩthayo arĩĩagwo nĩ ngʼaragu.
english: Slothfulness casts into a deep sleep. The idle soul shall suffer hunger.

kikuyu: Paũlũ aarĩkia kũheo rũũtha nĩ mũnene ũcio wa thigari, akĩrũgama ngathĩ-inĩ agĩkiria kĩrĩndĩ kĩu na moko. Rĩrĩa kĩrĩndĩ gĩakirire, agĩkĩarĩria na rũthiomi rwa Kĩhibirania. Agĩkĩĩra atĩrĩ:
english: When he had given him permission, Paul, standing on the stairs, beckoned with his hand to the people. When there was a great silence, he spoke to them in the Hebrew language, saying,

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo mũnene wa arĩa maamũtwaragĩra ndibei akĩĩra Firaũni atĩrĩ, “Ũmũthĩ nĩndaririkanio mahĩtia makwa.
english: Then the chief cup bearer spoke to Pharaoh, saying, “I remember my faults today.

kikuyu: Ezara Gũthoma Watho Na rĩrĩ, mweri wa mũgwanja wagĩkinya-rĩ, andũ a Isiraeli magĩkorwo maatũũrĩte thĩinĩ wa matũũra mao. Nao andũ othe makĩũngana marĩ ta mũndũ ũmwe kĩhaaro-inĩ kĩrĩa kĩarĩ mbere ya Kĩhingo kĩa Maaĩ. Nao makĩĩra Ezara ũrĩa mwandĩki-marũa arute Ibuku rĩrĩa rĩa Watho wa Musa ũrĩa Jehova aathĩte andũ a Isiraeli. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, mũthenya wa mbere wa mweri wa mũgwanja, Ezara ũrĩa mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai agĩtwara ibuku rĩu rĩa Watho mbere ya kĩũngano kĩu kĩonganĩte kĩa arũme na andũ-a-nja, na arĩa angĩ othe mangĩahotire gũtaũkĩrwo nĩ ũhoro ũcio.
english: 1 All the people gathered themselves together as one man into the wide place that was in front of the water gate; and they spoke to Ezra the scribe to bring the book of the law of Moses, which Yahweh had commanded to Israel.

kikuyu: Rĩu-rĩ, nĩ kĩĩ gĩgũtũma ũcooke bũrũri wa Misiri ũkanyue maaĩ ma Shihoru? Na ningĩ-rĩ, ũgũthiĩ Ashuri nĩkĩ, ũkanyue maaĩ ma Rũũĩ rwa Farati?
english: Now what do you gain by going to Egypt, to drink the waters of the Shihor? Or why do you go on the way to Assyria, to drink the waters of the River?‡

kikuyu: Mũhaandĩte irio nyingĩ, no mũkagetha o tũnini. Mũrĩĩaga no mũtihũũnaga. Mũnyuuaga no mũtinyootokaga. Mwĩhumbaga nguo no mũtiiguaga ũrugarĩ. Ningĩ ũrĩa ũthũkũmaga, aanyiita mũcaara wake awĩkagĩra mũhuko ũrĩ na marima.”
english: For this is what Yahweh of Armies says: ‘Yet once more, it is a little while, and I will shake the heavens, the earth, the sea, and the dry land;

kikuyu: “Ta twĩre, maũndũ macio mageekĩka rĩ? Na kĩmenyithia atĩ maũndũ macio marĩ hakuhĩ gwĩkĩka nĩ kĩrĩkũ?” Nake Jesũ akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Mwĩmenyagĩrĩrei mũtikanaheenio nĩ mũndũ o na ũrĩkũ.
english: “Tell us, when will these things be? What is the sign that these things are all about to be fulfilled?”

kikuyu: Sanibalati na Geshemu makĩndũmĩra ndũmĩrĩri ĩno: “Ũka, reke tũcemanie itũũra-inĩ rĩmwe werũ-inĩ wa Ono.” No rĩrĩ, maathugundaga kũnjĩka ũũru;
english: For there were some who said, “We, our sons and our daughters, are many. Let us get grain, that we may eat and live.”

kikuyu: Wee Jakubu, nĩ kĩĩ gĩgũtũma warie, na wee Isiraeli ũgateta, ũkooria atĩrĩ, “Mĩthiĩre yakwa nĩĩhithĩtwo Jehova; naguo ciira wakwa wa kĩhooto ndũrũmbũyagio nĩ Ngai wakwa”?
english: Why do you say, Jacob, and speak, Israel, “My way is hidden from Yahweh, and the justice due me is disregarded by my God”?

kikuyu: “Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, Jehova ekuuga atĩrĩ: Inyuĩ nĩmwagĩte kũnjathĩkĩra: mũtianĩrĩire kuohorithio kwa andũ a bũrũri wanyu. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio rĩu nĩngwanĩrĩra ‘kuohorithio’ kwanyu, ũguo nĩguo Jehova ekuuga, nakuo nĩ ‘kuohorithio’ nĩguo mũũragwo na rũhiũ rwa njora, na mũthiro, na ngʼaragu. Nĩngatũma mũtuĩke kĩndũ gĩa gũthũũrwo mũno mothamaki-inĩ mothe ma gũkũ thĩ.
english: “Therefore Yahweh, the God of Armies, the God of Israel, says: ‘Behold, I will bring on Judah and on all the inhabitants of Jerusalem all the evil that I have pronounced against them, because I have spoken to them, but they have not heard; and I have called to them, but they have not answered.’ ”

kikuyu: Magĩcooka makĩoya kanjũ ĩyo yagemetio, makĩmĩtwarĩra ithe wao makĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Kanjũ ĩno nĩ kũmĩona tũramĩonire. Mĩrore wega wone kana hihi nĩ kanjũ ĩrĩa ya mũrũguo.” Nake akĩmĩmenya, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Nĩ kanjũ ya mũrũ wakwa! Nyamũ njũru nĩĩmũrĩĩte. Ti-itherũ Jusufu nĩarĩkĩtie kũũragwo, agatambuurwo icunjĩ.” Ningĩ Jakubu agĩtembũranga nguo ciake, agĩĩkĩra nguo ya ikũnia, na agĩcakaĩra mũriũ matukũ maingĩ.
english: They took the tunic of many colors, and they brought it to their father, and said, “We have found this. Examine it, now, and see if it is your son’s tunic or not.”

kikuyu: Niĩ nĩndamũcookeria ũhoro ũcio ũmũthĩ, no rĩrĩ, mũtiathĩkĩire Jehova Ngai wanyu harĩ maũndũ macio mothe aandũmĩte ndĩmwĩre. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, rĩu menyai wega ũũ: Nĩmũkooragwo na rũhiũ rwa njora, na ngʼaragu, o na mũthiro mũrĩ kũndũ kũu mũrenda gũthiĩ gũtũũra.”
english: I have declared it to you today; but you have not obeyed Yahweh your God’s voice in anything for which he has sent me to you.

kikuyu: ũngĩkorwo ũtegeetwo nĩ ũrĩa uugĩte, ũkagwatithio nĩ ciugo cia kanua gaku-rĩ,
english: you are trapped by the words of your mouth; you are ensnared with the words of your mouth.

kikuyu: Balaki akĩruta igongona rĩa ngʼombe na ngʼondu, na akĩhe Balamu na anene arĩa maarĩ nake nyama imwe.
english: Balak sacrificed cattle and sheep, and sent to Balaam, and to the princes who were with him.

kikuyu: na kuuma kĩrĩma kĩa Halaku, kĩrĩa kĩrorete mwena wa Seiru, nginya Baali-Gadi kũu Gĩtuamba kĩa Lebanoni mũhuro wa kĩrĩma kĩa Herimoni. Akĩnyiita athamaki ao othe, na akĩmahũũra, akĩmooraga.
english: from Mount Halak, that goes up to Seir, even to Baal Gad in the valley of Lebanon under Mount Hermon. He took all their kings, struck them, and put them to death.

kikuyu: Ithuĩ tũtikaamũkĩra igai hamwe nao mũrĩmo ũrĩa ũngĩ wa Jorodani, nĩ ũndũ igai riitũ tũrĩ narĩo mwena wa irathĩro wa Rũũĩ rwa Jorodani.” Nake Musa akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Mũngĩĩka ũguo-rĩ, na mwĩohe indo cianyu cia mbaara mbere ya Jehova nĩguo mũkarũe,
english: “Encamp outside of the camp for seven days. Whoever has killed any person, and whoever has touched any slain, purify yourselves on the third day and on the seventh day, you and your captives.

kikuyu: Mũgacooka mũkoiga atĩrĩ, ‘Tũngĩarĩ muoyo hĩndĩ ya maithe maitũ-rĩ, tũtingĩagwatanĩire nao gũita thakame ya anabii.’ Nĩ ũndũ ũcio mũkaruta ũira wa atĩ inyuĩ mũrĩ njiaro cia arĩa mooragire anabii. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio-rĩ, kĩiyũrĩrĩriei kĩgeri kĩa mehia ma maithe manyu ma tene.
english: and say, ‘If we had lived in the days of our fathers, we wouldn’t have been partakers with them in the blood of the prophets.’

kikuyu: Nacio thaa cia kũrĩa ciakinya, agĩtũma ndungata yake ĩkeere andũ arĩa meetĩtwo atĩrĩ, ‘Ũkai, nĩgũkorwo maũndũ mothe nĩmahaarĩrie.’
english: He sent out his servant at supper time to tell those who were invited, ‘Come, for everything is ready now.’

kikuyu: Mũndũ mũũru arongooragia o ũremi; nĩ ũndũ ũcio nĩagatũmĩrwo mũnene ũtarĩ tha amũũkĩrĩre. Nĩ kaba gũcemania na nduba ĩtunyĩtwo ciana ciayo gũkĩra gũcemania na mũndũ mũkĩĩgu na ũrimũ wake.
english: The rich man’s wealth is his strong city, like an unscalable wall in his own imagination.

kikuyu: Moima Abirona, magĩthiĩ makĩamba hema ciao Ezioni-Geberi.
english: They traveled from Abronah, and encamped in Ezion Geber.

kikuyu: na Pelatia, na Hanani, na Anaia,
english: Pelatiah, Hanan, Anaiah,

kikuyu: “We ndarĩĩaga irio iria irutĩirwo mahooero-inĩ marĩa marĩ irĩma igũrũ, kana agatiirĩra maitho mĩhianano ya andũ a nyũmba ya Isiraeli. Ndathaahagia mũtumia wa mũndũ ũrĩa ũngĩ.
english: who hasn’t eaten on the mountains, hasn’t lifted up his eyes to the idols of the house of Israel, hasn’t defiled his neighbor’s wife,

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo Jehova nĩagatuĩra andũ othe ciira, arĩ na mwaki na rũhiũ rwake rwa njora, na arĩa makooragwo nĩ Jehova magaakorwo marĩ aingĩ.
english: For Yahweh will execute judgment by fire and by his sword on all flesh; and those slain by Yahweh will be many.

kikuyu: Wee Mwathani, arahũka! Ũgĩkomete nĩkĩ? Wĩyarahũre! Ndũgatũtiganĩrie nginya tene.
english: Wake up! Why do you sleep, Lord?† Arise! Don’t reject us forever.

kikuyu: Guoko gwaku kũroikara na mũndũ ũrĩa ũrĩ guoko-inĩ gwaku kwa ũrĩo, ũcio mũrũ wa mũndũ wĩrereire wee mwene. No ithuĩ-rĩ, tũtigatigana nawe o rĩ; tũrurumũkie na nĩtũrĩkayagĩra rĩĩtwa rĩaku.
english: Let your hand be on the man of your right hand, on the son of man whom you made strong for yourself.

kikuyu: Gũtirĩ mũndũ wa njiaro cia Harũni ũrĩa mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai ũrĩ kaũũgũ ũgaakuhĩrĩria nĩguo atware maruta marĩa marutĩirwo Jehova ma njino. Ũcio ũrĩ na kaũũgũ ndakanakuhĩrĩrie atĩ nĩguo arute irio icio cia Ngai wake cia igongona.
english: No man of the offspring of Aaron the priest who has a defect shall come near to offer the offerings of Yahweh made by fire. Since he has a defect, he shall not come near to offer the bread of his God.

kikuyu: Makĩũria atĩrĩ, “Tũgũkĩenda ũira ũngĩ wakĩ? Nĩtweiguĩra ũhoro kuuma kanua gake mwene.”
english: They said, “Why do we need any more witness? For we ourselves have heard from his own mouth!”

kikuyu: Ũndũ mũtaanye ũngĩikario mũno nĩũrwaragia mũndũ ngoro, no kĩĩrĩgĩrĩro kĩhingĩtio gĩtuĩkaga ta mũtĩ wa muoyo.
english: There is a way which seems right to a man, but in the end it leads to death.

kikuyu: Arĩ kũu andũ amwe makĩmũrehere mũndũ ũtaiguaga na ũtaaragia, makĩmũthaitha amũigĩrĩre guoko. Na thuutha wa kũmũtwara handũ keheri-inĩ, haraaya na kĩrĩndĩ, Jesũ agĩtoonyia ciara ciake matũ ma mũndũ ũcio. Agĩcooka agĩtua mata na akĩhutia rũrĩmĩ rwa mũndũ ũcio namo.
english: They brought to him one who was deaf and had an impediment in his speech. They begged him to lay his hand on him.

kikuyu: “O ta ũrĩa gwatariĩ matukũ-inĩ ma Nuhu, noguo gũkaahaana matukũ-inĩ ma Mũrũ wa Mũndũ.
english: As it was in the days of Noah, even so it will also be in the days of the Son of Man.

kikuyu: Kĩu nĩkĩo gĩtũmĩte nyiitwo nĩ kĩmako ndĩ mbere yake; ndeciiria maũndũ macio mothe-rĩ, ngamwĩtigĩra.
english: Therefore I am terrified at his presence. When I consider, I am afraid of him.

kikuyu: “Inyuĩ mũtaiguaga, ta iguai, na inyuĩ atumumu-rĩ, ta cũthĩrĩriai, nĩguo muone!
english: “Hear, you deaf, and look, you blind, that you may see.

kikuyu: “Mũrũ wa mũndũ, wee ũtũũranagia na andũ aremi. Nĩ marĩ na maitho ma kuona no mationaga, na nĩ marĩ na matũ ma kũigua no matiiguaga, nĩgũkorwo nĩ andũ aremi.
english: “Son of man, you dwell in the middle of the rebellious house, who have eyes to see, and don’t see, who have ears to hear, and don’t hear; for they are a rebellious house.

kikuyu: Meere atĩrĩ, ‘Ũũ nĩguo Mwathani Jehova ekuuga: Nĩngũniina thimo ĩno, na matigacooka kũmĩgweta rĩngĩ gũkũ Isiraeli.’ Meere atĩrĩ, ‘Matukũ nĩmakuhĩrĩirie rĩrĩa kĩoneki o gĩothe gĩkaahingio.
english: Tell them therefore, ‘The Lord Yahweh says: “I will make this proverb to cease, and they will no more use it as a proverb in Israel;” ’ but tell them, ‘ “The days are at hand, and the fulfillment of every vision.

kikuyu: Jehova ekuuga atĩrĩ: “Ta rora ũrĩa kĩguũ kĩa maaĩ kĩroima na kũu mwena wa gathigathini; nĩgĩgũtuĩka ta rũũĩ rũiyũru mũno, rũkoina. Megũtherera moinĩre bũrũri na kĩrĩa gĩothe kĩrĩ kuo, na moinĩre matũũra-inĩ na arĩa matũũraga kuo. Andũ nĩmagakaya; arĩa othe maikaraga bũrũri ũcio makaarĩra na magirĩke,
english: Yahweh says: “Behold, waters rise up out of the north, and will become an overflowing stream, and will overflow the land and all that is therein, the city and those who dwell therein. The men will cry, and all the inhabitants of the land will wail.

kikuyu: o na angĩkaagaacĩra arĩ gatagatĩ-inĩ ka ariũ a ithe. Rũhuho rwa mwena wa irathĩro ruumĩte harĩ Jehova nĩrũgooka, rũhurutanĩte kuuma werũ-inĩ; narĩo itherũkĩro rĩake rĩa maaĩ rĩkooma, nakĩo gĩthima gĩake kĩhũe. Nyũmba yake ya mũthiithũ nĩĩgatahwo indo ciayo ciothe cia goro.
english: Though he is fruitful among his brothers, an east wind will come, the breath of Yahweh coming up from the wilderness; and his spring will become dry, and his fountain will be dried up. He will plunder the storehouse of treasure.

kikuyu: ndĩgaatithia kana ĩkue ngoro nginya hĩndĩ ĩrĩa ĩgaatũma gũkũ thĩ kũgĩe ciira wa kĩhooto. Andũ arĩa matũũraga icigĩrĩra-inĩ makehoka watho wayo.”
english: Since you have been precious and honored in my sight, and I have loved you,

kikuyu: Esaũ ahikirie atumia ake kuuma kũrĩ andũ-a-nja a Kaanani: Ada aarĩ mwarĩ wa Eloni ũrĩa Mũhiti, na Oholibama mwarĩ wa Ana ũrĩa warĩ mwarĩ wa Zibeoni ũrĩa Mũhivi,
english: Esau took his wives from the daughters of Canaan: Adah the daughter of Elon, the Hittite; and Oholibamah the daughter of Anah, the daughter of Zibeon, the Hivite;

kikuyu: “Ũriai atũũri othe a Shekemu atĩrĩ, ‘Nĩ ũndũ ũrĩkũ mwega na inyuĩ. Nĩ mwathwo nĩ ariũ mĩrongo mũgwanja a Jerubu-Baali, kana mũthamakĩrwo nĩ mũndũ ũmwe?’ Ririkanai ndĩ wanyu, tuumĩte mũthiimo ũmwe.” Rĩrĩa aa mamawe meerire atũũri a Shekemu ũhoro ũcio wothe, andũ a Shekemu makĩenda kũrũmĩrĩra Abimeleku nĩ ũndũ nĩmoigire atĩrĩ, “Ũcio nĩ mũrũ wa ithe witũ.” Nao makĩmũhe cekeri mĩrongo mũgwanja cia betha* irutĩtwo hekarũ-inĩ ya Baali-Berithu, nake Abimeleku agĩcihũthĩra kwandĩka andũ matarĩ kĩene na a mbũkĩrĩra, arĩa maatuĩkire arũmĩrĩri ake.
english: “Please speak in the ears of all the men of Shechem, ‘Is it better for you that all the sons of Jerubbaal, who are seventy persons, rule over you, or that one rule over you?’ Remember also that I am your bone and your flesh.”

kikuyu: ngĩcooka ngĩgwĩkĩra gĩcũhĩ iniũrũ, na icũhĩ matũ, na tanji thaka mũtwe. Nĩ ũndũ
english: Behold, when the wall has fallen, won’t it be said to you, “Where is the plaster with which you have plastered it?”

kikuyu: na mũndũ-wa-nja warĩ na cuba ya maguta ma goro mũno na manungi wega, agĩũka kũrĩ Jesũ, akĩmũitĩrĩria maguta mũtwe arĩ metha-inĩ akĩrĩa. Rĩrĩa arutwo moonire ũguo, makĩrakara, makĩũrania atĩrĩ, “Ũitangi ũyũ nĩ wakĩ?
english: a woman came to him having an alabaster jar of very expensive ointment, and she poured it on his head as he sat at the table.

kikuyu: Mĩromo ya andũ arĩa oogĩ ĩtambagia o ũũgĩ, no tiguo ngoro cia andũ arĩa akĩĩgu ciĩkaga.
english: When a man’s ways please Yahweh, he makes even his enemies to be at peace with him.

kikuyu: arogoocwo thĩinĩ wa kanitha na thĩinĩ wa Kristũ Jesũ, o nginya njiarwa-inĩ ciothe, tene na tene! Ameni.
english: if indeed you heard him and were taught in him, even as truth is in Jesus:

kikuyu: “Mũrutani, Musa aatwandĩkĩire atĩ mũrũ wa nyina na mũndũ angĩkua atige mũtumia atarĩ na ciana-rĩ, mũndũ ũcio no nginya ahikie mũtumia ũcio watigwo nĩguo aciarĩre mũrũ wa nyina ciana. Na rĩrĩ, kwarĩ aanake mũgwanja a nyina ũmwe. Wa mbere akĩhikania na agĩkua, agĩtiga mũtumia atarĩ na ciana. Nake wa keerĩ,
english: They asked him, “Teacher, Moses wrote to us that if a man’s brother dies having a wife, and he is childless, his brother should take the wife and raise up children for his brother.

kikuyu: Tũkoragwo na gĩkeno rĩrĩa rĩothe tũtarĩ na hinya, no inyuĩ mũrĩ na hinya; na nĩtũmũhooyagĩra mũtuĩke andũ aagĩrĩru kũna.
english: Greet Urbanus, our fellow worker in Christ, and Stachys, my beloved.

kikuyu: Rĩu-rĩ, anga Isiraeli nĩ ndungata, o mũndũ ũciarĩirwo ũkombo-inĩ? Nĩ kĩĩ gĩtũmĩte atuĩke mũndũ wa gũtahwo?
english: Is Israel a slave? Is he born into slavery? Why has he become a captive?

kikuyu: “Kũrĩ indo ithatũ irĩ mũkinyũkĩrie mũgaacĩru, o na nĩ inya ngaacĩru rĩrĩa igũthiĩ:
english: “There are three things which are stately in their march, four which are stately in going:

kikuyu: Nĩnjeheretie mahĩtia maku magathira biũ, o ta ũrĩa itu rĩa mbura rĩthiraga,
english: I have blotted out, as a thick cloud, your transgressions, and, as a cloud, your sins. Return to me, for I have redeemed you.

kikuyu: “O na gũtuĩka ũũru ũrĩ mũrĩo ũrĩ kanua gake, na nĩaũhithaga rungu rwa rũrĩmĩ rwake,
english: “Though wickedness is sweet in his mouth, though he hide it under his tongue,

kikuyu: “Mwarakara mũtikehie”: Mũtikareke riũa rĩthũe mũrĩ o na marakara,
english: “Be angry, and don’t sin.”* Don’t let the sun go down on your wrath,

kikuyu: “ ‘Tondũ ũcio rũciũ kĩroko mũkeyumĩria inyuĩ ene o mũhĩrĩga, o mũhĩrĩga. Na rĩrĩ, mũhĩrĩga ũrĩa Jehova agaathuura nĩũkeyumĩria o mbarĩ, o mbarĩ; nayo mbarĩ ĩrĩa Jehova agaathuura nĩĩkeyumĩria o nyũmba, o nyũmba; nayo nyũmba ĩrĩa Jehova agaathuura nĩĩkeyumĩria o mũndũ, o mũndũ.
english: He used to remind you of the scripture of Esaias, which says, “Even if you pass through the fire, it won’t burn you.”

kikuyu: Andũ acio othe marĩ hamwe maarĩ 603,550. No rĩrĩ, andũ a nyũmba ya mũhĩrĩga wa Lawi matiataranĩirio hamwe na andũ arĩa angĩ a Isiraeli.
english: all those who were counted were six hundred three thousand five hundred fifty.

kikuyu: nĩcirĩkweheragia harĩ mũndũ-wa-nja ũcio mũtharia, o na kuuma kũrĩ mũtumia ũcio ũtarĩ mwĩhokeku, hamwe na ciugo ciake cia kũheenanĩrĩria.
english: that they may keep you from the strange woman, from the foreigner who flatters with her words.

kikuyu: “Atĩrĩrĩ, wee Jakubu, ta thikĩrĩria, o na wee Isiraeli ũrĩa niĩ njĩtĩĩte: Niĩ nĩ niĩ Ngai; nĩ niĩ wa mbere, o na ningĩ nĩ niĩ wa kũrigĩrĩria.
english: Behold, these shall come from afar, and behold, these from the north and from the west, and these from the land of Sinim.”

kikuyu: One, ũmũthĩ nĩndagũtua mũnene wa ndũrĩrĩ o na wa mothamaki, nĩgeetha ũmunyage na ũmomorage, wanangage na ũngʼaũranagie, na wakage na ũhaandage.”
english: Behold, I have today set you over the nations and over the kingdoms, to uproot and to tear down, to destroy and to overthrow, to build and to plant.”

kikuyu: Ũtukũ gatagatĩ njarahũkaga ngũcookerie ngaatho nĩ ũndũ wa mawatho maku ma kĩhooto.
english: At midnight I will rise to give thanks to you, because of your righteous ordinances.

kikuyu: nĩwe waringire athamaki maarĩ anene, Wendo wake nĩ wa tene na tene.
english: to him who struck great kings, for his loving kindness endures forever;

kikuyu: Ndũkanareke ciehere maitho-inĩ maku, ciigage ngoro-inĩ yaku;
english: For the ways of man are before Yahweh’s eyes.  Sheol is the place of the dead.

kikuyu: Aaciiragĩrĩra andũ arĩa athĩĩni na arĩa abatari, na nĩ ũndũ ũcio maũndũ make mothe makĩgaacĩra. Githĩ ũguo tiguo kũmenyana na niĩ?” ũguo nĩguo Jehova ekuuga.
english: He judged the cause of the poor and needy; so then it was well. Wasn’t this to know me?” says Yahweh.

kikuyu: Kwĩhinga kũrĩa irio kwa mũthemba ũcio wa mũndũ kwĩnyiihia mũthenya o ũmwe tu-rĩ, nĩkuo niĩ thuurĩte? Kwĩhinga no kũinamia mũtwe ta ithanjĩ, na gũkomera nguo ya ikũnia, o na mũhu? * 58:1 Karumbeta kaahũthagĩrwo gwĩta thigari nĩguo ithiĩ mbaara.
english: Is this the fast that I have chosen? A day for a man to humble his soul? Is it to bow down his head like a reed, and to spread sackcloth and ashes under himself? Will you call this a fast, and an acceptable day to Yahweh?

kikuyu: Andũ arĩa mendaga kũnjũraga nĩ mĩtego mandegagĩra, arĩa mendaga kũnjĩka ũũru maaragia ũrĩa mangĩnyũnũha; mũthenya wothe mathugundaga o maheeni.
english: “Hear my prayer, Yahweh, and give ear to my cry. Don’t be silent at my tears. For I am a stranger with you, a foreigner, as all my fathers were.

kikuyu: na agatũtua andũ a ũthamaki na athĩnjĩri-Ngai, nĩguo tũtungatage Ngai na Ithe wake:† ũgooci na wathani irogĩa kũrĩ we tene na tene! Ameni.
english: and he made us to be a Kingdom, priests* to his God and Father—to him be the glory and the dominion forever and ever. Amen.

kikuyu: Nĩngũhingĩria Jehova mĩĩhĩtwa ĩrĩa ndehĩtire andũ ake othe marĩ ho,
english: But all things are of God, who reconciled us to himself through Jesus Christ, and gave to us the ministry of reconciliation;

kikuyu: Matukũ-inĩ macio Isiraeli gũtiarĩ na mũthamaki; o mũndũ ekaga ũrĩa oonaga kwagĩrĩire.
english: In those days there was no king in Israel. Everyone did that which was right in his own eyes.

kikuyu: igũrũ rĩa Kĩhingo kĩa Efiraimu, na Kĩhingo kĩa Jeshana, na Kĩhingo gĩa Thamaki, na Mũthiringo wa Hananeli, na Mũthiringo wa Igana, nginya Kĩhingo-inĩ kĩa Ngʼondu. Makĩrũgama Kĩhingo-inĩ kĩa Mũrangĩri.
english: and above the gate of Ephraim, and by the old gate, and by the fish gate, the tower of Hananel, and the tower of Hammeah, even to the sheep gate; and they stood still in the gate of the guard.

kikuyu: O ũmwe aarĩ na mothiũ mana, na mathagu mana, na rungu rwa mathagu mao nĩ haarĩ na kĩndũ gĩatariĩ ta moko ma mũndũ.
english: Every one had four faces, and every one four wings. The likeness of the hands of a man was under their wings.

kikuyu: Nuhu aarĩ na mĩaka magana matandatũ rĩrĩa mũiyũro ũcio wa maaĩ waiyũrire thĩ. Nake Nuhu na ariũ ake na mũtumia wake na atumia a ariũ ake magĩtoonya thabina morĩre mũiyũro ũcio wa maaĩ.
english: Noah was six hundred years old when the flood of waters came on the earth.  or, giants  A cubit is the length from the tip of the middle finger to the elbow on a man’s arm, or about

kikuyu: Mũtitũ wa Lebanoni-rĩ, ndũngĩigania ngũ cia mwaki wa kĩgongona, kana nyamũ ciakuo ciigane kũmũrutĩra magongona ma njino.
english: Live as free people, yet not using your freedom for a cloak of wickedness, but as bondservants of God.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo aaririe, akĩarahũra kĩhuhũkanio kĩrĩa gĩatũmire iria rĩgĩe na makũmbĩ.
english: For he commands, and raises the stormy wind, which lifts up its waves.

kikuyu: Niĩ ndĩ hakuhĩ gũgũitĩrĩria mangʼũrĩ makwa, na ndĩĩrute marakara nawe; nĩngagũtuĩra ciira kũringana na mĩthiĩre yaku, na ngũrĩhe nĩ ũndũ wa mĩtugo yaku yothe ĩrĩ magigi.
english: Then he said to me, “Son of man, dig now in the wall.” When I had dug in the wall, I saw a door.

kikuyu: Wee ũrĩ mũthaka mũno gũkĩra andũ othe, nacio iromo ciaku nĩciitĩrĩirio maũndũ ma ũtugi, kuona atĩ Ngai nĩakũrathimĩte nginya tene.
english: You are the most excellent of the sons of men. Grace has anointed your lips, therefore God has blessed you forever.

kikuyu: “ ‘Mweri wa mbere, mũthenya wa ikũmi na ĩna wa mweri o ũcio nĩguo mũrĩgĩaga na gĩathĩ kĩa Bathaka, ihinda rĩa mĩthenya mũgwanja, ihinda rĩrĩa mũrĩrĩĩaga mĩgate ĩtarĩ mĩĩkĩre ndawa ya kũimbia.
english: “ ‘ “In the first month, on the fourteenth day of the month, you shall have the Passover, a feast of seven days; unleavened bread shall be eaten.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo ũrĩa wothe ũhingagia watho wothe, no akahĩngwo nĩ kaũndũ o kamwe ka guo, mũndũ ũcio nĩagararĩte watho wothe.
english: For whoever keeps the whole law, and yet stumbles in one point, he has become guilty of all.

kikuyu: Nĩacookire akĩgĩa na aanake mũgwanja na airĩtu atatũ.
english: He had also seven sons and three daughters.

kikuyu: Nĩthĩĩnĩkĩte mũno; Wee Jehova, tũũria muoyo wakwa, kũringana na kiugo gĩaku.
english: I am afflicted very much. Revive me, Yahweh, according to your word.

kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo mũmaraya atũmaga mũndũ athĩĩne nginya ahooyage kenyũ ka mũgate, na mũtumia mũtharia aguĩmaga muoyo waku guo mwene.
english: for she has thrown down many wounded. Yes, all her slain are a mighty army.

kikuyu: Herithagia mũrũguo, na nĩagatũma ũgĩe na thayũ; nĩagatũma ũgĩe na gĩkeno ngoro-inĩ.
english: “The eye that mocks at his father, and scorns obedience to his mother, the ravens of the valley shall pick it out, the young eagles shall eat it.

kikuyu: njagathi no inyiitwo na rũhĩ, no ĩtũũraga thĩinĩ wa nyũmba cia athamaki.
english: You can catch a lizard with your hands, yet it is in kings’ palaces.

kikuyu: Mũtikanethaahie na kĩũmbe o na kĩmwe gĩacio. Mũtikaneĩkĩre thaahu nĩ ũndũ wacio kana mũreke imũgwatie thaahu.
english: You shall not make yourselves abominable with any creeping thing that creeps. You shall not make yourselves unclean with them, that you should be defiled by them.

kikuyu: Mwathani Jehova ekuuga ũũ: “Atĩrĩrĩ, nĩngeeta ndũrĩrĩ, njooke nyambararie bendera yakwa kũrĩ andũ a mabũrũri; nao nĩmakarehe ariũ aku mamakuuĩte na moko, na makuue airĩtu aku na ciande ciao.
english: The Lord Yahweh says, “Behold, I will lift up my hand to the nations, and lift up my banner to the peoples. They shall bring your sons in their bosom, and your daughters shall be carried on their shoulders.

kikuyu: Aakorwo nĩ mũndũ wa mĩaka mĩrongo ĩtandatũ kana makĩrĩa, ũgaatua irĩhi rĩa mũndũ mũrũme rĩtuĩke nĩ cekeri ikũmi na ithano,§ na rĩa mũndũ-wa-nja nĩ cekeri ikũmi.*
english: You shall chase your enemies, and they shall fall before you by the sword.

kikuyu: Nĩngananga ũgo wanyu, na mũtigacooka kũragũra rĩngĩ.
english: I will destroy witchcraft from your hand. You shall have no soothsayers.

kikuyu: Jehova aathana makaamba hema, na ningĩ Jehova aathana makoimagara magathiĩ. Maathĩkagĩra watho wa Jehova kũringana na ũrĩa aathaga Musa.
english: At the commandment of Yahweh they encamped, and at the commandment of Yahweh they traveled. They kept Yahweh’s command, at the commandment of Yahweh by Moses.

kikuyu: Nake akĩrehe kaana agĩkarũgamia gatagatĩ kao. Na aarĩkia gũkaiyũkia moko-inĩ make, akĩmeera atĩrĩ,
english: He took a little child and set him in the middle of them. Taking him in his arms, he said to them,

kikuyu: Atĩrĩrĩ, Jehova arooka arĩ na mwaki, na ngaari ciake cia ita ihaana ta kĩhuhũkanio kĩnene; akaamũikũrũkĩria marakara make arĩ na mangʼũrĩ, na amũkũũme na ikũũmana rĩhaana ta nĩnĩmbĩ cia mwaki.
english: For, behold, Yahweh will come with fire, and his chariots will be like the whirlwind; to give his anger with fierceness, and his rebuke with flames of fire.

kikuyu: Cumbĩ ũcio ndũrĩ bata gũitwo tĩĩri-inĩ o na kana gũitwo kĩara-inĩ, nĩgũteeo ũteagwo. “Ũrĩa ũrĩ na matũ ma kũigua, nĩakĩigue.”
english: Behold, your house is left to you desolate. I tell you, you will not see me until you say, ‘Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord!’ ” *

kikuyu: agĩĩta andũ a Isiraeli othe, na athuuri ao, na atongoria, na atuithania a maciira, na anene, akĩmeera atĩrĩ: “Rĩu niĩ ndĩ mũkũrũ na ngaingĩhia mĩaka.
english: Joshua called for all Israel, for their elders and for their heads, and for their judges and for their officers, and said to them, “I am old and well advanced in years.

kikuyu: Wee ũthondekete ũrĩrĩ waku karĩma-inĩ karĩa karaaya na igũrũ, na gatũũgĩru; na kũu igũrũ nĩkuo waambatire, ũgĩthiĩ kũrutĩra magongona maku kuo.
english: Isn’t it to distribute your bread to the hungry, and that you bring the poor who are cast out to your house? When you see the naked, that you cover him;

kikuyu: Aathiire o nginya kĩhingo-inĩ kĩa mũciĩ wa mũthamaki, tondũ gũtirĩ mũndũ wetĩkagĩrio gũtoonya kũu thĩinĩ ehumbĩte nguo ya ikũnia.
english: All the king’s servants who were in the king’s gate bowed down and paid homage to Haman, for the king had so commanded concerning him. But Mordecai didn’t bow down or pay him homage.

kikuyu: Atongoria a Juda mahaana andũ arĩa meheragia mahiga ma mĩhaka. Nĩngamaitĩrĩria mangʼũrĩ makwa, taarĩ kĩguũ kĩa maaĩ.
english: The princes of Judah are like those who remove a landmark. I will pour out my wrath on them like water.

kikuyu: “No rĩrĩ, arũgamaga arĩ o wiki; nũũ ũngĩhota kũregana nake? Ekaga o ũrĩa ekwenda.
english: But he stands alone, and who can oppose him? What his soul desires, even that he does.

kikuyu: Macio nĩmo mawatho marĩa makoniĩ indo inyiitĩtwo nĩ ũgumu, irĩ cia guoya wa ngʼondu, kana taama wa gatani, kana ndigi njogothe, kana taama mũtume, kana kĩndũ o gĩothe kĩa rũũa, nĩmo mawatho ma gũtua kana indo icio irĩ na thaahu kana itirĩ na thaahu.
english: This is the law of the plague of mildew in a garment of wool or linen, either in the warp, or the woof, or in anything of skin, to pronounce it clean, or to pronounce it unclean.

kikuyu: we wiki nĩwe wĩkaga maũndũ manene ma magegania, Wendo wake nĩ wa tene na tene.
english: to him who alone does great wonders, for his loving kindness endures forever;

kikuyu: “Rubeni, ũrĩ irigithathi rĩakwa, ũhoti wakwa, kĩonithania kĩa mbere kĩa hinya wakwa; ũkĩrĩte arĩa angĩ na gũtĩĩka, ũkamakĩra na hinya.
english: “Reuben, you are my firstborn, my might, and the beginning of my strength, excelling in dignity, and excelling in power.

kikuyu: na mũthamaki wa Taanaka, ũmwe, na mũthamaki wa Megido, ũmwe,
english: the king of Taanach, one; the king of Megiddo, one;

kikuyu: Ũcio nĩguo warĩ mũigana wa mbarĩ cia Agerishoni arĩa maatungataga thĩinĩ wa Hema-ya-Gũtũnganwo. Musa na Harũni maamatarire kũringana na watho wa Jehova.
english: Nabuchodonosor also took some of the Lord’s holy vessels, carried them away, and stored them in his own temple at Babylon.

kikuyu: Korwo mũrĩ a gũkũ thĩ, andũ a thĩ no mamwende ta mũrĩ ao. No rĩrĩ, inyuĩ mũtirĩ a gũkũ thĩ, no nĩndĩmũthuurĩte kuuma kũrĩ andũ a thĩ. Nĩkĩo andũ a thĩ mamũthũire.
english: Therefore Jesus perceived that they wanted to ask him, and he said to them, “Do you inquire among yourselves concerning this, that I said, ‘A little while, and you won’t see me, and again a little while, and you will see me’?

kikuyu: “Inyuĩ no inyuĩ ndĩthuurĩire kuuma harĩ nyũmba ciothe cia thĩ; nĩ ũndũ ũcio nĩngamũherithia tondũ wa mehia manyu mothe.”
english: “I have only chosen you of all the families of the earth. Therefore I will punish you for all of your sins.”

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo andũ a Isiraeli makĩũria atĩrĩ, “Mĩhĩrĩga-inĩ yothe ya Isiraeli-rĩ, nũũ ũtokĩte kũngana haha mbere ya Jehova?” Nĩgũkorwo nĩmeehĩtĩte mwĩhĩtwa mũrũmu atĩ ũrĩa ũkwaga gũũka kĩũngano mbere ya Jehova kũu Mizipa ti-itherũ nĩagĩrĩire kũũragwo. Na rĩrĩ, andũ a Isiraeli magĩcakaya nĩ ũndũ wa ariũ a ithe wao, andũ a Benjamini, na makiuga atĩrĩ, “Ũmũthĩ mũhĩrĩga ũmwe nĩmweherie kuuma Isiraeli.
english: The children of Israel said, “Who is there among all the tribes of Israel who didn’t come up in the assembly to Yahweh?” For they had made a great oath concerning him who didn’t come up to Yahweh to Mizpah, saying, “He shall surely be put to death.”

kikuyu: Nĩegũkũrehera ndũmĩrĩri ĩrĩa ĩgũtũma wee na nyũmba yaku yothe mũhonoke.’
english: who will speak to you words by which you will be saved, you and all your house.’

kikuyu: Andũ a Madimena nĩ kũũra maroora; nao andũ a Gebimu nĩ kwĩhitha marehitha.
english: Madmenah is a fugitive. The inhabitants of Gebim flee for safety.

kikuyu: Nĩngakũhũgũrania na ngũguucũrũrie. Nĩngakũruta kũndũ kũraya mwena wa gathigathini, ngũtware ũgookĩrĩre irĩma cia bũrũri wa Isiraeli. Ningĩ nĩngagũtha ũta waku uume guoko-inĩ gwaku kwa ũmotho, na ndũme mĩguĩ yaku ĩgwe yume guoko-inĩ gwaku kwa ũrĩo.
english: I will turn you around, will lead you on, and will cause you to come up from the uttermost parts of the north; and I will bring you onto the mountains of Israel.

kikuyu: O na inyuĩ mũrĩ amwe a acio metĩtwo matuĩke a Jesũ Kristũ.
english: who “will pay back to everyone according to their works:”*

kikuyu: Handũ ha ũguo-rĩ, wee nĩwĩnenehetie makĩria, ũgakararia Mwathani-wa-Igũrũ. Wetirie ikombe iria ciarutĩtwo hekarũ-inĩ yake, ũkĩreherwo, nawe na andũ aku arĩa marĩ igweta, na atumia aku na thuriya ciaku, mũkĩnyua ndibei nacio. Na inyuĩ mũkĩgooca ngai cia betha na cia thahabu, na cia gĩcango, na cia kĩgera, na cia mĩtĩ, o na cia mahiga, iria itoonaga, kana ikaigua, kana igakũũrana ũndũ. No rĩrĩ, wee ndũigana gũtĩĩa Ngai ũrĩa ũgwete muoyo waku guoko-inĩ gwake, na akamenya mĩthiĩre yaku yothe. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio agĩtũma guoko kũu gũũke kwandĩke maandĩko macio.
english: but have lifted up yourself against the Lord of heaven; and they have brought the vessels of his house before you, and you and your lords, your wives, and your concubines, have drunk wine from them. You have praised the gods of silver and gold, of bronze, iron, wood, and stone, which don’t see, or hear, or know; and you have not glorified the God in whose hand your breath is, and whose are all your ways.

kikuyu: Gideoni agĩthondeka thahabu ĩyo ĩgĩtuĩka ebodi, ĩrĩa aigire kũu Ofira, itũũra rĩake. Isiraeli othe * 8:8 Penieli nĩho Jakubu aagianĩire na Ngai. † 8:26 nĩ ta kilo
english: Gideon made an ephod out of it, and put it in Ophrah, his city. Then all Israel played the prostitute with it there; and it became a snare to Gideon and to his house.

kikuyu: Ti-itherũ andũ arĩa matũũraga icigĩrĩra-inĩ nĩ niĩ metagĩrĩra; matongoretio nĩ marikabu cia Tarishishi, ikĩrehe ariũ anyu kuuma kũndũ kũraya, marĩ na betha na thahabu ciao nĩ ũndũ wa gũtĩĩa Jehova Ngai wanyu, Ũrĩa Mũtheru wa Isiraeli, nĩgũkorwo nĩakũheete riiri.
english: Surely the islands will wait for me, and the ships of Tarshish first, to bring your sons from far away, their silver and their gold with them, for the name of Yahweh your God, and for the Holy One of Israel, because he has glorified you.

kikuyu: Agĩtũma andũ bũrũri wothe wa Manase, akĩmeera meeohe indo cia mbaara, o na agĩtũmana mabũrũri ma Asheri, na Zebuluni, na Nafitali, o nao makĩambata makamatũnge. Nake Gideoni akĩĩra Ngai atĩrĩ, “Ũngĩkorwo nĩũkũhonokia Isiraeli na guoko gwakwa o ta ũrĩa weranĩire-rĩ,
english: He sent messengers throughout all Manasseh, and they also were gathered together to follow him. He sent messengers to Asher, to Zebulun, and to Naphtali; and they came up to meet them.

kikuyu: Atĩrĩrĩ, angĩkorwo wee wĩĩtaga Mũyahudi; na ũngĩkorwo wĩhocagia ũhoro-inĩ wa watho na nĩwĩĩrahagĩra ngwatanĩro yaku na Ngai;
english: Indeed you bear the name of a Jew, rest on the law, glory in God,

kikuyu: “Arĩria kĩũngano gĩothe kĩa Isiraeli, ũmeere atĩrĩ, ‘Tuĩkai atheru tondũ niĩ, Jehova Ngai wanyu, ndĩ mũtheru.
english: “Speak to all the congregation of the children of Israel, and tell them, ‘You shall be holy; for I, Yahweh your God, am holy.

kikuyu: Andũ othe nĩmaiguĩte ngumo ya wathĩki wanyu, nĩ ũndũ ũcio nĩnjiyũrĩtwo nĩ gĩkeno nĩ ũndũ wanyu; no nĩngwenda mũgĩe na ũũgĩ harĩ maũndũ marĩa mega, na mwage ũcuuke harĩ maũndũ marĩa mooru.
english: in the power of signs and wonders, in the power of God’s Spirit; so that from Jerusalem and around as far as to Illyricum, I have fully preached the Good News of Christ;

kikuyu: Ciũmbe ciothe iria irĩ mĩhũmũ ya muoyo igĩũka kũrĩ Nuhu igĩtoonya thabina igĩrĩ igĩrĩ.
english: This is how you shall make it. The length of the ship shall be three hundred cubits,‡ its width fifty cubits, and its height thirty cubits.

kikuyu: Mĩthenya ĩtatũ ĩtanathira-rĩ, Firaũni nĩegũgũtũũgĩria na agũcookie wĩra-inĩ waku, na ũcooke kũnengagĩra Firaũni gĩkombe gĩake, o ta ũrĩa wekaga rĩrĩa warĩ mũmũhei ndibei. No rĩrĩa maũndũ maku makaagaacĩra-rĩ, ũkandirikana ũnjiguĩre tha, ũngweete harĩ Firaũni, ũndute gũkũ njeera.
english: Within three more days, Pharaoh will lift up your head, and restore you to your office. You will give Pharaoh’s cup into his hand, the way you did when you were his cup bearer.

kikuyu: “Atĩrĩ wee Isiraeli, o na gũtuĩka nĩũtharagia-rĩ, Juda nĩagĩtige kwĩhia. “Ndũgathiĩ Giligali;
english: Don’t hate hard labor or farm work, which the Most High has created.

kikuyu: O thikĩrĩirie ũguo, akĩĩra acio angĩ atĩrĩ, “Mũrũmĩrĩrei, mũtuĩkanĩrie itũũra rĩu inene mũũragane mũtekũiguanĩra tha kana gũcaayanĩra.
english: The sound of the wings of the cherubim was heard even to the outer court, as the voice of God Almighty when he speaks.

kikuyu: Mũraika wa Jehova agĩkora Hagari hakuhĩ na handũ haatherũkaga maaĩ werũ-inĩ; gathima kau kaarĩ mũkĩra-inĩ wa njĩra ĩrĩa ya gũthiĩ Shuri.
english: Be subject therefore to God. Resist the devil, and he will flee from you.

kikuyu: na tondũ Paũlũ aarĩ mũtumi wa hema o tao-rĩ, agĩikarania na akĩrutithania wĩra nao.
english: and because he practiced the same trade, he lived with them and worked, for by trade they were tent makers.

kikuyu: Maakinya kũu magĩcookanĩrĩria andũ a kanitha hamwe, makĩmeera ũrĩa wothe Ngai ekĩte agereire harĩo na ũrĩa aahingũrĩire andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ mũrango wa gwĩtĩkia. Nao magĩikara na arutwo kũu ihinda iraaya.
english: When they had arrived and had gathered the assembly together, they reported all the things that God had done with them, and that he had opened a door of faith to the nations.

kikuyu: Mwĩmenyererei, nĩgeetha ũhoro ũrĩa anabii maaririe ndũkanamũkore, rĩrĩa moigire atĩrĩ:
english: Beware therefore, lest that come on you which is spoken in the prophets:

kikuyu: “Mũirĩtu gathirange, nĩwe Isiraeli, nĩagwĩte, na ndagookĩra rĩngĩ, atiganĩirio bũrũri-nĩ wake mwene, hatarĩ na mũndũ wa kũmũũkĩria.”
english: “The virgin of Israel has fallen; She shall rise no more. She is cast down on her land; there is no one to raise her up.”

kikuyu: O na ningĩ akĩerekeria rũhuho rwa mwena wa irathĩro kuuma igũrũ, na akĩrehithia rũhuho rwa mwena wa gũthini na ũndũ wa hinya wake.
english: He caused the east wind to blow in the sky. By his power he guided the south wind.

kikuyu: “Mĩmero yao ĩhaana ta mbĩrĩra hingũre; nĩmĩ ciao ciaragia o maheeni.” “Mĩromo yao yaragia maũndũ marũrũ ta thumu wa nduĩra.”
english: For the promise to Abraham and to his offspring that he would be heir of the world wasn’t through the law, but through the righteousness of faith.

kikuyu: Rũthanju na kũrũithio ciĩkagĩra mũndũ ũũgĩ, no mwana wa kũrekererio aconorithagia nyina.
english: “The leech has two daughters: ‘Give, give.’ “There are three things that are never satisfied; four that don’t say, ‘Enough!’:

kikuyu: Mũthenya wa ĩnana wakinya, magĩũka nĩguo maruithie kaana kau, nao meendaga gũgatua Zakaria o ta ũrĩa ithe eetagwo,
english: On the eighth day, they came to circumcise the child; and they would have called him Zacharias, after the name of his father.

kikuyu: Mũcirutanĩrie na thenge ĩmwe ĩrĩ igongona rĩa kũhoroherio mehia, yongererwo harĩ igongona rĩa njino rĩa hĩndĩ ciothe, hamwe na iruta rĩa mũtu na iruta rĩa kũnyuuo.
english: Moses did as Yahweh commanded him. He took Joshua, and set him before Eleazar the priest and before all the congregation.

kikuyu: Ũcio nĩguo mũthia wa arĩa othe magĩaga na indo na njĩra ya ungumania; indo icio iniinaga mĩoyo ya arĩa magĩaga nacio. Mwĩmenyererei Mũtikarege Ũũgĩ
english: None who go to her return again, neither do they attain to the paths of life.

kikuyu: Hoorerai harĩ Jehova inyuĩ kĩrĩndĩ gĩa thĩ tondũ nĩeyarahũrĩte kuuma gĩikaro-ini gĩake gĩtheru.
english: Yahweh answered the angel who talked with me with kind and comforting words.

kikuyu: Itĩrĩria ndũrĩrĩ iria itakũũĩ mangʼũrĩ maku, ũmaitĩrĩrie mothamaki marĩa matakayagĩra rĩĩtwa rĩaku;
english: You make us a source of contention to our neighbors. Our enemies laugh among themselves.

kikuyu: Jehova aanjĩĩrire atĩrĩ: “Thiĩ ũrũgame kĩhingo-inĩ kĩrĩa gĩĩtagwo kĩhingo kĩa andũ, kĩrĩa athamaki a Juda mageraga magĩtoonya itũũra-inĩ na makiuma; ningĩ ũrũgame ihingo-inĩ iria ingĩ ciothe cia
english: Yahweh said this to me: “Go and stand in the gate of the children of the people, through which the kings of Judah come in and by which they go out, and in all the gates of Jerusalem.

kikuyu: Andũ arĩa manyuuaga ndibei matigacooka kũnyua makĩinaga rwĩmbo, nayo njoohi nĩĩrũrĩire arĩa mamĩnyuuaga.
english: They will not drink wine with a song. Strong drink will be bitter to those who drink it.

kikuyu: Nĩehokete Ngai, nĩakĩmũhonokie rĩu aakorwo nĩamwendete, nĩgũkorwo oigire atĩrĩ, ‘Niĩ ndĩ Mũrũ wa Ngai.’ ”
english: He came again and found them sleeping, for their eyes were heavy.

kikuyu: “ ‘Mũndũ mũrũme angĩhikia mũndũ-wa-nja na ahikie nyina, ũcio nĩ waganu. Mũndũ mũrũme ũcio na andũ-a-nja acio eerĩ no nginya macinwo na mwaki, nĩgeetha gũtikanakorwo na waganu thĩinĩ wanyu.
english: Remind them of these things, charging them in the sight of the Lord that they don’t argue about words to no profit, to the subverting of those who hear.

kikuyu: ingĩkorwo ndanoona mũndũ agĩkua nĩ ũndũ wa kwaga nguo, kana ngoona mũndũ mũbatari atarĩ kĩndũ gĩa kwĩhumba,
english: if I have seen any perish for want of clothing, or that the needy had no covering;

kikuyu: Naarĩ korwo nĩnjũũĩ kũrĩa ingĩmuona; Naarĩ korwo no hote gũthiĩ kũrĩa aikaraga!
english: Oh that I knew where I might find him! That I might come even to his seat!

kikuyu: na Simoni ũrĩa Mũzelote, na Judasi Mũisikariota, ũrĩa wamũkunyanĩire.
english: Jesus answered them, “Go and tell John the things which you hear and see:

kikuyu: Agĩcooka akiuga atĩrĩ, “Ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ na ma, tiga mũgarũrũkire mũtuĩke o ta twana, mũtigatoonya ũthamaki wa igũrũ. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, ũrĩa wothe wĩnyiihagia ta kaana gaka nĩwe mũnene gũkĩra arĩa angĩ ũthamaki-inĩ wa igũrũ.
english: and said, “Most certainly I tell you, unless you turn and become as little  NU omits verse 21.  A didrachma is a Greek silver coin worth

kikuyu: Ũhonokio wa andũ arĩa athingu uumaga kũrĩ Jehova; we nĩwe kĩĩhitho kĩao kĩa hinya hĩndĩ ya mathĩĩna.
english: But the salvation of the righteous is from Yahweh. He is their stronghold in the time of trouble.

kikuyu: Hema yakwa nĩyanangĩtwo, nayo mĩkanda yayo yothe ĩgatuĩkanga. Ariũ akwa nĩmanjehereire magethiĩra na rĩu matikĩrĩ ho; rĩu gũtirĩ mũndũ mũtigaru wa kwamba hema yakwa, o na kana wa gũtambũrũkia mataama mayo.
english: My tent has been destroyed, and all my cords are broken. My children have gone away from me, and they are no more. There is no one to spread my tent any more, to set up my curtains.

kikuyu: Kũrathimwo-rĩ, nĩ inyuĩ mũhũũtaga rĩu, nĩgũkorwo nĩmũkahũũnio. Kũrathimwo-rĩ, nĩ inyuĩ mũrarĩra rĩu, nĩgũkorwo nĩmũgatheka.
