english: But the humble shall inherit the land, and shall delight themselves in the abundance of peace.

kikuyu: “Nĩ ũndũ ũcio njĩra yao nĩĩgũtuĩka ndenderũ; magaathaamĩrio nduma-inĩ, na kũu nĩkuo makaagũĩra. Nĩngamarehithĩria mwanangĩko mwaka-inĩ ũrĩa makaaherithio,” ũguo nĩguo Jehova ekuuga.
english: Therefore their way will be to them as slippery places in the darkness. They will be driven on, and fall therein; for I will bring evil on them, even the year of their visitation,” says Yahweh.

kikuyu: Atĩrĩrĩ, ũkaai inyuĩ nyamũ ciothe cia gĩthaka, o na inyuĩ nyamũ ciothe cia mũtitũ ũkaai mũrĩe!
english: You went to the king with oil, increased your perfumes, sent your ambassadors far off, and degraded yourself even to Sheol.†  Sheol is the place of the dead.

kikuyu: MARŨA MA PAŨLŨ KŨRĨ AROMA Nĩ niĩ Paũlũ, ndungata ya Kristũ Jesũ, o niĩ njĩtĩĩtwo nduĩke mũtũmwo, na ngaamũrwo hunjagie Ũhoro-ũrĩa-Mwega wa Ngai,
english: Paul, a servant of Jesus Christ,† called to be an apostle, set apart for the Good News of God,

kikuyu: Nĩndarorire, ngĩona magũrũ mana ma ngaari hau mĩena-inĩ ya akerubi acio, o kũmwe gũkagĩa mwena-inĩ wa o ũmwe wa akerubi acio; magũrũ macio maahenagia ta rangi wa thumarati.
english: I looked, and behold, there were four wheels beside the cherubim, one wheel beside one cherub, and another wheel beside another cherub. The appearance of the wheels was like a beryl stone.  or, lapis lazuli

kikuyu: Andũ akwa mahinyagĩrĩrio nĩ ciana, nao andũ-a-nja nĩo mamaathaga. Atĩrĩrĩ inyuĩ andũ akwa, atongoria anyu nĩo mamũhĩtithagia, magatũma muume njĩra ĩrĩa yagĩrĩire.
english: As for my people, children are their oppressors, and women rule over them. My people, those who lead you cause you to err, and destroy the way of your paths.

kikuyu: “Ndungata cia mwene mũgũnda igĩũka kũrĩ we, ikĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, ‘Mwathi witũ, githĩ ndwahaandire mbeũ njega mũgũnda waku? Kaĩ itindiĩ rĩkiumĩte kũ?’
english: But immediately Jesus spoke to them, saying, “Cheer up! It is I! ‡ Don’t be afraid.”

kikuyu: Mahinda mamwe itu rĩu rĩaikaraga o kuuma hwaĩ-inĩ nginya rũciinĩ, na itu rĩehera rũciinĩ- rĩ, makoimagara magathiĩ. Kana nĩ mũthenya kana nĩ ũtukũ, hĩndĩ o yothe itu rĩu rĩehera nao makoimagara magathiĩ.
english: Sometimes the cloud was from evening until morning; and when the cloud was taken up in the morning, they traveled; or by day and by night, when the cloud was taken up, they traveled.

kikuyu: Mũgoocei, inyuĩ araika ake othe, mũmũgooce, inyuothe mbũtũ ciake cia igũrũ.
english: Let Israel rejoice in him who made them. Let the children of Zion be joyful in their King.

kikuyu: Mwathani Jehova ekuuga atĩrĩ: Ti-itherũ o ta ũrĩa Niĩ ndũũraga muoyo-rĩ, mwarĩ wa nyũkwa Sodomu na airĩtu ake matiekire ta ũrĩa wee na airĩtu aku mwĩkĩte.
english: As I live,” says the Lord Yahweh, “Sodom your sister has not done, she nor her daughters, as you have done, you and your daughters.

kikuyu: Andũ acio othe makĩgega mũno nginya makĩũrania atĩrĩ, “Ũndũ ũyũ nĩ ũrĩkũ? Ũyũ nĩ ũrutani mwerũ, wĩ na ũhoti! Nĩ araatha o na ngoma thũku na ikamwathĩkĩra.”
english: They were all amazed, so that they questioned among themselves, saying, “What is this? What can this new doctrine be? For with authority he commands even the unclean spirits, and they obey him!”

kikuyu: na mũthamaki wa Madoni, ũmwe, na mũthamaki wa Hazoru, ũmwe,
english: the king of Madon, one; the king of Hazor, one;

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo tũtingĩhota gwĩka ũndũ ũreganĩte na ũhoro wa ma, tiga o ũrĩa ũrĩ wa ũhoro wa ma wiki.
english: Greet Amplias, my beloved in the Lord.

kikuyu: Heanai matũũra matatũ mwena ũyũ wa Rũũĩ rwa Jorodani na matatũ kũu Kaanani matuĩke matũũra manene ma kũũrĩrwo.
english: for the tribe of the children of Reuben according to their fathers’ houses, the tribe of the children of Gad according to their fathers’ houses, and the half-tribe of Manasseh have received their inheritance.

kikuyu: Nĩmakarehe kĩrĩndĩ gĩgũũkĩrĩre, nao nĩmagakũhũũra na mahiga nyuguto, na magũtinangie na hiũ ciao cia njora.
english: They will also bring up a company against you, and they will stone you with stones, and thrust you through with their swords.

kikuyu: na ithuĩ tũrũmbũiye ũhoro wa mahooya na ũtungata wa kiugo.”
english: But we will continue steadfastly in prayer and in the ministry of the word.”

kikuyu: Mahĩtia makwa nĩmanditũhĩire mũno o ta mũrigo mũritũ itangĩhota gũkuua.
english: Blessed is the man who makes Yahweh his trust, and doesn’t respect the proud, nor such as turn away to lies.

kikuyu: Nĩũgagwĩkĩra ithaga rĩa ũtugi mũtwe waku, na ũkũhe thũmbĩ thaka mũno.”
english: For he who lacks these things is blind, seeing only what is near, having forgotten the cleansing from his old sins.

kikuyu: na rĩrĩ, mĩigua yamerete kũndũ guothe, na mũgũnda ũkaiyũra riya, naruo rũthingo rwa mahiga rũkamomoka.
english: Behold, it was all grown over with thorns. Its surface was covered with nettles, and its stone wall was broken down.

kikuyu: O hĩndĩ ĩyo Boazu agĩkinya oimĩte Bethilehemu, na akĩgeithia acio maagethaga, akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Jehova aroikara na inyuĩ!” Nao makĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Jehova arokũrathima!”
english: They took for themselves wives of the women of Moab. The name of the one was Orpah, and the name of the other was Ruth. They lived there about ten years.

kikuyu: Andũ arĩa athĩĩni marĩrĩĩaga na makahũũna, arĩa marongoragia Jehova, nĩmarĩmũgoocaga; ngoro cianyu irotũũra nginya tene.
english: The humble shall eat and be satisfied. They shall praise Yahweh who seek after him. Let your hearts live forever.

kikuyu: Wahota atĩa kwĩra mũrũ wa thoguo atĩrĩ, ‘Reke ngũrute kĩhuti kĩu kĩrĩ riitho rĩaku,’ o rĩrĩa wee mwene ũrĩ na mũgogo riitho rĩaku?
english: Or how will you tell your brother, ‘Let me remove the speck from your eye,’ and behold, the beam is in your own eye?

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo mahĩtia maitũ nĩ maingĩ maitho-inĩ maku, namo mehia maitũ makoimbũra ũrĩa tũtariĩ. Hĩndĩ ciothe mahĩtia maitũ makoragwo hamwe na ithuĩ, namo mehia maitũ nĩtũmooĩ mothe:
english: For our transgressions are multiplied before you, and our sins testify against us; for our transgressions are with us, and as for our iniquities, we know them:

kikuyu: Niĩ Jehova, nĩ niĩ ndĩũmenyagĩrĩra, na nĩ niĩ ndĩunithagĩria maaĩ hĩndĩ ciothe. Ndĩũrangagĩra mũthenya na ũtukũ, nĩgeetha mũndũ o na ũrĩkũ ndakaũthũkie.
english: The crown of pride of the drunkards of Ephraim will be trodden under foot.

kikuyu: Andũ-a-nja nĩmanyiitĩtwo na hinya gũkũ Zayuni, nao airĩtu gathirange makanyiitwo na hinya matũũra-inĩ ma Juda.
english: They ravished the women in Zion, the virgins in the cities of Judah.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa aatũtũngire kũu Aso, tũkĩmũkuua na marikabu, tũgĩthiĩ nginya Mitilene.
english: When he met us at Assos, we took him aboard and came to Mitylene.

kikuyu: Rũciinĩ tũhũũnagie na wendo waku ũtathiraga, nĩguo tũinage nĩ gũkena na tũcanjamũkage matukũ maitũ mothe.
english: Righteousness and justice are the foundation of your throne. Loving kindness and truth go before your face.

kikuyu: kĩrĩkanĩro kĩrĩa aarĩkanĩire na Iburahĩmu, na noguo mwĩhĩtwa ũrĩa eehĩtire harĩ Isaaka.
english: the covenant which he made with Abraham, his oath to Isaac,

kikuyu: Niĩ ndĩ hinya ta ihiga? Mwĩrĩ wakwa nĩ wa gĩcango?
english: He frustrates the plans of the crafty, so that their hands can’t perform their enterprise.

kikuyu: Tondũ rũrĩrĩ ruumĩte mwena wa gathigathini nĩrũkamũtharĩkĩra, na rwanange bũrũri wake. Gũtirĩ mũndũ ũgaatũũra kuo; andũ nĩmakoora o hamwe na nyamũ.
english: For a nation comes up out of the north against her, which will make her land desolate, and no one will dwell in it. They have fled. They are gone, both man and animal.

kikuyu: Mũthenya ũcio aingĩ makanjĩĩra atĩrĩ, ‘Mwathani, Mwathani, githĩ tũtiarathaga maũndũ na rĩĩtwa rĩaku, na thĩinĩ wa rĩĩtwa rĩaku tũkaingata ndaimono na tũkaringa ciama nyingĩ?’
english: “The lamp of the body is the eye. If therefore your eye is sound, your whole body will be full of light.

kikuyu: Jakubu akĩona Jehova arũgamĩte igũrũ rĩa ngathĩ ĩyo akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Nĩ niĩ Jehova, Ngai wa thoguo Iburahĩmu na Ngai wa Isaaka. Bũrũri ũyũ ũkomete nĩngakũhe, wee hamwe na njiaro ciaku.
english: His mother said to him, “Let your curse be on me, my son. Only obey my voice, and go get them for me.”

kikuyu: Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe ekuuga atĩrĩ: “Naarĩ korwo ũmwe wanyu aahinga mĩrango ya hekarũ nĩguo mũtikanaakie mĩaki ya tũhũ kĩgongona-inĩ gĩakwa! Ndikenetio nĩ inyuĩ na ndingĩĩtĩkĩra iruta o na rĩrĩkũ kuuma kũrĩ inyuĩ.
english: “Oh that there were one among you who would shut the doors, that you might not kindle fire on my altar in vain! I have no pleasure in you,” says Yahweh of Armies, “neither will I accept an offering at your hand.

kikuyu: ‘Nake nĩagaciathaga na rũthanju rwa kĩgera; agaacimemenda o ta ũrĩa nyũngũ ya rĩũmba ĩmemendagwo,’ o ta ũrĩa niĩ heetwo wathani uumĩte kũrĩ Baba. Na ningĩ nĩngamũhe njata ĩrĩa ya rũciinĩ.
english: He will rule them with a rod of iron, shattering them like clay pots,* as I also have received of my Father;

kikuyu: Matukũ maitũ mothe maniinagwo nĩ mangʼũrĩ maku; tũninũkagia mĩaka iitũ na gũcaaya.
english: For all our days have passed away in your wrath. We bring our years to an end as a sigh.

kikuyu: Ti-itherũ, aanake a rĩo nĩmakarũndwo barabara-inĩ ciarĩo; thigari ciakuo ciothe igaakirio ki mũthenya ũcio,” ũguo nĩguo Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe ekuuga.
english: Therefore her young men will fall in her streets, and all the men of war will be brought to silence in that day,” says Yahweh of Armies.

kikuyu: “Benjamini ahaana ta njũũi ĩrĩa ngoroku; kĩroko-inĩ atambuuraga kĩrĩa aguĩmĩte, hwaĩ-inĩ akagayania kĩrĩa atahĩte.”
english: “Benjamin is a ravenous wolf. In the morning he will devour the prey. At evening he will divide the plunder.”

kikuyu: “O ta ũrĩa kwandĩkĩtwo Watho-inĩ, nĩtũrĩĩrehaga marigithathi maitũ ma aanake, na ma ngʼombe, na ma ndũũru cia ngʼombe na cia mbũri ciitũ gũkũ nyũmba-inĩ ya Ngai witũ kũrĩ athĩnjĩri- Ngai arĩa matungataga kuo.
english: also the firstborn of our sons and of our livestock, as it is written in the law, and the firstborn of our herds and of our flocks, to bring to the house of our God, to the priests who minister in the house of our God;

kikuyu: Mĩthenya ikũmi yathira, ndũmĩrĩri ya Jehova nĩyakinyĩrĩire Jeremia. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio agĩĩta Johanani mũrũ wa Karea, marĩ na anene othe a mbũtũ cia ita acio maarĩ nake, o na andũ acio angĩ othe kuuma ũrĩa mũnini nginya ũrĩa mũnene, makĩũngana hamwe.
english: They came into the land of Egypt, for they didn’t obey Yahweh’s voice; and they came to Tahpanhes.

kikuyu: kuuma kũrĩ Hezironi, nĩ mbarĩ ya Ahezironi; kuuma kũrĩ Karimi, nĩ mbarĩ ya Akarimi.
english: of Hezron, the family of the Hezronites; of Carmi, the family of the Carmites.

kikuyu: Nakuo kũu werũ-inĩ matũũra maarĩ: Bethi-Araba, na Midini, na Sekaka,
english: In the wilderness, Beth Arabah, Middin, Secacah,

kikuyu: Ũtukũ ũtheecangaga mahĩndĩ makwa; ruo rwa gũthegenya rũtindigithagĩria.
english: In the night season my bones are pierced in me, and the pains that gnaw me take no rest.

kikuyu: Kĩũria Gĩkoniĩ Ũhoti wa Jesũ Na rĩrĩ, mũthenya ũmwe akĩruta andũ kũu hekarũ-inĩ na akĩhunjagia Ũhoro-ũrĩa-Mwega-rĩ, athĩnjĩri-Ngai arĩa anene, na arutani a watho, hamwe na athuuri magĩũka kũrĩ we.
english: 1 On one of those days, as he was teaching the people in the temple and preaching the Good News, the† priests and scribes came to him with the elders.

kikuyu: nake Dario ũrĩa Mũmedia akĩoya ũthamaki ũcio, arĩ na ũkũrũ ta wa mĩaka mĩrongo ĩtandatũ na ĩĩrĩ.
english: Darius the Mede received the kingdom, being about sixty-two years old.

kikuyu: Nũũ ũngĩũmĩrĩria kũmĩathamia kanua, kau kaiyũrĩte magego ma kũmakania?
english: Who can open the doors of his face? Around his teeth is terror.

kikuyu: Magĩaga nda cia mathĩĩna, na magaciara waganu; nda ciao ithugundaga o maheeni.”
english: They conceive mischief and produce iniquity. Their heart prepares deceit.”

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo nĩnũmĩtie njĩra cia Jehova; ndiĩkĩte ũũru ngahutatĩra Ngai wakwa.
english: For I have kept the ways of Yahweh, and have not wickedly departed from my God.

kikuyu: Arahũkai, inyuĩ arĩĩu aya mũrĩre! Girĩkai, inyuothe anyui a ndibei; girĩkai nĩ ũndũ wa ndibei ya mũhihano, nĩgũkorwo nĩĩhurĩtio kuuma tũnua-inĩ twanyu.
english: Wake up, you drunkards, and weep! Wail, all you drinkers of wine, because of the sweet wine, for it is cut off from your mouth.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo riũa rĩrathaga rĩrĩ na ũrugarĩ mũhiũ wa gũcina narĩo rĩkahoohia mũmera; nakĩo kĩro kĩaguo gĩgaitĩka na ũthaka waguo ũkanangwo. Ũguo no taguo mũndũ ũrĩa mũtongu akaahwererekera o akĩrutaga wĩra wake.
english: For the sun arises with the scorching wind and withers the grass; and the flower in it falls, and the beauty of its appearance perishes. So the rich man will also fade away in his pursuits.

kikuyu: Arĩithi aku othe makaahurutwo nĩ rũhuho, nao athiritũ aku nĩmagathaamĩrio kũngĩ. Hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩũgaconoka na ũnyararithio nĩ ũndũ wa waganu waku wothe.
english: The wind will feed all your shepherds, and your lovers will go into captivity. Surely then you will be ashamed and confounded for all your wickedness.

kikuyu: Magĩcooka makĩhooya makiuga atĩrĩ, “Mwathani, wee nĩũũĩ ngoro ya mũndũ wothe. Tuonie nũũ wa aya eerĩ ũthuurĩte 25nĩguo oe ũtungata ũyũ wa gũtuĩka mũtũmwo, ũrĩa Judasi aatigire nĩguo athiĩ kũrĩa kũrĩ gwake.”
english: whom God raised up, having freed him from the agony of death, because it was not possible that he should be held by it.

kikuyu: inyuĩ ene nĩmwagĩrĩirwo nĩ gwĩtigĩra rũhiũ rwa njora; nĩgũkorwo mangʼũrĩ nĩmakarehithia kũherithanio na rũhiũ rwa njora, na hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩmũkamenya atĩ nĩ kũrĩ ũtuanĩri wa ciira.”
english: be afraid of the sword, for wrath brings the punishments of the sword, that you may know there is a judgment.”

kikuyu: Rĩu-rĩ, hĩndĩ ya kũriũka agaakorwo arĩ mũtumia wa ũ harĩ acio mũgwanja, nĩgũkorwo othe nĩmamũhikĩtie?”
english: Therefore in the resurrection whose wife of them will she be? For the seven had her as a wife.”

kikuyu: Kaĩ arĩa maguucagia mehia na mĩhĩndo ya maheeni marĩ na haaro-ĩ! o acio maguucagia waganu na mĩkanda ta ĩrĩa ĩguucagia ngaari cia ngʼombe,
english: Woe to those who draw iniquity with cords of falsehood, and wickedness as with cart rope,

kikuyu: Wee Ngai nĩũtũtiganĩirie na ũgatũharagania; ũkoretwo ũtũrakarĩire, no rĩu tũcookerere!
english: Hear my cry, God. Listen to my prayer.

kikuyu: Hĩ! Kaĩ kũgĩa ũũgĩ nĩ kwega gũkĩra thahabu-ĩ, gũthuura ũtaũku gũgakĩra kũgĩa na betha!
english: Why is there money in the hand of a fool to buy wisdom, since he has no understanding?

kikuyu: na Apathirusi, na Akasuluhi (Afilisti moimire kuuma harĩ o), na Akafitori.
english: Pathrusim, Casluhim (which the Philistines descended from), and Caphtorim.

kikuyu: Twĩneanĩte kũrĩ andũ a Misiri na a Ashuri nĩgeetha tuone irio cia gũtũigana.
english: We have given our hands to the Egyptians, and to the Assyrians, to be satisfied with bread.

kikuyu: Ndengũ itihũũragwo na mũgogo ũkururĩtio, kana kumini ĩkahũũrwo na kũgũrũ kwa ngaari; ndengũ ihũũragwo na mũtĩ, na kumini ĩkahũũrwo na rũthanju.
english: For the dill isn’t threshed with a sharp instrument, neither is a cart wheel turned over the cumin; but the dill is beaten out with a stick, and the cumin with a rod.

kikuyu: Ambĩrĩriai kũina, hũũrai kĩhembe, na mũhũũre kĩnanda kĩa mũgeeto kĩrĩa kĩgambi wega, o na gĩa kĩnũbi.
english: “How long will you judge unjustly, and show partiality to the wicked?” Selah.

kikuyu: Thahabu nĩ kũrĩ, o na ruru no nyingĩ, no mĩromo ĩrĩa yaragia ũmenyo nĩ ithaga rĩa goro rĩrĩa rĩtonekaga.
english: There is gold and abundance of rubies, but the lips of knowledge are a rare jewel.

kikuyu: na ngoro yakwa ĩkĩheenererio nĩcio na hitho, kana guoko gwakwa gũgĩcikinyĩria kĩmumunyano gĩa gũcitĩĩa-rĩ,
english: and my heart has been secretly enticed, and my hand threw a kiss from my mouth;

kikuyu: Thũ nĩĩthingatĩte, ĩkangurumania thĩ tĩĩri-inĩ; ĩtũmaga ndũũre nduma-inĩ ta arĩa maakuire tene.
english: For the enemy pursues my soul. He has struck my life down to the ground. He has made me live in dark places, as those who have been long dead.

kikuyu: na mũndũ o wothe ũrĩa wonire ũndũ ũcio akiuga atĩrĩ, “Ũndũ ta ũyũ ndũrĩ wonwo kana ũgĩĩkwo, kuuma mũthenya ũrĩa andũ a Isiraeli moimire bũrũri wa Misiri. Teciriei ũhoro ũcio na mũwĩcũũranie, mũtwĩre ũrĩa tũgwĩka!” * 19:21 Gĩĩko gĩa gũthambia mũndũ magũrũ kĩarĩ gĩĩko gĩa kũnyiita mũndũ ũgeni.
english: It was so, that all who saw it said, “Such a deed has not been done or seen from the day that the children of Israel came up out of the land of Egypt to this day! Consider it, take counsel, and speak.”

kikuyu: Mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai ũmwe nĩagereire njĩra ĩyo, na rĩrĩa onire mũndũ ũcio, akĩhĩtũkĩra mũkĩra-inĩ ũrĩa ũngĩ wa njĩra.
english: By chance a certain priest was going down that way. When he saw him, he passed by on the other side.

kikuyu: “Mũrũ wa mũndũ, erekeria ũthiũ waku harĩ Gogu wa kũu bũrũri wa Magogu, ũcio mũnene wa anene a Mesheki na Tubali; mũrathĩre ũhoro wa kũmũũkĩrĩra,
english: “Son of man, set your face toward Gog, of the land of Magog, the prince of Rosh, Meshech, and Tubal, and prophesy against him,

kikuyu: “Mwanangĩko wakwa ũngĩkorwo na uumithio ũrĩkũ, ingĩtoonyerera irima-inĩ? Rũkũngũ-rĩ, no rũkũgooce? No rwanĩrĩre wĩhokeku waku?
english: Have mercy on me, Yahweh, for I am in distress. My eye, my soul, and my body waste away with grief.

kikuyu: Ngai arotũkinyĩria wega wake, na atũrathime, na atũme ũthiũ wake ũtwarĩre,
english: Let God arise! Let his enemies be scattered! Let them who hate him also flee before him.

kikuyu: Mwaki ũgĩakana gatagatĩ-inĩ ka arũmĩrĩri ao; rũrĩrĩmbĩ rũgĩcina andũ acio aaganu.
english: A fire was kindled in their company. The flame burned up the wicked.

kikuyu: akamũguthũkĩra angʼathĩtie, arĩ na ngo yake mata ya hinya.
english: he runs at him with a stiff neck, with the thick shields of his bucklers,

kikuyu: Iria rĩrĩa inene nĩrĩkaiyũra Babuloni guothe;
english: The sea has come up on Babylon. She is covered with the multitude of its waves.

kikuyu: Nĩndĩhĩtĩte ngĩĩgwetaga rĩĩtwa rĩakwa niĩ mwene, naguo ũhoro ũkoima kanua gakwa na kĩhooto, naguo ndũkagarũrũka o na atĩa: Maru mothe nĩmagaturio mbere yakwa; nacio nĩmĩ ciothe nĩcikeehĩta na rĩĩtwa rĩakwa.
english: I have sworn by myself. The word has gone out of my mouth in righteousness, and will not be revoked, that to me every knee shall bow, every tongue shall take an oath.

kikuyu: “ ‘Mwathani Jehova ekuuga ũũ: Mũthenya ũcio wee nĩũkagĩa na meciiria, nawe nĩũgathugunda ũndũ mũũru.
english: so that they will take no wood out of the field, and not cut down any out of the forests; for they will make fires with the weapons. They will plunder those who plundered them, and rob those who robbed them,” says the Lord Yahweh.

kikuyu: Kuuma kũrĩ njiaro cia Harimu nĩ: Maaseia, na Elija, na Shemaia, na Jehieli, na Uzia.
english: Jehoash was seven years old when he began to reign.

kikuyu: “Atĩrĩrĩ, nĩngũũkĩrĩire, wee mwĩtĩĩi ũyũ, nĩgũkorwo mũthenya waku nĩmũkinyu, o rĩo ihinda rĩaku rĩa kũherithio,” ũguo nĩguo Mwathani, o we Jehova-Mwene-Hinya-Wothe, ekuuga.
english: “Behold, I am against you, you proud one,” says the Lord, Yahweh of Armies; “for your day has come, the time that I will visit you.

kikuyu: Atĩrĩrĩ, andũ othe nĩ a maheeni! Ciĩko ciao no cia tũhũ; mĩhianano yao ya gwacũhio ĩhaana o ta rũhuho, na nĩ ya tũhũ mũtheri.
english: Behold, all of their deeds are vanity and nothing. Their molten images are wind and confusion.

kikuyu: Magĩcooka makĩinũka, makĩhaarĩria mahuti manungi wega na maguta manungi wega. No rĩrĩ, mũthenya wa Thabatũ makĩhurũka kũringana na ũrĩa gwathanĩtwo.
english: They returned and prepared spices and ointments. On the Sabbath they rested according to the commandment.

kikuyu: kuuma mũhĩrĩga wa Asheri, aarĩ Sethuri mũrũ wa Mikaeli;
english: Of the tribe of Asher, Sethur the son of Michael.

kikuyu: Ũũgĩ nĩ aretana anĩrĩire njĩra-inĩ, ningĩ agakayũrũrũka arĩ ihaaro-inĩ cia mũingĩ;
english: Therefore walk in the way of good men, and keep the paths of the righteous.

kikuyu: Ariũ a Gomeri nĩ aya: Ashikenazu, na Rifathu, na Togarima.
english: The sons of Gomer were: Ashkenaz, Riphath, and Togarmah.

kikuyu: Kwarĩ mũthenya wa mĩrongo ĩĩrĩ na ĩna, mweri wa gatandatũ, mwaka-inĩ wa keerĩ wa gũthamaka kwa Dario.
english: in the twenty-fourth day of the month, in the sixth month, in the second year of Darius the king.

kikuyu: Jehova nĩakamacookeria atĩrĩ: “Nĩngũmũrehithĩria ngano, na ndibei ya mũhihano, na maguta, indo cia kũmũigana biũ; na ndigacooka kũreka mũtuĩke kĩndũ gĩa kũnyũrũrio nĩ ndũrĩrĩ rĩngĩ.
english: Yahweh answered his people, “Behold,† I will send you grain, new wine, and oil, and you will be satisfied with them;  “Behold”, from “הִ ͏נֵּה”, means look at, take notice, observe, see, or gaze at. It is often used as an interjection.

kikuyu: “Twerekeire kwambata Jerusalemu, na kũu Mũrũ wa Mũndũ nĩagakunyanĩrwo aneanwo kũrĩ athĩnjĩri-Ngai arĩa anene na arutani a watho. Nao nĩmakamũtuĩra kũũragwo
english: inches, although that varies with height of the man doing the measurement. There is also a “long” cubit that is longer than a regular cubit by a handbreadth. (

kikuyu: “We ti mũndũ ta niĩ atĩ nĩguo ndĩmũcookerie, nĩguo tũngʼethanĩre igooti-inĩ.
english: For he is not a man, as I am, that I should answer him, that we should come together in judgment.

kikuyu: Amwe mookire kuuma Efiraimu, arĩa iruka ciao ciarĩ kũu Amaleki; Benjamini aarĩ hamwe na andũ arĩa maakũrũmĩrĩire. Anene a ita cia mbaara nĩmaikũrũkire kuuma Makiru, nakuo Zebuluni gũkiuma arĩa maanyiitaga rũthanju rwa mũnene wa mbũtũ cia ita.
english: Yahweh looked at him, and said, “Go in this your might, and save Israel from the hand of Midian. Haven’t I sent you?”

kikuyu: nĩ ũndũ ũcio mũthamaki akĩnjũũria atĩrĩ, “Nĩ kĩĩ gĩtũmĩte ũtukie gĩthiithi ũguo na ndũrĩ mũrũaru? Ũndũ ũcio no uumanire na kĩeha kĩa ngoro.” Na niĩ ngĩĩtigĩra mũno,
english: The king said to me, “Why is your face sad, since you are not sick? This is nothing else but sorrow of heart.” Then I was very much afraid.

kikuyu: Ũguo nĩguo andũ a Benjamini meekire. O rĩrĩa airĩtu maainaga-rĩ, o mũndũ akĩnyiita ũmwe, na akĩmũkuua agatuĩke mũtumia wake. Hĩndĩ ĩyo magĩcooka kũrĩ igai rĩao, magĩaka matũũra mao rĩngĩ, na magĩtũũra kuo. Na ihinda rĩu andũ a Isiraeli makiuma kũndũ kũu, makĩinũka mũciĩ kũrĩ mĩhĩrĩga yao na nyũmba ciao, o mũndũ kũrĩ igai rĩake.
english: The children of Benjamin did so, and took wives for themselves according to their number, of those who danced, whom they carried off. They went and returned to their inheritance, built the cities, and lived in them.

kikuyu: Abimeleku akĩhũũrana na itũũra rĩu inene mũthenya wothe nginya akĩrĩtaha na akĩũraga andũ a rĩo othe. Ningĩ akĩananga itũũra rĩu inene, na akĩrĩitĩrĩria cumbĩ.
english: Abimelech fought against the city all that day; and he took the city and killed the people in it. He beat down the city and sowed it with salt.

kikuyu: Andũ a Diboni nĩmambatĩte magathiĩ hekarũ-inĩ yao, mambatĩte kũrĩa gũtũũgĩru makarĩrĩre kuo; andũ a Moabi maraagirĩka nĩ ũndũ wa Nebo na Medeba. Mĩtwe yao yothe nĩyenjetwo, na nderu ciao ciothe ikenjwo.
english: For it will be that as wandering birds, as a scattered nest, so will the daughters of Moab be at the fords of the Arnon.

kikuyu: Jehova agĩcinwo nĩ marakara mũthenya ũcio, akĩĩhĩta na mwĩhĩtwa ũyũ:
english: All their cities in the places in which they lived, and all their encampments, they burned with fire.

kikuyu: Mũndũ mwega aaragia maũndũ mega moimĩte mũthiithũ-inĩ wake mwega, nake mũndũ mũũru aaragia maũndũ mooru moimĩte mũthiithũ-inĩ wake mũũru. No ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ, mũthenya wa itua rĩa ciira, andũ nĩmakaheana ũhoro wa ciugo ciothe cia tũhũ iria manaaria.
english: The good man out of his good treasure† brings out good things, and the evil man out of his evil treasure brings out evil things.

kikuyu: Ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ inyuĩ nyenda mũno, menyai ũũ: O mũndũ nĩahiũhage gũthikĩrĩria, no ndakahiũhage kwaria o na kana kũrakara,
english: So, then, my beloved brothers, let every man be swift to hear, slow to speak, and slow to anger;

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo kanitha wa Judea guothe, na Galili, na Samaria ũgĩkenera ihinda rĩa thayũ. Nĩwon- gereirwo hinya; na ũkĩũmĩrĩrio nĩ Roho Mũtheru, na ũkĩongererwo andũ, nao magĩtũũra metigĩrĩte Mwathani. Ainea na Dorokasi Na rĩrĩ, rĩrĩa Petero aaceeraga kũndũ na kũndũ bũrũri-inĩ, nĩathiire gũceerera andũ arĩa aamũre a kũu Lida.
english: So the assemblies throughout all Judea, Galilee, and Samaria had peace and were built up. They were multiplied, walking in the fear of the Lord and in the comfort of the Holy Spirit.

kikuyu: nĩguo aiguĩre maithe maitũ tha, na aririkane kĩrĩkanĩro gĩake gĩtheru,
english: to show mercy toward our fathers, to remember his holy covenant,

kikuyu: Arĩithi acio matikoona kũndũ gwa kũũrĩra, na atongoria a rũũru rwa mbũri matikoona gwa gwĩtharĩra.
english: The shepherds will have no way to flee. The leader of the flock will have no escape.

kikuyu: “Mũndũ angĩgeria gũkũgwetera koigo kamwe-rĩ, hihi wamũkirĩrĩria?
english: “If someone ventures to talk with you, will you be grieved? But who can withhold himself from speaking?

kikuyu: Nĩtũkũringa tũrĩ mbere ya Jehova tũtoonye bũrũri wa Kaanani twĩohete indo cia mbaara, no kũndũ kũrĩa ithuĩ tũkaagaya-rĩ, gũgaakorwo kũrĩ mbarĩ ĩno ya Jorodani.”
english: We will pass over armed before Yahweh into the land of Canaan, and the possession of our inheritance shall remain with us beyond the Jordan.”

kikuyu: Nũũ ũngĩambata kĩrĩma-inĩ kĩa Jehova? Nũũ ũngĩrũgama handũ harĩa hake haamũre? Nĩ mũndũ ũrĩa ũrĩ na moko matheru na akaaga ũũru ngoro, na ũrĩa ngoro yake ĩtahooyaga mũhianano, o na kana akeehĩta na maheeni.
english: Yes, no one who waits for you will be shamed. They will be shamed who deal treacherously without cause.

kikuyu: akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Ngaathikĩrĩria ciira waku andũ arĩa magũthitangĩte mooka.” Agĩcooka agĩathana Paũlũ aikare arangĩrĩtwo kũu nyũmba-inĩ ya ũthamaki ya Herode.
english: “I will hear you fully when your accusers also arrive.” He commanded that he be kept in Herod’s palace.

kikuyu: Angĩkorwo Ngai nĩagoocithĩtio thĩinĩ wake, Ngai nĩekũgoocithia Mũriũ arĩ thĩinĩ wake mwene, na amũgoocithie o narua.
english: If God has been glorified in him, God will also glorify him in himself, and he will glorify him immediately.

kikuyu: Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe ekuuga atĩrĩ, “Niĩ nĩngũũkĩrĩire. Nĩngacina ngaari ciaku cia ita na mwaki, naruo rũhiũ rwa njora nĩrũkaniina mĩrũũthi yaku ĩrĩa mĩĩthĩ. Ndigagũtigĩria kĩndũ gĩa kũguĩma gũkũ thĩ. Nayo mĩgambo ya andũ arĩa ũtũmaga ndĩgaacooka kũiguuo rĩngĩ.”
english: You who have purer eyes than to see evil, and who cannot look on perversity, why do you tolerate those who deal treacherously and keep silent when the wicked swallows up the man who is more righteous than he,

kikuyu: “Mũthenya ũcio mũnabii o wothe nĩagaconoka nĩ ũndũ wa kĩoneki kĩa ũrathi wake. Ndakehumba nguo ya maguoya ya mũnabii nĩguo aheenie andũ.
english: But when day had already come, Jesus stood on the beach; yet the disciples didn’t know that it was Jesus.

kikuyu: Mũthenya ũrĩa Jehova aaneanire Aamori kũrĩ a Isiraeli-rĩ, Joshua akĩarĩria Jehova, andũ a Isiraeli makĩiguaga, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ: “We riũa-rĩ, rũgama o ũguo wĩhaande igũrũ rĩa Gibeoni, O nawe mweri-rĩ, rũgama o ro ũguo wĩhaande igũrũ rĩa Gĩtuamba kĩa Aijaloni.” Nĩ ũndũ ũcio riũa rĩkĩrũgama o ro ũguo, naguo mweri ũkĩrũgama, o nginya rĩrĩa rũrĩrĩ rũu rwerĩhĩirie harĩ thũ ciao, o ta ũrĩa kwandĩkĩtwo Ibuku-inĩ rĩa Jasharu. Riũa rĩarũgamire o kũu matu-inĩ gatagatĩ na rĩkĩamba gũtiga gũthũa handũ ha mũthenya mũgima.
english: Then Joshua spoke to Yahweh in the day when Yahweh delivered up the Amorites before the children of Israel. He said in the sight of Israel, “Sun, stand still on Gibeon! You, moon, stop in the valley of Aijalon!”

kikuyu: Wee ũrĩ mwega, na nĩwĩkaga o wega; nduta kĩrĩra kĩa watho waku.
english: You are good, and do good. Teach me your statutes.

kikuyu: Nĩmeyohage iremba cia gatani mĩtwe, na nguo cia gatani cia thĩinĩ njohero-inĩ. Matikanehumbe kĩndũ o na kĩrĩkũ kĩngĩtũma mathigine.
english: They shall have linen turbans on their heads, and shall have linen trousers on their waists. They shall not clothe themselves with anything that makes them sweat.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩmũkamenya atĩ niĩ ndĩ thĩinĩ wa Isiraeli, na atĩ niĩ nĩ niĩ Jehova Ngai wanyu, na atĩ gũtirĩ ũngĩ; andũ akwa matigacooka gũconorithio rĩngĩ. Mũthenya wa Jehova
english: You will know that I am among Israel, and that I am Yahweh, your God, and there is no one else; and my people will never again be disappointed.

kikuyu: Arĩ kũu agĩkora mũndũ wetagwo Ainea warĩ na mũrimũ wa gũkua ciĩga, na aatũũrĩte akomete gĩtanda-inĩ ihinda rĩa mĩaka ĩnana.
english: There he found a certain man named Aeneas, who had been bedridden for eight years because he was paralyzed.

kikuyu: Magĩcaria ũrĩa mangĩmũnyiita tondũ nĩmamenyire aaragia ũhoro wao na ngerekano ĩyo, no nĩmetigagĩra kĩrĩndĩ; nĩ ũndũ ũcio magĩtigana nake, magĩthiĩ. Kũrĩha Igooti kũrĩ Kaisari
english: “Brother will deliver up brother to death, and the father his child. Children will rise up against parents and cause them to be put to death.

kikuyu: Ũguo nĩguo ndũũrĩte gwaku mũciĩ handũ-inĩ ha mĩaka ĩyo mĩrongo ĩĩrĩ. Ndakũrutĩire wĩra mĩaka ikũmi na ĩna nĩ ũndũ wa airĩtu aku eerĩ, na mĩaka ĩtandatũ nĩ ũndũ wa mahiũ maku; no rĩrĩ, wagarũrire mũcaara wakwa maita ikũmi.
english: These twenty years I have been in your house. I served you fourteen years for your two daughters, and six years for your flock, and you have changed my wages ten times.

kikuyu: na thenge ĩmwe ya igongona rĩa kũhoroherio mehia;
english: one male goat for a sin offering;

kikuyu: “Ningĩ agĩtũma ndungata ingĩ agĩciĩra atĩrĩ, ‘Ĩrai acio metĩtwo atĩ nĩndĩkĩtie kũhaarĩria iruga: Ndegwa ciakwa na ngʼombe iria noru nĩirĩkĩtie gũthĩnjwo na maũndũ mothe nĩmahaarĩrie. Ũkai iruga-inĩ rĩa ũhiki.’
english: Again he sent out other servants, saying, ‘Tell those who are invited, “Behold, I have prepared my dinner. My cattle and my fatlings are killed, and all things are ready. Come to the wedding feast!” ’

kikuyu: Wĩra wothe wa hinya ũrehaga uumithio, no mĩario mĩtheri ĩrehaga o ũthĩĩni.
english: In all hard work there is profit, but the talk of the lips leads only to poverty.

kikuyu: Hĩ! Ĩ meciiria maku matikĩrĩ ma bata harĩ niĩ, Wee Mũrungu! Kaĩ mũigana wamo nĩ mũnene-ĩ!
english: How precious to me are your thoughts, God! How vast is their sum!

kikuyu: ngĩrega Esaũ, bũrũri wake wa irĩma ngĩwananga ũgĩtuĩka tũhũ, narĩo igai rĩake ngĩrĩtua werũ wa gũikaragwo nĩ mbwe.”
english: Behold, I will rebuke your offspring,† and will spread dung on your faces, even the dung of your feasts; and you will be taken away with it.

kikuyu: Nayo hitho ya njata icio mũgwanja wonire guoko-inĩ gwakwa kwa ũrĩo, na ya mĩtĩ ĩyo mũgwanja ya thahabu ya kũigĩrĩra matawa nĩ ĩno: Njata icio mũgwanja nĩ araika a makanitha marĩa mũgwanja, nayo mĩtĩ ĩyo mũgwanja ya kũigĩrĩra matawa nĩ makanitha macio mũgwanja.
english: The mystery of the seven stars which you saw in my right hand, and the seven golden lamp stands is this: The seven stars are the angels§ of the seven assemblies. The seven lamp stands are seven assemblies.

kikuyu: Akĩĩra andũ acio othe atĩrĩ, “Atĩrĩrĩ! Ihiga rĩĩrĩ nĩrĩo rĩgũtuĩka mũira witũ wa ũrĩa tuoiga. Nĩrĩiguĩte ciugo ciothe iria Jehova atwĩrĩte. Nĩrĩo rĩgũtuĩka mũira wanyu mũngĩkaaga kwĩhokeka harĩ Ngai wanyu.” Gũthikwo Bũrũri-inĩ wa Kĩĩranĩro Nake Joshua agĩcooka akĩĩra andũ acio mathiĩ, o mũndũ acooke kũrĩa igai rĩake rĩarĩ.
english: Joshua said to all the people, “Behold, this stone shall be a witness against us, for it has heard all Yahweh’s words which he spoke to us. It shall be therefore a witness against you, lest you deny your God.”

kikuyu: Acookanĩrĩirie mbũtũ cia thigari iria njamba, no ciũkaga ikĩhĩngagwo. Iguthũkaga nginya rũthingo-inĩ rwa itũũra inene; ituĩkaga ta ngo ya kũrĩgitĩra.
english: He summons his picked troops. They stumble on their way. They dash to its wall, and the protective shield is put in place.

kikuyu: Nĩngũmũũkĩrĩra, inyuĩ andũ a Jerusalemu, o inyuĩ mũtũũraga rũgongo igũrũ rwa gĩtuamba gĩkĩ, kũrĩa kwaraganu ndwaro-inĩ cia mahiga, ũguo nĩguo Jehova ekuuga, o wee uugaga atĩrĩ, “Nũũ ũngĩhota gũtũũkĩrĩra? Nũũ ũngĩkĩhota gũtoonya kĩũrĩro-inĩ giitũ?”
english: “Woe to him who builds his house by unrighteousness, and his rooms by injustice; who uses his neighbor’s service without wages, and doesn’t give him his hire;

kikuyu: Maarĩkia kũrĩhwo, makĩambĩrĩria kũnugunĩkĩra mwene mũgũnda.
english: When they received it, they murmured against the master of the household,

kikuyu: Akĩiga ndungata cia andũ-a-nja na ciana ciacio mbere, nake Lea na ciana ciake makĩrũmĩrĩra, nake Rakeli na Jusufu makĩrigia thuutha. Nake we mwene akĩmatongoria, na rĩrĩa aakuhĩrĩirie mũrũ wa nyina, akĩinamĩrĩra, agĩturumithia ũthiũ wake thĩ maita mũgwanja.
english: He put the servants and their children in front, Leah and her children after, and Rachel and Joseph at the rear.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo ũndũ ũyũ wĩkĩtwo nĩ mũtumia wa mũthamaki nĩũkũmenyeka nĩ andũ-a-nja othe, na nĩ ũndũ ũcio nĩmarĩmenagĩrĩria athuuri ao makoiga atĩrĩ, ‘Mũthamaki Ahasuerusu araathanire Vashiti mũtumia wa mũthamaki arehwo mbere yake, no ndaigana gũthiĩ.’
english: The king loved Esther more than all the women, and she obtained favor and kindness in his sight more than all the virgins; so that he set the royal crown on her head, and made her queen instead of Vashti.

kikuyu: TEKEL nĩ ta kuuga: Wee nĩũthimĩtwo na ratiri, ũkoneka nĩũtigairie.
english: TEKEL: you are weighed in the balances, and are found wanting.

kikuyu: Ũndũ ũcio moigire nĩwakenirie gĩkundi kĩu gĩothe. Nao magĩthuura Stefano, mũndũ waiyũrĩtwo nĩ wĩtĩkio na Roho Mũtheru, na Filipu, na Porokoro, na Nikanora, na Timoni, na Paramena, na Nikola, mũndũ wa kuuma Antiokia ũrĩa wagarũrũkĩte agatuĩka Mũyahudi.
english: These words pleased the whole multitude. They chose Stephen, a man full of faith and of the Holy Spirit, Philip, Prochorus, Nicanor, Timon, Parmenas, and Nicolaus, a proselyte of Antioch,

kikuyu: Agĩthiĩ na mbere, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Nĩkĩo ndamwĩrire atĩ gũtirĩ mũndũ ũngĩhota gũũka kũrĩ niĩ atahotithĩtio nĩ Baba.”
english: He said, “For this cause I have said to you that no one can come to me, unless it is given to him by my Father.”

kikuyu: Nĩgeetha ngoro iringĩke, nao arĩa magwĩte maingĩhe, nĩnjigĩte rũhiũ rwa njora rwa kũũragana ihingo-inĩ ciao ciothe. Hĩ! Rũthondeketwo rũhenagie ta rũheni,
english: As it is written, “He who gathered much had nothing left over, and he who gathered little had no lack.”*

kikuyu: Jakubu akĩmaka mũno na akĩnyamarĩka, akĩgayania andũ arĩa aarĩ nao ikundi igĩrĩ, o na ndũũru cia mbũri na ndũũru cia ngʼombe o na ngamĩĩra nacio.
english: Then Jacob was greatly afraid and was distressed. He divided the people who were with him, along with the flocks, the herds, and the camels, into two companies.

kikuyu: Mũthenya ũmwe, ngoma thũku ĩkĩmacookeria ĩkĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Jesũ nĩndĩmũũĩ na nĩnjũũĩ ũhoro wa Paũlũ, no inyuĩ mũrĩ a?”
english: but if they are questions about words and names and your own law, look to it yourselves. For I don’t want to be a judge of these matters.”

kikuyu: Ta thikĩrĩriai ũrĩa arĩithi maraarĩra, makĩgirĩkaga: ũrĩithio wao ũrĩa mũnoru nĩwanangĩtwo! Ta thikĩrĩriai mũraramo wa mĩrũũthi;
english: A voice of the wailing of the shepherds! For their glory is destroyed—a voice of the roaring of young lions! For the pride of the Jordan is ruined.

kikuyu: Nao makĩanĩrĩra, magĩcookia atĩrĩ, “Aca, tũtikwenda ũcio! Tuohorere Baraba!” Na rĩrĩ, Baraba ũcio aarĩ mũtunyani.
english: Then they all shouted again, saying, “Not this man, but Barabbas!” Now Barabbas was a robber.

kikuyu: Wee ũcookagia andũ magatuĩka rũkũngũ, ũkoiga atĩrĩ, “Cookai rũkũngũ-inĩ, inyuĩ ciana ici cia andũ.”
english: You turn man to destruction, saying, “Return, you children of men.”

kikuyu: Rũrĩmĩ rũrĩa rũrehaga kĩhonia nĩ ta mũtĩ wa muoyo, no rũrĩmĩ rwa maheeni rũthuthaga ngoro.
english: Yahweh has made everything for its own end— yes, even the wicked for the day of evil.

kikuyu: Ngai-rĩ, no agĩkũrĩhe kũringana na ũrĩa wee ũkwenda rĩrĩa ũregete kwĩrira? No nginya we ũtue itua, no ti niĩ; nĩ ũndũ ũcio kĩnjĩĩre ũrĩa ũũĩ.
english: Shall his recompense be as you desire, that you refuse it? For you must choose, and not I. Therefore speak what you know.

kikuyu: Mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai nĩagaacinĩra maguta macio kĩgongona-inĩ, no gĩthũri gĩtuĩke kĩa Harũni na ariũ ake. No nginya mũheage mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai kĩero kĩa ũrĩo kĩa maruta manyu ma ũiguano kĩrĩ kĩheo kĩanyu kũrĩ o.
english: The priest shall burn the fat on the altar, but the breast shall be Aaron’s and his sons’.

kikuyu: Anene akuo, na andũ arĩa ogĩ, o na abarũthi akuo, o ũndũ ũmwe na anene akuo, o na njamba ciakuo iria irĩ hinya, othe nĩngatũma marĩĩo nĩ njoohi; magaakoma nginya tene, na matiũkĩre,” nĩguo Mũthamaki ekuuga, o we ũrĩa rĩĩtwa rĩake arĩ Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe.
english: I will make her princes, her wise men, her governors, her deputies, and her mighty men drunk. They will sleep a perpetual sleep, and not wake up,” says the King, whose name is Yahweh of Armies.

kikuyu: Jehova ekuuga atĩrĩ, “Githĩ mũthenya ũcio ndikaananga andũ arĩa oogĩ a Edomu, na nyanange andũ arĩa marĩ ũmenyo kũu irĩma-inĩ cia Esaũ?
english: “Won’t I in that day”, says Yahweh, “destroy the wise men out of Edom, and understanding out of the mountain of Esau?

kikuyu: Thuutha wa mũgambo ũcio kũiguĩka, makĩona atĩ Jesũ aarĩ wiki. Nao arutwo magĩkira ki na ũhoro ũcio, gũtirĩ mũndũ o na ũmwe meerire matukũ-inĩ macio maũndũ marĩa moonete. Jesũ Kũhonia Kamwana kaarĩ na Ngoma Thũku
english: When the voice came, Jesus was found alone. They were silent, and told no one in those days any of the things which they had seen.

kikuyu: Nowe Mariamu akĩiga maũndũ macio mothe ngoro-inĩ yake, na agĩikara akĩmeciiragia. Nao arĩithi acio makĩhũndũka, magĩkumagia na makĩgoocaga Ngai nĩ ũndũ wa maũndũ macio mothe maiguĩte na makoona, o ta ũrĩa meerĩĩtwo. Jesũ Gũtwarwo Hekarũ
english: The angel answered him, “I am Gabriel, who stands in the presence of God. I was sent to speak to you and to bring you this good news.

kikuyu: Kĩgũũta gĩticimbaga hĩndĩ ya irĩma; nĩ ũndũ ũcio hĩndĩ ya magetha gĩathiĩ kũmaatha gĩtirĩ kĩndũ kĩonaga.
english: Wealth adds many friends, but the poor is separated from his friend.

kikuyu: (Nĩ mũnegeni na mũngʼathia, na magũrũ make matikiragĩrĩria mũciĩ;
english: She is loud and defiant. Her feet don’t stay in her house.

kikuyu: Mũgambo ũkĩmwarĩria riita rĩa keerĩ ũkĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Ndũkanoige kĩndũ o na kĩrĩkũ kĩrĩa Ngai atheretie atĩ ti kĩamũre.”
english: But the Lord said to him, “Go your way, for he is my chosen vessel to bear my name before the nations and kings, and the children of Israel.

kikuyu: Nĩngatũma matũũra maku manangwo nawe nĩũgaakira ihooru. Hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩũkamenya atĩ niĩ nĩ niĩ Jehova.
english: I will lay your cities waste, and you will be desolate. Then you will know that I am Yahweh.

kikuyu: Hanyũkaga o na njĩra ya maathani maku, nĩgũkorwo nĩwohorithĩtie ngoro yakwa. Kũhooya Kũmenya Watho
english: I run in the path of your commandments, for you have set my heart free. HE

kikuyu: na Jezireeli, na Jokideamu, na Zanoa,
english: Jezreel, Jokdeam, Zanoah,

kikuyu: Babuloni nĩgũkũgũa o rĩmwe na kũhehenjwo. Kũrĩrĩrei! Rehei ndawa ya kũniina ruo rwakuo; hihi kwahota kũhona.
english: but the army of the Chaldeans pursued the king, and overtook Zedekiah in the plains of Jericho; and all his army was scattered from him.

kikuyu: Akĩanĩrĩra na mũgambo mũnene, akiuga atĩrĩ: Temai mũtĩ ũcio na mũhũrũre honge ciaguo; itithiai mathangũ maguo, na mũhurunje maciaro maguo. Nyamũ nacio ciũre ciehere rungu waguo, o na nyoni ciume honge-inĩ ciaguo. No rĩrĩ, mũreke gĩthukĩ na mĩri yakĩo ciohwo na kĩgera na gĩcango, nacio itigwo tĩĩri-inĩ, kũu nyeki-inĩ ya gĩthaka. “Nĩaihũgagio nĩ ime rĩa igũrũ, na atũũranagie na nyamũ kũu nyeki-inĩ ĩrĩa nduru ya gĩthaka.
english: He cried aloud and said this: ‘Cut down the tree, and cut off its branches! Shake off its leaves and scatter its fruit! Let the animals get away from under it and the birds from its branches.

kikuyu: Wee nĩwe ũtũmaga kũgĩe nduma, gũgatuĩka ũtukũ, nacio nyamũ ciothe cia gĩthaka ikoimĩra icemeete.
english: You make darkness, and it is night, in which all the animals of the forest prowl.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩingĩacooka kwaragia itekũmwĩtigĩra, no ũrĩa ndariĩ rĩu-rĩ, ndingĩhota. * 9:26 Tũtarũ tũtaarĩ tũnene twa ithanjĩ twathondekagĩrwo Misiri.
english: then I would speak, and not fear him, for I am not so in myself.

kikuyu: rĩrĩa ĩkomereire imamo-inĩ ciayo, kana rĩrĩa ĩikaraga yoheirie nyamũ ihinga-inĩ?
english: when they crouch in their dens, and lie in wait in the thicket?

kikuyu: Andũ a gũkũ thĩ matingĩmũthũũra, no niĩ nĩmathũire tondũ nĩnyumbũraga atĩ ũrĩa mekaga nĩ ũũru.
english: and said to him, “Go, wash in the pool of Siloam” (which means “Sent”). So he went away, washed, and came back seeing.

kikuyu: Inyuĩ nĩmũkaherithio nĩ ũndũ wa ũũra-thoni wanyu, na mũcookererwo nĩ maciaro ma mehia manyu ma kũhooya mĩhianano. Na inyuĩ hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩmũkamenya atĩ niĩ nĩ niĩ Mwathani Jehova.”
english: They will recompense your lewdness on you, and you will bear the sins of your idols. Then you will know that I am the Lord Yahweh.’ ”

kikuyu: Watho o ro ũmwe nĩguo ũrĩhũthagĩrwo harĩ mũndũ o wothe ũkehia atekwenda, arĩ Mũisiraeli ũciarĩirwo kwanyu kana arĩ mũgeni.
english: Your dead bodies shall fall in this wilderness; and all who were counted of you, according to your whole number, from twenty years old and upward, who have complained against me,

kikuyu: Kĩhingo gĩa Gĩthima gĩacookereirio nĩ Shalumu mũrũ wa Koli-Hoze, mwathi wa gĩcigo kĩa Mizipa. Agĩgĩaka rĩngĩ, agĩkĩgita, na agĩĩkĩra mĩrango na igera cia kũmĩhinga na icuuma ciakĩo. Ningĩ nĩacookereirierũthingorwaKariagaSiloamukũriganianaMũgũndawaMũthamaki, onginyangathĩ- inĩ ya gũikũrũka kuuma Itũũra Inene rĩa Daudi.
english: Shallun the son of Colhozeh, the ruler of the district of Mizpah, repaired the spring gate. He built it, covered it, and set up its doors, its bolts, and its bars; and he repaired the wall of the pool of Shelah by the king’s garden, even to the stairs that go down from David’s city.

kikuyu: “ ‘Mũndũ wa bũrũri wanyu angĩthĩĩna arĩ gatagatĩ kanyu, nake eyendie harĩ inyuĩ, mũtikanamũrutithie wĩra ta ngombo.
english: “ ‘If your brother has grown poor among you, and sells himself to you, you shall not make him to serve as a slave.

kikuyu: Ũrĩa ũcookanagĩrĩria irio hĩndĩ ya riũa nĩ mwana mũũgĩ, no ũrĩa ũkomaga hĩndĩ ya magetha nĩ mwana wa kũrehe thoni.
english: The righteousness of the blameless will direct his way, but the wicked shall fall by his own wickedness.

kikuyu: Niĩ ngaatuĩka ta mũrũũthi kũrĩ Efiraimu, nduĩke ta mũrũũthi ũrĩ hinya kũrĩ Juda. Ngaamatambuuranga, njooke ndĩthiĩre; ngaamataha, na gũtirĩ mũndũ ũkaamahonokia. Ningĩ niĩ nĩngacooka gwakwa, nginya rĩrĩa magetĩkĩra mahĩtia mao. Nao nĩmakarongooria ũthiũ wakwa; nao mona mathĩĩna nĩmakanongoria na kĩo.”
english: For I will be to Ephraim like a lion, and like a young lion to the house of Judah. I myself will tear in pieces and go away. I will carry off, and there will be no one to deliver.

kikuyu: Ta rorai moko makwa na nyarĩrĩ ciakwa. Nĩ niĩ mwene! Huutiai muone; roho ndũrĩ nyama na mahĩndĩ, ta ũrĩa mũrona ndĩ nacio.”
english: See my hands and my feet, that it is truly me. Touch me and see, for a spirit doesn’t have flesh and bones, as you see that I have.”

kikuyu: Ti-itherũ marũa macio ingĩmaigĩrĩra kĩande, ndĩmehumbe taarĩ thũmbĩ.
english: Surely I would carry it on my shoulder, and I would bind it to me as a crown.

kikuyu: Ũnjaramagĩria njĩra ya kũrĩa thiiagĩra, nĩgeetha magũrũ makwa matigatirũke.
english: You have enlarged my steps under me, My feet have not slipped.

kikuyu: Isaaka na Abimeleku Na rĩrĩ, nĩ kwagĩire ngʼaragu ĩngĩ bũrũri-inĩ ũcio, tiga ĩrĩa ya mbere ya hĩndĩ ya Iburahĩmu, nake Isaaka agĩthiĩ Gerari kũrĩ Abimeleku mũthamaki wa Afilisti.*
english: 1 There was a famine in the land, in addition to the first famine that was in the days of Abraham. Isaac went to Abimelech king of the Philistines, to Gerar.

kikuyu: Nĩmwendirie andũ a Juda na a Jerusalemu kũrĩ Ayunani nĩguo mũmatware kũndũ kũraya na bũrũri wao.
english: and have sold the children of Judah and the children of Jerusalem to the sons of the Greeks, that you may remove them far from their border.

kikuyu: Nebukadinezaru agĩkuhĩrĩria mũromo-inĩ wa icua rĩu rĩakanaga mwaki, akĩanĩrĩra, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Inyuĩ Shadiraka, na Meshaki, na Abedinego, inyuĩ ndungata cia Ngai-Ũrĩa-ũrĩ Igũrũ-Mũno, umĩrai, mũũke haha!” Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Shadiraka, na Meshaki, na Abedinego makiumĩra mwaki-inĩ ũcio,
english: For she is a reflection of everlasting light, an unspotted mirror of the working of God, and an image of his goodness.

kikuyu: tondũ matietĩkĩrire mataaro makwa, na nĩmamenire ikaanania rĩakwa,
english: They wanted none of my counsel. They despised all my reproof.

kikuyu: “Nayo ndungata yaku ũcio ithe witũ agĩtwĩra atĩrĩ, ‘Inyuĩ nĩmũũĩ atĩ mũtumia wakwa Rakeli aan- jiarĩire ariũ eerĩ.
english: Your servant, my father, said to us, ‘You know that my wife bore me two sons.

kikuyu: njata nacio nĩikaagũa kuuma igũrũ, namo maahinya ma igũrũ nĩmakenyenyio.’
english: the stars will be falling from the sky, and the powers that are in the heavens will be shaken.*

kikuyu: Nĩũrũithĩtie ndũrĩrĩ na ũkaniina andũ arĩa aaganu; namo marĩĩtwa mao ũkamatharia tene na tene.
english: You have rebuked the nations. You have destroyed the wicked. You have blotted out their name forever and ever.

kikuyu: Kũriũka kwa Jesũ Na rĩrĩ, mũthenya wa kwambĩrĩria kiumia, rũciinĩ tene, atumia magĩthiĩ mbĩrĩra-inĩ makuuĩte indo nungi wega iria maathondekete. Nao magĩkora ihiga rĩgaragarĩtio rĩkeherio mũromo-inĩ wa mbĩrĩra,
english: 1 But on the first day of the week, at early dawn, they and some others came to the tomb, bringing the spices which they had prepared.

kikuyu: maũndũ marĩa marĩ bata gũkĩra marĩa mangĩ tondũ wa ũrĩa ũrutĩtwo maũndũ ma watho; 19angĩkorwo nĩwĩtĩkĩtie atĩ wee ũrĩ mũtongoria wa andũ arĩa atumumu, o na ũtheri wa gũtherera arĩa marĩ nduma- inĩ,
english: Now we know that whatever things the law says, it speaks to those who are under the law, that every mouth may be closed, and all the world may be brought under the judgment of God.

kikuyu: Wee nĩũkanĩte kĩrĩkanĩro kĩrĩa ũrĩkanĩire na ndungata yaku, na ũkarangĩrĩria thũmbĩ yake rũkũngũ-inĩ.
english: You have renounced the covenant of your servant. You have defiled his crown in the dust.

kikuyu: Oigire ũguo nĩgeetha onanie gĩkuũ kĩrĩa agaakua.
english: But he said this, signifying by what kind of death he should die.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo o ta ũrĩa mbura yuraga, narĩo ira rĩkaharũrũka kuuma igũrũ, na iticookaga kuo itaihũgĩtie thĩ na igatũma mĩmera ĩthundũke na ĩciare, nĩguo mũhandi aheo mbeũ na ũrĩa wendaga kũrĩa aheo irio ciake,
english: For as the rain comes down and the snow from the sky, and doesn’t return there, but waters the earth, and makes it grow and bud, and gives seed to the sower and bread to the eater;

kikuyu: Heshiboni nĩrĩo rĩarĩ itũũra inene rĩa Sihoni mũthamaki wa Aamori, ũrĩa warũĩte na mũthamaki wa Moabi ũrĩa waathanaga mbere ĩyo, na akamũtunya bũrũri wake wothe o nginya Arinoni.
english: For Heshbon was the city of Sihon the king of the Amorites, who had fought against the former king of Moab, and taken all his land out of his hand, even to the Arnon.

kikuyu: Jehova arogoocwo nginya tene! Ameni, na ningĩ Ameni. Ibuku rĩa Kana
english: Blessed be Yahweh forever more. Amen, and Amen. BOOK

kikuyu: “O na gũtuĩka ũthiũ wake nĩ mũnoru, na njohero yake ĩkaneneha nĩ kũnora-rĩ,
english: because he has covered his face with his fatness, and gathered fat on his thighs.

kikuyu: Mũtikanathaahie bũrũri ũrĩa mũtũũraga na kũrĩa niĩ ndũũraga, nĩ ũndũ niĩ Jehova ndũũraga gatagatĩ ka andũ a Isiraeli.’ ”
english: You shall not defile the land which you inhabit, where I dwell; for I, Yahweh, dwell among the children of Israel.’ ”

kikuyu: Inyuĩ mũrĩĩaga nyama cia mĩĩrĩ ya andũ akwa, mũkamonũra ikonde na mũkoinanga mahĩndĩ mao tũcunjĩ; O inyuĩ mũmatinangagia ta nyama cia gũkarangwo na rũgĩo, o na ta nyama cia gũtherũkio na nyũngũ.”
english: who also eat the flesh of my people, and peel their skin from off them, and break their bones, and chop them in pieces, as for the pot, and as meat within the cauldron.

kikuyu: Mũrũ wakwa, ehia mangĩgakũheenereria-rĩ, ndũkanetĩkanie nao.
english: so your barns will be filled with plenty, and your vats will overflow with new wine.

kikuyu: “Na rĩrĩ, ta thikĩrĩria, wee wendete ikeno, na ũikaraga ũtarĩ ũgwati ũngĩĩtigĩra, na ũkeĩraga atĩrĩ, ‘Niĩ no niĩ, na gũtirĩ ũngĩ tiga niĩ. Ndirĩ hĩndĩ ngaatuĩka mũtumia wa ndigwa, kana menye ũhoro wa gũkuĩrwo nĩ ciana.’
english: “Now therefore hear this, you who are given to pleasures, who sit securely, who say in your heart, ‘I am, and there is no one else besides me. I won’t sit as a widow, neither will I know the loss of children.’

kikuyu: Mũthamaki mũndũ-wa-nja wa mwena wa Gũthini nĩakarũgama hĩndĩ ya ciira hamwe na rũciaro rũrũ arũtuĩre ciira; nĩgũkorwo nĩoimire ituri cia thĩ oke athikĩrĩrie ũũgĩ wa Solomoni, na rĩu ũmwe mũnene gũkĩra Solomoni arĩ haha.
english: The Queen of the South will rise up in the judgment with this generation and will condemn it, for she came from the ends of the earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon; and behold, someone greater than Solomon is here.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo maathani maya nĩ tawa, na ũrutani ũyũ nĩ ũtheri, namo mataaro ma kũrũnga mũndũ-rĩ, nĩmo njĩra ya muoyo,
english: Until an arrow strikes through his liver, as a bird hurries to the snare, and doesn’t know that it will cost his life.

kikuyu: Maaĩ mahehu makĩo marĩa moimaga matherũkĩro-inĩ ma kũraya-rĩ, marĩ maga gũtherera? No rĩrĩ, andũ akwa nĩmariganĩirwo nĩ niĩ; macinagĩra mĩhianano ĩtarĩ kĩene ũbumba, ĩrĩa yatũmire mahĩngwo mĩthiĩre-inĩ yao, makĩaga kũrũmĩrĩra mĩthiĩre ĩrĩa ya tene. Yatũmire magerere tũcĩra twa mĩkĩra, o na barabara iria itarĩ thondeke.
english: For my people have forgotten me. They have burned incense to false gods. They have been made to stumble in their ways in the ancient paths, to walk in byways, in a way not built up,

kikuyu: Nĩthũire muoyo wakwa; ndikwenda gũtũũra nginya tene. Tigana na niĩ tondũ matukũ makwa no ma tũhũ.
english: He is green before the sun. His shoots go out along his garden.  Sheol is the place of the dead.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo matukũ makwa marathira o ta ũrĩa ndogo ĩthiraga; namo mahĩndĩ makwa maraakana o ta gĩcinga.
english: For my days consume away like smoke. My bones are burned as a torch.

kikuyu: Wee ũrũmĩrĩire mĩthiĩre ya mwarĩ wa nyũkwa; nĩ ũndũ ũcio nĩngakũnengera gĩkombe gĩake ũkĩnyiite na guoko gwaku.
english: You have walked in the way of your sister; therefore I will give her cup into your hand.’

kikuyu: wee mũthamaki, nĩwe mũtĩ ũcio! Nĩũnenehete na ũkagĩa na hinya; ũnene waku ũgakũra nginya ũkahutia matu mairũ, naguo wathani waku ũgatambũrũka ũgakinya kũndũ kũraya gũkũ thĩ.
english: it is you, O king, that have grown and become strong; for your greatness has grown, and reaches to the sky, and your dominion to the end of the earth.

kikuyu: O rĩmwe kairĩtu kau gagĩũkĩra, gakĩambĩrĩria gũthiĩ (nako kaarĩ na ũkũrũ wa mĩaka ikũmi na ĩĩrĩ). Nao mona ũguo makĩgega mũno. Nake akĩmakaania mũno matikareke mũndũ o na ũrĩkũ amenye ũhoro ũcio, na akĩmeera makahe kĩndũ gĩa kũrĩa.
english: Immediately the girl rose up and walked, for she was twelve years old. They were amazed with great amazement.

kikuyu: We ũcio Rũgai rwa Jakubu ndatariĩ tayo, nĩgũkorwo nĩwe Mũũmbi wa indo ciothe, o hamwe na Isiraeli mũhĩrĩga ũrĩa, eegwatĩire ũtuĩke igai rĩake, Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe, nĩrĩo rĩĩtwa rĩake. Mwanangĩko ũrĩa Ũroka
english: The portion of Jacob is not like these; for he is the maker of all things; and Israel is the tribe of his inheritance. Yahweh of Armies is his name.

kikuyu: Igũrũ wake kwarũgamĩte aserafi, na o mũserafi ũmwe aarĩ na mathagu matandatũ: nao makehumbĩra mothiũ § 5:26 Ashuri na Babuloni nĩcio ndũrĩrĩ icio igwetetwo.
english: Above him stood the seraphim. Each one had six wings. With two he covered his face. With two he covered his feet. With two he flew.

kikuyu: O hau hau akĩrũgama mbere yao, akĩoya kĩbarĩ kĩrĩa akomeire, na akĩinũka mũciĩ akĩgoocaga Ngai. Nao andũ othe nĩmagegire na magĩkumia Ngai. Makĩiyũrwo nĩ guoya, makiuga atĩrĩ, “Ũmũthĩ nĩtuonete maũndũ tũtarĩ tuona.” Jesũ gwĩta Lawi
english: Immediately he rose up before them, and took up that which he was laying on, and departed to his house, glorifying God.

kikuyu: “Rĩrĩa ũkeera andũ aya maũndũ macio mothe nao makũũrie atĩrĩ, ‘Nĩ kĩĩ gĩtũmĩte Jehova atũtuĩre mwanangĩko mũnene ũguo? Kaĩ twĩkĩte ihĩtia rĩrĩkũ? Nĩ mehia marĩkũ twĩhĩirie Jehova Ngai witũ?’
english: It will happen, when you tell this people all these words, and they ask you, ‘Why has Yahweh pronounced all this great evil against us?’ or ‘What is our iniquity?’ or ‘What is our sin that we have committed against Yahweh our God?’

kikuyu: Akĩĩra mũndũ ũngĩ atĩrĩ, “Nũmĩrĩra.” No mũndũ ũcio akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Mwathani, reke nyambe thiĩ ngathike baba.”
english: He said to another, “Follow me!” But he said, “Lord, allow me first to go and bury my father.”

kikuyu: Anene akuo nĩ macuurĩtio na moko mao; athuuri nao matiheagwo gĩtĩĩo.
english: Princes were hanged up by their hands. The faces of elders were not honored.

kikuyu: Njũũi ĩgaatũũranagia na kagondu, nayo ngarĩ ĩgaakomanagia na mbũri, nayo njaũ, na mũrũũthi, na nyamũ ya gĩcegũ igaaikaraga hamwe; nacio igaatongoragio nĩ kaana kanini.
english: The wolf will live with the lamb, and the leopard will lie down with the young goat, the calf, the young lion, and the fattened calf together; and a little child will lead them.

kikuyu: Ũrathi wa Gatatũ wa Balamu Ningĩ Balaki akĩĩra Balamu atĩrĩ, “Ũka tũthiĩ ngũtware handũ hangĩ. Hihi Ngai no agwĩtĩkĩrie ũnumĩre andũ aya ũrĩ hau.”
english: Balak said to Balaam, “Come now, I will take you to another place; perhaps it will please God that you may curse them for me from there.”

kikuyu: Maigua ũguo, mũnene wa arangĩri a hekarũ agĩthiĩ na anene a thigari ciake na makĩrehe atũmwo. Matiigana kũhũthĩra hinya, tondũ nĩmetigagĩra andũ matikamahũũre na mahiga nyuguto.
english: Then the captain went with the officers, and brought them without violence, for they were afraid that the people might stone them.

kikuyu: Jehova nĩ mũigua tha na nĩ mũtugi, ndahiũhaga kũrakara, na nĩaiyũirwo nĩ wendo mũingĩ.
english: Yahweh is merciful and gracious, slow to anger, and abundant in loving kindness.

kikuyu: Jehova akĩĩra Musa atĩrĩ,
english: Yahweh spoke to Moses, saying,  A shekel is about

kikuyu: Kũrĩa guothe ngaamathaamĩria, matigari mothe ma rũrĩrĩ rũrũ rwaganu, makeeriragĩria gũkua handũ ha gũtũũra muoyo, ũguo nĩguo Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe ekuuga.’ Mehia na Kũherithio
english: Death will be chosen rather than life by all the residue that remain of this evil family, that remain in all the places where I have driven them,” says Yahweh of Armies.

kikuyu: Ũhoro wa ma nĩ atĩ, gũtirĩ o na ũmwe wa acio mũgwanja watigire ciana. Thuutha wao othe, mũtumia ũcio o nake agĩkua.
english: and the seven took her and left no children. Last of all the woman also died.

kikuyu: Kĩroto gĩa Keerĩ kĩa Nebukadinezaru Nĩ niĩ Mũthamaki Nebukadinezaru, Ndĩramwandĩkĩra marũa maya inyuĩ andũ a iruka ciothe, na ndũrĩrĩ, na andũ a rwario o ruothe arĩa matũũraga thĩ yothe: Mũrogaacĩra makĩria!
english: They thirsted, and they called upon you, and water was given to them out of the† flinty rock, and healing of their thirst out of the hard stone.

kikuyu: Ndũrĩrĩ nĩigooka kũrĩ ũtheri waku, nao athamaki moke kũrĩa ũcangararĩte ta riũa rĩkĩratha.
english: Nations will come to your light, and kings to the brightness of your rising.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa indo ciaku cia wonjoria ciatwaragwo iria-inĩ-rĩ, nĩwaiganithirie mabataro ma ndũrĩrĩ nyingĩ; nĩ ũndũ wa ũtonga waku mũnene, na ũingĩ wa indo ciaku cia wonjoria, ũgĩtongia athamaki a thĩ.
english: When your wares came from the seas, you filled many peoples. You enriched the kings of the earth with the multitude of your riches and of your merchandise.

kikuyu: Ndĩrogĩtũũra muoyo nĩguo ngũgoocage, namo mawatho maku nĩmatũũre mandeithagia.
english: Let my soul live, that I may praise you. Let your ordinances help me.

kikuyu: “Ũndũ ũyũ ũkwenda gwĩciirio na ũũgĩ. Ciongo icio mũgwanja nĩ irĩma mũgwanja iria ciikarĩirwo nĩ mũndũ-wa-nja ũcio. Ningĩ nĩ athamaki mũgwanja. Atano nĩmeherete, ũmwe arĩ ho, na ũrĩa ũngĩ ndarĩ arooka; no ooka-rĩ, no nginya aikare o kahinda kanini.
english: The kings of the earth who committed sexual immorality and lived wantonly with her will weep and wail over her, when they look at the smoke of her burning,

kikuyu: Agaatuĩka ta mũthabibũ ũhũrũrĩtwo thabibũ ciaguo itarĩ njĩru, ahaane ta mũtamaiyũ ũraita kĩro kĩaguo.
english: He will shake off his unripe grape as the vine, and will cast off his flower as the olive tree.

kikuyu: Ngingo-inĩ yayo nĩho hinya wayo ũkoragwo, ũrĩa ũmakagia kĩrĩa gĩothe gĩtũnganaga nayo.
english: There is strength in his neck. Terror dances before him.

kikuyu: magũrũ maguo maarĩ ma kĩgera, namo makinya maarĩ ma kĩgera gĩtukanĩte na rĩũmba rĩcine.
english: its legs of iron, its feet part of iron and part of clay.

kikuyu: We nĩaũhaandire ũtuĩke kĩrĩra harĩ Jusufu hĩndĩ ĩrĩa aathiire gũtharĩkĩra bũrũri wa Misiri, kũrĩa twaiguire mwario tũtaamenyire.
english: He appointed it in Joseph for a covenant, when he went out over the land of Egypt, I heard a language that I didn’t know.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo Saũlũ, ũrĩa wetagwo na rĩĩtwa rĩngĩ Paũlũ aiyũrĩtwo nĩ Roho Mũtheru, agĩkũũrĩra Elumo maitho, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ,
english: He was listening to Paul speaking, who, fastening eyes on him and seeing that he had faith to be made whole,

kikuyu: Wee-rĩ, nĩwe wahehenjire mĩtwe ya Leviathani, ũkĩmĩheana ĩtuĩke irio cia ciũmbe cia werũ-inĩ.
english: You broke the heads of Leviathan in pieces. You gave him as food to people and desert creatures.

kikuyu: na rĩrĩa maamuonire, makĩanĩrĩra, makĩmwĩra atĩrĩ: “Andũ othe nĩwe maracaria!”
english: They found him and told him, “Everyone is looking for you.”

kikuyu: Mũtikehere itoonyero-inĩ rĩa Hema-ya-Gũtũnganwo ihinda rĩa mĩthenya mũgwanja, nginya matukũ manyu ma kwamũrwo mahinge, nĩ ũndũ kwamũrwo kwanyu gũgaikara
english: You shall not go out from the door of the Tent of Meeting for seven days, until the days of your consecration are fulfilled: for he shall consecrate you seven days.

kikuyu: “Njĩra ya gũthiĩ kũrĩa ũtheri ũtũũraga nĩĩrĩkũ? Nayo nduma ĩikaraga kũ? No ũcitware gwacio? Nĩũũĩ tũcĩra twa gũthiĩ kũrĩa itũũraga?
english: “Have you given the horse might? Have you clothed his neck with a quivering mane?

kikuyu: Andũ arĩa anini na arĩa anene othe marĩ kuo, nayo ngombo nĩĩrekereirio kuuma kũrĩ mũmĩathi.
english: How much more those who dwell in houses of clay, whose foundation is in the dust, who are crushed before the moth!

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo ũrĩa wothe wendaga kũhonokia muoyo wake nĩakoorwo nĩguo, no ũrĩa wothe ũkoorwo nĩ muoyo wake nĩ ũndũ wakwa nĩakaũhonokia.
english: For whoever desires to save his life will lose it, but whoever will lose his life for my sake will save it.

kikuyu: Gũtirĩ ũrogi ũngĩũraga Jakubu, gũtirĩ ũragũri ũngĩthũkia Isiraeli. Rĩu ha ũhoro wa Jakubu na wa Isiraeli kũrĩĩranagwo atĩrĩ, ‘Ta kĩonei ũrĩa Ngai ekĩte!’
english: Surely there is no enchantment with Jacob; neither is there any divination with Israel. Now it shall be said of Jacob and of Israel, ‘What has God done!’

kikuyu: Jesũ akĩmũrora, akiugaatĩrĩ, “Kaĩnĩhinyaandũarĩaatongugũtoonyaũthamaki-inĩwaNgai-ĩ!
english: Jesus, seeing that he became very sad, said, “How hard it is for those who have riches to enter into God’s Kingdom!

kikuyu: Ngĩcooka ngĩona nyamũ ĩngĩ, ĩkiuma na thĩ. Nyamũ ĩyo yarĩ na hĩa igĩrĩ ta cia gatũrũme, no yaaragia ta ndamathia.
english: I saw another beast coming up out of the earth. He had two horns like a lamb and it spoke like a dragon.

kikuyu: Inyuĩ ngombo-rĩ, athĩkagĩrai aathani anyu a gũkũ thĩ, mũkĩmatĩĩaga na mũkĩmetigĩraga, mũtarĩ na ũhinga ngoro-inĩ, o taarĩ Kristũ mũrathĩkĩra.
english: Servants, be obedient to those who according to the flesh are your masters, with fear and trembling, in singleness of your heart, as to Christ,

kikuyu: Mũthenya wa Kũhoroherio Mehia Nake Jehova akĩarĩria Musa thuutha wa gĩkuũ kĩa ariũ arĩa eerĩ a Harũni arĩa maakuire rĩrĩa mookire magĩkuhĩrĩria mbere ya Jehova.
english: 1 Yahweh spoke to Moses after the death of the two sons of Aaron, when they came near before Yahweh, and died;

kikuyu: Ũndũ ũyũ ũmenyithanagia ũtiganu wa ciana cia Ngai na cia mũcukani: Mũndũ o wothe ũtekaga ũrĩa kwagĩrĩire ti mwana wa Ngai; o hamwe na ũrĩa ũtendete mũrũ kana mwarĩ wa ithe.
english: In this the children of God are revealed, and the children of the devil. Whoever doesn’t do righteousness is not of God, neither is he who doesn’t love his brother.

kikuyu: Warĩkia gũgĩtheria, nĩũkaruta ndegwa ĩtarĩ ngũrũ, na ndũrũme kuuma rũũru-inĩ, cierĩ ikorwo itarĩ na kaũũgũ.
english: The temple and the sanctuary had two doors.

kikuyu: Musa, na Harũni marĩ na kĩrĩndĩ kĩu gĩothe kĩa andũ a Isiraeli magĩĩka Alawii o ta ũguo Jehova aathĩte Musa.
english: Moses, and Aaron, and all the congregation of the children of Israel did so to the Levites. According to all that Yahweh commanded Moses concerning the Levites, so the children of Israel did to them.

kikuyu: Matũũra macio hamwe na tũtũũra twamo nĩmo maarĩ igai rĩa mũhĩrĩga wa Asheri, kũringana na ũrĩa mbarĩ ciao ciatariĩ. Igai rĩa Nafitali
english: This is the inheritance of the tribe of the children of Asher according to their families, these cities with their villages.

kikuyu: Matukũ-inĩ ma thĩĩna na kũũrũũra gwake-rĩ, Jerusalemu aririkanaga igĩĩna ciothe iria ciarĩ ciake matukũ ma tene. Rĩrĩa andũ ake maagũire moko-inĩ ma thũ-rĩ, gũtiarĩ mũndũ o na ũmwe wa kũmũteithia. Thũ ciake ikĩmũrora, igĩthekerera mwanangĩko wake.
english: Jerusalem remembers in the days of her affliction and of her miseries all her pleasant things that were from the days of old; when her people fell into the hand of the adversary, and no one helped her. The adversaries saw her. They mocked at her desolations.

kikuyu: Andũ a Sidoni‡ na Arivadi nĩo maatwarithagia marikabu ciaku; nao andũ aku arĩa oogĩ, wee Turo, nĩo maarutaga wĩra marikabu-inĩ.
english: He will kill your daughters in the field with the sword. He will make forts against you, cast up a mound against you, and raise up the buckler against you.

kikuyu: Arũme othe arĩa mooimire bũrũri wa Misiri nĩmaruĩte, no andũ othe arĩa maaciarĩirwo werũ-inĩ marĩ rũgendo-inĩ rwa kuuma bũrũri wa Misiri matiaruĩte.
english: About the time of the shutting of the gate, when it was dark, the men went out. Where the men went, I don’t know. Pursue them quickly. You may catch up with them.”

kikuyu: Makomaga harĩa hothe harĩ na kĩgongona, magakomera nguo iria irutĩtwo irũgamĩrĩre thiirĩ.* Kũu thĩinĩ wa nyũmba ya ngai yao, nĩkuo manyuuagĩra ndibei ĩrĩa ĩrutĩtwo ya ndooco.
english: The lion has roared. Who will not fear?  The Hebrew word rendered “God” is “אֱֹלהִ ֑ים” (Elohim).  “Behold”, from “הִ ͏נֵּה”, means look at, take notice, observe, see, or gaze at. It is often used as an interjection.

kikuyu: Mũthenya wa keerĩ Nethaneli mũrũ wa Zuaru, mũtongoria wa Aisakaru, nake akĩrehe maruta make.
english: The Nazirite shall shave the head of his separation at the door of the Tent of Meeting, take the hair of the head of his separation, and put it on the fire which is under the sacrifice of peace offerings.

kikuyu: Nĩateithĩtie Isiraeli ndungata yake, akaririkana ũhoro wake wa kũiguanĩra tha,
english: He has given help to Israel, his servant, that he might remember mercy,

kikuyu: Nũũ ũcio inyuĩ mũranyũrũria? Nũũ ũcio mũratembanĩria mĩromo na mũkamũrutĩra rũrĩmĩ? Githĩ inyuĩ ti inyuĩ ciana cia aremi, o na rũciaro rwa aheenania?
english: Whom do you mock? Against whom do you make a wide mouth and stick out your tongue? Aren’t you children of disobedience and offspring of falsehood,

kikuyu: atĩ ciũmbe cio nyene nĩigakũũrwo kuuma ũkombo-inĩ wa kũbutha, ikinye wĩyathi-inĩ wa riiri wa ciana cia Ngai.
english: Or hasn’t the potter a right over the clay, from the same lump to make one part a vessel for honor, and another for dishonor?

kikuyu: Andũ aingĩ macaragia ũrĩa mangĩendwo nĩ mũnene, na mũndũ mũheani iheo-rĩ, nĩ mũrata wa andũ othe.
english: Many will entreat the favor of a ruler, and everyone is a friend to a man who gives gifts.

kikuyu: Maaĩ ma Dimoni maiyũrĩtwo nĩ thakame, no nĩngũrehe maũndũ mangĩ mooru makĩria kũu Dimoni, mahaana ta mũrũũthi ũgũtharĩkĩra andũ arĩa morĩte kuuma Moabi, o na ũtharĩkĩre arĩa matigaire kũu bũrũri-inĩ. * 15:1 Ari na Kiri maarĩ matũũra ma Moabi ma bata mũno.
english: For the waters of Dimon are full of blood; for I will bring yet more on Dimon, a lion on those of Moab who escape, and on the remnant of the land.

kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũceeraga na arĩa oogĩ akĩragĩrĩria kũũhĩga, no ũrĩa ũtwaranaga na mũndũ mũkĩĩgu onaga o ũũru.
english: The poor person is shunned even by his own neighbor, but the rich person has many friends.

kikuyu: Jakubu na andũ arĩa othe aarĩ nao magĩkinya Luzu (na nĩkuo Betheli), bũrũri-inĩ wa Kaanani.
english: So Jacob came to Luz (that is, Bethel), which is in the land of Canaan, he and all the people who were with him.

kikuyu: Tũtuĩkĩte andũ a kũmenwo nĩ arĩa tũriganĩtie nao, tũgatuĩka andũ a kũnyararwo na gũthekererwo nĩ arĩa matũthiũrũrũkĩirie.
english: Yahweh God of Armies, how long will you be angry against the prayer of your people?

kikuyu: Akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, mũrutani wa watho o wothe ũrĩa ũrutĩtwo ũhoro wa ũthamaki wa igũrũ-rĩ, ahaanaine na mwene nyũmba ũrĩa ũrutaga mũthiithũ-inĩ wake indo iria njerũ o na iria ngũrũ.” Mũnabii ũtarĩ Mũtĩĩe Nake Jesũ aarĩkia kũheana ngerekano icio-rĩ, akiuma kũu.
english: He said to them, “Therefore every scribe who has been made a disciple in the Kingdom of Heaven is like a man who is a householder, who brings out of his treasure new and old things.”

kikuyu: Thĩ ĩthingithaga mbere yacio, na igũrũ rĩkainaina, riũa na mweri ikagĩa nduma, na njata igatiga kwara.
english: The earth quakes before them. The heavens tremble. The sun and the moon are darkened, and the stars withdraw their shining.

kikuyu: Rũmia ũrutani wakwa, na ndũkanareke ũkweherere; ũgitagĩre wega, nĩgũkorwo nĩguo muoyo waku.
english: I think it right, as long as I am in this tent, to stir you up by reminding you,

kikuyu: o na rĩrĩa methondekeire mũhianano wa njaũ, makiuga atĩrĩ, ‘Ĩno nĩyo ngai yanyu ĩrĩa yamũrutire bũrũri wa Misiri,’ kana o na rĩrĩa meekire maũndũ mooru mũno ma gũkũruma.
english: Yes, when they had made themselves a molded calf, and said, ‘This is your God who brought you up out of Egypt,’ and had committed awful blasphemies,

kikuyu: “Ithuĩ-rĩ, nĩtwĩhĩtie na tũgakũremera, wee nawe ũkaaga gũtũrekera.
english: “We have transgressed and have rebelled. You have not pardoned.

kikuyu: ĩĩ hĩndĩ ĩrĩa guoya ũkaamũkorerera ta kĩhuhũkanio, rĩrĩa mwanangĩko ũkaamũhaata ta rũhuho rwa kĩhuhũkanio, rĩrĩa mũgaakinyĩrĩrwo nĩ mĩnyamaro na ruo rwa ngoro.
english: when calamity overtakes you like a storm, when your disaster comes on like a whirlwind, when distress and anguish come on you.

kikuyu: na a mũhĩrĩga wa Asheri maarĩ 12,000, na a mũhĩrĩga wa Nafitali maarĩ 12,000, na a mũhĩrĩga wa Manase† maarĩ 12,000,
english: The seven angels who had the seven trumpets prepared themselves to sound.  or, oppression

kikuyu: Herode nĩongereire ũndũ ũngĩ mũũru makĩria; naguo nĩ wa kũnyiitithia Johana na kũmuohithia njeera. Kũbatithio na Rũciaro rwa Jesũ
english: added this also to them all, that he shut up John in prison.

kikuyu: Gwĩka ũrĩa kwagĩrĩire na ũrĩa ũrĩ kĩhooto nĩ gwĩtĩkagĩrwo nĩ Jehova gũkĩra kũruta igongona.
english: To do righteousness and justice is more acceptable to Yahweh than sacrifice.

kikuyu: Erekeria makinya maku kũrĩ mwanangĩko ũyũ ũtũũrĩte kuo, wone ũrĩa handũ harĩa haku haamũre hathũkangĩtio nĩ thũ.
english: Lift up your feet to the perpetual ruins, all the evil that the enemy has done in the sanctuary.

kikuyu: Wega-rĩ, no ũrĩhwo na ũũru? No rĩrĩ, andũ acio nĩmanyenjeire irima. Ta ririkana ũrĩa ndaarũgamire mbere yaku, na ngĩmarĩrĩria, nĩguo ngirĩrĩrie marakara maku matikamakore. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio rekereria ciana ciao ngʼaragu; cineane, iniinwo na rũhiũ rwa njora. Reke atumia ao matigwo matarĩ ciana, na marĩ a ndigwa; rekereria arũme ao mooragwo, o nao aanake ao mooragĩrwo mbaara-inĩ na rũhiũ rwa njora.
english: Should evil be recompensed for good? For they have dug a pit for my soul. Remember how I stood before you to speak good for them, to turn away your wrath from them.

kikuyu: Hĩ! Kaĩ mũrĩ rũrĩrĩ rwĩhagia-ĩ. Mũrĩ andũ matitikĩte mahĩtia, na rũruka rwa andũ mekaga ũũru, na ciana ciĩrutĩire gũthũkia maũndũ! Nĩmatiganĩirie Jehova; nĩmamenete Ũrĩa Mũtheru wa Isiraeli, makamũhutatĩra, magacooka na thuutha. Nĩ kĩĩ gĩgũtũma mwende gũikara mũkĩhũũragwo? Nĩ kĩĩ gĩgũtũma mũthiĩ o na mbere kũrema? Mũtwe wothe nĩmũtihangie, nayo ngoro yothe ĩkanyamarĩka.
english: He will judge between the nations, and will decide concerning many peoples. They shall beat their swords into plowshares, and their spears into pruning hooks. Nation shall not lift up sword against nation, neither shall they learn war any more.

kikuyu: O nacio thũ ciitũ ikiuga atĩrĩ, “Mũira wa mamenye ũhoro kana matuone, tũgũkorwo hamwe nao, tũmoorage na tũtũme wĩra ũcio ũtige kũrutwo.”
english: Malchijah the son of Harim and Hasshub the son of Pahathmoab repaired another portion and the tower of the furnaces.

kikuyu: na mũtuĩrie muone maũndũ marĩa makenagia Mwathani.
english: proving what is well pleasing to the Lord.

kikuyu: Mwathani Jehova ekũũria atĩrĩ: Anga niĩ nĩngenagio nĩ gĩkuũ kĩa andũ arĩa aaganu? Githĩ ti kwenda ingĩenda magarũrũke matigane na mĩthiĩre ĩyo yao nĩguo matũũre muoyo?
english: Have I any pleasure in the death of the wicked?” says the Lord Yahweh, “and not rather that he should return from his way, and live?

kikuyu: Mũndũ mũũgĩ oonaga ũgwati ũgĩũka akehitha, no arĩa matarĩ ũũgĩ mathiiaga o na mbere, nao magatoonya thĩĩna-inĩ.
english: When the righteous triumph, there is great glory; but when the wicked rise, men hide themselves.

kikuyu: Acio nĩmakahunjia ũthingu wake kũrĩ andũ arĩa magaaciarwo, nĩgũkorwo nĩwe wĩkĩte ũguo.
english: They shall come and shall declare his righteousness to a people that shall be born, for he has done it.

kikuyu: “Mũrũ wa mũndũ, nĩnyunĩte guoko kwa Firaũni, mũthamaki wa Misiri. Gũtiohetwo na taama ũrĩ na ndawa nĩguo kũhone, kana gũkoohanio na mĩtĩ nĩguo kũgwate kũgĩe na hinya wa kuoya rũhiũ rwa njora. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, Mwathani Jehova ekuuga ũũ: Niĩ nĩngũũkĩrĩra Firaũni mũthamaki wa bũrũri wa Misiri. Nĩngũmuuna moko meerĩ, kũrĩa kũgima na kũrĩa kuunĩku, na ndũme rũhiũ rwa njora ruume guoko-inĩ gwake rũgwe thĩ.
english: “Son of man, I have broken the arm of Pharaoh king of Egypt. Behold, it has not been bound up, to apply medicines, to put a bandage to bind it, that it may become strong to hold the sword.

kikuyu: Nĩagaciara kahĩĩ, nawe nĩũgagatua Jesũ, tondũ nĩwe ũkaahonokia andũ ake kuuma mehia-inĩ mao.” Na rĩrĩ, maũndũ macio mothe meekĩkire nĩguo ũrĩa Mwathani aarĩtie na kanua ka mũnabii ũhinge, rĩrĩa oigire atĩrĩ,
english: She shall give birth to a son. You shall name him Jesus,‡ for it is he who shall save his people from their sins.”

kikuyu: Icio nĩcio ciarĩ mbarĩ cia Juda; arĩa maatarirwo maarĩ andũ 76,500.
english: These are the families of Judah according to those who were counted of them, seventy-six thousand five hundred.

kikuyu: “Mũndũ o wothe angĩĩhia aage kwĩhokeka harĩ Jehova na ũndũ wa kũheenia mũndũ ũrĩa ũngĩ igũrũ rĩa kĩndũ ehokeirwo kana atigĩirwo amenyerere, kana kĩndũ kĩiyĩtwo, kana amũheenie,
english: “ ‘Or if anyone touches any unclean thing, whether it is the carcass of an unclean animal, or the carcass of unclean livestock, or the carcass of unclean creeping things, and it is hidden from him, and he is unclean, then he shall be guilty.

kikuyu: Mwathani nĩatũmĩte ndũmĩrĩri ya gũũkĩrĩra Jakubu;* nayo nĩĩgakinyĩra Isiraeli. Nao andũ othe nĩmakamenya ũhoro wayo, andũ acio a Efiraimu, na arĩa matũũraga Samaria, o acio moigaga na mwĩtĩĩo na nguthi ngoro-inĩ atĩrĩ,
english: Be sober and self-controlled. Be watchful. Your adversary, the devil, walks around like a roaring lion, seeking whom he may devour.

kikuyu: nĩguo ũkaahingio rĩrĩa mahinda marĩa magĩrĩire magaakinya, nĩguo acookanĩrĩrie indo ciothe iria irĩ igũrũ na iria irĩ gũkũ thĩ harĩ mũtongoria ũmwe, na nĩwe Kristũ. Ningĩ nĩ thĩinĩ wa Kristũ o na ithuĩ twamũkĩrire igai kuuma kũrĩ Ngai, kũringana na ũrĩa aatuĩte o mbere, o we wĩkaga maũndũ mothe kũringana na muoroto wa wendi wake,
english: to an administration of the fullness of the times, to sum up all things in Christ, the things in the heavens and the things on the earth, in him.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo mũthamaki, aikaranĩtie na mũtumia wake, akĩnjũũria atĩrĩ, “Rũgendo rwaku nĩ rwa matukũ maigana na ũgaacooka rĩ?” Mũthamaki akĩona arĩ wega andũme; nĩ ũndũ ũcio ngĩtua ihinda rĩa gũthiĩ. Ningĩ ngĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Mũthamaki angĩona kwagĩrĩire, reke heo marũa ndwarĩre abarũthi a Mũrĩmo-wa-Farati, nĩgeetha manjĩtĩkĩrie hĩtũkĩre kuo kinya nginye Juda.
english: Joiada the son of Paseah and Meshullam the son of Besodeiah repaired the old gate. They laid its beams and set up its doors, its bolts, and its bars.

kikuyu: Ndagacooka kũinũka gwake mũciĩ; harĩa aaikaraga ndagacooka kuonwo ho.
english: Let it still be my consolation, yes, let me exult in pain that doesn’t spare, that I have not denied the words of the Holy One.

kikuyu: Rora wone kana nĩ harĩ mĩthiĩre mĩũru thĩinĩ wakwa, na ũkĩndongorie na njĩra ĩrĩa ya tene na tene.
english: See if there is any wicked way in me, and lead me in the everlasting way.

kikuyu: Ũrĩa wothe wĩ na indo nĩakongererwo nyingĩ; na ũrĩa wothe ũtarĩ nĩagatuunywo o na kĩrĩa arĩ nakĩo.” Ngerekano ya Mbeũ Ĩrakũra
english: A certain woman who had a discharge of blood for twelve years,

kikuyu: O ta ũrĩa itu rĩthiiaga rĩkabuĩria, ũguo noguo ũrĩa ũthiiaga mbĩrĩra-inĩ atacookaga kuoneka.
english: even that it would please God to crush me; that he would let loose his hand, and cut me off!

kikuyu: Bũthi wa bũrũri ndũkaririkanwo, tondũ ngʼaragu ĩrĩa ĩgacooka kũgĩa nĩĩgakorwo ĩrĩ nene mũno.
english: and the plenty will not be known in the land by reason of that famine which follows; for it will be very grievous.

kikuyu: “Ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ na ma, ũndũ o wothe mũkooha gũkũ thĩ, o na igũrũ nĩũkoohwo, na ũrĩa wothe mũkoohora gũkũ thĩ, o na igũrũ nĩũkohorwo.
english: He said to him, “Which ones?” Jesus said, “ ‘You shall not murder.’ ‘You shall not commit adultery.’ ‘You shall not steal.’ ‘You shall not offer false testimony.’

kikuyu: kuona atĩ nĩmũrarũa mbaara ĩrĩa muonete ngĩrũa, na noyo mũraigua atĩ nĩ ndĩrarũa o na rĩu.
english: because for the work of Christ he came near to death, risking his life to

kikuyu: Ngũtũũra ngũgoocaga nginya tene nĩ ũndũ wa ũrĩa wĩkĩte; ndĩrĩĩhokaga rĩĩtwa rĩaku, nĩgũkorwo rĩĩtwa rĩaku nĩ rĩega. Ndĩrĩkũgoocaga ndĩ mbere ya andũ aku arĩa aamũre.
english: I will give you thanks forever, because you have done it. I will hope in your name, for it is good,

kikuyu: Ndĩ na ruo na ngathĩĩnĩka; Wee Ngai kĩĩhonokie na ũngitĩre.
english: But I am in pain and distress. Let your salvation, God, protect me.

kikuyu: “Atĩrĩrĩ, wee mũthamaki, ũtaũri wa kĩroto kĩu nĩguo ũyũ, narĩo ituĩro rĩrĩa rĩtuĩtwo nĩ Ũrĩa-ũrĩ- Igũrũ-Mũno nĩguo rĩgũkore wee, mũthamaki, mwathi wakwa, nĩrĩo rĩĩrĩ:
english: “This is the interpretation, O king, and it is the decree of the Most High, which has come on my lord the king:

kikuyu: na Kedeshi, na Hazoru, na Ithinani,
english: Kedesh, Hazor, Ithnan,

kikuyu: Mbaũ iria ciothe ciagemetio magĩciunanga na mathanwa na ibũi ciao.
english: For neither from the east, nor from the west, nor yet from the south, comes exaltation.

kikuyu: nake ũrĩa wothe ũkwenda gũtuĩka wa mbere thĩinĩ wanyu, no nginya atuĩke ngombo ya andũ othe.
english: Whoever of you wants to become first among you shall be bondservant of all.

kikuyu: “ ‘Mwarĩ wa mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai angĩĩthaahia na gũtuĩka mũmaraya, nĩaconorithĩtie ithe; no nginya acinwo na mwaki.
english: “ ‘The daughter of any priest, if she profanes herself by playing the prostitute, she profanes her father. She shall be burned with fire.

kikuyu: Ũrĩithio ũcio wa cianda-inĩ ũtarĩ ũgwati nĩũkanangwo tondũ wa marakara mahiũ ma Jehova.
english: The peaceful folds are brought to silence because of the fierce anger of Yahweh.

kikuyu: Niĩ ndaarĩ o kuo hĩndĩ ĩrĩa aathondekire igũrũ na akĩrĩiga handũ ha rĩo, hĩndĩ ĩrĩa eekĩrire rũkiriri rwa kũrĩa kũriku,
english: When he established the heavens, I was there. When he set a circle on the surface of the deep,

kikuyu: O nacio njamba cia ita iria nyũmĩrĩru mũno, ikoora irĩ njaga mũthenya ũcio,” ũguo nĩguo Jehova ekuuga.
english: He who is courageous among the mighty will flee away naked on that day,” says Yahweh.

kikuyu: Ekuuga atĩrĩ, “Nĩndeheririe mũrigo kuuma ciande-inĩ ciao; moko mao nĩndameheririe kuuma thĩinĩ wa gĩkabũ.
english: “I removed his shoulder from the burden. His hands were freed from the basket.

kikuyu: akiuga atĩrĩ: “Rĩĩtwa rĩa Ngai rĩrotũũra rĩkumagio tene na tene; ũũgĩ na ũhoti nĩ ciake.
english: In every matter of wisdom and understanding concerning which the king inquired of them, he found them ten times better than all the magicians and enchanters who were in all his realm.

kikuyu: “Ningĩ moe gĩtambaya kĩa rangi wa bururu, mahumbĩre mũtĩ ũrĩa wa kũigĩrĩrwo matawa marĩa mamũrĩkaga, hamwe na matawa maguo, na magathĩ ma gũtinia ndaambĩ, na thaani cia mũrarĩ wa ndaambĩ, na ndigithũ cia gwĩkĩrwo maguta ma matawa macio. Ningĩ makũnje mũtĩ ũcio na indo ciaguo ciothe ciohetwo na gĩthii kĩa njũũa cia pomboo, na macigĩrĩre igũrũ rĩa gĩkuui gĩacio.
english: “They shall take a blue cloth and cover the lamp stand of the light, its lamps, its snuffers, its snuff dishes, and all its oil vessels, with which they minister to it.

kikuyu: Mĩkuru yothe nĩĩgathikwo, nacio irĩma ciothe na tũrĩma nĩikaaraganio. Njĩra iria njogomu nĩikarũngario, nacio njĩra iria itarĩ njaraganu nĩikaaraganio. Nao andũ othe nĩmakona ũhonokio wa Ngai.’ ”
english: Every valley will be filled. Every mountain and hill will be brought low. The crooked will become straight, and the rough ways smooth.

kikuyu: “ ‘Gũtirĩ mũndũ o na ũrĩkũ ũgaathengerera mũndũ wa nyũmba yake nĩguo makomanie; niĩ nĩ niĩ Jehova.
english: “ ‘None of you shall approach any close relatives, to uncover their nakedness: I am Yahweh.

kikuyu: Makenja mĩtwe yao nĩ ũndũ waku, na mehumbe nguo cia makũnia. Nĩmagakũrĩrĩra marĩ na ruo rwa ngoro, na macakae nĩ kĩeha. Na hĩndĩ ĩyo maragirĩka magĩgũcakayagĩra-rĩ, nĩmakambĩrĩria macakaya magũkoniĩ, makiugaga atĩrĩ: “Nũũ ũrĩ wakirio ta Turo, athiũrũrũkĩirio nĩ iria?”
english: They will make themselves bald for you, and clothe themselves with sackcloth. They will weep for you in bitterness of soul, with bitter mourning.

kikuyu: kana amũgũithĩrie ihiga rĩngĩmũũraga atekũmuona, nake mũndũ ũcio akue, no tondũ ti thũ yake na ningĩ ndaciirĩire kũmũgera ngero-rĩ,
english: or with any stone, by which a man may die, not seeing him, and cast it on him so that he died, and he was not his enemy and not seeking his harm,

kikuyu: Nawe ũkĩnguhĩrĩria rĩrĩa ndagũkaĩire, ũkĩnjĩĩra atĩrĩ, “Tiga gwĩtigĩra.”
english: You came near in the day that I called on you. You said, “Don’t be afraid.”
