kikuyu: Naomi akĩĩra Ruthu ũcio mũtumia wa mũriũ atĩrĩ, “Ũndũ ũcio wa gũtwarana na airĩtu ake nĩũgũtuĩka mwega harĩwe, wee mwarĩ wakwa, tondũ mũgũnda-inĩ wa mũndũ ũngĩ wahota kũgerwo ngero.” Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Ruthu agĩikara hakuhĩ na ndungata cia airĩtu cia Boazu akĩhaaraga nginya magetha ma cairi na ma ngano magĩthira. Nake agĩtũũra na nyaciarawe.
english: So Naomi returned, and Ruth the Moabitess, her daughter-in-law, with her, who returned out of the country of Moab. They came to Bethlehem in the beginning of barley harvest.  “Yahweh” is God’s proper Name, sometimes rendered “LORD” (all caps) in other translations.  “Behold”, from “הִ ͏נֵּה”, means look at, take notice, observe, see, or gaze at. It is often used as an interjection.  The Hebrew word rendered “God” is “אֱֹלהִ ֑ים” (Elohim).  “Naomi” means “pleasant”.  “Mara” means “bitter”.

kikuyu: Juda gũgaatũũrwo tene na tene, nakuo Jerusalemu gũtũũrwo njiarwa na njiarwa.
english: But Judah will be inhabited forever, and Jerusalem from generation to generation.

kikuyu: Nacio ndũrĩrĩ nĩikamenya atĩ andũ a Isiraeli maatahĩtwo nĩ ũndũ wa wĩhia wao, tondũ nĩmagire kũnjĩhoka. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio ngĩmahitha ũthiũ wakwa, ngĩmaneana kũrĩ thũ ciao, nao othe makĩũragwo na rũhiũ rwa njora.
english: The nations will know that the house of Israel went into captivity for their iniquity, because they trespassed against me, and I hid my face from them; so I gave them into the hand of their adversaries, and they all fell by the sword.

kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũkaagwĩra ihiga rĩrĩ nĩagathuthĩka, no ũrĩa ũkaagwĩrwo nĩrĩo nĩrĩkamũthethera.” Rĩrĩa athĩnjĩri-Ngai arĩa anene na Afarisai maaiguire ngerekano icio cia Jesũ, makĩmenya atĩ aaragia ũhoro wao.
english: He who falls on this stone will be broken to pieces, but on whomever it will fall, it will scatter him as dust.”

kikuyu: Ũũ nĩguo Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe ekuuga: “Atĩrĩrĩ, nĩngoinanga ũta wa Elamu, ũrĩa arĩ guo kĩhumo kĩa hinya wao.
english: “Yahweh of Armies says: ‘Behold, I will break the bow of Elam, the chief of their might.

kikuyu: niĩ na niĩ nĩngamũũkĩrĩra, na nĩngakĩrĩrĩria kũmũhũũra nĩ ũndũ wa mehia manyu maita mangĩ mũgwanja.
english: then I will also walk contrary to you; and I will strike you, even I, seven times for your sins.

kikuyu: “O na kana ingĩrehithia rũhiũ rwa njora njũkĩrĩre bũrũri ũcio, njuge atĩrĩ, ‘Rũhiũ rwa njora nĩrũtuĩkanĩrie bũrũri ũyũ wothe,’ na njũrage andũ akuo na mahiũ mao-rĩ,
english: You, son of man, set your face against the daughters of your people, who prophesy out of their own heart; and prophesy against them,

kikuyu: Kuumakũrĩaguothemũtũũraga, mũrehemĩgateĩĩrĩĩthondeketwonatũcunjĩ twĩrĩ twa ikũmi twa eba ĩmwe ya mũtu ũrĩa mũhinyu mũno, ĩrugĩtwo na ndawa ya kũimbia, ĩtuĩke ya iruta rĩa gũthũngũthio rĩa maciaro ma mbere marutĩirwo Jehova.
english: You shall bring out of your habitations two loaves of bread for a wave offering made of two tenths of an ephah§ of fine flour. They shall be baked with yeast, for first fruits to Yahweh.

kikuyu: Mũthamaki na andũ ake arĩa maarĩ igweta magĩkenio nĩ mataaro macio, nĩ ũndũ ũcio mũthamaki agĩĩka o ta ũrĩa Memukani oigĩte.
english: This advice pleased the king and the princes, and the king did according to the word of Memucan:

kikuyu: O nake nĩeriragĩria andũ a Ashuri, arĩa maarĩ aathani na anene a ita, na njamba cia ita ciĩhumbĩte nguo irĩ riiri, o arĩa maathiiaga mahaicĩte mbarathi, acio othe maarĩ andũ ethĩ ciĩrorerwa.
english: In you have they taken bribes to shed blood. You have taken interest and increase, and you have greedily gained of your neighbors by oppression, and have forgotten me,” says the Lord Yahweh.

kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũrĩ na gũtũ nĩakĩigue.
english: If anyone has an ear, let him hear.

kikuyu: Ĩkaga ndungata yaku kũringana na wendo waku, na ũndutage kĩrĩra kĩa watho waku.
english: Deal with your servant according to your loving kindness. Teach me your statutes.

kikuyu: Nũũ ũũĩ hinya wa marakara maku? Nĩgũkorwo mangʼũrĩ maku nĩ manene o ta gwĩtigĩrwo kũrĩa wagĩrĩire.
english: Who knows the power of your anger, your wrath according to the fear that is due to you?

kikuyu: Labani akĩmĩĩta Jagari-Sahadutha, nake Jakubu akĩmĩĩta Galeedi.
english: Laban called it Jegar Sahadutha,‡ but Jacob called it Galeed.§

kikuyu: Ngĩcooka ngĩona mũraika ũmwe arũgamĩte thĩinĩ wa riũa, nake akĩanĩrĩra na mũgambo mũnene, agĩĩta nyoni iria ciarerete rĩera-inĩ, agĩciĩra atĩrĩ, “Ũkai, mũngane hamwe iruga-inĩ rĩrĩa inene rĩa Ngai,
english: Its wall is one hundred forty-four cubits,† by the measure of a man, that is, of an angel.

kikuyu: Korwo nĩmwĩtĩkĩtie Musa-rĩ, no mũnjĩtĩkie, nĩgũkorwo nĩ ũhoro wakwa aandĩkire. No kuona atĩ mũtiĩtĩkĩtie ũrĩa aandĩkire-rĩ, mũngĩgĩtĩkia atĩa ũrĩa njugaga?”
english: For if you believed Moses, you would believe me; for he wrote about me.

kikuyu: Ngai angũnjakũnjaga ta nguo na ũndũ wa ũhoti wake mũnene; aanyiitaga ta kanjũ yakwa ngingo-inĩ.
english: My garment is disfigured by great force. It binds me about as the collar of my tunic.

kikuyu: Ithuĩ o na nĩtũragũcookeirie betha iria twakorire mĩromo-inĩ ya makũnia maitũ kuuma bũrũri wa Kaanani; nĩ kĩĩ kĩngĩtũma tũiye betha kana thahabu kuuma nyũmba ya mwathi waku?
english: Behold, the money, which we found in our sacks’ mouths, we brought again to you out of the land of Canaan. How then should we steal silver or gold out of your lord’s house?

kikuyu: Mĩromo ya mũndũ mũkĩĩgu ĩmũrehagĩra ngũĩ, nako kanua gake geetagia rũthanju.
english: A fool’s lips come into strife, and his mouth invites beatings.

kikuyu: Nĩngaherithĩria Beli kũu Babuloni na ndũme ĩtahĩke kĩndũ kĩrĩa gĩothe ĩmeretie. Ndũrĩrĩ itigacooka kũhatĩkana igĩthiĩ kũrĩ yo. Naruo rũthingo rwa Babuloni nĩrũkaagũa.
english: I will execute judgment on Bel in Babylon, and I will bring out of his mouth that which he has swallowed up. The nations will not flow any more to him. Yes, the wall of Babylon will fall.

kikuyu: “ ‘Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Mwathani Jehova ekuuga ũũ: Kuona atĩ nĩwaraihĩte na igũrũ, na ũkaraihia mũthia waguo igũrũ rĩa mathangũ marĩa matumanu, na tondũ nĩwetĩĩagĩra kũraiha kwaguo-rĩ, 11nĩndaũneanire kũrĩ mwathi wa ndũrĩrĩ, nĩguo aũherithie kũringana na ũrĩa waganu waguo watariĩ. Nĩndaũteanirie,
english: Yes, I will make many peoples amazed at you, and their kings will be horribly afraid for you, when I brandish my sword before them. They will tremble at every moment, every man for his own life, in the day of your fall.”

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo ndũmĩrĩri ya Jehova nĩyakinyĩrĩire Jeremia, akĩĩrwo atĩrĩ:
english: Then Yahweh’s word came to Jeremiah, saying,

kikuyu: “Nebukadinezaru mũthamaki wa Babuloni nĩatũmeretie, nĩatũreheire kĩrigiicano, agatũma tũtuĩke ta nyũngũ ya rĩũmba ĩtarĩ kĩndũ. Nĩatũmerũkĩtie o ta ndamathia, nayo nda yake akamĩiyũria na indo ciitũ iria irĩ mũrĩo, na nĩacookete agatũtahĩka.
english: “Nebuchadnezzar the king of Babylon has devoured me. He has crushed me. He has made me an empty vessel. He has, like a monster, swallowed me up. He has filled his mouth with my delicacies. He has cast me out.

kikuyu: Balaki agĩĩka o ta ũrĩa Balamu oigĩte, na o kĩgongona-inĩ agĩthĩnjĩra ndegwa ĩmwe na ndũrũme ĩmwe.
english: Balak did as Balaam had said, and offered up a bull and a ram on every altar.

kikuyu: Matũ makwa nĩmaiguĩte ũhoro waku no rĩu maitho makwa nĩgũkuona makuonete. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio nĩngwĩmena niĩ mwene, na ndĩrire, ndĩhurĩrie rũkũngũ na mũhu.” Ndeto cia Kũrĩkĩrĩria
english: I had heard of you by the hearing of the ear, but now my eye sees you.

kikuyu: Agĩtiirĩrĩra Isiraeli mĩaka mĩrongo ĩĩrĩ na ĩtatũ; agĩcooka agĩkua, na agĩthikwo kũu Shamiru. Jairu Nake Tola akĩrũmĩrĩrwo nĩ Jairu wa kuuma Gileadi, ũrĩa watiirĩrĩire Isiraeli mĩaka mĩrongo ĩĩrĩ na ĩĩrĩ.
english: Gilead’s wife bore him sons. When his wife’s sons grew up, they drove Jephthah out and said to him, “You will not inherit in our father’s house, for you are the son of another woman.”

kikuyu: Agĩcooka kũrĩ ariũ a ithe akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Kamwana karĩa gatirĩ ho! Niĩ ngwĩka atĩa?” Nao makĩoya kanjũ ya Jusufu, magĩthĩnja mbũri na magĩtobokia kanjũ ĩyo thĩinĩ wa thakame ĩyo.
english: He returned to his brothers, and said, “The child is no more; and I, where will I go?”

kikuyu: Nũũ wacũhagia mũhianano wa kũhooywo, o na ageturĩra mũhianano wa gũtwekio ũrĩa ũtangĩmũguna?
english: Who has fashioned a god, or molds an image that is profitable for nothing?

kikuyu: (Andũ othe a Athene na andũ a kũngĩ arĩa maatũũraga kuo matirĩ ũndũ ũngĩ meekaga tiga kwaria na gũthikĩrĩria ũhoro wa maũndũ marĩa mageni).
english: Now all the Athenians and the strangers living there spent their time in nothing else, but either to tell or to hear some new thing.

kikuyu: Ngĩcooka ngĩigua mũraika ũrĩa wathaga maaĩ akiuga atĩrĩ: “Wee ũrĩ na kĩhooto ũgĩtua matuĩro maya, Wee ũrĩa ũrĩ ho, na nowe warĩ ho, o Wee Mũtheru, tondũ wa gũtua ciira ũguo;
english: I heard the angel of the waters saying, “You are righteous, who are and who were, O Holy One, because you have judged these things.

kikuyu: “ ‘Mũndũ mũrũme kana mũndũ-wa-nja ũrĩ maroho marĩa maragũraga kana mũrogi thĩinĩ wanyu, no nginya makooragwo. Mũmahũũre na mahiga nyuguto. Thakame yao nĩĩkamacookerera.’ ”
english: “ ‘A man or a woman that is a medium or is a wizard shall surely be put to death. They shall be stoned with stones. Their blood shall be upon themselves.’ ”

kikuyu: Tũkĩhaica marikabu yoimĩte Aduramutio ĩrĩa yakirie gũthiĩ icukĩro-inĩ cia marikabu iria ciarĩ ndwere-inĩ cia iria rĩrĩa inene bũrũri-inĩ wa Asia, na ithuĩ tũkĩambĩrĩria rũgendo tũgĩthiĩ. Nake Arisitariko, Mũmakedonia woimĩte Thesalonike, aarĩ hamwe na ithuĩ.
english: Embarking in a ship of Adramyttium, which was about to sail to places on the coast of Asia, we put to sea, Aristarchus, a Macedonian of Thessalonica being with us.

kikuyu: na nĩguo ũhoro wa hitho ũrĩa ndaamenyithirio na ũndũ wa kũguũrĩrio nĩ Ngai, o ta ũrĩa nyandĩkĩte na njĩra nguhĩ.
english: how that by revelation the mystery was made known to me, as I wrote before in few words,

kikuyu: na akĩheana bũrũri wao ũtuĩke igai,
english: and gave their land as an inheritance, for his loving kindness endures forever;

kikuyu: Ũgaakorwo wĩnyiihĩtie gũkĩra mothamaki mothe, na ndũgacooka rĩngĩ gwĩtũũgĩria igũrũ rĩa ndũrĩrĩ iria ingĩ. Nĩ niĩ ngaawagithia hinya ũũ atĩ ndũrĩ hĩndĩ ĩngĩ ũgaatha ndũrĩrĩ.
english: It will be the lowest of the kingdoms. It won’t lift itself up above the nations any more. I will diminish them so that they will no longer rule over the nations.

kikuyu: ũrĩ na mathangũ mathaka na maciaro maingĩ, na ũkaheaga andũ othe irio, na nyamũ cia gĩthaka cikaarahaga kĩĩruru-inĩ kĩaguo, na ũkagĩa na itara honge-inĩ ciaguo cia nyoni cia rĩera-inĩ-rĩ,
english: whose leaves were beautiful and its fruit plentiful, and in it was food for all; under which the animals of the field lived, and on whose branches the birds of the sky had their habitation—

kikuyu: Sisera aamũhoire maaĩ, nake akĩmũhe iria; akĩmũrehera iria imata na mbakũri ĩngĩnyuĩrwo nĩ andũ arĩa me igweta.
english: He asked for water. She gave him milk. She brought him butter in a lordly dish.

kikuyu: Ndirĩ na ũgima wa mwĩrĩ tondũ wa mangʼũrĩ maku; mahĩndĩ makwa nĩmarũaru nĩ ũndũ wa mehia makwa.
english: There is no soundness in my flesh because of your indignation, neither is there any health in my bones because of my sin.

kikuyu: Mũndũ mũkĩĩgu acookagĩra ũrimũ wake o ta ũrĩa ngui ĩcookagĩrĩra matahĩko mayo.
english: A word fitly spoken is like apples of gold in settings of silver.

kikuyu: na ndegwa igĩrĩ, na ndũrũme ithano, na thenge ithano na tũtũrũme tũtano twa mwaka ũmwe, irutwo irĩ igongona rĩa ũiguano. Ici nĩcio indo iria ciarutirwo nĩ Nethaneli mũrũ wa Zuaru. * 7:13 nĩ ta kilo ĩmwe na nuthu (1:5) † 7:13 nĩ ta kilo robo ithatũ (0.75) ‡ 7:14 nĩ ta giramu
english: “Speak to Aaron and to his sons, saying, ‘This is how you shall bless the children of Israel.’ You shall tell them,

kikuyu: Nĩũgakinyĩrĩria mũrũũthi, o na nduĩra; ũkarangĩrĩria mũrũũthi ũrĩa mũnene, o na nyoka.
english: You will tread on the lion and cobra. You will trample the young lion and the serpent underfoot.

kikuyu: “Ayubu, thikĩrĩria ũhoro ũyũ; tithĩria hau wĩcũũranie ũhoro wa magegania ma Mũrungu.
english: “Listen to this, Job. Stand still, and consider the wondrous works of God.

kikuyu: Makĩmũcookeria o rĩngĩ makĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Mũthamaki nĩetĩkĩre kwĩra ndungata ciake ũrĩa arotete, na ithuĩ nĩtũkũmũtaũrĩra kĩroto kĩu.” Nake mũthamaki akĩmacookeria atĩrĩ, “Nĩndĩramenya kũna atĩ mũrenda o kũraiharaihia ihinda, tondũ nĩmũramenya atĩ ũndũ ũyũ nĩguo nduĩte ngwatĩirie mũno:
english: The prince of the eunuchs gave names to them: to Daniel he gave the name Belteshazzar; to Hananiah, Shadrach; to Mishael, Meshach; and to Azariah, Abednego.

kikuyu: Angĩkorwo nĩmũhingagia watho ũrĩa mũtue nĩ mũthamaki ũrĩa wandĩkĩtwo Maandĩko-inĩ atĩrĩ, “Enda mũndũ ũrĩa ũngĩ o ta ũrĩa wĩyendete wee mwene,” nĩmwĩkaga wega. No angĩkorwo mũtĩĩagĩra andũ maũthĩ-rĩ, nĩ kwĩhia mwĩhagia na mũrĩ atuĩre nĩ watho atĩ mũrĩ aagarari watho.
english: However, if you fulfill the royal law according to the Scripture, “You shall love your neighbor as yourself,”* you do well.

kikuyu: nĩweyakĩire kĩhumbu, na ũgĩaka ihooero itũũgĩru handũ harĩa hothe haarĩ na kĩhaaro gĩa kũgomanagwo.
english: “that you have built for yourselves a vaulted place, and have made yourselves a lofty place in every street.

kikuyu: kuuma mũhĩrĩga wa Zebuluni, nĩ Eliabu mũrũ wa Heloni;
english: “ ‘If it is an animal of which men offer an offering to Yahweh, all that any man gives of such to Yahweh becomes holy.

kikuyu: nake mũtumia wa Amuramu eetagwo Jokebedi, wa rũciaro rwa Lawi, waciarĩirwo Alawii kũu bũrũri wa Misiri. Nake Jokebedi agĩciarĩra Amuramu Harũni, na Musa, na mwarĩ wa nyina wao Miriamu.
english: The name of Amram’s wife was Jochebed, the daughter of Levi, who was born to Levi in Egypt. She bore to Amram Aaron and Moses, and Miriam their sister.

kikuyu: Jehova nĩeerĩte Musa atĩrĩ,
english: For Yahweh spoke to Moses, saying,

kikuyu: Ngano no nginya ĩthĩo nĩguo ĩthondekwo mũgate; nĩ ũndũ ũcio mũndũ ndatũũraga amĩhũũraga nginya tene. O na angĩtwarĩra magũrũ ma ngaari ya kũhũũra igũrũ rĩayo-rĩ, mbarathi ciake itingĩmĩthĩa.
english: Bread flour must be ground; so he will not always be threshing it. Although he drives the wheel of his threshing cart over it, his horses don’t grind it.

kikuyu: “Mũrũ wa mũndũ, andĩka mũthenya wa ũmũthĩ nĩ tarĩki ciigana, o ũyũ wa ũmũthĩ, tondũ mũthenya ũyũ wa ũmũthĩ mũthamaki wa Babuloni nĩarigiicĩirie Jerusalemu.
english: “Son of man, write the name of the day, this same day. The king of Babylon drew close to Jerusalem this same day.

kikuyu: O rĩngĩ andũ a Isiraeli magĩĩka ũũru maitho-inĩ ma Jehova, na tondũ wa gwĩka ũũru ũcio Jehova akĩhe Egiloni mũthamaki wa Moabi hinya wa gũtooria Isiraeli.
english: The children of Israel again did that which was evil in Yahweh’s sight, and Yahweh strengthened Eglon the king of Moab against Israel, because they had

kikuyu: Macio nĩmo mawatho makoniĩ mũrimũ o ro wothe wa ngoothi ũrĩa ũngĩgwatanio, na ũhere, 55na ũgumu ũrĩ nguo-inĩ kana thĩinĩ wa nyũmba,
english: This is the law for any plague of leprosy, and for an itch,

kikuyu: Maarĩ na mbarathi 736, na nyũmbũ 245,
english: Their horses were seven hundred thirty-six; their mules, two hundred forty-five;

kikuyu: Mahambaataga nduma-inĩ kũrĩa gũtarĩ ũtheri; ningĩ atũmaga matũgũũge ta mũndũ mũrĩĩu.
english: Will you harass a driven leaf? Will you pursue the dry stubble?

kikuyu: na tondũ nĩnduĩte mawatho maku mothe nĩmagĩrĩire, nĩthũire njĩra o yothe ĩtarĩ ya ma. Kwenda Gwathĩkĩra Watho wa Jehova
english: Therefore I consider all of your precepts to be right. I hate every false way. PE

kikuyu: Nũũ wathimire mũigana wayo? Ti-itherũ wee nĩũũĩ! Nũũ watambũrũkirie rũrigi rwa gũthima igũrũ rĩayo?
english: Who determined its measures, if you know? Or who stretched the line on it?

kikuyu: Mĩgũtha ya nyama ciayo nĩmĩnyiitanu mũno; nĩ mĩrũmu ũũ atĩ ndĩngĩenyenyeka.
english: The flakes of his flesh are joined together. They are firm on him. They can’t be moved.

kikuyu: O akĩaragia-rĩ, Judasi, ũmwe wa arutwo arĩa ikũmi na eerĩ, agĩkinya. Ookire arĩ na gĩkundi kĩnene kĩa andũ marĩ na hiũ cia njora na njũgũma, matũmĩtwo nĩ athĩnjĩri-Ngai arĩa anene, na arutani a watho na athuuri.
english: Immediately, while he was still speaking, Judas, one of the twelve, came—and with him a multitude with swords and clubs, from the chief priests, the scribes, and the elders.

kikuyu: Andũ othe nĩmawonete; andũ mawĩroragĩra marĩ o kũraya.
english: All men have looked on it. Man sees it afar off.

kikuyu: “Nĩtũtuĩrĩtie ũhoro ũyũ, na nĩ wa ma. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio kĩũigue na ũũmenye wee mwene.”
english: Behold, we have researched it. It is so. Hear it, and know it for your good.”

kikuyu: Atambũrũkagia matu ma mwena wa gathigathini igũrũ rĩa kũndũ gũtarĩ kĩndũ; nayo thĩ ndarĩ kĩndũ amĩcunjurĩtie nakĩo.
english: They lie all night naked without clothing, and have no covering in the cold.

kikuyu: “Akombori acio makĩnyiita ndungata ciake; makĩhũũra ĩmwe, na makĩũraga ĩrĩa ĩngĩ, na ya gatatũ makĩmĩhũũra na mahiga nyuguto.
english: The farmers took his servants, beat one, killed another, and stoned another.

kikuyu: HĩndĩĩyoAyahudiacioothemakĩgarũrũkĩraSosithenemũtongoriawathunagogi, na makĩmũhũũrĩra hau mbere ya igooti. No Galio ndaigana kũrũmbũyania na ũhoro ũcio o na atĩa. Pirisila na Akula, na Apolo
english: So he reasoned in the synagogue with the Jews and the devout persons, and in the marketplace every day with those who met him.

kikuyu: Agaatuĩka o ta kĩhinga kĩrĩ werũ-inĩ ũtarĩ kĩndũ; ndakoona ũgaacĩru ihinda rĩakinya. Agaatũũra kũndũ kũmũ na kũrĩ nyoota werũ-inĩ, o kũu bũrũri-inĩ wa cumbĩ gũtatũũraga mũndũ.
english: For he will be like a bush in the desert, and will not see when good comes, but will inhabit the parched places in the wilderness, an uninhabited salt land.

kikuyu: Nowe Petero akĩmũrũmĩrĩra arĩ haraaya, o nginya agĩkinya nja
english: But Peter followed him from a distance to the court of the high priest, and entered in and sat with the officers, to see the end.

kikuyu: “Wee Mwarĩ wa Zayuni-rĩ, anĩrĩra na ũkene, nĩgũkorwo nĩndĩrooka gũtũũra gatagatĩ-inĩ kanyu,” ũguo nĩguo Jehova ekuuga.
english: The man who stood among the myrtle trees answered, “They are the ones Yahweh has sent to go back and forth through the earth.”

kikuyu: Atwari ayo othe makĩmaka na o mũndũ agĩkaĩra ngai yake; magĩcooka magĩikia mĩrigo iria-inĩ, nĩgeetha marikabu ĩhũthe. No Jona nĩaikũrũkĩte kũu marikabu thĩinĩ, na agakoma toro mũnene.
english: The waters surrounded me, even to the soul. The deep was around me. The weeds were wrapped around my head.  “Yahweh” is God’s proper Name, sometimes rendered “LORD” (all caps) in other translations.  or, gods  or, gods  The Hebrew word rendered “God” is “אֱֹלהִ ֑ים” (Elohim).  Sheol is the place of the dead.

kikuyu: Nĩguo ũhoro ũrĩa waaririo na kanua ka mũnabii ũhinge rĩrĩa oigire atĩrĩ: “Ngaatumũra kanua gakwa njarie na ngerekano, nĩngaaria maũndũ marĩa matũire marĩ mahithe kuuma rĩrĩa thĩ yombirwo.” Gũtaarĩria Ngerekano ya Itindiĩ
english: When the people of that place recognized him, they sent into all that surrounding region and brought to him all who were sick;

kikuyu: nĩguo kũmenyekage rĩrĩa kĩndũ gĩtarĩ na thaahu na rĩrĩa kĩrĩ na thaahu. Macio nĩmo mawatho makoniĩ mĩrimũ ya ngoothi ĩrĩa ĩngĩgwatanio, na makoniĩ ũgumu.
english: to teach when it is unclean, and when it is clean. This is the law of leprosy.

kikuyu: akĩmũtigithia kũrĩithia ngʼondu, akĩmutua mũrĩithi wa andũ ake, nĩo mbarĩ ya Jakubu, arĩithagie Isiraeli igai rĩake. Nake Daudi akĩmarĩithia arĩ na wagĩrĩru wa ngoro; aamatongoragia na ũũgĩ wa moko make.
english: from following the ewes that have their young, he brought him to be the shepherd of Jacob, his people, and Israel, his inheritance.

kikuyu: Jehova ekuuga atĩrĩ: “Njookererai, inyuĩ andũ aya mũũhutatĩire, nĩgũkorwo nĩ niĩ mũthuuri wanyu; nĩngamũcookanĩrĩria, ndute mũndũ ũmweitũũra-inĩrĩmwe, na angĩ eerĩ ndĩmarute mũhĩrĩga ũmwe, ndĩmũtware Zayuni.
english: “Return, backsliding children,” says Yahweh, “for I am a husband to you. I will take one of you from a city, and two from a family, and I will bring you to Zion.

kikuyu: Nĩtuonire Anefili kuo (njiaro cia Anaki, a kĩruka kĩa Anefili). Tweyonaga tũhaana ta ndaahi, o nao moonaga tũhaana o ta cio.”
english: There we saw the Nephilim,† the sons of Anak, who come from the Nephilim.‡ We were in our own sight as grasshoppers, and so we were in their sight.”

kikuyu: Tondũ nĩmwatũmire arĩa athingu morwo nĩ hinya nĩ ũndũ wa kũmaheenia o rĩrĩa niĩ itaamareheire kĩeha, na mũgĩteithĩrĩria arĩa aaganu mage gũtiga njĩra ciao cia waganu nĩguo mahonokie mĩoyo yao,
english: Because with lies you have grieved the heart of the righteous, whom I have not made sad; and strengthened the hands of the wicked, that he should not return from his wicked way, and be saved alive.

kikuyu: Mwaka ũcio wa mĩrongo ĩtano ũgaatuĩka wa Jubilii kũrĩ inyuĩ; mũtikanahaande na mũtikanagethe kĩrĩa gĩĩkũrĩtie kĩo kĩene, kana mũgethe mĩthabibũ ĩrĩa ĩtarĩ mĩrutĩre wĩra.
english: That fiftieth year shall be a jubilee to you. In it you shall not sow, neither reap that which grows of itself, nor gather from the undressed vines.

kikuyu: Nĩũtũmĩte itũũra rĩrĩa inene rĩtuĩke hĩba ya mahiga, itũũra rĩu rĩirigĩre na hinya ũgatũma rĩanangwo, itũũra inene rĩrĩa rĩarĩ kĩĩhitho kĩa andũ a kũngĩ, ti itũũra rĩngĩ, na rĩtigaakwo rĩngĩ. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio andũ arĩa marĩ hinya nĩmagagũtĩĩa, namo matũũra manene ma ndũrĩrĩ iria itarĩ tha magwĩtigĩre.
english: For you have made a city into a heap, a fortified city into a ruin, a palace of strangers to be no city. It will never be built.

kikuyu: “ ‘Mũndũ o wothe ũngĩka ũndũ o na ũmwe wa maũndũ macio marĩ magigi, andũ ta acio no nginya maingatwo kuuma kũrĩ andũ ao.
english: “ ‘For whoever shall do any of these abominations, even the souls that do them shall be cut off from among their people.

kikuyu: Zayuni atambũrũkĩtie moko, no gũtirĩ mũndũ wa kũmũhooreria. Jehova nĩathanĩte ũhoro wa Jakubu atĩ arĩa mariganĩtie nake matuĩke thũ ciake; Jerusalemu nĩatuĩkĩte kĩndũ kĩrĩ thaahu gatagatĩ-inĩ kao.
english: Zion spreads out her hands. There is no one to comfort her. Yahweh has commanded concerning Jacob, that those who are around him should be his adversaries. Jerusalem is among them as an unclean thing.

kikuyu: o wee twanakenanĩra tũrĩ na ngwatanĩro njega, tũgĩthiĩ hamwe na andũ arĩa angĩ nyũmba-inĩ ya Ngai.
english: We took sweet fellowship together. We walked in God’s house with company.

kikuyu: Goocai Jehova, inyuĩ araika ake, inyuĩ njamba irĩ hinya, inyuĩ mwĩkaga ũrĩa oigĩte, o inyuĩ mwathĩkagĩra kiugo gĩake.
english: Praise Yahweh, you angels of his, who are mighty in strength, who fulfill his word, obeying the voice of his word.

kikuyu: Kũgĩa na ũtonga na njĩra ya rũrĩmĩ rwa maheeni nĩ kĩbii gĩa gũthira, na nĩ mũtego wa gĩkuũ.
english: Many men claim to be men of unfailing love, but who can find a faithful man?

kikuyu: Wee Ngai wakwa, ndirikana, ũnjĩke wega nĩ ũndũ wa maũndũ marĩa mothe njĩkĩire andũ aya.
english: Remember me, my God, for all the good that I have done for this people.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo nĩndamaheire ciugo iria waheire, nao magĩciĩtĩkĩra. Nĩmamenyire hatarĩ nganja atĩ ndoimire kũrĩ wee, na magĩĩtĩkia atĩ wee nĩwe wandũmire.
english: When he has come, he will convict the world about sin, about righteousness, and about judgment;

kikuyu: Mwathani Jehova ekuuga atĩrĩ: Ti-itherũ o ta ũrĩa niĩ ndũũraga muoyo, tondũ rũũru rwakwa nĩ rwagĩte mũrĩithi, na nĩrũtahĩtwo rũgatuĩka irio cia nyamũ ciothe cia gĩthaka, na ningĩ tondũ arĩithi akwa matiigana gũcaria rũũru rwakwa, no nĩo ene meemenyereire handũ ha kũmenyerera rũũru rwakwa-rĩ,
english: “As I live,” says the Lord Yahweh, “surely because my sheep became a prey, and my sheep became food to all the animals of the field, because there was no shepherd, and my shepherds didn’t search for my sheep, but the shepherds fed themselves, and didn’t feed my sheep,

kikuyu: O na gũtuĩka mwĩtĩĩo wake nĩ ũkinyaga o igũrũ, naguo mũtwe wake ũkahutia matu-rĩ,
english: Though his height mount up to the heavens, and his head reach to the clouds,

kikuyu: Makĩmatũma na marũa maya: Nĩ ithuĩ atũmwo na athuuri, o ithuĩ ariũ a Ithe wanyu, Kũrĩ andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ arĩa metĩkĩtie kũu Antiokia, na Suriata, na Kilikia: Amũkĩrai ngeithi.
english: They wrote these things by their hand: “The apostles, the elders, and the brothers, to the brothers who are of the Gentiles in Antioch, Syria, and Cilicia: greetings.

kikuyu: Oyai icooki rĩakwa mwĩigĩrĩre na mwĩrute na niĩ, nĩgũkorwo ndĩ mũhooreri na mwĩnyiihia ngoro-inĩ, nacio ngoro cianyu nĩikũhurũkio.
english: Take my yoke upon you and learn from me, for I am gentle and humble in heart; and you will find rest for your souls.

kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũrĩ na ũũgĩ wa ngoro nĩetĩkagĩra mawatho, no mũndũ wa kũhũhũtĩka nĩakanangĩka.
english: A righteous person is delivered out of trouble, and the wicked takes his place.

kikuyu: nĩ ũndũ matukũ macio gũgaakorwo na mĩnyamaro ĩtarĩ yoneka ĩngĩ tayo kuuma kĩambĩrĩria rĩrĩa Ngai ombire thĩ nginya rĩu, na gũtikoneka ĩngĩ tayo.
english: For in those days there will be oppression, such as there has not been the like from the beginning of the creation which God created until now, and never will be.

kikuyu: na Pashuru, na Amaria, na Malikija,
english: Pashhur, Amariah, Malchijah,

kikuyu: Mũrũ wakwa, ngoro yaku ĩngĩgĩa na ũũgĩ-rĩ, hĩndĩ ĩyo ngoro yakwa no ĩkene;
english: My son, if your heart is wise, then my heart will be glad, even mine.

kikuyu: “Na rĩrĩ, nĩ ũndũ wa kũringĩrĩrio nĩ Roho Mũtheru, ndĩrathiĩ Jerusalemu itekũmenya maũndũ marĩa makaangora ndĩ kũu.
english: Having sent into Macedonia two of those who served him, Timothy and Erastus, he himself stayed in Asia for a while.

kikuyu: Aakorwo nĩ mũndũ wa kuuma mweri ũmwe nginya mĩaka ĩtano, ũgaatua irĩhi rĩa kahĩĩ rĩtuĩke nĩ cekeri ithano cia betha,† na irĩhi rĩa kairĩtu nĩ cekeri ithatũ cia betha.‡
english: If the person is from a month old to five years old, then your valuation shall be for a male five shekels of silver, and for  A shekel is about

kikuyu: Roho wake ũgakuhĩrĩria mbĩrĩra, na muoyo wake ũgakuhĩrĩria arĩa mareehage gĩkuũ.
english: Yes, his soul draws near to the pit, and his life to the destroyers.

kikuyu: no ti-itherũ Ngai nĩathikĩrĩirie, na akĩigua mũgambo wakwa ngĩmũhooya.
english: But most certainly, God has listened. He has heard the voice of my prayer.

kikuyu: “ ‘Hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩndahĩtũkĩire hau warĩ ngĩkuona ũkĩĩgaragaria thakame-inĩ yaku, na rĩrĩa wakomete hau thakame-inĩ yaku-rĩ, ngĩkwĩra atĩrĩ, “Tũũra muoyo!”
english: “Therefore tell the house of Israel, ‘The Lord Yahweh says: “Return, and turn yourselves from your idols! Turn away your faces from all your abominations.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo maitho mao eerĩ makĩhingũka, nao makĩmenya atĩ maarĩ njaga; nĩ ũndũ ũcio magĩtumania mathangũ ma mũkũyũ, magĩĩthondekera kĩndũ gĩa kũhumbĩra njaga yao.
english: For I do not wish to see you now in passing, but I hope to stay a while with you, if the Lord permits.

kikuyu: Jeremia ũcio mũnabii akĩmacookeria atĩrĩ, “Nĩndamũigua, na no nginya ndĩmũhooere harĩ Jehova Ngai wanyu o ta ũguo mũnjũũrĩtie njĩke; nĩngamwĩra ũrĩa wothe Jehova ekuuga, na ndirĩ ũndũ o na ũmwe ngaaga kũmwĩra.”
english: Then Jeremiah the prophet said to them, “I have heard you. Behold, I will pray to Yahweh your God according to your words; and it will happen that whatever thing Yahweh answers you, I will declare it to you. I will keep nothing back from you.”

kikuyu: Paũlũ akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Niĩ ndĩ Mũyahudi wa kuuma Tariso, bũrũri wa Kilikia, na itũũra rĩrĩa ndaciarĩirwo rĩrĩ igweta. Ndagũthaitha, reke njarĩrie andũ aya.”
english: But Paul said, “I am a Jew from Tarsus in Cilicia, a citizen of no insignificant city. I beg you, allow me to speak to the people.”

kikuyu: Ikĩrĩ kũ ngai icio mwethondekeire? Kĩrekei ciũke, aakorwo no imũhonokie rĩrĩa mũrĩ thĩĩna-inĩ! Nĩgũkorwo ngai ciaku cingĩhĩte o ta matũũra maku, wee Juda.
english: “But where are your gods that you have made for yourselves? Let them arise, if they can save you in the time of your trouble, for you have as many gods as you have towns, O Judah.

kikuyu: Alekisandero ũrĩa mũturi wa indo cia rũthuku nĩanjĩkire ũũru mũno. Mwathani nĩwe ũkaamũrĩha kũringana na ciĩko ciake.
english: that you keep the commandment without spot, blameless until the appearing of our Lord Jesus Christ,

kikuyu: Oya nguo ya mũndũ ũrĩa ũrũgamagĩrĩra thiirĩ wa mũndũ wa kũngĩ; mĩige ĩrũgamĩrĩre thiirĩ ũcio wa mũndũ-wa-nja wa kũngĩ.
english: He who conceals his sins doesn’t prosper, but whoever confesses and renounces them finds mercy.

kikuyu: na Jaala, na Darikoni, na Gideli,
english: the children of Jaala, the children of Darkon, the children of Giddel,

kikuyu: O nake agĩthondeka irio njega na agĩcirehere ithe. Agĩcooka akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Baba, wĩtiire ũrĩe nyama imwe cia ũguĩmi wakwa nĩgeetha ũndathime na kĩrathimo gĩaku.” Nake ithe Isaaka akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “We nĩwe ũ?” Nake akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Nĩ niĩ Esaũ, mũrũ waku wa irigithathi.”
english: He also made savory food, and brought it to his father. He said to his father, “Let my father arise, and eat of his son’s venison, that your soul may bless me.”

kikuyu: Jehova nĩ mũnene Zayuni; nĩatũũgĩrĩte igũrũ rĩa ndũrĩrĩ ciothe.
english: Serve Yahweh with gladness. Come before his presence with singing.

kikuyu: Nĩũkaneana ndegwa ĩtarĩ ngũrũ ĩtuĩke ya kũrutwo iruta rĩa mehia, ũmĩneane kũrĩ athĩnjĩri-Ngai, arĩa Alawii, a nyũmba ya Zadoku, arĩa manguhagĩrĩria makĩndungatĩra, ũguo nĩguo Mwathani Jehova ekuuga.
english: so that there was the face of a man toward the palm tree on the one side, and the face of a young lion toward the palm tree on the other side. It was made like this through all the house all around.

kikuyu: Rĩathani rĩakwa nĩ rĩĩrĩ: Endanagai inyuĩ
english: “This is my commandment, that you love one another, even as I have loved you.

kikuyu: Aini o nao nĩmacookanĩrĩirio hamwe kuuma ngʼongo iria ciathiũrũrũkĩirie Jerusalemu, magĩcookanĩrĩrio kuuma tũtũũra-inĩ twa Anetofathi,
english: The sons of the singers gathered themselves together, both out of the plain around Jerusalem and from the villages of the Netophathites;

kikuyu: na Jakubu wa Zebedi na mũrũ wa nyina Johana (nao acio akĩmatua Boanerigesi, ũguo nĩ kuuga Ariũ a Marurumĩ); 18na Anderea, na Filipu na Baritholomayo, na Mathayo, na Toma, na Jakubu wa Alufayo na Thadayo, na Simoni ũrĩa Mũzelote
english: When Jesus heard it, he said to them, “Those who are healthy have no need for a physician, but those who are sick. I came not to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance.”

kikuyu: kuuma mũhĩrĩga wa Isakaru, ikĩheo Kishioni, na Deberathu, 29na Jaramuthu, na Eni-Ganimu, hamwe na ithaka ciamo cia ũrĩithio; mothe maarĩ matũũra mana;
english: Out of the tribe of Issachar, Kishion with its pasture lands, Daberath with its pasture lands,

kikuyu: Mũndũ mũkĩĩgu akenagĩra mĩtugo mĩũru, no mũndũ ũrĩ na ũtaũku akenagĩra ũũgĩ.
english: It is a fool’s pleasure to do wickedness, but wisdom is a man of understanding’s pleasure.

kikuyu: Maamũre, ũmatherie na ũhoro ũrĩa wa ma; ũhoro waku nĩguo ũhoro wa ma.
english: Then the maid who kept the door said to Peter, “Are you also one of this man’s disciples?” He said, “I am not.”

kikuyu: Ngai aracangarara arĩ Zayuni itũũra rĩu ithaka mũno.
english: Out of Zion, the perfection of beauty, God shines out.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo ngĩririkana ũrĩa Mwathani oigĩte atĩrĩ, ‘Johana aabatithanagia na maaĩ, no inyuĩ mũkaabatithio na Roho Mũtheru.’
english: I remembered the word of the Lord, how he said, ‘John indeed baptized in water, but you will be baptized in the Holy Spirit.’

kikuyu: “Na rĩrĩ, ihinda-inĩ rĩa athamaki acio, Ngai wa igũrũ nĩakahaanda ũthamaki ũtagacooka kwanangwo, kana ũtigĩrwo andũ angĩ. Nĩũkahehenja mothamaki macio mangĩ mothe ũmaniine,
english: “In the days of those kings the God of heaven will set up a kingdom which will never be destroyed, nor will its sovereignty be left to another people; but it will break in pieces and consume all these kingdoms, and it will stand forever.

kikuyu: “Ũngĩgirĩrĩria magũrũ maku gũthũkia mũthenya wa Thabatũ, na wage gwĩka maũndũ maku we mwene mũthenya ũcio wakwa ũrĩa mwamũre, nayo Thabatũ ĩyo ũmĩtue mũthenya wa gĩkeno, na mũthenya ũcio mwamũre wa Jehova ũũtue wa gũtĩĩo-rĩ, na ũngĩũtĩĩa na kwaga kũrũmĩrĩra njĩra ciaku, na kwaga gwĩka maũndũ maku we mwene, o na kana kwaria ndeto cia tũhũ-rĩ,
english: “If you turn away your foot from the Sabbath, from doing your pleasure on my holy day, and call the Sabbath a delight, and the holy of Yahweh honorable, and honor it, not doing your own ways, nor finding your own pleasure, nor speaking your own words,

kikuyu: “Nĩ ũndũ ũcio nguuga atĩrĩ, ‘Thikĩrĩriai; o na niĩ nĩngũmwĩra ũrĩa njũũĩ.’
english: Behold, he finds occasions against me. He counts me for his enemy.

kikuyu: Stefano Kwarĩria Kĩama Nake mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai ũrĩa mũnene akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Thitango ici nĩ cia ma?” Nake Stefano agĩcookia atĩrĩ, “Ariũ a Baba na maithe maitũ, ta thikĩrĩriai! Ngai mwene riiri nĩoimĩrĩire ithe witũ Iburahĩmu hĩndĩ ĩrĩa aatũũraga bũrũri wa Mesopotamia, atanathiĩ gũtũũra bũrũri wa Harani.
english: ‘I will judge the nation to which they will be in bondage,’ said God, ‘and after that they will come out and serve me in this place.’*

kikuyu: Gĩkuũ nĩkĩguũranie mbere ya Ngai; mwanangĩko ũikarĩte ũtarĩ mũhumbĩre.
english: They cut their food in the field. They glean the vineyard of the wicked.

kikuyu: na tondũ wa ũguo andũ a Asheri magĩtũũrania na andũ a Kaanani arĩa maatũũraga bũrũri ũcio.
english: but the Asherites lived among the Canaanites, the inhabitants of the land, for they didn’t drive them out.

kikuyu: “Na rĩrĩ, mũgwĩciiria atĩa? Nĩ kwarĩ na mũndũ warĩ na ariũ eerĩ. Agĩthiĩ harĩ ũrĩa wa mbere, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, ‘Mũriũ, thiĩ ũkarute wĩra ũmũthĩ mũgũnda-inĩ wa mĩthabibũ.’
english: But what do you think? A man had two sons, and he came to the first, and said, ‘Son, go work today in my vineyard.’

kikuyu: Ariũ a Shemu nĩ aya: Elamu, na Ashuri, na Arafakasadi, na Ludi, na Aramu. Nao ariũ a Aramu nĩ aya: Uzu, na Hulu, na Getheru, na Mesheki.
english: The sons of Shem were: Elam, Asshur, Arpachshad, Lud, and Aram.

kikuyu: Ngenagĩra kĩĩranĩro gĩaku, o ta ũrĩa mũndũ atahĩte indo nyingĩ akenaga.
english: I rejoice at your word, as one who finds great plunder.

kikuyu: Ũthaka waguo warĩ mũkaru, na mathĩgĩ maguo magatheerema, nĩgũkorwo mĩri yaguo nĩyatoonyete thĩ tĩĩri-inĩ kũrĩa kwarĩ na maaĩ maingĩ.
english: When I extinguish you, I will cover the heavens and make its stars dark. I will cover the sun with a cloud, and the moon won’t give its light.

kikuyu: “ ‘Mũndũmũrũmeangĩkomanamũndũmũrũmeũngĩtaũrĩamũndũakomaganamũndũ-wa-nja- rĩ, andũ acio eerĩ nĩmekĩte ũndũ ũrĩ magigi. No nginya mooragwo; thakame yao nĩĩkamacookerera.
english: But evil men and impostors will grow worse and worse, deceiving and being deceived.

kikuyu: “Andũ nĩmaiguĩte mũcaayo wakwa, no gũtirĩ mũndũ o na ũmwe wa kũũhooreria. Thũ ciakwa ciothe nĩciguĩte ũhoro wa hatĩka ciakwa, nacio igakena nĩ ũndũ wa ũguo wĩkĩte. Nawe gĩkinyie mũthenya ũrĩa wanĩrĩire nĩguo nao mahaane ta niĩ.
english: “They have heard that I sigh. There is no one to comfort me. All my enemies have heard of my trouble. They are glad that you have done it. You will bring the day that you have proclaimed, and they will be like me.

kikuyu: nĩguo agirie mũndũ ũtarĩ na ũngai gwathana, amũgirie kũigĩra kĩrĩndĩ mĩtego.
english: that the godless man may not reign, that there be no one to ensnare the people.

kikuyu: Nakĩo gĩkonde gĩkĩ gĩakwa kĩarĩkia gũthira, na mwĩrĩ ũyũ wa nyama ũkorwo ũtarĩ ho, hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩ ngoona Ngai;
english: After my skin is destroyed, then I will see God in my flesh,

kikuyu: Wee Jehova, hatũra kuuma moko-inĩ ma andũ arĩa ooru; ngitĩra harĩ andũ arĩa mahũthagĩra ũhinya,
english: Yahweh, I have called on you. Come to me quickly! Listen to my voice when I call to you.

kikuyu: na hũngũ ĩrĩa ndune, na mũthemba o wothe wa hũngũ iria njirũ,
english: the red kite, any kind of black kite,

kikuyu: nĩngaherithia wĩhia wao na rũthanju, herithie waganu wao na kũmahũũra iboko;
english: then I will punish their sin with the rod, and their iniquity with stripes.

kikuyu: Mikaia aarĩkia kũmahe ũhoro wothe ũrĩa aiguĩte Baruku agĩthomera andũ kuuma ibuku-inĩ rĩa gĩkũnjo-rĩ,
english: When he was in Benjamin’s gate, a captain of the guard was there, whose name was Irijah, the son of Shelemiah, the son of Hananiah; and he seized Jeremiah the prophet, saying, “You are defecting to the Chaldeans!”

kikuyu: Ti wega kũherithia mũndũ ũtekĩte mahĩtia, kana kũhũũra anene nĩ ũndũ wa ũrũngĩrĩru wao.
english: Also to punish the righteous is not good, nor to flog officials for their integrity.

kikuyu: Mĩthenya ĩyo ĩtatũ ĩtanathira, arũme othe a Juda na Benjamini makĩũngana Jerusalemu. Naguo mũthenya wa mĩrongo ĩĩrĩ wa mweri wa kenda, andũ othe nĩmaikarĩte thĩ kĩhaaro-inĩ kĩrĩa kĩarĩ mbere ya nyũmba ya Ngai, mathĩnĩkĩte mũno tondũ wa ũndũ ũcio na nĩ ũndũ wa mbura.
english: But Jehoiada the priest took a chest and bored a hole in its lid, and set it beside the altar, on the right side as one comes into Yahweh’s house; and the priests who kept the threshold put all the money that was brought into Yahweh’s house into it.

kikuyu: Jehova ekuuga atĩrĩ: “Nĩ ũndũ wa mehia matatũ ma Edomu, o na mana-rĩ, niĩ ndikahũndũra mangʼũrĩ makwa. Tondũ nĩatengʼeririe mũrũ wa nyina na rũhiũ rwa njora, atarĩ na tha, * 1:1 Tekoa rĩarĩ itũũra rĩa Juda, gũthini wa itũũra rĩa Bethilehemu. † 1:6 Gaza rĩarĩ itũũra rĩmwe rĩa bata rĩa matũũra matano ma Afilisti, hamwe na matũũra ma Ashidodi, na Ashikeloni, na Ekironi, o na Gathu.
english: Yahweh says: “For three transgressions of Edom, yes, for four, I will not turn away its punishment, because he pursued his brother with the sword and cast off all pity, and his anger raged continually, and he kept his wrath forever;

kikuyu: Jusufu akĩhaarĩria ngaari yake ya mbarathi, na agĩthiĩ Gosheni gũtũnga ithe Isiraeli. Na Jusufu aakinya o ũguo harĩ ithe, akĩmũhĩmbĩria na akĩrĩra ihinda inene. Nake Isiraeli akĩĩra Jusufu atĩrĩ, “Rĩu no ngĩkue, kuona atĩ nĩndeyonera niĩ mwene atĩ ũrĩ o muoyo.”
english: Joseph prepared his chariot, and went up to meet Israel, his father, in Goshen. He presented himself to him, and fell on his neck, and wept on his neck a good while.

kikuyu: na ageni arĩa othe matũũraga kuo; na ningĩ athamaki othe a Uzu; na ningĩ athamaki othe a Afilisti (aya nĩ andũ a Ashikeloni, na Gaza, na Ekironi, o na andũ arĩa maatigaire Ashidodi);
english: and all the mixed people, and all the kings of the land of Uz, all the kings of the Philistines, Ashkelon, Gaza, Ekron, and the remnant of Ashdod;

kikuyu: Thuutha ũcio Elizabethi mũtumia wake akĩgĩa nda, na handũ ha mĩeri ĩtano agĩikara ehithĩte.
english: After these days Elizabeth his wife conceived, and she hid herself five months, saying,

kikuyu: “Inyuĩ matigari maya ma Juda, Jehova amwĩrĩte atĩrĩ, ‘Mũtigaathiĩ bũrũri wa Misiri.’ Menyai wega ũndũ ũyũ: Ũmũthĩ ũyũ nĩndamũmenyithia
english: “Yahweh has spoken concerning you, remnant of Judah, ‘Don’t go into Egypt!’ Know certainly that I have testified to you today.

kikuyu: Rĩu-rĩ, angĩkorwo mũtingĩhota gwĩka ũndũ mũnini ũguo-rĩ, mũgĩtangĩkaga na maũndũ macio mangĩ nĩkĩ?”
english: Then you will begin to say, ‘We ate and drank in your presence, and you taught in our streets.’

kikuyu: Ũũ nĩguo thugundĩte gwĩka thĩ yothe; nakuo guoko kũrĩa gũtambũrũkĩirio ndũrĩrĩ ciothe nĩkuo gũkũ.
english: This is the plan that is determined for the whole earth. This is the hand that is stretched out over all the nations.

kikuyu: Thagathagai ndũrĩrĩ ikahũũrane nake; thagathagai athamaki a Amedi, na abarũthi, na anene ao othe, na andũ a mabũrũri mothe marĩa maathagwo nĩo.
english: Prepare against her the nations, the kings of the Medes, its governors, and all its deputies, and all the land of their dominion!

kikuyu: Mũnjĩtaga ‘Mũrutani’ na ‘Mwathani’, na ũguo nĩguo kũrĩ, nĩgũkorwo ũguo nĩguo ndĩ. Na rĩrĩ, kuona atĩ Niĩ, Mwathani na Mũrutani wanyu, nĩndamũthambia nyarĩrĩ-rĩ, o na inyuĩ nĩmwagĩrĩirwo mũthambanagie nyarĩrĩ mũndũ na ũrĩa ũngĩ.
english: 1 Now before the feast of the Passover, Jesus, knowing that his time had come that he would depart from this world to the Father, having loved his own who were in the world, he loved them to the end.

kikuyu: Ti-itherũ wee nĩũũĩ, nĩgũkorwo warĩ mũciare! Wee-rĩ, nĩũtũũrĩte mĩaka mĩingĩ!
english: He paws in the valley, and rejoices in his strength. He goes out to meet the armed men.

kikuyu: ndibei ĩrĩa ĩkenagia ngoro ya mũndũ na maguta ma gũtũma ũthiũ wake ũkenge, o na irio iria ciĩkagĩra ngoro yake hinya.
english: wine that makes the heart of man glad, oil to make his face to shine, and bread that strengthens man’s heart.

kikuyu: Mwathani Jehova ekuuga atĩrĩ: Ti-itherũ o ta ũrĩa niĩ ndũũraga muoyo, ngaamwathaga na guoko kũrĩ hinya, na guoko gũtambũrũkĩtio, na mangʼũrĩ maitanĩrĩirio.
english: As I live,” says the Lord Yahweh, “surely with a mighty hand, with an outstretched arm, and with wrath poured out, I will be king over you.

kikuyu: Ĩrimũthagĩra irĩma-inĩ ĩgĩetha gwa kũrĩa, ĩkahaara kahuti o gothe karuru.
english: The range of the mountains is his pasture. He searches after every green thing. 9“Will the wild ox be content to serve you? Or will he stay by your feeding trough?

kikuyu: Akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Thiĩi thĩ yothe mũkahunjĩrie andũ othe Ũhoro-ũrĩa-Mwega. Na mũndũ o wothe ũgetĩkia na abatithio nĩakahonoka, no ũrĩa wothe ũtagetĩkia nĩagatuĩrwo ciira.
english: He said to them, “Go into all the world and preach the Good News to the whole creation.

kikuyu: Agĩtũma bũrũri ũcio ũgĩe ngʼaragu, na akĩniina mĩthiithũ yao yothe ya irio;
english: He called for a famine on the land. He destroyed the food supplies.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo nĩohagwo kaingĩ na mĩnyororo moko na magũrũ, no akamĩtuanga icunjĩ na akoinanga igera iria ciamuohaga magũrũ. Na gũtirĩ mũndũ warĩ na hinya wa kũmũhooreria.
english: because he had been often bound with fetters and chains, and the chains had been torn apart by him, and the fetters broken in pieces. Nobody had the strength to tame him.

kikuyu: Ngoga nĩ cia kũheenania, naguo ũthaka nĩ wa tũhũ; no mũtumia ũrĩa mwĩtigĩri Jehova nĩ wa kũgaathĩrĩrio.
english: Charm is deceitful, and beauty is vain; but a woman who fears Yahweh, she shall be praised.

kikuyu: Jakubu akĩhaanda gĩtugĩ kĩa ihiga handũ hau Ngai aarĩtie nake, agĩgĩitĩrĩria ndibei; agĩcooka agĩgĩitĩrĩria maguta.
english: These were the sons of Oholibamah, the daughter of Anah, the daughter of Zibeon, Esau’s wife: she bore to Esau Jeush, Jalam, and Korah.

kikuyu: Andũ a Isiraeli nĩmekuuĩire indo ciothe iria maatahire, na mahiũ ma matũũra macio, no makĩũraga andũ othe na rũhiũ rwa njora o nginya makĩmaniina biũ, na matiigana gũtiga mũndũ o na ũmwe warĩ na mĩhũmũ.
english: The children of Israel took all the plunder of these cities, with the livestock, as plunder for themselves; but every man they struck with the edge of the sword, until they had destroyed them. They didn’t leave any who breathed.

kikuyu: Paũlũ na Sila marĩ Thesalonike * 16:27 Mũndũ muohe angĩorire njeera, mũthigari nĩwe woragagwo, nĩkĩo mũthigari ũyũ endaga kwĩyũraga o kabere.
english: 1 Now when they had passed through Amphipolis and Apollonia, they came to Thessalonica, where there was a Jewish synagogue.

kikuyu: Arĩhaanaga ta mbura ĩkiurĩra mũgũnda mũtũgũte, ahaane ta rũthuthuũ ũrĩa rũihũgagia thĩ.
english: He will come down like rain on the mown grass, as showers that water the earth.

kikuyu: Ta mwĩcũraniei ũrĩa itoka ikũraga. Itirutaga wĩra kana ikogotha ndigi. No ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ, o na Solomoni arĩ na riiri wake wothe, ndaagemete ta kĩmwe gĩacio.
english: He will say, ‘I tell you, I don’t know where you come from. Depart from me, all you workers of iniquity.’  literally, lepton. A lepton is a very small brass Jewish coin worth half a Roman quadrans each, which is worth a quarter of the copper assarion. Lepta are worth less than 1% of an agricultural worker’s daily wages.  literally, three sata.

kikuyu: nyamũ cia gĩthaka, na ngʼombe ciothe, na ciũmbe iria nini, o na nyoni iria ciũmbũkaga,
english: wild animals and all livestock, small creatures and flying birds,

kikuyu: Harĩ andũ arĩa maatigaire kũu itũũra-inĩ inene, akĩnyiita mũnene ũrĩa warũgamĩrĩire andũ a mbaara, na akĩnyiita andũ mũgwanja arĩa maataaraga mũthamaki. Ningĩ akĩnyiita karani ũrĩa mũnene, ũrĩa wandĩkithagia andũ a gũthiĩ ita a bũrũri ũcio, na andũ ake mĩrongo ĩtandatũ arĩa maakorirwo itũũra-inĩ rĩu inene.
english: and out of the city he took an officer who was set over the men of war; and seven men of those who saw the king’s face, who were found in the city; and the scribe of the captain of the army, who mustered the people of the land; and sixty men of the people of the land, who were found in the middle of the city.

kikuyu: Atongoria a nyũmba cia njiaro cia Lawi nginya hĩndĩ ya Johanani mũrũ wa Eliashibu o naonĩmandĩkĩtwoibuku-inĩrĩamatukũmacio.
english: The sons of Levi, heads of fathers’ households, were written in the book of the chronicles, even until the days of Johanan the son of Eliashib.

kikuyu: Gũtirĩ mũndũ ũrĩ ona Ngai; no tũngĩendana mũndũ na ũrĩa ũngĩ-rĩ, Ngai nĩatũũraga thĩinĩ witũ, naguo wendo wake ũgakinyanĩra thĩinĩ witũ.
english: No one has seen God at any time. If we love one another, God remains in us, and his love has been perfected in us.

kikuyu: Ngʼombe iria mũgwanja njega nĩ mĩaka mũgwanja, na magira marĩa mũgwanja mega ma ngano nĩ mĩaka mũgwanja; kĩroto kĩu no kĩmwe.
english: The seven good cattle are seven years; and the seven good heads of grain are seven years. The dream is one.

kikuyu: Ũmũthĩ ũyũ nĩũranyingata kuuma mũgũnda ũyũ na ndũũre hithĩtwo ũthiũ waku; ngũtuĩka mũndũ ũtakindagĩria, wa kũũrũũraga gũkũ thĩ, na ũrĩa wothe ũkaanyona nĩakanjũraga.” No Jehova akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Aca, ti ũguo; mũndũ o na ũrĩkũ angĩkooraga Kaini, nĩakarĩhio maita mũgwanja.” Jehova agĩcooka agĩĩkĩra Kaini rũũri nĩgeetha ũrĩa wothe ũngĩmuona ndakamũũrage! Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Kaini akĩehera akiuma harĩ Jehova, na agĩtũũra bũrũri-inĩ wa Nodu, mwena wa irathĩro rĩa Edeni.
english: Behold, you have driven me out today from the surface of the ground. I will be hidden from your face, and I will be a fugitive and a wanderer in the earth. Whoever finds me will kill me.”

kikuyu: Jehova nĩamomoraga nyũmba ya mũndũ ũrĩa mwĩtĩĩi, no nĩamenyagĩrĩra mĩhaka ya mũtumia wa ndigwa.
english: Yahweh will uproot the house of the proud, but he will keep the widow’s borders intact.

kikuyu: Kũrigania nake, Ezeri mũrũ wa Jeshua, mwathi wa Mizipa, nĩacookereirie gĩcunjĩ kĩngĩ kuuma harĩa haangʼetheire mwambato nginya harĩa haigagwo indo cia mbaara, o nginyagia koine- inĩ.
english: Next to him, Ezer the son of Jeshua, the ruler of Mizpah, repaired another portion across from the ascent to the armory at the turning of the wall.

kikuyu: “Nao maare gĩtambaya kĩa rangi wa bururu igũrũ rĩa metha ya mĩgate ĩrĩa ĩigagwo mbere ya Jehova, na mamĩigĩrĩre thaani, na thaani, nene na mbakũri, na ndigithũ cia magongona ma kũnyuuo; na mĩgate ĩrĩa ĩigagwo hau hĩndĩ ciothe ĩikare o hau igũrũ.
english: “On the table of show bread they shall spread a blue cloth, and put on it the dishes, the spoons, the bowls, and the cups with which to pour out; and the continual bread shall be on it.

kikuyu: Ũũ nĩguo Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe ekuuga: “Atĩrĩrĩ! Mwanangĩko nĩũrahunja kuuma rũrĩrĩ rũmwe nginya rũrĩa rũngĩ; kĩhuhũkanio nĩkĩrahuhũkana kuuma ituri-inĩ cia thĩ.”
english: Yahweh of Armies says, “Behold, evil will go out from nation to nation, and a great storm will be raised up from the uttermost parts of the earth.”

kikuyu: wa ingĩ igĩrĩ. No ndungata ĩrĩa yaheetwo taranda ĩmwe ĩgĩthiĩ, ĩkĩenja irima, ĩkĩhitha mbeeca cia mwathi wayo.
english: He said, “Go into the city to a certain person, and tell him, ‘The Teacher says, “My time is at hand. I will keep the Passover at your house with my disciples.” ’ ”

kikuyu: Aatiga kũhooya agĩcooka harĩ arutwo, akĩmakora makomete, moorĩtwo nĩ hinya nĩ kĩeha.
english: When he rose up from his prayer, he came to the disciples and found them sleeping because of grief,

kikuyu: Thuutha wa matukũ macio, Ayubu agĩtumũra kanua, akĩruma mũthenya ũrĩa aaciarirwo.
english: There was a man in the land of Uz, whose name was Job. That man was blameless and upright, and one who feared God,† and turned away from evil.

kikuyu: “Mwĩhĩtwa o wothe kana ũndũ ũrĩa mũtumia wa ndigwa kana mũtumia ũrĩa ũtiganĩte na mũthuuriwe angĩkorwo ehĩtĩte nĩatũũre aũrũmĩtie.
english: “ ‘On the Sabbath day, you shall offer two male lambs a year old without defect, and two tenths of an ephah† of fine flour for a meal offering mixed with oil, and its drink offering:

kikuyu: O na gũtuĩka rĩrĩa aarĩ muoyo eyoonaga arĩ mũrathime, nĩgũkorwo andũ nĩmagũkumagia rĩrĩa wagaacĩra,
english: When you saw a thief, you consented with him, and have participated with adulterers.

kikuyu: Jeremia ũcio mũnabii, makĩmwĩra atĩrĩ: “Twagũthaitha ĩtĩkĩra kũigua gũthaithana gwitũ, ũhooe Jehova Ngai waku nĩ ũndũ wa matigari maya mothe. Nĩgũkorwo o ta ũrĩa ũroona, o na gũtuĩka rĩmwe twarĩ aingĩ, rĩu tũtigaire o andũ anini.
english: and said to Jeremiah the prophet, “Please let our supplication be presented before you, and pray for us to Yahweh your God, even for all this remnant, for we are left but a few of many, as your eyes see us,

kikuyu: “Mũrũ wa mũndũ, erekeria ũthiũ waku Kĩrĩma gĩa Seiru: kĩrathĩre ũhoro wa gũgĩũkĩrĩra,
english: “Son of man, set your face against Mount Seir, and prophesy against it,

kikuyu: Jehova ekuuga atĩrĩ: “Atĩrĩrĩ! Nderi nĩĩracuuhũka, ĩtambũrũkĩtie mathagu mayo igũrũ rĩa Moabi.
english: For Yahweh says: “Behold, he will fly as an eagle, and will spread out his wings against Moab.

kikuyu: Makĩmũkuua makĩmũtwara bũrũri wa Kaanani, na makĩmũthika ngurunga-inĩ ĩrĩa yarĩ gĩthaka-inĩ kĩa Makipela, hakuhĩ na Mamure, ĩrĩa Iburahĩmu aagũranĩirie na gĩthaka kĩu kĩrĩ handũ ha gũthikanwo kuuma kũrĩ Efironi ũrĩa Mũhiti.
english: for his sons carried him into the land of Canaan, and buried him in the cave of the field of Machpelah, which Abraham bought with the field, as a possession for a burial site, from Ephron the Hittite, near Mamre.

kikuyu: O na ningĩ no harĩ Maandĩko mangĩ moigĩte atĩrĩ, “Makeerorera mũndũ ũrĩa maathecire.” Gũthikwo kwa Jesũ
english: Again another Scripture says, “They will look on him whom they pierced.”*

kikuyu: Namo maũndũ marĩa mwerutire, kana mũkĩamũkĩra kana mũkĩigua kuuma kũrĩ niĩ, o na kana mũkĩona thĩinĩ wakwa, mekagei arĩ mo. Nake Ngai wa thayũ nĩarĩkoragwo hamwe na inyuĩ. Ngaatho nĩ ũndũ wa Iheo Ciao
english: and be found in him, not having a righteousness of my own, that which is of the law, but that which is through faith in Christ, the righteousness which is from God by faith,

kikuyu: Niĩ nĩkũgega ndĩragega nĩ ũndũ wa ũrĩa mũrahiũha gũtigana na ũrĩa wamwĩtire na ũndũ wa wega wa Kristũ, mũkagarũrũkĩra ũhoro mwega wa mũthemba ũngĩ,
english: I marvel that you are so quickly deserting him who called you in the grace of Christ to a different “good news”,

kikuyu: Angĩ matariĩ ta mbeũ iria ciagũire tĩĩri- inĩ ũrĩa mũnoru. Acio nĩ arĩa maiguaga ũhoro, makawĩtĩkĩra, na magaciara maciaro, amwe maita mĩrongo ĩtatũ, na angĩ maita mĩrongo ĩtandatũ, o na angĩ maita igana ma kĩrĩa kĩahaandirwo.” Tawa Ũigĩrĩirwo Handũ Igũrũ
english: He went his way, and began to proclaim in Decapolis how Jesus had done great things for him, and everyone marveled.

kikuyu: Mũndũ o wothe ũngĩhutia kĩndũ o gĩothe aikarĩire no nginya athambie nguo ciake na ethambe mwĩrĩ na maaĩ, nake atiinde arĩ na thaahu o nginya hwaĩ-inĩ.
english: Whoever touches anything that she sits on shall wash his clothes, and bathe himself in water, and be unclean until the evening.

kikuyu: Acio marĩĩtagwo Andũ arĩa Aamũre, o arĩa Jehova Akũũrĩte; nawe ũrĩĩtagwo Wa Kwendeka, Itũũra-Inene-rĩrĩa-Rĩtagacooka-Gũthaamwo.
english: Who caused his glorious arm to be at Moses’ right hand? Who divided the waters before them, to make himself an everlasting name?

kikuyu: Atĩrĩrĩ, ringai mũthiĩ mũrĩmo wa Tarishishi, ta girĩkai inyuĩ mũtũũraga kũu gĩcigĩrĩra-inĩ.
english: Pass over to Tarshish! Wail, you inhabitants of the coast!

kikuyu: Mwĩrĩ wakwa ũiyũrĩtwo nĩ igunyũ na ngũcĩ, nakĩo gĩkonde gĩakwa gĩatũkangĩte na gĩgatogota.
english: My flesh is clothed with worms and clods of dust. My skin closes up, and breaks out afresh.

kikuyu: Nĩakarehe o kĩndũ kĩmwe kĩmwe kĩrĩ iruta, kĩheo kĩheanĩtwo harĩ Jehova; nĩ kĩa mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai ũrĩa ũminjaminjaga thakame ĩyo ya iruta rĩu rĩa ũiguano.
english: “ ‘This is the law of the meal offering: the sons of Aaron shall offer it before Yahweh, before the altar.

kikuyu: O mũndũ nĩarorage wĩra wake mwene. Hĩndĩ ĩyo no ahote kwĩraha, atekwĩiganania na mũndũ ũngĩ, 5nĩgũkorwo o mũndũ no nginya akuue mũrigo wake.
english: But let each man examine his own work, and then he will have reason to boast in himself, and not in someone else.

kikuyu: Angĩmũtuĩria inyuĩ-rĩ, ũndũ ũcio no ũtuĩke mwega? No mũmũheenie ta ũrĩa mũngĩheenia mũndũ?
english: Is it good that he should search you out? Or as one deceives a man, will you deceive him?

kikuyu: Nĩngahehenja Elamu maitho-inĩ ma thũ ciao, marĩ o mbere ya andũ arĩa macaragia kũmaruta muoyo; nĩngamarehithĩria mwanangĩko, o na marakara makwa mahiũ,” ũguo nĩguo Jehova ekuuga. “Ngaamatengʼeria na rũhiũ rwa njora nginya ndĩmaniine biũ.
english: I will cause Elam to be dismayed before their enemies, and before those who seek their life. I will bring evil on them, even my fierce anger,’ says Yahweh; ‘and I will send the sword after them, until I have consumed them.

kikuyu: Ica‑ikuhĩ‑rĩ, andũ akwa maarahũkĩte magatuĩka ta thũ. Mũtunyaga andũ arĩa mahooreire nguo ciao cia goro, mũgatunya ehĩtũkĩri arĩa marahĩtũka matekũmaka, o acio matakoragwo na meciiria ma mbaara na inyuĩ.
english: But as for me, I am full of power by Yahweh’s Spirit, and of judgment, and of might, to declare to Jacob his disobedience,  The Hebrew word rendered “God” is “אֱֹלהִ ֑ים” (Elohim).

kikuyu: Ti-itherũ ũtonga ũrĩa wothe Ngai atunyĩte baba nĩ witũ na ciana ciitũ. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio ĩka ũrĩa wothe Ngai akwĩrĩte wĩke.” Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Jakubu akĩĩhaarĩria, akĩigĩrĩra ciana ciake na atumia ake igũrũ rĩa ngamĩĩra,
english: He delivered them into the hands of his servants, every herd by itself, and said to his servants, “Pass over before me, and put a space between herd and herd.”

kikuyu: Mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai acooke oe iruta rĩu rĩa mũtu nĩ ũndũ wa ũiru kuuma moko-inĩ ma mũtumia ũcio, arĩthũngũthie mbere ya Jehova, na acooke arĩrehe kĩgongona-inĩ. Ningĩ mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai ũcio arũme ngundi ĩmwe ya mũtu ũcio ũrutĩtwo ũrĩ wa kĩririkano na aũcinĩre igũrũ rĩa kĩgongona; thuutha ũcio atũme mũtumia ũcio anyue maaĩ macio.
english: The priest shall take the meal offering of jealousy out of the woman’s hand, and shall wave the meal offering before Yahweh, and bring it to the altar.

kikuyu: O ta ũrĩa ikundi cia atunyani ciĩhithagĩra mũndũ, no taguo ikundi cia athĩnjĩri-Ngai ciĩkaga; moraganagĩra njĩra-inĩ ya gũthiĩ Shekemu, makagera ngero cia thoni.
english: Strangers have devoured his strength, and he doesn’t realize it. Indeed, gray hairs are here and there on him, and he doesn’t realize it.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo Jehova ndateanagĩria andũ nginya tene.
english: For the Lord will not cast off forever.

kikuyu: MARŨA MA JAKUBU Nĩ niĩ Jakubu, ndungata ya Ngai na ya Mwathani Jesũ Kristũ, Ndĩramwandĩkĩra inyuĩ a mĩhĩrĩga ĩrĩa ikũmi na ĩĩrĩ mũhurunjĩtwo thĩ yothe: Nĩndamũgeithia. Mathĩĩna na Magerio
english: James, a servant of God and of the Lord Jesus Christ,† to the twelve tribes which are in the Dispersion: Greetings.

kikuyu: Moima Diboni-Gadi, magĩthiĩ makĩamba hema ciao Alimoni-Dibilathaimu.
english: They traveled from Dibon Gad, and encamped in Almon Diblathaim.

kikuyu: naguo nĩ atĩ Ngai eetĩkĩre kũũmemenda, arekererie guoko gwake kũũniine!
english: who does great things that can’t be fathomed, marvelous things without number;

kikuyu: Gũtiagĩrĩire mũndũ mũkĩĩgu gũikaraga egangarĩte; gũkĩrĩ ũũru makĩria ngombo gwatha anene!
english: Delicate living is not appropriate for a fool, much less for a servant to have rule over princes.

kikuyu: Ingata mũnyũrũrania, nayo ngũĩ nĩĩgũkweherera; inegene na irumi o nacio nĩcigũthira.
english: The soul of the wicked desires evil; his neighbor finds no mercy in his eyes.

kikuyu: Ndũkaahithe ũthiũ waku, tiga kũingata ndungata yaku na marakara; Wee nĩwe ũtũũrĩte ũndeithagia. Ndũkandege kana ũndiganĩrie, Wee Ngai Mũhonokia wakwa.
english: Yahweh’s voice makes the deer calve, and strips the forests bare. In his temple everything says, “Glory!”

kikuyu: Kuuma harĩ mũndũ ũmwe, * 17:18 Arutani a Epikurio maarutanaga atĩ ũndũ ũrĩa mwega mũno makĩria warĩ gwĩkenia. Nao arutani a Sitoiko maarutanaga atĩ andũ matũũranagie wega na indo ciothe cia thĩ, matekũmakio nĩ ũhoro wa ruo kana mwago, o na kana wa maũndũ mega na moru ngoro-inĩ. † 17:19 Areopago nĩ kuuga “Kĩrĩma kĩa Aresi”; Aresi yarĩ ngai ya Ayunani ya marurumĩ na mbaara.
english: He made from one blood every nation of men to dwell on all the surface of the earth, having determined appointed seasons and the boundaries of their dwellings,

kikuyu: o ũndũ ũmwe na Nebo na Baali-Meoni (marĩĩtwa macio nĩmagarũrirwo) na Sibima. Nao makĩhe matũũra macio manene maakire marĩĩtwa mangĩ.
english: Nebo, and Baal Meon, (their names being changed), and Sibmah. They gave other names to the cities which they built.

kikuyu: ; na 36:11, Akenizi o nao maarĩ Akaanani, no maarĩ njiaro cia Esaũ.
english: the land of the Kenites, the Kenizzites, the Kadmonites,

kikuyu: Agĩtoonya thĩinĩ akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Kĩagago gĩkĩ gĩothe na kĩrĩro nĩ cia kĩ? Mwana ti mũkuũ, no nĩ gũkoma akomete.” Nao makĩmũthekerera. Na thuutha wa kũmoimia nja othe, akĩoya ithe na nyina wa mwana na arutwo arĩa aarĩ nao magĩtoonya harĩa mwana ũcio aarĩ.
english: When he had entered in, he said to them, “Why do you make an uproar and weep? The child is not dead, but is asleep.”

kikuyu: ĩ hangĩaga andũ atano a acio mĩrongo ĩtano athingu? Nĩ ũngĩniina itũũra rĩu inene rĩothe nĩ ũndũ wa kwaga acio atano?” Jehova akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Ingĩkora andũ mĩrongo ĩna na atano kuo, ndikũrĩniina.”
english: What if there will lack five of the fifty righteous? Will you destroy all the city for lack of five?” He said, “I will not destroy it if I find forty-five there.”

kikuyu: Maareheire Jehova iheo ikuuĩtwo nĩ ngaari ithathatũ cia ngʼombe irĩ humbĩre, hamwe na ndegwa ikũmi na igĩrĩ, ndegwa ĩmwe ĩkarutwo nĩ o mũtongoria, na ngaari ĩmwe ĩkarutwo nĩ atongoria eerĩ. Indo icio nĩcio maarehire mbere ya Hema-ĩrĩa-Nyamũre. Nake Jehova akĩĩra Musa atĩrĩ:
english: and they brought their offering before Yahweh, six covered wagons and twelve oxen; a wagon for every two of the princes, and for each one an ox. They presented them before the tabernacle.

kikuyu: Kĩu nĩkĩo gĩtũmi kĩrĩa kĩgirĩtie andũ a Isiraeli meetirie thũ ciao; na nĩkĩo marorĩra thũ ciao tondũ nĩmatuĩkĩte a kũniinwo. Na niĩ ndigũcooka gũkorwo hamwe na inyuĩ o tiga mũniinire kĩndũ kĩu kĩamũrĩtwo gĩa kũniinwo kĩehere thĩinĩ wanyu.
english: He used to tell you of the zealous Phinehas, and informed you of Ananias, Azarias, and Misael in the fire.

kikuyu: “Niĩ ngaakũhurunja o ta ũrĩa mũũngũ ũmbũragwo nĩ rũhuho rwa werũ-inĩ.
english: “Therefore I will scatter them as the stubble that passes away by the wind of the wilderness.

kikuyu: agĩathana Jeremia ũcio mũnabii ahũũrwo na oohererwo magũrũ ndungu-inĩ hau Kĩhingo-inĩ kĩa Rũgongo kĩa Benjamini, o kũu hekarũ-inĩ ya Jehova.
english: Then Pashhur struck Jeremiah the prophet and put him in the stocks that were in the upper gate of Benjamin, which was in Yahweh’s house.

kikuyu: Jesũ Gũtoonya Jerusalemu na Ũhootani Na rĩrĩa maakuhĩrĩirie Jerusalemu, maakinya itũũra rĩa Bethifage kũu Kĩrĩma-inĩ kĩa Mĩtamaiyũ- rĩ, Jesũ agĩtũma arutwo ake eerĩ,
english: 1 When they came near to Jerusalem and came to Bethsphage,† to the Mount of Olives, then Jesus sent two disciples,

kikuyu: “Juda, ariũ a thoguo nĩmagagũkumia; nakuo guoko gwaku nĩgũgatooria thũ ciaku; ariũ a thoguo nĩmagakũinamĩrĩra.
english: “Judah, your brothers will praise you. Your hand will be on the neck of your enemies. Your father’s sons will bow down before you.

kikuyu: Nakĩo gĩkĩ nĩkĩo kĩrĩkanĩro kĩrĩa ngaarĩkanĩra nao, rĩrĩa ngaamehereria mehia mao.”
english: This is my covenant with them, when I will take away their sins.”*

kikuyu: Matikaahũũta kana manyoote, kana macinwo nĩ ũrugarĩ wa werũ-inĩ o na kana mahĩe nĩ riũa. Nĩgũkorwo ũrĩa ũmaiguagĩra tha nĩwe ũkaamoonia njĩra na amatongoragie amatware ithima-inĩ cia maaĩ.
english: For godly sorrow produces repentance leading to salvation, which brings no regret. But the sorrow of the world produces death.

kikuyu: Jesũ agĩĩta arutwo ake harĩ we, akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ na ma, mũtumia ũyũ wa ndigwa mũthĩĩni nĩekĩra mbeeca nyingĩ kĩgĩĩna-inĩ gũkĩra andũ arĩa angĩ othe.
english: He called his disciples to himself and said to them, “Most certainly I tell you, this poor widow gave more than all those who are giving into the treasury,

kikuyu: “Mũrũ wa mũndũ, gwatania ndaĩ na warĩrie andũ a nyũmba ya Isiraeli na ngerekano.
english: “Son of man, tell a riddle, and speak a parable to the house of Israel;

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa Jesũ aamuonire akĩrĩra, na Ayahudi arĩa mokĩte hamwe nake o nao makĩrĩra-rĩ, agĩcaaya mũno roho-inĩ thĩinĩ na agĩtangĩka ngoro.
english: When Jesus therefore saw her weeping, and the Jews weeping who came with her, he groaned in the spirit and was troubled,

kikuyu: Rutagĩrai andũ othe kĩrĩa mwagĩrĩirwo nĩ kũmarutĩra: Angĩkorwo mũtirutĩte magooti, rutai magooti; na angĩkorwo nĩ kũruta mbeeca cia kũrutithia wĩra wa bũrũri, rutai; nao andũ arĩa a kũheo gĩtĩĩo, maheagei; na arĩa a gwĩkĩrĩrwo, mekagĩrĩrei. Endanagai tondũ Mũthenya Nĩũkuhĩrĩirie
english: Therefore give everyone what you owe: if you owe taxes, pay taxes; if customs, then customs; if respect, then respect; if honor, then honor.

kikuyu: Njũkĩte thĩinĩ wa rĩĩtwa rĩa Baba na mũtinjĩtĩkagĩra; no mũndũ ũngĩ angĩũka na rĩĩtwa rĩake mwene, nĩmũkũmwĩtĩkĩra.
english: I have come in my Father’s name, and you don’t receive me. If another comes in his own name, you will receive him.

kikuyu: Ũũ nĩguo mũngĩhota gũkũũrana Roho wa Ngai: Roho o wothe ũrĩa uumbũraga atĩ Jesũ Kristũ nĩokĩte arĩ na mwĩrĩ, ũcio uumĩte kũrĩ Ngai,
english: By this you know the Spirit of God: every spirit who confesses that Jesus Christ has come in the flesh is of God,

kikuyu: “Nĩ ũndũ ũcio uga atĩrĩ: ‘Mwathani Jehova ekuuga ũũ: O na gũtuĩka nĩndamatwarithirie kũraya gatagatĩ-inĩ ka ndũrĩrĩ, na ngĩmahurunjĩra mabũrũri-inĩ, no niĩ nduĩkĩte handũ-hao-harĩa-haamũre, mabũrũri-inĩ kũu mathiĩte, gwa kahinda kanini.’
english: When the cherubim went, the wheels went beside them; and when the cherubim lifted up their wings to mount up from the earth, the wheels also didn’t turn from beside them.

kikuyu: Nĩaharũrũkĩtie aathani kuuma itĩ-inĩ ciao cia ũnene, no agatũũgĩria arĩa menyiihagia.
english: He has put down princes from their thrones, and has exalted the lowly.

kikuyu: akĩũrehe na kiuga, akĩnengera mũirĩtu ũcio, nake akĩũnengera nyina. Nao arutwo a Johana maigua ũhoro ũcio, magĩũka, makĩoya mwĩrĩ wake, magĩthiĩ, makĩũthika. Jesũ kũhe Andũ Ngiri Ithano Irio
english: and brought his head on a platter, and gave it to the young lady; and the young lady gave it to her mother.

kikuyu: Mũthenya ũrĩa maaitĩrĩirio maguta, Jehova nĩathanire atĩ andũ a Isiraeli mamaheage nyama icio hĩndĩ ciothe ituĩke rwĩga rwao nginya njiarwa iria igooka.
english: which Yahweh commanded to be given them of the children of Israel, in the day that he anointed them. It is their portion forever throughout their generations.

kikuyu: Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe ekuuga atĩrĩ: “Temai mĩtĩ mũmĩige ihũba nene ingʼetheire Jerusalemu. Itũũra rĩrĩ inene no nginya rĩherithio; nĩ ũndũ rĩiyũrĩte ũhoro wa kũhinyanĩrĩria.
english: For Yahweh of Armies said, “Cut down trees, and cast up a mound against Jerusalem. This is the city to be visited. She is filled with oppression within herself.

kikuyu: Aakuhĩrĩria Bethifage na Bethania, o kũu Kĩrĩma-inĩ kĩa Mĩtamaiyũ, agĩtũma arutwo ake eerĩ, akĩmeera atĩrĩ, 30“Thiĩi itũũra rĩrĩa rĩrĩ mbere yanyu, na mũgĩtoonya, nĩmũkuona njaũ ya ndigiri yohetwo hau, nayo ndĩrĩ yakuua mũndũ; mĩohorei mũmĩrehe.
english: When he came near to Bethsphage‡ and Bethany, at the mountain that is called Olivet, he sent two of his disciples,

kikuyu: Mũthia-inĩ akĩmatũmĩra mũrũwe, akiuga atĩrĩ, ‘Nĩmagatĩĩa mũrũ wakwa.’
english: But afterward he sent to them his son, saying, ‘They will respect my son.’

kikuyu: Mĩrũũthi nĩĩraramĩte; nĩĩmũraramĩire. Nĩyanangĩte bũrũri wake; matũũra make macinĩtwo na mwaki, na magatigwo matarĩ mũndũ.
english: The young lions have roared at him and raised their voices. They have made his land waste. His cities are burned up, without inhabitant.

kikuyu: andũ othe nĩmagaconorithio nĩ ũndũ wa andũ matangĩmaguna na ũndũ o na ũrĩkũ, arĩa matangĩmateithia kana mamagune, tiga o kũmaconorithia na kũmarehera thoni.”
english: Who then is Apollos, and who is Paul, but servants through whom you believed, and each as the Lord gave to him?

kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo matiigana gwĩtĩkia Ngai, kana kwĩhoka ũhonokania wake. No rĩrĩ, we nĩathire matu ma igũrũ na akĩhingũra mĩrango ya igũrũ;
english: because they didn’t believe in God, and didn’t trust in his salvation.

kikuyu: Mĩguĩ ndĩngĩtũma yũre; mahiga ma kĩgũtha no ta mũũngũ harĩ yo.
english: The arrow can’t make him flee. Sling stones are like chaff to him.

kikuyu: Ũkirĩrĩria no ũguucĩrĩrie ũrĩa waathanaga etĩkĩre ũndũ, naruo rũrĩmĩ ruororo no ruune ihĩndĩ.
english: By patience a ruler is persuaded. A soft tongue breaks the bone.

kikuyu: O nacio ici ciarĩ mbarĩ cia Alawii: mbarĩ ya Alibini, na mbarĩ ya Ahebironi, na mbarĩ ya Amahali, na mbarĩ ya Amushi, na mbarĩ ya Akora.
english: These are the families of Levi: the family of the Libnites, the family of the Hebronites, the family of the Mahlites, the family of the Mushites, and the family of the Korahites. Kohath became the father of Amram.

kikuyu: narĩo itũũra rĩu rĩgaakorwo na ithimo ici: mwena wa gathigathini nĩ mĩkono 4,500,§ na mwena wa gũthini nĩ mĩkono 4,500, na mwena wa irathĩro nĩ mĩkono 4,500, na mwena wa ithũĩro nĩ 4,500.
english: Hamath, Berothah, Sibraim (which is between the border of Damascus and the border of Hamath), to Hazer Hatticon, which is by the border of Hauran.

kikuyu: Mangʼũrĩ ma mũthamaki nĩ ta mũtũmwo wa gĩkuũ, no mũndũ mũũgĩ nĩakamahooreria.
english: The beginning of strife is like breaching a dam, therefore stop contention before quarreling breaks out.

kikuyu: o na Gomeri na thigari ciakuo ciothe, o na andũ a Bethi Togarima moimĩte kũraya mwena wa gathigathini marĩ na mbũtũ cia ita ciakuo ciothe, o ndũrĩrĩ icio nyingĩ irĩ hamwe nawe.
english: Gomer, and all his hordes; the house of Togarmah in the uttermost parts of the north, and all his hordes—even many peoples with you.

kikuyu: “Maitho make maikaraga macũthĩrĩirie mĩthiĩre ya andũ; nĩonaga mĩthiĩre yao yothe.
english: “For his eyes are on the ways of a man. He sees all his goings.

kikuyu: “ ‘Ndũkanakome na mũtumia wa mũrũ wa nyũkwa; ũguo nĩgũconorithia mũrũ wa nyũkwa.
english: “ ‘You shall not uncover the nakedness of your brother’s wife. It is your brother’s nakedness.

kikuyu: Mũũthaithanĩrĩri nĩ mũrata wakwa rĩrĩa maitho makwa maraita maithori harĩ Ngai;
english: My friends scoff at me. My eyes pour out tears to God,

kikuyu: Ici nĩcio njiaro cia Efiraimu kũringana na mbarĩ ciao: kuuma kũrĩ Shuthela, nĩ mbarĩ ya Ashuthela; kuuma kũrĩ Bekeri, nĩ mbarĩ ya Abekeri; kuuma kũrĩ Tahani nĩ mbarĩ ya Atahani.
english: These are the sons of Ephraim after their families: of Shuthelah, the family of the Shuthelahites; of Becher, the family of the Becherites; of Tahan, the family of the Tahanites.

kikuyu: Matukũ-inĩ macio, o ihinda-inĩ rĩu-rĩ, ihĩtia rĩa Isiraeli nĩrĩgathuthuurio, no rĩtikoneka, o na mehia ma Juda mathuthuurio, no matikooneka, nĩgũkorwo matigari marĩa ngaahonokia nĩngamarekera,” ũguo nĩguo Jehova ekuuga.
english: In those days, and in that time,” says Yahweh, “the iniquity of Israel will be sought for, and there will be none, also the sins of Judah, and they won’t be found; for I will pardon them whom I leave as a remnant.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa ciũmbe icio irĩ muoyo ciathiĩ, magũrũ macio maarĩ mwena-inĩ wacio magathiĩ; na rĩrĩa ciũmbe icio irĩ muoyo ciambata na igũrũ ciumĩte thĩ, magũrũ macio o namo makaambata.
english: When the living creatures went, the wheels went beside them. When the living creatures were lifted up from the earth, the wheels were lifted up.

kikuyu: Ũngĩona ũũkĩ-rĩ, rĩa o wa gũkũigana, ũngĩrĩa mũingĩ mũno nĩũgũtahĩka.
english: Have you found honey? Eat as much as is sufficient for you, lest you eat too much, and vomit it.

kikuyu: Ndaacũthĩrĩria irĩma, ngĩona atĩ nĩgũthingitha ciathingithaga; natuo tũrĩma tuothe nĩ kwereera twereeraga.
english: I saw the mountains, and behold, they trembled, and all the hills moved back and forth.

kikuyu: Inyuĩ mũhonokete kũũragwo na rũhiũ rwa njora-rĩ, mwĩtharei na mũtikagonderere! Ririkanagai Jehova mũrĩ bũrũri wa kũraya, na mwĩciiragie ũhoro wa Jerusalemu.”
english: You who have escaped the sword, go! Don’t stand still! Remember Yahweh from afar, and let Jerusalem come into your mind.”

kikuyu: Andũ a Isiraeli mangʼathĩtie o ta moori ngʼathia. Kũngĩhoteka atĩa Jehova amarĩithie ta tũgondu tũrĩ nyeki-inĩ nduru?
english: For Israel has behaved extremely stubbornly, like a stubborn heifer. Then how will Yahweh feed them like a lamb in a meadow?

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo magĩĩtĩkia ciĩranĩro ciake, na makĩmũinĩra, makĩmũgooca. No makĩriganĩrwo o narua nĩ ũrĩa ekĩte, na matiigana gweterera ũtaaro wake.
english: Therefore he brought down their heart with labor. They fell down, and there was no one to help.

kikuyu: Athĩnjĩri-Ngai, arĩa matungataga mbere ya Jehova, nĩmarĩrĩre gatagatĩ ga gĩthaku kĩa Hekarũ na kĩgongona. Nĩmoige atĩrĩ, “Kĩhonokie andũ aku, Wee Jehova. Ndũkareke igai rĩaku rĩtuĩke kĩndũ gĩa kũnyũrũrio, kana tuunagwo thimo gatagatĩ-inĩ ka ndũrĩrĩ. Nĩ kĩĩ kĩngĩtũma ndũrĩrĩ ciũranagie atĩrĩ, ‘Kaĩ Ngai wao akĩrĩ ha?’ ” Icookia rĩa Jehova Ningĩ Jehova nĩakaigua ũiru nĩ ũndũ wa bũrũri wake, na aiguĩre andũ ake tha.
english: Let the priests, the ministers of Yahweh, weep between the porch and the altar, and let them say, “Spare your people, Yahweh, and don’t give your heritage to reproach, that the nations should rule over them. Why should they say among the peoples, ‘Where is their God?’ ”

kikuyu: Mbeeca itarĩ cia wĩhokeku ithiiaga igĩthiraga, no ũrĩa ũcookanagĩrĩria mbeeca tũnini o tũnini nĩatũmaga ciongerereke.
english: The house of the wicked will be overthrown, but the tent of the upright will flourish.

kikuyu: Atĩrĩrĩ, Wee Jehova, nĩ kĩĩ gĩtũmaga ũtũrekererie tuume njĩra-inĩ ciaku, na ũkoomia ngoro ciitũ nĩguo twage gũgũtĩĩa? Garũrũka nĩ ũndũ wa ndungata ciaku, o mĩhĩrĩga ĩyo ĩtuĩkĩte igai rĩaku.
english: O Yahweh, why do you make us wander from your ways, and harden our heart from your fear? Return for your servants’ sake, the tribes of your inheritance.

kikuyu: “Niĩ nĩnjũũĩ wega ũrĩa mũreciiria, ngamenya mathugunda manyu maakũhĩtĩria namo.
english: “Behold, I know your thoughts, the plans with which you would wrong me.

kikuyu: Mũndũ o wothe nĩakoona atĩ niĩ Jehova nĩ niĩ ndĩwakĩtie; ndũkahoreka.’ ”
english: All flesh will see that I, Yahweh, have kindled it. It will not be quenched.” ’ ”

kikuyu: Andũ-a-nja eerĩ magaakorwo magĩthĩa hamwe; ũmwe nĩakoywo na ũrĩa ũngĩ atigwo.
english: Two women will be grinding at the mill: one will be taken and one will be left.

kikuyu: Mĩanya yakĩo, na gĩthaku gĩakĩo na magemio * 40:3 Rũthanju rũrũ rwa gũthima rwarĩ na ũraihu wa ta mita
english: Its windows, its arches, and its palm trees were the same as the measure of the gate which faces toward the east. They went up to it by seven steps. Its arches were before them.

kikuyu: Mũtigaite thakame. Mũikiei irima-inĩ rĩĩrĩ rĩ gũkũ werũ-inĩ, no mũtikamwĩke ũũru.” Rubeni oigaga ũguo nĩgeetha amũhonokie kuuma kũrĩ o, nĩguo amũcookie kũrĩ ithe. Na rĩrĩa Jusufu aakinyire harĩ ariũ a ithe, makĩmũruta kanjũ yake ĩrĩa eekĩrĩte, o ĩrĩa yarĩ ya goro na ngʼemie wega mũno.
english: Reuben said to them, “Shed no blood. Throw him into this pit that is in the wilderness, but lay no hand on him”—that he might deliver him out of their hand, to restore him to his father.

kikuyu: Thiĩi mũgakaĩre ngai icio mwĩthuurĩire. Nĩimũhonokagie hĩndĩ ĩrĩa mũranyamarĩka!” * 10:6 Ngai cia Aramu ciarĩ Hadadi (kana Baali), Motu, Anathi, na Rimoni; ngai ĩrĩa nene ya andũ a Moabi yarĩ Kemoshu, nayo ya andũ a Amoni yarĩ Moleku.
english: Go and cry to the gods which you have chosen. Let them save you in the time of your distress!”

kikuyu: Anga mũrenda kũruta mahĩtia ũrĩa njugĩte, mũgatua ciugo cia mũndũ ũũmĩirwo tha taarĩ rũhuho?
english: Do you intend to reprove words, since the speeches of one who is desperate are as wind?

kikuyu: Mũthenya ũyũ ũngĩ rũciinĩ tene-rĩ, Iburahĩmu agĩũkĩra na agĩcooka handũ harĩa aarũgamĩte mbere ya Jehova.
english: Abraham went up early in the morning to the place where he had stood before Yahweh.

kikuyu: Jesũ akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Atĩ ‘korwo ndahota’? Maũndũ mothe nĩmahotekaga kũrĩ mũndũ ũrĩa wĩtĩkĩtie.”
english: Jesus said to him, “If you can believe, all things are possible to him who believes.”

kikuyu: Tofeti gũtũũrĩĩte kũhaarĩirio; gũthondeketwo nĩ ũndũ wa mũthamaki. Irima rĩakuo rĩa mwaki nĩirikie na rĩkaaramio, narĩo rĩrĩ na mwaki mũingĩ na ngũ nyingĩ, nayo mĩhũmũ ya Jehova nĩyo ĩraakia mwaki ũcio, ĩhaana ta rũũĩ rwa ũbiriti.
english: For his burning place has long been ready. Yes, it is prepared for the king. He has made its pyre deep and large with fire and much wood. Yahweh’s breath, like a stream of sulfur, kindles it.

kikuyu: Andũ a Edomu no moige atĩrĩ, “O na gũtuĩka tũrĩ ahehenje-rĩ, no tũgaaka kũrĩa kwanangĩku rĩngĩ.” No Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe ekuuga atĩrĩ, “No maake rĩngĩ, no nĩngakũmomora. Mageetagwo Bũrũri Mwaganu, andũ matũũraga marakarĩirwo nĩ Jehova hĩndĩ ciothe.
english: You will know that I have sent this commandment to you, that my covenant may be with Levi,” says Yahweh of Armies.

kikuyu: Kaĩ mũndũ atakĩrĩ wa bata mũno gũkĩra ngʼondu! Nĩ ũndũ ũcio watho nĩwĩtĩkĩrĩtie gwĩka wega mũthenya wa Thabatũ.”
english: Of how much more value then is a man than a sheep! Therefore it is lawful to do good on the Sabbath day.”

kikuyu: Mũgoocei, inyuĩ igũrũ rĩa igũrũ, o na inyuĩ maaĩ ma kũu igũrũ wa matu.
english: For Yahweh takes pleasure in his people. He crowns the humble with salvation.

kikuyu: Airĩtu a Zelofehadi, nĩo Mahala, na Tiriza, na Hogila, na Milika, na Noa, maahikirio nĩ ariũ a mũrũ wa nyina na ithe wao.
english: for Mahlah, Tirzah, Hoglah, Milcah, and Noah, the daughters of Zelophehad, were married to their father’s brothers’ sons.

kikuyu: Hanameli mũrũ wa Shalumu, ũrĩa mamaguo, nĩegũũka kũrĩ we, akwĩre atĩrĩ, ‘Wĩgũrĩre mũgũnda wakwa ũrĩa ũrĩ Anathothu, nĩ ũndũ wee nĩwe mũndũ wa ndĩra ciitũ wa hakuhĩ makĩria, na nĩwe ũrĩ na kĩhooto gĩa kũũgũra.’
english: ‘Behold, Hanamel the son of Shallum your uncle will come to you, saying, “Buy my field that is in Anathoth; for the right of redemption is yours to buy it.” ’ ”

kikuyu: Tuonaga irio na ũndũ wa gũtwarĩrĩria mĩoyo iitũ ũgwati-inĩ, nĩ ũndũ wa rũhiũ rwa njora rũrĩa rũrĩ werũ-inĩ.
english: We get our bread at the peril of our lives, because of the sword in the wilderness.

kikuyu: Mũthamaki nĩakenetio mũno nĩ ũthaka waku; mũtĩĩe, nĩgũkorwo nĩwe mwathi waku.
english: Yahweh of Armies is with us. The God of Jacob is our refuge. Selah.

kikuyu: Akĩgarũra maaĩ mao magĩtuĩka thakame, agĩtũma thamaki ciao ikue.
english: He turned their waters into blood, and killed their fish.

kikuyu: Atĩrĩrĩ, Mwathani, Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe, nĩagaceeha honge na hinya mũnene. Mĩtĩ ĩrĩa mĩnene mũno nĩĩgatemwo, ĩgũithio thĩ, na ĩrĩa mĩraihu ĩnyiihio.
english: Behold, the Lord, Yahweh of Armies, will lop the boughs with terror. The tall will be cut down, and the lofty will be brought low.

kikuyu: Ti-itherũ arĩa magũtiganagĩria nĩmagathira; nĩũkanangaga arĩa othe matakwĩhokete. No ha ũhoro wakwa-rĩ, nĩ wega gũkuhĩrĩria Ngai. Nĩnduĩte Mwathani Jehova rĩũrĩro rĩakwa; nĩndĩrĩheanaga ũhoro wa ciĩko ciaku ciothe.
english: For, behold, those who are far from you shall perish. You have destroyed all those who are unfaithful to you.

kikuyu: kuuma mũhĩrĩga wa Dani, aarĩ Amieli mũrũ wa Gemali;
english: Of the tribe of Dan, Ammiel the son of Gemalli.

kikuyu: Mũgambo wa Mũrungu ũrurumaga na njĩra ya magegania; ekaga maũndũ manene tũtangĩhota kũmenya.
english: God thunders marvelously with his voice. He does great things, which we can’t comprehend.

kikuyu: Rĩu-rĩ, mabũrũri marĩa marĩ ndwere-inĩ cia iria nĩmakainaina mũthenya wa kũgũa gwaku; icigĩrĩra cia iria-inĩ nĩikamakio nĩ kũmomoka gwaku.’
english: Now the islands will tremble in the day of your fall. Yes, the islands that are in the sea will be dismayed at your departure.’

kikuyu: Akiuga atĩrĩ, “Mwathi wakwa, angĩkorwo nĩndetĩkĩrĩka maitho-inĩ maku, ndũkahĩtũke ndungata yaku.
english: and said, “My lord, if now I have found favor in your sight, please don’t go away from your servant.

kikuyu: Nĩwonaga mũndũ mũũgĩ na wĩra wake? Ũcio agatungata o na mbere ya athamaki; ndagatungata mbere ya andũ matooĩkaine.
english: Do you see a man skilled in his work? He will serve kings. He won’t serve obscure men.

kikuyu: Nĩagathĩnja gatũrũme ka iruta rĩa mahĩtia na acooke oe thakame ĩmwe yako amĩhake moni ya gũtũ kwa ũrĩo kwa mũndũ ũcio ũratherio, na amĩhake kĩara kĩrĩa kĩnene gĩa guoko gwake kwa ũrĩo na kĩara kĩrĩa kĩnene gĩa kũgũrũ gwake kwa ũrĩo.
english: then the priest shall examine it; and behold, if the hair in the bright spot has turned white, and its appearance is deeper than the skin, it is leprosy. It has broken out in the burning, and the priest shall pronounce him unclean. It is the plague of leprosy.

kikuyu: “Huhai karumbeta kũu Gibea, na mũhuhe coro kũu Rama. Muuge mbu ya gwĩtana mbaara kũu Bethi-Aveni; wee Benjamini, wĩhũũge.
english: They feed on the sin of my people, and set their heart on their iniquity.

kikuyu: Nũũ ũngĩhota kũmatua ehia? Gũtirĩ! Kristũ Jesũ, o we ũrĩa wakuire, na akĩriũka akiuma kũrĩ arĩa akuũ, nĩwe ũikarĩte guoko-inĩ kwa ũrĩo kwa Ngai agĩtũthaithanĩrĩra.
english: Who is he who condemns? It is Christ who died, yes rather, who was raised from the dead, who is at the right hand of God, who also makes intercession for us.

kikuyu: Ikaragai mũkenete hĩndĩ ciothe mũrĩ thĩinĩ wa Mwathani. O na nĩ nguuga o rĩngĩ: Ikaragai mũkenete!
english: Rejoice in the Lord always! Again I will say, “Rejoice!”

kikuyu: “Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, mũrũ wa mũndũ, ratha ũhoro wĩre Gogu atĩrĩ: ‘Mwathani Jehova ekuuga ũũ: Ihinda rĩu, rĩrĩa andũ akwa Isiraeli magaakorwo maikarĩte marĩ na thayũ-rĩ, githĩ o nawe ndũkarĩmenya?
english: “ ‘ “They will set apart men of continual employment who will pass through the land. Those who pass through will go with those who bury those who remain on the surface of the land, to cleanse it. After the end of seven months they will search.

kikuyu: Ũtare arũme othe a ũkũrũ wa kuuma mĩaka mĩrongo ĩtatũ nginya mĩaka mĩrongo ĩtano arĩa mokaga gũtungata wĩra-inĩ wa Hema-ya-Gũtũnganwo.
english: The works of Josias were upright before his Lord with a heart full of godliness.

kikuyu: Ti-itherũ ũmaigaga kũndũ kũrĩa gũtenderũ; ũmagũithagia thĩ makaanangĩka.
english: Surely you set them in slippery places. You throw them down to destruction.
