english: The night is far gone, and the day is near. Let’s therefore throw off the deeds of darkness, and let’s put on the armor of light.

kikuyu: “Rĩu ngoro yakwa nĩĩratangĩka, na niĩ ngũkiuga atĩa? Njuge, ‘Baba honokia harĩ ihinda rĩĩrĩ’? Aca, gĩtũmi gĩkĩ nĩkĩo gĩtũmĩte nginyie ihinda rĩĩrĩ.
english: “Now my soul is troubled. What shall I say? ‘Father, save me from this time’? But I came to this time for this cause.

kikuyu: “Mwathani nĩaregete njamba ciothe cia ita iria ndĩ nacio, akanjĩtĩra ita nĩguo rĩnjũkĩrĩre, * 1:10 Andũ a ndũrĩrĩ matietĩkĩrĩtio gũtoonya hekarũ matikamĩthaahie ( -9).
english: “The Lord has set at nothing all my mighty men within me. He has called a solemn assembly against me to crush my young men. The Lord has trodden the virgin daughter of Judah as in a wine press.

kikuyu: “ ‘Matikanenjwo njuĩrĩ ĩthire mĩtwe, kana mareke njuĩrĩ ciao iraihe mũno, no nĩ maigage njuĩrĩ ciao cia mĩtwe irĩ nenge. Ningĩ gũtikanagĩe na mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai ũkũnyua ndibei rĩrĩa egũtoonya nja ya na thĩinĩ.
english: “ ‘ “They shall not shave their heads, or allow their locks to grow long. They shall only cut off the hair of their heads.

kikuyu: Tondũ andũ a Isiraeli nĩ ndungata ciakwa, iria ndaarutire bũrũri wa Misiri, matikanendio matuĩke ngombo.
english: For they are my servants, whom I brought out of the land of Egypt. They shall not be sold as slaves.

kikuyu: Mũrĩithi wa mũcaara tiwe mũrĩithi ũrĩa mwene ngʼondu. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio rĩrĩa ona njũũi ĩgĩũka, atiganagĩria ngʼondu, akoora. Hĩndĩ ĩyo nayo njũũi ĩgatharĩkĩra rũũru, ĩkarũhurunja.
english: The disciples therefore said, “Lord, if he has fallen asleep, he will recover.”

kikuyu: Nĩhotaga gwĩka maũndũ mothe na ũndũ wa Kristũ ũrĩa ũheaga hinya. No, nĩmwekire wega nĩkũnyiitanĩra na niĩ mathĩĩna-inĩ makwa.
english: Brothers, I don’t regard myself as yet having taken hold, but one thing I do: forgetting the things which are behind and stretching forward to the things which are before,

kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa uunagĩra andũ riitho akoragwo agĩthugunda waganu, ũrĩa ũtembanagia mĩromo akoragwo agĩthugunda ũũru.
english: One who winks his eyes to plot perversities, one who compresses his lips, is bent on evil.

kikuyu: nduĩkĩte ta mũndũ ũtaiguaga, ũrĩa kanua gake gatarĩ ũndũ kangĩhota gũcookia.
english: Yes, I am as a man who doesn’t hear, in whose mouth are no reproofs.

kikuyu: “Inyuĩ nyũmba ya Isiraeli-rĩ, nĩmwandutagĩra magongona na maruta mĩaka mĩrongo ĩna mũrĩ kũu werũ-inĩ?
english: “Did you bring to me sacrifices and offerings in the wilderness forty years, house of Israel?

kikuyu: nĩguo agarũre roho wake ndũkaharũrũkio mbĩrĩra, nĩgeetha ũtheri ũcio wa muoyo ũmũtherere.
english: to bring back his soul from the pit, that he may be enlightened with the light of the living.

kikuyu: Arata na athiritũ akwa matiendaga kũnguhĩrĩria nĩ ũndũ wa ironda ciakwa; o na andũ a itũũra rĩakwa manjikaraga haraihu.
english: When you rebuke and correct man for iniquity, you consume his wealth like a moth. Surely every man is but a breath.” Selah.

kikuyu: O hĩndĩ ĩyo mũtumia ũcio agĩtiga ndigithũ yake ya maaĩ, agĩcooka itũũra-inĩ, akĩĩra andũ atĩrĩ,
english: So the woman left her water pot, went away into the city, and said to the people,

kikuyu: Wee Ngai amũheaga Roho wake na akaringaga ciama gatagatĩ kanyu nĩ ũndũ wanyu gwathĩkĩra watho, kana nĩ ũndũ wa gwĩtĩkia ũhoro ũrĩa mwaiguire?
english: He therefore who supplies the Spirit to you and does miracles among you, does he do it by the works of the law, or by hearing of faith?

kikuyu: Ta rora mwena wakwa wa ũrĩo wone; gũtirĩ o na ũmwe ũranũmbũiya. Ndirĩ na ha kũũrĩra; muoyo wakwa gũtirĩ mũndũ o na ũmwe ũngĩũmenyerera.
english: Therefore my spirit is overwhelmed within me. My heart within me is desolate.

kikuyu: Maitho mao marogĩa nduma matige gũcooka kuona, nayo mĩgongo yao ĩrohocerera nginya tene.
english: Let their eyes be darkened, so that they can’t see. Let their backs be continually bent.

kikuyu: “Ti-itherũ nĩũũĩ ũrĩa gũtũire kuuma tene rĩrĩa mũndũ aigirwo gũkũ thĩ ĩno,
english: Don’t you know this from old time, since man was placed on earth,

kikuyu: Waitĩrĩirio maguta ũtuĩke ta kerubi mũgitanĩri, na nĩkĩo ndaakwamũrire. Wee warĩ kĩrĩma-igũrũ kĩrĩa gĩtheru kĩa Ngai; waceeragĩra mahiga-inĩ ma mwaki.
english: You were the anointed cherub who covers. Then I set you up on the holy mountain of God. You have walked up and down in the middle of the stones of fire.

kikuyu: Baranaba aarĩ mũndũ mwega waiyũrĩtwo nĩ Roho Mũtheru na warĩ na wĩtĩkio, nao andũ aingĩ magĩĩtĩkia Mwathani. Nake Baranaba agĩthiĩ Tariso gũcaria Saũlũ,
english: On the next day they entered into Caesarea. Cornelius was waiting for them, having called together his relatives and his near friends.

kikuyu: Niĩ ndĩ rũthingo, nacio nyondo ciakwa ihaana ta mĩthiringo mĩraihu na igũrũ. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio maitho-inĩ make-rĩ, nduĩkĩte o ta mũndũ ũtũmaga aiganĩre.
english: -31 form an acrostic, with each verse starting with each letter of the Hebrew alphabet, in order.

kikuyu: “Ũrĩa wothe ũkaanyumbũra mbere ya andũ, o na niĩ nĩngamumbũra mbere ya Baba ũrĩa ũrĩ kũu igũrũ. No ũrĩa wothe ũkangaana mbere ya andũ, na niĩ nĩngamũkaana mbere ya Baba ũrĩa ũrĩ igũrũ.
english: Everyone therefore who confesses me before men, I will also confess him before my Father who is in heaven.

kikuyu: Rĩu ndamũthaitha mũrĩe irio. Nĩmũbatarĩte kũrĩa irio nĩguo imũige muoyo. Gũtirĩ rũcuĩrĩ rwa mũtwe wa mũndũ o na ũmwe wanyu rũkũũra.”
english: Therefore I beg you to take some food, for this is for your safety; for not a hair will perish from any of your heads.”

kikuyu: Jaeli akiumagara agatũnge Sisera, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Ũka, mwathi wakwa, toonya thĩinĩ. Ndũgetigĩre.” Nĩ ũndũ ũcio agĩtoonya hema thĩinĩ nake Jaeli akĩmũhumbĩra.
english: Jael went out to meet Sisera, and said to him, “Turn in, my lord, turn in to me; don’t be afraid.” He came in to her into the tent, and she covered him with a rug.

kikuyu: Mũthamaki ũcio agĩũkĩra marĩ hamwe na barũthi, na Berinike, na andũ arĩa maikarĩte hamwe nao.
english: The king rose up with the governor and Bernice, and those who sat with them.

kikuyu: Jesũ agĩcooka akĩanĩrĩra na mũgambo mũnene, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Mũndũ ũrĩa ũnjĩtĩkĩtie, to niĩ nyiki etĩkĩtie, no etĩkĩtie o na ũrĩa wandũmire.
english: Jesus cried out and said, “Whoever believes in me, believes not in me, but in him who sent me.

kikuyu: atĩ nĩmweĩĩkire ũũru rĩrĩa mwandũmire thiĩ kũrĩ Jehova Ngai wanyu, mũkĩnjĩĩra atĩrĩ, ‘Tũhooere kũrĩ Jehova Ngai witũ; twĩre ũrĩa wothe ekuuga, na ithuĩ nĩtũkũwĩka.’
english: For you have dealt deceitfully against your own souls; for you sent me to Yahweh your God, saying, ‘Pray for us to Yahweh our God; and according to all that Yahweh our God says, so declare to us, and we will do it.’

kikuyu: Njiaro cia Benjamini kuuma Geba ciatũũraga Mikimashi, na Aija, na Betheli na matũũra marĩo, 32na kũu Anathothu, na Nobu na Anania, 33na Hazoru, na Rama na Gitaimu, 34na Hadidi, na Zeboimu na Nebalati,
english: The children of Benjamin also lived from Geba onward, at Michmash and Aija, and at Bethel and its towns,

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa mwagerio, mũndũ o naũ ndakanoige atĩrĩ, “Ngai nĩarangeria.” Nĩgũkorwo Ngai ndangĩhota kũgerio nĩ ũũru, o nake mwene ndageragia mũndũ o na ũrĩkũ; 14no rĩrĩ, mũndũ nĩkũgerio ageragio hĩndĩ ĩrĩa ekũguucĩrĩrio na akaheenererio nĩ merirĩria make mooru.
english: Let no man say when he is tempted, “I am tempted by God,” for God can’t be tempted by evil, and he himself tempts no one.

kikuyu: “Mahaandaga rũhuho, makagetha kĩhuhũkanio; ngano ndĩrĩ na magira, ndĩngiumia mũtu; korwo no ĩciare ngano, andũ a kũngĩ nĩo mangĩmĩrĩa.
english: The days of visitation have come. The days of reckoning have come. Israel will consider the prophet to be a fool, and the man who is inspired to be insane, because of the abundance of your sins, and because your hostility is great.

kikuyu: Atĩrĩrĩ, ndũkanakũũme mũndũ mũkũrũ, kaba ũmũthaithage taarĩ thoguo, nao andũ arĩa ethĩ ta marĩ ariũ a thoguo,
english: Paul, an apostle of Jesus Christ according to the commandment of God our Savior and the Lord Jesus Christ† our hope,

kikuyu: mũgĩtanyaga na mũkĩĩriragĩria mũthenya ũcio ũkinye narua. Mũthenya ũcio wakinya-rĩ, igũrũ nĩrĩkaanangwo na mwaki, na indo ciothe iria irĩ kuo ihĩe nĩ mwaki, itweke. No kũringana na ũrĩa eeranĩire-rĩ, nĩtwĩrĩgĩrĩire igũrũ rĩerũ na thĩ njerũ, o kuo kũrĩa gũtũũraga ũthingu. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, inyuĩ arata akwa nyendete mũno, kuona atĩ nĩmwĩrĩgĩrĩire ũndũ ũyũ-rĩ, kĩĩrutanĩriei mũkoragwo mũtarĩ na kĩmeni, na mũtarĩ na ũcuuke, na mũrĩ na thayũ nake.
english: To them it was revealed that they served not themselves, but you, in these things, which now have been announced to you through those who preached the Good News to you by the Holy Spirit sent out from heaven; which things angels desire to look into.

kikuyu: Ũragai athuuri, na aanake na airĩtu, na andũ-a-nja na ciana, no mũtikahutie mũndũ o na ũrĩkũ mwĩkĩre rũũri rũu. Ambĩrĩriai o haha hakwa haamũre.” Nĩ ũndũ ũcio makĩambĩrĩria na athuuri arĩa maarĩ hau mbere ya hekarũ. Ningĩ akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Thaahiai hekarũ ĩno na mũiyũrie nja ciayo andũ arĩa moragĩtwo. Thiĩi!” Nĩ ũndũ ũcio makiumagara, makĩambĩrĩria kũũragana kũu itũũra-inĩ inene.
english: It came to pass, when he commanded the man clothed in linen, saying, “Take fire from between the whirling wheels, from between the cherubim,” that he went in and stood beside a wheel.

kikuyu: O na ningĩ warĩ na Gibeoni, na Rama, na Beerothu,
english: Gibeon, Ramah, Beeroth,

kikuyu: Ũguo nĩguo andũ a Midiani maahootirwo nĩ andũ a Isiraeli, na matiacookire kwambararia mĩtwe rĩngĩ. Matukũ-inĩ marĩa Gideoni aatũũrire muoyo, bũrũri ũcio ũkĩgĩa na thayũ mĩaka mĩrongo ĩna.
english: So Midian was subdued before the children of Israel, and they lifted up their heads no more. The land had rest forty years in the days of Gideon.

kikuyu: Mwĩmenyererei nĩguo ũndũ ũrĩa mũrĩkĩtie kũigua kuuma o kĩambĩrĩria ũikarage thĩinĩ wanyu. Ũndũ ũcio ũngĩikara thĩinĩ wanyu-rĩ, o na inyuĩ mũrĩikaraga thĩinĩ wa Mũriũ, o na mũrĩ thĩinĩ wa Ithe. Na ũndũ ũyũ nĩguo atwĩrĩire: o muoyo wa tene na tene.
english: Therefore, as for you, let that remain in you which you heard from the beginning. If that which you heard from the beginning remains in you, you also will remain in the Son, and in the Father.

kikuyu: Njooke ndĩmũhe ũhoro wakwa ikinya gwa ikinya; ndĩmũkuhĩrĩrie ta ndĩ mũnene.)
english: I would declare to him the number of my steps. I would go near to him like a prince.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo Jehova nĩ mũthingu, na nĩendete ciira wa kĩhooto; andũ arĩa arũngĩrĩru nĩo marĩonaga ũthiũ wake.
english: For Yahweh is righteous. He loves righteousness. The upright shall see his face.

kikuyu: Jehova akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Niĩ nĩngũkorwo ndĩ hamwe nawe, na nĩũkũhũũra Amidiani ũmaniine othe me hamwe.” Nake Gideoni akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Ingĩkorwo nĩnjĩtĩkĩrĩkĩte maitho-inĩ maku-rĩ, kĩnyonie kĩmenyithia atĩ ti-itherũ nĩwe ũranjarĩria.
english: He divided the three hundred men into three companies, and he put into the hands of all of them trumpets and empty pitchers, with torches within the pitchers.

kikuyu: Ndũkanatirike ũũgĩ, naguo nĩũrĩkũgitagĩra;
english: and in knowledge, self-control; and in self-control, perseverance; and in perseverance, godliness;

kikuyu: Samusoni akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Rekei ndĩmũgwatie ndaĩ, muona mwamĩtaũra mĩthenya-inĩ ĩno mũgwanja ya iruga-rĩ, nĩngamũhe nguo cia gatani mĩrongo ĩtatũ na nguo cia kũgarũrĩra mĩrongo ĩtatũ.
english: Samson said to them, “Let me tell you a riddle now. If you can tell me the answer within the seven days of the feast, and find it out, then I will give you thirty linen garments and thirty changes of clothing;

kikuyu: Jesũ Gũtũma Arutwo arĩa Ikũmi na Eerĩ Na rĩrĩ, Jesũ aarĩkia gwĩta arutwo arĩa ikũmi na eerĩ hamwe, nĩamaheire hinya na ũhoti wa kũingata ndaimono ciothe na kũhonia mĩrimũ,
english: Herod said, “I beheaded John, but who is this about whom I hear such things?” He sought to see him.

kikuyu: “Kaĩ ndĩ Ngai wa o gũkũ gũkuhĩ, na ndirĩ Ngai wa kũraya?” ũguo nĩguo Jehova ekũũria.
english: “Am I a God at hand,” says Yahweh, “and not a God afar off?

kikuyu: Rũrĩmĩ rwa gakenge rũnyiitanĩte na karakara nĩ ũndũ wa kũnyoota; twana tũhooyaga irio, no gũtirĩ mũndũ ũtũheaga.
english: The tongue of the nursing child clings to the roof of his mouth for thirst. The young children ask for bread, and no one breaks it for them.

kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũhĩkaga gwĩka maũndũ ateciirĩtie wega, nĩakamenya maũndũ na ataũkĩrwo nĩmo, naruo rũrĩmĩ rũrĩa rũtondoiraga nĩrũkaaria o wega.
english: The heart of the rash will understand knowledge, and the tongue of the stammerers will be ready to speak plainly.

kikuyu: Tondũ nĩngũkwehereria andũ arĩa athingu na arĩa aaganu-rĩ, rũhiũ rwakwa rwa njora nĩrũgacomorerwo mũndũ o wothe kuuma mwena wa gũthini nginya mwena wa gathigathini.
english: lest by any means, if anyone from Macedonia comes there with me and finds you unprepared, we (to say nothing of you) would be disappointed in this confident boasting.

kikuyu: Mwĩcookanĩrĩriei mbere ya ihinda rĩrĩa rĩtuĩtwo gũkinya, mũthenya ũcio ũmũkorerere ta ũrĩa mũũngũ ũmbũragwo nĩ rũhuho, mbere ya marakara mahiũ ma Jehova mamũkore, mbere ya mũthenya ũcio wa mangʼũrĩ ma Jehova kũmũkora.
english: before the appointed time when the day passes as the chaff, before the fierce anger of Yahweh comes on you, before the day of Yahweh’s anger comes on you.

kikuyu: na Maarathu, na Bethi-Anothu, na Elitekoni; mothe maarĩ matũũra matandatũ na tũtũũra twamo.
english: Maarath, Beth Anoth, and Eltekon; six cities with their villages.

kikuyu: Acooke oe kamũtĩ ka mũtarakwa, na mũthobi, na rũrigi rwa rangi wa gakarakũ na nyoni ĩyo ĩrĩ muoyo, acitobokie thĩinĩ wa thakame ĩyo ya nyoni ĩyo thĩnje, na maaĩ-inĩ macio matahĩtwo o hĩndĩ ĩyo, aminjaminjĩrie nyũmba ĩyo maita mũgwanja.
english: He shall examine the plague on the seventh day. If the plague has spread in the garment, either in the warp, or in the woof, or in the skin, whatever use the skin is used for, the plague is a destructive mildew. It is unclean.

kikuyu: Mũndũ ũcio akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Nĩmoimĩte gũkũ. Ndĩraiguire makiuga atĩrĩ, ‘Rekei tũthiĩ Dothani.’ ” Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Jusufu akĩrũmĩrĩra ariũ a ithe, na akĩmakora hakuhĩ na Dothani. No makĩmuona arĩ haraaya, na ataanakinya harĩa maarĩ, magĩciirĩra kũmũũraga.
english: The man said, “They have left here, for I heard them say, ‘Let’s go to Dothan.’ ” Joseph went after his brothers, and found them in Dothan.

kikuyu: Nũũ ũkĩhaana ta niĩ? Nĩarekwo oimbũre ũhoro ũcio. Nĩaheane ũhoro ũcio na oimbũre arĩ mbere yakwa, ũrĩa gwatariĩ kuuma rĩrĩa ndoombire andũ akwa a tene, na onanie ũrĩa gũgaikara thuutha-inĩ; ĩĩ, nĩakĩrathe ũhoro wa maũndũ marĩa magooka.
english: Who is like me? Who will call, and will declare it, and set it in order for me, since I established the ancient people? Let them declare the things that are coming, and that will happen.

kikuyu: “Niĩ, o niĩ mwene, nĩ niĩ tharagia mahĩtia maku, mathire biũ, nĩ ũndũ wakwa Niĩ mwene, na ngaaga kũririkana mehia manyu rĩngĩ.
english: I, even I, am he who blots out your transgressions for my own sake; and I will not remember your sins.

kikuyu: “Ũcooke ũmeere atĩrĩ, ‘Ũũ nĩguo Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe, Ngai wa Isiraeli, ekuuga: Nyuai, mũrĩĩo na mũtahĩke, mũcooke mũgũe na mũtikanacooke gũũkĩra, nĩ ũndũ wa rũhiũ rwa njora rũrĩa ngaarehithia gatagatĩ-inĩ kanyu.’ No rĩrĩ, mangĩkaarega kuoya gĩkombe kĩu kuuma guoko- inĩ gwaku manyue-rĩ, ũkameera atĩrĩ, ‘Ũũ nĩguo Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe ekuuga: No nginya mũnyue!
english: “You shall tell them, ‘Yahweh of Armies, the God of Israel says: “Drink, and be drunk, vomit, fall, and rise no more, because of the sword which I will send among you.” ’

kikuyu: Goocai Mwathani, o we Mũrungu Mũhonokia witũ, o we ũkuuaga mĩrigo iitũ mũthenya o mũthenya.
english: Blessed be the Lord, who daily bears our burdens, even the God who is our salvation. Selah.

kikuyu: “O na gũtuĩka ndirĩ na ũcuuke-rĩ, ndikwĩrĩrĩra; muoyo wakwa nĩndĩwagĩire kĩene.
english: before I go where I will not return from, to the land of darkness and of the shadow of death;

kikuyu: Thĩinĩ wa watho-rĩ, Musa aatwathire tũhũũrage atumia ta aya na mahiga nyuguto. Rĩu we ũkuuga atĩa?”
english: While I am in the world, I am the light of the world.”

kikuyu: na Hafaraimu, na Shioni, na Anaharathu,
english: Hapharaim, Shion, Anaharath,

kikuyu: Iherithia rĩa andũ akwa nĩ inene gũkĩra rĩa itũũra rĩa Sodomu, rĩrĩa rĩangʼaũranirio o rĩmwe, gũtarĩ guoko kwarĩteithirie.
english: For the iniquity of the daughter of my people is greater than the sin of Sodom, which was overthrown as in a moment. No hands were laid on her.

kikuyu: Ndũgetũũgĩrie harĩ mũthamaki, na ndũkerũgamie gatagatĩ ka anene;
english: Don’t exalt yourself in the presence of the king, or claim a place among great men;

kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo iheo iria Ngai aheanaga na gwĩtana kũrĩa etanaga nakuo ndacookaga kwĩricũkwo.
english: For the gifts and the calling of God are irrevocable.

kikuyu: Nĩngũkũrutĩra igongona rĩa kwĩyendera; nĩngũgooca rĩĩtwa rĩaku, Wee Jehova, tondũ nĩ rĩega.
english: I said, “Oh that I had wings like a dove! Then I would fly away, and be at rest.

kikuyu: Kũngĩkĩhaana ũguo-rĩ, mwene iruga ũrĩa ũmwĩtĩte inyuĩ eerĩ no ooke, akwĩre atĩrĩ, ‘Eherera mũndũ ũyũ gĩtĩ kĩu.’ Hĩndĩ ĩyo, nĩũgaaikara thuutha biũ ũconokete. No hĩndĩ ĩrĩa ũngĩtwo-rĩ, ikaragĩra itĩ cia thuutha, nĩgeetha ũrĩa ũgwĩtĩte ooka, akwĩre atĩrĩ, ‘Mũrata wakwa, thiĩ ũgaikare harĩa mbere.’ Hĩndĩĩyonĩũkaheogĩtĩĩo maitho-inĩma ageniarĩa angĩothe.
english: and he who invited both of you would come and tell you, ‘Make room for this person.’ Then you would begin, with shame, to take the lowest place.

kikuyu: Wee wakinyire iria igũrũ na mbarathi ciaku, ũgĩthuka maaĩ macio maingĩ, magĩtuĩka makũmbĩ manene.
english: You trampled the sea with your horses, churning mighty waters.

kikuyu: “ ‘Nĩ ũndũ ũcio-rĩ, Mwathani Jehova ekuuga atĩrĩ: Marakara na mangʼũrĩ makwa nĩmagaitĩrĩrio kũndũ gũkũ, magaaitĩrĩrio mũndũ na nyamũ, na mĩtĩ ya gĩthaka o na maciaro ma thĩ, namo nĩmagaakana na matikahoreka.
english: Therefore the Lord Yahweh says: “Behold, my anger and my wrath will be poured out on this place, on man, on animal, on the trees of the field, and on the fruit of the ground; and it will burn and will not be quenched.”

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo wendi wa Baba nĩ atĩ ũrĩa wothe wonete Mũriũ na akamwĩtĩkia nĩakagĩa na muoyo wa tene na tene, na nĩngamũriũkia mũthenya wa kũrigĩrĩria.” * 6:31 Ayahudi meciiragia atĩ Mesia oka nĩangĩamaheire irio iria cietagwo mana, iria maithe mao maarĩĩaga marĩ werũ-inĩ.
english: This is the will of the one who sent me, that everyone who sees the Son and believes in him should have eternal life; and I will raise him up at the last day.”

kikuyu: O na mũtĩ waruo ũgĩtoonya na rũhiũ rũkiumanĩra na thuutha. Ehudu ndaacomorire rũhiũ, namo maguta makĩrũhumbĩra. Ningĩ Ehudu agĩthiĩ gĩthaku-inĩ, akĩhinga mĩrango ya nyũmba ĩyo ya igũrũ, akĩmũhingĩrĩria kuo.
english: The handle also went in after the blade; and the fat closed on the blade, for he didn’t draw the sword out of his body; and it came out behind.

kikuyu: Roho ũrĩa wĩtanaga ũkiugaga atĩrĩ, “Abba, Baba.” Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, we ndũrĩ ngombo rĩngĩ, no ũrĩ mwana wake; na tondũ rĩu ũrĩ mwana wake, Ngai nĩagũtuĩte mũgai wa indo ciake. Paũlũ Kũrũmbũiya Agalatia
english: You were running well! Who interfered with you that you should not obey the truth?

kikuyu: O ihinda-inĩ rĩu nĩarũarire na agĩkua, naguo mwĩrĩ wake ũgĩthambio, ũkĩigwo nyũmba ya igũrũ.*
english: In those days, she became sick and died. When they had washed her, they laid her in an upper room.

kikuyu: Mũthenya nĩ waku o na ũtukũ no waku; Wee nĩwe wahaandire riũa na mweri.
english: The day is yours, the night is also yours. You have prepared the light and the sun.

kikuyu: “ ‘Na rĩrĩ, mũngĩkaaga kũrũmĩrĩra rĩathani o na rĩmwe rĩa maya Jehova aaheire Musa mũtekwenda-rĩ,
english: because all those men who have seen my glory and my signs, which I worked in Egypt and in the wilderness, yet have tempted me these ten times, and have not listened to my voice;

kikuyu: ngoro yakwa nĩĩgacanjamũka rĩrĩa mĩromo yaku ĩkaaria maũndũ marĩa magĩrĩire.
english: Yes, my heart will rejoice when your lips speak what is right.

kikuyu: Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe arĩ na mũthenya aigĩte wa gũũkĩrĩra andũ arĩa othe etĩĩi na arĩa meĩkagĩrĩra, na arĩa othe metũũgagĩria (nao othe nĩmakanyiihio),
english: For there will be a day of Yahweh of Armies for all that is proud and arrogant, and for all that is lifted up, and it shall be brought low—

kikuyu: Mũthenya ũcio-rĩ, mbugi cia mbarathi ikaandĩkwo, ICI CIAMŨRĨIRWO JEHOVA, nacio nyũngũ cia kũruga iria irĩ nyũmba ya Jehova thĩinĩ igaatuĩka ta mbakũri iria nyamũre irĩ mbere ya kĩgongona.
english: In that day there will be inscribed on the bells of the horses, “HOLY TO YAHWEH”; and the pots in Yahweh’s house will be like the bowls before the altar.

kikuyu: Nĩakaigua mahooya ma andũ arĩa aagi; ndakanyarara mahooya mao.
english: But Yahweh’s loving kindness is from everlasting to everlasting with those who fear him, his righteousness to children’s children,

kikuyu: Mũthenya ũcio makaararama nĩ ũndũ wa kĩrĩa manyiitĩte, o ta ũrĩa iria rĩrurumaga rĩgĩikia ndiihũ. Na mũndũ angĩgaacũthĩrĩria bũrũri-inĩ, akoona no nduma ĩrĩ kuo na mĩnyamaro, o naguo ũtheri nĩũgaathiĩkwo nĩ matu.
english: They will roar against them in that day like the roaring of the sea. If one looks to the land, behold, darkness and distress. The light is darkened in its clouds.

kikuyu: Nũũ ũngĩmenya? Hihi no agarũrũke na aigue tha, atige atũrathima: atũhe indo iria irutagwo magongona ma mũtu, na iria irutagwo magongona ma kũnyuuo, cia kũrutĩrwo Jehova Ngai wanyu.
english: Who knows? He may turn and relent, and leave a blessing behind him, even a meal offering and a drink offering to Yahweh, your God.

kikuyu: nĩo Sihoni mũthamaki wa Aamori, Wendo wake nĩ wa tene na tene.
english: Sihon king of the Amorites, for his loving kindness endures forever;

kikuyu: “Nake Juda aheo gĩcigo kĩmwe; nakĩo gĩkaahakana na rũgongo rwa Rubeni kuuma mwena wa irathĩro nginya mwena wa ithũĩro.
english: Now when I had returned, behold, on the bank of the river were very many trees on the one side and on the other.

kikuyu: “No ithuĩ nĩtũmangʼaũranĩtie; Heshiboni kwanangĩtwo guothe nginya o Diboni. Tũmamomorete tũgakinya Nofa, itũũra rĩrĩa rĩkuhanĩrĩirie na Medeba.” Nĩ ũndũ ũcio andũ a Isiraeli magĩtũũra bũrũri ũcio wa Aamori.
english: We have shot at them. Heshbon has perished even to Dibon. We have laid waste even to Nophah, Which reaches to Medeba.”

kikuyu: Ĩ mũngĩkĩona Mũrũ wa Mũndũ akĩambata igũrũ kũrĩa aarĩ mbere!
english: Then what if you would see the Son of Man ascending to where he was before?

kikuyu: niĩ ngeeka atĩa rĩrĩa Mũrungu akaanjũkĩrĩra? Ngaacookia atĩa rĩrĩa ngeetwo ndĩĩtetere ũhoro-inĩ ũcio?
english: what then will I do when God rises up? When he visits, what will I answer him?

kikuyu: Nĩtwĩkĩte maũndũ ma waganu mũnene harĩwe. Tũtiathĩkĩire maathani na irĩra cia watho waku cia kũrũmĩrĩrwo, o na mawatho marĩa waheire Musa ndungata yaku.
english: Moreover I said to the king, “If it pleases the king, let letters be given me to the governors beyond the River, that they  The Hebrew word rendered “God” is “אֱֹלהִ ֑ים” (Elohim).  “Yahweh” is God’s proper Name, sometimes rendered “LORD” (all caps) in other translations.  The word translated “Lord” is “Adonai.”

kikuyu: Aya nĩo atongoria a bũrũri arĩa maatũũrire Jerusalemu (na rĩrĩ, andũ amwe a Isiraeli, na athĩnjĩri- Ngai, na Alawii, na ndungata cia hekarũ, na njiaro cia ndungata cia Solomoni maatũũraga matũũra- inĩ ma Juda, o mũndũ gĩthaka-inĩ gĩake thĩinĩ wa matũũra mao.
english: Now these are the chiefs of the province who lived in Jerusalem; but in the cities of Judah, everyone lived in his possession in their cities—Israel, the priests, the Levites, the temple servants, and the children of Solomon’s servants.

kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo magũrũ mao mahanyũkaga mathiĩ mehia-inĩ, nao mahiũhaga magaite thakame.
english: to deliver you from the strange woman, even from the foreigner who flatters with her words,

kikuyu: Ta kanyoni-ka-nja gekũbatabata, kana thũngũrũrũ ĩkũhiũka-hiũka, noguo kĩrumi gĩtarĩ gĩtũmi gĩtarĩ ha kwĩgwatĩrĩra.
english: Like a fluttering sparrow, like a darting swallow, so the undeserved curse doesn’t come to rest.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ nĩĩgakinya rĩrĩa thũ ciaku igaaka ihumbu cia tĩĩri ikũrigiicĩrie, na ikũhingĩrĩrie na mĩena yothe.
english: For the days will come on you when your enemies will throw up a barricade against you, surround you, hem you in on every side,

kikuyu: Magai mao maagairwo na njĩra ya gũcuukĩra mĩtĩ kũrĩ o mĩhĩrĩga ĩyo kenda na nuthu, o ta ũrĩa Jehova aathĩte Musa,
english: Their south border was from the uttermost part of the Salt Sea, from the bay that looks southward;

kikuyu: Ngai arogoocwo, o we ũtaaregire kũigua ihooya rĩakwa, o na kana akĩaga kũnyonia wendo wake!
english: Blessed be God, who has not turned away my prayer, nor his loving kindness from me.

kikuyu: Thiiaga njirĩte biũ, no ti ũndũ wa kũhĩa nĩ riũa; ngarũgama kĩũngano-inĩ ngakaya ndeithio.
english: I go mourning without the sun. I stand up in the assembly, and cry for help.

kikuyu: kuuma kũrĩ Zera, nĩ mbarĩ ya Azera; kuuma kũrĩ Shauli, nĩ mbarĩ ya Ashaulu.
english: of Zerah, the family of the Zerahites; of Shaul, the family of the Shaulites.

kikuyu: maanagĩrĩre, makiugaga atĩrĩ, “Jehova nĩ mũrũngĩrĩru; we nĩwe Rwaro rwakwa rwa Ihiga, na gũtirĩ waganu ũngĩoneka thĩinĩ wake.”
english: For judgment will return to righteousness. All the upright in heart shall follow it.

kikuyu: Mĩtĩ ya Jehova nĩyuragĩrio mbura wega, mĩtarakwa ĩyo ya Lebanoni o ĩrĩa aahaandire.
english: Yahweh’s trees are well watered, the cedars of Lebanon, which he has planted,

kikuyu: Mũraathirĩkia nĩkĩ, mũgatũma ndaakare nĩ ũndũ wa kĩrĩa mũthondekete na moko manyu na ũndũ wa gũcinĩra ngai ingĩ ũbumba gũkũ bũrũri wa Misiri, kũrĩa mũũkĩte gũtũũra? Nĩmũkwĩniina na mũtuĩke kĩndũ gĩa kũrumagwo na gĩa kũnyararwo thĩinĩ wa ndũrĩrĩ ciothe cia thĩ.
english: in that you provoke me to anger with the works of your hands, burning incense to other gods in the land of Egypt where you have gone to live, that you may be cut off, and that you may be a curse and a reproach among all the nations of the earth?

kikuyu: rĩrĩa ndoigire atĩrĩ, ‘Ũkinyage o haha na ndũkanahakĩre; makũmbĩ maku macio metĩĩi-rĩ, hau nĩho marĩĩkinyaga’?
english: and said, ‘You may come here, but no further. Your proud waves shall be stopped here’?

kikuyu: Mũndũ mwĩtĩĩi na mwĩgereri, etagwo “Mũnyũrũria”; ekaga maũndũ make na mwĩtĩĩo mũnene.
english: The proud and arrogant man—“Scoffer” is his name— he works in the arrogance of pride.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩmakamenya atĩ niĩ nĩ niĩ Jehova, rĩrĩa ngaananga bũrũri ũcio ũkire ihooru, nĩ ũndũ wa maũndũ marĩa mothe marĩ magigi maneeka.’
english: Then they will know that I am Yahweh, when I have made the land a desolation and an astonishment because of all their abominations which they have committed.” ’

kikuyu: nao andũ othe, arĩa anini na arĩa anene, makamũthikagĩrĩria na makoiga na mũgambo mũnene atĩrĩ, “Mũndũ ũyũ nĩwe hinya wa ũngai ũrĩa ũĩkaine ta Hinya Mũnene.”
english: to whom they all listened, from the least to the greatest, saying, “This man is that great power of God.”

kikuyu: Marakara macio ma Jehova matigaathira, o nginya rĩrĩa akaahingia muoroto wa ngoro yake. Matukũ-inĩ marĩa magooka-rĩ, nĩmũgataũkĩrwo nĩ ũhoro ũcio wega.
english: Yahweh’s anger will not return until he has executed and performed the intents of his heart. In the latter days, you will understand it perfectly.

kikuyu: Arĩithi marĩ na ndũũru ciao cia mbũri nĩmagooka nĩguo mamũũkĩrĩre; nĩmakaamba hema ciao imũthiũrũrũkĩirie mĩena yothe, o mũndũ o mũndũ akarangĩra handũ hake.”
english: Shepherds with their flocks will come to her. They will pitch their tents against her all around. They will feed everyone in his place.”

kikuyu: Gũtirĩ mũndũ ũkwĩhokete ũgaaconorithio o na rĩ, no arĩa mekaga andũ ũũru hatarĩ gĩtũmi-rĩ, acio nĩo magaconorithio.
english: Though an army should encamp against me, my heart shall not fear. Though war should rise against me, even then I will be confident.

kikuyu: Tũmai ũmwe wanyu athiĩ agĩĩre mũrũ wa thoguo ũcio ũngĩ; inyuĩ aya angĩ nĩ mũgũikio njeera nĩgeetha ciugo cianyu icio mwarĩtie irorwo kana nĩ ũhoro wa ma. Mũngĩkorwo ũguo muugĩte tiguo-rĩ, o ta ũrĩa Firaũni atũũraga muoyo, inyuĩ mũrĩ athigaani!” Nake akĩmaikia othe njeera, magĩikara kuo mĩthenya ĩtatũ.
english: When Joseph saw Benjamin with them, he said to the steward of his house, “Bring the men into the house, and butcher an animal, and prepare; for the men will dine with me at noon.”

kikuyu: Bũrũri ũngĩaga ũtongoria mwega nĩkũgũa ũgũũaga, no ataarani aingĩ matũmaga kũgĩe na ũhootani kũna.
english: Where there is no wise guidance, the nation falls, but in the multitude of counselors there is victory.

kikuyu: magatuĩka a thiritũ ya aici; othe nĩmendete mahaki, nao mahanyũkanagia na indo cia kũheo. Maticiiragĩra ciana cia ngoriai na kĩhooto; na matithikagĩrĩria ciira wa mũtumia wa ndigwa. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, Mwathani, Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe, O we Mwene Hinya wa Isiraeli, ekuuga atĩrĩ, “Hĩ! Nĩngaitĩrĩria amuku akwa mangʼũrĩ makwa, na ndĩĩrĩhĩrie harĩ thũ ciakwa.
english: Therefore the Lord,† Yahweh of Armies, the Mighty One of Israel, says: “Ah, I will get relief from my adversaries, and avenge myself on my enemies.

kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo nĩoinangaga ihingo cia gĩcango na akoinanga mĩgĩĩko ya kĩgera.
english: For he has broken the gates of bronze, and cut through bars of iron.

kikuyu: Mwahota kuuga atĩrĩ, “Jehova nĩatwarahũrĩire anabii gũkũ Babuloni,”
english: Why do you cry over your injury? Your pain is incurable. For the greatness of your iniquity, because your sins have increased, I have done these things to you.

kikuyu: na hunjanagĩrie ũhoro wa mwaka ũrĩa Jehova akoonania ũtugi wake, na mũthenya ũrĩa Ngai witũ akerĩhĩria, ningĩ hooragĩrie arĩa othe marĩ na kĩeha,
english: to proclaim the year of Yahweh’s favor and the day of vengeance of our God, to comfort all who mourn,

kikuyu: Itugĩ cia Ashera iria mũrĩ nacio nĩngacimunya na momore matũũra manyu manene.
english: I will uproot your Asherah poles from among you; and I will destroy your cities.

kikuyu: o na ũhoti wake mũnene ũtangĩgereka nĩ ũndũ witũ, ithuĩ arĩa twĩtĩkĩtie. Ũhoti ũcio no ta hinya wake mũnene ũkĩruta wĩra,
english: and what is the exceeding greatness of his power toward us who believe, according to that working of the strength of his might

kikuyu: Agĩtũma Eliakimu mũrori wa maũndũ ma nyũmba ya ũthamaki, na Shebina ũrĩa mwandĩki-marũa, na athĩnjĩri-Ngai arĩa atongoria, othe mehumbĩte nguo cia makũnia, mathiĩ kũrĩ mũnabii Isaia mũrũ wa Amozu.
english: He sent Eliakim, who was over the household, and Shebna the scribe, and the elders of the priests, covered with sackcloth, to Isaiah the prophet, the son of Amoz.

kikuyu: Mũndũ nĩakanyiita ũmwe wa ariũ a nyina arĩ gwa ithe mũciĩ, amwĩre atĩrĩ, “Wee ũrĩ na nguo ya igũrũ, tuĩka mũtongoria witũ; ũreke kũndũ gũkũ kwanangĩtwo kũgĩe wathani-inĩ waku!”
english: Indeed a man shall take hold of his brother in the house of his father, saying, “You have clothing, you be our ruler, and let this ruin be under your hand.”

kikuyu: “ ‘Mũndũ ũmwe thĩinĩ wa kĩrĩndĩ angĩĩhia atekwenda na eke ũndũ ũrĩa mũkananie thĩinĩ wa maathani mothe ma Jehova, nĩahĩtĩtie. Rĩrĩa angĩmenyithio amenye mehia marĩa ekĩte, no nginya arehe harika ĩtarĩ na kaũũgũ arĩ iruta rĩake nĩ ũndũ wa mehia macio ekĩte.
english: “ ‘If anyone of the common people sins unwittingly, in doing any of the things which Yahweh has commanded not to be done, and is guilty,

kikuyu: nĩ ũndũ ainainagĩria Mũrungu ngundi, na akangʼathĩria Mwene-Hinya-Wothe,
english: Because he has stretched out his hand against God, and behaves himself proudly against the Almighty,

kikuyu: Tondũ ũcio nĩ mwaki ũrĩa ũcinaga nginya ũkaananga; na nĩũngĩamunyĩte magetha makwa mothe.
english: for it is a fire that consumes to destruction, and would root out all my increase.

kikuyu: Ciana cia ndungata ciaku igaatũũra na thayũ mbere yaku; nacio njiaro ciacio cigaikara irũmĩte irĩ mbere yaku.”
english: The children of your servants will continue. Their offspring will be established before you.”

kikuyu: Magĩcooka makĩũria atĩrĩ, “Nĩ mũhĩrĩga ũrĩkũ wa Isiraeli ũtokĩte kĩũngano-inĩ mbere ya Jehova kũu Mizipa?” Nao makĩmenya atĩ gũtirĩ mũndũ o na ũmwe kuuma Jabeshi-Gileadi wokĩte kambĩ-inĩ ĩyo nĩ ũndũ wa kĩũngano kĩu.
english: They said, “What one is there of the tribes of Israel who didn’t come up to Yahweh to Mizpah?” Behold, no one came from Jabesh Gilead to the camp to the assembly.

kikuyu: Mũndũ angĩruma ithe kana nyina, tawa wake ũkaahorera nduma-inĩ ĩrĩa ndumanu.
english: Whoever curses his father or his mother, his lamp shall be put out in blackness of darkness.

kikuyu: “Wee Efiraimu-rĩ, ingĩgwĩka atĩa? Na wee Juda-rĩ, ngũgwĩka atĩa? Wendo waku ũhaana ta kĩbii kĩa rũciinĩ, ũkahaana ta ime rĩa rũciinĩ rĩrĩa rĩcookaga rĩgathira.
english: “Ephraim, what shall I do to you? Judah, what shall I do to you? For your love is like a morning cloud, and like the dew that disappears early.

kikuyu: ũhoro wa gĩthima kĩrĩa anene menjete, kĩrĩa gĩathikũririo nĩ andũ arĩa marĩ igweta, andũ arĩa marĩ igweta marĩ na njũgũma cia ũnene na mĩthĩĩgi yao.” Ningĩ makiuma werũ-inĩ magĩthiĩ Matana,
english: the well, which the princes dug, which the nobles of the people dug, with the scepter, and with their poles.” From the wilderness they traveled to Mattanah;

kikuyu: “Ingĩkorwo nĩnjigĩte mwĩhoko wakwa harĩ thahabu, kana ngeera thahabu ĩrĩa therie mũno atĩrĩ, ‘Wee nĩwe ũgitĩri wakwa,’
english: “If I have made gold my hope, and have said to the fine gold, ‘You are my confidence;’

kikuyu: Tũrĩ kũu mũnene ũcio wa thigari igana rĩmwe akĩona marikabu ya Alekisanderia yathiiaga Italia, nake agĩtũhaicia yo.
english: There the centurion found a ship of Alexandria sailing for Italy, and he put us on board.

kikuyu: rĩrĩa rũkũngũ rwatondora, nayo ndoro ĩkooma ĩkanyiitana?
english: when the dust runs into a mass, and the clods of earth stick together?

kikuyu: Inyuĩ arĩa mũtũũraga ndwere-inĩ cia iria, o inyuĩ Akerethi, kaĩ mũrĩ na haaro-ĩ!
english: those who worship the army of the sky on the housetops, those who worship and swear by Yahweh and also swear by Malcam,

kikuyu: Riitho rĩaku rĩa ũrĩo rĩngĩtũma wĩhie-rĩ, rĩkũũre, ũrĩte. Nĩ kaba kĩĩga kĩmwe kĩa mwĩrĩ waku kĩũre gũkĩra mwĩrĩ waku wothe ũikio Jehanamu. Na guoko gwaku kwa ũrĩo kũngĩtũma wĩhie-rĩ, gũtinie ũgũte. Nĩ kaba kĩĩga kĩmwe kĩa mwĩrĩ waku kĩũre gũkĩra mwĩrĩ waku wothe ũikio Jehanamu. Gũtharia Ũhiki
english: If your right eye causes you to stumble, pluck it out and throw it away from you. For it is more profitable for you that one of your members should perish than for your whole body to be cast into Gehenna.§

kikuyu: Andũ a Arivadi na Heleki nĩo maarangagĩra thingo ciaku mĩena yothe; nao andũ a Gamadi nĩo maakoragwo marĩ mĩthiringo-inĩ yaku ĩrĩa mĩraihu na igũrũ. Maacuuragia ngo ciao gũthiũrũrũkĩria thingo ciaku; magĩtũma ũthaka waku ũkinyanĩre.
english: The men of Arvad with your army were on your walls all around, and valiant men were in your towers. They hung their shields on your walls all around. They have perfected your beauty.

kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa mũnogu nĩamũheaga hinya, nake ũrĩa ũũrĩtwo nĩ hinya akamuongerera ũhoti.
english: He gives power to the weak. He increases the strength of him who has no might.

kikuyu: Ũreka atĩa gũkũ na nũũ ũgwĩtĩkĩrĩtie kwĩyenjera mbĩrĩra gũkũ, ũgetemera mbĩrĩra handũ hatũũgĩru, na ũkeyacũhĩria harĩa ũkaahurũkio rwaro-inĩ rwa ihiga?
english: Take a harp; go about the city, you prostitute that has been forgotten. Make sweet melody. Sing many songs, that you may be remembered.

kikuyu: “Kuuma o kĩambĩrĩria-rĩ, ũrĩ waathana atĩ rũciinĩ rũgĩe ho, kana ũkĩonereria ũtheri wa rũciinĩ harĩa ũkuumĩrĩra,
english: “Have you commanded the morning in your days, and caused the dawn to know its place,

kikuyu: Mũthenya ũmwe ta thaa kenda cia mĩaraho-rĩ, akĩona kĩoneki. Akĩona mũraika wa Ngai wega, ũrĩa wokire harĩ we na akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Korinelio!”
english: At about the ninth hour of the day,† he clearly saw in a vision an angel of God coming to him and saying to him, “Cornelius!”

kikuyu: Thĩinĩ wa nyũmba ya Baba kũrĩ na ciikaro nyingĩ; gũtangĩrĩ ũguo ingĩrĩ kũmwĩra. Nĩngũthiĩ kuo kũmũthondekera handũ. Na ndathiĩ na ndamũthondekera handũ-rĩ, nĩngacooka ndĩmũgĩĩre nĩgeetha o na inyuĩ mũkoragwo harĩa ndĩ.
english: In my Father’s house are many homes. If it weren’t so, I would have told you. I am going to prepare a place for you.

kikuyu: Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe ekũũria atĩrĩ, “Mwanake atĩĩaga ithe, nayo ndungata ĩgatĩĩa mũmĩathi. Angĩkorwo ndĩ mũciari-rĩ, gĩtĩĩo kĩrĩa kĩnjagĩrĩire gĩkĩrĩ ha? Angĩkorwo ndĩ mwathani-rĩ, gwĩtigĩrwo kũrĩa kũnjagĩrĩire gũkĩrĩ ha? Inyuĩ, athĩnjĩri-Ngai, nĩinyuĩ mũmenagĩrĩria rĩĩtwa rĩakwa. “No mũũragia atĩrĩ, ‘Tũmenagĩrĩria rĩĩtwa rĩaku atĩa?’
english: “A son honors his father, and a servant his master. If I am a father, then where is my honor? And if I am a master, where is the respect due me?” says Yahweh of Armies to you priests who despise my name. “You say, ‘How have we despised your name?’

kikuyu: Agĩthoma ibuku rĩu aanĩrĩire kuuma rũciinĩ nginya mĩaraho, angʼetheire kĩhaaro kĩrĩa kĩarĩ mbere ya Kĩhingo kĩa Maaĩ, arĩ mbere ya arũme, na andũ- a-nja, na andũ arĩa angĩ mangĩahotire gũtaũkĩrwo nĩ ũhoro ũcio. Nao andũ othe magĩtega matũ, magĩthikĩrĩria ciugo cia Ibuku rĩa Watho. * 7:70 nĩ ta kilo inyanya na nuthu (8.5) † 7:71 nĩ ta kilo
english: He read from it before the wide place that was in front of the water gate from early morning until midday, in the presence of the men and the women, and of those who could understand. The ears of all the people were attentive to the book of the law.

kikuyu: Wee ndũrĩ na handũ, o na ndũrĩ na igai ũtungata-inĩ ũyũ, tondũ ngoro yaku ti njagĩrĩru mbere ya Ngai.
english: You have neither part nor lot in this matter, for your heart isn’t right before God.

kikuyu: Ngoro o yothe nĩyũũĩ ũrũrũ wayo, o na gĩkeno-inĩ kĩayo gũtirĩ mũndũ ũngĩnyiitanĩra nayo.
english: There is stern discipline for one who forsakes the way. Whoever hates reproof shall die.

kikuyu: Mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai ũcio oe rũkũ rwa mũtarakwa, na mũthobi, na guoya wa rangi wa gakarakũ, aciikie igũrũ wa moori ĩyo ĩrahĩa.
english: The priest shall take cedar wood, hyssop, and scarlet, and cast it into the middle of the burning of the heifer.

kikuyu: Aikia a mĩguĩ nĩmamũtharĩkĩire marĩ na marũrũ; makĩmũikĩria mĩguĩ marĩ na ũũru mũingĩ. No ũta wake ndwenyenyekire, moko make marĩ hinya magĩikara merũmĩtie nĩ ũndũ wa guoko kwa Mwene-Hinya wa Jakubu, na nĩ ũndũ wa Mũrĩithi, o we Ihiga rĩa Isiraeli,
english: Joseph saw Ephraim’s children to the third generation. The children also of Machir, the son of Manasseh, were born on Joseph’s knees.

kikuyu: nĩ ũndũ wa riiri waku. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio ngĩgũikia thĩ; ndaagũtuire kĩnyararo ũrĩ mbere ya athamaki. Nĩ ũndũ wa mehia maku maingĩ na wonjoria ũtarĩ wa ma, nĩũthaahĩtie kũndũ gwaku kũrĩa-gũtheru. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio ngĩtũma mwaki uume thĩinĩ waku, naguo ũgĩkũniina, na ngĩgũtua mũhu hau thĩ, maitho-inĩ ma arĩa meeroragĩra.
english: By the multitude of your iniquities, in the unrighteousness of your commerce, you have profaned your sanctuaries. Therefore I have brought out a fire from the middle of you. It has devoured you. I have turned you to ashes on the earth in the sight of all those who see you.

kikuyu: Meere atĩrĩ, ‘Mwathani Jehova ekuuga atĩrĩ: Nderi nene ĩrĩ na mathagu marĩ hinya, na njoya ndaaya, na macoya ma marangi maingĩ nĩyokire Lebanoni. Ĩkĩnyiita gacũmbĩrĩ ka mũtarakwa,
english: and say, ‘The Lord Yahweh says: “A great eagle with great wings and long feathers, full of feathers which had various colors, came to Lebanon and took the top of the cedar.

kikuyu: Akĩona rũkambucu rwa ndigiri rũtaarĩ rũkũrũ, akĩrũhuria na akĩũraga andũ ngiri ĩmwe.
english: He found a fresh jawbone of a donkey, put out his hand, took it, and struck a thousand men with it.

kikuyu: “Na rĩu nĩnjũũĩ gũtirĩ o na ũmwe thĩinĩ wanyu wa andũ arĩa ndanathiĩ ngĩhunjagĩria ũhoro wa ũthamaki ũgacooka kũnyona rĩngĩ. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, ngũmwĩra ũmũthĩ atĩ gũtirĩ thakame ya mũndũ o na ũmwe ngoorio. Nĩ ũndũ niĩ ndirĩ ndatithia kũmũhunjĩria wendi wa Ngai ũrĩ wothe.
english: whom he gathered together with the workmen of like occupation, and said, “Sirs, you know that by this business we have our wealth.

kikuyu: Ezara akĩhingũra ibuku rĩu. Andũ othe nĩmamuonaga tondũ aarũgamĩte handũ hatũũgĩru. Na rĩrĩa aahingũrire ibuku, andũ othe makĩrũgama na igũrũ.
english: Whoever desires to go into the sea to look at it, or to rule it, if he didn’t go through the narrow entrance, how could he come into the broad part?

kikuyu: Kũrathimwo-rĩ, nĩ mũndũ ũrĩa Mwathani atatuaga mwĩhia.”
english: Blessed is the man whom the Lord will by no means charge with sin.”*

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa ndaciirithirio rĩa mbere-rĩ, gũtirĩ mũndũ wokire kũndeithia, andũ othe nĩmandi- ganĩirie. Maroaga gũtuĩrwo ũũru. No Mwathani nĩarũgamire na niĩ na akĩnjĩkĩra hinya, nĩgeetha ndũmĩrĩri ĩhunjio biũ nĩ ũndũ wakwa na andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ othe mamĩigue. Na niĩ ngĩhonokio kuuma kanua ka mũrũũthi.
english: He alone has immortality, dwelling in unapproachable light, whom no man has seen nor can see, to whom be honor and eternal power. Amen.

kikuyu: “Na o ta ũrĩa ndeera Firaũni: Ngai nĩ oneetie Firaũni ũrĩa akiriĩ gwĩka.
english: That is the thing which I have spoken to Pharaoh. God has shown Pharaoh what he is about to do.

kikuyu: Mwendia ndagakũũra gĩthaka kĩrĩa endetie rĩrĩa rĩothe acio eerĩ marĩ muoyo, nĩgũkorwo kĩoneki kĩu gĩkoniĩ kĩrĩndĩ kĩu gĩothe gĩtikagarũrwo. Tondũ wa mehia mao, gũtirĩ o na ũmwe wao ũgaatũũria muoyo wake.
english: He said also to me, “You will again see more of the great abominations which they do.”

kikuyu: NaoatongoriaaAlawiimaarĩHashabia, naSherebia, na Jeshua mũrũ wa Kadimieli, na athiritũ ao, arĩa maarũgamaga mamangʼetheire makĩina nyĩmbo cia kũgooca na cia gũcookia ngaatho, gĩkundi kĩmwe gĩkaamũkagĩria kĩrĩa kĩngĩ, kũringana na ũrĩa gwathanĩtwo nĩ Daudi mũndũ wa Ngai.
english: The chiefs of the Levites: Hashabiah, Sherebiah, and Jeshua the son of Kadmiel, with their brothers close to them, to praise and give thanks according to the commandment of David the man of God, section next to section.

kikuyu: “Nĩmũiguĩte atĩ hĩndĩ ya tene nĩ kwerirwo atĩrĩ, ‘Endaga mũndũ wa itũũra rĩaku, na ũthũũrage thũ yaku.’ No niĩ ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ: Endagai thũ cianyu, na mũhooyagĩre arĩa mamũnyariiraga,
english: “You have heard that it was said, ‘You shall love your neighbor * and hate your enemy.’‡

kikuyu: Angĩkorwo tũtũire muoyo nĩ ũndũ wa Roho-rĩ, kĩrekei tũtongoragio nĩ Roho.
english: If we live by the Spirit, let’s also walk by the Spirit.

kikuyu: Ũũ nĩguo Jehova ekuuga, “Nĩngũũkĩrĩra anabii acio maaragia ndeto ciao ene, na magacooka makoiga atĩrĩ, ‘Ũguo nĩguo Jehova oiga.’
english: Behold, I am against the prophets,” says Yahweh, “who use their tongues, and say, ‘He says.’

kikuyu: “Kaĩ mũrĩ na haaro inyuĩ Afarisai-ĩ! Tondũ mũrutagĩra Ngai gĩcunjĩ gĩa ikũmi gĩa terere, na togotia na nyeni cia mĩthemba yothe no mũkaaga kũrũmbũiya kĩhooto na wendani wa Ngai. Mwaagĩrĩirwo nĩ gwĩkaga maũndũ macio mũtegũtiganĩria macio mangĩ ma mbere.
english: But woe to you Pharisees! For you tithe mint and rue and every herb, but you bypass justice and God’s love. You ought to have done these, and not to have left the other undone.

kikuyu: Kũrathimwo na gũthera nĩ arĩa magwatanĩire na kũriũka kũu kwa mbere. Gĩkuũ gĩa keerĩ gĩtirĩ na hinya igũrũ rĩao, no magaatuĩka athĩnjĩri-Ngai o na Kristũ, na magaathamaka hamwe nake mĩaka ngiri ĩmwe. Shaitani Gũtuĩrwo Ciira Rĩrĩa mĩaka ĩyo ngiri ĩgaathira, Shaitani nĩkuohorwo akoohorwo oime njeera ĩyo yake,
english: He said to me, “I am the Alpha and the Omega, the Beginning and the End. I will give freely to him who is thirsty from the spring of the water of life.  TR and NU omit “who deceives the whole inhabited earth”.  or, Hell  or,

kikuyu: Nowe Jakubu, agĩthiĩ Sukothu, kũrĩa eyakĩire nyũmba, na agĩthondeka ciugũ cia mahiũ make. Nĩkĩo handũ hau hetagwo Sukothu.
english: But if you will not listen to us and be circumcised, then we will take our sister,† and we will be gone.”

kikuyu: Njĩra ĩrĩa agookĩra no yo agaacookera; ndagatoonya itũũra rĩĩrĩ inene,” ũguo nĩguo Jehova ekuuga.
english: He will return the way that he came, and he won’t come to this city,’ says Yahweh.

kikuyu: “ ‘Angĩkorwo mũndũ nĩakamũrĩra Jehova gĩcunjĩ kĩa mũgũnda wa nyũmba yao, thogora waguo ũgaatuuokũringananambeũiriaingĩhaandwo, nacionĩcekerimĩrongoĩtanociabetha, iriaarĩkĩbaba * 27:3 nĩ ta nuthu kilo (0.5) † 27:3 nĩ ta giramu ikũmi na ĩmwe na nuthu (11:5) ‡ 27:4 nĩ ta giramu
english: I also will do this to you: I will appoint terror over you, even consumption and fever, that shall consume the eyes, and make the soul to pine away. You will sow your seed in vain, for your enemies will eat it.

kikuyu: Musa agĩcooka akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Ũũ nĩguo Jehova aathanĩte mwĩke, nĩguo riiri wa Jehova ũmuumĩrĩre.”
english: Moses said, “This is the thing which Yahweh commanded that you should do; and Yahweh’s glory shall appear to you.”

kikuyu: Ngoro yakwa ĩrĩ hamwe na anene a Isiraeli, o hamwe na andũ arĩa merutĩire na kwĩyendera gatagatĩ-inĩ ka andũ. Goocai Jehova.
english: I delivered you out of the hand of the Egyptians and out of the hand of all who oppressed you, and drove them out from before you, and gave you their land.

kikuyu: Andũ a Isiraeli, hamwe na Alawii, nĩmarĩĩrehaga maruta mao ma ngano, na ndibei ya mũhihano, na maguta thĩinĩ wa makũmbĩ marĩa indo cia handũ-harĩa-haamũre ciigagwo, na kũrĩa athĩnjĩri-Ngai, arĩa maratungata, na aikaria a ihingo, na aini maikaraga. “Tũtirĩrekagĩrĩria nyũmba ya Ngai witũ.”
english: For the children of Israel and the children of Levi shall bring the wave offering of the grain, of the new wine, and of the oil, to the rooms where the vessels of the sanctuary are, and the priests who minister, with the gatekeepers and the singers. We will not forsake the house of our God.

kikuyu: ũrĩa eekĩrire thĩinĩ wa Kristũ rĩrĩa aamũriũkirie kuuma kũrĩ arĩa akuũ, na akĩmũiga guoko-inĩ gwake kwa ũrĩo kũu igũrũ,
english: which he worked in Christ when he raised him from the dead and made him to sit at his right hand in the heavenly places,

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo ũndũ ũrĩa watho wa Musa ũtangĩahotire gwĩka nĩ ũndũ wa kwagithio hinya nĩ mwĩrĩ ũyũ wĩhagia-rĩ, Ngai nĩekire ũndũ ũcio na ũndũ wa gũtũma Mũriũ wake mwene arĩ na mwĩrĩ ũhaanaine na mwĩrĩ ũyũ wĩhagia, nĩgeetha atuĩke igongona rĩa kũhoroheria mehia. Na nĩ ũndũ ũcio agĩtuĩra wĩhia ũrĩa warĩ thĩinĩ wa mũndũ ũrĩa wĩhagia ciira,
english: For what the law couldn’t do, in that it was weak through the flesh, God did, sending his own Son in the likeness of sinful flesh and for sin, he condemned sin in the flesh,

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo makaarĩĩo nĩ memenyi mathire biũ o ta nguo; na marĩĩo nĩ igunyũ o ta marĩ guoya wa ngʼondu. No rĩrĩ, ũhoro wakwa wa ũthingu nĩ wa gũtũũra nginya tene, naguo ũhonokio wakwa ũtũũre nginya njiarwa-inĩ ciothe.”
english: For the moth will eat them up like a garment, and the worm will eat them like wool; but my righteousness will be forever, and my salvation to all generations.”

kikuyu: “Mũtikanahutie andũ arĩa akwa aitĩrĩrie maguta; mũtikaneke anabii akwa ũũru.”
english: “Don’t touch my anointed ones! Do my prophets no harm!”

kikuyu: Jehova ekuuga atĩrĩ, “O na kũrĩ ũguo, matukũ nĩmarooka rĩrĩa andũ matagacooka kuuga atĩrĩ, ‘Ti- itherũ o ta ũrĩa Jehova atũũraga muoyo, ũrĩa warutire andũ a Isiraeli kuuma bũrũri wa Misiri,’
english: I will make them to pass with your enemies into a land which you don’t know; for a fire is kindled in my anger, which will burn on you.”

kikuyu: “Kĩu nĩkĩo kĩroto kĩrĩa warootire, na rĩu nĩtũgũgĩtaũrĩra mũthamaki.
english: “This is the dream; and we will tell its interpretation before the king.

kikuyu: Akĩmataara, akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Ũũ nĩguo mũgũthiĩ kwĩra mwathi wakwa Esaũ: ‘Ndungata yaku Jakubu ĩkuuga atĩrĩ, ndĩraikaraga na Labani, na ngoretwo kuo nginya rĩu.
english: He commanded them, saying, “This is what you shall tell my lord, Esau: ‘This is what your servant, Jacob, says. I have lived as a foreigner with Laban, and stayed until now.

kikuyu: Nĩo marĩkuuaga itambaya cia gũcuurio cia Hema-ĩrĩa-Nyamũre, na Hema-ya-Gũtũnganwo, na indo cia kũmĩhumbĩra, o na gĩthii kĩa njũũa cia pomboo cia kũmĩhumbĩra na igũrũ, na itambaya cia gũcuurio cia itoonyero rĩa Hema-ya-Gũtũnganwo,
english: *Now after all these acts of Josias, it came to pass that Pharaoh the king of Egypt came to make war at Carchemish on the Euphrates; and Josias went out against him.

kikuyu: Nĩngamũcookanĩrĩria na ndĩmũhurutĩre na mangʼũrĩ makwa mahiũ, na inyuĩ nĩmũgatwekerio o kũu thĩinĩ warĩo.
english: Yes, I will gather you, and blow on you with the fire of my wrath, and you will be melted in the middle of it.

kikuyu: Moko ma andũ-a-nja arĩa marĩ tha nĩmarugire ciana ciao ene, igĩtuĩka irio ciao hĩndĩ ĩyo andũ akwa manangirwo.
english: The hands of the pitiful women have boiled their own children. They were their food in the destruction of the daughter of my people.

kikuyu: Bũrũri ũcio wothe warĩ hamwe na matũũra mothe na tũtũũra twamo tũrĩa Efiraimu maagaĩirwo thĩinĩ wa igai rĩa andũ a mũhĩrĩga wa Manase. No andũ a mũhĩrĩga wa Efiraimu matiigana kũrutũrũra andũ a Kaanani arĩa maatũũraga Gezeri; nginya ũmũthĩ andũ a Kaanani matũũranagia hamwe na andũ a Efiraimu, nao magĩtuĩka ndungata marutage wĩra o ũrĩa mangĩathwo marute.
english: The border went down to the brook of Kanah, southward of the brook. These cities belonged to Ephraim among the cities of Manasseh. The border of Manasseh was on the north side of the brook, and ended at the sea.

kikuyu: Ndagacooka gũkorwo arĩ mũtongu, naguo ũtonga wake ndũgatũũra, o na kana indo ciake itheereme kũu bũrũri-inĩ.
english: He will not be rich, neither will his substance continue, neither will their possessions be extended on the earth.

kikuyu: O igai rĩkaheanwo na ũndũ wa gũcuuka mĩtĩ kũrĩ ikundi iria nene na iria nini.”
english: According to the lot shall their inheritance be divided between the more and the fewer.”

kikuyu: Wee Ngai, gĩkũũre andũ a Isiraeli, moime mathĩĩna-inĩ mao mothe!
english: God, redeem Israel out of all his troubles.

kikuyu: Goocai Jehova, inyuothe ndungata cia Jehova, inyuĩ mũtungataga mũrĩ nyũmba-inĩ ya Jehova ũtukũ.
english: Look! Praise Yahweh, all you servants of Yahweh, who stand by night in Yahweh’s house!

kikuyu: Mũthamaki wakuo nĩagatahwo, athaamio, we mwene, hamwe na anene ake,” ũguo nĩguo Jehova ekuuga.
english: and their king will go into captivity, he and his princes together,” says Yahweh.

kikuyu: Kũrigania nao, Zadoku mũrũ wa Imeri agĩcookereria rũthingo kũngʼethera nyũmba yake. Kũrigania nake, Shemaia mũrũ wa Shekania, mũikaria wa Kĩhingo kĩa Irathĩro, nĩwe wacookereirie rũthingo rũrĩa rwarũmĩrĩire.
english: After them, Zadok the son of Immer made repairs across from his own house. After him, Shemaiah the son of Shecaniah, the keeper of the east gate, made repairs.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa mũndũ ũcio andora, onaga ũrĩa wandũmire.
english: He who sees me sees him who sent me.

kikuyu: Andũ arĩa maikaraga kĩhingo-inĩ gĩa itũũra nĩ niĩ manyũrũragia, na nduĩkĩte rwĩmbo rwa arĩĩu.
english: God, don’t be far from me. My God, hurry to help me.

kikuyu: Maitho maku makoonaga maũndũ mageni, na meciiria maku meciirie maũndũ ma waganu.
english: Your eyes will see strange things, and your mind will imagine confusing things.

kikuyu: Makĩriganĩrwo nĩ Mũrungu ũrĩa wamahonokirie, ũrĩa wekĩte maũndũ manene kũu bũrũri wa Misiri,
english: They forgot God, their Savior, who had done great things in Egypt,

kikuyu: “ ‘Ta rorai, inyuĩ anyũrũrania, gegaai na mũthire, nĩgũkorwo nĩngwĩka ũndũ matukũ-inĩ manyu, ũrĩa mũtangĩtĩkia, o na mũngĩĩrwo nĩ mũndũ.’ ” Rĩrĩa Paũlũ na Baranaba moimaga thunagogi, andũ makĩmeera moke mũthenya ũrĩa warũmĩrĩire wa Thabatũ nĩguo mathiĩ na mbere kwaria ũhoro ũcio.
english: ‘Behold, you scoffers! Wonder and perish, for I work a work in your days, a work which you will in no way believe, if one declares it to you.’ ”*

kikuyu: Andũ a Babuloni makiunanga itugĩ cia gĩcango, na metha cia kũigĩrĩrwo indo, na Karia karĩa kaarĩ ga gĩcango, iria ciarĩ thĩinĩ wa hekarũ ya Jehova, magĩkuua gĩcango kĩu gĩothe magĩgĩtwara Babuloni. Ningĩ magĩkuua nyũngũ, na icakũri, na magathĩ ma gũtinia ndaambĩ, na mbakũri cia kũminjaminjĩria maaĩ, na thaani, o na indo ciothe cia gĩcango iria ciahũthagĩrwo wĩra-inĩ wa hekarũ, magĩthiĩ nacio.
english: The Chaldeans broke the pillars of bronze that were in Yahweh’s house and the bases and the bronze sea that were in Yahweh’s house in pieces, and carried all of their bronze to Babylon.

kikuyu: ningĩ athamaki othe a Turo na Sidoni; o na athamaki a icigĩrĩra iria irĩ mũrĩmo wa iria;
english: and all the kings of Tyre, all the kings of Sidon, and the kings of the isle which is beyond the sea;

kikuyu: Inyuĩ athuuri-rĩ, endagai atumia anyu, o ta ũrĩa Kristũ eendire kanitha na akĩĩruta nĩ ũndũ waguo
english: Husbands, love your wives, even as Christ also loved the assembly and gave himself up for her,

kikuyu: “Atĩrĩrĩ, inyuĩ andũ a Jakubu, ti‑itherũ nĩngamũcookanĩrĩria, inyuothe, o na inyuĩ matigari ma Isiraeli nĩngamũrehe hamwe. Ngamarehe hamwe ta ngʼondu irĩ kiugũ, matuĩke ta rũũru rwa mbũri rũrĩ ũrĩithio-inĩ waruo; nakĩo kĩrĩndĩ kĩa andũ kĩiyũre kuo.
english: I will surely assemble all of you, Jacob. I will surely gather the remnant of Israel. I will put them together as the sheep of Bozrah, as a flock in the middle of their pasture. They will swarm with people.

kikuyu: Esaũ aakinyia mĩaka mĩrongo ĩna, akĩhikia Judithi mwarĩ wa Beeri ũrĩa Mũhiti, o na ningĩ akĩhikia Basemathu mwarĩ wa Eloni ũrĩa Mũhiti. Nao magĩtuĩka kĩhumo gĩa kĩeha kũrĩ Isaaka na Rebeka.
english: When Esau was forty years old, he took as wife Judith, the daughter of Beeri the Hittite, and Basemath, the daughter of Elon the Hittite.

kikuyu: Kũu kĩrĩma-igũrũ gĩa Isiraeli nĩkuo ngakahaanda; nĩgakaruta honge na gaciare maciaro, naguo nĩũgatuĩka mũtarakwa ũrĩ na riiri. Nyoni cia mĩthemba yothe nĩigaaka itara ciacio honge-inĩ ciaguo; nĩigatũũra kĩĩruru-inĩ kĩa honge ciaguo.
english: I will plant it in the mountain of the height of Israel; and it will produce boughs, and bear fruit, and be a good cedar. Birds of every kind will dwell in the shade of its branches.

kikuyu: Adamu aakinyia mĩaka igana na mĩrongo ĩtatũ-rĩ, akĩgĩa na mũriũ wamũtũkĩtie na wa mũhianĩre wake; nake akĩmũtua Sethi. Na thuutha wa Sethi gũciarwo-rĩ, Adamu agĩtũũra muoyo mĩaka ĩngĩ magana manana, na akĩgĩa na ciana ingĩ cia aanake na cia airĩtu.
english: Adam lived one hundred thirty years, and became the father of a son in his own likeness, after his image, and named him Seth.

kikuyu: Ũrĩa ũroimbũra maũndũ macio ekuuga atĩrĩ, “Ĩĩ nĩguo, ngũũka o narua.” Ameni. Ũka, Mwathani Jesũ!
english: He who testifies these things says, “Yes, I am coming soon.” Amen! Yes, come, Lord Jesus!

kikuyu: Nĩtwambararie ngoro ciitũ na moko maitũ na igũrũ kũrĩ Mũrungu ũrĩa ũrĩ igũrũ, tuuge atĩrĩ:
english: Let’s lift up our heart with our hands to God† in the heavens.

kikuyu: Nethaneli mũrũ wa Zuaru nĩwe warĩ mũtongoria wa gĩkundi kĩa mũhĩrĩga wa Isakaru, 16nake Eliabu mũrũ wa Heloni nĩwe warĩ mũtongoria wa gĩkundi kĩa mũhĩrĩga wa Zebuluni. Ningĩ Hema-ĩrĩa-Nyamũre ĩkĩambũrwo, nao Agerishoni na Amerari, arĩa maamĩkuuaga, makiumagara.
english: On the day that the tabernacle was raised up, the cloud covered the tabernacle, even the Tent of the Testimony. At evening it was over the tabernacle, as it were the appearance of fire, until morning.

kikuyu: “Ngũmũhe rĩathani rĩerũ: Endanagai mũndũ na ũrĩa ũngĩ. O ta ũrĩa ndĩmwendete, o na inyuĩ no nginya mwendanage mũndũ na ũrĩa ũngĩ.
english: A new commandment I give to you, that you love one another. Just as I have loved you, you also love one another.

kikuyu: Mũndũ o wothe wĩkaga ũguo-rĩ, arĩ ũ kana ũ, Jehova aromweheria hema-inĩ cia Jakubu, o na angĩkorwo nĩarehage maruta make kũrĩ Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe.
english: Yahweh will cut off the man who does this, him who wakes and him who answers, out of the tents of Jacob and him who offers an offering to Yahweh of Armies.

kikuyu: Weka ũguo-rĩ, nĩũkũmĩigĩrĩra makara ma mwaki mũtwe, nake Jehova nĩagakũrĩha.
english: for you will heap coals of fire on his head, and Yahweh will reward you.

kikuyu: Magĩtunyana itũũrarĩu, namũthamakiwarĩo, natũtũũratwarĩo, makĩmaniinanarũhiũrwanjora. Makĩanangaarĩa maarĩ thĩinĩ warĩo biũ. Matiatigirie mũndũ o na ũmwe arĩ muoyo. Nao magĩĩka Debiri na mũthamaki warĩo o ta ũrĩa meekĩte Libina na mũthamaki warĩo, na noguo meekĩte Hebironi. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Joshua agĩtooria mwena ũcio wothe, naguo bũrũri ũrĩa ũrĩ irĩma, o na Negevu, na ituamba-inĩ cia irĩma cia mwena wa ithũĩro, o na kũu ciikũrũko-inĩ cia irĩma, hamwe na athamaki othe akuo. Ndaatigirie mũndũ o na ũmwe arĩ muoyo. Nĩanangire arĩa othe maarĩ na mĩhũmũ, o ta ũrĩa Jehova, Ngai wa Isiraeli aathanĩte.
english: He took it, with its king and all its cities. They struck them with the edge of the sword, and utterly destroyed all the souls who were in it. He left no one remaining. As he had done to Hebron, so he did to Debir, and to its king; as he had done also to Libnah, and to its king.

kikuyu: Mũthuuriwe nĩagatuuo ndarĩ na ihĩtia o na rĩrĩkũ, no mũtumia ũcio nĩwe ũgaacookererwo nĩ mehia make.’ ” Nake Jehova akĩĩra Musa atĩrĩ,
english: The man shall be free from iniquity, and that woman shall bear her iniquity.’ ”

kikuyu: Wee Jehova, nĩ harĩwe njũrĩire; ndũkanareke njonorithio; nĩ ũndũ wa ũthingu waku-rĩ, ndeithũra.
english: In you, Yahweh, I take refuge. Let me never be disappointed. Deliver me in your righteousness.

kikuyu: Ta thikĩrĩriai! Nĩtũrakinyĩrio ũhoro, tũkeerwo atĩrĩ, kũrĩ na gacagaca nene kuuma bũrũri wa mwena wa gathigathini! Gacagaca ĩyo nĩĩgatũma matũũra ma Juda makire ihooru, ĩtũme matuĩke ma gũtũũragwo nĩ mbwe. Ihooya rĩa Jeremia
english: The voice of news, behold, it comes, and a great commotion out of the north country, to make the cities of Judah a desolation, a dwelling place of jackals.

kikuyu: kuuma mũhĩrĩga wa Gadi, aarĩ Geueli mũrũ wa Maki.
english: Now if you killed this people as one man, then the nations which have heard the fame of you will speak, saying,

kikuyu: no taguo mũndũ ahaana ũrĩa ũheenagia mũndũ wa itũũra rĩake, agacooka kũmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Nĩ itherũ nyuma narĩo!”
english: is the man who deceives his neighbor and says, “Am I not joking?”

kikuyu: Ũndũ ũyũ nĩũtũmaga nyone atĩ watho ũyũ nĩguo ũrutaga wĩra thĩinĩ wakwa: Rĩrĩa ngwenda gwĩka wega-rĩ, ũũru ũkoragwo o hamwe na niĩ.
english: I find then the law that, while I desire to do good, evil is present.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo Zedekia mũthamaki wa Juda nĩamuohithĩtie kũu, akoiga atĩrĩ, “Ũtũrathagĩra ũguo nĩkĩ? uugaga atĩrĩ, ‘Jehova ekuuga atĩrĩ: Ndĩ hakuhĩ kũneana itũũra rĩĩrĩ inene kũrĩ mũthamaki wa Babuloni, nake nĩakarĩĩnyitĩra.
english: For Zedekiah king of Judah had shut him up, saying, “Why do you prophesy, and say, ‘Yahweh says, “Behold, I will give this city into the hand of the king of Babylon, and he will take it;

kikuyu: Thenge ĩyo nĩĩgakuuithio mehia mao mothe, ĩmatware kũndũ kũrĩa gũtatũũragwo nĩ Andũ; nake mũndũ ũcio nĩakarekereria thenge ĩyo na kũu werũ-inĩ.
english: The goat shall carry all their iniquities on himself to a solitary land, and he shall release the goat in the wilderness.

kikuyu: mĩĩhĩtwa ĩrĩa mĩromo yakwa yeranĩire, na ngĩmĩaria na kanua gakwa, rĩrĩa ndaarĩ thĩĩna-inĩ.
english: which my lips promised, and my mouth spoke, when I was in distress.

kikuyu: Mwathani ekuuga atĩrĩ, “Nĩngaruta thũ cianyu kuuma Bashani; ndĩcirute iria-inĩ kũrĩa kũriku,
english: The Lord said, “I will bring you again from Bashan, I will bring you again from the depths of the sea,

kikuyu: Mũrũ wa Mũndũ egũthiĩ o ta ũrĩa ũhoro wake wandĩkĩtwo. No mũndũ ũrĩa ũgaakunyanĩra Mũrũ wa Mũndũ kaĩ arĩ na haaro-ĩ! Nĩ kaba mũndũ ũcio atangĩaciarirwo.” Ningĩ o makĩrĩĩaga-rĩ, Jesũ akĩoya mũgate, agĩcookia ngaatho, agĩcooka akĩwenyũranga, akĩhe arutwo ake, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Oyai; ũyũ nĩguo mwĩrĩ wakwa.”
english: They compelled one passing by, coming from the country, Simon of Cyrene, the father of Alexander and Rufus, to go with them that he might bear his cross.

kikuyu: Ndeithũra na ũũhonokie nĩ ũndũ wa ũthingu waku; thikĩrĩria ũũhonokie.
english: Deliver me in your righteousness, and rescue me. Turn your ear to me, and save me.

kikuyu: Tũhonokie na ũtũteithie na guoko gwaku kwa ũrĩo, nĩguo arĩa wendete mahonoke.
english: So that your beloved may be delivered, save with your right hand, and answer us.

kikuyu: Mũtikanatoorio nĩ ũũru, no tooragiai ũũru na wega.
english: Don’t be overcome by evil, but overcome evil with good.

kikuyu: Mũrĩhaandaga no mũtikaagetha; mũrĩĩhihaga ndamaiyũ no mũtikehaka maguta ma cio; mũrĩĩhihaga thabibũ no mũtikanyua ndibei yacio.
english: You will sow, but won’t reap. You will tread the olives, but won’t anoint yourself with oil; and crush grapes, but won’t drink the wine.

kikuyu: 1 Ũrĩa ndĩroiga nĩ atĩrĩ, ihinda rĩothe rĩrĩa mũgai arĩ o mwana-rĩ, ndarĩ ũtiganu na ngombo, o na atuĩkanĩwemweneindoiciociothe.
english: But when the fullness of the time came, God sent out his Son, born to a woman, born under the law,

kikuyu: Ũũ nĩguo Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe ekuuga: “Atĩrĩrĩ, andũ nĩmarekwo mahaare matigari ma Isiraeli mahinyĩtie, o ta ũrĩa mũthabibũ ũhaaragwo; cookerithia guoko honge-inĩ rĩngĩ, o ta ũrĩa mũcookereria wa thabibũ ekaga.”
english: Yahweh of Armies says, “They will thoroughly glean the remnant of Israel like a vine. Turn again your hand as a grape gatherer into the baskets.”

kikuyu: Anabii akuo mamahakagĩra ciĩko icio coka mwerũ, na njĩra ya kũmoonera cioneki cia maheeni, na ũragũri wa maheeni. Moigaga atĩrĩ, ‘Mwathani Jehova ekuuga ũũ’, o rĩrĩa Jehova atarĩ ũndũ oigĩte.
english: Her prophets have plastered for them with whitewash, seeing false visions, and divining lies to them, saying, ‘The Lord Yahweh says,’ when Yahweh has not spoken.

kikuyu: Nĩmũkaheanaga iruta rĩrĩ rĩa mũtu ũrĩa mũgathĩa rĩa mbere kũrĩ Jehova njiarwa-inĩ cianyu ciothe iria igooka thuutha. Maruta nĩ ũndũ wa Mehia Matarĩ ma Rũtũrĩko * 15:4 nĩ ta kilo
english: but in very deed—as I live, and as all the earth shall be filled with Yahweh’s glory—

kikuyu: “ ‘Nĩgũkorwo Mwathani Jehova ekuuga atĩrĩ: “ ‘Kaĩ itũũra rĩu inene rĩitithagia thakame rĩrĩ na haro-ĩ, o na nyũngũ ĩyo ĩrĩ na mũcura, ĩrĩa gĩkũrĩ kĩayo gĩtangĩthira! Mĩonorie kĩrungʼo, o kĩrungʼo, itegũcuukĩrwo mĩtĩ.
english: “ ‘Therefore the Lord Yahweh says: “Woe to the bloody city, to the cauldron whose rust is in it, and whose rust hasn’t gone out of it! Take out of it piece after piece without casting lots for it.

kikuyu: mũthamaki wa Jeriko, ũmwe, na mũthamaki wa Ai (hakuhĩ na Betheli), ũmwe,
english: from Aroer, that is on the edge of the valley of the Arnon, and the city that is in the middle of the valley, and all the plain of Medeba to Dibon;

kikuyu: Gũtirĩ mũndũ ũkaanaga Mũriũ ũkĩrĩ na Ithe witũ; ũrĩa wothe uumbũraga Mũriũ arĩ na Ithe o nake.
english: Whoever denies the Son doesn’t have the Father. He who confesses the Son has the Father also.†

kikuyu: Nayo ndungata ĩyo ĩkĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, ‘Mũrũ wa maitũguo nĩokĩte, nake thoguo nĩamũthĩnjĩire njaũ ya gĩcegũ ĩrĩa noru, tondũ nĩamũcookereire arĩ mwega na arĩ mũgima.’
english: He said to him, ‘Your brother has come, and your father has killed the fattened calf, because he has received him back safe and healthy.’

kikuyu: Ngai wa Iburahĩmu, na Ngai wa Nahoru,* o we Ngai wa ithe wao-rĩ, arotũtuithania ciira.” Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Jakubu akĩĩhĩta na mwĩhĩtwa rĩĩtwa-inĩ rĩa ũcio Wĩtigĩrĩtwo nĩ ithe Isaaka.
english: The God of Abraham, and the God of Nahor, the God of their father, judge between us.” Then Jacob swore by the fear of his father, Isaac.

kikuyu: Mũrũ wakwa, ũngĩtĩkĩra ciugo ciakwa, na ũige maathani makwa ta mũthiithũ thĩinĩ waku,
english: The proverbs of Solomon, the son of David, king of Israel:

kikuyu: na ndundu ĩrĩa ĩrĩ rũhĩa, na ndundu ĩrĩa ĩgambaga, na nyoni ya iria-inĩ ĩrĩa ĩrĩ mathagu maraaya, na mũthemba o wothe wa rwĩgĩ,
english: the horned owl, the screech owl, the gull, any kind of hawk,

kikuyu: higo cierĩ hamwe na maguta marĩa macihumbĩire hakuhĩ na honge, na maguta marĩa mahumbĩire ini, amarutanĩrie na higo icio, 10akaaruta maguta macio o ta ũrĩa marutagwo ndegwa ĩrĩa ĩthĩnjagwo ĩrĩ iruta rĩa ũiguano. Nake mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai nĩagacicinĩra kĩgongona-inĩ kĩa igongona rĩa njino,
english: and the two kidneys, and the fat that is on them, which is by the loins, and the cover on the liver, with the kidneys, he shall remove,

kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũngĩhĩtithia andũ arĩa arũngĩrĩru nĩguo magere njĩra ya ũũru, akaagũa mũtego-inĩ wake we mwene, no ũrĩa ũtarĩ ũcuuke, wega nĩguo ũgaatuĩka igai rĩake.
english: Whoever causes the upright to go astray in an evil way, he will fall into his own trap; but the blameless will inherit good.

kikuyu: “ ‘Angĩkorwo ekũruta nĩ ũndũ wa gũcookia ngaatho-rĩ, nĩ arutanĩrie iruta rĩu rĩa gũcookia ngaatho hamwe na tũmĩgate tũrugĩtwo tũtarĩ na ndawa ya kũimbia tũtukanĩtio na maguta na tũmĩgate tũrĩa tũhũthũ tũrugĩtwo tũtarĩ na ndawa ya kũimbia tũrĩ tũhake maguta, na tũmĩgate twa mũtu ũrĩa mũhinyu mũno, ũkimanĩtio wega na ũgatukanio na maguta. Ningĩ hamwe na iruta rĩake rĩa ũiguano rĩa gũcookia ngaatho-rĩ, nĩakarĩrutanĩria na tũmĩgate tũrugĩtwo na ndawa ya kũimbia.
english: The fire on the altar shall be kept burning on it, it shall not go out; and the priest shall burn wood on it every morning. He shall lay the burnt offering in order upon it, and shall burn on it the fat of the peace offerings.

kikuyu: Nĩngamaneana kũrĩ andũ arĩa mendaga kũmaruta muoyo, ndĩmaneane kũrĩ Nebukadinezaru mũthamaki wa Babuloni na anene ake. No rĩrĩ, thuutha-inĩ bũrũri wa Misiri nĩgũgatũũrwo nĩ andũ rĩngĩ o ta tene,” ũguo nĩguo Jehova ekuuga.
english: I will deliver them into the hand of those who seek their lives, and into the hand of Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon, and into the hand of his servants. Afterwards it will be inhabited, as in the days of old,” says Yahweh.

kikuyu: “Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, ũthamaki wa igũrũ ũhaananĩtio na mũthamaki ũrĩa wendaga kuonio mathabu ma mbeeca ciake nĩ ndungata ciake.
english: Therefore the Kingdom of Heaven is like a certain king who wanted to settle accounts with his servants.

kikuyu: Mũtikanariganĩrwo nĩgũtuga ageni, nĩ ũndũ andũ amwe tondũ wa gwĩka ũguo maananyiita araika ũgeni matekũmenya.
english: spies in Jericho (Joshua 2,6) and later became an ancestor of Jesus (

kikuyu: Nayo mbũtũ ĩyo ya thigari na mũnene wayo, hamwe na anene acio a Ayahudi makĩnyiita Jesũ. Makĩmuoha na
english: So the detachment, the commanding officer, and the officers of the Jews seized Jesus and bound him,

kikuyu: Nguo ĩyo, kana ndigi icio njogothe, kana taama mũtume, kana kĩndũ kĩa rũũa kĩrĩa gĩthambĩtio gĩkaniinwo ũgumu ũcio no nginya gĩthambio rĩngĩ, na nĩ gĩgaathirwo nĩ thaahu.”
english: The garment, either the warp, or the woof, or whatever thing of skin it is, which you shall wash, if the plague has departed from them, then it shall be washed the second time, and it will be clean.”

kikuyu: Kũu maaĩ-inĩ maingĩ nĩkuo kwageragĩrio ngano ya Shihoru, namo magetha ma Nili nĩmo maarĩ uumithio wa itũũra rĩa Turo, narĩo rĩgĩtuĩka ndũnyũ ya ndũrĩrĩ.
english: The earth will be utterly emptied and utterly laid waste; for Yahweh has spoken this word.

kikuyu: Mwene nyũmba ĩyo akiuma na nja akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Aca, arata akwa, mũtigeke ũndũ wa waganu ũguo. Kuona atĩ mũndũ ũyũ nĩ mũgeni wakwa-rĩ, tigai gwĩka ũndũ ũyũ wa thoni ũguo.
english: The man, the master of the house, went out to them, and said to them, “No, my brothers, please don’t act so wickedly; since this man has come into my house, don’t do this folly.

kikuyu: Ũmwe wa ageri ngero acio maambanĩirio nake akĩmũruma, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Githĩ wee tiwe Kristũ? Wĩhonokie, na ũtũhonokie!” No mũgeri ngero ũcio ũngĩ akĩmũkaania, akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Ndũngĩĩtigĩra Ngai, kuona atĩ ũtuĩrĩirwo o ũrĩa atuĩrĩirwo?
english: One of the criminals who was hanged insulted him, saying, “If you are the Christ, save yourself and us!”

kikuyu: Mĩaka yothe ĩrĩa Jaredi aatũũrire muoyo yarĩ mĩaka magana kenda na mĩrongo ĩtandatũ na ĩĩrĩ, agĩcooka agĩkua.
english: All of the days of Jared were nine hundred sixty-two years, then he died.

kikuyu: Mũthamaki Dario agĩcooka akĩandĩkĩra andũ othe, na ndũrĩrĩ, na andũ a mĩario yothe bũrũri-inĩ guothe marũa, akĩmeera atĩrĩ: “Mũrogaacĩra mũno makĩria!
english: Then King Darius wrote to all the peoples, nations, and languages who dwell in all the earth: “Peace be multiplied to you.

kikuyu: nĩgeetha marũmagie mĩthenya ĩyo ya Purimu mahinda marĩa matuĩtwo, o ta ũrĩa Moridekai ũrĩa Mũyahudi marĩ na Esiteri, mũtumia wa mũthamaki maamatuĩrĩire, na o ta ũrĩa meetuĩrĩire o ene, hamwe na njiaro ciao, ũhoro wĩgiĩ mahinda mao ma kwĩhinga kũrĩa irio na gũcakaya.
english: to confirm these days of Purim in their appointed times, as Mordecai the Jew and Esther the queen had decreed, and as they had imposed upon themselves and their descendants in the matter of the fastings and their mourning.

kikuyu: Wee Ngai-Mwene-Hinya-Wothe-rĩ, tũma tũgaacĩre rĩngĩ; tũma ũthiũ waku ũtwarĩre, nĩguo tũhonoke. * 80:2 Mĩhĩrĩga ĩno ĩtatũ yarĩ njiarwa cia Rakeli, na nĩcio ciarĩ cia mbere kũrũmĩrĩra ithandũkũ rĩa kĩrĩkanĩro rĩrĩa Isiraeli mangĩoimagarire marĩ werũ-inĩ.
english: But when the town gates were closed, he withdrew, intending to come again to root out the whole community of the men of Joppa.

kikuyu: Ikara bũrũri-inĩ ũyũ kwa ihinda, na nĩngũkorwo hamwe nawe na nĩngũkũrathima. Nĩgũkorwo nĩwe na njiaro ciaku ngũhe mabũrũri maya mothe na hingie mwĩhĩtwa ũrĩa ndehĩtire kũrĩ thoguo Iburahĩmu.
english: Live in this land, and I will be with you, and will bless you. For I will give to you, and to your offspring, all these lands, and I will establish the oath which I swore to Abraham your father.

kikuyu: Ngĩcooka ngĩona kĩndũ kĩahaanaga ta iria rĩa gĩcicio gĩtukanĩtio na mwaki, na ngĩcooka ngĩona arĩa maatooretie nyamũ ĩrĩa na mũhiano wayo, na namba ya rĩĩtwa rĩayo, marũgamĩte rũtere-inĩ rwa iria rĩu. Nao maanyiitĩte inanda cia mũgeeto iria maaheetwo nĩ Ngai,
english: The first went, and poured out his bowl into the earth, and it became a harmful and painful sore on the people who had the mark of the beast, and who worshiped his image.

kikuyu: Nĩakũũrire roho wakwa ndũkaharũrũkio mbĩrĩra, na niĩ ngũtũũra ngenagĩra ũtheri ũcio.’
english: He has redeemed my soul from going into the pit. My life will see the light.’

kikuyu: Mũthamaki akĩaria nao, na ndonire mũndũ ũngĩaigananirio na Danieli, na Hanania, na Mishaeli na Azaria; na nĩ ũndũ ũcio magĩtoonyio wĩra-inĩ wa gũtungatagĩra mũthamaki.
english: “The south side southward shall be from Tamar as far as the waters of Meriboth Kadesh, to the brook, to the great sea. This is the south side southward.

kikuyu: Ihinda-inĩ o rĩu no ehokaga atĩ Paũlũ nĩangĩamũheire ihaki, na nĩ ũndũ ũcio nĩaikaraga akĩmũtũmanagĩra, akaaria nake.
english: Meanwhile, he also hoped that money would be given to him by Paul, that he might release him. Therefore also he sent for him more often and talked with him.

kikuyu: nĩwe wagayũkanirie Iria Itune rĩgĩtigana, Wendo wake nĩ wa tene na tene.
english: to him who divided the Red Sea apart, for his loving kindness endures forever;

kikuyu: Tũhĩĩ nĩtuo ngaatua anene ao; na maathagwo nĩ tũkenge.
english: I will give boys to be their princes, and children shall rule over them.

kikuyu: Kũu Jopa nĩ kwarĩ mũrutwo wetagwo Tabitha (narĩo rĩĩtwa rĩu rĩgĩtaũrwo nĩ kuuga Dorokasi), nake aatũũrĩte ekaga maũndũ mega, na agateithagia athĩĩni.
english: Now there was at Joppa a certain disciple named Tabitha, which when translated means Dorcas.‡ This woman was full of good works and acts of mercy which she did.

kikuyu: Andũ magaathiiaga kuo marĩ na ũta na mĩguĩ, nĩgũkorwo bũrũri ũcio ũkaiyũra congʼe, na mĩtĩ ya mĩigua.
english: People will go there with arrows and with bow, because all the land will be briers and thorns.

kikuyu: Nĩmenete na ngathũũra maheeni, no nĩnyendete watho waku.
english: I hate and abhor falsehood. I love your law.

kikuyu: Wee nĩwe wekĩrire mũhaka ũrĩa matangĩkĩra; gũtirĩ hĩndĩ magaacooka kũhumbĩra thĩ.
english: You have set a boundary that they may not pass over, that they don’t turn again to cover the earth.

kikuyu: Ciugo cia kanua ka mũndũ nĩ ta ndia ndiku, no gĩthima kĩa ũũgĩ nĩ karũũĩ gatherũkaga.
english: The words of a man’s mouth are like deep waters. The fountain of wisdom is like a flowing brook.

kikuyu: kuuma mũhĩrĩga wa Manase (na nĩguo mũhĩrĩga wa Jusufu), aarĩ Gadi mũrũ wa Susi;
english: Of the tribe of Joseph, of the tribe of Manasseh, Gaddi the son of Susi.

kikuyu: Ngũũra kuuma ũhiinyanĩrĩria wa andũ, nĩguo njathĩkagĩre mawatho maku.
english: Redeem me from the oppression of man, so I will observe your precepts.

kikuyu: “Dani agaaciirĩrĩra andũ ake na kĩhooto, arĩ ta mũhĩrĩga ũmwe wa Isiraeli.
english: They sent a message to Joseph, saying, “Your father commanded before he died, saying,

kikuyu: Jesũ akĩmũcookeria, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Nĩ kwandĩkĩtwo atĩrĩ: ‘Hooyaga Mwathani Ngai waku na ũmũtungatagĩre o we wiki.’ ” Nake mũcukani akĩmũtwara Jerusalemu, na akĩmũrũgamia gathũrũmũndũ-inĩ ka hekarũ, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Angĩkorwo wee nĩwe Mũrũ wa Ngai, wĩgũithie thĩ kuuma haha.
english: Jesus answered him, “Get behind me, Satan! For it is written, ‘You shall worship the Lord your God, and you shall serve him only.’ ”*

kikuyu: Niĩ ndĩ mũrata wa andũ arĩa othe magwĩtigĩrĩte, o arĩa othe marũmagĩrĩra mawatho maku.
english: I am a friend of all those who fear you, of those who observe your precepts.

kikuyu: Mũndũ wothe wa ngoro ya mwĩtĩĩo nĩ kĩndũ kĩrĩ magigi harĩ Jehova. Menyai na ma atĩrĩ, ndakaaga kũherithio.
english: Whoever mocks the poor reproaches his Maker. He who is glad at calamity shall not be unpunished.

kikuyu: no andũ a gũkũ thĩ no nginya mamenye atĩ nĩnyendete Baba, na atĩ njĩkaga o ũrĩa Baba anjathĩte. “Ũkai rĩu; nĩtũthiĩi.
english: But that the world may know that I love the Father, and as the Father commanded me, even so I do. Arise, let’s go from here.

kikuyu: ndaciarirwo irĩma itanahaandwo harĩa irĩ, na tũrĩma tũtanagĩa,
english: Before the mountains were settled in place, before the hills, I was born;

kikuyu: Niĩ ndirakarĩte. Naarĩ korwo nĩ congʼe na mĩigua iranjũkĩrĩra! No thiĩ ngahũũrane mbaara nacio; Ingĩcicina ciothe na mwaki.
english: The fading flower of his glorious beauty, which is on the head of the fertile valley, shall be like the first-ripe fig before

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo igũrũ na thĩ na kĩrĩa gĩothe kĩrĩ kuo nĩikanĩrĩra nĩ gũkena igĩkenerera Babuloni, nĩgũkorwo aniinani nĩmakarĩtharĩkĩra, moimĩte mwena wa gathigathini,” ũguo nĩguo Jehova ekuuga.
english: Then the heavens and the earth, and all that is therein, will sing for joy over Babylon; for the destroyers will come to her from the north,” says Yahweh.

kikuyu: “Iguai ũhoro ũyũ, inyuĩ athamaki! Thikĩrĩriai, inyuĩ aathani! Nĩngũinĩra Jehova, nĩngũina; nĩngũtungĩra Jehova rwĩmbo, o we Ngai wa Isiraeli.
english: “Hear, you kings! Give ear, you princes! I, even I, will sing to Yahweh. I will sing praise to Yahweh, the God of Israel.

kikuyu: “Jehova, Ngai wa Isiraeli, ekuuga atĩrĩ: ‘Andĩka ũhoro ũrĩa wothe ngwarĩirie ibuku-inĩ.’
english: “Yahweh, the God of Israel, says, ‘Write all the words that I have spoken to you in a book.

kikuyu: matarĩtwo na mbarĩ na nyũmba ciao, maarĩ andũ 2,630.
english: King Nabuchodonosor of Babylon came up against him, bound him with a chain of brass, and carried him to Babylon.

kikuyu: Wee Jehova, nduta kũrũmĩrĩra kĩrĩra kĩa watho waku, na niĩ ndĩkĩrũmie nginya mũthia.
english: Teach me, Yahweh, the way of your statutes. I will keep them to the end.

kikuyu: Mwathoma ũhoro ũyũ, nĩmũkũhota kũmenya ũrĩa ndaakuũkĩirwo nĩ ũhoro ũcio wa hitho wa Kristũ,
english: by which, when you read, you can perceive my understanding in the mystery of Christ,

kikuyu: Ariũ na aarĩ a nyina othe, na mũndũ o wothe wamũũĩ mbere, magĩũka makĩrĩĩanĩra nake mũciĩ gwake, nao makĩmũhooreria na makĩmũcakaithia nĩ ũndũ wa mathĩĩna marĩa mothe Jehova aamũreheire. Andũ acio o mũndũ akĩmũhe gĩcunjĩ kĩa betha na gĩcũhĩ gĩa thahabu.
english: Then all his brothers, all his sisters, and all those who had been of his acquaintance before, came to him and ate bread with him in his house. They comforted him, and consoled him concerning all the evil that Yahweh had brought on him. Everyone also gave him a piece of money,† and everyone a ring of gold.

kikuyu: Moima Sukothu, magĩthiĩ makĩamba hema ciao Ethamu, ndeere-inĩ cia werũ.
english: They traveled from Succoth, and encamped in Etham, which is in the edge of the wilderness.

kikuyu: Njiaro cia Rubeni, irigithathi rĩa Isiraeli ciarĩ ici: kuuma kũrĩ Hanoku, nĩ mbarĩ ya Ahanoku; kuuma kũrĩ Palu, nĩ mbarĩ ya Apalu;
english: Reuben, the firstborn of Israel; the sons of Reuben: of Hanoch, the family of the Hanochites; of Pallu, the family of the Palluites;

kikuyu: tiga kĩheo gĩaku o hau mbere ya kĩgongona. Amba ũthiĩ ũkaiguane na mũrũ wa thoguo; ũcooke ũũke ũrutĩre Ngai kĩheo gĩaku.
english: leave your gift there before the altar, and go your way. First be reconciled to your brother, and then come and offer your gift.

kikuyu: Matũũra marĩa manene ma Aroeri nĩmagatiganĩrio, matigĩrwo ndũũru cia mbũri iria igaakomaga kũu, gũtarĩ mũndũ wa gũcimakia.
english: that sends ambassadors by the sea, even in vessels of papyrus on the waters, saying, “Go, you swift messengers, to a nation tall and smooth, to a people awesome from their beginning onward, a

kikuyu: Andũ a Isiraeli magĩtũũrania na Akaanani, na Ahiti, na Aamori, na Aperizi, na Ahivi, na Ajebusi. Nao makĩhikia airĩtu ao, na makĩheana airĩtu ao mahikio nĩ aanake a andũ acio, na magĩtungatĩra ngai ciao. Othinieli
english: The children of Israel lived among the Canaanites, the Hittites, the Amorites, the Perizzites, the Hivites, and the Jebusites.

kikuyu: Kana amwĩtie thamaki nake amũhe nyoka?
english: Or if he asks for a fish, who will give him a serpent?

kikuyu: Ndungata ndĩngĩhera nĩ kwarĩrio gũtheri; o na ĩngĩtaũkĩrwo nĩ ũhoro ndĩngĩũrũmbũiya.
english: The way of an eagle in the air, the way of a serpent on a rock, the way of a ship in the middle of the sea, and the way of a man with a maiden.

kikuyu: Matigaatũũra bũrũri wa Jehova; Efiraimu agaacooka bũrũri wa Misiri, na arĩe irio irĩ thaahu kũu Ashuri.
english: Surely now they will say, “We have no king; for we don’t fear Yahweh; and the king, what can he do for us?”

kikuyu: kana angĩona kĩndũ kĩũrĩte na aheenanie ũhoro wakĩo, kana ehĩte na maheeni, kana eke rĩĩhia o rĩothe rĩrĩa rĩĩhagio nĩ andũ,
english: “ ‘Or if he touches the uncleanness of man, whatever his uncleanness is with which he is unclean, and it is hidden from him; when he knows of it, then he shall be guilty.

kikuyu: Andũ a Parithi, na a Media, na a Elamu; o na andũ arĩa matũũraga Mesopotamia, na Judea, na Kapadokia, na Ponto, na Asia,
english: Parthians, Medes, Elamites, and people from Mesopotamia, Judea, Cappadocia, Pontus, Asia,

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo ngĩthiĩ, ngĩkoma na mũtumia wakwa, nake akĩgĩa nda, agĩciara kahĩĩ. Nake Jehova akĩnjĩĩra atĩrĩ, “Gatue Maheri-Shalali-Hashi-Bazu.
english: Then Yahweh said to Isaiah, “Go out now to meet Ahaz, you, and Shearjashub your son, at the end of the conduit of the upper pool, on the highway of the fuller’s field.

kikuyu: Macio nĩmo mawatho marĩa makoniĩ mũndũ ũrĩa ũkuura, na ma mũndũ o wothe ũrĩa ũnyiitĩtwo nĩ thaahu nĩ ũndũ wa kuumwo nĩ hinya wa arũme,
