english: This is the law of him who has a discharge, and of him who has an emission of semen, so that he is unclean by it;

kikuyu: Wee Jehova-rĩ, ĩtĩkĩra kũũhonokia, Wee Jehova-rĩ, ũka narua ũndeithie.
english: Be pleased, Yahweh, to deliver me. Hurry to help me, Yahweh.

kikuyu: “Nĩ ũndũ ũcio ngoro yakwa nĩĩracakaĩra Moabi, nganĩrĩra o ta mũtũrirũ; ĩracakaya yanĩrĩire ta mũtũrirũ nĩ ũndũ wa andũ a Kiri-Haresethi, tondũ ũtonga ũrĩa megĩrĩire nĩmũthiru biũ.
english: Therefore my heart sounds for Moab like flutes, and my heart sounds like flutes for the men of Kir Heres. Therefore the abundance that he has gotten has perished.

kikuyu: Ndũkaneherie ihiga rĩa tene rĩa mũhaka rĩrĩa rĩahaandirwo nĩ maithe manyu ma tene.
english: Don’t move the ancient boundary stone which your fathers have set up.

kikuyu: Iguai ũndũ ũyũ inyuĩ arĩa mũrangagĩrĩria abatari na mũkaniina athĩĩni a bũrũri,
english: Hear this, you who desire to swallow up the needy, and cause the poor of the land to fail,

kikuyu: Ngĩcooka ngiuga atĩrĩ, “Muohei kĩremba gĩtheru mũtwe.” Nĩ ũndũ ũcio makĩmuoha kĩremba gĩtheru mũtwe, na makĩmũhumba nguo, mũraika wa Jehova arũgamĩte o hau.
english: Your fathers, where are they? And the prophets, do they live forever?

kikuyu: Tũũrai mũrũmĩtie kĩrĩra kĩa watho wakwa wa kũrũmĩrĩrwo, na mawatho makwa, nĩgũkorwo mũndũ ũrĩa ũrĩĩmathĩkagĩra nĩarĩtũũrio muoyo nĩmo. Niĩ nĩ niĩ Jehova.
english: You shall therefore keep my statutes and my ordinances, which if a man does, he shall live in them. I am Yahweh.

kikuyu: “ ‘Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, Mwathani Jehova ekũmeera ũũ: Atĩrĩrĩ, niĩ mwene nĩ niĩ ngaatuithania ciira gatagatĩ ka ngʼondu iria noru na iria hĩnju.
english: “Therefore the Lord Yahweh says to them: ‘Behold, I, even I, will judge between the fat sheep and the lean sheep.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo mũnabii Isaia agĩthiĩ kũrĩ Mũthamaki Hezekia, akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Andũ acio moiga atĩa, na mekuumĩte kũ?” Nake Hezekia agĩcookia atĩrĩ, “Moimĩte bũrũri wa kũraya; megũkĩte kuuma bũrũri wa Babuloni.”
english: The voice of one who calls out, “Prepare the way of Yahweh in the wilderness! Make a level highway in the desert for our God.

kikuyu: Nĩwahaandĩtwo tĩĩri-inĩ mwega handũ harĩ na maaĩ maingĩ nĩguo ũrute honge, na ũciare maciaro, na ũtuĩke mũthabibũ ũrĩ na riiri.’
english: It was planted in a good soil by many waters, that it might produce branches and that it might bear fruit, that it might be a good vine.” ’

kikuyu: NahĩndĩoĩyoJesũakĩĩraarutwoakematoonyegatarũmathiiagembereyakemakinyeBethisaida, mũira wa oigĩre kĩrĩndĩ ũhoro.
english: Immediately he made his disciples get into the boat and go ahead to the other  TR reads “The multitudes” instead of “They”

kikuyu: Ngĩrũithia andũ arĩa maarĩ igweta a Juda, ngĩmooria atĩrĩ, “Nĩ ũndũ ũrĩkũ ũyũ mũreka wa waganu wa gũthaahia mũthenya wa Thabatũ?
english: Then I contended with the nobles of Judah, and said to them, “What evil thing is this that you do, and profane the Sabbath day?

kikuyu: Nakĩo kiugo kĩa Jehova gĩkĩnginyĩrĩra, ngĩĩrwo atĩrĩ:
english: I speak not by way of commandment, but as proving through the earnestness of others the sincerity also of your love.

kikuyu: Ũtukũ ũcio andũ a Gibea nĩmaanũmĩrĩire, na magĩthiũrũrũkĩria nyũmba, mehaarĩirie kũnjũraga. Makĩnyiita thuriya yakwa na hinya, nginya ĩgĩkua.
english: The men of Gibeah rose against me, and surrounded the house by night. They intended to kill me and they raped my concubine, and she is dead.

kikuyu: Niĩ haana memenyi kũrĩ Efiraimu, na ngahaana ta ũbuthu kũrĩ andũ a Juda.
english: Therefore I am to Ephraim like a moth, and to the house of Judah like rottenness.

kikuyu: Nyũmba-inĩ ya Mũũmbi wa Nyũngũ
english: 1 The word which came to Jeremiah from Yahweh, saying,

kikuyu: Eba na bathi ikorwo irĩ kĩgeri kĩmwe kĩiganaine, bathi ĩmwe ĩiganagĩre gĩcunjĩ gĩa ikũmi kĩa homeri o ĩmwe, o nayo eba ĩmwe ĩiganagĩre gĩcunjĩ gĩa ikũmi kĩa homeri o ĩmwe; homeri ĩmwe ituĩke nĩyo kĩgeri kĩrĩa gĩĩtĩkĩrĩku gĩa gũcithima cierĩ.
english: The ephah and the bath shall be of one measure, that the bath may contain one tenth of a homer,§ and the ephah one tenth of a homer. Its measure shall be the same as the homer.

kikuyu: 1 O ta ũrĩa tharunji ĩtaagĩrĩire kũgĩa hĩndĩ ya riũa, kana mbura kuura hĩndĩ ya magetha-rĩ, noguo gĩtĩĩo gĩtagĩrĩire mũndũ mũkĩĩgu.
english: 1 Like snow in summer, and as rain in harvest, so honor is not fitting for a fool.

kikuyu: Nĩahinyagĩrĩria mũthĩĩni na mũbatari. Nĩatunyanaga indo. Ndacookagia kĩrĩa oete gĩa kũrũgamĩrĩra thiirĩ. Nĩatiiragĩra mĩhianano maitho. Nĩekaga maũndũ marĩa marĩ magigi.
english: But it was plucked up in fury. It was cast down to the ground, and the east wind dried up its fruit. Its strong branches were broken off and withered. The fire consumed them.

kikuyu: Mũgoocei, inyuĩ riũa na mweri, mũgoocei, inyuothe njata iria icangararaga.
english: Let them praise his name in the dance! Let them sing praises to him with tambourine and harp!

kikuyu: Tũkiuge atĩa ũhoro ũkoniĩ maũndũ macio? Angĩkorwo Ngai arĩ mwena witũ-rĩ, nũũ ũngĩkĩhota gũtũtuma ũthũ?
english: What then shall we say about these things? If God is for us, who can be against us?

kikuyu: Matuĩro ma ciira nĩmakinyĩte bũrũri-inĩ ũrĩa mwaraganu: magakinya Holoni, na Jahaza, na Mefaathu,
english: Judgment has come on the plain country— on Holon, on Jahzah, on Mephaath,

kikuyu: Nyoni cia rĩera-inĩ ciakaga itara hakuhĩ na maaĩ macio; ciinaga irĩ honge-inĩ cia mĩtĩ.
english: The birds of the sky nest by them. They sing among the branches.

kikuyu: Thũ ciakwa nĩciambĩire magũrũ makwa mũtego wa wabu, na niĩ ngĩĩthikĩra nĩ kũnyamarĩka. Cienjete irima njĩra-inĩ yakwa, no nĩcio igũĩte thĩinĩ warĩo.
english: They conspire and lurk, watching my steps. They are eager to take my life.

kikuyu: Arĩĩaga mũhu, ahĩtithĩtio nĩ ngoro ĩrĩa ĩheenekete; ndangĩhota kwĩhonokia, kana oige atĩrĩ, “Githĩ kĩndũ gĩkĩ kĩrĩ guoko gwakwa kwa ũrĩo ti maheeni?”
english: He feeds on ashes. A deceived heart has turned him aside; and he can’t deliver his soul, nor say, “Isn’t there a lie in my right hand?”

kikuyu: o anabii acio a Isiraeli, arĩa maarathĩire itũũra rĩa Jerusalemu makĩrĩonera cioneki cia thayũ, gũtaarĩ na thayũ, ũguo nĩguo Mwathani Jehova ekuuga.” ’
english: But I will leave a few men of them from the sword, from the famine, and from the pestilence, that they may declare all their abominations among the nations where they come. Then they will know that I am Yahweh.’ ”

kikuyu: Amwe ao, o na kũrĩ ũguo, nĩmamathirĩkirie, makiuga atĩrĩ, “Aya nĩkũrĩĩo marĩĩtwo nĩ ndibei.” Petero Kwarĩria Gĩkundi kĩa Andũ
english: Others, mocking, said, “They are filled with new wine.”

kikuyu: kuuma kũrĩ Shefufamu, nĩ mbarĩ ya Ashefufamu; kuuma kũrĩ Hufamu, nĩ mbarĩ ya Ahufamu.
english: of Shephupham, the family of the Shuphamites; of Hupham, the family of the Huphamites.

kikuyu: Ndũkanarũithie mũndũ tũhũ, rĩrĩa atarĩ ũndũ mũũru agwĩkĩte.
english: Don’t strive with a man without cause, if he has done you no harm.

kikuyu: Andũ acio othe a Isiraeli makĩnuguna nĩ ũndũ wa Musa na Harũni, na kĩũngano kĩu gĩothe gĩkĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Naarĩ korwo twakuĩrĩire Misiri! Kana werũ-inĩ ũyũ! Nĩ kĩĩ gĩgũtũma Jehova atũrehe bũrũri ũyũ atũrekererie tũũragwo na rũhiũ rwa njora? Atumia aitũ na ciana ciitũ megũtuĩka a gũtahwo. Githĩ wega ti tũcooke bũrũri wa Misiri?” Nao makĩĩrana mũndũ na ũrĩa ũngĩ atĩrĩ, “Twagĩrĩirwo nĩ tũthuure mũtongoria tũcooke bũrũri wa Misiri.”
english: All the children of Israel murmured against Moses and against Aaron. The whole congregation said to them, “We wish that we had died in the land of Egypt, or that we had died in this wilderness!

kikuyu: Moimire matũũra-inĩ mao, makĩũngana hamwe kũu Gibea nĩguo mahũũrane na andũ a Isiraeli.
english: The children of Benjamin gathered themselves together out of the cities to Gibeah, to go out to battle against the children of Israel.

kikuyu: Tũngĩhota atĩa kuonera arĩa matigarĩte atumia, kuona atĩ nĩtwĩhĩtĩte mwĩhĩtwa mbere ya Jehova atĩ tũtikaamahe airĩtu aitũ mamahikie?”
english: How shall we provide wives for those who remain, since we have sworn by Yahweh that we will not give them of our daughters to wives?”

kikuyu: nĩakĩĩhoke rĩĩtwa rĩa Jehova, na egiritanie na Ngai wake. No rĩrĩ, rĩu inyuĩ arĩa othe mwakagia mwaki, na mũkeehe imũrĩ igwakana-rĩ, thiĩi, mũgerere ũtheri-inĩ wa mwaki wanyu, na wa imũrĩ cia mwaki ũrĩa mũgwatĩtie. Ũndũ ũyũ nĩguo mũkaamũkĩra kuuma guoko-inĩ gwakwa: Mũgaakoma thĩ na mĩnyamaro.
english: Those ransomed by Yahweh will return, and come with singing to Zion. Everlasting joy shall be on their heads. They will obtain gladness and joy. Sorrow and sighing shall flee away.

kikuyu: Ndũkanetigĩre mũndũ rĩrĩa aatuĩka gĩtonga, rĩrĩa riiri wa nyũmba yake wongerereka;
english: But to the wicked God says, “What right do you have to declare my statutes, that you have taken my covenant on your lips,

kikuyu: Ũndũ ũcio ũkĩmeekĩra guoya, makĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Nĩ atĩa wĩkĩte?” (Nĩmamenyete atĩ nĩ kũũra ooragĩra Jehova, tondũ nĩameerĩte ũguo.)
english: Then the men were exceedingly afraid, and said to him, “What have you done?” For the men knew that he was fleeing from the presence of Yahweh, because he had told them.

kikuyu: nĩgeetha makegwatĩra matigari ma Edomu, na ndũrĩrĩ ciothe iria ciĩtanagio na rĩĩtwa rĩakwa,” ũguo nĩguo Jehova ekuuga, ũrĩa ũgeeka maũndũ macio.
english: But don’t look down on your brother in the day of his disaster, and don’t rejoice over the children of Judah in the day of their destruction. Don’t speak proudly in the day of distress.

kikuyu: “Arĩria andũ a Isiraeli, ũmeere atĩrĩ: ‘Mũtumia wa mũndũ angĩhĩngĩcania mĩthiĩre yake, atuĩke ti mwĩhokeku harĩ we
english: He shall separate to Yahweh the days of his separation, and shall bring a male lamb a year old for a trespass offering; but the former days shall be void, because his separation was defiled.

kikuyu: “O na inyuĩ, andũ a Kushi, nĩmũkooragwo na rũhiũ rwakwa rwa njora.” Ũrathi wa Gũũkĩrĩra Ashuri
english: You Cushites also, you will be killed by my sword.

kikuyu: Mũnyamariei, na mũmũhe kĩeha kĩiganaine na mwĩgaatho na mwĩĩkĩrĩro wake. Ngoro-inĩ yake erahaga, akoiga atĩrĩ, ‘Niĩ njikaraga ta mũthamaki-mũndũ-wa-nja; ndirĩ mũtumia wa ndigwa, na ndirĩ hĩndĩ ngaacakaya.’ Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, mĩthiro yake nĩĩkamũkinyĩrĩra mũthenya o ro ũmwe: nayo nĩ gĩkuũ, na macakaya, o na ngʼaragu. Agaacinwo na mwaki,
english: However much she glorified herself and grew wanton, so much give her of torment and mourning. For she says in her heart, ‘I sit a queen, and am no widow, and will in no way see mourning.’

kikuyu: Ezekieli agaatuĩka rũũri harĩ inyuĩ; mũgeeka o ta ũguo we ekĩte. Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa maũndũ macio mageekĩka-rĩ, nĩrĩo mũkaamenya atĩ niĩ nĩ niĩ Mwathani Jehova.’
english: Thus Ezekiel will be a sign to you; according to all that he has done, you will do. When this comes, then you will know that I am the Lord Yahweh.’ ” ’ ”

kikuyu: “Rĩrĩa athamaki a thĩ arĩa maatharagia nake na makagwatanĩra nake mĩago-inĩ yake makoona ndogo yake akĩhĩa-rĩ, nĩmakamũrĩrĩra na mamũcakaĩre. Nao makaarũgama haraaya nĩ ũndũ wa gwĩtigĩra kĩnyariirĩko gĩake, nao marĩre, makiugaga atĩrĩ: “ ‘Ĩiya-ĩ! Ĩiya-ĩ, wee itũũra inene, wee Babuloni, itũũra rĩa hinya! Ituĩro rĩaku rĩrĩ gũkinya ithaa-inĩ o rĩmwe!’
english: He said to me, “Write, ‘Blessed are those who are invited to the wedding supper of the Lamb.’ ” He said to me, “These are true words of God.”

kikuyu: Mũgũnda wa mũndũ mũthĩĩni no ũciare irio nyingĩ, no kwaga kĩhooto gũciniinaga ciothe.
english: An abundance of food is in poor people’s fields, but injustice sweeps it away.

kikuyu: “Andũ a Isiraeli na a Juda mekaga o maũndũ mooru maitho-inĩ makwa kuuma ũnini-inĩ wao; ti-itherũ, andũ a Isiraeli matirĩ ũndũ mekaga tiga gũũthirĩkia maathirĩkagia nĩguo ndaakare na indo iria methondekeire na moko mao, ũguo nĩguo Jehova ekuuga.
english: But everyone will die for his own iniquity. Every man who eats the sour grapes, his teeth will be set on edge.

kikuyu: Mũthenya ũrĩa wamũtiganĩirie, rĩrĩa andũ a kũngĩ maatahire ũtonga wake, nao ageni magĩtoonyera ihingo-inĩ ciake, na magĩcuukĩra Jerusalemu mĩtĩ-rĩ, we watariĩ o ta ũmwe wao. No wee ndũngĩanyararire mũrũ wa nyũkwa * 1:9 Rĩĩtwa Temani nĩ kuuga Edomu guothe, na rĩgĩtaũrwo nĩ kuuga “Gũthini.” Obadia
english: In the day that you stood on the other side, in the day that strangers carried away his substance and foreigners entered into his gates and cast lots for Jerusalem, even you were like one of them.

kikuyu: Jesũ agĩcooka akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Menya ndũkeere mũndũ o na ũrĩkũ. No thiĩ ũkeyonanie kũrĩ mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai, na ũrute kĩheo kĩrĩa Musa aathanire gĩtuĩke ũira kũrĩ o.” Wĩtĩkio wa Mũnene-wa-Thigari-Igana Na Jesũ aarĩkia gũtoonya Kaperinaumu, mũnene-wa-thigari-igana agĩũka kũrĩ we, akĩmũhooya ũteithio.
english: Jesus said to him, “See that you tell nobody; but go, show yourself to the priest, and offer the gift that Moses commanded, as a testimony to them.”

kikuyu: andũ othe mangĩthiranĩra hamwe, nake mũndũ acooke rũkũngũ-inĩ.
english: In a dream, in a vision of the night, when deep sleep falls on men, in slumbering on the bed,

kikuyu: Manooraga nĩmĩ ciao ikohĩga ta rũrĩmĩ rwa nyoka; mĩromo yao yaragia maũndũ marũrũ ta thumu wa nduĩra.
english: Set a watch, Yahweh, before my mouth. Keep the door of my lips.

kikuyu: Angĩkorwo nĩethaahĩtie na ti mwĩhokeku harĩ mũthuuriwe-rĩ, rĩrĩa akaanyuithio maaĩ macio marehaga kĩrumi, nĩ makaamũtoonya matũme aigue ruo rũnene mũno, na nda yake ĩimbe na kĩero gĩake kĩhinyare, nake atuĩke thaahu kũrĩ andũ ao. No rĩrĩ, angĩkorwo mũtumia ũcio ndethaahĩtie na akorwo nĩ mũthingu-rĩ, ndagatuuo mũhĩtia, na nĩakahota kũgĩa na ciana.
english: When he has made her drink the water, then it shall happen, if she is defiled and has committed a trespass against her husband, that the water that causes the curse will enter into her and become bitter, and her body will swell, and her thigh will fall away; and the woman will be a curse among her people.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa Afarisai maaiguire atĩ Jesũ nĩahootete Asadukai, magĩcookanĩrĩra hamwe.
english: But the Pharisees, when they heard that he had silenced the Sadducees, gathered themselves together.

kikuyu: Tondũ o na harĩa maamenyete Ngai, matiigana kũmũgooca taarĩ Ngai, kana makĩmũcookeria ngaatho, no meciiria mao maatuĩkire ma tũhũ, nacio ngoro ciao ngĩĩgu ikĩgĩa nduma. Na o rĩrĩa moigaga atĩ o nĩ oogĩ-rĩ, noguo maatuĩkire irimũ,
english: You therefore who teach another, don’t you teach yourself? You who preach that a man shouldn’t steal, do you steal?

kikuyu: Akĩmooria atĩrĩ, “Mũkomete nĩkĩ? Ũkĩrai mũhooe nĩgeetha mũtikagwe magerio-inĩ.” Jesũ Kũnyiitwo
english: and said to them, “Why do you sleep? Rise and pray that you may not enter into temptation.”

kikuyu: We Kiugo agĩtuĩka mũndũ na agĩtũũrania na ithuĩ. Nĩtuonete riiri wake, riiri wa Mũrũ wake wa Mũmwe, ũrĩa woimire kũrĩ Ithe aiyũrĩtwo nĩ ũtugi na ũhoro wa ma.
english: The Word became flesh and lived among us. We saw his glory, such glory as of the only born‡ Son of the Father, full of grace and truth.

kikuyu: Niĩ haana ta mũndũ gĩtaigua, ũrĩa ũtaiguaga, ngahaana ta mũndũ ũtaaragia, ũrĩa ũtangĩtumũra kanua gake;
english: Oh spare me, that I may recover strength, before I go away and exist no more.”

kikuyu: Atĩrĩrĩ Wee Jehova, nĩnjũũĩ atĩ muoyo wa mũndũ ti wake we mwene; mũndũ we mwene ti we ũrũngagĩrĩria makinya make.
english: Yahweh, I know that the way of man is not in himself. It is not in man who walks to direct his steps.

kikuyu: rĩrĩa tũirĩtio nĩ mbarabu ĩgĩtweka, na tũkaiyũrwo nĩ tharunji ĩrĩa ĩratweka,
english: So the poor has hope, and injustice shuts her mouth.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo Jehova akĩĩyũria atĩrĩ, “No hithe Iburahĩmu ũrĩa ndĩrenda gwĩka?
english: Then Abraham fell on his face, and laughed, and said in his heart, “Will a child be born to him who is one hundred years old? Will Sarah, who is ninety years old, give birth?”

kikuyu: “Ihinda rĩu riiri wa Jakubu nĩ ũgaatheũka; naguo mwĩrĩ ũcio wake mũnoru ũhĩnje.
english: “It will happen in that day that the glory of Jacob will be made thin, and the fatness of his flesh will become lean.

kikuyu: “Jehova nĩ mũthingu, no rĩrĩ, nĩndaremeire watho wake. Thikĩrĩriai, inyuĩ andũ othe, na mũrore gũthĩĩnĩka gwakwa. Aanake akwa na airĩtu akwa nĩmatahĩtwo magatwarwo thĩ ngʼeni.
english: “Yahweh is righteous, for I have rebelled against his commandment. Please hear all you peoples, and see my sorrow. My virgins and my young men have gone into captivity.

kikuyu: Makĩgĩa na gũkararania kũnene o nginya makĩamũkana. Baranaba akĩoya Mariko, makĩhaica marikabu, magĩthiĩ Kuporo,
english: Then the contention grew so sharp that they separated from each other. Barnabas took Mark with him and sailed away to Cyprus,

kikuyu: ingĩkorwo ndanakenera ũtonga wakwa mũnene, kana ngakenera uumithio ũrĩa moko makwa mecarĩirie-rĩ,
english: If I have rejoiced because my wealth was great, and because my hand had gotten much;

kikuyu: no nĩ harĩ ũndũ ũmwe tu ũbataranĩtie. Mariamu nĩethuurĩire ũndũ ũrĩa mwega makĩria, naguo ũndũ ũcio ndangĩtunywo.”
english: but one thing is needed. Mary has chosen the good part, which will not be taken away from her.”

kikuyu: Eeheragia irĩma itekũmenya, na agacingʼaũrania nĩ kũrakara.
english: He removes the mountains, and they don’t know it, when he overturns them in his anger.

kikuyu: mbere ya waganu waku kũguũrio. O na kũrĩ ũguo-rĩ, rĩu ũrĩ mũnyarare nĩ airĩtu a Edomu, marĩ hamwe na andũ othe arĩa mariganĩtie nao, o na nĩ airĩtu a Afilisti, acio othe magũthũire na magakũrigiicĩria na mĩena yothe.
english: before your wickedness was uncovered, as at the time of the reproach of the daughters of Syria, and of all who are around her, the daughters of the Philistines, who despise you all around.

kikuyu: Moko maku nĩmo manyũmbire na magĩĩthondeka ũguo ndariĩ; he ũtaũku nĩguo ndĩĩrute maathani maku.
english: Your hands have made me and formed me. Give me understanding, that I may learn your commandments.

kikuyu: Nĩwamamenyithirie Thabatũ yaku theru na ũkĩmahe maathani, na kĩrĩra gĩaku gĩa kũrũmĩrĩrwo, na mawatho kũgerera kanua ka Musa ndungata yaku.
english: The chiefs of the people: Parosh, Pahathmoab, Elam, Zattu, Bani,

kikuyu: Athamaki othe makaamũinamĩrĩra, nacio ndũrĩrĩ ciothe imũtungatagĩre.
english: Yes, all kings shall fall down before him. All nations shall serve him.

kikuyu: Nĩũkamenya atĩ ciana ciaku nĩikaingĩha, nacio njiaro ciaku cingĩhe o ta nyeki ya gũkũ thĩ.
english: You will know also that your offspring† will be great, your offspring as the grass of the earth.

kikuyu: Kĩndũ gĩothe gĩtũũraga muoyo na gĩthiiaga-rĩ, gĩgũtuĩka irio cianyu. O ta ũrĩa ndamũheire mĩmera ĩrĩa mĩruru mũrĩĩage, rĩu nĩ ndamũhe indo ciothe ituĩke irio cianyu.
english: Every moving thing that lives will be food for you. As I gave you the green herb, I have given everything to you.

kikuyu: Mũriũ ũmwe wa Joiada mũrũ wa Eliashibu mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai ũrĩa mũnene aarĩ mũthoni-we wa Sanibalati ũrĩa Mũhoroni. Nĩndamũingatire oime harĩ niĩ.
english: One of the sons of Joiada, the son of Eliashib the high priest, was son-in-law to Sanballat the Horonite; therefore I chased him from me.

kikuyu: Ĩrĩ hau nĩguo ĩcaragĩria gĩa kũrĩa; maitho mayo moonaga irio irĩ o kũraya.
english: From there he spies out the prey. His eyes see it afar off.

kikuyu: Ningĩ Johana akĩheana ũira ũyũ: “Nĩndonire Roho agĩikũrũka kuuma igũrũ atariĩ ta ndutura, agĩikara igũrũ rĩake.
english: John testified, saying, “I have seen the Spirit descending like a dove out of heaven, and it remained on him.

kikuyu: Makĩnegenia Musa, makiuga atĩrĩ, “Naarĩ korwo nĩtwakuire rĩrĩa ariũ a ithe witũ maakuire marĩ mbere ya Jehova! Nĩ kĩĩ gĩatũmire mũrehe kĩrĩndĩ kĩa Jehova werũ-inĩ ũyũ, nĩguo ithuĩ na mahiũ maitũ tũkuĩre gũkũ?
english: The people quarreled with Moses, and spoke, saying, “We wish that we had died when our brothers died before Yahweh!

kikuyu: na Kina, na Dimona, na Adada,
english: Kinah, Dimonah, Adadah,

kikuyu: Amũrai mwaka ũcio wa mĩrongo ĩtano, na mũhunjanĩrie ũhoro wa kũohorwo kwa andũ othe arĩa matũũraga bũrũri ũcio wothe. Mwaka ũcio ũgaatuĩka wa Jubilii* nĩ ũndũ wanyu; o mũndũ wanyu nĩagacooka kũrĩa ithaka cia nyũmba yao irĩ, na kũrĩa andũ a mũhĩrĩga wao marĩ.
english: You shall make the fiftieth year holy, and proclaim liberty throughout the land to all its inhabitants. It shall be a jubilee to you; and each of you shall return to his own property, and each of you shall return to his family.

kikuyu: Ngũkũneana kũrĩ arĩa magũcaragia makũrute muoyo, o acio wĩtigagĩra, ngũneane kũrĩ Nebukadinezaru, mũthamaki wa Babuloni na kũrĩ andũ a Babuloni.
english: I would give you into the hand of those who seek your life, and into the hand of them of whom you are afraid, even into the hand of Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon, and into the hand of the Chaldeans.

kikuyu: mangĩgathũkia kĩrĩra kĩa watho wakwa wa kũrũmĩrĩrwo na mage kũmenyerera maathani makwa,
english: if they break my statutes, and don’t keep my commandments;

kikuyu: Gũtirĩ mũndũ wendaga kũmenyeka nĩ andũ ũhithaga ũrĩa areeka. Na tondũ nĩũreeka maũndũ maya-rĩ, wĩonanie kũrĩ kĩrĩndĩ.”
english: For no one does anything in secret while he seeks to be known openly. If you do these things, reveal yourself to the world.”

kikuyu: Jehova Ngai wa Isiraeli ekuuga atĩrĩ, “Nĩthũire ndigano ya mũthuuri na mũtumia wake, na nĩthũire mũndũ wĩhumbaga haaro ta nguo,” ũguo nĩguo Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe ekuuga. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio gitĩra roho waku, na ndũkaage kwĩhokeka. Mũthenya wa Ciira
english: One who hates and divorces”, says Yahweh, the God of Israel, “covers his garment with violence!” says Yahweh of Armies. “Therefore pay attention to your spirit, that you don’t be unfaithful.

kikuyu: Tũrutage kana tũtikarutage?” No tondũ Jesũ nĩamenyaga ũhinga wao, akĩmooria atĩrĩ, “Mũrenda kũngeria nĩkĩ? Ta ndeherai dinari ndĩmĩone.”
english: and let him who is on the housetop not go down, nor enter in, to take anything out of his house.

kikuyu: Aatũmire kiugo gĩake akĩmahonia; aamateithũrire kuuma mbĩrĩra-inĩ.
english: He sends his word, and heals them, and delivers them from their graves.

kikuyu: Niĩ nĩnyonete ũndũ mũũru mũno nyũmba-inĩ ya Isiraeli. Kũu nĩkuo Efiraimu eheanĩte ahũũre ũmaraya, nake Isiraeli nĩakanyiitwo nĩ thaahu.
english: The pride of Israel testifies to his face; yet they haven’t returned to Yahweh their God, nor sought him, for all this.

kikuyu: Arĩkũhumbagĩra na njoya ciake, na rungu rwa mathagu make nĩho ũrĩĩgitagia; wĩhokeku wake nĩũgatuĩka ngo yaku na kĩĩhitho gĩaku.
english: For you, Yahweh, have made me glad through your work. I will triumph in the works of your hands.

kikuyu: Wee Jehova, githĩ maitho maku ti ũhoro ũrĩa wa ma macaragia? Wamaringire iringa, no matiigana kũigua ruo; † 4:30 Ũyũ nĩ rangi wehakagwo maitho arĩ njĩra ĩmwe ya ũthaka na gũtũma maitho moneke marĩ manene ( ; ).
english: O Yahweh, don’t your eyes look on truth? You have stricken them, but they were not grieved. You have consumed them, but they have refused to receive correction. They have made their faces harder than a rock. They have refused to return.

kikuyu: “Nguhĩrĩriai mũthikĩrĩrie ũhoro ũyũ: “Kuuma o kĩambĩrĩria ndirĩ ndaaria ũhoro na hitho; maũndũ macio makĩoneka, niĩ ngoragwo ho.” Na rĩu Mwathani Jehova nĩwe ũndũmĩte, we mwene o na Roho wake.
english: Behold, I have engraved you on the palms of my hands. Your walls are continually before me.

kikuyu: Isiraeli nĩmehĩtie; nĩmathũkĩtie kĩrĩkanĩro gĩakwa kĩrĩa ndamaathire marũmagĩrĩre. Nĩmoete indo imwe iria ciamũrĩtwo cia kũniinwo; nĩmaiyĩte, na makaheenania, o na magaciiganĩria hamwe na indo ciao o ene.
english: He used to read to you about the slaying of Abel by Cain, the offering up of Isaac, and the imprisonment of Joseph.

kikuyu: Kuuma kũrĩ niĩ Mũthuuri wa Kanitha, Ndakwandĩkĩra, wee mũrata wakwa mwendwa Gayo, ũrĩa nyendete kũna kũna.
english: Jude,† a servant of Jesus Christ,‡ and brother of James, to those who are called, sanctified by God the Father, and kept for Jesus Christ:

kikuyu: (o na harĩa ithe ateekire ũndũ o na ũmwe wa mo): “We nĩarĩĩaga irio iria irutĩirwo mahooero-inĩ marĩa marĩ irĩma-igũrũ. Nĩathũkagia mũtumia wa mũndũ ũrĩa ũngĩ.
english: It had strong branches for the scepters of those who ruled. Their stature was exalted among the thick boughs. They were seen in their height with the multitude of their branches.

kikuyu: Rĩrai na mũgirĩke, inyuĩ arĩithi aya; mwĩgaragariei rũkũngũ-inĩ, inyuĩ atongoria a rũũru rwa mbũri. Nĩgũkorwo ihinda rĩanyu rĩa gũthĩnjwo nĩ ikinyu; mũkaagũa mũthuthĩke o ta rĩũmba rĩrĩa rĩega mũno.
english: Wail, you shepherds, and cry. Wallow in dust, you leader of the flock; for the days of your slaughter and of your dispersions have fully come, and you will fall like fine pottery.

kikuyu: O na gũtuĩka andũ aku, wee Isiraeli maingĩhĩte ta mũthanga wa iria-inĩ-rĩ, no matigari magaacooka kwao. Mwanangĩko naguo nĩwathanĩtwo, mwanangĩko wa kuonania atĩ itua rĩake nĩ rĩa kĩhooto kũna.
english: For though your people, Israel, are like the sand of the sea, only a remnant of them will return. A destruction is determined, overflowing with righteousness.

kikuyu: Enjaga mungu ũgatũrĩkia rwaro rwa ihiga; maitho make makĩonaga igĩĩna ciaruo ciothe.
english: He cuts out channels among the rocks. His eye sees every precious thing.

kikuyu: Mũgaatũũra thĩinĩ wa bũrũri ũrĩa ndaaheire maithe manyu ma tene; nĩmũgatuĩka andũ akwa, na niĩ nduĩke Ngai wanyu.
english: You will dwell in the land that I gave to your fathers. You will be my people, and I will be your God.

kikuyu: Wee Jehova, ũigaga tawa wakwa wakanĩte; Ngai wakwa atũmaga nduma yakwa ĩtuĩke ũtheri.
english: For you will light my lamp, Yahweh. My God will light up my darkness.

kikuyu: Thuutha wa mĩthenya ĩyo mũgwanja-rĩ, kiugo kĩa Jehova gĩkĩnginyĩrĩra, ngĩĩrwo atĩrĩ,
english: At the end of seven days, Yahweh’s word came to me, saying,

kikuyu: Reke tũhe ithe witũ ndibei anyue nĩgeetha tũkome nake, na tũtũũrie rũciaro rwitũ na ũndũ wa ithe witũ.”
english: Come, let’s make our father drink wine, and we will lie with him, that we may preserve our father’s family line.”

kikuyu: na Dilani, na Mizipe, na Jokitheeli,
english: Dilean, Mizpah, Joktheel,

kikuyu: NaguoŨhoro-ũrĩa-Mweganonginyawambeũhunjiokũrĩndũrĩrĩciothe.
english: The Good News must first be preached to all the nations.

kikuyu: Ndaamũkĩrirwo maru-inĩ nĩkĩ? Ndaamũkĩrirwo nyondo-inĩ atĩ nĩguo nyongithio nĩkĩ?
english: “Now a thing was secretly brought to me. My ear received a whisper of it.

kikuyu: Mũtigatuĩke o a gũthikagĩrĩria kiugo tu, no tuĩkai a gwĩka ũrĩa kiugĩte, nĩguo mũtikeheenie inyuĩ ene.
english: But be doers of the word, and not only hearers, deluding your own selves.

kikuyu: Andũ arĩa mehokete Jehova matariĩ ta Kĩrĩma gĩa Zayuni, kĩrĩa gĩtangĩenyenyeka, no gĩtũũraga nginya tene.
english: Blessed is everyone who fears Yahweh, who walks in his ways.

kikuyu: Ngoro ciitũ nĩithirĩte gĩkeno; ndũrũhĩ ciitũ igarũrũkĩte igatuĩka macakaya.
english: The joy of our heart has ceased. Our dance is turned into mourning.

kikuyu: Kũrĩ ũndũ ũngĩ mwĩkaga. Mũiyũragia kĩgongona kĩa Jehova na maithori. Mũrĩraga na mũkagirĩka tondũ nĩatigire kũrũmbũiya maruta manyu, kana kũmaamũkĩra na gĩkeno kuuma harĩ inyuĩ.
english: “This again you do: you cover Yahweh’s altar with tears, with weeping, and with sighing, because he doesn’t regard the offering any more, neither receives it with good will at your hand.

kikuyu: Thũ nĩũcikinyĩirie mwanangĩko ũtathiraga, nĩwanangĩte matũũra macio manene; o na kũririkanwo kwao nĩgũthirĩte.
english: The enemy is overtaken by endless ruin. The very memory of the cities which you have overthrown has perished.

kikuyu: “Tara Agerishoni o nao kũringana na nyũmba ciao na mbarĩ ciao.
english: This Passover was held in the eighteenth year of the reign of Josias.

kikuyu: “Niĩ ndĩ Mũyahudi, wa gũciarĩrwo Tariso itũũra rĩa bũrũri wa Kilikia, no ndaarereirwo itũũra-inĩ rĩĩrĩ. Nĩndathomithirio wega nĩ Gamalieli ũhoro wa watho wa maithe maitũ, na ndaarĩ na kĩyo kĩa maũndũ ma Ngai o ta ũrĩa inyuĩ inyuothe mũtariĩ ũmũthĩ.
english: “I am indeed a Jew, born in Tarsus of Cilicia, but brought up in this city at the feet of Gamaliel, instructed according to the strict tradition of the law of our fathers, being zealous for God, even as you all are today.

kikuyu: Mũtĩ wa gatandatũ wagwĩrĩire mũhĩrĩga wa Nafitali, kũringana na ũrĩa mbarĩ ciao ciatariĩ:
english: The sixth lot came out for the children of Naphtali, even for the children of Naphtali according to their families.

kikuyu: Mũndũ ta ũcio wakĩmũkũũrĩra maitho? Mũndũ ta ũcio wamũrehe mbere yaku aciirithio?
english: Should he reason with unprofitable talk, or with speeches with which he can do no good?

kikuyu: Jehova nĩ mwega harĩ andũ othe; nĩaiguagĩra tha kĩrĩa gĩothe ombĩte.
english: He provides food for the livestock, and for the young ravens when they call.  Some manuscripts omit these last two lines.

kikuyu: andũ arĩa matũũraga nduma-inĩ nĩmonete ũtheri mũnene; nao arĩa matũũraga bũrũri ũrĩ kĩĩruru gĩa gĩkuũ-rĩ, nĩmarĩirwo nĩ ũtheri.”
english: Jesus, when he was baptized, went up directly from the water: and behold, the heavens were opened to him. He saw the Spirit of God descending as a dove, and coming on him.

kikuyu: Hihi ihinda rĩĩrĩ rĩothe mũkoretwo mũgĩĩciiria atĩ nĩ gwĩciirĩrĩra tũkoretwo tũgĩciirĩrĩra harĩ inyuĩ? Tũkoretwo tũkĩaria mbere ya Ngai ta andũ marĩ thĩinĩ wa Kristũ; na rĩrĩ, arata akwa nyenda, maũndũ marĩa mothe twĩkaga nĩ ma gũtũma inyuĩ muongererwo hinya.
english: For your obedience has become known to all. I rejoice therefore over you. But I desire to have you wise in that which is good, but innocent in that which is evil.

kikuyu: Acio nĩo andũ arĩa mamwamũranagia, arĩa marũmagĩrĩra merirĩria ma mwĩrĩ, no matirĩ na Roho. Judasi
english: These are those who cause divisions and are sensual, not having the Spirit.

kikuyu: Itanya rĩa mũndũ mũthingu nĩ gĩkeno, no kĩĩrĩgĩrĩro kĩa mũndũ mwaganu no gĩa tũhũ.
english: The prospect of the righteous is joy, but the hope of the wicked will perish.

kikuyu: Nĩatwathire tũhunjĩrie andũ na tũrute ũira atĩ we nĩ we Ngai aamũrire atuĩke mũtuanĩri-ciira wa andũ arĩa marĩ muoyo na arĩa makuĩte.
english: He commanded us to preach to the people and to testify that this is he who is appointed by God as the Judge of the living and the dead.

kikuyu: Nĩũkagĩa na iria rĩingĩ rĩa mbũri rĩa gũkũigana na rĩa kũnyuuo nĩ andũ a nyũmba yaku, o na rĩa gũtũũria airĩtu aku a wĩra.
english: There will be plenty of goats’ milk for your food, for your family’s food, and for the nourishment of your servant girls.

kikuyu: O ta ũrĩa gĩthima gĩtherũkaga maaĩ-rĩ, ũguo noguo itũũra rĩu rĩtherũkaga waganu. Maũndũ ma ũhinya na wanangi no mo maiguagwo kuo; ndwari ciakuo na nguraro nĩcio ndũũraga nyonaga.
english: As a well produces its waters, so she produces her wickedness. Violence and destruction is heard in her. Sickness and wounds are continually before me.

kikuyu: Ũthingu waku nĩ wa gũtũũra tene na tene, naguo watho waku nĩ wa ma.
english: Your righteousness is an everlasting righteousness. Your law is truth.

kikuyu: “Niĩ-rĩ, kaĩ mateta makwa meerekeirio mũndũ? Ngũkĩaga kũremwo nĩ gũkirĩrĩria nĩkĩ?
english: As for me, is my complaint to man? Why shouldn’t I be impatient?

kikuyu: ndigakorwo nĩ andũ arĩa aaganu arĩa maatharĩkagĩra, na kuuma kũrĩ thũ cia muoyo wakwa iria cindigiicĩirie.
english: from the wicked who oppress me, my deadly enemies, who surround me.

kikuyu: Kuuma njiaro-inĩ cia Manase: Arũme othe arĩa maakinyĩtie mĩaka mĩrongo ĩĩrĩ na makĩria, arĩa mangĩahotire gũthiĩ ita-inĩ makĩandĩkwo o mũndũ o mũndũ na rĩĩtwa rĩake kũringana na maandĩko ma mbarĩ ciao na nyũmba ciao.
english: Of the children of Manasseh, their generations, by their families, by their fathers’ houses, according to the number of the names, from twenty years old and upward, all who were able to go out to war:

kikuyu: Ikarai nyũmba ĩyo, mũrĩĩage na mũkanyua kĩrĩa gĩothe marĩmũheaga, nĩgũkorwo mũruti wĩra nĩagĩrĩirwo kũheagwo mũcaara wake. Mutikoimage nyũmba ĩmwe mũgathiĩ ĩrĩa ĩngĩ.
english: and delivered righteous Lot, who was very distressed by the lustful life of the wicked

kikuyu: Mũthenya ũcio mũrigo wao nĩũkeherio ciande-inĩ ciaku, narĩo icooki rĩao rĩeherio ngingo-inĩ cianyu; icooki rĩu nĩrĩkoinangwo nĩ ũndũ wa ũrĩa wee ũitĩrĩirio maguta. * 10:20 Ũcio wamahũũrĩte nĩ Ashuri.
english: It will happen in that day that his burden will depart from off your shoulder, and his yoke from off your neck, and the yoke shall be destroyed because of the anointing oil.

kikuyu: “Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, kuona atĩ tũrĩ a rũciaro rwa Ngai, tũtiagĩrĩirwo nĩ gwĩciiria atĩ Ngai ahaana ta mũhianano wa thahabu kana wa betha, o na kana wa ihiga, ũrĩa ũthondeketwo na mũthugundĩre na ũũgĩ wa mũndũ.
english: Being then the offspring of God, we ought not to think that the Divine Nature is like gold, or silver, or stone, engraved by art and design of man.

kikuyu: Shemuonakenĩaciarĩirwoariũake. MũrũwanyinanaShemuũrĩawarĩmũkũrũeetagwoJafethu. Shemu nĩwe warĩ ithe wa rũruka ruothe rwa ariũ a Eberi.
english: Children were also born to Shem (the elder brother of Japheth), the father of all the children of Eber.

kikuyu: Ariũ a Lotani maarĩ: Hori, na Homani. Timina aarĩ mwarĩ wa nyina na Lotani.
english: The children of Lotan were Hori and Heman. Lotan’s sister was Timna.

kikuyu: Johana akĩmacookeria na ciugo iria ciarĩtio nĩ mũnabii Isaia, akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Niĩ ndĩ mũgambo wa mũndũ ũranĩrĩra werũ-inĩ akoiga atĩrĩ, ‘Rũngariai njĩra ya Mwathani.’ ” Na rĩrĩ, Afarisai amwe arĩa maatũmĩtwo
english: He said, “I am the voice of one crying in the wilderness, ‘Make straight the way of the Lord,’* as Isaiah the prophet said.”

kikuyu: “ ‘Thuutha ũcio nĩndahĩtũkĩire hau, na rĩrĩa ndaakũrorire ngĩona atĩ warĩ mũgima wa kwendeka, ngĩgũtambũrũkĩria gĩcũrĩ kĩa nguo yakwa, ngĩhumbĩra njaga yaku. Na niĩ ngĩkwĩhĩtĩra mwĩhĩtwa mũrũmu, na tũkĩgĩa kĩrĩkanĩro nawe, ũgĩtuĩka wakwa, ũguo nĩguo Mwathani Jehova ekuuga.
english: I will set my face against that man and will make him an astonishment, for a sign and a proverb, and I will cut him off from

kikuyu: na Lakishi, na Bozikathu, na Egiloni,
english: Lachish, Bozkath, Eglon,

kikuyu: 1 Mũndũ ũrĩa wothe wendete ũtaaro nĩendete ũmenyi, no ũrĩa ũthũire gũkaanio nĩ mũndũ mũkĩĩgu.
english: 1 Whoever loves correction loves knowledge, but he who hates reproof is stupid.

kikuyu: Niĩ Jehova nĩ niĩ ngaatuĩka Ngai wacio, nake Daudi ndungata yakwa nĩagatuĩka mũthamaki gatagatĩ-inĩ ga cio. Niĩ Jehova nĩ niĩ njarĩtie ũhoro ũcio.
english: I, Yahweh, will be their God, and my servant David prince among them. I, Yahweh, have spoken it.

kikuyu: Inĩrai Ngai, o we hinya witũ, nĩ ũndũ wa gũkena; anĩrĩrai mũinĩre Ngai wa Jakubu!
english: God presides in the great assembly. He judges among the gods.

kikuyu: Cookanĩrĩriai andũ, amũrai kĩũngano kĩu; rehei athuuri hamwe, na mũcookanĩrĩrie ciana, o na tũkenge tũrĩa tũronga. Mũhikania nĩoime kanyũmba gake, nake mũhiki nĩoime kĩrĩrĩ gĩake.
english: Gather the people. Sanctify the assembly. Assemble the elders. Gather the children, and those who nurse from breasts. Let the bridegroom go out of his room, and the bride out of her chamber.

kikuyu: Ariũ a Zilipa ndungata ya mũirĩtu ya Lea maarĩ: Gadi na Asheri. Acio nĩo maarĩ ariũ a Jakubu, arĩa aaciarĩirwo arĩ kũu Padani-Aramu. Na Jakubu agĩkinya mũciĩ kũrĩ ithe Isaaka kũu Mamure, hakuhĩ na Kiriathu-Ariba (nĩkuo Hebironi), kũrĩa Iburahĩmu na Isaaka maatũũrĩte.
english: The sons of Zilpah (Leah’s servant): Gad and Asher. These are the sons of Jacob, who were born to him in Paddan Aram.

kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũkũnyiitwo na indo icio ciamũrĩtwo cia kũniinwo nĩagacinwo na mwaki hamwe na indo ciake ciothe. Nĩgũkorwo nĩathũkĩtie kĩrĩkanĩro kĩa Jehova, na nĩekĩte ũndũ wa thoni thĩinĩ wa Isiraeli!’ ”
english: He chanted to you David, the hymn writer, who says, “Many are the afflictions of the just.”

kikuyu: “Na thuutha wa maũndũ macio-rĩ, nĩngaitĩrĩria andũ othe Roho wakwa. Ariũ anyu na airĩtu anyu nĩmakarathaga mohoro, nao athuuri anyu nĩmakarootaga irooto, nao aanake anyu monage cioneki.
english: You made known to me the ways of life. You will make me full of gladness with your presence.’*

kikuyu: Ngũtũũra nyinagĩra Jehova matukũ mothe ma muoyo wakwa; ndĩrĩinaga ngĩgoocaga Ngai wakwa hĩndĩ ĩrĩa yothe ngũtũũra muoyo.
english: I will sing to Yahweh as long as I live. I will sing praise to my God while I have any being.

kikuyu: Ingĩmeera mainũke mahũũtiĩ mekũringĩkĩra njĩra, tondũ amwe ao moimĩte kũraya.” Nao arutwo makĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Mũndũ angĩruta irio cia kũmahũũnia kũ gũkũ werũ-inĩ ũyũ?”
english: If I send them away fasting to their home, they will faint on the way, for some of them have come a long way.”

kikuyu: Maya nĩmo marĩĩtwa ma ariũ a Esaũ: nĩ Elifazu, mũrũ wa Ada mũtumia wa Esaũ, na Reueli, mũrũ wa Basemathu mũtumia wa Esaũ.
english: He told it to his father and to his brothers. His father rebuked him, and said to him, “What is this dream that you have dreamed? Will I and your mother and your brothers indeed come to bow ourselves down to the earth before you?”

kikuyu: “Nĩ ũndũ ũcio-rĩ, niĩ ngũtua atĩrĩ, tũtige kũritũhĩria ũhoro andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ arĩa maragarũrũka kũrĩ Ngai.
english: Therefore my judgment is that we don’t trouble those from among the Gentiles who turn to God,

kikuyu: “Wee Jehova, ta rora wone ũrĩa ndĩ hatĩka-inĩ! Nĩndĩratangĩka thĩinĩ wakwa, na ngathĩĩnĩka thĩinĩ wa ngoro yakwa, nĩ ũndũ ngoretwo ndĩ mũremi mũno. Na kũu nja, hiũ cia njora nĩirooraga andũ, nakuo thĩinĩ no gĩkuũ kũrĩ.
english: “Look, Yahweh; for I am in distress. My heart is troubled. My heart turns over within me, for I have grievously rebelled. Abroad, the sword bereaves. At home, it is like death.

kikuyu: Nĩngakũrehere ngʼaragu na nyamũ cia gĩthaka igũtharĩkĩre, nacio nĩigakũniinĩra ciana. Mũthiro na ũiti wa thakame nĩigakũhaata, na nĩngarehithia rũhiũ rwa njora rũgũũkĩrĩre. Niĩ Jehova nĩ niĩ ndoiga ũguo.”
english: I will send on you famine and evil animals, and they will bereave you. Pestilence and blood will pass through you. I will bring the sword on you. I, Yahweh, have spoken it.’ ”

kikuyu: Kũhithĩrĩra ũndũ nĩ ũgooci wa Ngai; nakuo gũtuĩria ũndũ nĩkuo kũgoocithagia athamaki.
english: It is the glory of God to conceal a thing, but the glory of kings is to search out a matter.

kikuyu: Nakĩo gĩkundi kĩa andũ kĩrĩa kĩarĩ ho na gĩkĩigua ũguo, gĩkiuga atĩ mũgambo ũcio nĩwarurumaga; nao arĩa angĩ makiuga atĩ nĩ mũraika wamwarĩria. Nake Jesũ akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Mũgambo ũcio ndũnaaria nĩ ũndũ wakwa, no waria nĩ ũndũ wanyu.
english: Therefore the multitude who stood by and heard it said that it had thundered. Others said, “An angel has spoken to him.”

kikuyu: Nĩnganeana andũ a bũrũri wa Misiri wathani-inĩ wa mwathi ũtarĩ tha,* na mũthamaki mũũru amathamakĩre,” ũguo nĩguo Mwathani Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe ekuuga.
english: My heart flutters. Horror has frightened me. The twilight that I desired has been turned into trembling for me.

kikuyu: Andũ a Juda Gũthũkia Kĩrĩkanĩro
english: 1 The word that came to Jeremiah from Yahweh, saying,

kikuyu: “ ‘Mwĩyamũrei mũtuĩke atheru tondũ niĩ nĩ niĩ Jehova Ngai wanyu.
english: always learning and never able to come to the knowledge of the truth.

kikuyu: Ĩkĩra icunjĩ cia nyama thĩinĩ wayo, icunjĩ ciothe iria njega, ta cia kũgũrũ na guoko. Mĩiyũrie na mahĩndĩ marĩa mega mũno;
english: Gather its pieces into it, even every good piece: the thigh and the shoulder. Fill it with the choice bones.

kikuyu: “ ‘Na angĩkorwo iruta rĩake nĩ mbũri, nĩamĩneane mbere ya Jehova.
english: “ ‘If his offering is a goat, then he shall offer it before Yahweh.

kikuyu: “ ‘Rĩu ũkĩrai mũthiĩ Shilo, kũndũ kũrĩa ndambire gũtua gĩikaro kĩa Rĩĩtwa rĩakwa, nĩguo mwĩonere ũrĩa ndeekire kuo nĩ ũndũ wa waganu wa andũ akwa a Isiraeli.
english: Were they ashamed when they had committed abomination? No, they were not at all ashamed. They couldn’t blush. Therefore they will fall among those who fall. In the time of their visitation they will be cast down, says Yahweh.

kikuyu: Matũũraga mĩaka yao magaacĩire, na magathiĩ mbĩrĩra-inĩ marĩ na thayũ. Na no-o meeraga Mũrungu atĩrĩ, ‘Thiĩ! Tigana na ithuĩ!
english: They spend their days in prosperity. In an instant they go down to Sheol.†

kikuyu: “Ũrĩa ũngĩ akiuga atĩrĩ, ‘No hĩndĩ ndĩragũrire ndegwa ikũmi cia kũrĩma, na nĩndĩrathiĩ gũcigeria. Thiĩ ũmwĩre andekere.’
english: “Another said, ‘I have bought five yoke of oxen, and I must go try them out. Please have me excused.’

kikuyu: “Mwakĩhota atĩa kũũhooreria na ndeto cia tũhũ? Hatirĩ kĩndũ gĩtigaru harĩ macookio manyu tiga o maheeni!” Nake Elifazu ũrĩa Mũtemaani agĩcookia atĩrĩ:
english: So how can you comfort me with nonsense, because in your answers there remains only falsehood?”

kikuyu: Nacio mbũtũ ciake cia thigari ciothe iria ikoora nĩikooragwo na rũhiũ rwa njora, namo matigari mao mahurunjĩrwo huho-inĩ ciothe. Hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩmũkamenya atĩ niĩ Jehova nĩ niĩ njarĩtie.
english: All his fugitives in all his bands will fall by the sword, and those who remain will be scattered toward every wind. Then you will know that I, Yahweh, have spoken it.’

kikuyu: Ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ, thuurai andũ mũgwanja kuuma gatagatĩ-inĩ kanyu, andũ arĩa moĩkaine atĩ nĩ maiyũrĩtwo nĩ Roho, o na ũũgĩ, na nĩtũkũmehokera wĩra ũcio,
english: Therefore, select from among you, brothers, seven men of good report, full of the Holy Spirit and of wisdom, whom we may appoint over this business.

kikuyu: Mũgambo wa andũ maragirĩka nĩũraiguuo kuuma Zayuni: ‘Hĩ, kaĩ rĩu tũrĩ aanange-ĩ! Kaĩ nĩtũconorithĩtio mũno-ĩ! No nginya tuume bũrũri witũ, tondũ nyũmba ciitũ nĩcimomoretwo.’ ” No rĩrĩ, inyuĩ atumia aya, ta thikĩrĩriai kiugo kĩa Jehova; hingũrai matũ manyu mũigue ciugo cia kanua gake. Oniai airĩtu anyu kũgirĩka; o mũndũ nĩarute ũrĩa ũngĩ gũcakaya.
english: Again, do you think that we are excusing ourselves to you? In the sight of God we speak in Christ. But all things, beloved, are for your edifying.

kikuyu: Wakoma-rĩ, ndũgetigĩra; wakoma-rĩ, ũrĩkomaga toro mwega.
english: When you lie down, you will not be afraid. Yes, you will lie down, and your sleep will be sweet.

kikuyu: “Atĩrĩrĩ, mũndũ ũmwe wanyu angĩenda gwaka nyũmba ndaaya na igũrũ-rĩ, githĩ ndangĩamba gũikara thĩ atare thogora nĩguo one kana wĩra na mbeeca cia kũmĩrĩkia?
english: There will be weeping and gnashing of teeth when you see Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and all the prophets in God’s Kingdom, and yourselves being thrown outside.

kikuyu: Ohai mĩrigo yanyu mũthaame bũrũri ũyũ, inyuĩ arĩa mũtũũrĩte mũrigiicĩirio nĩ thũ.
english: Gather up your wares out of the land, you who live under siege.

kikuyu: Gĩtĩ gĩaku kĩa ũnene gĩtũũrĩte kĩrĩ kĩrũmu kuuma o tene; wee ũtũũrĩte kuo kuuma tene na tene.
english: Rise up, you judge of the earth. Pay back the proud what they deserve.

kikuyu: Hau nĩ haarĩ na cuba yarĩ na thiki, nĩ ũndũ ũcio thigari igĩtobokia thibũnji ho, igĩcooka igĩthecerera thibũnji ĩyo rũthanju-inĩ rwa mũthobi, ikĩmĩtwarĩrĩria kanua-inĩ ka Jesũ.
english: Now a vessel full of vinegar was set there; so they put a sponge full of the vinegar on hyssop, and held it at his mouth.

kikuyu: na mũndũ-wa-nja ũtangĩendeka ahikĩte, na ndungata ya mũirĩtu ĩtuĩkĩte mũtumia wa mũthuuri wa mũmĩathi.
english: for an unloved woman when she is married, and a servant who is heir to her mistress.

kikuyu: “No rĩrĩ, Ũrĩa-ũrĩ-Igũrũ-Mũno ndaikaraga nyũmba ciakĩtwo nĩ andũ. O ta ũrĩa mũnabii oigĩte atĩrĩ:
english: However, the Most High doesn’t dwell in temples made with hands, as the prophet says,

kikuyu: Akĩũria arutwo ake atĩrĩ, “Wĩtĩkio wanyu ũrĩ ha?” Nao makĩiyũrwo nĩ guoya na makĩgega, makĩũrania atĩrĩ, “Kaĩ mũndũ ũyũ arĩ ũ? Aathaga huho o na maaĩ, nacio ikamwathĩkĩra.” Jesũ Kũhonia Mũndũ warĩ na Ndaimono
english: But what did you go out to see? A man clothed in soft clothing? Behold, those who are gorgeously dressed and live delicately are in kings’ courts.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo ũũ nĩguo Mwathani Jehova ekuuga: Tondũ inyuĩ nĩmũhũũrĩte hĩ na mũkaringithia makinya manyu thĩ, mũgĩkenagĩrĩra bũrũri wa Isiraeli mũrĩ na rũmena ruothe rwa ngoro cianyu,
english: For the Lord Yahweh says: “Because you have clapped your hands, stamped with the feet, and rejoiced with all the contempt of your soul against the land of Israel,

kikuyu: “ ‘Nĩ ũndũ ũcio tiga gwĩtigĩra, wee Jakubu ndungata yakwa; tiga kũmaka, wee Isiraeli,’ nĩguo Jehova ekuuga. ‘Ti-itherũ nĩngakũhonokia ngũrute kũu kũraya, na njiaro ciaku ndĩcirute bũrũri ũrĩa maathaamĩirio. Jakubu nĩagacookererwo nĩ thayũ o na ũgitĩri, na gũtirĩ mũndũ o na ũmwe ũgaatũma etigĩre.
english: Therefore don’t be afraid, O Jacob my servant, says Yahweh. Don’t be dismayed, Israel. For, behold, I will save you from afar, and save your offspring from the land of their captivity. Jacob will return, and will be quiet and at ease. No one will make him afraid.

kikuyu: Tũkiuge atĩa? Ithuĩ Ayahudi tũkĩrĩ ega gũkĩra andũ arĩa angĩ? Aca, tiguo! Nĩtũrĩkĩtie kuonania hau mbere atĩ Ayahudi o hamwe na andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ othe nĩmagwĩte hinya-inĩ wa mehia.
english: What then? Are we better than they? No, in no way. For we previously warned both Jews and Greeks that they are all under sin.

kikuyu: na ũngĩgaakayũrũrũka na ũhooe gũkũũrana maũndũ, na wĩtane wanĩrĩire ũheo ũtaũku,
english: Don’t let kindness and truth forsake you. Bind them around your neck. Write them on the tablet of your heart.

kikuyu: Aarĩkia kuuga ũguo, agĩcooka akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Mũrata witũ Lazaro nĩakomete; no nĩngũthiĩ kũu ngamũũkĩrie.”
english: because on account of him many of the Jews went away and believed in Jesus.

kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo Mwathani nĩarũithagia arĩa endete, na akaherithia ũrĩa wothe etĩkĩrĩte atuĩke mwana wake.”
english: Likewise, exhort the younger men to be sober minded.

kikuyu: O kĩambĩrĩria-inĩ, nĩwaarire mĩthingi ya thĩ, o narĩo igũrũ nĩ wĩra wa moko maku.
english: Of old, you laid the foundation of the earth. The heavens are the work of your hands.

kikuyu: Angĩkorwo mũndũ ũrĩa wĩhĩtĩte mwĩhĩtwa nĩ mũthĩĩni mũno ndaangĩhota kũrĩha irĩhi rĩrĩa rĩtuĩtwo, nĩagatwara mũndũ ũcio kũrĩ mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai, ũrĩa ũgaatua irĩhi rĩrĩa mũndũ ũcio wĩhĩtĩte mwĩhĩtwa angĩhota kũrĩha.
english: Five of you shall chase a hundred, and a hundred of you shall chase ten thousand; and your enemies shall fall before you by the sword.

kikuyu: Mũndũ o wothe ũrĩa ũtendanaga ũcio ndooĩ Ngai, tondũ Ngai nĩ wendo.
english: He who doesn’t love doesn’t know God, for God is love.

kikuyu: Aya nĩo andũ a bũrũri arĩa maacookire kuuma bũrũri ũrĩa maatahĩirwo, arĩa Nebukadinezaru mũthamaki wa Babuloni aatahĩte akamatwara Babuloni bũrũri wa ũkombo (nao magĩcooka Jerusalemu na Juda, o mũndũ itũũra-inĩ rĩake,
english: These are the children of the province who went up out of the captivity of those who had been carried away, whom

kikuyu: Kĩrĩro nĩkĩiguĩtwo kũndũ kũrĩa gũtũũgĩru gũtarĩ kĩndũ, kũgirĩka na gũthaithana kwa andũ a Isiraeli, nĩ ũndũ nĩmogometie njĩra ciao, na makariganĩrwo nĩ Jehova Ngai wao.
english: A voice is heard on the bare heights, the weeping and the petitions of the children of Israel; because they have perverted their way, they have forgotten Yahweh their God.

kikuyu: no mũngĩkanjookerera na mwathĩkĩre maathani makwa, hĩndĩ ĩyo o na andũ anyu arĩa maatahĩtwo magatwarwo ituri cia thĩ kũrĩa kũraya mũno, nĩngamacookereria ndĩmarute kuo, ndĩmarehe kũndũ kũrĩa thuurĩte gũtuĩke gĩikaro kĩa Rĩĩtwa rĩakwa.’
english: but if you return to me, and keep my commandments and do them, though your outcasts were in the uttermost part of the heavens, yet I will gather them from there, and will bring them to the place that I have chosen, to cause my name to dwell there.’

kikuyu: Thoguo na maitũguo marocanjamũka nĩ ũndũ waku; mũtumia ũrĩa wagũciarire arokena!
english: Let your father and your mother be glad! Let her who bore you rejoice!

kikuyu: Ũũ nĩguo Jehova ekuuga: “Mũndũ ũyũ nĩandĩkwo maandĩko-inĩ, ta mũndũ ũtarĩ mwana, mũndũ ũtakagaacĩra mũtũũrĩre-inĩ wake wothe, nĩgũkorwo gũtirĩ mwana wake ũkaagaacĩra, gũtirĩ ũgaikarĩra gĩtĩ gĩa ũthamaki kĩa Daudi kana athamake rĩngĩ thĩinĩ wa Juda.”
english: Yahweh says, “Record this man as childless, a man who will not prosper in his days; for no more will a man of his offspring prosper, sitting on David’s throne and ruling in Judah.”

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo Jakubu akiuma Birishiba, nao ariũ a Isiraeli magĩkuua ithe wao Jakubu na ciana ciao na atumia ao na makaari marĩa Firaũni aatũmĩte ma kũmũkuua.
english: Now don’t be grieved, nor angry with yourselves, that you sold me here, for God sent me before you to preserve life.

kikuyu: na Zanoa, na Adulamu na tũtũũra twarĩo, na Lakishi na mĩgũnda ya rĩo, na Azeka na matũũra marĩo. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio maatũũraga kuuma Birishiba o nginya Kĩanda kĩa Hinomu.
english: Zanoah, Adullam, and their villages, Lachish and its fields, and Azekah and its towns. So they encamped from Beersheba to the valley of Hinnom.

kikuyu: na Ahia, na Hanani, na Anani,
english: Ahiah, Hanan, Anan,

kikuyu: Atĩrĩrĩ, Rĩĩtwa rĩa Jehova rĩrooka riumĩte kũraya, rĩrĩ na marakara mahiũ na matu mathimbu ma ndogo; nacio iromo ciake ciyũrĩtwo nĩ mangʼũrĩ, na rũrĩmĩ rwake nĩ mwaki ũniinanaga.
english: Behold, Yahweh’s name comes from far away, burning with his anger, and in thick rising smoke. His lips are full of indignation. His tongue is as a devouring fire.

kikuyu: Kĩmakania gĩoka-rĩ, magaacaragia thayũ, no ndũkooneka.
english: Destruction comes! They will seek peace, and there will be none.

kikuyu: Ariũ aku nĩmaringĩkĩte; nao makomete macemanĩrio-inĩ ma njĩra ciothe, magakoma o ta thiiya ĩnyiitĩtwo nĩ mũtego. Maiyũrĩtwo nĩ mangʼũrĩ ma Jehova, na ikũũma rĩa Ngai wanyu. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio igua ũhoro ũyũ, wee ũnyariirĩtwo, ũkarĩĩo, no ti ndibei ĩkũrĩĩte.
english: Your sons have fainted. They lie at the head of all the streets, like an antelope in a net. They are full of Yahweh’s wrath, the rebuke of your God.

kikuyu: Handũ ha ũguo-rĩ, amũrai Alawii matuĩke arori a hema ya Ũira, mamenyagĩrĩre indo ciothe ciayo, na kĩndũ o gĩothe kĩa hema ĩyo. Nĩo marĩkuuaga hema ĩyo o na indo ciothe ciayo; na mamĩmenyagĩrĩre na mamĩthiũrũrũkagĩrie na hema ciao. Rĩrĩa rĩothe hema ĩyo ĩgũthaamio, Alawii acio nĩo marĩmĩambũraga, na rĩrĩa rĩothe hema ĩyo ĩĩkwambwo-rĩ, Alawii acio nĩo marĩmĩambaga. Mũndũ ũngĩ o na ũrĩkũ angĩkamĩkuhĩrĩria nĩakooragwo.
english: but appoint the Levites over the Tabernacle of the Testimony, and over all its furnishings, and over all that belongs to it. They shall carry the tabernacle and all its furnishings; and they shall take care of it, and shall encamp around it.

kikuyu: o ta ũrĩa Baba anjũũĩ, na niĩ ngamenya Baba, na nĩndutĩte muoyo wakwa nĩ ũndũ wa ngʼondu.
english: I am glad for your sakes that I was not there, so that you may believe. Nevertheless, let’s go to him.”

kikuyu: Anenehagia ndũrĩrĩ, agacooka agaciananga; aingĩhagia ndũrĩrĩ, agacooka agacihurunja.
english: How many are my iniquities and sins? Make me know my disobedience and my sin.

kikuyu: “Rĩu-rĩ, Jehova Ngai Mwene-Hinya-Wothe, Ngai wa Isiraeli, ekũũria ũũ: Nĩ kĩĩ gĩgũtũma mwĩrehere mwanangĩko mũnene ũguo na njĩra ya kweheria arũme na andũ-a-nja, na ciana na ngenge kuuma Juda, na inyuĩ mũkeniina, gũkaaga mũndũ o na ũmwe ũtigarĩte?
english: “Therefore now Yahweh, the God of Armies, the God of Israel, says: ‘Why do you commit great evil against your own souls, to cut off from yourselves man and woman, infant and nursing child out of the middle of Judah, to leave yourselves no one remaining,

kikuyu: Gũtirĩ o na ũmwe wao ũraanoga kana akahĩngwo, gũtirĩ o na ũmwe ũracũũnga kana agakoma; gũtirĩ ũraregeria mũcibi njohero, o na gũtirĩ rũrigi rwa kĩraatũ rũtuĩkĩte.
english: No one shall be weary nor stumble among them; no one shall slumber nor sleep, neither shall the belt of their waist be untied, nor the strap of their sandals be broken,

kikuyu: Athĩnjĩri-Ngai amatongoragia marutĩtwo nguo, na akangʼaũrania andũ arĩa mehaandire tene.
english: Who is he who will contend with me? For then I would hold my peace and give up the spirit.

kikuyu: Kũhe mũndũ mũkĩĩgu gĩtĩĩo no ta kuoherera ihiga kĩgũtha-inĩ.
english: As one who binds a stone in a sling, so is he who gives honor to a fool.

kikuyu: Isiraeli nĩameretio; rĩu arĩ gatagatĩ ka ndũrĩrĩ ahaana ta kĩndũ gĩtarĩ bata.
english: A prophet watches over Ephraim with my God. A fowler’s snare is on all of his paths, and hostility in the house of his God.

kikuyu: Ciatigirio nĩgeetha igerie andũ a Isiraeli, nĩguo monwo kana nĩmarĩathĩkagĩra maathani ma Jehova marĩa aaheete maithe mao ma tene na guoko kwa Musa.
english: They were left to test Israel by them, to know whether they would listen to Yahweh’s commandments, which he commanded their fathers by Moses.

kikuyu: Abimeleku akĩmũtengʼeria, nao andũ aingĩ makĩgũa magurarĩtio makĩũra kũu guothe o nginya itoonyero-inĩ rĩa kĩhingo.
english: Abimelech chased him, and he fled before him, and many fell wounded, even to the entrance of the gate.

kikuyu: Atĩrĩrĩ, inyuĩ ariũ akwa, ta thikĩrĩriai ũrũtani wa thoguo; njiguai nĩguo mwĩgĩĩre na kũmenya maũndũ.
english: Simon Peter, a servant and apostle of Jesus Christ,† to those who have obtained a like precious faith with us in the righteousness of our God and Savior, Jesus Christ:

kikuyu: Nĩnjũũĩ atĩ rĩathani rĩake rĩkinyagia mũndũ muoyo-inĩ wa tene na tene. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio ũhoro ũrĩa wothe njaragia no ũrĩa Baba anjĩĩrĩte njarie.”
english: I know that his commandment is eternal life. The things therefore which I speak, even as the Father has said to me, so I speak.”

kikuyu: Thĩinĩ wa maũndũ macio mothe-rĩ, Ayubu ndaigana kwĩhia na ũndũ wa gũtuĩra Ngai mahĩtia.
english: In all this, Job didn’t sin, nor charge God with wrongdoing.

kikuyu: Githĩ igai rĩakwa rĩtituĩkĩte ta nyoni ndĩa-nyama ĩrĩ maroro harĩ niĩ, ĩrĩa nyoni iria ingĩ ndĩa-nyama ĩthiũrũrũkagĩria ikamĩtharĩkĩra? Thiĩ ũkonganie nyamũ ciothe cia gĩthaka; cirehe imatambuure.
english: Is my heritage to me as a speckled bird of prey? Are the birds of prey against her all around? Go, assemble all the animals of the field. Bring them to devour.

kikuyu: Girĩrĩria maitho makwa matige kwĩrorera maũndũ ma tũhũ; tũũria muoyo wakwa kũringana na kiugo gĩaku.
english: Turn my eyes away from looking at worthless things. Revive me in your ways.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo mũtwe wakwa nĩũkambarario igũrũ wa thũ iria iithiũrũrũkĩirie; nĩngaruta igongona ngĩanagĩrĩra na gĩkeno ndĩ hema-inĩ yake; nĩngainĩra Jehova na ndĩmũgooce.
english: He makes them also to skip like a calf; Lebanon and Sirion like a young, wild ox.

kikuyu: Nĩagacuukĩra thenge icio cierĩ mĩtĩ, ũmwe nĩ wa Jehova, naguo ũcio ũngĩ nĩ wa mbũri ya kũrekererio ĩkuuithĩtio mehia.
english: Aaron shall cast lots for the two goats: one lot for Yahweh, and the other lot for the scapegoat.

kikuyu: Ngakũhũthĩra kũhehenja mũrĩithi na rũũru rwake rwa mbũri, ngakũhũthĩra kũhehenja mũrĩmi na ndegwa ciake cia kũrĩma, o na ngakũhũthĩra kũhehenja abarũthi na anene.
english: With you I will break in pieces the shepherd and his flock. With you I will break in pieces the farmer and his yoke. With you I will break in pieces governors and deputies.

kikuyu: Githĩ waganu waku ti mũnene? Mehia maku-rĩ, makĩrĩ mũthia?
english: Isn’t your wickedness great? Neither is there any end to your iniquities.

kikuyu: Andũ acio metuaga ta meyamũrĩire Ngai no nĩmakaanaga hinya wake. Wĩthemage andũ ta acio.
english: constant friction of people of corrupt minds and destitute of the truth, who suppose that godliness is a means of gain. Withdraw yourself from such.†

kikuyu: Magũrũ makwa moimĩte makinya make thuutha; nũmagĩrĩra njĩra yake itekwahũka na mwena.
english: My foot has held fast to his steps. I have kept his way, and not turned away.

kikuyu: “Simoni, Simoni, atĩrĩ, Shaitani nĩahooete rũtha nĩguo agũthũngũthie o ta ũrĩa ngano ĩthũngũthagio. No wee, Simoni nĩngũhooeire nĩgeetha wĩtĩkio waku ndũgathire. Na warĩkia kũnjookerera, ũũmagĩrĩrie ariũ na aarĩ a thoguo.”
english: The Lord said, “Simon, Simon, behold, Satan asked to have all of you, that he might sift you as wheat,

kikuyu: Koine-inĩ icio inya cia nja ya nyumĩrĩra nĩ haarĩ na nja ciarĩ njirigĩre. Ciarĩ na gĩthimo kĩaiganaine kĩa ũraihu wa mĩkono mĩrongo ĩna,* na wariĩ wa mĩkono mĩrongo ĩtatũ†; o nja ĩmwe ya nja icio ciarĩ koine-inĩ icio inya nĩyaiganaine na ĩrĩa ĩngĩ.
english: On that day the prince shall prepare for himself and for all the people of the land a bull for a sin offering.

kikuyu: “ ‘Ningĩ andũ a Dedani nĩmoonjorithanagia nawe, na mabũrũri maingĩ ma ndwere-inĩ cia iria maarĩ agũri a indo ciaku; maakũrĩhaga na mĩguongo na mĩbĩngũ.
english: “ ‘ “The men of Dedan traded with you. Many islands were the market of your hand. They brought you horns of ivory and ebony in exchange.

kikuyu: Njĩra ciothe cia Jehova nĩ cia wendani na wĩhokeku, harĩ arĩa mamenyagĩrĩra kĩrĩkanĩro gĩake.
english: When my father and my mother forsake me, then Yahweh will take me up.

kikuyu: “Ningĩ mahinda ma kũrigĩrĩria ma ũthamaki wao, rĩrĩa andũ aremi magaakorwo maaganĩte biũ, nĩgũkarahũka mũthamaki ũngĩ ũtarĩ tha, mũndũ mwara na mũũgĩ na maũndũ ma ungumania.
english: “So he said, ‘The fourth animal will be a fourth kingdom on earth, which will be different from all the kingdoms, and will devour the whole earth, and will tread it down and break it in pieces.

kikuyu: Ciimba cia ndungata ciaku macineanĩte ituĩke irio cia nyoni cia rĩera-inĩ, na mĩĩrĩ ya andũ aku aamũre makamĩheana kũrĩ nyamũ cia gĩthaka.
english: Before Ephraim, Benjamin, and Manasseh, stir up your might! Come to save us!

kikuyu: Ndũkareke mũiyũro wa maaĩ ũũhubĩkanie, kana merio nĩ kũrĩa kũriku, o na kana ndumĩrio kanua nĩ irima rĩa gĩkuũ.
english: My mouth will tell about your righteousness, and of your salvation all day, though I don’t know its full measure. The word translated “anew” here and in

kikuyu: hĩndĩ ĩyo tũgondu nĩtũgaatũma ũgĩe na nguo, nacio mbũri itũme ũgĩe na gĩa kũgũra mũgũnda.
english: The lambs are for your clothing, and the goats are the price of a field.

kikuyu: Niĩ nĩ niĩ ndombire thĩ na andũ arĩa marĩ kuo o na nyamũ iria irĩ kuo na hinya wakwa mũnene na ndambũrũkĩtie guoko gwakwa, na ndĩmĩheaga o mũndũ ũrĩa ingĩenda kũhe. Na rĩu nĩngũneana mabũrũri manyu mothe kũrĩ ndungata yakwa Nebukadinezaru, mũthamaki wa Babuloni; nĩngũtũma o na nyamũ cia gĩthaka imũtungatagĩre.
english: to listen to the words of my servants the prophets whom I send to you, even rising up early and sending them—but you have not listened—

kikuyu: Niĩ mwene ndiamũũĩ, no ndokire ngĩbatithanagia na maaĩ nĩgeetha aguũrĩrio andũ a Isiraeli.”
english: I didn’t know him, but for this reason I came baptizing in water, that he would be revealed to Israel.”

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo Kristũ a maheeni na anabii a maheeni nĩmakeyumĩria monanie morirũ na maringe ciama nĩguo mahĩtithie andũ arĩa aamũre, korwo no kũhoteke. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio-rĩ, ikaragai mwĩiguĩte tondũ nĩndamwĩra maũndũ mothe mbere ya ihinda rĩtanakinya.
english: For false christs and false prophets will arise and will show signs and wonders, that they may lead astray, if possible, even the chosen ones.

kikuyu: Wetirie ũgitĩri kuuma kũrĩ ariũ a thoguo hatarĩ gĩtũmi; nĩwaũrire andũ nguo, ũkĩmatiga njaga. Arĩa anogu ndwamaheire maaĩ, na nĩwaimire arĩa ahũtu irio,
english: For you have taken pledges from your brother for nothing, and stripped the naked of their clothing.

kikuyu: Ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ na ma, mũkaarĩra na mũcakae rĩrĩa andũ a thĩ marakena. Inyuĩ mũkanyiitwo nĩ kĩeha, no kĩeha, kĩanyu nĩgĩkagarũrũka gĩtuĩke gĩkeno.
english: Most certainly I tell you that you will weep and lament, but the world will rejoice. You will be sorrowful, but your sorrow will be turned into joy.

kikuyu: Jerusalemu nĩehĩtie mũno, na nĩ ũndũ ũcio akanyiitwo nĩ thaahu. Arĩa othe maamũtĩĩte-rĩ, rĩu nĩmamũnyararĩte, nĩ ũndũ nĩmonete njaga yake; we mwene no gũcaaya aracaaya, na akamahutatĩra.
english: Jerusalem has grievously sinned. Therefore she has become unclean. All who honored her despise her, because they have seen her nakedness. Yes, she sighs and turns backward.

kikuyu: Ũguonĩguongoonaniaũneneonaũtheruwakwa, nandũmemenyekemaitho-inĩmandũrĩrĩnyingĩ. Hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩmakamenya atĩ niĩ nĩ niĩ Jehova.’
english: I will magnify myself and sanctify myself, and I will make myself known in the eyes of many nations. Then they will know that I am Yahweh.” ’

kikuyu: Arũme anyu makooragwo na rũhiũ rwa njora, nacio njamba cianyu cia ita ikaaniinĩrwo mbaara-inĩ.
english: Your men shall fall by the sword, and your mighty in the war.

kikuyu: Wee Jehova, thikĩrĩria na ũnjiguĩre tha; Wee Jehova, tuĩka ũteithio wakwa.”
english: For my life is spent with sorrow, my years with sighing. My strength fails because of my iniquity. My bones are wasted away.

kikuyu: Mũrũ wakwa, ta thikĩrĩria ũrĩa ngũkwĩra; tega gũtũ ũigue ciugo ciakwa.
english: For why should you, my son, be captivated with an adulteress? Why embrace the bosom of another?

kikuyu: “Aruti a wĩra arĩa maandĩkĩtwo thaa ikũmi na ĩmwe moka, makĩrĩhwo dinari ĩmwe o mũndũ. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, rĩrĩa arĩa maandĩkĩtwo mbere mookire-rĩ, meeciirĩtie mekũrĩhwo makĩria. No rĩrĩ, o nao maarĩhirwo dinari ĩmwe o mũndũ.
english: “When those who were hired at about the eleventh hour came, they each received a denarius.

kikuyu: we atanoomba thĩ kana ithaka ciayo, o na kana rũkũngũ o ruothe rwa thĩ.
english: while as yet he had not made the earth, nor the fields, nor the beginning of the dust of the world.

kikuyu: Wee Moabi, kaĩ ũrĩ na haaro-ĩ! Andũ a Kemoshu nĩaniine; aanake aku manyiitĩtwo mĩgwate magathaamio nao airĩtu aku magatahwo.
english: Woe to you, O Moab! The people of Chemosh are undone; for your sons are taken away captive, and your daughters into captivity.

kikuyu: Akĩmatongoria makiuma Misiri na agĩĩka morirũ na akĩringa ciama arĩ kũu Misiri, o na Iria-inĩ Itune, na ihinda rĩa mĩaka mĩrongo ĩna kũu werũ-inĩ.
english: This man led them out, having worked wonders and signs in Egypt, in the Red Sea, and in the wilderness for forty years.

kikuyu: “Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, Jehova ekuuga ũũ: ‘Nĩngacooka itũũra rĩa Jerusalemu ndĩrĩiguĩre tha, nayo nyũmba yakwa yakwo kuo rĩngĩ. Naruo rũrigi rũa gũthima nĩrũgatambũrũkio Jerusalemu,’ ũguo nĩguo Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe ekuuga.
english: Therefore Yahweh says: “I have returned to Jerusalem with mercy. My house shall be built in it,” says Yahweh of Armies, “and a line shall be stretched out over Jerusalem.” ’

kikuyu: “Andũ arĩa metharĩte makoorĩra ciĩruru-inĩ cia Heshiboni matirĩ na ũteithio, nĩgũkorwo mwaki nĩumĩte Heshiboni, rũrĩrĩmbĩ rũkoima gatagatĩ ka Sihoni; rũracina mothiũ ma Moabi, na rũgacina ciongo cia acio merahaga makarehe ngũĩ.
english: “Those who fled stand without strength under the shadow of Heshbon; for a fire has gone out of Heshbon, and a flame from the middle of Sihon, and has devoured the corner of Moab, and the crown of the head of the tumultuous ones.

kikuyu: Ti-itherũ, hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩũkona gĩkeno thĩinĩ wa ũcio Mwene-Hinya-Wothe, na nĩũgatiirĩra Ngai ũthiũ waku.
english: For then you will delight yourself in the Almighty, and will lift up your face to God.

kikuyu: nĩ ũndũ ũcio nĩngaguũria rũbaru rwa Moabi, nyambĩrĩirie na matũũra makuo ma mĩhaka-inĩ, na nĩmo Bethi-Jeshimothu, na Baali-Meoni, na Kiriathaimu, na nĩmo monagwo marĩ riiri wa bũrũri ũcio.
english: therefore, behold, I will open the side of Moab from the cities, from his cities which are on its frontiers, the glory of the country, Beth Jeshimoth, Baal Meon, and Kiriathaim,

kikuyu: Rũciaro rũtarĩ kĩene na rũtarĩ rĩĩtwa, nĩ rwarutũrũrirwo ruume kũu bũrũri-inĩ.
english: They are children of fools, yes, children of wicked men. They were flogged out of the land.

kikuyu: Rĩu-rĩ, umbũrai mehia manyu kũrĩ Jehova Ngai wa maithe manyu, na mwĩke ũrĩa endaga. Mwĩyamũraniei na andũ arĩa mamũthiũrũrũkĩirie, o na atumia anyu a kũngĩ.”
english: The guard stood, every man with his weapons in his hand, from the right side of the house to the left side of the house, along by the altar and the house, around the king.

kikuyu: Imakania nĩcihootete; gĩtĩĩo gĩakwa kĩũmbũrĩtwo ta kĩhurutĩtwo nĩ rũhuho, naguo ũgitĩri wakwa ũkabuĩria ta itu.
english: Terrors have turned on me. They chase my honor as the wind. My welfare has passed away as a cloud.

kikuyu: nayo mĩhianano ya kũhooywo nĩĩgathira biũ.
english: The idols shall utterly pass away.

kikuyu: “No rĩrĩ, ta gĩũkei inyuothe, geriai o rĩngĩ! Niĩ ndirĩ ndĩrona mũndũ mũũgĩ gatagatĩ kanyu.
english: But as for you all, come back. I will not find a wise man among you.

kikuyu: o hamwe na Maaseia, na Shemaia, na Eleazaru, na Uzi, na Jehohanani, na Malikija, na Elamu, na Ezeri. Nao aini maatongoragio nĩ Jezerahia. Na mũthenya ũcio nĩmarutire magongona
english: and Maaseiah, Shemaiah, Eleazar, Uzzi, Jehohanan, Malchijah, Elam, and Ezer. The singers sang loud, with Jezrahiah their overseer.

kikuyu: Mũhaka ũcio ũgĩthiĩ nginya Abudoni, na Rehobu, na Hamoni, na Kana, ũgĩkinya Sidoni ĩrĩa Nene.
english: and Ebron, Rehob, Hammon, and Kanah, even to great Sidon.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo Jesũ akiuga atĩrĩ, “Baba, wee Mwathani wa igũrũ na thĩ, nĩngũkũgaatha tondũ nĩũhithĩte andũ arĩa oogĩ na marĩ na ũmenyo maũndũ maya, na ũkamaguũrĩria tũkenge.
english: At that time, Jesus answered, “I thank you, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, that you hid these things from the wise and understanding, and revealed them to infants.

kikuyu: Kũigua nĩmũiguĩte maũndũ macio; ta kĩmarorei mothe. Githĩ mũtikamoimbũra atĩ nĩguo matariĩ? “Kuuma rĩu ndĩĩkwĩraga ũhoro wa maũndũ merũ, ngakwĩra ũhoro wa maũndũ mahithe, o marĩa wee ũtooĩ.
english: Indeed, he says, “It is too light a thing that you should be my servant to raise up the tribes of Jacob, and to restore the preserved of Israel. I will also give you as a light to the nations, that you may be my salvation to the end of the earth.”

kikuyu: Inyuĩ o na rĩu mũrĩ atheru nĩ ũndũ wa ũhoro ũrĩa ndĩmũheete.
english: You are already pruned clean because of the word which I have spoken to you.

kikuyu: Ishumaeli mũrũ wa Nethania hamwe na andũ acio ikũmi mokĩte nake magĩũkĩra, magĩtemanga Gedalia mũrũ wa * 40:10 Mũthamaki Nebukadinezaru aakinyire Jerusalemu hĩndĩ ĩrĩa ngũyũ na thabibũ ciagethagwo.
english: In the eleventh year of Zedekiah, in the fourth month, the ninth day of the month, a breach was made in the city.

kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo o ta ũrĩa rũheni ruumĩte irathĩro ruonekaga ithũĩro-rĩ, ũguo noguo gũũka kwa Mũrũ wa Mũndũ gũgaakorwo kũhaana.
english: For as the lightning flashes from the east, and is seen even to the west, so will the coming of the Son of Man be.

kikuyu: Nĩngananga Mwarĩ wa Zayuni, o ũcio mũthaka na mwĩbacĩrĩri mũno.
english: I will cut off the beautiful and delicate one, the daughter of Zion.

kikuyu: Marĩ hamwe magaatuĩka ta njamba cia ita irĩ hinya, marangĩrĩrie thũ ciao ndoro-inĩ ya njĩra hĩndĩ ya mbaara. Tondũ Jehova agaakorwo hamwe nao, makaarũa na mangʼaũranie ahaici a mbarathi.
english: Their buyers slaughter them and go unpunished. Those who sell them say, ‘Blessed be Yahweh, for I am rich;’ and their own shepherds don’t pity them.

kikuyu: Ũtukũ araaraga akĩrĩra na ruo, maithori makanyũrũrũkĩra makai-inĩ make. Arĩa othe marendanĩte nake-rĩ, hatirĩ o na ũmwe wa kũmũhooreria. Arata ake othe nĩmamũkunyanĩire; othe matuĩkĩte thũ ciake.
english: The Lord has swallowed up all the dwellings of Jacob without pity. He has thrown down in his wrath the strongholds of the daughter of Judah. He has brought them down to the ground. He has profaned the kingdom and its princes.

kikuyu: Matigari ma Isiraeli matigekaga ũũru; matikaheenanagia, kana ũhinga woneke tũnua-inĩ twao. Makaarĩĩaga na magakoma, na gũtirĩ mũndũ ũkaamamakia.”
english: The remnant of Israel will not do iniquity nor speak lies, neither will a deceitful tongue be found in their mouth, for they will feed and lie down, and no one will make them afraid.”

kikuyu: Nayo mĩtĩ ya gũtwarithia marikabu makĩmĩthondeka na mĩgandi kuuma Bashani; nacio mbaũ cia mĩthithinda cioimĩte ndwere-inĩ cia iria rĩa Kitimu, magĩgwakĩra nacio marikabu thĩinĩ, igemetio na mĩguongo.
english: Her daughters who are in the field will be slain with the sword. Then they will know that I am Yahweh.’

kikuyu: Ngĩcooka ngĩĩra athĩnjĩri-Ngai na andũ acio angĩ othe atĩrĩ: “Ũũ nĩguo Jehova ekuuga: Tigai gũthikĩrĩria anabii acio mamwĩraga atĩrĩ, ‘Hatigairie o kahinda kanini, nacio indo iria ciarutirwo nyũmba ya Jehova icookio kuuma Babuloni.’ Maramũrathĩra ũhoro wa maheeni.
english: Then the princes and all the people said to the priests and to the prophets: “This man is not worthy of death; for he has spoken to us in the name of Yahweh our God.”

kikuyu: Wĩhoke Jehova na wĩkage wega; tũũra bũrũri-inĩ ũkenagĩra ũrĩithio wa thayũ.
english: Trust in Yahweh, and do good. Dwell in the land, and enjoy safe pasture.

kikuyu: Mũndũ o wothe ũtũũraga thĩinĩ wa Kristũ ndehagia. Mũndũ o wothe wĩhagia ndarĩ amuona o na kana akamũmenya.
english: Whoever remains in him doesn’t sin. Whoever sins hasn’t seen him and doesn’t know him.

kikuyu: Ndĩĩmũrigiicĩrie nĩ magui; ndĩĩmũthiũrũrũkĩrie nĩ gĩkundi kĩa andũ arĩa meekaga ũũru, nĩmatheecangĩte moko na magũrũ makwa.
english: For dogs have surrounded me. A company of evildoers have enclosed me. They have pierced my hands and feet.†

kikuyu: nĩguo Jehova ndakone ũndũ ũcio arakare, akĩhũndũre marakara make kuuma kũrĩ thũ ĩyo.
english: lest Yahweh see it, and it displease him, and he turn away his wrath from him.

kikuyu: Wee Jehova, korwo no ũtare maita marĩa tũkwĩhagĩria-rĩ, nũũ ũngĩgwĩtiiria, Wee Jehova? No rĩrĩ, Wee nĩũrekanagĩra; nĩ ũndũ ũcio nĩwĩtigĩrĩtwo.
english: “Surely I will not come into the structure of my house, nor go up into my bed;

kikuyu: Andũ ta 40,000 methagathagĩte na meeohete indo cia mbaara makĩringa mũrĩmo ũcio, marĩ mbere ya Jehova, magĩkinya werũ-inĩ wa Jeriko.
english: and that you will save alive my father, my mother, my brothers, and my sisters, and all that they have, and will deliver our lives from death.”

kikuyu: Nĩaiyũragia matu na ũigũ; ahurunjaga rũheni rũkamatũrĩkia.
english: Yes, he loads the thick cloud with moisture. He spreads abroad the cloud of his lightning.

kikuyu: Iguanagĩrai tha, o ta ũrĩa Ithe wanyu aiguanagĩra tha. Gũtuanĩra Ciira
english: “Therefore be merciful, even as your Father is also merciful.

kikuyu: “Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, andĩka maũndũ marĩa wona, marĩa marĩ kuo rĩu na marĩa magooka thuutha-inĩ.
english: Write therefore the things which you have seen, and the things which are, and the things which will happen hereafter.

kikuyu: Ndũkanacumĩkĩre maũndũ ma mũndũ wa ngũĩ, kana ũthuure njĩra o na ĩmwe yake,
english: Don’t envy the man of violence. Choose none of his ways.

kikuyu: Ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ na ma, amwe a aya marũngiĩ haha matigacama gĩkuũ matonete ũthamaki wa Ngai.” Jesũ Kũgarũrũka ũrĩa Aatariĩ
english: But I tell you the truth: There are some of those who stand here who will in no way taste of death until they see God’s Kingdom.”

kikuyu: Niĩ nĩndĩkĩtie kwambanĩrio hamwe na Kristũ, na rĩu ti niĩ ndũũraga muoyo, no Kristũ nĩwe ũtũũraga muoyo thĩinĩ wakwa. Muoyo ũrĩa ndũũraga mwĩrĩ-inĩ ũyũ ndũũraga na ũndũ wa gwĩtĩkia Mũrũ wa Ngai, ũrĩa wanyendire na akĩĩruta nĩ ũndũ wakwa.
english: I have been crucified with Christ, and it is no longer I who live, but Christ lives in me. That life which I now live in the flesh, I live by faith in the Son of God, who loved me and gave himself up for me.

kikuyu: Kaĩ, inyuĩ arĩa mũikaraga mũkĩĩongagĩrĩra nyũmba mũrĩ na haro-ĩ! O inyuĩ mũnyiitithanagia gĩthaka kĩmwe na kĩrĩa kĩngĩ gĩgatuĩka kĩmwe, nginya hakaaga handũ ha gũtigĩria andũ arĩa angĩ, nĩguo mũtũũre bũrũri-inĩ mũrĩ inyuiki.
english: I heard the Lord’s voice, saying, “Whom shall I send, and who will go for us?” Then I said, “Here I am. Send me!”

kikuyu: Kũu igũrũ njata nĩciarũire, o kũu njĩra-inĩ ciacio ikĩrũa mbaara na Sisera.
english: From the sky the stars fought. From their courses, they fought against Sisera.

kikuyu: Gwĩtigĩra Jehova gũkinyagia mũndũ muoyo-inĩ: Hĩndĩ ĩyo mũndũ akahurũka aiganĩire, atarĩ na thĩĩna.
english: The fear of Yahweh leads to life, then contentment; he rests and will not be touched by trouble.

kikuyu: Thuutha wa ũguo mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai ũcio no nginya athambie nguo ciake na ethambe mwĩrĩ na maaĩ. Thuutha ũcio no acooke kambĩ, no arĩ na thaahu o nginya hwaĩ-inĩ.
english: Then the priest shall wash his clothes, and he shall bathe his flesh in water, and afterward he shall come into the camp, and the priest shall be unclean until the evening.

kikuyu: Garũrũkai mwĩthare, mũthiĩ mũkehithe thĩinĩ wa ngurunga iria ndiku, inyuĩ arĩa mũtũũraga kũu Dedani, tondũ nĩngarehithĩria Esaũ mwanangĩko rĩrĩa ngaamũherithia.
english: “Flee out of the middle of Babylon! Go out of the land of the Chaldeans, and be as the male goats before the flocks.

kikuyu: Mũndũ ũcio mũhakũre akĩũria Filipu atĩrĩ, “Ndagũthaitha, ta njĩĩra atĩrĩ, mũnabii araaria ũhoro wa ũ, nĩ ũhoro wake we mwene kana nĩ ũhoro wa mũndũ ũngĩ?”
english: The eunuch answered Philip, “Who is the prophet talking about? About himself, or about someone else?”

kikuyu: Ndungata ĩmwe ya mũirĩtu ĩkĩmuona na ũtheri wa mwaki aikarĩte hau, ĩkĩmũrora wega, ĩkiuga atĩrĩ, “Mũndũ ũyũ oima hamwe nake.”
english: A certain servant girl saw him as he sat in the light, and looking intently at him, said, “This man also was with him.”

kikuyu: Igũrũ rĩa wariĩ ũcio warĩ igũrũ rĩa mĩtwe yacio nĩ haarĩ kĩndũ kĩahaanaga ta gĩtĩ kĩa ũnene gĩakĩtwo na kahiga ka yakuti ĩrĩa ya bururu, na hau igũrũ rĩa gĩtĩ kĩu kĩa ũnene nĩ haarĩ na kĩndũ kĩonekaga kĩrĩ na mũhianĩre ta wa mũndũ.
english: Above the expanse that was over their heads was the likeness of a throne, as the appearance of a sapphire† stone. On the likeness of the throne was a likeness as the appearance of a man on it above.

kikuyu: Mwathani Jehova ekuuga atĩrĩ: “Matukũ nĩmarooka, rĩrĩa ngaatũma ngʼaragu bũrũri-inĩ, na ti ngʼaragu ya irio kana nyoota wa maaĩ, no nĩ ngʼaragu ya kũigua ciugo cia Jehova.
english: and whoever doesn’t fall down and worship shall be cast into the middle of a burning fiery furnace.

kikuyu: inyuĩ irĩma, na tũrĩma tuothe, na mĩtĩ ya matunda, na mĩtarakwa yothe,
english: to execute on them the written judgment. All his saints have this honor. Praise Yah!

kikuyu: Matianyootire hĩndĩ ĩrĩa aamatoongoreirie werũ-inĩ; nĩamarutĩire maaĩ kuuma rwaro-inĩ rwa ihiga; o na ningĩ agĩatũra rwaro rwa ihiga, namo maaĩ makiuma ho.
english: They didn’t thirst when he led them through the deserts. He caused the waters to flow out of the rock for them. He also split the rock and the waters gushed out.

kikuyu: Atĩrĩrĩ, ngiuga ũguo-rĩ, kaĩ arĩ andũ ndĩrenda kwĩyendithia kũrĩ o, kana nĩ kũrĩ Ngai? Kana hihi nĩ andũ ndĩrenda gũkenia? Korwo nĩ andũ ngeragia gũkenia-rĩ, ndingĩkorwo ndĩ ndungata ya Kristũ. Paũlũ gwĩtwo nĩ Ngai
english: For am I now seeking the favor of men, or of God? Or am I striving to please men? For if I were still pleasing men, I wouldn’t be a servant of Christ.

kikuyu: Mũtũmwo mwaganu atoonyaga thĩĩna-inĩ, no mũtũmwo mwĩhokeku nĩarehaga kĩhonia.
english: He who is quick to become angry will commit folly, and a crafty man is hated.

kikuyu: Anĩrĩrai mũkenete mũrĩ mbere ya Jehova, inyuĩ andũ othe a thĩ, tumũkai mũkenũrũrũke na nyĩmbo mũkĩinaga;
english: Enter into his gates with thanksgiving, and into his courts with praise. Give thanks to him, and bless his name.

kikuyu: Mũthenya ũcio andũ a Moabi magĩtuĩka ndungata cia andũ a Isiraeli, naguo bũrũri ũgĩkorwo na thayũ mĩaka mĩrongo ĩnana. Shamigari
english: So Moab was subdued that day under the hand of Israel. Then the land had rest eighty years.

kikuyu: Kwarĩ mweri wa kenda, nake mũthamaki aikarĩte nyũmba-inĩ ĩrĩa aikaraga hĩndĩ ya kĩmera kĩa heho, na haarĩ na mwaki waakanaga riiko-inĩ hau mbere yake.
english: Now the king was sitting in the winter house in the ninth month, and there was a fire in the brazier burning before him.

kikuyu: Jehova nĩahaandĩte gĩtĩ gĩake kĩa ũnene kũu igũrũ, na ũthamaki wake wathaga maũndũ mothe.
english: Yahweh has established his throne in the heavens. His kingdom rules over all.

kikuyu: Icio nĩcio ciarĩ mbarĩ cia Isakaru; arĩa maatarirwo maarĩ andũ 64,300.
english: These are the families of Issachar according to those who were counted of them, sixty-four thousand three hundred.

kikuyu: “Wee nĩwe ngaahe bũrũri wa Kaanani ũtuĩke igai rĩrĩa ũkaagaya.”
english: saying, “To you I will give the land of Canaan, the lot of your inheritance,”

kikuyu: Mĩhũmũ ya Mũrungu nĩĩthondekaga mbarabu, namo maaĩ harĩa maaramĩire makanyiitana.
english: By the breath of God, ice is given, and the width of the waters is frozen.

kikuyu: Angĩkorwo nĩmũũĩ atĩ we nĩ mũthingu, kĩmenyei atĩ mũndũ ũrĩa wĩkaga maũndũ marĩa magĩrĩire nĩaciarĩtwo nĩwe.
english: If you know that he is righteous, you know that everyone who practices righteousness has been born of him.

kikuyu: Atĩrĩrĩ, nĩtwagĩrĩirwo nĩ kũrutĩra Kaisari igooti kana ca?”*
english: For these are days of vengeance, that all things which are written may be fulfilled.

kikuyu: na Zikilagi, na Bethi-Marikabothu, na Hazari-Susa,
english: Ziklag, Beth Marcaboth, Hazar Susah,

kikuyu: Mwena wa gũthini nĩho ikundi cia kambĩ ya mũhĩrĩga wa Rubeni igaakorwo, harĩa bendera yao ĩrĩ. Mũtongoria wa andũ a Rubeni nĩ Elizuru mũrũ wa Shedeuru.
english: “On the south side shall be the standard of the camp of Reuben according to their divisions. The prince of the children of Reuben shall be Elizur the son of Shedeur.

kikuyu: “Tiga gwĩtigĩra, wee Jakubu ndungata yakwa: o nawe Isiraeli-rĩ, ndũkamake. Ti-itherũ nĩngakũhonokia ngũrutĩte kũndũ kũraya, njiaro ciaku ciume bũrũri ũrĩa ciatahirwo igĩtwarwo. Jakubu nĩagakorwo na thayũ na ũgitĩri rĩngĩ, na gũtirĩ mũndũ ũgaatũma acooke gwĩtigĩra.”
english: “But don’t you be afraid, Jacob my servant. Don’t be dismayed, Israel; for, behold, I will save you from afar, and your offspring from the land of their captivity. Jacob will return, and will be quiet and at ease. No one will make him afraid.

kikuyu: Ngoro yakwa nĩĩrarĩrĩra Moabi, andũ arĩa maingatĩtwo kuo morĩire Zoari, magathiĩ nginya Egilathu-Shelishiya. Mambatĩte na njĩra ya gũthiĩ Luhithu, magathiĩ makĩrĩraga; o na ningĩ njĩra-inĩ ya gũthiĩ Horonaimu, maracakaya nĩ ũndũ wa mwanangĩko ũrĩa ũmakorete.
english: A throne will be established in loving kindness. One will sit on it in truth, in the tent of David, judging, seeking justice, and swift to do righteousness.

kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa andũ maiguire ciugo ciake, amwe makiuga atĩrĩ, “Ti-itherũ mũndũ ũyũ nĩ we Mũnabii ũrĩa.” Arĩa angĩ makiuga atĩrĩ, “Ũyũ nĩwe Kristũ.” Nao angĩ makĩũria atĩrĩ, “Kwahoteka atĩa Kristũ oime Galili?
english: But now you seek to kill me, a man who has told you the truth which I heard from God. Abraham didn’t do this.

kikuyu: na Hazari-Shuali, na Biri-Shiba na matũũra marĩo;
english: in Hazar Shual, in Beersheba and its towns, 28in Ziklag, in Meconah and in its towns,

kikuyu: Nĩndĩrendaga kũmenya kĩrĩa maramũthitangagĩra, na nĩ ũndũ ũcio ndĩramũtwara Kĩama-inĩ kĩao.
english: Desiring to know the cause why they accused him, I brought him down to their council.

kikuyu: Akorwo ti ũguo ndĩmũrute nguo aikare arĩ njaga, na ndũme aikare njaga o ta mũthenya ũrĩa aaciarirwo; ngaamũtua ta werũ, na ndĩmũgarũre atuĩke bũrũri mũngʼaru, na ndĩmũũrage na nyoota.
english: lest I strip her naked, and make her bare as in the day that she was born, and make her like a wilderness, and set her like a dry land, and kill her with thirst.

kikuyu: makĩamba kũmũtwara kwa Anasi, ũrĩa warĩ mũthoniwe wa Kaiafa, ũrĩa warĩ mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai ũrĩa mũnene mwaka ũcio.
english: and led him to Annas first, for he was father-in-law to Caiaphas, who was high priest that year.

kikuyu: Ndamũthaitha, inyuĩ ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ, tuĩkai ta niĩ, nĩgũkorwo niĩ nduĩkĩte ta inyuĩ. Mũtirĩ mwanjĩka ũũru.
english: I beg you, brothers, become as I am, for I also have become as you are. You did me no wrong,
